title: Set up Tailwind v4 + PostCSS build pipeline
design:
## Goal
Tailwind CSS v4 integrated with shadow-cljs via PostCSS. Dev server runs both watchers in parallel. A single Tailwind class renders correctly in the browser.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Install npm dependencies
```bash
npm install --save-dev tailwindcss @tailwindcss/postcss postcss postcss-cli concurrently
```

### 2. Create PostCSS config
Create file: postcss.config.mjs
```javascript
export default {
  plugins: {
    "@tailwindcss/postcss": {},
  }
}
```

### 3. Create main CSS file
Create file: src/css/app.css
```css
@import "tailwindcss";

@theme {
  /* Surface colors */
  --color-surface: #0d0d1a;
  --color-surface-raised: #1a1a2a;
  --color-surface-hover: #2a2a3a;

  /* Border colors */
  --color-border: #555;
  --color-border-accent: #4A9BD9;
  --color-border-selected: #FFD700;

  /* Text colors */
  --color-text: #eee;
  --color-text-muted: #AAA;

  /* Accent colors */
  --color-accent: #4A9BD9;
  --color-gold: #FFD700;

  /* MTG mana colors */
  --color-mana-white: #FFF;
  --color-mana-blue: #4A9BD9;
  --color-mana-black: #A080C0;
  --color-mana-red: #D9534F;
  --color-mana-green: #5CB85C;
  --color-mana-colorless: #AAA;
}
```

### 4. Link compiled CSS in HTML
Edit: resources/public/index.html
Add before closing </head> tag:
```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/app.css">
```

Output path: resources/public/css/app.css (compiled, gitignored)

### 5. Update .gitignore
Add line: resources/public/css/

### 6. Update Makefile
Update dev target:
```makefile
dev:
	npx concurrently --kill-others \
	  "npx shadow-cljs watch app" \
	  "npx postcss src/css/app.css -o resources/public/css/app.css --watch"
```

Add build-css target and update clean:
```makefile
build-css:
	mkdir -p resources/public/css
	npx postcss src/css/app.css -o resources/public/css/app.css

clean:
	rm -rf out/ .shadow-cljs/ resources/public/js/ resources/public/css/
```

Update .PHONY to include build-css.

### 7. Verify integration
- Run make dev
- Add class bg-surface to the body div in core.cljs: [:div.bg-surface ...]
- Confirm dark background renders in browser at localhost:8080
- Confirm changing bg-surface to bg-surface-raised hot-reloads
- Revert test class after verification
- Run make validate to confirm no regressions

## Success Criteria
- [ ] package.json devDependencies includes tailwindcss, @tailwindcss/postcss, postcss, postcss-cli, concurrently
- [ ] postcss.config.mjs exists with @tailwindcss/postcss plugin (exact content above)
- [ ] src/css/app.css exists with @import tailwindcss and all 16 @theme tokens listed above
- [ ] resources/public/index.html has <link rel="stylesheet" href="/css/app.css">
- [ ] resources/public/css/ added to .gitignore
- [ ] make dev starts both shadow-cljs and postcss watchers (--kill-others ensures both stop on Ctrl+C)
- [ ] make clean removes resources/public/css/ alongside existing JS cleanup
- [ ] resources/public/css/app.css is generated when make dev runs (non-browser verification: file exists and contains Tailwind reset styles)
- [ ] A Tailwind utility class (e.g., bg-surface) renders correctly in the browser
- [ ] make validate still passes (no regressions)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO tailwind.config.js (Tailwind v4 uses CSS-first @theme, not JS config)
- ❌ NO @tailwind directives (v3 pattern — v4 uses @import "tailwindcss")
- ❌ NO compiled CSS committed to git (generated artifact, must be gitignored)
- ❌ NO CSS output in resources/public/js/ (conflicts with shadow-cljs output)
- ❌ NO content array in config (v4 auto-detects, no manual content paths needed)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Tailwind Content Auto-Detection:**
- Tailwind v4 auto-scans for class usage in files. During dev, shadow-cljs compiles to .shadow-cljs/ and resources/public/js/cljs-runtime/. Tailwind should pick up classes from the compiled JS.
- If classes don't appear in compiled CSS, check that postcss is watching the correct source and Tailwind is scanning the output directory.
- Verify by checking generated CSS file contains the expected utility class rules.

**Process Lifecycle:**
- concurrently --kill-others ensures both shadow-cljs and postcss die together on Ctrl+C.
- If postcss crashes independently, concurrently will kill shadow-cljs too, making failure visible.

**Output Directory:**
- resources/public/css/ must exist before postcss runs. The Makefile build-css target creates it with mkdir -p.
- For make dev, postcss-cli creates output directories automatically.

**Existing Tests:**
- This task only touches build tooling. No ClojureScript source changes (except a temporary test class that gets reverted). make validate should pass unchanged.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Add trigger/description to schema and create-trigger
design:
## Goal
Add :trigger/description attribute to schema and pass it through the trigger creation pipeline so triggers on the stack include their description.

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- db/schema.cljs:64-72 - trigger schema attributes
- engine/triggers.cljs:14-31 - create-trigger function (receives data, returns trigger map)
- engine/triggers.cljs:240-246 - fire-matching-triggers (calls create-trigger, only passes :effects)
- engine/triggers.cljs:296-302 - fire-and-resolve-triggers (same pattern, also needs update)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create test in test/fizzle/engine/triggers_test.cljs:
- test_trigger_with_description_includes_description_in_data
  - Create trigger with {:trigger/description "deals 1 damage"}
  - Verify trigger map has :trigger/description in :trigger/data
- test_trigger_without_description_still_works
  - Create trigger without :trigger/description
  - Verify trigger map created successfully (backwards compatible)

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] db/schema.cljs:72 - Add `:trigger/description {}` after :trigger/data
- [ ] engine/triggers.cljs:31 - Add :trigger/description to create-trigger return map:
      ```clojure
      {:trigger/id (random-uuid)
       :trigger/type trigger-type
       :trigger/source source-id
       :trigger/controller controller-id
       :trigger/data data
       :trigger/description (:description data)}  ;; NEW: extract from data
      ```
- [ ] engine/triggers.cljs:245 - Update fire-matching-triggers to pass description:
      ```clojure
      {:effects (:trigger/effects trigger)
       :description (:trigger/description trigger)}  ;; NEW
      ```
- [ ] engine/triggers.cljs:301 - Update fire-and-resolve-triggers same way:
      ```clojure
      {:effects (:trigger/effects trigger)
       :description (:trigger/description trigger)}  ;; NEW
      ```
- [ ] Run `make test` - verify existing tests pass
- [ ] Run `make validate` - verify lint/format pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :trigger/description attribute exists in schema (db/schema.cljs)
- [ ] create-trigger extracts description from data map and includes in return value
- [ ] fire-matching-triggers passes :trigger/description in data map
- [ ] fire-and-resolve-triggers passes :trigger/description in data map
- [ ] Test: trigger with description has it in :trigger/description
- [ ] Test: trigger without description still works (nil description OK)
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] `make validate` passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Data Flow Understanding (CRITICAL)**:
The description flows: card definition → fire-matching-triggers → create-trigger → trigger on stack
- Card has `:card/triggers [{:trigger/type :x :trigger/description "..." :trigger/effects [...]}]`
- fire-matching-triggers reads the trigger from card, must pass description in data
- create-trigger receives data map, extracts description to top-level attribute
- Trigger entity stored in Datascript with :trigger/description

**Backwards Compatibility**:
- Triggers without :trigger/description must still work
- (:trigger/description trigger) returns nil when not present - this is OK
- Do NOT require description, just support it

**Two Call Sites**:
- fire-matching-triggers (line 240-246) - for stack-based triggers
- fire-and-resolve-triggers (line 296-302) - for immediate triggers (mana abilities)
- BOTH must be updated to pass description

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO adding description as required field (must be optional for backwards compatibility)
- ❌ NO modifying create-trigger signature (keep 4 args, pass description in data map)
- ❌ NO forgetting fire-and-resolve-triggers (both call sites must be updated)
close_reason: Closed

title: Remove 20 RED tautological/duplicate tests
design:
## Goal
Remove tests that provide false confidence by passing regardless of code correctness.

## Tests to Remove (by file)

### engine/scry_effect_test.cljs (DELETE ENTIRE FILE - 5 tests)
All 5 tests verify a no-op pass-through returns DB unchanged. The actual scry logic lives in the event layer (scry_confirm_test.cljs already tests it).

### engine/effects_test.cljs (2 tests)

- test-add-mana-missing-mana-key (line 221): Tests Clojure empty? on nil

### cards/careful_study_test.cljs (2 tests)
- test-selection-with-invalid-object-ids (line 269): Tests Datascript query semantics
- test-empty-selection-confirmation (line 288): Asserts (not= 0 2)

### cards/cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs (1 test)

### cards/polluted_delta_test.cljs (1 test)

### events/routing_test.cljs (2 tests)

- test-set-active-screen-switches-back-to-game (line 17): Tests two assoc calls

### events/init_game_test.cljs (4 tests)

- test-iggy-pop-decklist-all-card-ids-valid (line 207): Data validation, not behavior

### events/mode_selection_test.cljs (1 test)

### history/integration_test.cljs (1 test)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] make validate passes

close_reason: Closed

title: Extract selection/costs.cljs with X-cost detection, builders, and event handlers
design:
## Goal

Extract cost-related selection types from selection.cljs into events/selection/costs.cljs. Includes exile-cards-cost and x-mana-cost builders, detection helpers, defmethods, and re-frame event handlers.

## Context
Completed fizzle-115w (targeting.cljs extraction). selection.cljs now has ~351 lines with costs, zone-ops, and shared event handlers remaining.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create events/selection/costs.cljs
Move from selection.cljs:

**Detection helpers:**
- has-exile-cards-x-cost? (line 35)
- get-exile-cards-x-cost (line 44)
- has-mana-x-cost? (line 53)

**Builders:**
- build-exile-cards-selection (line 63)
- build-x-mana-selection (line 99)

**Defmethods on core/execute-confirmed-selection:**
- :exile-cards-cost (line 248)
- :x-mana-cost (line 269)

**Re-frame event handlers:**
- ::cancel-exile-cards-selection (line 321)
- ::increment-x-value (line 328)
- ::decrement-x-value (line 339)
- ::cancel-x-mana-selection (line 349)

Requires for costs.cljs:
- datascript.core :as d (for d/q, d/db-with in defmethods)
- fizzle.db.queries :as queries (for query-zone-by-criteria, get-mana-pool)
- fizzle.engine.mana :as mana (for resolve-x-cost)
- fizzle.engine.rules :as rules (for cast-spell-mode)
- fizzle.engine.zones :as zones (for move-to-zone in exile-cards-cost)
- fizzle.events.selection.core :as core (for defmethod registrations)
- re-frame.core :as rf (for event handlers)

Does NOT need: grants, effects, targeting, resolution, stack

### 2. Update selection.cljs
Remove all moved functions, defmethods, and event handlers. After this extraction, selection.cljs will have ONLY:
- Zone-ops code (discard, cleanup-discard, graveyard-return builders + defmethods)
- build-selection-for-effect :discard, :return-from-graveyard, :default defmethods
- Shared event handlers (set-pending, toggle, confirm, cancel)

Remove requires no longer needed:
- REMOVE: fizzle.engine.mana (only used by x-mana-cost)
- KEEP: datascript.core (still used by zone-ops defmethods? Check — none use d/q)
  Actually check if d is still needed after removing exile-cards and x-mana defmethods.
  If not, REMOVE datascript.core too.
- KEEP: fizzle.db.queries (for build-graveyard-selection)
- KEEP: fizzle.engine.grants (for cleanup-discard)
- KEEP: fizzle.engine.rules (check if still needed — no, rules only used by costs code. REMOVE)
- KEEP: fizzle.engine.zones (for discard, cleanup, graveyard defmethods)
- KEEP: fizzle.events.selection.core (for defmethods)
- KEEP: re-frame.core (for shared event handlers)

### 3. Update game.cljs
game.cljs uses 5 cost functions from selection/:
- selection/has-exile-cards-x-cost? → sel-costs/has-exile-cards-x-cost?
- selection/get-exile-cards-x-cost → sel-costs/get-exile-cards-x-cost
- selection/build-exile-cards-selection → sel-costs/build-exile-cards-selection
- selection/has-mana-x-cost? → sel-costs/has-mana-x-cost?
- selection/build-x-mana-selection → sel-costs/build-x-mana-selection

Add [fizzle.events.selection.costs :as sel-costs] to requires.

### 4. Update views/modals.cljs
modals.cljs dispatches 4 cost events via ::selection-events/ alias:
- ::selection-events/cancel-exile-cards-selection
- ::selection-events/increment-x-value
- ::selection-events/decrement-x-value
- ::selection-events/cancel-x-mana-selection

After extraction, these move to costs namespace. Options:
A. Add [fizzle.events.selection.costs :as cost-events] and update dispatches
B. Keep events in selection.cljs and just forward to costs

Prefer A (clean extraction). Update the 4 dispatch calls.

### 5. Update test files
Search for test files using cost functions. Expected: exile_cost_test.cljs and possibly flash_of_insight_test.cljs.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events/selection/costs.cljs exists with all cost domain code
- [ ] costs.cljs requires only core.cljs and engine/* (no other domain files)
- [ ] All 3 detection helpers, 2 builders, 2 defmethods, 4 event handlers moved
- [ ] game.cljs updated to import cost functions from new namespace
- [ ] modals.cljs updated to dispatch cost events from new namespace
- [ ] selection.cljs no longer has cost code
- [ ] All ~1266 tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] No circular dependencies

## Anti-Patterns
- NO importing domain files in costs.cljs (only core.cljs and engine/*)
- NO leaving orphaned event handlers in selection.cljs
- NO keeping stale requires in selection.cljs after extraction
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract modal components from core.cljs
description:
## Problem
core.cljs is 1,592 lines with 13 modal components that are 80-95% identical. Massive DRY violations in UI code.

## Specifics
- 3 player-target modals (player-target-modal, ability-player-target-modal, cast-time-player-target-modal) are structurally identical with different :on-click handlers
- 2 object-target modals share 95% code
- Modal overlay style (position fixed, rgba backdrop, z-index 1000) copy-pasted 13 times
- Modal container style (#1a1a2a, 2px solid #4A9BD9, border-radius 12px) copy-pasted 13 times
- Confirm/cancel button pattern repeated 8+ times with only event keyword changing
- Card selection grid rendering: identical for loop in 5+ modals

## Proposed Refactoring
- Extract shared style constants (modal-overlay-style, modal-container-style)
- Create generic modal-base component
- Create parameterized player-target-modal and object-target-modal factories
- Extract confirm-button and cancel-button helpers
- Extract card-selection-grid helper
- Split into views/modals.cljs, views/zones.cljs, views/controls.cljs
- Keep core.cljs as ~200 line router/init

## Success Criteria
- core.cljs under 300 lines
- No duplicated modal structure
- Shared style constants
- Estimated 400-500 LOC net reduction
- All modals still function correctly
close_reason: Superseded by epic fizzle-x38 with refined design and iterative task approach

title: Task 1: Implement :destroy effect type
design:
## Goal
Add :destroy effect type to effects.cljs that moves target permanent to owner's graveyard.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing effect patterns (reference)
- src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:484-528 - :grant-flashback shows target-ref resolution pattern
- src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:391-406 - :sacrifice shows zone movement pattern
- src/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs:39 - move-to-zone function signature

### 2. Write tests first (TDD) - RED phase
Create test file or add to existing effects_test.cljs:

```clojure
;; Test 1: Happy path - destroy moves target to graveyard
(deftest test-destroy-effect-moves-target-to-graveyard
  ;; Setup: enchantment on battlefield, spell targeting it
  ;; Action: execute :destroy effect
  ;; Assert: enchantment is in graveyard zone)

;; Test 2: Owner vs controller - target goes to OWNER's graveyard
(deftest test-destroy-effect-uses-owner-graveyard-not-controller
  ;; Setup: Player A owns enchantment, Player B controls it (e.g., stolen)
  ;; Action: destroy effect on the enchantment
  ;; Assert: enchantment in Player A's graveyard, not Player B's)

;; Test 3: Edge case - nil object-id returns db unchanged
(deftest test-destroy-effect-nil-object-id-is-noop
  ;; Setup: valid db
  ;; Action: execute :destroy with object-id nil
  ;; Assert: db unchanged)

;; Test 4: Edge case - missing target-ref returns db unchanged  
(deftest test-destroy-effect-missing-target-ref-is-noop
  ;; Setup: source object has no stored targets
  ;; Action: execute :destroy with target-ref :foo
  ;; Assert: db unchanged)

;; Test 5: Edge case - target object no longer exists
(deftest test-destroy-effect-missing-target-object-is-noop
  ;; Setup: stored target-id points to non-existent object
  ;; Action: execute :destroy
  ;; Assert: db unchanged, no error)
```

### 3. Implementation checklist (GREEN phase)

- [ ] effects.cljs - Add multimethod:
```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect-impl :destroy
  ;; Destroy target permanent - move it to owner's graveyard.
  ;;
  ;; Effect keys:
  ;;   :effect/target-ref - Target reference keyword to look up from
  ;;                        :object/targets on the casting object.
  ;;
  ;; Handles edge cases:
  ;;   - object-id nil: no-op (returns db unchanged)
  ;;   - No stored target for target-ref: no-op
  ;;   - Target object doesn't exist: no-op
  ;;
  ;; Note: Goes to OWNER's graveyard, not controller's. This matters
  ;; for stolen permanents (Control Magic effects).
  [db _player-id effect object-id]
  (if-not object-id
    db  ; No source object - no-op
    (let [source-obj (q/get-object db object-id)
          target-ref (:effect/target-ref effect)
          stored-targets (:object/targets source-obj)
          target-id (get stored-targets target-ref)]
      (if-not target-id
        db  ; No stored target for this ref - no-op
        (if-let [_target-obj (q/get-object db target-id)]
          (zones/move-to-zone db target-id :graveyard)
          db)))))  ; Target doesn't exist - no-op
```

- [ ] Verify zones/move-to-zone handles owner correctly (it should - zone is per-player)
- [ ] Add tests to effects_test.cljs following patterns from existing tests

## Success Criteria
- [ ] test-destroy-effect-moves-target-to-graveyard passes
- [ ] test-destroy-effect-uses-owner-graveyard-not-controller passes
- [ ] test-destroy-effect-nil-object-id-is-noop passes
- [ ] test-destroy-effect-missing-target-ref-is-noop passes
- [ ] test-destroy-effect-missing-target-object-is-noop passes
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Nil object-id**
- Source object not provided (effect run without context)
- MUST return db unchanged, not throw
- Pattern: `(if-not object-id db ...)`

**Edge Case: Missing stored targets**
- Source object exists but has no :object/targets map
- Or target-ref key doesn't exist in map
- MUST return db unchanged
- Pattern: `(if-not target-id db ...)`

**Edge Case: Target object already gone**
- Target-id stored but object moved zones/removed
- MUST return db unchanged
- Pattern: `(if-let [_target-obj (q/get-object db target-id)] ... db)`

**Owner vs Controller**
- MTG rule: destroyed permanents go to owner's graveyard
- zones/move-to-zone should handle this (zones are per-player based on owner)
- VERIFY: Check that move-to-zone uses object's owner, not controller

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow :grant-flashback pattern (lines 484-528) for target-ref resolution
- Follow :sacrifice pattern (lines 391-406) for zone movement

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN for this task)
- ❌ NO using :effect/target directly (must use :effect/target-ref with stored targets)
- ❌ NO throwing on edge cases (return db unchanged)
- ❌ NO assuming target exists (must check with q/get-object)
- ❌ NO using controller's graveyard (must be owner's)
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Always-visible zone counts (GY, Library, Exile)
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Zone counts for Graveyard, Library, and Exile always visible on game screen without expanding any panel
- Both player and opponent counts shown
- Graveyard count visually indicates threshold status (>= 7 cards in graveyard)
- Placed below mana pool/storm count area in main left column

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Player GY, Library, Exile counts always visible on game screen
- [ ] Opponent GY, Library, Exile counts always visible on game screen
- [ ] GY count changes to accent color when >= 7 (threshold active)
- [ ] Counts update reactively as zones change (cast, draw, mill, exile, etc.)
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO putting counts inside collapsible right panels (defeats 'always visible' — the entire point of this feature)
- NO click-to-expand card lists from zone counts (keep simple — graveyard panel already exists for browsing cards)
- NO fetching card images or detailed card data for display (just integer counts)
- NO new CSS grid columns or layout restructuring (add component within existing main column flow)

## Approach
Add a new zone-counts view component below the existing mana pool / storm count area in the main left column. The component renders two compact rows — one for player zones, one for opponent zones — showing GY, Library, and Exile counts. Graveyard count uses accent color when threshold (>= 7) is met, matching the existing threshold badge style in the graveyard panel.

New re-frame subscriptions derive zone counts from game-db. A simple query helper counts entities in a zone for a given player, keeping the subscription layer thin.

## Architecture
- New: src/fizzle/views/zone_counts.cljs — zone-counts-view component
- New subs in: src/fizzle/subs/game.cljs — player/opponent zone count subscriptions
- Possibly new query in: src/fizzle/db/queries.cljs — zone-count-by-player helper
- Modified: src/fizzle/core.cljs — add zone-counts-view after mana pool flex row
- Tests: test/fizzle/views/zone_counts_test.cljs for component, test/fizzle/subs/game_test.cljs for subs

## Parallelism Map

### Independent Work Streams
1. **Subscriptions + queries** (subs/game.cljs, db/queries.cljs)
   - Add zone count subscriptions for both players
   - Estimated complexity: low
2. **View component** (views/zone_counts.cljs)
   - Build the zone-counts-view Reagent component
   - Estimated complexity: low
3. **Integration** (core.cljs)
   - Wire component into game layout
   - Estimated complexity: trivial

### Stream Dependencies
- Stream 2 depends on Stream 1 (component subscribes to zone count subs)
- Stream 3 depends on Stream 2 (layout requires component)

### Suggested Waves
- All sequential — each stream depends on previous

### Parallelism Assessment
- Independent streams: 0 (fully sequential)
- Recommendation: solo execution
- Rationale: Small feature, all streams depend on each other, no parallel opportunity

## Design Rationale
### Problem
During gameplay, players need to track graveyard size (threshold at 7+) and opponent library size (Brain Freeze lethality). Currently graveyard count is only visible when the graveyard panel is expanded, and library count is not shown at all during gameplay. Players must mentally track these critical numbers.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- views/core.cljs:57-79 — Game screen is CSS grid: 1 main column + 3 collapsible right panels
- views/graveyard.cljs — Already shows 'GRAVEYARD (N)' with threshold badge, but only when panel expanded
- subs/game.cljs — Has ::graveyard sub returning full card data, but no count-only subs
- db/queries.cljs:79 — get-objects-in-zone can fetch any zone's objects
- views/mana_pool.cljs — Mana pool + storm count rendered as flex row at bottom of main column

**Existing patterns:**
- Storm count displayed as compact inline element next to mana pool
- Threshold badge in graveyard panel uses accent color styling
- Zone queries use player entity + zone keyword to filter objects

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. New zone-counts component below mana pool ✓
**What it is:** Dedicated Reagent component in views/zone_counts.cljs, placed after mana pool flex row in main column. Two rows showing player and opponent zone counts.

**Pros:**
- Clean separation of concerns (own file, own tests)
- Follows existing pattern of small focused view components
- Slots naturally into layout flow

**Cons:**
- Adds one more visual element to main column

**Chosen because:** Matches codebase pattern of separate view components, doesn't modify existing components

#### 2. Extend mana-pool view ❌
**What it is:** Add zone counts into the existing mana-pool.cljs component as a sub-section.

**Why explored:** Would keep zone info near other game metrics without a new file.

**Pros:** Fewer files, compact

**Cons:** Mixes mana and zone concerns, mana-pool.cljs grows beyond its purpose

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Violates single-responsibility — mana pool component should only handle mana display

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We're redesigning the entire bottom panel as a unified game-stats dashboard

#### 3. Floating fixed-position bar ❌
**What it is:** CSS fixed-position bar at bottom of viewport, always visible regardless of scroll.

**Why explored:** Guarantees visibility even on small screens with scrolling.

**Pros:** Truly always visible

**Cons:** Overlaps content, non-standard layout, extra CSS complexity, fights with grid layout

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Over-engineered — the main column doesn't scroll in normal use, and a floating bar adds CSS complexity for no practical gain

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Users report scrolling issues hiding zone counts on small screens

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- GY, Library, Exile counts for both players
- Threshold visual indicator on graveyard count
- New subscriptions and view component

**Out of scope:**
- Clicking counts to open zone details (graveyard panel exists for this)
- Hand or battlefield counts (already visible on screen)
- Opponent life total in this component (already shown in opponent view)
- Zone count animations or transitions

### Open Questions
- Exact Tailwind classes for layout — resolve during implementation by matching existing mana pool styling

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Where to place counts? | Below mana pool area | Component goes after mana-pool/storm flex row in core.cljs |
| Which zones? | GY + Library + Exile | Three counts per player, six total |
| Show opponent? | All opponent zones | Need opponent-specific subscriptions |
| Threshold indicator? | Yes, color change at 7+ | GY count uses accent color class when >= 7 |

### Research Deep-Dives
#### Current Zone Display
**Question:** Where are zone counts currently shown?
**Sources:** views/graveyard.cljs, views/core.cljs, subs/game.cljs
**Findings:**
- Graveyard count only in collapsible panel header
- Library count only in opening-hand screen and game-over modal
- Exile not visualized anywhere
- No always-visible zone count component exists
**Conclusion:** New component needed — cannot enhance existing components to meet 'always visible' requirement

### Dead-End Paths
None — straightforward feature with clear requirements.

### Open Concerns Raised
None raised during brainstorming.
close_reason: All success criteria met. Zone counts always visible below mana pool for both players. Threshold indicator on GY. 11 new tests total, make validate passes.

title: Create interactive-effect? predicate and unify detection functions
design:
## Goal
Create a single `interactive-effect?` predicate in selection/resolution.cljs that is the canonical source of truth for effect interactivity. Rewrite `has-selection-effect?` and `find-selection-effect-index` as thin wrappers. Investigate :pile-choice gap.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### Step 1: Read existing code
- `src/main/fizzle/events/selection/resolution.cljs:23-53` — current `has-selection-effect?` and `find-selection-effect-index`
- `src/main/fizzle/events/selection/core.cljs:35-54` — `build-selection-for-effect` multimethod dispatch function
- `src/main/fizzle/events/selection/library.cljs` — :pile-choice builder registration

### Step 2: Investigate :pile-choice
- Find card definitions using :pile-choice effects: `grep -r ':pile-choice' src/`
- Trace call path: does :pile-choice reach `resolve-spell-with-selection` or is it triggered differently?
- Decision: add to predicate if same path, document as separate path otherwise

### Step 3: Write tests (TDD — RED phase)
Test file: `src/test/fizzle/events/selection_resolution_test.cljs` (new file)

**Predicate tests (interactive-effect?):**
- `test-tutor-is-interactive`: `{:effect/type :tutor}` → true (catches: missing always-interactive type)
- `test-peek-and-select-is-interactive`: `{:effect/type :peek-and-select}` → true (catches: type that was missing from has-selection-effect?)
- `test-scry-positive-amount-is-interactive`: `{:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 3}` → true (catches: conditional check bug)
- `test-scry-zero-amount-is-not-interactive`: `{:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 0}` → false (catches: missing amount guard)
- `test-scry-nil-amount-is-not-interactive`: `{:effect/type :scry}` → false (catches: NPE on nil amount)
- `test-discard-player-selection-is-interactive`: `{:effect/type :discard :effect/selection :player}` → true (catches: missing :selection :player check)
- `test-discard-random-selection-is-not-interactive`: `{:effect/type :discard :effect/selection :random}` → false (catches: overly broad type matching)
- `test-return-from-graveyard-player-is-interactive`: `{:effect/type :return-from-graveyard :effect/selection :player}` → true (catches: covered by :selection :player)
- `test-return-from-graveyard-random-is-not-interactive`: `{:effect/type :return-from-graveyard :effect/selection :random}` → false (catches: type-only matching bug)
- `test-any-player-target-is-interactive`: `{:effect/type :draw :effect/target :any-player}` → true (catches: missing target check)
- `test-non-interactive-effect-types`: `{:effect/type :add-mana}`, `{:effect/type :draw}`, `{:effect/type :mill}`, `{:effect/type :exile-self}` → all false (catches: overly broad predicate)
- `test-empty-effect-map-is-not-interactive`: `{}` → false (catches: NPE on missing keys)
- `test-overlapping-conditions`: `{:effect/type :tutor :effect/selection :player}` → true (catches: double-match doesn't cause issues, predicate uses `or` so fine)

**Wrapper tests (has-selection-effect? and find-selection-effect-index):**
- `test-has-selection-effect-empty-list`: `[]` → falsy (catches: nil/empty handling)
- `test-has-selection-effect-finds-peek-and-select`: list with :peek-and-select → truthy (catches: the detection gap bug — was missing before)
- `test-find-selection-effect-index-returns-correct-position`: 3-effect list with interactive at index 1 → 1 (catches: index calculation bug)
- `test-find-selection-effect-index-nil-when-none`: list of non-interactive effects → nil

**Sync test (predicate covers all builders):**
- `test-predicate-covers-all-builder-dispatch-values`: get all dispatch values from `(methods build-selection-for-effect)`, construct representative effect map for each, verify `interactive-effect?` returns true. Catches: future drift when adding new interactive effect types.

### Step 4: Create interactive-effect? predicate (GREEN phase)
Add to resolution.cljs between line 22 (section comment) and line 23 (current has-selection-effect?):

```clojure
(defn interactive-effect?
  "Returns true if this effect requires player selection/interaction.
   This is the single source of truth for which effects pause resolution
   to collect player input via the selection system.

   Checks:
   - :effect/selection :player — explicit player-choice marker
   - :effect/type :tutor — always interactive (search library)
   - :effect/type :scry with amount > 0 — arrange top N cards
   - :effect/target :any-player — choose target player
   - :effect/type :peek-and-select — choose from revealed cards"
  [effect]
  (or (= :player (:effect/selection effect))
      (= :tutor (:effect/type effect))
      (and (= :scry (:effect/type effect))
           (pos? (or (:effect/amount effect) 0)))
      (= :any-player (:effect/target effect))
      (= :peek-and-select (:effect/type effect))))
```

Note: :pile-choice may be added here if investigation shows it uses the same path.

### Step 5: Rewrite has-selection-effect? (GREEN phase)
Replace lines 23-30 with:
```clojure
(defn has-selection-effect?
  "Check if any effect in the list requires player selection."
  [effects]
  (some interactive-effect? effects))
```

### Step 6: Rewrite find-selection-effect-index (GREEN phase)
Replace lines 33-53 with:
```clojure
(defn find-selection-effect-index
  "Find the index of the first effect that requires player selection.
   Returns nil if no selection effect found."
  [effects]
  (first (keep-indexed (fn [i effect]
                         (when (interactive-effect? effect) i))
                       effects)))
```

### Step 7: Verify (REFACTOR phase)
- `make validate` — all tests pass, lint clean, format clean
- Verify no callers of has-selection-effect? or find-selection-effect-index changed behavior

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `interactive-effect?` predicate exists in selection/resolution.cljs
- [ ] Predicate handles all 5 detection patterns: :selection :player, :tutor, :scry+pos?, :any-player target, :peek-and-select
- [ ] `has-selection-effect?` body is `(some interactive-effect? effects)`
- [ ] `find-selection-effect-index` uses `interactive-effect?` in keep-indexed
- [ ] :pile-choice investigated: either added to predicate or documented as separate code path
- [ ] 15+ new tests exist covering predicate, wrappers, and sync with builder multimethod
- [ ] Sync test verifies predicate covers all `build-selection-for-effect` dispatch values
- [ ] All existing 1265+ tests pass unchanged
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (task-specific)
- ❌ NO changes to engine/effects.cljs (epic anti-pattern: engine stays pure)
- ❌ NO changes to build-selection-for-effect dispatch logic (scope: detection only)
- ❌ NO new requires in resolution.cljs that could create circular dependencies
- ❌ NO second predicate — interactive-effect? is THE source of truth
- ❌ NO changing behavior of has-selection-effect? or find-selection-effect-index for existing callers beyond fixing the documented gap

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Overlapping conditions**
- An effect can match multiple conditions (e.g., `{:effect/type :tutor :effect/selection :player}`)
- The predicate uses `or` so this is safe — returns true on first match
- Test explicitly to document this behavior

**Edge Case: Malformed effect maps**
- Effect with no :effect/type key: `{}` → should return false
- Effect with nil values: `{:effect/type nil}` → should return false
- All keyword comparisons use `=` which handles nil safely (returns false)

**Edge Case: Empty effects list**
- `(has-selection-effect? [])` → `(some pred [])` → nil (falsy) ✅
- `(find-selection-effect-index [])` → `(first (keep-indexed ...))` → nil ✅

**Edge Case: :return-from-graveyard detection**
- `find-selection-effect-index` currently checks `(= :return-from-graveyard (:effect/type effect))` as a standalone condition
- But :return-from-graveyard is only interactive when `:effect/selection :player`
- The new predicate does NOT have a standalone :return-from-graveyard check — it's covered by the :selection :player check
- This means `{:effect/type :return-from-graveyard}` (no :selection key) will no longer match
- VERIFY: all :return-from-graveyard card effects that are interactive have :selection :player
- This is a behavior change in find-selection-effect-index — test to confirm correctness

**:pile-choice Investigation Guide**
- If :pile-choice goes through resolve-spell-with-selection → add to predicate
- If :pile-choice is triggered by a different path (e.g., separate event handler) → document in a code comment, skip adding to predicate
- The sync test will flag this either way

**Reference Implementation**
- Existing code: resolution.cljs:23-53
- Builder multimethod: core.cljs:35-54
- Pattern to follow: simple predicate function, same style as existing helpers
close_reason: interactive-effect? predicate created, wrappers rewritten, 26 tests added, sync test covers all builder dispatch values, :pile-choice documented as separate chained path, :return-from-graveyard over-matching fixed

title: Extract selection/targeting.cljs with cast-time and player-target domains
design:
## Goal

Extract targeting-related selection types from selection.cljs into events/selection/targeting.cljs. This includes cast-time-targeting and player-target builders, helpers, defmethods, and builder multimethod registrations.

## Context
Completed fizzle-du6w (library.cljs extraction). selection.cljs now has ~524 lines with targeting, costs, zone-ops, and shared event handlers remaining.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create events/selection/targeting.cljs
Move these from selection.cljs:

**Builders:**
- build-cast-time-target-selection (selection.cljs ~line 238)
- build-player-target-selection (private → stays private, callers co-located) (selection.cljs ~line 186)

**Helpers:**
- cast-spell-with-targeting (selection.cljs ~line 264)
- confirm-cast-time-target (selection.cljs ~line 302)

**Defmethods on core/execute-confirmed-selection:**
- :cast-time-targeting (selection.cljs ~line 414)
- :player-target (selection.cljs ~line 375)

**Defmethods on core/build-selection-for-effect:**
- :player-target (selection.cljs ~line 216)

Requires for targeting.cljs:
- fizzle.events.selection.core :as core
- fizzle.events.selection.resolution :as resolution (used by :player-target defmethod for find-selection-effect-index, split-effects-around-index)
- datascript.core :as d (used by confirm-cast-time-target)
- fizzle.db.queries :as queries (used by cast-spell-with-targeting)
- fizzle.engine.effects :as effects (used by :player-target defmethod)
- fizzle.engine.rules :as rules (used by cast-spell-with-targeting, confirm-cast-time-target)
- fizzle.engine.targeting :as targeting (used by build-cast-time-target-selection, cast-spell-with-targeting)
- fizzle.engine.zones :as zones (used by :player-target defmethod — currently not used, check)

Does NOT need: engine/grants, engine/mana, engine/stack, clojure.set, re-frame

### 2. Update selection.cljs
Remove all moved functions and defmethods. Also remove 3 requires no longer needed:
- REMOVE: fizzle.engine.effects (only used by :player-target defmethod, moving out)
- REMOVE: fizzle.engine.targeting (only used by targeting code, moving out)
- REMOVE: fizzle.events.selection.resolution (only used by :player-target defmethod, moving out)

KEEP these requires (still used by remaining costs/zone-ops code):
- datascript.core (for :exile-cards-cost, :x-mana-cost defmethods)
- fizzle.db.queries (for builders)
- fizzle.engine.grants (for :cleanup-discard)
- fizzle.engine.mana (for :x-mana-cost)
- fizzle.engine.rules (for :exile-cards-cost, :x-mana-cost defmethods)
- fizzle.engine.zones (for :discard, :cleanup-discard, :graveyard-return, :exile-cards-cost)
- fizzle.events.selection.core (for defmethod registrations)
- re-frame.core (for event handlers)

### 3. Update game.cljs
game.cljs line 241 uses selection/build-cast-time-target-selection. Change to:
- Add [fizzle.events.selection.targeting :as sel-targeting] to requires
- Change selection/build-cast-time-target-selection → sel-targeting/build-cast-time-target-selection
NOTE: game.cljs does NOT call cast-spell-with-targeting (only test files do)

### 4. Update 2 test files
Exact callers identified:

1. src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs
   - selection/cast-spell-with-targeting (11 calls) → targeting/cast-spell-with-targeting
   - selection/confirm-cast-time-target (11 calls) → targeting/confirm-cast-time-target
   - Uses NO other selection functions → replace selection require with targeting

2. src/test/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis_test.cljs
   - selection/cast-spell-with-targeting (6 calls) → targeting/cast-spell-with-targeting
   - selection/confirm-cast-time-target (5 calls) → targeting/confirm-cast-time-target
   - Uses NO other selection functions → replace selection require with targeting

For each: add [fizzle.events.selection.targeting :as targeting] to requires.
Remove [fizzle.events.selection :as selection] since no other selection functions used.

### 5. Event handler namespace
targeting.cljs has NO re-frame event handlers (targeting is done via toggle/confirm on the base selection events). No namespace keyword issues.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events/selection/targeting.cljs exists with all targeting domain code
- [ ] targeting.cljs requires only core.cljs, resolution.cljs, and engine/* (no other domain files)
- [ ] All 2 builders, 2 helpers, 3 defmethods moved
- [ ] game.cljs updated to import build-cast-time-target-selection from new namespace
- [ ] selection.cljs no longer has targeting code
- [ ] selection.cljs no longer requires engine/effects, engine/targeting, or selection.resolution
- [ ] 2 test files updated (recoup_test.cljs, deep_analysis_test.cljs)
- [ ] All ~1266 tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] No circular dependencies

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: zones require in targeting.cljs**
The :player-target execute defmethod does NOT use zones directly (it uses effects/execute-effect). Verify before adding zones to targeting.cljs requires — may not be needed. If not needed, omit (cleaner dependency graph).

**Edge Case: game.cljs alias naming**
game.cljs already has [fizzle.engine.targeting :as targeting] require. The selection targeting module needs a DIFFERENT alias. Use sel-targeting to avoid conflict.

**Edge Case: resolution.cljs require creates deeper dependency**
targeting.cljs → resolution.cljs → core.cljs. This is fine (acyclic), but verify resolution doesn't transitively depend on targeting (would be circular).

**Edge Case: build-player-target-selection visibility**
Function is defn- (private) in selection.cljs. When moved to targeting.cljs, callers (:player-target builder defmethod) are co-located, so it stays defn- (private). No visibility change needed.

**Edge Case: deep_analysis_test.cljs has fizzle.engine.targeting require**
deep_analysis_test.cljs already has [fizzle.engine.targeting :as targeting]. The new require would use same alias. Must use DIFFERENT alias like sel-targeting, or replace the engine/targeting alias since the test may use it elsewhere.

**Reference: Pattern from library extraction**
Follow exact same pattern as fizzle-du6w: create file, move functions, update callers, run make validate.

## Anti-Patterns
- NO importing domain files in targeting.cljs (only core.cljs, resolution.cljs, and engine/*)
- NO leaving orphaned defmethods in selection.cljs for targeting types
- NO keeping stale requires in selection.cljs after extraction (clean up effects/targeting/resolution)
- NO using same alias as existing engine/targeting in test files (use sel-targeting or similar)
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Implement :exile-self effect type
design:
## Goal
Implement the :exile-self effect type that moves the resolving spell to the exile zone instead of the graveyard.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing effect patterns
Reference these files to understand the pattern:
- src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:106-108 - :add-mana handler (simplest example)
- src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:297-315 - :sacrifice handler (moves object to zone)
- src/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs:39-67 - move-to-zone function

**Handler signature:**
```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect-impl :exile-self
  [db player-id effect object-id]
  ;; object-id is the spell/permanent that is the SOURCE of this effect
  ;; For :exile-self, we move object-id to :exile zone
  ...)
```

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs (or add to existing)

**Test scenarios:**
- test_exile_self_moves_spell_to_exile_zone: Create spell on stack, apply :exile-self effect, verify spell is in :exile zone (catches: effect doesn't move to correct zone)
- test_exile_self_with_nil_object_id_returns_db_unchanged: Pass nil object-id, verify db unchanged (catches: nil pointer exception)
- test_exile_self_with_nonexistent_object_returns_db_unchanged: Pass invalid object-id, verify db unchanged (catches: missing guard clause)

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Add `(defmethod execute-effect-impl :exile-self ...)` handler
- [ ] Handler guards against nil object-id (return db unchanged)
- [ ] Handler guards against nonexistent object (return db unchanged)
- [ ] Handler calls `(zones/move-to-zone db object-id :exile)` for valid object
- [ ] src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs - Add 3 test cases listed above

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :exile-self effect moves spell to :exile zone (verified: query object zone after effect)
- [ ] Nil object-id returns db unchanged (no exception)
- [ ] Nonexistent object-id returns db unchanged (no exception)
- [ ] 3 unit tests pass: `make test`
- [ ] Existing tests still pass: `make test`
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing: `make validate`

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Nil object-id**
- object-id can be nil if effect is executed without a source object
- MUST guard: `(if-not object-id db ...)`
- See :grant-flashback handler at effects.cljs:411 for pattern

**Edge Case: Nonexistent object**
- Object may have been removed between effect queuing and execution
- MUST guard by checking object exists before moving
- See :sacrifice handler at effects.cljs:309-315 for pattern

**Reference Implementation**
Copy pattern from :sacrifice (effects.cljs:297-315):
```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect-impl :exile-self
  [db _player-id _effect object-id]
  (if-not object-id
    db  ; No source object - no-op
    (if (d/q '[:find ?e .
               :in $ ?oid
               :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
             db object-id)
      (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :exile)
      db)))  ; Object doesn't exist - no-op
```

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO throwing exceptions on nil/invalid object-id (robustness: return db unchanged)
- ❌ NO skipping existence check (safety: object may not exist)
- ❌ NO hardcoding zone keyword (use :exile from effect if needed for future flexibility)
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Unify selection system + auto-confirm single-select
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Unify all selection state to use `:selection/selected` (set) — remove `:selection/selected-target` field entirely
- Targeting selections (player, cast-time, ability) become set-based with select-count=1 and `:selection/valid-targets`
- Replace 6 separate toggle/select handlers with one unified `::toggle-selection` handler
- Replace per-type confirm event handlers with a `confirm-selection` multimethod dispatching on `:selection/type`
- Single-select selections auto-confirm when user clicks a card/target (no separate Confirm click)
- Auto-confirm applies to: tutor (select-count=1), cast-time-targeting, player-target, ability-targeting
- Multi-select selections keep their Confirm button unchanged
- Find Nothing button preserved for single-select tutors
- Deselecting a card (clicking already-selected) does NOT trigger auto-confirm
- All existing behavior preserved — this is a refactoring, not a behavior change (except auto-confirm addition)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] No `:selection/selected-target` field anywhere in codebase
- [ ] All selection types use `:selection/selected` (set) + `:selection/select-count`
- [ ] Targeting types use `:selection/valid-targets` for validation
- [ ] One `::toggle-selection` handler replaces all 6 toggle/select handlers
- [ ] One `confirm-selection` multimethod replaces per-type confirm event handlers
- [ ] Single-select tutor auto-confirms on card click
- [ ] All targeting types (cast-time object/player, ability object/player, player-target) auto-confirm
- [ ] Multi-select types (Intuition, discard, scry, pile-choice, graveyard-return, peek-and-select, exile-cards, x-mana) unchanged
- [ ] Deselecting does not auto-confirm
- [ ] Find Nothing still works
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] No increase in total LOC in selection.cljs (should decrease)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO `:selection/selected-target` field (must be fully removed, not coexisting)
- ❌ NO per-type toggle handlers (must be one unified handler)
- ❌ NO removing Find Nothing button (fail-to-find is MTG rules-correct)
- ❌ NO auto-confirm on multi-select types
- ❌ NO auto-confirm when deselecting
- ❌ NO auto-confirm on pile-choice even when hand-count=1
- ❌ NO auto-confirm on graveyard-return or peek-and-select even with max=1 (user may want 0)
- ❌ NO circular namespace dependencies
- ❌ NO changing behavior of any existing game mechanic (refactoring only + auto-confirm addition)
- ❌ NO big-bang refactoring (each phase must leave tests green)

## Approach
Phase the refactoring so tests stay green throughout:

**Phase 1: Unify data model** — Update all builders to use `:selection/selected` (set) + `:selection/select-count`. Update confirm handlers to read from `:selection/selected`. Remove `:selection/selected-target`. Update modals and subs.

**Phase 2: Unify toggle handler** — Merge 6 toggle/select handlers into one `::toggle-selection`. It handles both set-based and targeting selections (both are now set-based). Update modals to dispatch unified event.

**Phase 3: Multimethod confirm** — Extract confirm logic from per-type reg-event-db handlers into a multimethod `execute-confirmed-selection` dispatching on `:selection/type`. Shared wrapper handles: validation, call multimethod, remaining-effects, cleanup, clear/chain. One `::confirm-selection` event handler.

**Phase 4: Auto-confirm** — In unified toggle handler, after updating selection, check if single-select is complete and type supports auto-confirm. Call confirm-selection-impl directly (synchronous, no flicker).

## Architecture

### Unified Data Model
All selections use:
- `:selection/type` — discriminator (:tutor, :discard, :player-target, :cast-time-targeting, :ability-targeting, etc.)
- `:selection/selected` — set of selected IDs (object-ids, player keywords)
- `:selection/select-count` — max items to select (1 for targeting)
- `:selection/valid-targets` — (targeting only) set of valid IDs to validate against
- `:selection/player-id`, `:selection/spell-id`, `:selection/remaining-effects`, etc. — unchanged

### Unified Toggle Handler
`::toggle-selection [_ id]`
1. If `:selection/valid-targets` exists, validate id is in set — reject invalid
2. If already selected, deselect (remove from set)
3. If select-count=1, replace selection (single-select behavior)
4. If under limit, add to selection
5. If at limit, ignore
6. Auto-confirm check: if count matches select-count AND not deselecting AND auto-confirmable type → call confirm

### Multimethod Confirm
`(defmulti execute-confirmed-selection (fn [game-db selection] (:selection/type selection)))`

Each method returns one of:
- `{:db game-db}` — standard, wrapper handles remaining-effects + cleanup
- `{:db game-db :pending-selection next-sel}` — chain to next selection
- `{:db game-db :finalized? true}` — fully handled (pre-cast, ability types)

Shared wrapper (`confirm-selection-impl`):
1. Validate selection count/candidates
2. Call multimethod
3. If `:pending-selection` → set as new pending-selection
4. If `:finalized?` → just clear pending-selection
5. Else → execute remaining-effects → cleanup-selection-source → clear pending-selection

### Files Changed
- `selection.cljs` — Major: builders, toggle, confirm, cleanup (net LOC decrease)
- `abilities.cljs` — Remove `::select-ability-object-target`, update `build-ability-target-selection`, move/keep `confirm-ability-target` as multimethod impl
- `modals.cljs` — Read `:selection/selected` instead of `:selection/selected-target`, dispatch unified toggle/confirm events
- `subs/game.cljs` — Update subscriptions exposing selection state
- Test files — Update to use unified event names and data model

## Parallelism Map

### Independent Work Streams
1. **Phase 1: Data model unification** (selection.cljs builders + confirm reads, abilities.cljs builders, modals.cljs reads, subs)
   - Estimated complexity: high
2. **Phase 2: Unified toggle** (selection.cljs, modals.cljs dispatches)
   - Estimated complexity: medium
3. **Phase 3: Multimethod confirm** (selection.cljs, abilities.cljs)
   - Estimated complexity: high
4. **Phase 4: Auto-confirm** (selection.cljs toggle handler)
   - Estimated complexity: low

### Stream Dependencies
- Phase 2 depends on Phase 1 (needs unified data model)
- Phase 3 depends on Phase 1 (confirm handlers read unified fields)
- Phase 4 depends on Phases 2 and 3 (needs unified toggle + multimethod confirm)

### Suggested Waves
- Wave 1: Phase 1 (data model — must come first)
- Wave 2: Phases 2 + 3 could parallelize (different handlers in same file)
- Wave 3: Phase 4 (auto-confirm)

### File Ownership Boundaries
| Stream | Owns (exclusive) | Shared (needs coordination) |
|--------|-------------------|-----------------------------|
| Phase 1 | builder functions, subs | selection.cljs, modals.cljs (with Phase 2) |
| Phase 2 | toggle handlers section | selection.cljs, modals.cljs dispatch (with Phase 1) |
| Phase 3 | confirm handlers section | selection.cljs (with Phase 2) |
| Phase 4 | auto-confirm logic | toggle handler (with Phase 2) |

### Parallelism Assessment
- Independent streams: 0-1 (Phases 2+3 may parallelize but touch same file)
- Recommendation: solo execution
- Rationale: Sequential phases in same file, TDD requires tests green between each phase. Safe refactoring demands incremental changes.

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The selection system has 6 toggle handlers doing variations of the same thing, 11 confirm handlers with duplicated patterns, and two incompatible field conventions (`:selection/selected` vs `:selection/selected-target`). Adding auto-confirm on top of this requires patching 4 handlers with similar logic. Refactoring first makes auto-confirm trivial and reduces ongoing maintenance burden.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- 6 toggle/select handlers: `::toggle-selection` (L1017), `::toggle-peek-card-selection` (L1109), `::toggle-exile-card-selection` (L1407), `::select-player-target` (L1341), `::select-cast-time-object-target` (L1528), `::select-ability-object-target` (abilities.cljs:281)
- `::select-cast-time-object-target` and `::select-ability-object-target` are 100% identical code
- `::select-player-target` does no validation while object selectors do
- All confirm handlers follow: validate → execute → remaining-effects → cleanup → clear
- `cleanup-selection-source` already extracted as shared helper (L709)
- `confirm-ability-target` already extracted as standalone function (abilities.cljs:150)
- Three confirm handler families: resolution-phase, pre-cast, ability

**Duplication metrics:**
- ~73 lines duplicated across confirm handlers
- Toggle handlers: 3 set-based variants, 3 scalar variants doing the same thing

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Unified data model + toggle + multimethod confirm ✓
**What it is:** Normalize all selections to set-based, one toggle, multimethod confirm, auto-confirm in toggle.
**Pros:** Maximum simplification, auto-confirm trivial, single source of truth
**Cons:** Largest refactoring surface, touches many files
**Chosen because:** User wants to do it right. The duplication is maintenance burden.

#### 2. Just unify toggles + auto-confirm ❌
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Leaves confirm handler duplication untouched, doesn't fix data model inconsistency

#### 3. Just add auto-confirm on current code ❌
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Patches 4 handlers with similar logic, adds complexity to already complex code

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** data model unification, toggle unification, confirm multimethod, auto-confirm, test updates
**Out of scope:** scry modal UX changes (two-pile drag is structurally different, keep as-is), x-mana UI (increment/decrement is different from toggle), pile-choice auto-confirm (consequential, defer)

### Open Questions
- Should Phases 2+3 be combined into one task or kept separate? (decide during execution)
- `::select-player-target` is dispatched directly from modals.cljs:79 — unified toggle needs to handle player keywords alongside object-ids in the same set

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| How should fail-to-find work? | Keep Find Nothing button | Modal still renders, button preserved |
| Auto-confirm player targets? | Yes | All targeting types auto-confirm |
| Include ability targeting? | Yes, absolutely | Ability targeting in scope |
| How much refactoring? | Major refactor first | Full unification before auto-confirm |
| Unify data model too? | Yes, go further | `:selection/selected-target` removed entirely |
| Full scope comfortable? | Yes, do it right | Touch everything: selection.cljs, abilities.cljs, modals.cljs, subs, tests |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Toggle Handler Duplication
**Question explored:** How similar are the 6 toggle handlers?
**Findings:** Set-based handlers all do toggle-in-set with max-count check. Scalar handlers all do toggle-single-value. cast-time-object and ability-object are 100% identical. player-target lacks validation that object handlers have.
**Conclusion:** All 6 can be one handler with set-based data model

#### Confirm Handler Families
**Question explored:** Can confirm handlers be unified?
**Findings:** Three families (resolution-phase, pre-cast, ability). Shared pattern: validate → execute → remaining-effects → cleanup → clear. Differences: chaining (tutor→pile, player→discard), pre-cast skips remaining-effects, ability has own flow.
**Conclusion:** Multimethod for type-specific execution, shared wrapper for common pattern. Return-value protocol handles variations.

#### Cross-Namespace Dependencies
**Question explored:** Can ability targeting move into selection.cljs?
**Findings:** `confirm-ability-target` (abilities.cljs:150) already standalone function. Selection.cljs doesn't currently require abilities. game.cljs requires both.
**Conclusion:** Ability confirm can stay in abilities.cljs, registered as multimethod implementation. Or move to selection.cljs since it's selection logic.

### Dead-End Paths
- Considered per-handler auto-confirm patching → Abandoned due to 4-way duplication
- Considered interceptor approach → Over-engineered

### Open Concerns Raised
- Pile-choice with hand-count=1 could auto-confirm → Deferred (consequential)
- Graveyard-return with max=1 could auto-confirm → Deferred (allows 0)
close_reason: All 4 phases complete: unified data model, unified toggle handler, confirm-selection multimethod, auto-confirm. All 13 success criteria verified. 1542→1454 LOC (-88). All tests pass.

title: Task 1: Implement X cost system in mana.cljs
design:
## Goal
Implement generic X cost support in the mana system so cards with X in their mana cost can be cast.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- src/main/fizzle/engine/mana.cljs:25-42 - can-pay? currently handles :colorless as generic
- src/main/fizzle/engine/mana.cljs:55-88 - pay-mana with generic cost handling
- src/test/fizzle/engine/mana_test.cljs - 19 existing tests to not regress

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**Test file:** src/test/fizzle/engine/mana_test.cljs

Add these specific test functions:

```clojure
;; === X cost tests ===

(deftest can-pay-x-cost-always-true-test
  ;; X can always be 0, so any X cost is payable
  (testing "can-pay? returns true for X cost even with empty pool"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (true? (mana/can-pay? db :player-1 {:x 1 :blue 1}))))))

(deftest pay-mana-x-value-zero-test
  ;; When X=0, only pay the non-X portion
  (testing "pay-mana with x-value=0 pays only fixed costs"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:blue 2}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:x 1 :colorless 1 :blue 1} 0)]
      (is (= 0 (:blue (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest pay-mana-x-value-converts-to-colorless-test
  ;; {:x 1 :colorless 1 :blue 1} with x-value=3 -> {:colorless 4 :blue 1}
  (testing "pay-mana resolves X to colorless mana"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 5 :blue 1}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:x 1 :colorless 1 :blue 1} 3)]
      ;; Total: 4 colorless (3 from X + 1 fixed) + 1 blue
      ;; Should pay 4 from black (generic), 1 from blue
      (is (= 1 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))
      (is (= 0 (:blue (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest pay-mana-x-value-nil-defaults-zero-test
  ;; Backwards compatibility: omitting x-value treats X as 0
  (testing "pay-mana without x-value treats X as 0"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:blue 2}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:x 1 :blue 1})]  ; No x-value arg
      ;; X=0, so just pay 1 blue
      (is (= 1 (:blue (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest pay-mana-no-x-key-unchanged-test
  ;; Backwards compatibility: costs without :x work exactly as before
  (testing "pay-mana without :x key works unchanged (backwards compat)"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 3 :blue 1}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:colorless 1 :black 1})]
      (is (= 1 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))
      (is (= 1 (:blue (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest pay-mana-multiple-x-multiplies-test
  ;; Future-proofing: {:x 2} means "2X" (two X variables)
  (testing "pay-mana with {:x 2} multiplies x-value by 2"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 6}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:x 2} 3)]  ; 2X where X=3 -> 6
      (is (= 0 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] mana.cljs - Add resolve-x-cost helper function:
  ```clojure
  (defn resolve-x-cost
    "Convert X in mana cost to colorless. x-value defaults to 0 if nil.
     {:x 1 :colorless 1 :blue 1} with x-value=3 -> {:colorless 4 :blue 1}"
    [mana-cost x-value]
    (let [x-count (get mana-cost :x 0)
          x-val (or x-value 0)
          x-as-colorless (* x-count x-val)
          existing-colorless (get mana-cost :colorless 0)]
      (-> mana-cost
          (dissoc :x)
          (assoc :colorless (+ existing-colorless x-as-colorless)))))
  ```

- [ ] mana.cljs - Update can-pay? to ignore :x key (X is always payable as X can be 0):
  ```clojure
  ;; In can-pay?, before processing:
  (let [cost-without-x (dissoc cost :x)
        ...]
  ```

- [ ] mana.cljs - Update pay-mana signature to accept optional x-value:
  ```clojure
  (defn pay-mana
    ([db player-id cost] (pay-mana db player-id cost nil))
    ([db player-id cost x-value]
     ;; Call resolve-x-cost before existing logic
     (let [resolved-cost (resolve-x-cost cost x-value)]
       ...existing logic with resolved-cost...)))
  ```

- [ ] Verify all 19 existing mana tests still pass (regression check)
- [ ] Verify all 6 new X cost tests pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 6 new X cost tests pass (specific tests listed above)
- [ ] 19 existing mana tests pass (no regression)
- [ ] can-pay? handles costs with :x key (ignores X, always true for X portion)
- [ ] pay-mana accepts optional x-value parameter
- [ ] pay-mana with x-value resolves {:x N} to colorless correctly
- [ ] pay-mana without x-value defaults X to 0 (backwards compat)
- [ ] make validate passing

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Nil x-value**
- When x-value is nil or omitted, treat as 0
- This ensures backwards compatibility with existing callers
- Implementation: `(or x-value 0)` in resolve-x-cost

**Edge Case: Cost without :x key**
- Existing cards don't have :x in mana-cost
- MUST work exactly as before (resolve-x-cost is no-op when :x absent)
- Test: pay-mana-no-x-key-unchanged-test

**Edge Case: :x 0 in cost**
- Technically valid but weird (0X = always 0)
- resolve-x-cost handles correctly: (* 0 x-value) = 0
- No special handling needed

**Design Decision: :x multiplier**
- :x 1 means "1X" (standard)
- :x 2 means "2X" (e.g., a card costing XX)
- Formula: x-as-colorless = x-count * x-value
- Future-proofs for Fireball (XR) vs Blaze (XX)

**Not in scope for this task:**
- UI for choosing X value (handled at casting layer)
- Storing x-value on stack object (handled by rules.cljs casting flow)
- Minimum X validation (card-specific, handled by card definition)

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN for this task)
- ❌ NO mutating x-value parameter (pure functions only)
- ❌ NO validation of x-value >= 0 in mana.cljs (caller responsibility, like can-pay?)
- ❌ NO special-casing Flash of Insight (generic X support only)
- ❌ NO changing pay-mana's existing behavior when x-value is nil/omitted
close_reason: X cost system implemented: resolve-x-cost helper, can-pay? ignores :x, pay-mana accepts optional x-value. 6 new tests pass, 19 existing tests pass, make validate passes.

title: Implement storm trigger resolution and cast-spell integration
design:
## Goal

Implement the storm keyword behavior: when a spell with :storm is cast, create a storm trigger on the stack. When that trigger resolves, create N copies of the spell (where N = spells cast before this turn).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context

Completed fizzle-21w: Trigger infrastructure in place (create-trigger, add-trigger-to-stack, resolve-trigger multimethod, remove-trigger, get-stack-items).

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

Reference implementations:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs - resolve-trigger multimethod pattern
- src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:38-53 - cast-spell flow
- src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:56-69 - resolve-spell flow
- src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:31-39 - get-storm-count

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/storm_test.cljs

Test cases with bug-catching rationale:
- [ ] test-cast-storm-spell-creates-trigger: Cast spell with :storm keyword creates trigger on stack (catches: missing trigger creation)
- [ ] test-storm-trigger-on-top-of-spell: Storm trigger has higher stack-order than spell (catches: wrong resolution order)
- [ ] test-resolve-storm-creates-copies: Storm trigger resolution creates N copies where N = storm count BEFORE cast (catches: off-by-one error)
- [ ] test-storm-copies-have-is-copy-flag: Copies have :object/is-copy true (catches: missing copy flag)
- [ ] test-storm-copies-do-not-increment-storm: Casting 1 spell, then storm with 1 copy, storm count still 2 (catches: copies incrementing count)
- [ ] test-storm-count-zero-creates-no-copies: Storm as first spell creates 0 copies (catches: off-by-one at boundary)
- [ ] test-storm-count-one-creates-one-copy: Storm after 1 spell creates 1 copy (catches: calculation error)
- [ ] test-storm-count-two-creates-two-copies: Storm after 2 spells creates 2 copies (catches: calculation error)
- [ ] test-storm-source-missing-returns-unchanged-db: If source object gone, resolve-trigger returns db unchanged (catches: nil reference crash)

### 3. Implementation checklist

Schema addition (src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs):
- [ ] Add :object/is-copy {} - Boolean, marks spell copies (default: false/nil)

Add to src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs:
- [ ] defmethod resolve-trigger :storm [db trigger]
  - Get source-id from (:trigger/source trigger)
  - Get copy-count from (:count (:trigger/data trigger))
  - If source object not found, return db unchanged (defensive)
  - Loop copy-count times, calling create-spell-copy for each
  - Return final db with all copies on stack
- [ ] create-spell-copy [db source-object-id controller-id] -> db with copy on stack
  - Query source object to get :object/card ref
  - Create new object with :object/id (random-uuid), :object/card same-ref, :object/zone :stack, :object/owner/controller from controller-id, :object/is-copy true
  - Transact and return db
  - Note: Copy inherits card but :object/is-copy true prevents storm cascade

Modify src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:
- [ ] Add require for fizzle.engine.triggers
- [ ] cast-spell: After increment-storm, check for :storm keyword in card
  - (when (contains? (:card/keywords card) :storm) ...)
  - Get current storm count (after increment)
  - Create trigger with {:count (dec storm-count)} - copies = spells BEFORE this one
  - Call (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack db (triggers/create-trigger :storm object-id player-id {:count (dec storm-count)}))
  - Note: Storm count is AFTER increment, so dec gives spells cast before

Modify src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs (if needed):
- [ ] Update get-stack-items to include both triggers AND spell objects, ordered by position
  - Current: Only returns triggers
  - Needed: Return [{:type :trigger :item trigger} {:type :spell :item object}] for full stack view

### 4. Key invariants

- Storm count for copies = current storm count - 1 (not including the storm spell itself)
- Copies are created by trigger resolution, NOT by cast-spell
- Copies do not trigger storm (checked via :object/is-copy flag, NOT by removing keyword)
- Copies have :object/is-copy true
- If source object missing at resolution time, return db unchanged (no crash)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Casting spell with :storm creates storm trigger on top of spell
- [ ] Storm trigger resolves to create N copies (N = spells cast BEFORE storm spell)
- [ ] Copies have :object/is-copy true
- [ ] Copies do not increment storm count (verify: cast 1 spell, cast storm, count = 2 not 3)
- [ ] Copies do not trigger storm (verify: :object/is-copy prevents cascade check)
- [ ] Edge case: Storm as first spell (count 0) creates 0 copies
- [ ] Edge case: Source object missing at resolution returns db unchanged
- [ ] 9 unit tests pass (8 functional + 1 edge case)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass: make test
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: make validate

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Storm Count Zero**
- Storm as first spell has storm count 1 after increment
- Copies = 1 - 1 = 0
- MUST handle this gracefully (no copies created, no error)
- Test: test-storm-count-zero-creates-no-copies

**Edge Case: Source Object Missing**
- Source spell might be exiled or otherwise removed before trigger resolves
- resolve-trigger :storm MUST check if source exists
- If nil, return db unchanged (defensive, no crash)
- Test: test-storm-source-missing-returns-unchanged-db

**Preventing Storm Cascade**
- Copies must NOT trigger storm themselves
- Two options: (a) Remove :storm keyword from copy, (b) Check :object/is-copy in cast-spell
- CHOSEN: Option (b) - Don't modify card keywords, check flag instead
- Rationale: Card keywords are immutable; flag is cleaner
- Implementation: In cast-spell, only create storm trigger if NOT :object/is-copy

**Off-by-One Risk**
- Storm count is incremented BEFORE trigger creation
- So count after cast = total spells including this one
- Copies needed = count - 1 = spells BEFORE this one
- CRITICAL: Use (dec storm-count) not storm-count
- Test: test-storm-count-one-creates-one-copy verifies this

**Stack Ordering**
- Trigger must resolve BEFORE spell (copies resolve first)
- Trigger has higher stack-order than spell
- LIFO: Higher stack-order = resolves first
- Verified by test-storm-trigger-on-top-of-spell

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO incrementing storm count for copies (copies are not cast)
- ❌ NO storm cascade (copies must not trigger storm)
- ❌ NO creating copies during cast-spell (copies created by trigger resolution)
- ❌ NO removing :storm keyword from copies (check :object/is-copy flag instead)
- ❌ NO crashing on nil source (defensive check, return unchanged db)
- ❌ NO off-by-one in copy count (use dec of post-increment storm count)
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Add :scry effect type to engine
design:
## Goal
Add the :scry effect type to effects.cljs that triggers player selection, following the existing selection effect pattern (like :discard and :tutor).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
Reference implementations:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:306-331 - Discard effect with selection pattern
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:334-349 - Tutor effect (also returns db unchanged)
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:750-770 - Selection detection functions

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create src/test/fizzle/engine/scry_effect_test.cljs:

```clojure
;; Test: execute-effect-impl :scry returns db unchanged (triggers selection flow)
;; Bug caught: If scry modifies db directly, selection flow breaks
(deftest scry-effect-returns-db-unchanged
  (let [db (create-test-db-with-library 5)
        effect {:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 2}
        result (effects/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
    (is (= db result) "Scry must return db unchanged for selection flow")))

;; Test: scry effect with amount 1 is valid
;; Bug caught: Hardcoded scry 2+ assumption
(deftest scry-effect-amount-1-valid
  (let [effect {:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 1}]
    (is (= 1 (:effect/amount effect)))))

;; Test: scry effect with amount 0 treated as no-op
;; Bug caught: Division by zero or empty array issues
(deftest scry-effect-amount-0-no-op
  (let [db (create-test-db-with-library 5)
        effect {:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 0}
        result (effects/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
    (is (= db result) "Scry 0 should be no-op")))

;; Test: scry effect missing amount defaults safely
;; Bug caught: NPE on nil amount
(deftest scry-effect-missing-amount-defaults-to-0
  (let [db (create-test-db-with-library 5)
        effect {:effect/type :scry} ; no :effect/amount
        result (effects/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
    (is (= db result) "Missing amount should be safe")))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] effects.cljs - Add `(defmethod execute-effect-impl :scry [db _player-id effect] ...)`
- [ ] effects.cljs - Scry returns db unchanged (like discard :player selection)
- [ ] effects.cljs - Add docstring documenting :effect/amount key
- [ ] effects.cljs - Handle edge case: amount <= 0 returns db unchanged (no-op)
- [ ] effects.cljs - Handle edge case: missing amount defaults to 0 (no-op)
- [ ] game.cljs:753-754 - Add `(= :scry (:effect/type %))` to has-selection-effect? check
- [ ] game.cljs:767-769 - Add `(= :scry (:effect/type %))` to get-first-selection-index check

### 4. Implementation pattern to follow
```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect-impl :scry
  ;; Look at top N cards of library and rearrange them.
  ;;
  ;; Effect keys:
  ;;   :effect/amount - Number of cards to scry (default 0)
  ;;
  ;; This effect returns db UNCHANGED. The actual scry happens at the
  ;; app-db level via re-frame events when the player confirms their selection.
  ;; The calling code checks for :scry effect type and sets up pending selection
  ;; with the top N cards revealed.
  ;;
  ;; Edge cases:
  ;;   - amount <= 0: No-op (returns db unchanged, no selection needed)
  ;;   - amount > library size: Scry available cards only (handled in selection setup)
  [db _player-id effect]
  (let [amount (or (:effect/amount effect) 0)]
    (if (<= amount 0)
      db  ; No-op for scry 0 or negative
      db))) ; Return unchanged - selection handled at app-db level
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `(defmethod execute-effect-impl :scry ...)` exists in effects.cljs
- [ ] Scry effect returns db unchanged for amount > 0 (verified by test)
- [ ] Scry effect returns db unchanged for amount <= 0 (no-op, verified by test)
- [ ] Scry effect handles missing :effect/amount safely (verified by test)
- [ ] `has-selection-effect?` returns true for scry effects with amount > 0
- [ ] `get-first-selection-index` finds scry effects
- [ ] 4+ unit tests pass in scry_effect_test.cljs
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + fmt + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: amount <= 0**
- Scry 0 is legal in MTG (does nothing)
- Must not trigger selection UI for scry 0
- Return db unchanged without setting up selection

**Edge Case: missing :effect/amount**
- Malformed effect data should not crash
- Default to 0 (no-op) if missing
- Use `(or (:effect/amount effect) 0)`

**Edge Case: negative amount**
- Should never happen but be defensive
- Treat same as 0 (no-op)

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow exact pattern of :discard at effects.cljs:306-331
- Returns db unchanged, selection handled at app-db level
- Docstring documents all effect keys and edge cases

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN for this task)
- ❌ NO modifying db in execute-effect-impl (must return unchanged for selection)
- ❌ NO assuming amount is always present (must handle nil)
- ❌ NO assuming amount is always positive (must handle 0 and negative)
- ❌ NO hardcoding scry 1 behavior (must work for any N)
