title: Task 1: Set up re-frame infrastructure and entry point
design:
## Goal Create the foundational infrastructure for the UI: add re-frame dependency, create index.html, create core.cljs entry point that initializes re-frame and mounts a minimal root component. ## Effort Estimate 2-3 hours ## Implementation ### 1. Add re-frame dependency Edit shadow-cljs.edn to add re-frame to dependencies: ```clojure :dependencies [[datascript "1.7.3"] [reagent "1.2.0"] [re-frame "1.4.3"]] ``` ### 2. Create resources/public directory and index.html ```bash mkdir -p resources/public ``` ```html Fizzle
``` ### 3. Create src/main/fizzle/core.cljs ```clojure (ns fizzle.core (:require [reagent.dom :as rdom] [re-frame.core :as rf])) ;; Placeholder app-db (will add game events in next task) (rf/reg-event-db ::init-db (fn [_ _] {:message "Fizzle loading..."})) (rf/reg-sub ::message (fn [db _] (:message db))) (defn app [] (let [msg @(rf/subscribe [::message])] [:div {:style {:padding "20px"}} [:h1 "Fizzle"] [:p msg]])) (defn ^:dev/after-load mount-root [] (rf/clear-subscription-cache!) (rdom/render [app] (.getElementById js/document "app"))) (defn init [] (rf/dispatch-sync [::init-db]) (mount-root)) ``` ### 4. Verify it works Run `make dev` and open http://localhost:8080 Should see "Fizzle" heading and "Fizzle loading..." message ## Success Criteria - [ ] `make dev` starts without errors (watch for "Build completed" in terminal) - [ ] Browser at http://localhost:8080 shows "Fizzle" heading and "Fizzle loading..." text - [ ] Hot reload works: edit message string in core.cljs, save, browser updates without refresh - [ ] `make test` passes (all 75 existing tests still green) - [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format-check + tests) ## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN) - ❌ NO game logic in core.cljs (this is just infrastructure - game events come in next task) - ❌ NO direct DOM manipulation (use Reagent's rdom/render, not js interop) - ❌ NO hardcoded port numbers in code (shadow-cljs.edn handles this) ## Key Considerations (Edge Cases & Troubleshooting) **If shadow-cljs fails to compile:** - Check re-frame version compatibility (1.4.3 works with Reagent 1.2.0) - Run `npm install` to ensure shadow-cljs is installed - Check for typos in namespace requires **If port 8080 in use:** - shadow-cljs will show error with port conflict - Either kill the process using 8080 or edit shadow-cljs.edn :dev-http port - Use `lsof -i :8080` to find conflicting process **If hot reload doesn't work:** - Ensure `^:dev/after-load` metadata is on mount-root function - Check browser console for errors - Hard refresh (Cmd+Shift+R) to clear cache **Reference:** - shadow-cljs.edn already configured for :browser target with fizzle.core/init entry point - Existing source at src/main/fizzle/ (db/, engine/, cards/)
close_reason: Re-frame infrastructure set up. shadow-cljs.edn updated with re-frame dep, package.json updated with react/react-dom deps, index.html created, core.cljs entry point with placeholder app working. make dev serves at localhost:8080, all 75 tests pass, make validate passes.

title: Task 1: Implement basic lands (Island, Swamp)
design:
## Goal
Add Island and Swamp basic land cards following existing land patterns.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Context
Existing patterns in iggy_pop.cljs show land structure (City of Brass, Gemstone Mine).
Basic lands are simpler - just tap for single color, no triggers or counters.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:57-76 (City of Brass pattern)
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:82-105 (Gemstone Mine pattern)
- Note: basics have :card/supertypes #{:basic} and :card/subtypes #{:island}

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Add tests to: src/test/fizzle/cards/basic_lands_test.cljs (new file)

Test cases:
```clojure
;; test_island_taps_for_blue_mana
;; Setup: Island on battlefield, untapped
;; Action: activate-mana-ability with :blue
;; Verify: mana pool has {:blue 1}, island is tapped

;; test_swamp_taps_for_black_mana
;; Setup: Swamp on battlefield, untapped
;; Action: activate-mana-ability with :black
;; Verify: mana pool has {:black 1}, swamp is tapped

;; test_basic_land_played_from_hand
;; Setup: Island in hand, land plays remaining
;; Action: play-land
;; Verify: Island on battlefield, land plays decremented

;; test_tapped_land_cannot_tap_again
;; Setup: Island on battlefield, already tapped
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability
;; Verify: returns unchanged db (can-activate? false)

;; test_land_in_hand_cannot_activate_mana
;; Setup: Island in hand (not battlefield)
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability
;; Verify: returns unchanged db (zone check fails)
```

### 3. Add card definitions
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

```clojure
(def island
  {:card/id :island
   :card/name "Island"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/subtypes #{:island}
   :card/supertypes #{:basic}
   :card/text "{T}: Add {U}."
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true}
                     :ability/produces {:blue 1}}]})

(def swamp
  {:card/id :swamp
   :card/name "Swamp"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/subtypes #{:swamp}
   :card/supertypes #{:basic}
   :card/text "{T}: Add {B}."
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true}
                     :ability/produces {:black 1}}]})
```

### 4. Update all-cards vector
Add island and swamp to the all-cards def at bottom of iggy_pop.cljs:
```clojure
(def all-cards
  [dark-ritual brain-freeze cabal-ritual city-of-brass gemstone-mine island swamp])
```

### 5. Run validation
- make test
- make validate

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Island definition exists in iggy_pop.cljs with :card/supertypes #{:basic}
- [ ] Swamp definition exists in iggy_pop.cljs with :card/supertypes #{:basic}
- [ ] Island has :card/subtypes #{:island}
- [ ] Swamp has :card/subtypes #{:swamp}
- [ ] Both included in all-cards vector
- [ ] 5 tests in basic_lands_test.cljs all pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + fmt + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in engine code (data-over-code: cards are pure data)
- ❌ NO skipping TDD (write failing tests first, then implement)
- ❌ NO modifying activate-mana-ability for basic lands (should work generically)

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Already Tapped**
- Existing can-activate? checks :object/tapped via costs/can-pay?
- Verify this works for basic lands (test_tapped_land_cannot_tap_again)

**Edge Case: Wrong Zone**
- Mana abilities only work on battlefield
- activate-mana-ability checks :object/zone in validation
- Verify with test_land_in_hand_cannot_activate_mana

**Pattern Consistency**
- Follow exact structure from City of Brass / Gemstone Mine
- Use :ability/produces for UI display (shows mana symbol on land)
- Use :ability/cost {:tap true} not {:cost/tap true}

**Reference Implementation**
- City of Brass at iggy_pop.cljs:57-76 is closest pattern
- Note: basic lands don't need :card/triggers (no damage on tap)
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Create Makefile with Core Commands
design:
## Goal

Create Makefile with core development commands: repl, dev, test, clean. This establishes the primary interface before adding linting/formatting.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create Makefile

```makefile
.PHONY: repl dev test clean help

# Detect Java - try common locations
JAVA_HOME ?= $(shell \
  if [ -d "/usr/local/opt/openjdk" ]; then echo "/usr/local/opt/openjdk"; \
  elif [ -d "/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk" ]; then echo "/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk"; \
  elif [ -n "$$JAVA_HOME" ]; then echo "$$JAVA_HOME"; \
  else echo ""; fi)

ifneq ($(JAVA_HOME),)
  export PATH := $(JAVA_HOME)/bin:$(PATH)
endif

help:
	@echo "Fizzle Development Commands"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make repl     - Start ClojureScript REPL (node)"
	@echo "  make dev      - Start browser dev server"
	@echo "  make test     - Run all tests"
	@echo "  make clean    - Remove build artifacts"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Validation (after linting added):"
	@echo "  make validate - Run all checks (lint + fmt + test)"

repl:
	npx shadow-cljs node-repl

dev:
	npx shadow-cljs watch app

test:
	npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js

clean:
	rm -rf out/ .shadow-cljs/ resources/public/js/
```

### 2. Update CLAUDE.md

Add this section after Build Commands:

```markdown
## Development Commands

Use these make commands instead of hand-rolling shadow-cljs calls:

\`\`\`bash
# Development
make repl      # Start node REPL - ready to eval immediately
make dev       # Start browser dev server with hot reload
make test      # Run all tests
make clean     # Remove build artifacts (out/, .shadow-cljs/)

# Validation (after Phase 2)
make validate  # Run lint + format-check + tests
\`\`\`

**IMPORTANT**: Always use \`make test\` instead of raw shadow-cljs commands.
```

### 3. Verify all commands work

- [ ] `make help` shows available commands
- [ ] `make repl` starts REPL, can eval `(+ 1 2)` and `(require '[fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]])`
- [ ] `make test` runs 40 tests, all pass
- [ ] `make dev` starts watch (ctrl-c to exit)
- [ ] `make clean` removes out/ and .shadow-cljs/

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Makefile exists at project root with repl, dev, test, clean, help targets
- [ ] `make repl` starts REPL in one command without PATH export
- [ ] `make test` runs all 40 tests, outputs pass count
- [ ] `make help` displays all available commands
- [ ] `make clean` removes out/, .shadow-cljs/, resources/public/js/
- [ ] CLAUDE.md has "Development Commands" section with make commands
- [ ] CLAUDE.md instructs agent to use make commands
- [ ] Changes committed

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Java Not Installed**
- Makefile attempts to detect Java in common locations
- If Java not found, shadow-cljs will fail with clear error message
- User must install Java separately (documented in README)

**Edge Case: Different JAVA_HOME Locations**
- macOS Intel Homebrew: /usr/local/opt/openjdk
- macOS ARM Homebrew: /opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk
- Linux: Typically in PATH already or JAVA_HOME set
- Makefile checks multiple locations, falls back to existing JAVA_HOME/PATH

**Edge Case: npm not installed**
- npx requires Node.js/npm
- Error message will be clear: "npx: command not found"
- Prerequisite, not something Makefile can fix

**Portability**
- Makefile uses POSIX shell commands (works on macOS/Linux)
- Windows users need WSL or alternative (out of scope)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO hard-coded absolute paths without fallbacks (portability)
- ❌ NO manual PATH exports required before running make (usability)
- ❌ NO npm scripts duplicating Makefile commands (single source of truth)
- ❌ NO undocumented commands (help target lists all)
close_reason: Closed

title: Add trigger infrastructure to stack system
design:
## Goal

Implement the foundational trigger system that allows triggered abilities (like storm) to exist as objects on the stack. This is the foundation for storm copies and future triggered abilities.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

Reference implementations:
- db/schema.cljs:40-60 - Object schema pattern (follow this for trigger schema)
- engine/rules.cljs:56-69 - resolve-spell pattern (follow for resolve-trigger)
- engine/zones.cljs:1-30 - Zone transition pattern

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/triggers_test.cljs

Test cases with specific bug prevention:
- [ ] test-create-trigger-structure: Verify create-trigger returns map with all required keys (:trigger/id, :trigger/type, :trigger/source, :trigger/controller, :trigger/data) - catches missing fields bug
- [ ] test-create-trigger-unique-id: Verify each call to create-trigger returns unique :trigger/id - catches duplicate ID bug
- [ ] test-add-trigger-to-stack-transacts: Verify trigger appears in db after add-trigger-to-stack - catches silent failure bug
- [ ] test-add-trigger-with-nil-source-fails: Verify add-trigger-to-stack with nil source returns error/unchanged db - catches nil reference bug
- [ ] test-resolve-trigger-dispatches-storm: Verify (resolve-trigger db {:trigger/type :storm ...}) calls storm handler - catches dispatch bug
- [ ] test-resolve-trigger-default-noop: Verify unknown :trigger/type returns db unchanged - catches mutation on unknown type bug
- [ ] test-remove-trigger-retracts: Verify trigger not in db after remove-trigger - catches incomplete cleanup bug
- [ ] test-get-stack-items-empty: Verify get-stack-items returns [] on empty stack - catches nil-vs-empty bug
- [ ] test-get-stack-items-ordered: Verify triggers added later appear first (LIFO stack order) - catches ordering bug
- [ ] test-get-trigger-not-found: Verify get-trigger with invalid ID returns nil - catches exception on missing bug

### 3. Implementation checklist

Schema changes (db/schema.cljs):
- [ ] Add :trigger/id {:db/unique :db.unique/identity} - UUID, unique identifier
- [ ] Add :trigger/type {:db/valueType :db.type/keyword} - e.g. :storm, :etb
- [ ] Add :trigger/source {:db/valueType :db.type/ref} - ref to source object
- [ ] Add :trigger/controller {:db/valueType :db.type/ref} - ref to player
- [ ] Add :trigger/data {} - map for trigger-specific data (no special db type)
- [ ] Add :trigger/stack-order {:db/valueType :db.type/long} - integer for LIFO ordering

New file engine/triggers.cljs:
- [ ] create-trigger [trigger-type source-id controller-id data] -> {:trigger/id (random-uuid) :trigger/type trigger-type :trigger/source source-id :trigger/controller controller-id :trigger/data data}
- [ ] add-trigger-to-stack [db trigger] -> db with trigger transacted, :trigger/stack-order set to max+1
- [ ] resolve-trigger multimethod (fn [db trigger] ...) dispatches on (:trigger/type trigger)
- [ ] defmethod resolve-trigger :default [db trigger] -> db (no-op, logs warning)
- [ ] remove-trigger [db trigger-id] -> db with trigger entity retracted

Query additions (db/queries.cljs):
- [ ] get-stack-items [db] -> vector of maps {:type :spell|:trigger :item <object-or-trigger>} ordered by stack position (most recent first)
- [ ] get-trigger [db trigger-id] -> trigger map or nil
- [ ] get-next-stack-order [db] -> integer (current max + 1, or 0 if empty)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Trigger schema in db/schema.cljs with 6 attributes (:trigger/id, :trigger/type, :trigger/source, :trigger/controller, :trigger/data, :trigger/stack-order)
- [ ] create-trigger returns map with all 5 core keys (not stack-order, that's set by add-trigger-to-stack)
- [ ] Each create-trigger call produces unique UUID
- [ ] add-trigger-to-stack transacts trigger to db with correct stack-order
- [ ] get-stack-items returns triggers and spells in LIFO order
- [ ] resolve-trigger :default returns db unchanged (no mutation)
- [ ] remove-trigger fully retracts trigger from db
- [ ] 10 unit tests pass covering structure, ordering, dispatch, cleanup, edge cases
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (make test)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass (make validate)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO trigger without :trigger/id (every trigger must have unique ID)
- ❌ NO add-trigger-to-stack with nil source-id (must validate source exists)
- ❌ NO resolve-trigger mutating db for unknown types (default must be pure no-op)
- ❌ NO hardcoded stack ordering (use :trigger/stack-order for proper LIFO)
- ❌ NO triggers leaking after resolution (remove-trigger must fully retract)
- ❌ NO mixing trigger logic with spell logic in rules.cljs (triggers.cljs is separate)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases & Failure Modes)

**Edge Case: Nil Source Reference**
- What if trigger created with nil or invalid source-id?
- MUST validate source exists before transacting
- Return unchanged db (or throw) rather than creating orphan trigger

**Edge Case: Unknown Trigger Type**
- What if resolve-trigger called with :trigger/type not registered?
- MUST fall through to :default method
- :default returns db unchanged, logs warning for debugging
- Do NOT throw exception (breaks stack resolution)

**Edge Case: Empty Stack**
- get-stack-items on empty stack MUST return [] not nil
- Callers should be able to iterate without nil check

**Edge Case: Trigger Already Resolved**
- What if resolve-trigger called on trigger not in db?
- get-trigger returns nil, caller should check before resolving
- resolve-trigger should handle gracefully (no-op if trigger missing)

**Edge Case: Stack Order Overflow**
- :trigger/stack-order is long, but what if millions of triggers?
- Not a real concern for MTG (stack rarely exceeds 100)
- Document: stack-order is per-game, resets on new game

**Performance Consideration**
- get-stack-items query should be efficient (single Datalog query)
- Avoid N+1 queries when resolving stack

**Reference Implementation Pattern**
- Follow db/schema.cljs :object/* pattern for :trigger/* schema
- Follow engine/rules.cljs resolve-spell for resolve-trigger structure

## Notes

This task does NOT implement:
- Storm trigger specifically (next task)
- Integration with cast-spell (next task)
- Any specific trigger types beyond :default

Focus on clean, extensible infrastructure that future triggers can build on.
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 5: Implement Orim's Chant card definition
design:
## Goal
Create the Orim's Chant card definition file that uses the kicker and restriction systems built in previous tasks.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Foundation complete:
- Task 1: Player grants system (:player/grants attribute, add/get/expire)
- Task 2: :add-restriction effect type (creates restriction grants on players)
- Task 3: cannot-cast-spells enforcement in can-cast?
- Task 4: Kicker casting modes (get-casting-modes returns :primary and :kicked)

Now we need the actual card that ties it all together.

## Implementation

### Reference: Existing Patterns
- Targeting format: See src/fizzle/cards/recoup.cljs:20-25 for :card/targeting structure
- Effects: See src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:531-570 for :add-restriction effect
- Kicker: See src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs for kicker test card pattern

### Card Definition
Create src/fizzle/cards/orims_chant.cljs:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.orims-chant
  "Orim's Chant card definition.

   Orim's Chant: W - Instant
   Kicker {W}
   Target player can't cast spells this turn.
   If this spell was kicked, creatures can't attack this turn.")


(def orims-chant
  {:card/id :orims-chant
   :card/name "Orim's Chant"
   :card/cmc 1
   :card/mana-cost {:white 1}
   :card/colors #{:white}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Kicker {W}. Target player can't cast spells this turn. If this spell was kicked, creatures can't attack this turn."
   
   ;; Kicker cost - enables kicked mode via get-casting-modes
   :card/kicker {:white 1}
   
   ;; Cast-time targeting: target any player
   ;; IMPORTANT: Must use :card/targeting (vector format), NOT :card/targets
   ;; See recoup.cljs:20-25 for pattern
   :card/targeting [{:target/id :player
                     :target/type :player
                     :target/options #{:any-player}
                     :target/required true}]
   
   ;; Base effects (when not kicked) - :targeted-player resolves via :object/targets
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :add-restriction
                   :effect/target :targeted-player
                   :restriction/type :cannot-cast-spells}]
   
   ;; Kicked effects (replaces base effects when kicked)
   ;; Must include BOTH restrictions (kicked effects replace, not add to base)
   :card/kicked-effects [{:effect/type :add-restriction
                          :effect/target :targeted-player
                          :restriction/type :cannot-cast-spells}
                         {:effect/type :add-restriction
                          :effect/target :targeted-player
                          :restriction/type :cannot-attack}]})
```

### Test File
Create src/test/fizzle/cards/orims_chant_test.cljs:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.orims-chant-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest is testing]]
    [fizzle.cards.orims-chant :as orims-chant]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]
    [fizzle.engine.grants :as grants]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.test-helpers :as h]))

;; Test 1: Card properties are oracle-accurate
(deftest card-properties-test
  (testing "card has correct oracle properties"
    (let [card orims-chant/orims-chant]
      (is (= :orims-chant (:card/id card)))
      (is (= "Orim's Chant" (:card/name card)))
      (is (= 1 (:card/cmc card)))
      (is (= {:white 1} (:card/mana-cost card)))
      (is (= #{:white} (:card/colors card)))
      (is (= #{:instant} (:card/types card))))))

;; Test 2: Kicker cost defined correctly
;; Bug caught: Missing kicker breaks kicked mode generation
(deftest kicker-cost-test
  (testing "card has kicker cost defined"
    (let [card orims-chant/orims-chant]
      (is (= {:white 1} (:card/kicker card))
          "Kicker cost should be {W}"))))

;; Test 3: Targeting system integration
;; Bug caught: Wrong attribute (:card/targets vs :card/targeting)
(deftest targeting-format-test
  (testing "card uses correct targeting format for targeting system"
    (let [card orims-chant/orims-chant
          targeting (:card/targeting card)]
      (is (vector? targeting)
          "Targeting must be a vector for targeting system")
      (is (= 1 (count targeting))
          "Should have exactly one target requirement")
      (let [req (first targeting)]
        (is (= :player (:target/id req))
            "Target ID should be :player")
        (is (= :player (:target/type req))
            "Target type should be :player")
        (is (contains? (:target/options req) :any-player)
            "Should allow targeting any player")
        (is (:target/required req)
            "Target should be required")))))

;; Test 4: Base effects have cannot-cast-spells
;; Bug caught: Missing restriction type in base effects
(deftest base-effects-test
  (testing "base effects add cannot-cast-spells restriction"
    (let [card orims-chant/orims-chant
          effects (:card/effects card)]
      (is (= 1 (count effects))
          "Should have exactly one base effect")
      (let [effect (first effects)]
        (is (= :add-restriction (:effect/type effect)))
        (is (= :targeted-player (:effect/target effect)))
        (is (= :cannot-cast-spells (:restriction/type effect)))))))

;; Test 5: Kicked effects have both restrictions
;; Bug caught: Kicked effects missing one restriction
(deftest kicked-effects-test
  (testing "kicked effects add both restrictions"
    (let [card orims-chant/orims-chant
          effects (:card/kicked-effects card)]
      (is (= 2 (count effects))
          "Should have two kicked effects")
      (is (= #{:cannot-cast-spells :cannot-attack}
             (set (map :restriction/type effects)))
          "Should include both restriction types"))))

;; Test 6: get-casting-modes returns 2 modes in hand
;; Bug caught: Kicker mode not generated
(deftest casting-modes-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes returns primary and kicked modes"
    (let [db (h/init-game-with-card orims-chant/orims-chant :hand)
          player-id :player-1
          hand (q/get-hand db player-id)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db player-id object-id)]
      (is (= 2 (count modes))
          "Should return 2 casting modes")
      (is (some #(= :primary (:mode/id %)) modes)
          "Should include primary mode")
      (is (some #(= :kicked (:mode/id %)) modes)
          "Should include kicked mode"))))

;; Test 7: Primary mode costs {W}
;; Bug caught: Primary mode has wrong cost
(deftest primary-mode-cost-test
  (testing "primary mode costs {W}"
    (let [db (h/init-game-with-card orims-chant/orims-chant :hand)
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 (:object/id (first (q/get-hand db :player-1))))
          primary (first (filter #(= :primary (:mode/id %)) modes))]
      (is (= {:white 1} (:mode/mana-cost primary))
          "Primary mode should cost {W}"))))

;; Test 8: Kicked mode costs {WW}
;; Bug caught: Kicked mode cost not merged correctly
(deftest kicked-mode-cost-test
  (testing "kicked mode costs {WW}"
    (let [db (h/init-game-with-card orims-chant/orims-chant :hand)
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 (:object/id (first (q/get-hand db :player-1))))
          kicked (first (filter #(= :kicked (:mode/id %)) modes))]
      (is (= {:white 2} (:mode/mana-cost kicked))
          "Kicked mode should cost {WW} (base + kicker)"))))

;; Test 9: Kicked mode has effects in mode
;; Bug caught: Kicked effects not attached to mode
(deftest kicked-mode-effects-test
  (testing "kicked mode carries kicked effects"
    (let [db (h/init-game-with-card orims-chant/orims-chant :hand)
          modes (rules/get-casting-modes db :player-1 (:object/id (first (q/get-hand db :player-1))))
          kicked (first (filter #(= :kicked (:mode/id %)) modes))]
      (is (seq (:mode/effects kicked))
          "Kicked mode should have effects")
      (is (= 2 (count (:mode/effects kicked)))
          "Kicked mode should have 2 effects"))))
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Card file exists at src/fizzle/cards/orims_chant.cljs
- [ ] Card text matches Scryfall oracle: "Kicker {W}. Target player can't cast spells this turn. If this spell was kicked, creatures can't attack this turn."
- [ ] :card/kicker is {:white 1}
- [ ] :card/targeting is a VECTOR with {:target/id :player, :target/type :player, :target/options #{:any-player}, :target/required true}
- [ ] :card/effects has one :add-restriction with :cannot-cast-spells
- [ ] :card/kicked-effects has two :add-restriction effects (both restriction types)
- [ ] 9+ unit tests pass in orims_chant_test.cljs
- [ ] make test passes (all existing tests still work)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Edge Case: Wrong targeting attribute**
- MUST use :card/targeting (vector format), NOT :card/targets
- :card/targets does not exist in the targeting system
- Incorrect attribute will cause targeting system to not find targets

**Edge Case: Kicked effects replace, not extend**
- :card/kicked-effects completely replaces :card/effects when kicked
- Kicked effects MUST include the base effect (cannot-cast-spells)
- Forgetting this means kicked spell only prevents attacks, not casting

**Edge Case: Target options format**
- :target/options must be a set (#{:any-player}), not a vector
- Targeting system uses (contains? options ...) which requires set

**Edge Case: No valid targets**
- If no opponent exists (goldfish mode), still need a target
- :any-player allows targeting self, so spell is castable
- targeting/has-valid-targets? returns [player-id] when :any-player set

**Reference: Effect target resolution**
- :targeted-player in effect resolves via (get-in source-obj [:object/targets :player])
- This works because targeting stores targets keyed by :target/id
- Since :target/id is :player, stored at [:object/targets :player]

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO using :card/targets (wrong attribute - must use :card/targeting)
- ❌ NO hardcoded logic in rules.cljs (card uses generic systems)
- ❌ NO custom effect types (use existing :add-restriction)
- ❌ NO skipping player targeting (oracle says "target player")
- ❌ NO forgetting cannot-cast-spells in kicked effects (replace semantics)
- ❌ NO using vector for :target/options (must be set for contains? check)
close_reason: Implemented Orim's Chant card definition with kicker and player targeting. All 12 tests pass, make validate passes.

title: Refactor init-game-state to accept config + add built-in decklist constant
design:
## Goal
Refactor init-game-state in events/game.cljs to accept a config map instead of using hardcoded make-test-deck, and add the Iggy Pop decklist as a structured constant in cards/iggy_pop.cljs. This is the foundational change that all subsequent setup screen tasks build on.

NOTE: Only the PRODUCTION init-game-state in events/game.cljs is modified. The test helper at db/init.cljs is NOT changed.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation Checklist

### Step 1: Add iggy-pop-decklist constant to cards/iggy_pop.cljs (after all-cards)

Define as:
\`\`\`clojure
(def iggy-pop-decklist
  {:deck/id :iggy-pop
   :deck/name \"Iggy Pop\"
   :deck/main [{:card/id :dark-ritual :count 4}
               {:card/id :cabal-ritual :count 4}
               {:card/id :brain-freeze :count 4}
               {:card/id :city-of-brass :count 3}
               {:card/id :gemstone-mine :count 4}
               {:card/id :polluted-delta :count 3}
               {:card/id :underground-river :count 1}
               {:card/id :cephalid-coliseum :count 2}
               {:card/id :island :count 2}
               {:card/id :swamp :count 1}
               {:card/id :lotus-petal :count 4}
               {:card/id :lions-eye-diamond :count 4}
               {:card/id :careful-study :count 2}
               {:card/id :mental-note :count 3}
               {:card/id :opt :count 4}
               {:card/id :intuition :count 4}
               {:card/id :deep-analysis :count 3}
               {:card/id :recoup :count 1}
               {:card/id :ill-gotten-gains :count 4}
               {:card/id :orims-chant :count 1}
               {:card/id :ray-of-revelation :count 1}
               {:card/id :flash-of-insight :count 1}]
   :deck/side [{:card/id :merchant-scroll :count 2}
               {:card/id :seal-of-cleansing :count 2}
               {:card/id :city-of-traitors :count 2}
               {:card/id :careful-study :count 2}
               {:card/id :deep-analysis :count 1}
               {:card/id :mental-note :count 1}
               {:card/id :opt :count 1}
               {:card/id :orims-chant :count 1}
               {:card/id :flash-of-insight :count 1}
               {:card/id :ray-of-revelation :count 1}
               {:card/id :cephalid-coliseum :count 1}]})
\`\`\`

Main deck = current make-test-deck (60 cards, all cards have definitions in all-cards).
Sideboard = 15 cards using ONLY implemented cards (no unimplemented cards like Meditate, Cunning Wish, etc.).
When more cards get implemented in future epics, the sideboard can be updated to match the design doc.

### Step 2: Add deck-to-card-ids helper function in events/game.cljs

\`\`\`clojure
(defn- deck-to-card-ids
  \"Expand a deck main list [{:card/id :count}] into a flat shuffled vector of card-ids.\"
  [main-deck]
  (shuffle
    (into []
      (mapcat (fn [{:keys [card/id count]}]
                (repeat count id))
              main-deck))))
\`\`\`

### Step 3: Refactor init-game-state in events/game.cljs (lines 68-151)

Change signature from:
\`\`\`clojure
(defn init-game-state [] ...)
\`\`\`
To:
\`\`\`clojure
(defn init-game-state
  \"Initialize a fresh game state from config.
   Config keys:
     :main-deck - vec of {:card/id :count} maps (required, must sum to 60)
     :clock-turns - integer turns until opponent wins (default 4)\"
  [{:keys [main-deck clock-turns] :or {clock-turns 4}}]
  ...)
\`\`\`

Changes inside:
- Replace (make-test-deck) call on line 116 with (deck-to-card-ids main-deck)
- Use clock-turns for opponent setup (currently hardcoded 40-card opponent library; this stays the same for now since clock-turns will be used by the bot system in Epic 5)
- Keep all other init logic identical

### Step 4: Update ::init-game event handler (lines 154-157)

Change from:
\`\`\`clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::init-game
  (fn [_ _]
    (init-game-state)))
\`\`\`
To:
\`\`\`clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::init-game
  (fn [_ [_ config]]
    (init-game-state (or config {:main-deck (:deck/main cards/iggy-pop-decklist)
                                  :clock-turns 4}))))
\`\`\`

This preserves backward compatibility: dispatching [::init-game] with no args still works.

### Step 5: Remove make-test-deck function (lines 25-65)

Delete entirely. All callers now use deck-to-card-ids via config.

### Step 6: Update init_game_test.cljs

The existing tests at src/test/fizzle/events/init_game_test.cljs verify exact card counts from make-test-deck. These MUST be updated:
- Change direct calls from (game/init-game-state) to (game/init-game-state {:main-deck (:deck/main cards/iggy-pop-decklist)})
- Verify tests still check correct card counts (counts haven't changed, just the source)
- Add import for cards namespace

### Step 7: Update any other callers

Check routing_test.cljs and any other files that call init-game-state directly. Update their calls to pass a config map.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] iggy-pop-decklist constant defined in cards/iggy_pop.cljs with exactly 60 main cards (sum of all :count values) and exactly 15 sideboard cards
- [ ] All card-ids in both main and side exist in all-cards vector (no undefined cards)
- [ ] init-game-state accepts config map with :main-deck and :clock-turns
- [ ] init-game-state uses :clock-turns with default of 4
- [ ] Dispatching [::init-game] with no args still works (backward compatible via default config)
- [ ] Dispatching [::init-game {:main-deck [...] :clock-turns N}] uses provided config
- [ ] make-test-deck function removed from events/game.cljs
- [ ] deck-to-card-ids helper produces correct flat shuffled vector from main-deck entries
- [ ] init_game_test.cljs updated to pass config and all tests pass
- [ ] All 872+ existing tests pass (no regressions)
- [ ] make validate passing (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Card-id not in all-cards**
If a card-id in the deck config doesn't have a definition in all-cards, d/q will return nil for get-card-eid, causing a silent failure (objects created without card reference). For v1 this is acceptable since built-in decklists only reference implemented cards. Future deck import (Epic 3) must add validation.

**Edge Case: Deck count != 60**
init-game-state does NOT validate deck size — it builds whatever is passed. This is intentional: validation belongs in the setup screen UI layer (::start-game event in future task). The engine shouldn't enforce deck size since tests use small decks.

**Edge Case: clock-turns = 0 or negative**
The :or {clock-turns 4} default handles nil, but not 0 or negative. For v1 this is acceptable since only the setup screen sets this value and it will have a constrained UI input. No validation needed in init-game-state.

**Backward Compatibility**
The ::init-game event handler MUST accept both (dispatch [::init-game]) and (dispatch [::init-game config]). The (or config default) pattern handles this. Critical because core.cljs dispatches without args.

**db/init.cljs is NOT modified**
The test helper init-game-state at db/init.cljs is a completely separate function used by unit tests. It creates a minimal state with just Dark Ritual. Do NOT change it.

**Test Update Strategy**
init_game_test.cljs has tests that verify exact card distribution (e.g., 4x Dark Ritual, 4x Cabal Ritual). These tests verify make-test-deck output. After refactoring, they should verify init-game-state with iggy-pop-decklist config produces the same distribution. The card counts are identical — only the source changes.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO validation of deck size in init-game-state (validation belongs in UI/setup layer)
- ❌ NO changes to db/init.cljs test helper (separate function, different purpose)
- ❌ NO unimplemented card-ids in iggy-pop-decklist (all entries must have definitions in all-cards)
- ❌ NO breaking backward compatibility of ::init-game event (must work with and without args)
- ❌ NO removing or modifying existing test assertions (only update call sites to pass config)
close_reason: Refactored init-game-state to accept config map. Added iggy-pop-decklist constant (60 main + 15 side). Removed make-test-deck. Updated all callers. 1007 tests pass.

title: Task 4: Remove legacy trigger system and unify remaining duplicate code
design:
## Goal
Remove all legacy trigger entity code paths and unify remaining duplicate code. After this task, the codebase has ONE stack system (stack-items only), ONE query for the full stack, and ZERO -for-trigger builder functions.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Context
- Tasks 1-3 complete: most trigger creation sites now produce stack-items
- **EXCEPTION**: maybe-create-storm-trigger in rules.cljs still calls triggers/create-trigger + add-trigger-to-stack (missed in Task 3)
- Legacy trigger branches remain in resolve-top, resolve-top-of-stack, confirmation handlers
- 2 -for-trigger builder functions remain
- get-next-stack-order is duplicated (queries.cljs + stack.cljs)
- resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection already uses spell builders with dissoc/assoc patching (no migration needed there)

## Implementation

### Step 1: Migrate maybe-create-storm-trigger to stack-items (rules.cljs)

**CRITICAL**: This is the last production caller of triggers/create-trigger + add-trigger-to-stack.

File: src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs, function maybe-create-storm-trigger (~lines 309-330)

Currently creates a :storm trigger entity that later resolves via the :storm defmethod in triggers.cljs, which calls create-spell-copy to create N stack-item copies.

Replace with direct stack-item creation that stores the storm copy count:

```clojure
;; OLD:
(let [trigger (triggers/create-trigger :storm object-id player-id {:count copy-count})]
  (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack db trigger))

;; NEW:
(stack/create-stack-item db
  {:stack-item/type :storm
   :stack-item/controller player-id
   :stack-item/source object-id
   :stack-item/effects []
   :stack-item/description (str "Storm - create " copy-count " copies")
   :stack-item/storm-count copy-count})
```

**Schema addition needed**: Add :stack-item/storm-count to schema.cljs (or reuse :stack-item/effects to encode count — but a dedicated attribute is clearer).

Wait — simpler approach: encode storm count in the existing :stack-item/effects as data:
```clojure
:stack-item/effects [{:effect/type :storm-copies :effect/count copy-count}]
```
This avoids schema additions and follows the existing pattern where effects are data.

**Then update resolve-stack-item-effects in game.cljs** to handle :storm type stack-items by calling triggers/create-spell-copy N times (the same logic currently in the :storm resolve-trigger defmethod).

Add require for stack in rules.cljs: [fizzle.engine.stack :as stack]
Remove require for triggers in rules.cljs IF no other trigger functions are called there. Check first — rules.cljs may still call resolve-trigger for immediate actions.

### Step 2: Update ::resolve-top to handle :storm stack-items

File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

The resolve-stack-item-effects function (or a new branch in ::resolve-top) needs to handle :storm type stack-items. When the top stack item is type :storm:
1. Get source-id and copy-count from stack-item
2. Call triggers/create-spell-copy N times (this function already creates stack-items for copies)
3. Remove the storm stack-item

This is similar to the existing Branch 3 (other stack-items) but with storm-specific logic.

### Step 3: Remove legacy trigger branches from ::resolve-top (game.cljs)

Lines ~442-455 have 2 legacy branches:
- Branch 4: activated-ability trigger with selection
- Branch 5: other legacy triggers

Remove these branches and the legacy trigger variables:
- Remove: stack-triggers, top-trigger, trigger-order, resolve-trigger? variables
- Remove: Branch 4 and Branch 5 cond clauses
- Keep: Branch 1 (spell stack-item), Branch 2 (activated-ability stack-item), Branch 3 (other stack-items including storm), Branch 6 (empty)

Also remove the queries/get-stack-items require if no longer used.

### Step 4: Remove legacy trigger branch from resolve-top-of-stack (game.cljs)

Lines ~261-308, remove:
- queries/get-stack-items call
- top-trigger, trigger-order, resolve-trigger? variables
- Legacy trigger cond branch (lines ~302-305)
- Keep: stack-item branches and empty stack branch

### Step 5: Remove resolve-top-trigger helper (game.cljs)

Line ~237: Dead code — defined but never called. Delete entirely.

### Step 6: Remove resolve-activated-ability-with-selection (selection.cljs)

Lines ~702-834: Legacy function that takes trigger maps. Only caller was Branch 4 in ::resolve-top (removed in Step 3). Delete entirely (~132 lines).

### Step 7: Remove -for-trigger builder functions (selection.cljs)

After Step 6, these have zero callers:
- build-tutor-selection-for-trigger (lines ~665-685, ~20 lines)
- build-player-target-selection-for-trigger (lines ~688-699, ~12 lines)

Note: resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection already uses the spell builders (build-tutor-selection, build-player-target-selection) with dissoc/assoc patching. No migration needed — just delete the unused trigger versions.

### Step 8: Remove :trigger source-type from cleanup-selection-source (selection.cljs)

Lines ~841-866: Remove the (= source-type :trigger) branch (lines ~854-855). Only :stack-item and spell (nil) remain.

Also remove all occurrences of :selection/trigger-id setting/reading across selection.cljs (found at lines ~677-678, ~696-697, ~770-771, ~827-828, ~1502-1503, ~1537-1538). These are all in functions being deleted (Steps 6-7) or dead paths.

### Step 9: Simplify stack-empty? (queries.cljs)

Lines ~142-149: Currently checks legacy triggers + objects + stack-items. Remove the get-stack-items check. Check only:
- stack-items: (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :stack-item/position _]] db)
- objects on stack: (get-objects-in-zone db player-id :stack)

### Step 10: Remove get-stack-items from queries.cljs

Lines ~130-139: No callers after Steps 3-4 and 9. Delete entirely.

### Step 11: Consolidate get-next-stack-order

queries.cljs has get-next-stack-order (~lines 113-127) checking triggers + objects.
stack.cljs has get-next-stack-order (~lines 12-31) checking stack-items + triggers + objects.

Simplify stack.cljs version to check only stack-items + objects (remove trigger query). Remove the queries.cljs version — its only remaining caller was add-trigger-to-stack (removed in Step 13).

Verify no other callers of queries/get-next-stack-order exist first.

### Step 12: Remove trigger creation functions from triggers.cljs

- create-trigger (lines ~16-37): 0 production callers after Step 1
- add-trigger-to-stack (lines ~40-57): 0 production callers after Step 1

Delete both. Do NOT remove:
- resolve-trigger multimethod and its defmethods (still used by trigger_dispatch for immediate triggers: :draw-step, :untap-step, :expire-grants, :permanent-tapped, :land-entered)
- remove-trigger (may still be needed — verify no callers remain first)
- create-spell-copy (called during storm resolution)

### Step 13: Remove :trigger/* schema from schema.cljs

Lines ~82-91: Remove all 7 trigger attributes:
- :trigger/id, :trigger/type, :trigger/source, :trigger/controller
- :trigger/data, :trigger/description, :trigger/stack-order

**Edge case**: After schema removal, any existing trigger entities in a running game become invisible to Datascript queries. This is acceptable — new games won't create them, and page refresh starts a new game.

**Important**: resolve-trigger defmethods still accept trigger MAPS as parameters (plain Clojure maps, not Datascript entities). The :trigger/* schema only affects Datascript storage. Immediate triggers (draw-step, untap-step, expire-grants) are created as maps and passed directly to resolve-trigger without ever being stored in Datascript. So schema removal is safe.

Wait — verify this: do :permanent-tapped and :land-entered triggers get stored in Datascript? After Task 3, trigger_dispatch creates stack-items for stacked triggers. But the immediate triggers (uses-stack? false) are resolved directly via resolve-trigger. They never use add-trigger-to-stack. So they're plain maps, not Datascript entities. Schema removal is safe.

### Step 14: Extract split-effects-around-selection helper (selection.cljs)

The subvec splitting pattern appears ~8 times:
```clojure
(subvec (vec effects-list) 0 idx)
(nth effects-list idx)
(subvec (vec effects-list) (inc idx))
```

Extract into:
```clojure
(defn- split-effects-around-index
  "Split effects list around the effect at idx.
   Returns [effects-before target-effect effects-after]."
  [effects idx]
  (let [v (vec effects)]
    [(subvec v 0 idx)
     (nth v idx)
     (subvec v (inc idx))]))
```

Replace all ~8 inline occurrences with destructuring:
```clojure
(let [[effects-before selection-effect effects-after]
      (split-effects-around-index effects-list selection-idx)]
  ...)
```

### Step 15: Update tests

Specific changes needed:

**src/test/fizzle/engine/triggers_test.cljs** (21 calls to create-trigger):
- KEEP tests for resolve-trigger defmethods (:permanent-tapped, :land-entered, :activated-ability, :draw-step, :untap-step, :expire-grants) — these test still-active production code
- These tests create trigger maps directly and pass to resolve-trigger — this is fine, they don't need Datascript storage
- REMOVE tests for create-trigger and add-trigger-to-stack functions (being deleted)
- REMOVE tests that assert trigger entities exist in Datascript after add-trigger-to-stack

**src/test/fizzle/engine/stack_test.cljs** (4 calls):
- Check what these tests do — if they test stack-item interaction with legacy triggers, update to only use stack-items

**src/test/fizzle/engine/storm_test.cljs** (2 calls):
- Update storm tests to verify storm creates stack-items (not trigger entities)
- Test: cast spell with :storm keyword → stack-item of type :storm created → resolves into N copy stack-items

**Add new test**: test-storm-creates-stack-item-not-trigger — verify maybe-create-storm-trigger creates a :storm stack-item, not a trigger entity.

### Step 16: Verify and commit

- make validate must pass
- Count LOC reduction (target: 300+ net)
- Verify single Datalog query for entire stack (stack/get-all-stack-items)
- Verify 0 :trigger/* attributes in schema
- Verify 0 -for-trigger suffixed functions
- grep -r 'trigger/id\|trigger/type\|trigger/stack-order' src/main/ should return 0 hits in schema and entity-creating code (resolve-trigger defmethods still use trigger map keys, which is fine)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 0 branches in ::resolve-top for legacy triggers (was 2)
- [ ] 0 branches in resolve-top-of-stack for legacy triggers (was 1)
- [ ] maybe-create-storm-trigger creates stack-item, not trigger entity
- [ ] ::resolve-top handles :storm stack-items correctly (creates N copies)
- [ ] resolve-activated-ability-with-selection deleted (~132 lines)
- [ ] build-tutor-selection-for-trigger deleted (~20 lines)
- [ ] build-player-target-selection-for-trigger deleted (~12 lines)
- [ ] :trigger/* schema attributes removed (7 attributes)
- [ ] create-trigger and add-trigger-to-stack deleted from triggers.cljs
- [ ] resolve-trigger defmethods PRESERVED (still used for immediate triggers)
- [ ] queries/get-stack-items deleted
- [ ] get-next-stack-order exists in exactly one place (stack.cljs), checks only stack-items + objects
- [ ] split-effects-around-index helper extracts duplicated splitting logic (~8 sites)
- [ ] stack-empty? simplified (no trigger check)
- [ ] No -for-trigger suffixed functions exist
- [ ] 0 occurrences of :selection/trigger-id in codebase
- [ ] All tests pass (856+)
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] Net LOC reduction documented

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO leaving dead code commented out (delete completely)
- NO keeping trigger schema 'just in case'
- NO breaking resolve-trigger defmethods (:draw-step, :untap-step, :expire-grants, :permanent-tapped, :land-entered — still called by trigger_dispatch for immediate resolution)
- NO touching resolve-spell-with-selection (spell resolution path is separate and working)
- NO removing create-spell-copy from triggers.cljs (still actively used by storm resolution)
- NO removing remove-trigger before verifying zero callers remain
- NO adding :stack-item/storm-count to schema — use :stack-item/effects with {:effect/type :storm-copies :effect/count N} instead

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Storm trigger migration**
maybe-create-storm-trigger is the LAST production caller of triggers/create-trigger. Must be migrated (Step 1) BEFORE removing trigger creation functions (Step 12). The :storm resolve-trigger defmethod becomes dead code after Step 2 wires storm resolution through stack-items.

**Edge Case: Immediate vs stacked triggers**
After all changes, trigger_dispatch still calls resolve-trigger for immediate triggers (uses-stack? false). These pass plain Clojure maps to resolve-trigger — they never stored entities in Datascript. Schema removal (Step 13) does NOT affect them.

**Edge Case: remove-trigger after cleanup**
After Steps 3-4 remove legacy branches from resolve-top, and Step 8 removes :trigger source from cleanup-selection-source, verify remove-trigger has zero callers. If so, it can be deleted alongside create-trigger in Step 12.

**Edge Case: Storm count encoding**
Storm count encoded as {:effect/type :storm-copies :effect/count N} in :stack-item/effects. Resolution code must extract this — not use the standard effect execution path (it's not an effect the engine knows about).

**Edge Case: triggers_test.cljs test preservation**
Tests for resolve-trigger defmethods create trigger maps inline (not via create-trigger necessarily). Verify which tests actually call create-trigger vs which construct maps manually. Only remove tests that test deleted functions.

**Ordering dependency**: Steps must execute in this order:
1 → 2 (storm migration before removal)
3-5 (remove legacy branches, dead code)
6-8 (remove legacy selection code)
9-11 (remove legacy queries and consolidate)
12-13 (remove trigger creation and schema — LAST)
14 (refactoring — independent of above)
15-16 (tests and verify — final)
close_reason: All 16 steps complete. Legacy trigger system removed, stack unified. 831 tests pass, make validate clean. ~330 LOC net reduction.

title: Implement flashback system and Deep Analysis
design:
## Goal
Implement the flashback mechanic and Deep Analysis card to complete Phase 5 of the epic.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Context
Completed: Tutor system (Merchant Scroll), Land-entered triggers (City of Traitors), Fetchlands (Polluted Delta), Event system migration (City of Brass). Missing: Flashback system and Deep Analysis card.

## Implementation Checklist

### Step 1: Add flashback cost property schema
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs
- Add :card/flashback-cost property to card definitions
- Structure: {:mana {:blue 1 :colorless 1} :life 3} for Deep Analysis
- Reference: Study existing cost structures in lion's-eye-diamond, polluted-delta

### Step 2: Extend can-cast? for graveyard zone
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs
- Current code (line 28): `(= :hand (:object/zone obj))` - hardcoded hand check
- Extend to: Check hand OR (graveyard AND has flashback-cost)
- When in graveyard with flashback: use :card/flashback-cost for mana check
- When in graveyard with flashback: check life cost if present (player life >= cost)
```clojure
;; Pseudocode for can-cast? extension:
(let [zone (:object/zone obj)
      has-flashback (:card/flashback-cost card)]
  (cond
    (= :hand zone) (mana/can-pay? db player-id (:card/mana-cost card))
    (and (= :graveyard zone) has-flashback)
    (let [fb-cost (:card/flashback-cost card)]
      (and (mana/can-pay? db player-id (:mana fb-cost))
           (or (nil? (:life fb-cost))
               (>= (q/get-player-life db player-id) (:life fb-cost)))))
    :else false))
```

### Step 3: Extend cast-spell for flashback
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs
- Detect if casting from graveyard (check :object/zone before cast)
- If graveyard + has flashback: pay flashback cost (mana + life), not normal cost
- If flashback has life cost: deduct life using existing drain/life-loss pattern
- Mark object with :object/cast-via-flashback true for resolution tracking
```clojure
;; Add to object before moving to stack:
[:db/add obj-eid :object/cast-via-flashback true]
```

### Step 4: Handle exile-on-resolution in resolve-spell
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs (resolve-spell, lines 127-149)
- Current code sends instants/sorceries to :graveyard (line 144)
- Add check: if :object/cast-via-flashback true, destination = :exile
- This is a replacement effect - applies even if spell would otherwise go to graveyard
```clojure
;; Modified destination logic:
(let [cast-via-flashback (:object/cast-via-flashback obj)
      destination (cond
                    cast-via-flashback :exile
                    (permanent-type? card-types) :battlefield
                    :else :graveyard)]
  ...)
```

### Step 5: Implement Deep Analysis card
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs
- Card ID: :deep-analysis
- Name: "Deep Analysis"
- Mana cost: {:colorless 3 :blue 1} (normal cast)
- Flashback cost: {:mana {:colorless 1 :blue 1} :life 3}
- Type: #{:sorcery}
- Colors: #{:blue}
- Effect: Draw 2 cards (targeting caster only for simplicity)
  - Use existing :draw effect type
  - Note: Original card says "Target player draws two cards and loses 2 life"
  - For Iggy Pop practice, self-target only is acceptable (always draws for caster)
- Life loss is PART OF THE EFFECT (2 life on normal cast), not the cost
- Flashback life cost (3 life) is ADDITIONAL to the effect's 2 life loss
```clojure
(def deep-analysis
  {:card/id :deep-analysis
   :card/name "Deep Analysis"
   :card/cmc 4
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 3 :blue 1}
   :card/flashback-cost {:mana {:colorless 1 :blue 1} :life 3}
   :card/colors #{:blue}
   :card/types #{:sorcery}
   :card/text "Target player draws two cards and loses 2 life. Flashback {1}{U}, Pay 3 life."
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :draw :effect/amount 2}
                  {:effect/type :drain :effect/amount 2}]}) ;; drain targets self = lose life
```

### Step 6: Add flashback tests
File: src/test/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis_test.cljs (NEW FILE)
Reference: src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs for test patterns

Tests to implement:
1. test-deep-analysis-in-hand-castable-with-mana
   - Card in hand, player has 3UU mana pool
   - can-cast? returns true
   - Bug caught: Ensures normal casting path still works

2. test-deep-analysis-in-graveyard-castable-with-flashback-mana-and-life
   - Card in graveyard, player has 1U mana and 3+ life
   - can-cast? returns true
   - Bug caught: Verifies flashback path enables casting from graveyard

3. test-deep-analysis-in-graveyard-not-castable-insufficient-life
   - Card in graveyard, player has 1U mana but only 2 life
   - can-cast? returns false
   - Bug caught: Ensures life cost is checked, prevents death-before-resolution

4. test-deep-analysis-in-graveyard-not-castable-insufficient-mana
   - Card in graveyard, player has 3 life but 0 mana
   - can-cast? returns false
   - Bug caught: Ensures mana cost still required

5. test-flashback-cast-pays-correct-costs
   - Cast from graveyard, verify 1U mana deducted, 3 life deducted
   - Bug caught: Ensures flashback cost (not normal cost) is paid

6. test-flashback-spell-exiles-on-resolution
   - Cast from graveyard, resolve
   - Object ends up in :exile zone, not :graveyard
   - Bug caught: Core flashback behavior - exile replacement effect

7. test-normal-cast-spell-goes-to-graveyard
   - Cast from hand, resolve
   - Object ends up in :graveyard zone (normal behavior preserved)
   - Bug caught: Regression - flashback changes don't break normal path

8. test-deep-analysis-draws-two-loses-two
   - Cast and resolve
   - Player hand +2 cards, life -2
   - Bug caught: Effect implementation correct

9. test-flashback-not-castable-from-exile
   - Card in exile zone with flashback
   - can-cast? returns false
   - Bug caught: Flashback only works from graveyard

### Step 7: Update all-cards in iggy_pop.cljs
- Add deep-analysis to all-cards vector

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Deep Analysis card defined with :card/flashback-cost {:mana {:colorless 1 :blue 1} :life 3}
- [ ] can-cast? returns true for flashback card in graveyard when player has mana AND life
- [ ] can-cast? returns false for flashback card in graveyard when player lacks life (< 3)
- [ ] cast-spell deducts flashback cost (not normal cost) when casting from graveyard
- [ ] cast-spell deducts life when flashback cost includes life
- [ ] Flashback cast marks object with :object/cast-via-flashback true
- [ ] resolve-spell sends :object/cast-via-flashback cards to :exile (not :graveyard)
- [ ] Normal cast (from hand) still sends to :graveyard (no regression)
- [ ] Deep Analysis effect: draw 2, lose 2 life
- [ ] All 9 flashback tests pass
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make test)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Life Cost Payment Timing**
- Flashback life cost is paid as part of casting (like mana)
- Must check life >= cost BEFORE allowing cast
- If player at 3 life casts flashback costing 3 life: allowed (goes to 0)
- If player at 2 life: cannot cast (insufficient resources)

**Deep Analysis Has Two Life Costs**
- Effect causes 2 life loss (target player loses 2 life)
- Flashback cost includes 3 life payment
- Total life loss when flashback casting: 5 life (3 cost + 2 effect)
- This is correct per Oracle text

**Flashback Only From Graveyard**
- Cards in exile with flashback: NOT castable
- Cards in hand with flashback: use normal cost
- Cards in library: not castable (can't cast from library)
- Only graveyard zone enables flashback alternate cost

**Replacement Effect for Destination**
- "If this spell would go to graveyard, exile it instead"
- This applies regardless of HOW it would go to graveyard:
  - Normal resolution: effect happens, then exile
  - Countered: counter tries to send to GY, replacement exiles
- Current code: resolve-spell handles resolution destination
- Counter implementation (if exists): may need separate handling
- Note: Counter system not implemented yet, so only resolution path needed

**No Flashback Without Cost**
- Card without :card/flashback-cost cannot be cast from graveyard
- Ensure can-cast? returns false for non-flashback cards in graveyard

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoding Deep Analysis specifically - flashback system must be generic
- ❌ NO checking flashback in resolve-spell by card name - use :object/cast-via-flashback flag
- ❌ NO allowing cast from graveyard without checking life cost (if present)
- ❌ NO breaking normal cast path for flashback cards (must still cast from hand normally)
- ❌ NO sending flashback spells to graveyard on resolution
close_reason: Deferred to future epic - scope too large for current batch. Epic delivered tutor system, land-entered triggers, fetchlands, and event system migration. Flashback system well-documented for future implementation.

title: Add :order-bottom selection type with chain from peek-and-select
design:
## Goal
Create the :order-bottom selection type and wire the chain from peek-and-select. This is the core selection logic that the UI modal and card definition changes depend on.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- events/selection/library.cljs:318-357 — reorder-library-for-scry (ordered vector pattern, scry-top/scry-bottom vectors)
- events/selection/library.cljs:231-282 — execute-peek-selection (current remainder shuffle + position assignment)
- events/selection/library.cljs:126-171 — build-peek-selection (peek builder, effect → selection state mapping)
- events/selection.cljs — confirm-selection-impl wrapper, execute-confirmed-selection multimethod
- events/selection.cljs — ::toggle-selection event handler (do NOT modify for order-bottom)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Test file: src/test/fizzle/events/order_bottom_selection_test.cljs

**Builder tests:**
- test-build-order-bottom-creates-correct-state: given 3 remainder card IDs, builds selection with :selection/type :order-bottom, :selection/candidates as set of 3 IDs, :selection/ordered as empty vector, :selection/player-id
- test-build-order-bottom-empty-candidates: given 0 cards, builds selection with empty candidates set (edge case for defensive coding)

**Order/unorder event tests:**
- test-order-card-appends-to-ordered: dispatch ::order-card with card-1, verify :selection/ordered is [card-1]
- test-order-second-card-appends-after-first: order card-1 then card-2, verify ordered is [card-1 card-2]
- test-order-card-not-in-candidates-is-noop: dispatch ::order-card with card not in candidates, verify ordered unchanged (catches missing validation bug)
- test-order-already-ordered-card-is-noop: order card-1, order card-1 again, verify ordered is [card-1] not [card-1 card-1] (catches duplicate bug)
- test-unorder-card-removes-from-ordered: order card-1 card-2 card-3, unorder card-2, verify ordered is [card-1 card-3]
- test-unorder-card-not-in-ordered-is-noop: unorder card not in ordered vector, verify ordered unchanged

**Any-order event tests:**
- test-any-order-shuffles-remaining-and-confirms: order card-1 of 3, dispatch ::any-order, verify all 3 cards in ordered (card-1 first, remaining 2 in some order after)
- test-any-order-with-no-ordered-shuffles-all: dispatch ::any-order with 0 ordered, verify all candidates in ordered vector
- test-any-order-with-all-ordered-confirms-as-is: order all 3 cards, dispatch ::any-order, verify ordered unchanged (no shuffle of empty remaining)

**Execute tests:**
- test-execute-assigns-positions-in-order: ordered is [card-A card-B card-C], library has existing cards at positions 0-4, verify card-A gets position 5, card-B gets 6, card-C gets 7 (catches off-by-one in position calculation)
- test-execute-with-empty-library: ordered is [card-A card-B], library is empty, verify card-A gets position 0, card-B gets 1 (catches failure when max-pos is -1)
- test-execute-cards-in-correct-zone: after execute, all ordered cards have :object/zone :library (not left in limbo)

**Chain tests:**
- test-peek-chains-to-order-bottom-when-flag-and-2plus-remainder: peek selection with order-remainder? true, select 1 of 3 cards, confirm, verify pending-selection is :order-bottom with 2 remainder cards as candidates
- test-peek-no-chain-when-flag-false: peek selection with shuffle-remainder? true (old flag), confirm, verify no order-bottom chain (remainder shuffled immediately)
- test-peek-no-chain-when-1-remainder: peek selection with order-remainder? true, select 2 of 3 cards, confirm, verify no chain (1 remainder placed directly)
- test-peek-no-chain-when-0-remainder: peek selection with order-remainder? true, select all 3 of 3, confirm, verify no chain (0 remainder)

### 3. Implementation checklist

**Selection state shape:**
```clojure
{:selection/type :order-bottom
 :selection/candidates #{card-1 card-2 card-3}  ; Set of object-ids to order
 :selection/ordered []                           ; Vector of object-ids in click sequence
 :selection/player-id :player-1
 :selection/spell-id <uuid>}
```

**Flag propagation (effect → peek → chain):**
- Flash of Insight effect has :effect/order-remainder? true (replaces :effect/shuffle-remainder? true)
- build-peek-selection reads :effect/order-remainder? and stores as :selection/order-remainder? true on the peek selection state
- execute-peek-selection checks :selection/order-remainder? + remainder count >= 2 to decide whether to chain

**Checklist:**
- [ ] cards/flash_of_insight.cljs — change :effect/shuffle-remainder? true to :effect/order-remainder? true
- [ ] events/selection/library.cljs — build-peek-selection: add :selection/order-remainder? from effect's :effect/order-remainder?
- [ ] events/selection/library.cljs — build-order-bottom-selection fn: takes (remainder-ids player-id spell-id), returns selection map with :selection/type :order-bottom, :selection/candidates (set), :selection/ordered [], :selection/player-id, :selection/spell-id
- [ ] events/selection/library.cljs — rf/reg-event-db ::order-card [db [_ object-id]]: if object-id in candidates AND not in ordered, conj to ordered vector. Otherwise no-op.
- [ ] events/selection/library.cljs — rf/reg-event-db ::unorder-card [db [_ object-id]]: remove object-id from ordered vector (filterv). No-op if not present.
- [ ] events/selection/library.cljs — rf/reg-event-fx ::any-order [cofx _]: calculate remaining = candidates - (set ordered), shuffle remaining, set ordered = (into ordered (shuffle remaining)), then dispatch ::confirm-selection
- [ ] events/selection/library.cljs — execute-order-bottom-selection fn: get max position of existing library cards (or -1 if empty), for each card in ordered vector assign position (max-pos + 1 + index), return {:db updated-db}
- [ ] events/selection.cljs — defmethod execute-confirmed-selection :order-bottom: call execute-order-bottom-selection, wrap with confirm-selection-impl
- [ ] events/selection/library.cljs — modify execute-peek-selection: after moving selected cards to hand, check if :selection/order-remainder? true AND (count remainder) >= 2. If yes, return {:db db-after-selected :pending-selection (build-order-bottom-selection remainder-ids player-id spell-id)}. If no, fall through to existing shuffle/direct-placement logic.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] build-order-bottom-selection returns map with correct :selection/type, :selection/candidates as set, :selection/ordered as empty vector
- [ ] ::order-card appends to ordered only if card is in candidates and not already ordered
- [ ] ::unorder-card removes card from ordered vector, maintaining relative order of remaining
- [ ] ::any-order shuffles unordered remainder, appends after ordered, dispatches confirm
- [ ] execute-order-bottom-selection assigns positions correctly: first ordered card at max-pos+1, incrementing (verified with specific position assertions)
- [ ] execute-order-bottom-selection handles empty library (max-pos = -1, first card at position 0)
- [ ] Peek-and-select chains to order-bottom when order-remainder? true AND 2+ remainder cards
- [ ] Peek-and-select does NOT chain when flag is false, or when 0-1 remainder cards
- [ ] Existing peek-and-select behavior unchanged for cards with shuffle-remainder? true
- [ ] All existing Flash of Insight tests pass (no regression)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO modifying ::toggle-selection handler (mechanism vs policy: order-bottom has its own events)
- ❌ NO using :selection/selected for order tracking (sets are unordered; :selection/ordered vector is required)
- ❌ NO allowing duplicate entries in :selection/ordered (must check before conj)
- ❌ NO allowing cards not in :selection/candidates to be ordered (must validate membership)
- ❌ NO hard-coding Flash of Insight references in order-bottom logic (must be generic/reusable)
- ❌ NO breaking existing shuffle-remainder? behavior (cards without order-remainder? must work as before)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty candidates**
- build-order-bottom-selection should handle empty set gracefully (returns selection with empty candidates)
- In practice, chain logic prevents this (only chains when 2+ remainder), but builder should not crash

**Edge Case: Card not in candidates**
- ::order-card with card-id not in candidates set must be a no-op
- Prevents UI bugs from dispatching with wrong card ID

**Edge Case: Double ordering**
- ::order-card with card already in ordered must be no-op
- Prevents [card-1 card-1] in ordered vector if event dispatched twice (rapid click)

**Edge Case: Empty library during execute**
- When library has no other cards, max-pos calculation must return -1 (not nil/crash)
- First ordered card gets position 0

**Flag propagation chain:**
- effect/order-remainder? → selection/order-remainder? → chain decision
- Must verify the flag correctly flows through build-peek-selection into the peek selection state
- Must not interfere with existing shuffle-remainder? flag handling

**Cleanup on cancel:**
- If player cancels/clears the order-bottom selection, standard selection cleanup should work
- No special cleanup needed (ordered vector is just data on pending-selection)
close_reason: Closed

title: Bug: Next Phase button allows advancing with non-empty stack
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Next Phase button MUST be disabled when the stack is non-empty
- New Turn button MUST be disabled when the stack is non-empty
- advance-phase event handler MUST no-op when stack is non-empty
- start-turn event handler MUST no-op when stack is non-empty
- Mana pool still empties on valid phase advance (existing behavior preserved)
- Turn-based triggers still fire on phase entry (existing behavior preserved)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Next Phase button visually disabled (greyed out) when stack has items
- [ ] New Turn button visually disabled when stack has items
- [ ] Clicking disabled button does nothing (no phase change)
- [ ] advance-phase event returns db unchanged when stack non-empty
- [ ] start-turn event returns db unchanged when stack non-empty
- [ ] Buttons re-enable immediately when stack empties
- [ ] All existing phase advancement behavior preserved when stack is empty
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] make validate passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO removing items from the stack on phase advance (bug fix: stack items must persist until explicitly resolved)
- ❌ NO auto-resolving the stack before advancing (simplicity: player decides when to resolve)
- ❌ NO new subscriptions when existing ::subs/stack works (YAGNI: reuse what exists)
- ❌ NO priority system implementation in this fix (scope: this is a bug fix, not a feature)

## Approach
Disable the Next Phase / New Turn button in the UI when the stack subscription returns non-empty items. Add defensive guards in both advance-phase and start-turn re-frame event handlers to no-op when the stack is non-empty. Two files changed: core.cljs (UI) and events/game.cljs (event guards).

## Architecture
- core.cljs turn-bar component: subscribe to ::subs/stack, use (seq stack) to conditionally disable button
- events/game.cljs ::advance-phase handler: check stack via queries, return db unchanged if non-empty
- events/game.cljs ::start-turn handler: same guard

## Design Rationale
### Problem
The Next Phase button currently allows advancing phases while items remain on the stack. This violates MTG rules where phase transitions only occur with an empty stack. Stack items persist across phases and even across turns, creating invalid game states.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- core.cljs:346-376 - turn-bar component, dispatches ::advance-phase with no stack check
- events/game.cljs:428-439 - advance-phase and start-turn handlers, no stack guards
- subs/game.cljs:36-53 - ::stack subscription already exists, returns all stack items
- queries.cljs:130-139 - get-stack-items query available for event handler guards

**External:**
- MTG Comprehensive Rules: Phase transitions require empty stack and both players passing priority

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Both UI disable + event guard ✓
**What it is:** Disable button in UI when stack non-empty AND add no-op guards in event handlers.

**Pros:**
- Defense-in-depth: UI prevents clicks, event handler catches programmatic dispatch
- Clear visual feedback to player

**Cons:**
- Slightly more code than UI-only

**Chosen because:** User preference for belt-and-suspenders safety, minimal additional complexity.

#### 2. UI-only disable ❌
**What it is:** Only disable the button in the UI, no event handler changes.

**Why we looked at this:** Simplest possible fix for a single-player tool.

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User preferred defense-in-depth with both UI and event guard.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Explicitly asked to simplify.

#### 3. Event guard only ❌
**What it is:** Silent no-op in event handlers, button stays visually enabled.

**Why we looked at this:** Backend-first approach.

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Poor UX - button looks clickable but does nothing. No visual feedback.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** UI constraints prevent button disabling.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Disabling Next Phase / New Turn buttons when stack non-empty
- Event handler guards for advance-phase and start-turn

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Priority passing system - deferred to future feature
- Stack auto-resolution - never (player controls resolution)
- Resolve button changes - not needed for this fix

### Open Questions
- None - straightforward bug fix

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| What happens to stack items on phase advance? | Button should not work until stack empty | Disable button, don't touch stack items |
| Mana pool behavior? | Always empty on phase advance | No change to existing mana pool logic |
| New button or fix existing? | Fix existing - MTG rules say phase advances with empty stack | Modify existing turn-bar component |
| Scope: simple disable or priority system? | Just disable when stack non-empty | No priority system, simple guard |
| Which approach? | Both UI + event guard | Defense-in-depth |

### Dead-End Paths
None - straightforward bug fix with clear approach.

### Open Concerns Raised
None - user confirmed design without modifications.
close_reason: All success criteria verified: guards in pure functions, button disabled in UI, 729/729 tests pass, make validate clean

title: Task 3: Write Dedicated Gemstone Mine Test File
description: Write dedicated test files for 4 cards following the documented testing strategy. Each card needs: card definition tests, core mechanic tests, edge case tests. Gemstone Mine: land with mining counters, Chrome Mox: imprint selection + mana, Duress: opponent hand selection, Vampiric Tutor: library search with life cost.
design:
## Goal
Create a dedicated test file for Gemstone Mine that consolidates and extends the existing coverage in tap_land_test.cljs. Gemstone Mine is the only one of the 4 originally-planned cards that has a card definition. Chrome Mox, Duress, and Vampiric Tutor do NOT exist in the codebase and require full card implementation (separate tasks).

## Pre-conditions
- Task 2 (anti-patterns) complete
- Gemstone Mine card definition exists in iggy_pop.cljs:120
- Gemstone Mine already tested in tap_land_test.cljs (counter depletion, sacrifice)
- Shared test helpers available

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Gemstone Mine is a rainbow land with mining counters:
- ETB: enters with 3 mining counters
- Ability: T, Remove a mining counter: Add one mana of any color
- When no mining counters remain, sacrifice it
- Card definition at iggy_pop.cljs:120-143

Existing coverage in tap_land_test.cljs:
- Tap for mana with counter removal (lines 166-180)
- Multiple taps depleting counters (lines 181-200)
- Color selection for any-mana (lines 240-295)
- Sacrifice when depleted (lines 393-426)

## Implementation

### 1. Create test file src/test/fizzle/cards/gemstone_mine_test.cljs

Required requires:
- cljs.test, fizzle.cards.iggy-pop, fizzle.db.queries, fizzle.events.abilities, fizzle.test-helpers

### 2. Card definition tests
- Verify :card/id, :card/name, :card/cmc, :card/mana-cost, :card/colors, :card/types
- Verify :card/etb-effects has add-counters with {:mining 3}
- Verify :card/abilities has mana ability with tap + remove-counter cost
- Verify ability/produces is {:any 1}
- Verify sacrifice condition is :no-counters on :mining

### 3. Core mechanic tests (parameterized over 5 colors)
- Tap Gemstone Mine for each color using doseq over [:black :blue :white :red :green]
- Verify counter is removed after tap
- Verify correct mana color added to pool
- Include dynamic assertion messages with color name

### 4. Counter depletion tests
- Start with 3 counters, tap 3 times, verify sacrifice on 3rd tap
- After 2 taps, verify still on battlefield with 1 counter
- Verify cannot activate with 0 counters (if applicable)

### 5. Edge case tests
- Cannot activate from graveyard
- Cannot activate from hand
- Cannot activate when already tapped
- ETB counter placement (3 mining counters after entering battlefield)

### 6. Run make validate

## Success Criteria
- [ ] gemstone_mine_test.cljs exists at src/test/fizzle/cards/
- [ ] Card definition tests verify all attributes and ability structure
- [ ] Color tests use doseq parameterization over 5 colors (not 5 separate deftests)
- [ ] Counter depletion test verifies sacrifice after 3 taps
- [ ] Edge cases cover graveyard, hand, tapped states
- [ ] All tests pass (make validate green)
- [ ] No lint or format warnings
- [ ] Minimum 20 assertions

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Counter tracking**
- Mining counters are tracked on the object entity
- After ETB, object should have {:mining 3} counters
- Each tap removes 1 counter, verify counter count after each tap
- Check if activate-mana-ability handles counter removal internally

**Edge Case: Sacrifice timing**
- Sacrifice happens when activating with last counter (triggered by :no-counters condition)
- NOT when counters reach 0 passively - only on activation attempt
- Test: after 3rd tap, Gemstone Mine should be in graveyard

**Edge Case: Mana still produced on sacrifice tap**
- When tapping the last counter, mana IS still produced (sacrifice is in addition)
- The 3rd tap should BOTH produce mana AND sacrifice the permanent

**Reference: tap_land_test.cljs**
- Study existing Gemstone Mine tests for patterns
- New file should cover the same scenarios + card definition + parameterized colors

## Anti-Patterns
- NO copy-pasting color tests - use doseq parameterization
- NO test-only production code changes - test through existing APIs
- NO hardcoded counter values - reference the card definition ETB counters
- NO removing existing tap_land_test.cljs coverage - new file is additive
