title: Create engine/costs.cljs with multimethod for :tap and :remove-counter
design:
## Goal
Implement engine/costs.cljs with multimethod dispatch for cost payment. Support :tap and :remove-counter cost types.

## Context
Completed fizzle-isp: Schema fields and effect types in place. Now need cost payment system that abilities.cljs will use.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Implementation Steps

### 1. RED: Write tests for pay-cost multimethod
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/costs_test.cljs (new file)

Follow pattern from effects_test.cljs - use add-permanent helper.

**:tap cost tests (6 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-pay-tap-cost-untapped-permanent
  (testing "pay-cost with :tap on untapped permanent taps it and returns db"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent on battlefield
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:tap true})
    ;; Assert: returned db has permanent tapped, not nil
    ))

(deftest test-pay-tap-cost-already-tapped
  (testing "pay-cost with :tap on already tapped permanent returns nil"
    ;; Setup: tapped permanent on battlefield
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:tap true})
    ;; Assert: returns nil (cost cannot be paid)
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-tap-untapped
  (testing "can-pay? :tap returns true for untapped permanent"
    ;; Catches: missing can-pay? implementation
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-tap-already-tapped
  (testing "can-pay? :tap returns false for tapped permanent"
    ;; Catches: can-pay? not checking tapped state
    ))

(deftest test-pay-tap-invalid-object-id
  (testing "pay-cost with invalid object-id returns nil, no crash"
    ;; Catches: missing nil guard on object lookup
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-tap-invalid-object-id
  (testing "can-pay? with invalid object-id returns false, no crash"
    ;; Catches: missing nil guard
    ))
```

**:remove-counter cost tests (6 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-pay-remove-counter-has-counters
  (testing "pay-cost removes counter from permanent with sufficient counters"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: permanent now has {:mining 2}
    ;; Catches: counter not decremented
    ))

(deftest test-pay-remove-counter-exact-amount
  (testing "pay-cost removes last counter (edge case: counter goes to 0)"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 1}
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: permanent has {:mining 0} (counter exists with 0 value)
    ;; Catches: off-by-one error
    ))

(deftest test-pay-remove-counter-insufficient
  (testing "pay-cost returns nil when insufficient counters"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 1}
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:remove-counter {:mining 2}})
    ;; Assert: returns nil, counters unchanged
    ;; Catches: paying cost even when can't afford
    ))

(deftest test-pay-remove-counter-no-counters-at-all
  (testing "pay-cost returns nil when permanent has no counters (nil :object/counters)"
    ;; Setup: permanent with nil :object/counters
    ;; Action: (costs/pay-cost db object-id {:remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: returns nil
    ;; Catches: nil pointer when counters map missing
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-remove-counter-has-counters
  (testing "can-pay? returns true when counters sufficient"
    ;; Catches: can-pay? not checking counter count
    ))

(deftest test-can-pay-remove-counter-insufficient
  (testing "can-pay? returns false when counters insufficient"
    ;; Catches: can-pay? returning true when can't pay
    ))
```

### 2. GREEN: Implement engine/costs.cljs

Create new file: src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.costs
  "Cost payment system for abilities.
   
   Dispatches on cost type to pay costs.
   All functions are pure: (db, object-id, cost) -> db or nil."
  (:require
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]))


(defmulti pay-cost
  "Pay a single cost on an object.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent paying the cost
     cost - Map with single cost type key, e.g. {:tap true} or {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}
   
   Returns:
     New db with cost paid, or nil if cost cannot be paid."
  (fn [_db _object-id cost] (first (keys cost))))


(defmulti can-pay?
  "Check if a cost can be paid.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent
     cost - Map with single cost type key
   
   Returns:
     Boolean - true if cost can be paid, false otherwise."
  (fn [_db _object-id cost] (first (keys cost))))


;; Default: unknown cost types cannot be paid
(defmethod pay-cost :default [_db _object-id _cost] nil)
(defmethod can-pay? :default [_db _object-id _cost] false)
```

### 3. Implement :tap cost type

```clojure
(defmethod can-pay? :tap [db object-id _cost]
  ;; Can pay tap cost if:
  ;; 1. Object exists
  ;; 2. Object is not already tapped
  (when-let [obj-eid (q/get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (not (d/q '[:find ?tapped .
                :in $ ?e
                :where [?e :object/tapped ?tapped]]
              db obj-eid))))


(defmethod pay-cost :tap [db object-id cost]
  ;; Guard: check can pay first
  (when (can-pay? db object-id cost)
    (let [obj-eid (q/get-object-eid db object-id)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/tapped true]]))))
```

Note: May need to add q/get-object-eid to queries.cljs if it doesn't exist.

### 4. Implement :remove-counter cost type

```clojure
(defmethod can-pay? :remove-counter [db object-id cost]
  ;; Can pay remove-counter if:
  ;; 1. Object exists
  ;; 2. Object has sufficient counters of required type(s)
  (when-let [obj-eid (q/get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (let [required (:remove-counter cost)
          current (or (d/q '[:find ?c .
                             :in $ ?e
                             :where [?e :object/counters ?c]]
                           db obj-eid)
                      {})]
      (every? (fn [[counter-type amount]]
                (>= (get current counter-type 0) amount))
              required))))


(defmethod pay-cost :remove-counter [db object-id cost]
  ;; Guard: check can pay first
  (when (can-pay? db object-id cost)
    (let [obj-eid (q/get-object-eid db object-id)
          required (:remove-counter cost)
          current (or (d/q '[:find ?c .
                             :in $ ?e
                             :where [?e :object/counters ?c]]
                           db obj-eid)
                      {})
          new-counters (merge-with - current required)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/counters new-counters]]))))
```

### 5. Add helper query if needed

Check if q/get-object-eid exists. If not, add to db/queries.cljs:

```clojure
(defn get-object-eid
  "Get the entity ID for an object by its UUID."
  [db object-id]
  (d/q '[:find ?e .
         :in $ ?oid
         :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
       db object-id))
```

### 6. VERIFY
- Run: make validate
- Confirm all 12 new tests pass
- Confirm existing tests still pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] engine/costs.cljs exists at src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs
- [ ] pay-cost multimethod dispatches on (first (keys cost))
- [ ] can-pay? multimethod dispatches on (first (keys cost))
- [ ] :tap cost: taps untapped permanent, returns nil if already tapped
- [ ] :remove-counter cost: removes counters, returns nil if insufficient
- [ ] 12 tests pass in costs_test.cljs (6 tap + 6 counter)
- [ ] All 161+ existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO cond-> blocks for cost types (use multimethod dispatch)
- ❌ NO checking specific card IDs (data over code)
- ❌ NO mutating permanent state outside transaction
- ❌ NO paying cost when can-pay? returns false
- ❌ NO crashing on nil object-id (return nil/false gracefully)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Nil Object ID**:
- Both pay-cost and can-pay? must handle nil object-id
- Return nil/false gracefully, no exceptions
- Test: test-pay-tap-invalid-object-id

**Nil Counters Map**:
- Permanent may have nil :object/counters (never had counters)
- Treat as empty map {} for comparison
- Test: test-pay-remove-counter-no-counters-at-all

**Counter Goes to Zero**:
- Removing last counter should leave {:mining 0}, not delete key
- This is important for state-based actions that check counter count
- Test: test-pay-remove-counter-exact-amount

**Default Multimethod**:
- Unknown cost types should return nil/false (graceful degradation)
- Matches pattern in effects.cljs

## Reference Implementation
- Study engine/effects.cljs for multimethod pattern
- Study test helpers in effects_test.cljs for add-permanent
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement Recoup card with grant, targeting, and cleanup systems
description:
Implement the Recoup card (1R Sorcery) which requires three new infrastructure systems:

**Oracle Text:** Target sorcery card in your graveyard gains flashback until end of turn. The flashback cost is equal to the card's mana cost. Flashback {3}{R}

## Systems Required

1. **Grant System** - Temporarily grant abilities/alternate costs to card instances
   - New :object/grants schema attribute
   - Explicit expiration: {:expires/turn N :expires/phase :cleanup}
   - Source tracking for removal effects

2. **Cast-Time Targeting System** - Declare and validate targets when casting
   - New :card/targeting and :ability/targeting attributes
   - Targets stored as map: target-ref → target-id
   - Fizzle if targets invalid on resolution

3. **Cleanup System** - Remove expired grants at end of turn
   - Registered as turn-based trigger on :cleanup phase

## Implementation Phases

1. Grant System (schema + core functions)
2. Cleanup System (expiration + trigger)
3. Targeting Foundation (core functions)
4. Deep Analysis Migration (stepping stone)
5. Cephalid Coliseum Migration (stepping stone)
6. Grant Flashback Effect
7. Recoup Card

**Design Doc:** docs/design/recoup-design.md
close_reason: All features implemented and tested: grant system, cast-time targeting, cleanup expiration, Recoup card with flashback granting. Multiple bug fixes for targeting UI and flashback exile behavior.

title: Implement land-entered trigger and City of Traitors
design:
## Goal
Implement :land-entered event type and City of Traitors card which produces {C}{C} and sacrifices itself when another land enters the battlefield.

## Context
Completed:
- fizzle-h4l: Event system migration done, old scanning code removed
- fizzle-pk2: Tutor infrastructure built, Merchant Scroll working

This is Phase 3 of epic fizzle-wjl. Uses the event-based trigger system established in Phase 1.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add land-entered-event helper
- File: src/main/fizzle/engine/events.cljs
- Add after zone-change-event (line ~54)
- Pattern similar to permanent-tapped-event:
  \`\`\`clojure
  (defn land-entered-event
    \"Create a land-entered event.
     Arguments:
       object-id - ID of the land that entered
       controller-id - ID of the land's controller
     Returns:
       Event map with :event/type :land-entered\"
    [object-id controller-id]
    {:event/type :land-entered
     :event/object-id object-id
     :event/controller controller-id})
  \`\`\`

### Step 2: Dispatch land-entered event in play-land
- File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs
- Location: play-land function around line 451-462
- After ETB effects execute, dispatch land-entered event:
  \`\`\`clojure
  ;; After ETB effects processing, before returning:
  (dispatch/dispatch-event db-after-etb 
    (game-events/land-entered-event object-id player-id))
  \`\`\`

### Step 3: Extend matches-filter? for :exclude-self
- File: src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger_dispatch.cljs
- Location: matches-filter? function (lines 11-31)
- Add :exclude-self handling in the cond:
  \`\`\`clojure
  (defn matches-filter?
    [trigger event]
    (let [f (:trigger/filter trigger)]
      (if (and f (seq f))
        (every? (fn [[k v]]
                  (cond
                    ;; Existing: :self matches trigger's source
                    (= v :self) (= (get event k) (:trigger/source trigger))
                    ;; NEW: :exclude-self means trigger source must NOT match event object
                    (= k :exclude-self) (not= (:event/object-id event) (:trigger/source trigger))
                    :else (= (get event k) v)))
                f)
        true)))
  \`\`\`

### Step 4: Implement City of Traitors card definition
- File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs
- Add after city-of-brass definition (around line 77)
- Add to all-cards vector at end
  \`\`\`clojure
  (def city-of-traitors
    {:card/id :city-of-traitors
     :card/name "City of Traitors"
     :card/cmc 0
     :card/mana-cost {}
     :card/colors #{}
     :card/types #{:land}
     :card/text \"When you play another land, sacrifice City of Traitors. {T}: Add {C}{C}.\"
     ;; Trigger: When ANOTHER land enters (not self)
     :card/triggers [{:trigger/type :land-entered
                      :trigger/description \"sacrifice City of Traitors\"
                      :trigger/filter {:exclude-self true}
                      :trigger/effects [{:effect/type :sacrifice
                                         :effect/target :self}]}]
     ;; Mana ability: T: Add {C}{C}
     :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                       :ability/cost {:tap true}
                       :ability/produces {:colorless 2}}]})
  \`\`\`

### Step 5: Update register-card-triggers! for land-entered
- File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs
- Location: trigger-type->event-type function (lines 333-340)
- Add mapping for :land-entered:
  \`\`\`clojure
  (defn- trigger-type->event-type
    [trigger-type]
    (case trigger-type
      :becomes-tapped :permanent-tapped
      :land-entered :land-entered  ;; NEW
      trigger-type))
  \`\`\`

### Step 6: Write tests
- File: src/test/fizzle/cards/city_of_traitors_test.cljs (NEW)
- Tests to implement (see Success Criteria)

### Step 7: Run validation
- make validate passes
- All tests green

## Success Criteria
- [ ] land-entered-event helper exists in events.cljs
- [ ] play-land dispatches :land-entered event after land ETBs
- [ ] matches-filter? supports :exclude-self filter key
- [ ] City of Traitors produces {:colorless 2} via mana ability
- [ ] City of Traitors trigger registered on ETB with :exclude-self filter
- [ ] City of Traitors sacrifices when ANOTHER land enters battlefield
- [ ] City of Traitors does NOT sacrifice when it enters (exclude-self works)
- [ ] Test: test-cot-produces-colorless-mana - verifies {C}{C} production
- [ ] Test: test-cot-does-not-sacrifice-on-self-entry - verifies exclude-self
- [ ] Test: test-cot-sacrifices-when-another-land-enters - core trigger behavior
- [ ] Test: test-two-cots-both-sacrifice-when-land-enters - edge case
- [ ] Test: test-cot-trigger-uses-stack - trigger goes on stack, can respond
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: City of Traitors as First Land**
- When CoT enters as first land, trigger should NOT fire (no other lands entered)
- Verify :exclude-self filter is working correctly
- Test explicitly: play CoT, verify no trigger on stack

**Edge Case: Two City of Traitors on Battlefield**
- When a third land enters, BOTH CoT triggers should fire
- Both should sacrifice (or both triggers on stack)
- Test explicitly: play CoT #1, play CoT #2 (CoT #1 sacrifices), play Island (remaining CoT sacrifices)

**Edge Case: City of Traitors Sacrificed in Response**
- If player sacrifices CoT to another effect (e.g., mana ability that sacs)
- Trigger already on stack should still try to resolve
- But :self target won't find permanent - should handle gracefully
- Reference: cob-event-trigger-test.cljs:273-292 shows similar pattern

**Test Isolation**
- Use reset-registry fixture pattern from cob_event_trigger_test.cljs
- Clear registry before and after each test
- Re-register turn-based actions after clearing

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow City of Brass pattern in cob_event_trigger_test.cljs
- Use same test helpers (create-test-db, add-land-to-hand, add-land-to-battlefield)
- Similar register function for test setup

## Anti-Patterns (from epic)
- NO scanning-based triggers (use event system)
- NO check-trigger multimethod (use trigger-registry + dispatch-event)
- NO leaving dead code (clean up if anything obsolete)
close_reason: Implemented land-entered event type and City of Traitors card. All tests pass, make validate passes.

title: Flash of Insight: control order of cards placed on bottom of library
description: Flash of Insight exiles cards from graveyard and looks at top X cards, putting chosen cards in hand and the rest on the bottom of library. Currently the 'rest go to bottom' ordering is uncontrolled. Need to let the player choose the order cards are placed on the bottom. This requires updating the underlying bottom-of-library effect to support ordered placement, plus a UX for drag-to-reorder or click-to-sequence the rejected cards before they go to bottom. Affects any future cards that put multiple cards on bottom in a chosen order (e.g. scry-like effects).
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Flash of Insight remainder cards placed on bottom of library in player-chosen order (not random)
- New :order-bottom selection type with click-to-sequence UX
- Click a card to add it to ordered pile (first click = top of bottom pile, closest to rest of library)
- Click an ordered card to remove it from the sequence
- "Any Order" button always present: randomizes remaining unsequenced cards below ordered ones and finalizes
- Ordering step auto-skipped for 0-1 remainder cards (placed directly, no UI)
- Chained from peek-and-select confirm when :effect/order-remainder? flag is set
- Reusable for any future card needing player-ordered bottom placement (scry, Brainstorm, etc.)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Flash of Insight remainder cards placed in player-chosen order on bottom of library
- [ ] Click-to-sequence: clicking unordered card appends to ordered pile
- [ ] Clicking ordered card removes it from sequence
- [ ] "Any Order" button randomizes remaining unsequenced cards below ordered and finalizes
- [ ] Ordering step auto-skipped for 0-1 remainder cards
- [ ] Peek-and-select → order-bottom chain works seamlessly
- [ ] All existing Flash of Insight tests still pass
- [ ] New tests for order-bottom selection type (builder, toggle, execute, edge cases)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO modifying the generic toggle-selection handler (mechanism vs policy: order-bottom has its own toggle/ordering logic via custom events)
- ❌ NO storing order in :selection/selected set (sets are unordered; use :selection/ordered vector for sequence)
- ❌ NO hard-coding Flash of Insight logic in order-bottom type (reusable: any card can use :effect/order-remainder? true)
- ❌ NO showing ordering UI for 0-1 cards (unnecessary clicks, auto-place instead)
- ❌ NO breaking existing peek-and-select behavior for cards without order-remainder? flag

## Approach
Change Flash of Insight card definition from :effect/shuffle-remainder? true to :effect/order-remainder? true. After peek-and-select confirm, when this flag is set and 2+ remainder cards exist, chain into a new :order-bottom selection type. The selection presents unsequenced cards and lets the player click to sequence them (first click = top of bottom pile). An "Any Order" button is always visible and randomizes remaining unsequenced cards below the ordered ones, then finalizes. Execution assigns library positions based on the ordered vector. The :order-bottom type is generic and reusable for any future card that needs player-ordered bottom-of-library placement.

## Architecture
- Card flag: :effect/order-remainder? true replaces :effect/shuffle-remainder? true on Flash of Insight
- Chain point: execute-peek-selection in library.cljs checks flag + remainder count (>= 2)
- Selection state: :selection/type :order-bottom, :selection/ordered [] (vector, click sequence), :selection/candidates #{} (set of all remainder card IDs)
- Custom events: ::order-card (append to ordered), ::unorder-card (remove from ordered), ::any-order (shuffle remaining + finalize)
- Execute: positions assigned as max-pos + 1, +2, etc. following ordered vector
- Modal: new order-bottom-modal in views/modals.cljs with "Unsequenced" and "Ordered" sections
- Subscription: order-bottom selection state (candidates, ordered, player-id)

## Parallelism Map

### Independent Work Streams
1. **Selection logic** (events/selection/library.cljs, events/selection.cljs)
   - Chain from peek-and-select, builder, custom toggle events, execute, multimethod
   - Estimated complexity: medium
2. **UI modal + subscription** (views/modals.cljs, subs/)
   - New order-bottom-modal component, subscriptions for selection state
   - Estimated complexity: medium

### Stream Dependencies
- Stream 2 depends on Stream 1 (modal needs selection state shape defined first)

### Suggested Waves
- Wave 1: Stream 1 (selection logic — defines data model)
- Wave 2: Stream 2 (UI — consumes data model)

### File Ownership Boundaries
| Stream | Owns (exclusive) | Shared (needs coordination) |
|--------|-------------------|-----------------------------|
| Selection logic | events/selection/library.cljs (order-bottom fns), events/selection.cljs (multimethod) | — |
| UI modal | views/modals.cljs (order-bottom-modal), subs/ | events/selection.cljs (dispatches) |

### Parallelism Assessment
- Independent streams: 1 (sequential dependency)
- Recommendation: solo execution
- Rationale: Only 2 streams with sequential dependency. All changes tightly coupled. Coordination overhead not worthwhile.

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Flash of Insight says "put the rest on the bottom of your library in any order" but currently randomizes the order. Players need to control bottom-of-library ordering to set up future draws (e.g., ordering lands vs spells on bottom). This is rules-accurate MTG behavior.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- cards/flash_of_insight.cljs:11-36 - Card definition with :effect/shuffle-remainder? true flag
- events/selection/library.cljs:126-171 - build-peek-selection creates peek selection state
- events/selection/library.cljs:231-282 - execute-peek-selection handles zone transitions, shuffles remainder
- events/selection/library.cljs:318-357 - reorder-library-for-scry shows existing ordering pattern (top/bottom piles with click)
- views/modals.cljs:408-465 - scry-modal has precedent for card ordering UI with pile sections

**Patterns:**
- Selection chaining via multimethod returning {:db :pending-selection}
- Scry uses :selection/scry-top and :selection/scry-bottom vectors for ordered placement
- Library positions: :object/position integer, 0 = top, incrementing downward

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Chained selection with new :order-bottom type ✓

**What it is:** After peek-and-select confirms, chain into a new :order-bottom selection type with its own modal. Uses :selection/ordered vector for click-sequence ordering.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed selection chaining pattern in selection.cljs — multimethod returns {:db :pending-selection} to chain
- Reviewed scry ordering UI — confirms ordered vector pattern works well
- Checked library.cljs execute functions — position assignment pattern is clean

**Pros:**
- Follows existing chaining pattern
- Reusable for future cards (Brainstorm, scry, etc.)
- Clean separation of concerns
- :order-bottom type focused on single responsibility

**Cons:**
- New selection type + modal (more code)

**Chosen because:** Consistent with existing selection architecture, reusable, keeps each selection type focused

#### 2. Inline ordering phase in peek-and-select modal ❌

**What it is:** Same peek-and-select modal transitions to ordering phase after card selection.

**Why we looked at this:** Fewer moving parts, single modal experience

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed peek-selection-modal in modals.cljs — already complex with card grid + clear/confirm
- Adding ordering phase would mix two concerns in one modal

**Pros:**
- Fewer files changed
- Single modal (no transition flash)

**Cons:**
- Complicates peek-and-select (violates single responsibility)
- Not reusable for non-peek ordering scenarios
- Modal becomes stateful with phases

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Violates "separate mechanism from policy" design principle. Peek-and-select and ordering are different concerns.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We discover the modal transition creates unacceptable UX jank.

#### 3. Repurpose scry modal for bottom ordering ❌

**What it is:** Reuse existing scry-modal and scry selection type for the ordering step.

**Why we looked at this:** Less new code, existing pattern

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed scry-modal — designed for top/bottom split (two piles)
- Order-bottom only needs one pile (everything goes to bottom)
- Scry selection state has scry-top and scry-bottom vectors — wrong shape

**Pros:**
- Existing code, less to build

**Cons:**
- Scry is top/bottom split, order-bottom is just bottom
- Would need to hack scry to hide top pile
- Semantically wrong (not scrying)

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Wrong abstraction. Scry splits cards between two zones; order-bottom sequences cards for one zone.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We unify all library ordering into a single generic ordering component.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- :order-bottom selection type (builder, toggle, execute)
- Order-bottom modal component
- Flash of Insight flag change
- Chain from peek-and-select to order-bottom
- Tests for all new code

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Drag-to-reorder UX (deferred — click-to-sequence sufficient for now)
- Scry integration with order-bottom (deferred — scry has its own working UI)
- Other cards using order-remainder? (deferred — infrastructure ready, cards added later)

### Open Questions
- Should "Any Order" also work as "Confirm" when all cards are already ordered? (Likely yes — same effect)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Ordering UX pattern? | Click-to-sequence, top of pile first, "Any Order" button always present | Custom events for ordering (not generic toggle), always-visible finalize button |
| Auto-skip for few cards? | Skip if 0-1 cards | Chain logic needs count check before creating order-bottom selection |
| Approach? | Chained selection with new :order-bottom type | New selection type, follows existing chaining pattern |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Selection Chaining Pattern
**Question explored:** How does the selection system chain between selection types?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/selection.cljs — confirm-selection-impl wrapper
- Memory notes on multimethod return values

**Findings:**
- Multimethod returns {:db :pending-selection} to chain to next selection
- confirm-selection-impl handles cleanup and chaining automatically
- Scry already chains from peek-and-select in some scenarios

**Conclusion:** Standard chaining pattern works perfectly for peek → order-bottom chain

#### Library Position Management
**Question explored:** How are library card positions managed?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/selection/library.cljs:261-282 — execute-peek-selection position assignment
- db/queries.cljs — get-top-n-library query

**Findings:**
- Position 0 = top, incrementing downward
- Bottom placement: find max position, assign max+1, max+2, etc.
- Existing pattern in execute-peek-selection works for ordered placement

**Conclusion:** Same position assignment pattern, just use ordered vector instead of shuffled sequence

### Dead-End Paths
None — the selected approach was the natural fit from the start.

### Open Concerns Raised
- "Any Order" button behavior when all cards already ordered → functions as Confirm (no remaining to randomize)
close_reason: All success criteria met, review approved, committed as 99131f1

title: Task 3: Migrate card-selection, pile-choice, graveyard, exile-cards, and peek-selection modals
description:
## Goal
Migrate 5 modals that share the selection-card-view helper from core.cljs to modals.cljs. Also move the selection-card-view helper itself.

## Implementation

### Move to modals.cljs:
1. selection-card-view (core.cljs:421-442) - shared helper used by 5 modals
2. card-selection-modal (core.cljs:445-569) - discard/tutor with multi-select
3. pile-choice-modal (core.cljs:661-729) - Intuition-style pile choice
4. graveyard-selection-modal (core.cljs:732-804) - Ill-Gotten Gains style
5. exile-cards-selection-modal (core.cljs:807-876) - Flash of Insight flashback
6. peek-selection-modal (core.cljs:879-950) - Flash of Insight peek

### Key details:
- selection-card-view uses ::selection-events/toggle-selection as default event — already required in modals.cljs
- card-selection-modal also dispatches ::selection-events/confirm-tutor-selection, ::selection-events/confirm-selection, ::selection-events/cancel-selection — all in selection-events namespace
- graveyard-selection-modal has purple border (#6a4a8a) — use container-style with border-color param
- exile-cards, peek, graveyard modals also have purple border (#6a4a8a)
- card-selection-modal has blue border (#4A9BD9)
- pile-choice-modal has blue border (#4A9BD9)
- All modals use header margin "0 0 8px 0" (NOT 16px like header-style) — render custom h2, don't use modal-wrapper
- Use overlay-style and container-style from shared constants but render h2 with "0 0 8px 0" margin

### Changes to core.cljs:
- Delete selection-card-view (lines 421-442)
- Delete card-selection-modal (lines 445-569)
- Delete pile-choice-modal (lines 661-729)
- Delete graveyard-selection-modal (lines 732-804)
- Delete exile-cards-selection-modal (lines 807-876)
- Delete peek-selection-modal (lines 879-950)
- Update selection-modal router to use modals/ prefix for these 5 modals

### Verification:
- make fmt then make validate
- core.cljs should drop from 1278 to ~750 lines

## Success Criteria
- [ ] selection-card-view helper in modals.cljs
- [ ] 5 modals moved to modals.cljs, pixel-identical behavior
- [ ] core.cljs router updated to call modals/* for these 5
- [ ] Purple borders preserved on graveyard/exile/peek modals
- [ ] Blue borders preserved on card-selection/pile-choice modals
- [ ] make validate passes
close_reason: Migrated selection-card-view, card-selection-modal, pile-choice-modal, graveyard-selection-modal, exile-cards-selection-modal, and peek-selection-modal to modals.cljs. core.cljs reduced from 1278 to 836 lines (-442). All 741 tests pass, lint + format clean.

title: Extract selection/core.cljs with mechanism, multimethods, and data-driven validation
design:
## Goal

Create events/selection/core.cljs containing the selection mechanism: multimethod definitions, confirm-selection-impl wrapper, validate-selection, and cleanup helpers. Make validation and auto-confirm data-driven so core has zero knowledge of specific selection types.

This is the foundational file that all domain modules will register methods on. After this task, the multimethods and mechanism functions exist in core.cljs, but the original selection.cljs still contains everything else — subsequent tasks will extract domain code.

**CRITICAL BRIDGE CONSTRAINT**: Event handler REGISTRATIONS stay in selection.cljs during migration (keywords must not change). Core exports implementation functions only.

## Effort Estimate

4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

Key references:
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:1013-1025 — execute-confirmed-selection multimethod definition
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:1205-1241 — confirm-selection-impl wrapper
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:1248-1454 — event handlers (STAY in selection.cljs during bridge)
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:59-70 — remove-spell-stack-item
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:711-729 — cleanup-selection-source
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:1254-1257 — auto-confirm-types static set (to be replaced with :selection/auto-confirm? flag)
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:1312-1369 — confirm-selection validation (to be replaced with validate-selection)
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs:1268-1270 — pile-choice max-count type-specific logic (to be normalized)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**Data-driven validation tests** (new test file: test/fizzle/events/selection/core_test.cljs):
- test_exact_validation_passes_when_count_equals_select_count — catches regression if :exact logic wrong
- test_exact_validation_fails_when_count_differs — catches missing count check
- test_at_most_validation_passes_when_count_under_limit — catches regression for peek/graveyard types
- test_at_most_validation_fails_when_count_exceeds — catches missing upper-bound check
- test_at_least_one_validation_passes_with_one — catches regression for exile-cards
- test_at_least_one_validation_fails_when_empty — catches missing empty check
- test_always_validation_passes_with_empty — catches regression for scry/x-mana
- test_exact_or_zero_passes_with_zero — catches tutor fail-to-find regression
- test_exact_or_zero_passes_with_exact_count — catches tutor normal select regression
- test_exact_or_zero_fails_with_partial — catches missing partial-select rejection
- test_nil_validation_rejects — catches missing default-reject for unset builders (safety)
- test_candidate_membership_rejects_non_candidate — catches correctness regression where selected items not in candidate pool
- test_candidate_membership_skipped_when_no_candidates — catches false rejection for types without candidates (x-mana, scry)

**Auto-confirm tests** (same file):
- test_auto_confirm_flag_triggers_confirm — catches regression if auto-confirm logic broken
- test_no_auto_confirm_without_flag — catches false auto-confirm for types that shouldn't

**Bridge compatibility test**:
- test_existing_selection_events_still_dispatch — verifies ::selection/toggle-selection keyword still works

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] Create src/main/fizzle/events/selection/core.cljs with requires: engine/zones, engine/stack, engine/effects, engine/grants, db/queries, re-frame
- [ ] Move execute-confirmed-selection multimethod def to core.cljs (just the defmulti, NOT the defmethods)
- [ ] Define build-selection-for-effect multimethod (NEW):
      ```clojure
      (defmulti build-selection-for-effect
        "Build selection state for an effect requiring player interaction.
         Dispatches on effect type or :player-target for :any-player targeting."
        (fn [_db _player-id _object-id effect _remaining-effects]
          (if (= :any-player (:effect/target effect))
            :player-target
            (:effect/type effect))))
      ```
- [ ] Move confirm-selection-impl wrapper to core.cljs (keep public)
- [ ] Move remove-spell-stack-item to core.cljs
- [ ] Move cleanup-selection-source to core.cljs
- [ ] Create validate-selection function:
      ```clojure
      (defn validate-selection [selection]
        (let [selected (:selection/selected selection)
              count-selected (count selected)
              select-count (:selection/select-count selection 0)
              candidates (or (:selection/candidates selection)
                            (:selection/candidate-ids selection))
              ;; Universal candidate membership check
              candidates-valid? (if candidates
                                  (every? #(contains? candidates %) selected)
                                  true)
              ;; Count validation based on :selection/validation
              count-valid? (case (:selection/validation selection)
                            :exact (= count-selected select-count)
                            :at-most (<= count-selected select-count)
                            :at-least-one (pos? count-selected)
                            :always true
                            :exact-or-zero (or (zero? count-selected)
                                              (= count-selected select-count))
                            ;; nil or unknown: reject (safe default)
                            false)]
          (and candidates-valid? count-valid?)))
      ```
- [ ] Create toggle-selection-impl function (extracted logic from ::toggle-selection):
      - Use :selection/select-count universally (no pile-choice type check)
      - Use :selection/auto-confirm? flag instead of auto-confirm-types set
      - Preserve :selection/exact? false handling for unlimited select
      - Return updated app-db
- [ ] Create confirm-selection-handler function that calls validate-selection then confirm-selection-impl
- [ ] Update selection.cljs:
      - Add require: [fizzle.events.selection.core :as core]
      - Change all defmethod registrations to use core/execute-confirmed-selection
      - Replace ::toggle-selection handler body with call to (core/toggle-selection-impl db id)
      - Replace ::confirm-selection handler body with call to (core/confirm-selection-handler db)
      - Keep ALL rf/reg-event-db calls in selection.cljs (keywords must stay as :fizzle.events.selection/*)
      - Remove: auto-confirm-types def, inline validation logic, old confirm-selection-impl, remove-spell-stack-item, cleanup-selection-source
- [ ] Update ALL existing builders in selection.cljs to set new data-driven fields:
      - :selection/auto-confirm? true/false
      - :selection/validation (:exact, :at-most, :at-least-one, :always, :exact-or-zero)
      - :selection/select-count normalized (pile-choice: set to hand-count value)
- [ ] Ensure all existing tests pass with the bridge in place
- [ ] Run make validate

### 4. Bridge strategy (REVISED)

During migration, selection.cljs keeps ALL event registrations (preserving keyword namespaces). Event handlers become thin wrappers calling core functions:

```clojure
;; In selection.cljs (bridge phase):
(require '[fizzle.events.selection.core :as core])

;; Event registrations STAY HERE — keywords unchanged
(rf/reg-event-db ::toggle-selection
  (fn [db [_ id]] (core/toggle-selection-impl db id)))

(rf/reg-event-db ::confirm-selection
  (fn [db _] (core/confirm-selection-handler db)))

;; Defmethod registrations change target:
(defmethod core/execute-confirmed-selection :discard ...)
```

Event registrations move to core.cljs ONLY in the final import-update task when all keyword references are updated.

**Builder backward compat**: All builders in selection.cljs must be updated IN THIS TASK to set :selection/validation and :selection/auto-confirm? — the new validate-selection requires these fields (nil → reject). This is safe because we're editing the same file that defines the builders.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] events/selection/core.cljs exists with: both multimethods, confirm-selection-impl, toggle-selection-impl, confirm-selection-handler, validate-selection, cleanup helpers
- [ ] validate-selection handles all 5 validation types + nil→reject + candidate membership check
- [ ] toggle-selection-impl uses :selection/auto-confirm? flag (no static type set)
- [ ] toggle-selection-impl uses :selection/select-count universally (no pile-choice type check)
- [ ] confirm-selection-handler uses validate-selection (no type-specific branches)
- [ ] selection.cljs requires core.cljs, all defmethods register on core/execute-confirmed-selection
- [ ] ALL event keywords remain as :fizzle.events.selection/* (bridge preserves keywords)
- [ ] ALL existing builders updated with :selection/validation and :selection/auto-confirm? fields
- [ ] All existing ~1265 tests pass (behavior-preserving)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] core.cljs has zero type-specific logic (no cond/case on :selection/type)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Candidate Membership Regression**
Current confirm-selection checks every? in candidates for tutor, peek, pile-choice, graveyard-return. The new validate-selection MUST include universal candidate membership check when :selection/candidates or :selection/candidate-ids is present. Without this, users could confirm selections with invalid items.

**Edge Case: Pile-choice max-count Normalization**
Toggle-selection line 1268-1270 has: (if (= type :pile-choice) hand-count select-count). This is type-specific. Fix: pile-choice builder must set :selection/select-count to hand-count value. Core reads :selection/select-count universally. Builder can also store :selection/hand-count separately for the execution defmethod to use.

**Edge Case: nil :selection/validation**
If a builder forgets to set :selection/validation, validate-selection returns false (safe reject). This prevents accidentally allowing unvalidated selections. All builders MUST be updated in this task.

**Edge Case: :selection/exact? false (unlimited select)**
Toggle-selection line 1290-1294 checks (false? (:selection/exact? selection)) for exile-cards unlimited add. This is already data-driven and must be preserved in toggle-selection-impl.

**Edge Case: Event keyword preservation**
:fizzle.events.selection/toggle-selection MUST remain the dispatched keyword. If core.cljs registers with ::toggle-selection, the keyword becomes :fizzle.events.selection.core/toggle-selection — breaking all views and tests. Bridge keeps registrations in selection.cljs.

**Reference implementation for bridge pattern:**
game.cljs already does this: bare requires for abilities.cljs ensure namespace loading. The same pattern applies — domain files loaded via bare requires, event registrations stay in the entry-point namespace during migration.

## Anti-Patterns

- ❌ NO type-specific logic in core.cljs (no cond/case on :selection/type; if core knows about :tutor or :scry, the separation failed)
- ❌ NO event registrations in core.cljs during bridge phase (would change keyword namespaces, breaking dispatchers)
- ❌ NO validate-selection without candidate membership check (correctness regression for tutor, peek, pile-choice, graveyard-return)
- ❌ NO ignoring nil :selection/validation (must reject as safe default)
- ❌ NO pile-choice special-casing in toggle handler (builders must normalize select-count)
- ❌ NO forward declarations
close_reason: Extracted core.cljs with multimethods, data-driven validation (5 types), auto-confirm flag, cleanup helpers. Bridge in place. All 1266 tests pass, make validate green.

title: Task 3: Add trigger descriptions to iggy_pop.cljs

## Effort Estimate

Completed fizzle-fog: Updated stack-item-view to display card name + description.

The pipeline is complete. Now we need to add actual descriptions to the trigger data.

## Implementation

### 1. Identify all triggers in iggy_pop.cljs

### 2. Add descriptions
For City of Brass trigger:
```clojure
{:trigger/type :becomes-tapped
 :trigger/description "deals 1 damage to you"
 :trigger/effects [{:effect/type :deal-damage
                    :effect/amount 1
                    :effect/target :controller}]}
```

### 3. Run automated tests

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Description reads naturally when combined with card name
- [ ] make test passes
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] Manual test confirms display: 'Trigger: City of Brass deals 1 damage to you'

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Description Wording:**
- Description completes the sentence "Trigger: [Card Name] [description]"
- Should read naturally: "City of Brass deals 1 damage to you"
- Use lowercase start (card name provides context)
- Match MTG card text style where appropriate

**Future Triggers:**
- When adding new cards with triggers, follow same pattern
- Each trigger definition needs :trigger/description field
- Backwards compatible: triggers without description fall back to type name

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO auto-generating descriptions from effects (per user decision)
- ❌ NO changing description format (must work with existing display code)
- ❌ NO adding descriptions to cards outside iggy_pop.cljs scope

title: Epic: Fill test coverage gaps (queries, cards, regressions)
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Dedicated test file for all 19 public query functions in db/queries.cljs
- Unit test for remove-object in engine/zones.cljs
- Card integration tests (cast-resolve cycle) for 7 untested cards: brain-freeze, dark-ritual, cabal-ritual, city-of-brass, gemstone-mine, underground-river, ill-gotten-gains
- Regression tests for bug fixes from commits 46a281f (selection clearing, storm copies, sorcery speed) and earlier peek-and-select fixes
- Subscription testing explicitly deferred (out of scope)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] queries_test.cljs exists covering all 19 public functions with positive and negative cases
- [ ] Brain Freeze storm integration test verifies mill + storm copies
- [ ] Cabal Ritual threshold integration test verifies conditional effects
- [ ] City of Brass trigger integration test verifies damage-on-tap
- [ ] remove-object unit test verifies entity removal and no-op on missing ID
- [ ] Regression test: storm copies resolve correctly (not infinite loop)
- [ ] Regression test: sorcery-speed enforcement during non-main phases
- [ ] All 742+ existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO mocking Datascript (reason: queries are pure functions against real DB - mocking defeats purpose)
- NO test-only methods in production code (reason: tests should exercise public API only)
- NO subscription tests in this epic (reason: deferred by design - subs are thin wrappers over queries)
- NO duplicating test helpers across files (reason: extract shared helpers to a test-helpers namespace if needed)
- NO testing cards that are already well-covered as fixtures (reason: Dark Ritual is used in rules_test but needs its OWN integration test verifying BBB mana production)
- NO skipping edge cases for queries (reason: nil inputs, missing players, empty zones must be tested)

## Approach
Bottom-up by layer: start with query function tests (pure Datascript, simplest), then remove-object, then card integration tests, then regression tests. Each layer validates the foundation for the next.

Test pattern follows existing codebase convention: create Datascript DB directly, transact test state, call functions, assert results. No re-frame involvement. Card tests use cast-spell + resolve-spell cycle.

## Architecture
- src/test/fizzle/db/queries_test.cljs - NEW: comprehensive query function tests
- src/test/fizzle/engine/zones_test.cljs - EXTEND: add remove-object tests
- src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs - NEW: storm win condition
- src/test/fizzle/cards/dark_ritual_test.cljs - NEW: BBB mana production
- src/test/fizzle/cards/cabal_ritual_test.cljs - NEW: threshold conditional effects
- src/test/fizzle/cards/city_of_brass_test.cljs - NEW: tap trigger damage
- src/test/fizzle/cards/gemstone_mine_test.cljs - NEW: counter depletion
- src/test/fizzle/cards/underground_river_test.cljs - NEW: pain land
- src/test/fizzle/cards/ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs - NEW: symmetric effect
- src/test/fizzle/events/regression_test.cljs - NEW or extend existing: regression tests

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Significant test coverage gaps exist in foundational layers. 19 query functions have 0 dedicated tests. 7 of 24 cards lack any test coverage, including Brain Freeze (the deck's win condition). Recent bug fixes went in without regression tests. This blocks the P1 stack unification feature (fizzle-d3i) which needs a solid test foundation.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- db/queries.cljs:1-269 - 19 public query functions, all pure (db, args) -> result
- engine/zones.cljs:78-90 - remove-object, retracts entity from datascript
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - 24 card defs, 17 have tests, 7 don't
- Test pattern: create-test-db helper, d/create-conn + transact, direct function calls
- subs/game.cljs - 15+ subscriptions, thin wrappers over queries (deferred)

**Test infrastructure:**
- cljs.test framework with deftest/testing/is macros
- Datascript for in-memory DB
- No re-frame test utilities currently
- Test helpers duplicated across card test files (create-test-db, add-card-to-zone, etc.)

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Bottom-up by layer ✓
**What it is:** Test queries first (foundation), then zones/remove-object, then cards, then regressions.
**Investigation:** Reviewed existing test patterns - all use pure Datascript. Query tests will be simplest (pure functions, no game state setup needed beyond basic player/objects).
**Pros:** Validates foundation first. Simplest tests first establishes patterns. Card tests further validate queries.
**Cons:** Defers highest-risk items (Brain Freeze, storm) to later tasks.
**Chosen because:** Queries are the foundation everything builds on. Testing them first catches data layer bugs before they confuse card/regression tests.

#### 2. Risk-priority ordering ❌
**What it is:** Start with Brain Freeze + storm regressions (highest impact), then backfill.
**Why we looked at this:** Brain Freeze is the win condition - untested win condition is highest risk.
**Investigation:** Storm tests require cast-resolve cycle + storm trigger dispatch - most complex test setup.
**Pros:** Covers highest risk first.
**Cons:** Jumps into complex integration tests. May discover query bugs during card tests.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Starting with complex tests means debugging both the test setup AND potential query bugs simultaneously.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Query tests reveal no issues and we want to reprioritize.

#### 3. Feature-area grouping ❌
**What it is:** Group by subsystem (mana, storm, lands) covering queries+cards+regressions per area.
**Why we looked at this:** Natural mental model, complete coverage per area.
**Investigation:** Queries like get-player-eid, get-object, get-game-state don't map to one area.
**Pros:** Complete per-area coverage.
**Cons:** Mixes complexity levels. General-purpose queries don't fit.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Poor fit for cross-cutting query functions. Harder to track progress.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We reorganize queries by domain.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- All 19 public query functions in db/queries.cljs
- remove-object in engine/zones.cljs
- 7 untested cards (integration tests)
- Regression tests for recent bug fix commits

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Subscription testing (deferred - thin wrappers over queries, low risk)
- Card data validation tests (card defs are static EDN, low value)
- Performance testing of queries (premature)
- Testing private functions like matches-criteria? directly (test through public API)

### Open Questions
- Should test helpers be extracted to a shared namespace? (decide during query test implementation)
- How to test ill-gotten-gains symmetric effects without opponent bot? (decide during card test task)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Include subscription testing? | Defer (recommended) | Focus on queries, cards, regressions only |
| Card test depth? | Integration tests only | Cast-resolve cycle, no card data validation |
| Regression test scope? | Cover all recent fixes | Include selection clearing, storm copies, sorcery speed |
| Approach? | Bottom-up by layer | Queries -> remove-object -> cards -> regressions |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Query Function Coverage
**Question explored:** What query functions exist and how are they tested?
**Sources consulted:**
- db/queries.cljs - 19 public functions
- db/schema_test.cljs - only tests schema, not queries
- engine/rules_test.cljs - uses queries as fixtures but doesn't test them directly

**Findings:**
- All query functions are pure (db, args) -> result
- Many return nil for missing entities - edge case coverage needed
- stack-empty? composes get-stack-items + get-objects-in-zone
- matches-criteria? is private, tested through query-zone-by-criteria

**Conclusion:** Dedicated queries_test.cljs with positive, negative, and nil-input cases

#### Card Test Patterns
**Question explored:** How do existing card tests work?
**Sources consulted:**
- cards/led_test.cljs - exemplar: create-test-db, add-card-to-zone, activate-mana-ability
- cards/careful_study_test.cljs - cast-resolve with selection effects
- engine/rules_test.cljs - simpler cast-resolve without selection

**Findings:**
- Test helpers duplicated across files (create-test-db in led_test, rules_test, etc.)
- Card tests use full cast-resolve cycle through rules/cast-spell + rules/resolve-spell
- Selection-based cards (Careful Study) test through events/selection dispatch
- Simple effect cards (Dark Ritual) can test purely through rules layer

**Conclusion:** Follow existing patterns. Consider helper extraction if duplication becomes excessive.

### Dead-End Paths
None - straightforward coverage gap filling.

### Open Concerns Raised
- 'Will stack unification rewrite invalidate regression tests?' → Regression tests validate current behavior. Stack unification should preserve behavior, so tests remain valid as contracts.
- 'Are card tests redundant with rules_test coverage?' → No - rules_test uses Dark Ritual as fixture but doesn't test its specific effects (BBB production). Dedicated tests verify card-specific behavior.
close_reason: Epic complete. All 4 child tasks closed. 85 new tests added across 7 new test files. All 7 untested cards now covered (Dark Ritual, Cabal Ritual, Brain Freeze, City of Brass, Gemstone Mine, Underground River, Ill-Gotten Gains). All 19 query functions tested. remove-object tested. Storm copy and sorcery-speed regressions covered. make validate: 826 tests, 1826 assertions, 0 failures.

title: Feature: Implement Opt with Scry Effect
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Scry effect type exists and works for any amount N
- Scry N reveals top N cards, player assigns each to top or bottom pile
- Click order determines arrangement order within each pile
- Opt card defined: 1 blue instant, Scry 1 then draw 1
- Scry handles edge cases: fewer cards than N, empty library

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] :scry effect type implemented in effects.cljs
- [ ] Selection system supports :selection/type :scry
- [ ] Scry UI shows all cards with top/bottom buttons
- [ ] Click order determines final arrangement
- [ ] Opt card casts and resolves correctly (scry 1, draw 1)
- [ ] Edge cases handled (library < N cards)
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (make validate)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-scry without player choice (defeats purpose of scry)
- ❌ NO graveyard interaction for scry (scry never touches graveyard)
- ❌ NO hardcoding scry 1 only (must work for scry N)
- ❌ NO blocking confirm until cards assigned (UX: confirm disabled until all assigned)
- ❌ NO skipping edge case tests (library size edge cases are common in storm)

## Approach
Add new :scry effect type following existing selection pattern (like :discard with :effect/selection :player). The effect pauses execution, creates :selection/type :scry state with revealed cards. UI renders scry modal showing all N cards with top/bottom buttons. Player clicks assign cards to piles - click order is arrangement order. On confirm, library is reordered and remaining effects execute. Opt card uses this effect followed by :draw.

## Architecture
- engine/effects.cljs - Add :scry effect type (returns db unchanged, like selection effects)
- events/game.cljs - Add :scry to selection detection, create scry selection state
- events/game.cljs - Add scry confirmation handler to reorder library
- views/ - Add scry modal component (cards + top/bottom buttons + confirm)
- cards/opt.cljs - Opt card definition
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Add opt to all-cards

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Opt is a common cantrip in Premodern storm decks. It requires scry which doesn't exist in the engine yet. Scry is also needed for future cards (Preordain, Serum Visions, etc).

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- effects.cljs:308-329 - Selection pattern for player choice effects
- events/game.cljs:750-770 - has-selection-effect?, get-first-selection-index
- events/game.cljs:977-983 - Selection state creation for discard
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Card definition patterns, all-cards vector

**External:**
- MTG Scry rules: Look at top N, put any number on bottom in any order, rest on top in any order

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. New :scry effect type with selection ✓
**What it is:** Add scry as first-class effect type using existing selection system pattern.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed discard selection in effects.cljs:308-329
- Reviewed selection state handling in game.cljs:977-983
- Pattern is proven and extensible

**Pros:**
- Matches existing codebase patterns
- Reusable for all scry cards
- Clean separation of effect/selection/UI

**Cons:**
- Requires changes to multiple files

**Chosen because:** Follows established patterns, future-proof for other scry cards

#### 2. Composite effect using existing primitives ❌
**What it is:** Hack scry using mill + return-from-graveyard

**Why we looked at this:** Avoid adding new effect type

**Investigation:**
- Mill moves to graveyard, triggers graveyard effects
- Return-from-graveyard would fire triggers incorrectly

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Incorrect game semantics - scry never touches graveyard

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Complete rewrite of zone system

#### 3. Simplified always-bottom scry ❌
**What it is:** Auto-bottom without player choice

**Why we looked at this:** Faster to implement

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Defeats strategic purpose of scry in storm deck practice

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** User explicitly requests simplified mode

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Scry effect for any N
- Scry UI with full arrangement control
- Opt card implementation

**Out of scope:**
- Other scry cards (Preordain, etc) - separate cards, same effect
- Scry triggers (no cards in Premodern trigger on scry)

### Open Questions
- None - design is complete

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| How to handle missing scry? | Implement full scry effect | New effect type needed |
| Scry 1 UI? | Reveal card, choose top/bottom | Simple two-button UI |
| Scry N UI? | Show all cards, top/bottom buttons each, click order = arrangement | Full control, order matters |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Selection System Pattern
**Question explored:** How does player choice work in effects?
**Sources consulted:**
- effects.cljs:308-329 - discard with :effect/selection :player
- game.cljs:750-770 - selection detection functions
- game.cljs:977-983 - selection state creation

**Findings:**
- Effects return db unchanged when selection needed
- Selection state tracks type, player, and parameters
- UI handles actual selection, confirms via event

**Conclusion:** Scry follows same pattern - effect triggers selection state, UI handles choice, confirmation event reorders library

### Dead-End Paths

#### Mill-based scry
**Why explored:** Reuse existing effects
**Investigation:** Mill puts cards in graveyard, would trigger graveyard effects incorrectly
**Why abandoned:** Wrong game semantics

### Open Concerns Raised
- Multi-card scry arrangement → Solved: click order determines pile order
close_reason: Closed

title: Add re-frame events and subscriptions for history navigation
design:
## Goal
Wire up history/core.cljs pure functions as re-frame events and expose history state via subscriptions. This is the data layer that views will subscribe to.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create history/events.cljs (NOT events/history.cljs)

**CRITICAL**: File MUST be at `src/main/fizzle/history/events.cljs` with namespace `fizzle.history.events`.
The interceptor at `history/interceptor.cljs:10-21` skips events whose keyword namespace starts with `"fizzle.history"`.
If placed in `events/history.cljs` (namespace `fizzle.events.history`), navigation events would be RECORDED as history entries, corrupting the action log.

New re-frame events that wrap history/core.cljs operations:

- `::step-back` — Compute `(dec position)`, call `history/step-to` with that value. step-to already restores :game/db (see line 74).
- `::step-forward` — Compute `(inc position)`, call `history/step-to`. Same — step-to handles :game/db.
- `::switch-branch` — Takes fork-id arg, calls `history/switch-branch`. switch-branch already restores :game/db at tip (see line 126).

**Key insight**: Both `step-to` and `switch-branch` return a modified db that includes updated `:game/db`. Events just need to:
1. Extract history state from app-db (the 4 history keys are stored flat at top-level: `:history/main`, `:history/forks`, `:history/current-branch`, `:history/position`)
2. Call the pure function (which also reads/writes `:game/db` from the same map)
3. Merge result back to app-db

Since app-db IS the map that history functions operate on (history keys + :game/db are all top-level), event handlers can pass the full app-db directly to history functions.

**Pattern to follow**: See events/game.cljs line 1-22 for require/ns pattern. Use `rf/reg-event-db` for all three events.

**Guards**: step-back should check `(history/can-step-back? db)` before calling step-to. step-forward should check `(history/can-step-forward? db)`. If guard fails, return db unchanged.

### 2. Create subs/history.cljs

Subscriptions exposing history state to views.

**Pattern to follow**: See subs/game.cljs:10-11 for Layer 2 (raw extraction) and lines 16-20 for Layer 3 (derived with :\<-).

Layer 2 (raw access from app-db):
- `::entries` — `(fn [db _] (history/effective-entries db))` — calls history/core pure fn
- `::position` — `(fn [db _] (:history/position db))`
- `::current-branch` — `(fn [db _] (:history/current-branch db))`
- `::forks` — `(fn [db _] (history/list-forks db))`

Layer 3 (derived):
- `::can-step-back?` — `:<- [::position]` — `(fn [pos _] (and pos (> pos 0)))`
- `::can-step-forward?` — `:<- [::entries] :<- [::position]` — `(fn [[entries pos] _] (and pos entries (< pos (dec (count entries)))))`
- `::at-tip?` — `:<- [::entries] :<- [::position]` — `(fn [[entries pos] _] (or (and (= pos -1) (empty? entries)) (= pos (dec (count entries)))))`
- `::entry-count` — `:<- [::entries]` — `(fn [entries _] (count (or entries [])))`

**Edge case**: Before game init, app-db has no history keys. Subscriptions must handle nil gracefully — return nil, false, 0, or empty as appropriate for each sub.

### 3. Register in core.cljs

Add bare require `[fizzle.history.events]` in core.cljs ns form (follow pattern of existing bare require `[fizzle.events.abilities]` at line 19). Also add `[fizzle.subs.history]` require.

### 4. Write tests

File: `src/test/fizzle/history/events_test.cljs`

**Event tests** (use day9/re-frame-test or manual app-db manipulation):

Test setup helper: Create an app-db with history containing N entries at various positions. Use `history/make-entry` and `history/append-entry` to build state (see interceptor_test.cljs:91-97 for pattern).

Specific tests:
1. `test-step-back-decrements-position` — From position 2 with 3 entries, step-back goes to position 1 and :game/db equals entry-1 snapshot. Catches: position not updated.
2. `test-step-back-restores-snapshot` — From position 2, step-back restores :game/db to the snapshot stored in entry at position 1. Catches: snapshot not applied.
3. `test-step-back-at-zero-is-noop` — From position 0, step-back returns db unchanged (position still 0, :game/db unchanged). Catches: boundary underflow.
4. `test-step-forward-increments-position` — From position 1 with 3 entries, step-forward goes to position 2 and :game/db equals entry-2 snapshot. Catches: position not updated.
5. `test-step-forward-at-tip-is-noop` — From position 2 (tip of 3 entries), step-forward returns db unchanged. Catches: boundary overflow.
6. `test-switch-branch-changes-branch-and-restores` — Create a fork, switch to it, verify :history/current-branch is fork-id and :game/db is tip snapshot of fork. Catches: branch not switched or wrong snapshot.
7. `test-switch-branch-to-main` — From a fork, switch to nil (main), verify :history/current-branch is nil and :game/db is main tip snapshot. Catches: can't return to main.
8. `test-switch-branch-invalid-fork-id-is-noop` — Switch to non-existent fork-id, verify db unchanged. Catches: crash on bad input.

File: `src/test/fizzle/subs/history_test.cljs`

**Subscription tests**:
9. `test-entries-returns-effective-entries` — Verify ::entries returns entries for current branch. Catches: wrong entries returned.
10. `test-position-returns-current-position` — Verify ::position extracts position. Catches: wrong key read.
11. `test-can-step-back-false-at-zero` — Position 0, verify false. Catches: boundary bug.
12. `test-can-step-back-true-at-nonzero` — Position 2, verify true. Catches: always-false bug.
13. `test-can-step-forward-false-at-tip` — Position at tip, verify false. Catches: boundary bug.
14. `test-can-step-forward-true-when-not-at-tip` — Position 1 of 3, verify true. Catches: always-false bug.
15. `test-forks-returns-all-forks` — Create 2 forks, verify ::forks returns both. Catches: missing forks.
16. `test-subscriptions-handle-nil-history` — Empty app-db (no history keys), verify subscriptions return nil/false/0 without throwing. Catches: NPE before game init.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] history/events.cljs exists at src/main/fizzle/history/events.cljs (namespace fizzle.history.events)
- [ ] Events: step-back, step-forward, switch-branch registered as rf/reg-event-db
- [ ] Events use guard checks (can-step-back?, can-step-forward?) before calling history functions
- [ ] subs/history.cljs has entries, position, current-branch, forks, can-step-back?, can-step-forward?, at-tip?, entry-count subscriptions
- [ ] Subscriptions handle nil/missing history state gracefully (no throws before game init)
- [ ] Events correctly restore :game/db snapshot when navigating (via step-to/switch-branch return value)
- [ ] Navigation events are NOT recorded by the interceptor (verify keyword namespace starts with fizzle.history)
- [ ] 16 tests covering all events and subscriptions including boundary cases
- [ ] Namespaces registered in core.cljs (bare requires)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO placing events in fizzle.events.history namespace (MUST be fizzle.history.events — interceptor skip depends on this)
- NO manual :game/db restoration in events (step-to and switch-branch already handle this)
- NO history logic in events (delegate entirely to history/core.cljs pure functions)
- NO ignoring nil history state in subscriptions (must handle pre-game-init gracefully)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**CRITICAL: Namespace placement**
The interceptor at history/interceptor.cljs:10-21 uses `str/starts-with? "fizzle.history"` to detect history events. Events MUST be in namespace `fizzle.history.events` so keywords like `:fizzle.history.events/step-back` are correctly skipped. Wrong namespace = navigation pollutes history = broken feature.

**Edge Case: Empty history (position -1)**
Before any game actions, position is -1 and entries is []. Both step-back and step-forward should be no-ops. Guard with can-step-back?/can-step-forward? which handle this (can-step-back? checks > 0, can-step-forward? checks < dec(count)).

**Edge Case: Pre-game-init subscriptions**
Before ::init-game fires, app-db has no history keys. Subscriptions reading :history/position get nil. Derived subs must handle nil gracefully — e.g., `(and pos (> pos 0))` not just `(> pos 0)`.

**Design: Events pass full app-db to history functions**
History functions expect a map with :history/* keys and :game/db. Since app-db IS that map, events can pass db directly to history/step-to etc. No extraction/re-assembly needed.

## Reference Implementations
- Event registration pattern: events/game.cljs:1-22
- Subscription pattern: subs/game.cljs:10-20 (Layer 2 raw, Layer 3 derived)
- History test setup: history/interceptor_test.cljs:28-31 (make-db-with-history), lines 91-97 (building multi-entry state)
- Guard functions: history/core.cljs:78-85 (can-step-back?, can-step-forward?)
close_reason: All 3 files created (history/events.cljs, subs/history.cljs, core.cljs updated). 16 tests pass. make validate clean.

title: Gemstone Mine counter tracking and sacrifice
design:
## Goal
Gemstone Mine enters with mining counters and sacrifices when depleted.

## Context
Epic success criterion: 'Gemstone Mine tracks counters and sacrifices at 0'
Card text: 'Gemstone Mine enters with three mining counters. T, Remove a mining counter: Add one mana of any color. If there are no mining counters on Gemstone Mine, sacrifice it.'
Schema has :object/counters field but no counter system implemented.

## Investigation Needed
- Review :object/counters schema field
- Check Gemstone Mine card definition
- Review how permanents enter battlefield (for counter initialization)
- Check if sacrifice mechanic exists

## Implementation Steps
1. RED: Write test that Gemstone Mine enters battlefield with 3 counters
2. GREEN: Add counter initialization on ETB
3. RED: Write test that tapping removes a counter and adds mana
4. GREEN: Integrate counter removal with tap ability
5. RED: Write test that Gemstone Mine sacrifices at 0 counters
6. GREEN: Implement sacrifice trigger when counters depleted
7. VERIFY: All tests pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Gemstone Mine enters with 3 mining counters
- [ ] Each tap removes 1 counter and adds mana
- [ ] Counters tracked on the permanent
- [ ] Land sacrificed when counters reach 0
- [ ] Can tap 3 times total before sacrifice
