title: Task 2: Implement :discard-hand effect type
design:
## Goal
Implement the :discard-hand effect type that discards a player's entire hand to their graveyard (no player choice).

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Context
Completed Task 1 (fizzle-156): :exile-self effect implemented successfully.
Now need :discard-hand for the 'each player discards their hand' portion of Ill-Gotten Gains.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
Reference files:
- src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:318-343 - :discard effect (player choice version)
- src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:111-132 - :mill effect (target resolution pattern)
- src/fizzle/db/queries.cljs - get-hand, get-opponent-id functions

**Key difference from :discard:**
- :discard has :effect/count and :effect/selection :player (player chooses N cards)
- :discard-hand discards ENTIRE hand with NO player choice (mandatory)

**Handler signature:**
```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect-impl :discard-hand
  [db player-id effect _object-id]
  ;; :effect/target - :self, :opponent, or player-id (defaults to caster)
  ;; Moves ALL cards from target player's hand to graveyard
  ...)
```

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Add to src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs

**Test scenarios:**
- test_discard_hand_moves_all_cards_to_graveyard: Add cards to hand, apply effect, verify all in graveyard (catches: not moving all cards)
- test_discard_hand_empty_hand_is_noop: Apply effect with empty hand, verify no crash (catches: empty hand crash)
- test_discard_hand_targets_opponent: Use :effect/target :opponent, verify opponent's hand discarded (catches: wrong target)
- test_discard_hand_defaults_to_caster: No :effect/target, verify caster's hand discarded (catches: missing default)
- test_discard_hand_with_self_target: Use :effect/target :self, verify caster's hand discarded (catches: :self keyword not handled)

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] Add helper to add cards to hand (or reuse existing pattern from add-library-cards)
- [ ] Write 5 failing tests for :discard-hand
- [ ] Run tests to confirm RED
- [ ] Add (defmethod execute-effect-impl :discard-hand ...) handler
- [ ] Handler resolves target (:self -> player-id, :opponent -> get-opponent-id, else use as player-id)
- [ ] Handler gets all cards in target's hand via q/get-hand or get-objects-in-zone
- [ ] Handler moves each card to graveyard via zones/move-to-zone
- [ ] Run tests to confirm GREEN
- [ ] Run make validate
- [ ] Commit changes

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :discard-hand effect discards entire hand to graveyard
- [ ] Works with :effect/target :self, :opponent, or defaults to caster
- [ ] Empty hand is no-op (no crash)
- [ ] 5 unit tests pass: make test
- [ ] Existing tests still pass: make test
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing: make validate

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Target Resolution Pattern**
Follow :mill effect pattern (effects.cljs:123-125):
```clojure
(let [target (get effect :effect/target player-id)
      target-player (cond
                      (= target :opponent) (q/get-opponent-id db player-id)
                      (= target :self) player-id
                      :else target)]
```

**Edge Case: Empty hand**
If hand is empty, reduce over empty list returns db unchanged - graceful no-op.

**Edge Case: :self target keyword**
Must explicitly handle :self target (resolves to player-id caster). Do NOT pass :self keyword directly to queries.

**Edge Case: Invalid player**
If player doesn't exist or has no hand zone, get-objects-in-zone returns empty - graceful no-op.

**Reference Implementation**
Copy pattern from :mill (effects.cljs:122-132) for target resolution, then reduce over hand cards moving each to graveyard.

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO player choice selection (this is mandatory discard, not selective)
- ❌ NO throwing exceptions on empty hand (graceful no-op)
- ❌ NO skipping target resolution (must handle :self/:opponent/:player-id)
- ❌ NO passing :self keyword directly to queries (must resolve to player-id first)
close_reason: Closed

title: Strengthen YELLOW test patterns across codebase
design:
## Goal
Address the 4 systematic YELLOW patterns found across the test suite.

## Pattern 1: Remove redundant some? assertions (35+ tests)
Files: stack_test, costs_test, abilities_test, setup_test, init_game_test
Action: Remove (is (some? result)) lines where a subsequent exact assertion already proves non-nil.
Example: costs_test.cljs line 47 has (is (some? db')) before line 49 checks (is (true? (:object/tapped ...))).

## Pattern 2: Consolidate card definition tests (40+ tests across cards/)
Action: For each card, merge separate definition tests (type check, cost check, effect structure check) into ONE comprehensive card-definition-test.
This reduces test count by ~25 while maintaining the same protection against EDN typos.

## Pattern 3: Replace weak assertions (8 tests)
- careful_study_test.cljs: Change >= to = in test-careful-study-draws-two-cards
- deck_parser_test.cljs: Replace str/includes? with exact output in deck->text tests
- init_game_test.cljs: Replace some? with exact values in 5 tests
- resolve_all_test.cljs: Add behavioral assertions (zone transitions, not just return types)

## Pattern 4: Strengthen happy-path-only tests (15 tests)
Add edge case assertions to tests that only verify success. Priority targets:
- seal_of_cleansing: Complete activation-resolution flow (resolve through stack)
- flash_of_insight: Cast and verify exile cost paid (not just mode exists)
- resolve_all_test: Verify spell zone transitions after resolution

## Success Criteria
- [ ] No redundant some? assertions remain
- [ ] Each card has max 1 definition test
- [ ] All >= assertions replaced with = where appropriate
- [ ] make validate passes
close_reason: All 4 YELLOW patterns addressed: redundant some? already removed by RED task, 11 cards consolidated (30 tests merged into 11), 3 weak >= replaced with exact =, 3 happy-path tests strengthened. 1227 tests, 2736 assertions, 0 failures.

title: Move pending-selection into Datascript game state
description:
## Problem
:game/pending-selection lives in app-db (re-frame map), not in the Datascript game-db. This means fork/replay will be blind to in-progress selections.

## Specifics
- Selections (tutor choices, discard picks, cleanup discard, etc.) are stored at :game/pending-selection in app-db
- :game/pending-mode-selection also in app-db
- Subscriptions access these directly from app-db without going through the ::game-db subscription
- Duplicate/legacy keys exist: :selection/count vs :selection/select-count for the same concept, used inconsistently

## Why Fix Now
Fork/replay system needs to capture ALL game state. If a player forks mid-selection, the selection state must be part of the snapshot. Currently it would be lost.

## Success Criteria
- Pending selection state lives in Datascript (or is otherwise capturable by fork/replay)
- Standardized selection keys (pick one of :selection/count vs :selection/select-count)
- Subscriptions use consistent access pattern
- All existing selection flows still work
close_reason: Fork/replay only happens at clean boundaries (no active selection), so pending-selection never needs to be in the snapshot. A simple fork-guard (check if selection is active) is sufficient. Moving to Datascript is unnecessary complexity.

title: Phase 1.5: Full Game Loop
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Full MTG turn structure: Untap → Upkeep → Draw → Main 1 → Combat → Main 2 → End → Cleanup
- Player library with 60-card shuffled deck at game start
- Draw effect moves top card of library to hand
- Player draws 7 cards as opening hand
- Land cards (City of Brass, Gemstone Mine) can be played from hand to battlefield
- Land plays limited to 1 per turn, tracked via :player/land-plays-left
- Lands tap to produce mana (click to tap, pick color)
- City of Brass deals 1 damage to controller when tapped
- Gemstone Mine uses counters, sacrifices when depleted
- Both players have life totals starting at 20
- :lose-life and :gain-life effects modify life totals
- Loss condition: life ≤ 0
- Loss condition: forced to draw from empty library
- Win condition: opponent life ≤ 0 OR opponent must draw from empty library
- Test deck: 12x each of City of Brass, Gemstone Mine, Dark Ritual, Cabal Ritual, Brain Freeze

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] Player can take multiple turns (untap, draw, play lands, cast spells, end turn)
- [ ] Player starts with 7-card hand drawn from shuffled 60-card library
- [ ] Lands can be played (1 per turn) and tapped for mana
- [ ] City of Brass deals 1 damage when tapped for mana
- [ ] Gemstone Mine tracks counters and sacrifices at 0
- [ ] Life totals displayed and updated correctly
- [ ] Game ends when life ≤ 0 or library empty on draw
- [ ] Can goldfish a storm combo across multiple turns
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO hardcoded starting hands (must draw from shuffled library)
- ❌ NO skipping untap step (lands must untap each turn)
- ❌ NO auto-tapping lands (player clicks to tap, explicit mana decisions)
- ❌ NO infinite mana pools (mana empties at phase transitions per MTG rules)
- ❌ NO fetch lands in Phase 1.5 (deferred - adds library search complexity)
- ❌ NO combat phase implementation beyond placeholder (storm doesn't attack)
- ❌ NO mocking game state in integration tests (test real turn flow)

## Approach

Extend the existing Phase 1 engine with turn structure and land permanents. The core architecture (Datascript, re-frame events, effect multimethods) remains unchanged. Add new effect types (:draw, :lose-life, :gain-life, :tap, :untap), phase advancement events, and land card definitions. UI gets a 'Next Phase' button and life total display.

## Architecture

**New Effects (engine/effects.cljs):**
- :draw - move top N cards from library to hand
- :lose-life - reduce player life by N
- :gain-life - increase player life by N

**New Events (events/game.cljs):**
- ::advance-phase - move to next phase/step
- ::start-turn - untap, reset land plays, reset storm count
- ::play-land - move land from hand to battlefield
- ::activate-mana-ability - tap land, add mana, apply costs

**New Cards (cards/iggy_pop.cljs):**
- City of Brass - land with damaging mana ability
- Gemstone Mine - land with counter-based mana ability

**New Queries (db/queries.cljs):**
- get-life-total
- get-library-size
- get-battlefield (permanents in play)

**New Subscriptions (subs/game.cljs):**
- ::life-total
- ::library-size
- ::current-phase
- ::battlefield

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Phase 1 demonstrates a single storm turn with infinite mana. Cannot practice actual games: no drawing cards, no land drops, no life tracking, no turn progression. Storm practice requires managing resources across multiple turns.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- db/schema.cljs:48-49 - :player/life and :player/land-plays-left already in schema
- db/schema.cljs:54-57 - :game/turn, :game/phase, :game/step already defined
- engine/effects.cljs:38-59 - Mill effect pattern to follow for draw
- events/game.cljs:64-68 - Library creation pattern exists for opponent

**External:**
- MTG Comprehensive Rules 500-514 - Turn structure reference
- Existing Phase 1 patterns sufficient, no new libraries needed

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Extend existing architecture ✓

**What it is:** Add new effect types, events, and card definitions following established patterns. No architectural changes.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed effects.cljs - multimethod pattern extensible
- Reviewed events/game.cljs - event pattern clear
- Schema already has life/phase/land-plays fields

**Pros:**
- Consistent with Phase 1 patterns
- Minimal new concepts
- Tests follow existing patterns

**Cons:**
- None significant

**Chosen because:** Architecture already supports this; just needs content.

#### 2. Phase-based state machine ❌

**What it is:** Formal state machine for phase transitions with explicit allowed actions per phase.

**Why we looked at this:** Could prevent illegal actions (cast during untap step).

**Investigation:**
- Evaluated complexity vs benefit
- Storm rarely has phase-specific timing issues

**Pros:**
- Rigorous phase enforcement

**Cons:**
- Over-engineering for goldfish practice
- Adds complexity without clear benefit

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Goldfish practice doesn't need strict phase enforcement. Player is trusted to follow rules.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Adding multiplayer or competitive play.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Full turn structure (all phases, simplified combat)
- Land permanents with mana abilities
- Life totals and loss conditions
- Test deck with lands and storm cards

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Fetch lands (Phase 2 - needs library search UI)
- Combat damage (future - storm doesn't attack)
- Upkeep/end step triggers (future - no cards need them yet)
- Mulligan decisions (Phase 4 - hand sculpting)

### Open Questions
- Mana pool clearing: at phase change or end of step? (default: end of each phase per modern rules)
- Cleanup discard: enforce 7-card limit or trust player? (default: trust player for now)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Turn structure depth? | Full MTG structure | Implement all phases, can simplify combat |
| Library initialization? | Hardcoded test deck with lands | 60-card deck with City/Gemstone/rituals/Brain Freeze |
| Win/loss conditions? | Full life + decking | Track both players' life, check on each action |
| Mana ability activation? | Click to tap | Manual tapping, no auto-tap |
| Fetch land complexity? | Skip for now | Only City of Brass and Gemstone Mine |
| Test deck contents? | Minimal variety | 12x each of 5 card types |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Turn Structure Implementation
**Question explored:** How to implement phase advancement?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/game.cljs - existing event patterns
- db/schema.cljs:54-57 - phase/step fields exist

**Findings:**
- Schema already has :game/phase and :game/step
- Events update :game/db atomically
- Can use simple phase list and advance index

**Conclusion:** Add ::advance-phase event, cycle through phase list

#### Land Permanents
**Question explored:** How to handle lands differently from spells?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/zones.cljs - zone transition logic
- engine/rules.cljs - casting rules

**Findings:**
- Zones already support :battlefield
- Casting uses stack; lands bypass stack
- Need separate ::play-land event

**Conclusion:** Lands go hand → battlefield directly (not via stack)

### Dead-End Paths

#### Auto-Tap Mana System
**Why explored:** Could simplify UI
**Investigation:**
- Reviewed MTGO-style auto-tap complexity
- Considered edge cases (City of Brass damage, Gemstone counters)

**Why abandoned:** User explicitly chose manual tap. Auto-tap hides decisions important for storm practice (life management from City of Brass).

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Gemstone Mine counter tracking?' → Use :object/counters attribute (add to schema if needed)
- 'Mana pool clearing timing?' → Default to phase transitions, matches modern rules
close_reason: Phase 1.5 complete: Turn structure, library/hand, land mechanics, life tracking, loss conditions all working. All tests passing (143 tests). Gemstone Mine counter logic deferred to fizzle-dg4 (Data-Driven Ability System) as that epic specifically addresses removing hardcoded card logic.

title: Task 2: Implement Lotus Petal sacrifice-for-mana
design:
## Goal
Add Lotus Petal card that sacrifices itself to produce 1 mana of any color.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Context
Completed Task 1 (Island, Swamp). Lotus Petal requires :sacrifice-self cost type.
Existing costs.cljs has :tap and :remove-counter. Need to add :sacrifice-self.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing cost patterns
- src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs - Review can-pay? and pay-costs functions
- Pattern: multimethod dispatch on cost type key
- Need to add :sacrifice-self cost type that moves permanent to graveyard

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Add tests to: src/test/fizzle/cards/lotus_petal_test.cljs (new file)

Test cases (8 tests):
```clojure
;; test_lotus_petal_sacrifice_for_black_mana
;; Setup: Lotus Petal on battlefield, untapped
;; Action: activate-mana-ability with :black
;; Verify: mana pool has {:black 1}, Lotus Petal zone is :graveyard

;; test_lotus_petal_sacrifice_for_blue_mana
;; Same as above but :blue - verify any-color works

;; test_lotus_petal_sacrifice_for_white_mana
;; Same pattern - :white

;; test_lotus_petal_sacrifice_for_red_mana
;; Same pattern - :red

;; test_lotus_petal_sacrifice_for_green_mana
;; Same pattern - :green

;; test_lotus_petal_cannot_activate_from_graveyard
;; Setup: Lotus Petal in graveyard (simulating after sacrifice)
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability with :black
;; Verify: returns unchanged db, mana pool unchanged

;; test_lotus_petal_in_hand_cannot_activate
;; Setup: Lotus Petal in hand (not battlefield)
;; Action: attempt activate-mana-ability with :black
;; Verify: returns unchanged db, mana pool unchanged

;; test_lotus_petal_is_artifact_type
;; Verify card definition has :card/types #{:artifact}
```

### 3. Add :sacrifice-self cost type
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs

Add can-pay? multimethod for :sacrifice-self:
```clojure
(defmethod can-pay? :sacrifice-self [db object-id _cost]
  ;; Can sacrifice-self if:
  ;; 1. Object exists
  ;; 2. Object is on battlefield
  (if-let [obj-eid (get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (= :battlefield (d/q '[:find ?z .
                           :in $ ?e
                           :where [?e :object/zone ?z]]
                         db obj-eid))
    false))
```

Add pay-cost multimethod for :sacrifice-self:
```clojure
(defmethod pay-cost :sacrifice-self [db object-id cost]
  ;; Guard: check can pay first
  (when (can-pay? db object-id cost)
    (let [obj-eid (get-object-eid db object-id)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/zone :graveyard]]))))
```

### 4. Add Lotus Petal card definition
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

```clojure
;; Lotus Petal - Zero-cost artifact mana acceleration
;; {T}, Sacrifice Lotus Petal: Add one mana of any color.
(def lotus-petal
  {:card/id :lotus-petal
   :card/name "Lotus Petal"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:artifact}
   :card/text "{T}, Sacrifice Lotus Petal: Add one mana of any color."
   :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                     :ability/cost {:tap true
                                    :sacrifice-self true}
                     :ability/produces {:any 1}}]})
```

### 5. Update all-cards vector
Add lotus-petal to all-cards:
```clojure
(def all-cards
  [dark-ritual brain-freeze cabal-ritual city-of-brass gemstone-mine island swamp lotus-petal])
```

### 6. Run validation
- make test (expect 8 new tests pass)
- make validate (lint + fmt + tests)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] can-pay? :sacrifice-self returns true only for battlefield objects
- [ ] pay-cost :sacrifice-self moves object to :graveyard zone
- [ ] Lotus Petal definition exists with :card/types #{:artifact}
- [ ] Lotus Petal mana ability has both :tap and :sacrifice-self costs
- [ ] activate-mana-ability works for all 5 colors (white, blue, black, red, green)
- [ ] Cannot activate from graveyard (already sacrificed)
- [ ] Cannot activate from hand (not on battlefield)
- [ ] 8 tests in lotus_petal_test.cljs all pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in costs.cljs (sacrifice-self is generic for all cards)
- ❌ NO skipping TDD (write failing tests first)
- ❌ NO checking card ID in can-pay?/pay-cost (must be generic)
- ❌ NO implementing sacrifice without zone check (must be on battlefield)

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Cost Payment Order**
- Both :tap and :sacrifice-self costs must be paid
- activate-mana-ability should iterate through ability costs and pay each
- Verify existing pay-costs handles multiple cost types

**Zone Change for Sacrifice**
- Sacrifice = move to graveyard (not exile)
- Use :object/zone :graveyard (same as spell resolution)
- Verify zone update triggers any on-death effects (future concern)

**Edge Case: Double Activation**
- After sacrifice, object is in graveyard
- can-pay? :sacrifice-self returns false (zone check fails)
- Second activation returns unchanged db (safe, no error)

**Edge Case: Tap Cost Already Paid**
- If somehow object is tapped before sacrifice, tap cost fails
- can-pay? :tap returns false for tapped objects (existing behavior)
- Unlikely scenario but correctly handled

**Reference Implementation**
- Study existing :remove-counter cost pattern in costs.cljs
- Same structure: can-pay? checks, pay-cost executes
- :sacrifice-self is simpler (no counter tracking, just zone change)
close_reason: Implemented :sacrifice-self cost type and Lotus Petal card. All 8 tests passing.

title: Task 4: Extend get-casting-modes for kicker support
design:
## Goal
Extend get-casting-modes to return a :kicked mode when a card has :card/kicker, enabling players to choose between normal and kicked versions of a spell.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Context
Completed tasks:
- fizzle-d3y: Player grants system
- fizzle-w0x: :add-restriction effect type
- fizzle-gq0: cannot-cast-spells restriction checking

The casting modes system already handles primary + alternate modes (e.g., flashback). Kicker fits this pattern: another mode with different cost and effects.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing mode system
Reference implementations:
- rules.cljs:22-32 - get-primary-mode (returns mode with base cost/effects)
- rules.cljs:35-42 - alternate-to-mode (converts alternate definition to mode map)
- rules.cljs:62-83 - get-casting-modes (combines primary + alternates)
- rules.cljs:291-311 - get-active-effects (selects effects at resolution)

### 2. Design Decision: Kicked Effects Storage

Kicked mode includes :mode/effects key with kicked effects. get-active-effects checks for mode effects first. Mode is available at resolution time.

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] Add merge-mana-costs helper at rules.cljs line ~20 (before get-primary-mode):
      Signature: (defn- merge-mana-costs [cost1 cost2])
      Implementation: (merge-with + (or cost1 {}) (or cost2 {}))
      Bug caught: Incorrect kicker cost calculation

- [ ] Add get-kicked-mode function at rules.cljs line ~33 (after get-primary-mode):
      Signature: (defn- get-kicked-mode [card])
      Returns: nil if no :card/kicker, or mode map with:
        :mode/id :kicked
        :mode/zone :hand
        :mode/mana-cost (merge-mana-costs base kicker)
        :mode/additional-costs []
        :mode/effects (:card/kicked-effects card)
        :mode/on-resolve (same logic as get-primary-mode)
      Bug caught: Missing kicked mode for kicker cards

- [ ] Modify get-casting-modes at rules.cljs:76-79 to include kicked mode:
      Change from: (into [primary] alternates)
      Change to: (cond-> [primary] kicked (conj kicked) true (into alternates))
      Where kicked = (get-kicked-mode card)
      Bug caught: Kicker cards only return primary mode

- [ ] Modify get-active-effects at rules.cljs:291-311:
      Add object-id parameter: (defn get-active-effects [db player-id card object-id])
      First check: (let [obj (q/get-object db object-id) mode-effects (:mode/effects (:object/cast-mode obj))])
      If mode-effects, return mode-effects
      Else fall through to existing conditional/default logic
      Bug caught: Kicked effects ignored at resolution

- [ ] Update resolve-spell at rules.cljs:335 to pass object-id to get-active-effects:
      Change from: (get-active-effects db player-id card)
      Change to: (get-active-effects db player-id card object-id)
      Bug caught: Signature mismatch crash

## Tests (TDD) - rules_test.cljs

### Test helper (add after add-card-to-zone):
```clojure
(def kicker-test-card
  "Card with kicker for testing kicker modes."
  {:card/id :test-kicker-spell
   :card/name "Test Kicker Spell"
   :card/cmc 1
   :card/mana-cost {:white 1}
   :card/colors #{:white}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Kicker {W}. If kicked, draw a card."
   :card/kicker {:white 1}
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :mill :effect/count 1}]
   :card/kicked-effects [{:effect/type :mill :effect/count 1}
                          {:effect/type :draw :effect/count 1}]})
```

### Test 1: kicker-returns-two-modes-test
Bug caught: Cards with kicker only return primary mode (missing kicked mode)
Assertion: (= 2 (count modes))
Assertion: (some #(= :primary (:mode/id %)) modes)
Assertion: (some #(= :kicked (:mode/id %)) modes)

### Test 2: kicked-mode-has-combined-cost-test
Bug caught: Kicked mode uses base cost instead of combined
Assertion: kicked-mode mana-cost = {:white 2} (not {:white 1})
Formula verified: {W} + {W} = {WW}

### Test 3: kicked-mode-includes-effects-test
Bug caught: Kicked mode missing :mode/effects field
Assertion: (:mode/effects kicked-mode) equals kicked-effects from card

### Test 4: primary-mode-no-effects-test
Bug caught: Primary mode incorrectly includes effects
Assertion: (nil? (:mode/effects primary-mode))

### Test 5: no-kicker-single-mode-test
Bug caught: Cards without kicker affected by kicker logic
Assertion: (= 1 (count modes)) for regular card
Assertion: (= :primary (:mode/id (first modes)))

### Test 6: kicker-not-from-graveyard-test
Bug caught: Kicker mode available from graveyard (should only be from hand)
Setup: Add kicker card to graveyard
Assertion: (= 0 (count modes)) - no modes available

### Test 7: get-active-effects-uses-mode-effects-test
Bug caught: Resolution ignores mode effects
Setup: Cast with kicked mode, resolve
Assertion: Kicked effects execute (both mill and draw)

### Test 8: kicker-with-flashback-independent-test
Bug caught: Kicker interferes with flashback
Setup: Card with both :card/kicker and :card/alternate-costs (flashback)
From hand: primary + kicked + flashback-from-hand modes
From graveyard: only flashback mode (no kicked from graveyard)

### Test 9: nil-kicker-cost-test
Bug caught: Crash when :card/kicker is present but nil
Setup: Card with :card/kicker nil
Assertion: Returns only primary mode (no crash)

### Test 10: empty-kicked-effects-test
Bug caught: Crash when :card/kicked-effects missing or empty
Setup: Card with kicker but no :card/kicked-effects
Assertion: Kicked mode has :mode/effects []

## Success Criteria
- [ ] kicker-returns-two-modes-test passes
- [ ] kicked-mode-has-combined-cost-test passes
- [ ] kicked-mode-includes-effects-test passes
- [ ] primary-mode-no-effects-test passes
- [ ] no-kicker-single-mode-test passes
- [ ] kicker-not-from-graveyard-test passes
- [ ] get-active-effects-uses-mode-effects-test passes
- [ ] kicker-with-flashback-independent-test passes
- [ ] nil-kicker-cost-test passes
- [ ] empty-kicked-effects-test passes
- [ ] All existing 627+ tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint, fmt, tests)

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO treating kicker as a boolean flag (use mode system)
- NO modifying how primary mode works (add kicked as separate mode)
- NO hardcoding Orims Chant logic (generic kicker support)
- NO changing flashback behavior (kicked is independent)
- NO using when-let for get-kicked-mode (nil kicker is valid case, use when)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Edge Case: Card has both kicker and flashback**
Both work independently. From hand: primary + kicked + any hand-alternates. From graveyard: flashback only (no kicked from graveyard). Test 8 verifies this.

**Edge Case: Kicker cost overlaps base cost**
merge-with + handles correctly: {W} + {W} = {:white 2}. Test 2 verifies.

**Edge Case: nil :card/kicker**
When :card/kicker key exists but value is nil, get-kicked-mode returns nil. Use (when (:card/kicker card) ...) not (when-let [k (:card/kicker card)] ...).

**Edge Case: nil or missing :card/kicked-effects**
Kicked mode should have :mode/effects [] if kicked-effects is nil/missing. Use (or (:card/kicked-effects card) []).

**Edge Case: Empty kicker cost {}**
Merged cost = base cost. Mode still created with :mode/id :kicked. Player can choose to kick for free.

**Reference: Existing Mode Pattern**
- Primary mode: base cost, default destination, no :mode/effects
- Flashback: different cost, different zone, :exile destination
- Kicked: combined cost, same zone, has :mode/effects override
close_reason: Closed

title: Add data model functions: rename-fork, delete-fork, entries-by-turn, fork-tree
design:
## Goal
Add four pure functions to history/core.cljs that provide the data layer for all three UI components. These are pure functions operating on the history state map — no re-frame, no side effects.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (with TDD)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- history/core.cljs:4-9 — init-history structure (`:history/main`, `:history/forks`, `:history/current-branch`, `:history/position`)
- history/core.cljs:20-22 — `get-fork` for fork lookup pattern
- history/core.cljs:25-32 — `effective-entries-for-branch` for recursive parent chain walking (reuse pattern for cascade delete descendant discovery)
- history/core.cljs:88-102 — `auto-fork` for fork structure: `:fork/id`, `:fork/name`, `:fork/branch-point`, `:fork/parent`, `:fork/entries`
- history/core.cljs:118-127 — `switch-branch` for how to restore game/db when changing branches (must replicate in delete-fork active branch case)
- test/fizzle/history/core_test.cljs — existing test patterns, `build-3-entry-db` and `build-6-entry-db` helpers

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**rename-fork tests:**
- test-rename-fork: renames fork, returns db with updated name, other fork fields unchanged
- test-rename-fork-nonexistent: fork-id not in map, returns db unchanged (no-op)

**delete-fork tests:**
- test-delete-fork-single: removes single fork with no children from :history/forks
- test-delete-fork-cascade-children: fork with 2 children — all 3 removed from map
- test-delete-fork-cascade-deep: 3-level chain (A→B→C) — deleting A removes A, B, C
- test-delete-fork-active-branch: deleting current branch switches to main, restores game/db from main tip snapshot, sets position to main tip
- test-delete-fork-ancestor-of-active: on Fork C (child of B, child of A) — deleting A removes A+B+C, switches to main (because current branch was deleted)
- test-delete-fork-inactive: deleting a fork that is NOT current branch — current-branch and position unchanged
- test-delete-fork-nil-noop: fork-id nil (main) — returns db unchanged
- test-delete-fork-nonexistent: fork-id not in map — returns db unchanged
- test-delete-fork-preserves-main: after cascade delete, :history/main entries untouched

**entries-by-turn tests:**
- test-entries-by-turn-empty: empty list returns empty vector
- test-entries-by-turn-single-turn: 3 entries all turn 1 → one group with 3 entries
- test-entries-by-turn-multiple-turns: entries across turns 1, 2, 3 → 3 groups in order
- test-entries-by-turn-preserves-order: within a turn group, entries appear in original order
- test-entries-by-turn-non-consecutive: turns 1, 3, 5 (gaps) → 3 groups, ordered by turn number

**fork-tree tests:**
- test-fork-tree-empty: empty forks map returns empty vector
- test-fork-tree-all-roots: 3 forks all with nil parent → 3 root nodes, no children
- test-fork-tree-parent-child: parent fork + child fork → parent node has child in :children
- test-fork-tree-deep-nesting: A→B→C chain → A has child B, B has child C, C has no children
- test-fork-tree-mixed: 2 roots, one with 2 children → correct tree structure

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] `rename-fork [db fork-id new-name]` — If fork-id exists in :history/forks, assoc-in [:history/forks fork-id :fork/name new-name]. If not, return db unchanged.

- [ ] `delete-fork [db fork-id]` — Steps:
  1. If fork-id is nil or not in :history/forks, return db unchanged
  2. Find all descendants: walk all forks, collect those whose :fork/parent chain includes fork-id (recursive)
  3. Dissoc fork-id + all descendants from :history/forks
  4. If :history/current-branch is fork-id OR any descendant: switch to main — set :history/current-branch to nil, set :history/position to (dec (count :history/main)), set :game/db to snapshot of last main entry (mirrors switch-branch logic at core.cljs:118-127)
  5. Return updated db

- [ ] `entries-by-turn [entries]` — Takes a seq of entry maps (from effective-entries). Returns vector of `{:turn N :entries [...]}` ordered by turn number ascending. Use `(group-by :entry/turn entries)` then `(sort-by first)` on the groups. Preserve original entry order within each group (group-by preserves insertion order in ClojureScript).

- [ ] `fork-tree [forks]` — Takes the :history/forks map (fork-id → fork). Returns vector of tree nodes. Each node is `(assoc fork :children [child-nodes...])`. Root nodes have `:fork/parent nil`. Build by partitioning forks by parent, then recursively attaching children. Sort roots and children by :fork/name for stable ordering.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] rename-fork updates name, no-ops on nonexistent fork-id (2 tests)
- [ ] delete-fork cascade removes fork + all descendants recursively (3 tests: single, children, deep)
- [ ] delete-fork switches to main when current branch (or ancestor of current branch) is deleted, restoring game/db from main tip (2 tests)
- [ ] delete-fork no-ops on nil fork-id and nonexistent fork-id (2 tests)
- [ ] delete-fork preserves :history/main entries unchanged (1 test)
- [ ] entries-by-turn groups correctly for empty, single-turn, multi-turn, non-consecutive turns (5 tests)
- [ ] fork-tree structures correctly for empty, all-roots, parent-child, deep nesting, mixed (5 tests)
- [ ] Total: ~20 tests covering all functions and edge cases
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Deleting ancestor of active branch**
When on Fork C (child of B, child of A) and deleting Fork A, all three forks are removed. Must detect that current-branch is among the deleted set and switch to main. Implementation: collect all fork-ids to delete first, then check if current-branch is in that set.

**Edge Case: Empty main after delete**
If delete-fork needs to switch to main but main has no entries (position -1), handle gracefully — set position to -1, game/db unchanged. Reference: init-history sets position -1 with empty main.

**Edge Case: fork-tree with orphaned references**
If data is inconsistent (fork references parent that doesn't exist), fork-tree should treat it as a root node rather than crashing. Defensive: if parent not in forks map, treat as root.

**Performance: Descendant discovery**
Cascade delete walks all forks to find descendants. With small fork counts (practice tool, likely <20 forks) this is fine. No optimization needed — linear scan is sufficient.

**Output structure consistency**
fork-tree returns forks with added :children key. entries-by-turn returns new map structure {:turn N :entries [...]}. These are the contracts the subscription layer will depend on.

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO mutation or side effects — all functions take db/data, return db/data
- ❌ NO re-frame dispatch or subscription calls in these functions
- ❌ NO game/db snapshot restoration in entries-by-turn or fork-tree (only delete-fork does this, mirroring switch-branch)
- ❌ NO throwing exceptions for invalid input (return db unchanged for no-ops)
close_reason: All 4 functions implemented with TDD: rename-fork (2 tests), delete-fork (9 tests), entries-by-turn (5 tests), fork-tree (5 tests). 955 total tests, make validate passes. Committed as 760dc6a.

title: Phase 6: Implement :grant-flashback effect type
description:
## Goal
Implement the :grant-flashback effect type that grants flashback to a target card, using the grant system and targeting system built in previous phases.

## Context
Phases 1-5 complete:
- Grant system: add-grant, remove-grant, expire-grants
- Cleanup system: expire-grants trigger at cleanup phase
- Targeting foundation: get-targeting-requirements, find-valid-targets, all-targets-legal?
- Deep Analysis: cast-time player targeting
- Cephalid Coliseum: ability targeting

Now need to implement the effect that grants flashback to a target sorcery.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Implement :grant-flashback effect type
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs

Add defmethod for :grant-flashback that:
1. Gets target from stored targets on object (:object/targets)
2. Gets target card mana cost
3. Creates grant with :alternate-cost type
4. Adds grant via grants/add-grant

### Step 2: Write tests for grant-flashback effect

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :grant-flashback effect type implemented
- [ ] Effect reads target from :object/targets
- [ ] Effect creates grant with copied mana cost
- [ ] Grant expires at cleanup phase
- [ ] Tests verify effect behavior
- [ ] make test passes

## Anti-Patterns
- Do NOT hardcode target - read from stored :object/targets
- Do NOT skip expiration - must set expires/turn and expires/phase
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Standardize selection state keys
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All selection builders use :selection/select-count exclusively (no :selection/count)
- All selection consumers read :selection/select-count exclusively
- The legacy dual-set pattern (setting both keys) is removed
- A canonical schema docstring documents all 10 selection types and their key sets at the top of selection.cljs
- All existing selection flows (tutor, discard, scry, pile-choice, graveyard-return, exile-cards, x-mana, player-target, cast-time-targeting, cleanup-discard) continue to work identically

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Zero occurrences of :selection/count in src/ (only :selection/select-count)
- [ ] Zero occurrences of :selection/count in test/ (only :selection/select-count)
- [ ] selection.cljs has top-level docstring documenting canonical selection state shapes
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)
- [ ] No regressions in selection flows

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO backwards-compatibility shims reading both keys (clean rename, not deprecation layer)
- NO changes to :selection/type or :selection/effect-type discriminators (separate ticket fizzle-cxa follow-up)
- NO boolean key renames like :allow-fail-to-find -> :allow-fail-to-find? (separate ticket)
- NO structural changes to selection flow (pure key rename + docs)
- NO spec/schema validation additions (out of scope - future Datascript migration)

## Approach
Direct mechanical rename of :selection/count -> :selection/select-count across all builders, consumers, views, subscriptions, and tests. Remove the dual-set pattern in tutor builder (selection.cljs lines 130-131). Add canonical schema docstring to selection.cljs namespace.

## Architecture
Files affected:
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs - Builders and confirmers (~40 locations)
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs - build-cleanup-discard-selection, confirm-selection
- src/main/fizzle/subs/game.cljs - Subscriptions reading selection state
- src/main/fizzle/views/modals.cljs - Modal components reading count
- src/test/fizzle/events/*_test.cljs - Test assertions and setup

## Design Rationale
### Problem
:selection/count and :selection/select-count represent the same concept (how many items to select) but are used inconsistently. The tutor builder sets BOTH for backwards compatibility (lines 130-131 in selection.cljs). This creates confusion and bug risk, and blocks the P1 Datascript migration (fizzle-4jc) which needs a clean selection schema.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- selection.cljs:130-131 - Explicit comment showing dual-set for backwards compat
- :selection/count used by: discard, graveyard-return, cleanup-discard builders
- :selection/select-count used by: tutor multi-select, peek-and-select, exile-cards-cost builders
- 35 unique :selection/* keys across 10 selection types
- Views in modals.cljs route via :selection/type OR :selection/effect-type (dual discriminator - separate issue)

**Key insight:** The boolean naming inconsistency (:allow-fail-to-find lacks ?) and discriminator duplication are real issues but orthogonal to the count key standardization.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Direct rename ✓
**What it is:** Rename :selection/count -> :selection/select-count everywhere. Remove dual-set pattern.
**Chosen because:** Mechanical, verifiable, no legacy code left behind.

#### 2. Deprecation layer ❌
**What it is:** Add reader helper checking both keys, gradually migrate.
**Why explored:** Lower per-change risk
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Adds indirection for a purely internal key. More code, not less.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Selection keys become part of a public API.

#### 3. Spec-driven migration ❌
**What it is:** Define clojure.spec for selection maps, use spec to enforce.
**Why explored:** Could catch issues at runtime
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Overkill for a key rename. Spec adds dependency for no proportional benefit.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We're adding spec validation as a general pattern.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- :selection/count -> :selection/select-count rename in all files
- Remove dual-set pattern in tutor builder
- Add canonical schema docstring to selection.cljs
- Update all tests

**Out of scope (separate tickets):**
- Boolean naming: :allow-fail-to-find -> :allow-fail-to-find? 
- Discriminator unification: :selection/type vs :selection/effect-type
- Datascript migration of selection state (fizzle-4jc)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Which count key to standardize on? | :selection/select-count | More descriptive, already used by newer code |
| Scope: keys only or also discriminators? | Keys only, create tickets for others | Keeps blast radius small |
| Add schema documentation? | Yes, docstring in selection.cljs | Documents canonical key sets per selection type |
| Approach? | Direct rename (Option 1) | No legacy code, no deprecation layer |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Count Key Usage Mapping
- :selection/count users: discard builder, graveyard-return builder, cleanup-discard builder, views/modals confirmation logic
- :selection/select-count users: tutor multi-select, peek-and-select, exile-cards-cost builders
- Both set simultaneously in: tutor builder (selection.cljs:130-131) for backwards compat

#### Selection Type Inventory
10 selection types identified with full key schemas documented by codebase-investigator agent. 9 builders in selection.cljs + 1 in game.cljs (cleanup-discard).

### Dead-End Paths
-  vs  conflict from original ticket description - DOES NOT EXIST. Only :selection/shuffle? variant exists.

### Open Concerns Raised
- Dual discriminator (:selection/type vs :selection/effect-type) needs its own ticket
- Boolean naming inconsistency needs its own ticket
close_reason: Shell mangled special chars, replaced by fizzle-whic

title: Create engine/abilities.cljs with generic mana ability activation
design:
## Goal
Implement engine/abilities.cljs with generic ability activation that reads card data and delegates to costs.cljs for payment.

## Context
Completed fizzle-31d: engine/costs.cljs now handles :tap and :remove-counter costs via multimethod dispatch. Now need the abilities layer that reads :card/abilities from card data and uses costs.cljs to pay costs.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Implementation Steps

### 1. RED: Write tests for abilities.cljs
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/abilities_test.cljs (new file)

Follow pattern from costs_test.cljs - use add-permanent helper.

**can-activate? tests (6 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-can-activate-tap-ability
  (testing "can-activate? returns true for untapped permanent with :tap cost"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent on battlefield
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true}}
    ;; Assert: (abilities/can-activate? db object-id ability) => true
    ;; Catches: Missing delegation to costs/can-pay?
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-already-tapped
  (testing "can-activate? returns false for tapped permanent with :tap cost"
    ;; Setup: tapped permanent on battlefield
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true}}
    ;; Assert: (abilities/can-activate? db object-id ability) => false
    ;; Catches: Not checking cost payability
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-counter-ability
  (testing "can-activate? returns true when permanent has sufficient counters"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}}
    ;; Assert: true
    ;; Catches: Not delegating counter check to costs.cljs
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-counter-ability-insufficient
  (testing "can-activate? returns false when insufficient counters"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 0}
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}}
    ;; Assert: false
    ;; Catches: Not checking counter count
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-nil-cost
  (testing "can-activate? returns true for ability with nil cost (free activation)"
    ;; Setup: any permanent
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost nil}  ;; or ability without :ability/cost key
    ;; Assert: true
    ;; Catches: NPE on nil cost map, treating nil as unpayable
    ))

(deftest test-can-activate-invalid-object-id
  (testing "can-activate? returns false for non-existent permanent"
    ;; Setup: db with no matching object
    ;; Action: (abilities/can-activate? db (random-uuid) ability)
    ;; Assert: false (not crash)
    ;; Catches: NPE on object lookup
    ))
```

**pay-all-costs tests (4 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-pay-all-costs-single
  (testing "pay-all-costs with single :tap cost taps permanent"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent
    ;; Action: (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id {:tap true})
    ;; Assert: returns db with permanent tapped
    ;; Catches: Not calling costs/pay-cost
    ))

(deftest test-pay-all-costs-multiple
  (testing "pay-all-costs with :tap and :remove-counter pays both"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Action: (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id {:tap true :remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: permanent tapped AND counters at {:mining 2}
    ;; Catches: Only paying first cost
    ))

(deftest test-pay-all-costs-fails-partial
  (testing "pay-all-costs returns nil if any cost fails, no partial payment"
    ;; Setup: tapped permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Action: (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id {:tap true :remove-counter {:mining 1}})
    ;; Assert: returns nil, counters still {:mining 3} (no change)
    ;; Catches: Paying counter cost even when tap fails
    ))

(deftest test-pay-all-costs-empty
  (testing "pay-all-costs with empty/nil costs returns db unchanged"
    ;; Setup: any permanent
    ;; Action: (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id {})
    ;; Assert: returns original db unchanged
    ;; Catches: NPE on empty map, returning nil for free costs
    ))
```

**activate-ability tests (4 tests):**
```clojure
(deftest test-activate-tap-ability
  (testing "activate-ability pays :tap cost and applies mana effect"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true} :ability/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 1}}]}
    ;; Action: (abilities/activate-ability db object-id ability :player-1)
    ;; Assert: permanent tapped AND player has 1 black mana
    ;; Catches: Not applying effects after paying costs
    ))

(deftest test-activate-tap-already-tapped
  (testing "activate-ability returns nil when cost cannot be paid"
    ;; Setup: tapped permanent
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true} :ability/effects [...]}
    ;; Action: (abilities/activate-ability db object-id ability :player-1)
    ;; Assert: nil, mana pool unchanged
    ;; Catches: Applying effects even when costs fail
    ))

(deftest test-activate-counter-ability
  (testing "activate-ability removes counter and applies mana effect"
    ;; Setup: permanent with {:mining 3}
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}} :ability/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:any 1}}]}
    ;; Action: (abilities/activate-ability db object-id ability :player-1)
    ;; Assert: counters at {:mining 2}, mana in pool
    ;; Catches: Counter not removed, effect not applied
    ))

(deftest test-activate-ability-empty-effects
  (testing "activate-ability with no effects still pays costs (for triggered abilities later)"
    ;; Setup: untapped permanent
    ;; Ability: {:ability/cost {:tap true} :ability/effects []}
    ;; Action: (abilities/activate-ability db object-id ability :player-1)
    ;; Assert: returns db with permanent tapped (not nil)
    ;; Catches: Returning nil when effects list empty
    ))
```

### 2. GREEN: Implement engine/abilities.cljs

Create new file: src/main/fizzle/engine/abilities.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.abilities
  "Generic ability activation for Fizzle.

   Provides can-activate?, pay-all-costs, and activate-ability functions
   that work with any ability defined as data."
  (:require
    [fizzle.engine.costs :as costs]
    [fizzle.engine.effects :as effects]))
```

### 3. Implement can-activate?

```clojure
(defn can-activate?
  "Check if an ability can be activated.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent
     ability - Map with :ability/cost and optionally :ability/effects
   
   Returns:
     Boolean - true if all costs can be paid (or no costs)."
  [db object-id ability]
  (let [cost (:ability/cost ability)]
    (if (or (nil? cost) (empty? cost))
      true  ;; Free activation
      (every? (fn [[cost-type cost-value]]
                (costs/can-pay? db object-id {cost-type cost-value}))
              cost))))
```

### 4. Implement pay-all-costs

```clojure
(defn pay-all-costs
  "Pay all costs for an ability.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent
     costs - Map of costs {:tap true :remove-counter {:mining 1}}
   
   Returns:
     New db with all costs paid, or nil if any cost cannot be paid.
     Empty/nil costs return db unchanged."
  [db object-id costs]
  (if (or (nil? costs) (empty? costs))
    db  ;; No costs to pay
    (reduce (fn [db' [cost-type cost-value]]
              (if-let [new-db (costs/pay-cost db' object-id {cost-type cost-value})]
                new-db
                (reduced nil)))
            db
            costs)))
```

### 5. Implement activate-ability

```clojure
(defn activate-ability
  "Activate an ability on a permanent.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     object-id - UUID of the permanent
     ability - Map with :ability/cost and :ability/effects
     player-id - The player activating
   
   Returns:
     New db with costs paid and effects applied, or nil if cannot activate."
  [db object-id ability player-id]
  (when (can-activate? db object-id ability)
    (let [cost (:ability/cost ability)
          db-after-costs (pay-all-costs db object-id cost)]
      (when db-after-costs
        ;; Apply effects (empty list is fine)
        (reduce (fn [db' effect]
                  (effects/execute-effect db' player-id effect))
                db-after-costs
                (:ability/effects ability []))))))
```

### 6. VERIFY
- Run: make validate
- Confirm all 14 new tests pass
- Confirm all 175+ existing tests still pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] engine/abilities.cljs exists at src/main/fizzle/engine/abilities.cljs
- [ ] can-activate? with :tap cost on untapped permanent returns true
- [ ] can-activate? with :tap cost on tapped permanent returns false
- [ ] can-activate? with nil/empty cost returns true (free activation)
- [ ] can-activate? with invalid object-id returns false (not crash)
- [ ] pay-all-costs with single cost pays it and returns db
- [ ] pay-all-costs with multiple costs pays all or returns nil
- [ ] pay-all-costs with nil/empty costs returns db unchanged
- [ ] activate-ability pays costs, applies effects, returns db
- [ ] activate-ability with unpayable cost returns nil, no side effects
- [ ] activate-ability with empty effects list still works (returns db)
- [ ] 14 tests pass in abilities_test.cljs
- [ ] All 175+ existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO card ID checks (data over code)
- ❌ NO duplicating cost logic (delegate to costs.cljs)
- ❌ NO hardcoded ability types
- ❌ NO partial cost payment (all-or-nothing)
- ❌ NO crashing on nil/empty costs or effects (handle gracefully)
- ❌ NO applying effects if costs cannot be paid

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Nil/Empty Cost Map**:
- Ability with no cost (nil :ability/cost or {}) should be activatable
- pay-all-costs with {} or nil should return db unchanged
- Test: test-can-activate-nil-cost, test-pay-all-costs-empty

**Multiple Costs - All-or-Nothing**:
- If :tap paid but :remove-counter fails, entire activation fails
- reduce returns nil on first failure via (reduced nil)
- Original db is never modified (immutable)
- Test: test-pay-all-costs-fails-partial

**Empty Effects List**:
- Ability with :ability/effects [] or nil is valid
- Should still pay costs and return db (not nil)
- Test: test-activate-ability-empty-effects

**Invalid Object ID**:
- can-activate? with non-existent object-id should return false
- Should not crash or throw exception
- Test: test-can-activate-invalid-object-id

**Effect Integration**:
- Effects use existing effects/execute-effect multimethod
- Mana abilities produce {:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 1}}
- Unknown effect types are no-op (graceful degradation)

**NOTE: Trigger firing is OUT OF SCOPE**:
- :becomes-tapped triggers will be handled by the trigger system
- This task focuses on ability activation only
- Triggers will be added in a separate task when we implement trigger firing

## Reference Implementation
- Study engine/costs.cljs for cost payment patterns
- Study engine/effects.cljs for effect execution pattern
- Study test helpers in costs_test.cljs for add-permanent helper
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 7: Implement Recoup card definition
description:
Implement the Recoup card (1R Sorcery) that grants flashback to target sorcery in graveyard.

## Oracle Text
Target sorcery card in your graveyard gains flashback until end of turn. The flashback cost is equal to the card's mana cost. Flashback {3}{R}

## Prerequisites (All Completed)
- Grant system (engine/grants.cljs) ✅
- Cleanup/expiration system (triggers.cljs, turn_based.cljs) ✅
- Targeting system (targeting.cljs) ✅
- :grant-flashback effect type (effects.cljs) ✅
- Deep Analysis and Cephalid Coliseum migrations ✅

## Implementation Steps

1. Create src/main/fizzle/cards/recoup.cljs with card definition:
   - :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :red 1}
   - :card/types #{:sorcery}
   - :card/targeting [{:target/id :graveyard-sorcery
                       :target/type :object
                       :target/zone :graveyard
                       :target/controller :self
                       :target/criteria {:card/types #{:sorcery}}
                       :target/required true}]
   - :card/effects [{:effect/type :grant-flashback
                     :effect/target-ref :graveyard-sorcery}]
   - :card/alternate-costs for Recoup's own flashback {3}{R}

2. Add Recoup to iggy-pop.cljs cards collection

3. Create src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs with comprehensive tests:
   - Basic flow: cast Recoup, grant flashback, cast target via flashback
   - Targeting fizzle: target removed before resolution
   - Flashback Recoup: cast from graveyard, verify exiles
   - End of turn cleanup: granted flashback expires
   - Multiple grants: verify grants work independently across turns

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Recoup card file exists with correct definition
- [ ] Card added to iggy-pop collection
- [ ] All 5 test scenarios pass
- [ ] Existing tests still pass (make test)
design:
Implement the Recoup card (1R Sorcery) that grants flashback to target sorcery in graveyard.

## Oracle Text
Target sorcery card in your graveyard gains flashback until end of turn. The flashback cost is equal to the card's mana cost. Flashback {3}{R}

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Prerequisites (All Completed)
- Grant system (engine/grants.cljs) ✅
- Cleanup/expiration system (triggers.cljs, turn_based.cljs) ✅
- Targeting system (targeting.cljs) ✅
- :grant-flashback effect type (effects.cljs) ✅
- Deep Analysis and Cephalid Coliseum migrations ✅

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Create src/main/fizzle/cards/recoup.cljs

Follow the pattern from deep_analysis.cljs:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.recoup
  "Recoup card definition.

   Recoup: 1R - Sorcery
   Target sorcery card in your graveyard gains flashback until end of turn.
   The flashback cost is equal to the card's mana cost.
   Flashback {3}{R}")

(def recoup
  {:card/id :recoup
   :card/name "Recoup"
   :card/cmc 2
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :red 1}
   :card/colors #{:red}
   :card/types #{:sorcery}
   :card/text "Target sorcery card in your graveyard gains flashback until end of turn. The flashback cost is equal to the card's mana cost. Flashback {3}{R}"

   ;; Cast-time targeting: target sorcery in your graveyard
   :card/targeting [{:target/id :graveyard-sorcery
                     :target/type :object
                     :target/zone :graveyard
                     :target/controller :self
                     :target/criteria {:card/types #{:sorcery}}
                     :target/required true}]

   ;; Effect: grant flashback to target
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :grant-flashback
                   :effect/target-ref :graveyard-sorcery}]

   ;; Recoup's own flashback
   :card/alternate-costs [{:alternate/id :flashback
                           :alternate/zone :graveyard
                           :alternate/mana-cost {:colorless 3 :red 1}
                           :alternate/on-resolve :exile}]})
```

### Step 2: Add to iggy-pop.cljs

1. Add require: `[fizzle.cards.recoup :as recoup]`
2. Add to all-cards vector: `recoup/recoup`

### Step 3: Create src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs

Follow pattern from deep_analysis_test.cljs for test helpers.

## Success Criteria

**Card Definition Tests (catch definition bugs):**
- [ ] test_recoup_card_definition - Verifies :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :red 1}, :card/types #{:sorcery}, :card/colors #{:red}
- [ ] test_recoup_has_targeting_requirement - Verifies :card/targeting with :graveyard-sorcery target
- [ ] test_recoup_has_grant_flashback_effect - Verifies :effect/type :grant-flashback
- [ ] test_recoup_has_own_flashback - Verifies :alternate/mana-cost {:colorless 3 :red 1}

**Cast Requirements Tests (catch targeting bugs):**
- [ ] test_recoup_requires_valid_target - Cannot cast if no sorceries in graveyard
- [ ] test_recoup_finds_valid_sorcery_target - Finds sorcery in graveyard as valid target
- [ ] test_recoup_does_not_target_instants - Instants in graveyard are NOT valid targets
- [ ] test_recoup_does_not_target_opponent_graveyard - Only targets self graveyard

**Grant Flashback Tests (catch grant system bugs):**
- [ ] test_recoup_grants_flashback_on_resolution - Target sorcery has :granted-flashback alternate cost after resolution
- [ ] test_granted_flashback_uses_original_mana_cost - If target costs {2BB}, granted flashback costs {2BB}
- [ ] test_granted_flashback_exiles_on_resolve - Target card using granted flashback exiles after resolution

**Fizzle Tests (catch targeting validation bugs):**
- [ ] test_recoup_fizzles_if_target_removed - If target exiled before resolution, Recoup has no effect and goes to graveyard
- [ ] test_recoup_still_resolves_if_other_sorceries_exist - Fizzle is per-target, not based on other cards

**Cleanup Tests (catch expiration bugs):**
- [ ] test_granted_flashback_expires_at_cleanup - Grant removed when cleanup phase reached
- [ ] test_granted_flashback_does_not_expire_before_cleanup - Grant still active during main phase

**Flashback Recoup Tests (catch Recoup-casting-itself bugs):**
- [ ] test_flashback_recoup_castable_from_graveyard - Recoup is castable for {3}{R} from graveyard
- [ ] test_flashback_recoup_exiles_after_resolution - Flashback Recoup exiles, not goes to graveyard
- [ ] test_flashback_recoup_can_target_sorceries - Flashback Recoup can still target sorceries

**Integration Tests (catch full flow bugs):**
- [ ] test_full_flow_cast_recoup_grant_flashback_use_it - Complete storm combo scenario

**Verification Commands:**
```bash
make test                          # All tests pass
make lint                          # No linter warnings
grep -c "deftest" src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs  # Should be >= 18
```

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: No Valid Targets**
- If player has no sorceries in graveyard, Recoup cannot be cast
- has-valid-targets? should return false
- Test: test_recoup_requires_valid_target

**Edge Case: Target Removed Before Resolution**
- If target is exiled (e.g., opponent's Tormod's Crypt effect) before Recoup resolves
- Recoup fizzles: no effect, goes to graveyard (not exile, since not flashback cast)
- Test: test_recoup_fizzles_if_target_removed

**Edge Case: Recoup Targeting Itself**
- Recoup is a sorcery, but it's on the stack when casting, not in graveyard
- Cannot target itself - targeting happens at cast time when Recoup not in graveyard yet
- This is handled naturally by the targeting system - no special case needed

**Edge Case: Granted Flashback on Instant vs Sorcery**
- Recoup only targets sorceries (:card/types #{:sorcery})
- Instants should NOT be valid targets
- Test: test_recoup_does_not_target_instants

**Reference Pattern:**
- deep_analysis.cljs for card structure
- deep_analysis_test.cljs for test patterns and helpers

## Anti-patterns

- ❌ Do NOT hardcode mana costs - use target's :card/mana-cost
- ❌ Do NOT skip targeting validation - use targeting/all-targets-legal?
- ❌ Do NOT duplicate grant system code - use grants/add-grant
- ❌ Do NOT forget :target/controller :self - only own graveyard
- ❌ Do NOT test only happy path - fizzle and edge cases are critical
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Extract Shared Test Helpers and Migrate All Test Files
design:
## Goal
Create src/test/fizzle/test_helpers.cljs with all commonly duplicated test helpers, then migrate all test files to use the shared namespace. Zero remaining helper duplication when complete.

## Effort Estimate
8-12 hours (2-3 hours creating shared namespace, 6-9 hours mechanical migration across 29+ files)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns and resolve signature conflicts

**Reference files:**
- src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs — uses db/init.cljs init-game-state, good model
- src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs — exemplar card test with local helpers
- src/test/fizzle/cards/dark_ritual_test.cljs — typical card test with standard helpers
- src/test/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis_test.cljs — uses DIFFERENT add-card-to-zone signature (CRITICAL)

**CRITICAL: Signature conflicts to resolve before coding:**

add-card-to-zone has TWO variants:
- Standard (75% of files): [db card-id zone player-id] returns [@conn obj-id]
- deep_analysis/ray_of_revelation variant: [db player-id card zone] returns [obj-id @conn]
- DECISION: Use standard signature [db card-id zone player-id] returning [db obj-id].
- deep_analysis/ray_of_revelation tests must be updated to match standard signature.
- Note: their variant also adds :object/position 0 for library zone — handle this in add-card-to-zone when zone is :library.

add-cards-to-library has TWO patterns:
- Pattern A (9 files): [db card-ids player-id] takes vector of card keywords, returns [db object-ids]
- Pattern B (2 files: brain_freeze, effects): [db player-id n] takes integer count, uses :dark-ritual, returns db only
- DECISION: Support Pattern A as the primary (more flexible). Files using Pattern B either switch to Pattern A or keep a local helper.

Return value convention:
- ALL shared helpers return [db result] tuples (db first, consistent with Datascript patterns)
- Tests expecting [obj-id db] must be updated to [db obj-id]

### 2. Create test_helpers.cljs with these helpers:

File: src/test/fizzle/test_helpers.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.test-helpers

Requires:
- [datascript.core :as d]
- [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
- [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
- [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards]

Functions:

(create-test-db) and (create-test-db opts)
- No-arg version: all-zero mana pool, standard player (:player-1, 20 life, storm 0, land-plays 1)
- opts map supports: {:mana {:blue 1 :black 3} :life 20 :storm-count 0 :land-plays 1}
- Loads all card defs from cards/all-cards
- Creates game entity with turn 1, phase :main1, active-player = player-1
- Returns immutable db value (NOT conn)

(add-card-to-zone db card-id zone owner)
- card-id: keyword like :dark-ritual
- zone: keyword like :hand, :battlefield, :graveyard, :library, :exile
- owner: keyword like :player-1
- When zone is :library, adds :object/position 0 (top of library)
- Returns [db obj-id] tuple

(add-cards-to-library db card-ids owner)
- card-ids: vector of card-id keywords
- Adds with sequential positions (0 = top, 1, 2, ...)
- Returns [db object-ids] tuple

(add-cards-to-graveyard db card-ids owner)
- card-ids: vector of card-id keywords
- Returns [db object-ids] tuple

(get-zone-count db zone owner)
- Returns integer count of objects in zone for owner

(get-object-zone db obj-id)
- Returns zone keyword for given object

(get-hand-count db owner)
- Shorthand for (get-zone-count db :hand owner)

(add-opponent db)
- Adds :player-2 with name "Opponent", life 20, zero mana, is-opponent true
- Returns updated db

### 3. Write tests for test_helpers.cljs (TDD)

File: src/test/fizzle/test_helpers_test.cljs

Tests:
- create-test-db-returns-valid-db: verify db has player entity, game entity, card definitions loaded. Specific: (q/get-player-eid db :player-1) returns non-nil, game phase is :main1
- create-test-db-with-mana-opts: (create-test-db {:mana {:blue 3}}) sets blue to 3, other colors to 0
- create-test-db-with-life-opts: (create-test-db {:life 10}) sets life to 10
- add-card-to-zone-hand: adds Dark Ritual to hand, verify (get-zone-count db :hand :player-1) is 1, obj-id is UUID
- add-card-to-zone-library-sets-position: adds card to library, verify :object/position is 0
- add-card-to-zone-invalid-card-id: what happens when card-id doesn't exist? Should throw or return nil (decide and document)
- add-cards-to-library-ordering: add [:dark-ritual :cabal-ritual :lions-eye-diamond], verify positions 0,1,2 respectively
- add-cards-to-library-empty: add empty vector, returns [db []] with db unchanged
- add-cards-to-graveyard-multiple: add 3 cards, verify graveyard count is 3
- get-zone-count-empty: no cards added, count is 0
- add-opponent-creates-player-2: verify :player-2 entity exists with is-opponent true

### 4. Migrate all 29 create-test-db files (mechanical extraction)

Complete list of files to migrate:
1. src/test/fizzle/cards/basic_lands_test.cljs
2. src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs
3. src/test/fizzle/cards/cabal_ritual_test.cljs
4. src/test/fizzle/cards/careful_study_test.cljs (uses custom mana - switch to opts)
5. src/test/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs
6. src/test/fizzle/cards/city_of_traitors_test.cljs
7. src/test/fizzle/cards/dark_ritual_test.cljs
8. src/test/fizzle/cards/flash_of_insight_test.cljs
9. src/test/fizzle/cards/ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs
10. src/test/fizzle/cards/intuition_test.cljs (uses custom mana - switch to opts)
11. src/test/fizzle/cards/led_test.cljs
12. src/test/fizzle/cards/lotus_petal_test.cljs
13. src/test/fizzle/cards/mental_note_test.cljs
14. src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs
15. src/test/fizzle/cards/opt_test.cljs
16. src/test/fizzle/cards/orims_chant_test.cljs
17. src/test/fizzle/cards/polluted_delta_test.cljs
18. src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs
19. src/test/fizzle/cards/underground_river_test.cljs
20. src/test/fizzle/db/queries_test.cljs
21. src/test/fizzle/engine/cob_event_trigger_test.cljs
22. src/test/fizzle/engine/trigger_db_integration_test.cljs
23. src/test/fizzle/events/integration/mana_allocation_test.cljs
24. src/test/fizzle/events/pile_choice_test.cljs
25. src/test/fizzle/events/play_land_test.cljs
26. src/test/fizzle/events/selection/costs_allocation_test.cljs
27. src/test/fizzle/events/selection/storm_split_test.cljs
28. src/test/fizzle/events/tap_land_test.cljs
29. src/test/fizzle/events/tutor_multi_select_test.cljs

For each file:
a. Add [fizzle.test-helpers :as th] to requires
b. Remove local create-test-db, add-card-to-zone, add-cards-to-library, add-cards-to-graveyard, get-zone-count, get-object-zone, get-hand-count, add-opponent definitions
c. Replace calls: create-test-db -> th/create-test-db, add-card-to-zone -> th/add-card-to-zone, etc.
d. For files with custom mana (careful_study, intuition): change (create-test-db) to (th/create-test-db {:mana {:blue 1}})
e. For files with reversed return tuple order (deep_analysis, ray_of_revelation): update destructuring from [obj-id db] to [db obj-id]
f. Remove zone-specific helpers (add-artifact-to-battlefield, add-land-to-battlefield) — replace with (th/add-card-to-zone db card-id :battlefield player-id)
g. Preserve any file-specific helpers that are NOT duplicated elsewhere (e.g., helpers that set up specific game scenarios)
h. Run make validate after each batch of 5 files

### 5. Handle files using init-game-state (32 files)

These 32 files use fizzle.db.init/init-game-state instead of create-test-db. They are NOT migrated in this task because:
- init-game-state serves a different purpose (minimal engine-level setup)
- These files are mainly engine/* and events/* tests that don't need card definitions
- Consolidating init-game-state into test_helpers would be a SEPARATE decision

DO NOT touch these files. They remain as-is.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] src/test/fizzle/test_helpers.cljs exists with all 8 listed helpers
- [ ] src/test/fizzle/test_helpers_test.cljs exists with 11+ tests for helper behavior
- [ ] Zero test files define their own create-test-db (verify: grep -r "defn create-test-db" src/test/ returns 0 results)
- [ ] Zero test files define their own add-card-to-zone (verify: grep -r "defn add-card-to-zone" src/test/ returns 0 results, excluding test_helpers.cljs)
- [ ] Zero test files define their own add-cards-to-library (verify: grep same pattern)
- [ ] Zero test files define their own add-cards-to-graveyard (verify: grep same pattern)
- [ ] All return types consistent: [db result] tuple convention
- [ ] All existing tests pass identically (make validate green)
- [ ] No lint or format warnings (make lint && make fmt-check)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Files with custom mana in create-test-db**
- careful_study_test.cljs and intuition_test.cljs initialize with non-zero mana
- MUST use opts parameter: (th/create-test-db {:mana {:blue 1}})
- Verify these tests still pass with the parameterized version

**Edge Case: Return tuple order differences**
- deep_analysis_test.cljs and ray_of_revelation_test.cljs expect [obj-id db]
- Shared helper returns [db obj-id] — MUST update destructuring in these files
- Search for all let bindings that destructure add-card-to-zone results and verify order

**Edge Case: Zone-specific helpers**
- add-artifact-to-battlefield and add-land-to-battlefield are just add-card-to-zone with hardcoded :battlefield
- Replace with (th/add-card-to-zone db card-id :battlefield player-id)
- May need to update variable names in let bindings

**Edge Case: Library position handling**
- Standard add-card-to-zone does NOT set position for non-library zones
- deep_analysis variant DOES set position 0 for library zone
- Shared version MUST set :object/position 0 when zone is :library

**Edge Case: Invalid card-id passed to add-card-to-zone**
- If card-id doesn't match any card definition, d/q returns nil
- Shared helper should throw informative error: "Card not found: :invalid-card"
- Prevents silent nil entity creation that causes confusing test failures downstream

**Performance: Batch migration strategy**
- Migrate in batches of ~5 files, run make validate between batches
- If a batch fails, narrow to individual file to find the issue
- Keep git commits per batch so failures can be bisected

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in shared helpers (helpers must be card-agnostic)
- ❌ NO breaking existing test assertions during migration (pure extraction)
- ❌ NO changing test logic — only change helper imports and call sites
- ❌ NO mutable conn leaking from helpers (return immutable db values, not connections)
- ❌ NO hard-coded card IDs in shared helpers (always pass as parameters)
- ❌ NO silently returning nil when card-id is invalid (throw with message)
close_reason: Superseded: epic restructured from wave-based to linear execution model

title: Fill test coverage gaps (queries, cards, subscriptions)
description:
## Problem
Significant test coverage gaps exist, especially in foundational layers. Many recent bug fixes went in without regression tests.

## Critical Gaps (HIGH)
- Database queries: 0 tests for 19 query functions (get-hand, get-object, get-player-eid, stack-empty?, get-max-hand-size, etc.)
- Subscriptions: completely untested
- remove-object function: added in commit 46a281f with no test

## Untested Cards (9/25)
- Brain Freeze (the win condition!)
- Dark Ritual (foundation card)
- Cabal Ritual (threshold mechanic)
- City of Brass, Gemstone Mine, Ill-Gotten Gains
- 3 others

## Bug Fixes Without Regression Tests
Recent commits fixed selection clearing, storm copies, sorcery speed, peek-and-select. The cleanup step commit had good tests, but earlier fixes are uncovered.

## Success Criteria
- queries_test.cljs exists covering all 19 query functions
- Brain Freeze and Dark Ritual have integration tests
- remove-object has a unit test
- Regression tests for recent bug fixes (storm copies, sorcery speed, selection clearing)
- All new tests pass
close_reason: All coverage gaps filled. 4 tasks completed: queries_test (51 tests), remove-object + Dark/Cabal Ritual (14 tests), Brain Freeze + storm regression (10 tests), Underground River + IGG (10 tests). Total: 826 tests, 1826 assertions. All epic success criteria met.

design:

## Implementation Steps

4. GREEN: Integrate with lose-life effect
