title: Task 2: Migrate object-target modals — merge 2 into 1 parameterized function
design:
## Goal
Migrate ability-object-target-modal and cast-time-object-target-modal from core.cljs into views/modals.cljs, merging them into 1 parameterized function. This is the second-biggest deduplication win (2 near-identical ~78-line functions).

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Completed Task 1 (fizzle-69a): Created views/modals.cljs with shared components (overlay-style, container-style, header-style, modal-wrapper, confirm-button, cancel-button, player-target-button) and parameterized player-target-modal. Core.cljs went from 1595 to 1428 lines.

Learning: confirm events passed as pre-resolved keywords from core.cljs work cleanly. Only need to require namespaces for events dispatched directly from modals.cljs.

## Implementation

### 1. Study the 2 object-target modals
- core.cljs:445-522 — ability-object-target-modal
- core.cljs:525-602 — cast-time-object-target-modal

Differences (everything else identical):
| Aspect | ability-object-target | cast-time-object-target |
|--------|----------------------|------------------------|
| Default zone | :battlefield | :graveyard |
| Instruction (selected) | "1 target selected" | "1 card selected" |
| Instruction (unselected) | "Select a target" | "Select a card" |
| Select event | ::ability-events/select-ability-object-target | ::selection-events/select-cast-time-object-target |
| Confirm event | ::ability-events/confirm-ability-target | ::selection-events/confirm-cast-time-target |
| Header margin | "0 0 8px 0" | "0 0 8px 0" (same) |

### 2. Create object-target-card-view helper in modals.cljs
Inline card rendering is duplicated in both modals. Extract as:
```clojure
(defn- object-target-card-view
  [obj selected-target select-event]
  (let [card-name (get-in obj [:object/card :card/name])
        object-id (:object/id obj)
        is-selected? (= object-id selected-target)]
    ^{:key object-id}
    [:div {:style {:border (if is-selected? "3px solid #4A9BD9" "2px solid #555")
                   :border-radius "6px"
                   :padding "10px 14px"
                   :cursor "pointer"
                   :background (if is-selected? "#1a3a5a" "#1a1a2a")
                   :color "#eee"
                   :min-width "90px"
                   :text-align "center"
                   :user-select "none"
                   :transition "all 0.1s ease"}
           :on-click #(rf/dispatch [select-event object-id])}
     card-name]))
```

### 3. Create parameterized object-target-modal in modals.cljs
Signature: `[selection cards {:keys [select-event confirm-event default-zone selected-label unselected-label]}]`

```clojure
(defn object-target-modal
  [selection cards {:keys [select-event confirm-event default-zone
                           selected-label unselected-label]}]
  (let [selected-target (:selection/selected-target selection)
        target-req (:selection/target-requirement selection)
        zone-name (name (or (:target/zone target-req) default-zone))
        valid? (some? selected-target)]
    [modal-wrapper {:title (str "Choose target from " zone-name)}
     ;; Instructions
     [:p {:style {:color (if valid? "#5CB85C" "#F0AD4E")
                  :margin "0 0 16px 0"
                  :font-size "14px"}}
      (if valid? selected-label unselected-label)]
     ;; Card grid
     [:div {:style {:display "flex" :flex-wrap "wrap" :gap "10px"
                    :margin-bottom "20px" :min-height "60px"}}
      (if (seq cards)
        (for [obj cards]
          [object-target-card-view obj selected-target select-event])
        [:div {:style {:color "#666"}} "No valid targets"])]
     ;; Confirm button
     [:div {:style {:display "flex" :justify-content "flex-end"}}
      [confirm-button {:label "Confirm"
                       :valid? valid?
                       :on-confirm #(rf/dispatch [confirm-event selected-target])}]]]))
```

NOTE: header-style uses "0 0 8px 0" margin in these modals vs "0 0 16px 0" in header-style constant. Solution: override header margin in modal-wrapper by passing custom title element, OR adjust header-style. Best approach: these modals don't use modal-wrapper's h2 directly — instead render title with custom margin. Use modal-wrapper with title nil and render custom h2.

ACTUALLY: Simpler approach — don't use modal-wrapper for this modal. Use overlay-style and container-style directly. modal-wrapper is a convenience, not a requirement. This preserves exact header margin without special-casing.

### 4. Update core.cljs router
```clojure
;; Was: [ability-object-target-modal selection cards]
(and (= selection-type :ability-targeting) (not targets-player?))
[modals/object-target-modal selection cards
 {:select-event ::ability-events/select-ability-object-target
  :confirm-event ::ability-events/confirm-ability-target
  :default-zone :battlefield
  :selected-label "1 target selected"
  :unselected-label "Select a target"}]

;; Was: [cast-time-object-target-modal selection cards]
(and (= selection-type :cast-time-targeting) (not targets-player?))
[modals/object-target-modal selection cards
 {:select-event ::selection-events/select-cast-time-object-target
  :confirm-event ::selection-events/confirm-cast-time-target
  :default-zone :graveyard
  :selected-label "1 card selected"
  :unselected-label "Select a card"}]
```

### 5. Delete old functions from core.cljs
- Delete ability-object-target-modal (lines 445-522)
- Delete cast-time-object-target-modal (lines 525-602)

### 6. Verify
- `make fmt` then `make validate`

## Success Criteria
- [ ] object-target-modal function in modals.cljs taking parameterized options
- [ ] object-target-card-view helper extracted (eliminates duplicate inline card rendering)
- [ ] core.cljs router calls modals/object-target-modal for both cases with options map
- [ ] core.cljs contains zero object-target modal definitions
- [ ] core.cljs reduced by ~155 more lines (2 modals removed)
- [ ] Header margin "0 0 8px 0" preserved exactly (not changed to "0 0 16px 0")
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format + all 741 tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO changing header-style constant to accommodate these modals (different margin)
- ❌ NO changing event keyword namespaces
- ❌ NO behavioral changes
close_reason: Merged 2 object-target modals into 1 parameterized function in modals.cljs. core.cljs reduced from 1429 to 1278 lines (-151). All 741 tests pass, lint + format clean.

title: Add goldfish opponent draw step between player turns
design:
## Goal
After the player's cleanup phase, before the next turn starts, the goldfish opponent draws a card. If the draw fails (empty library), the loss condition fires naturally through set-loss-condition. The draw appears as a history entry.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code:
   - events/game.cljs:535-569 — phase system (phases vector, next-phase, advance-phase)
   - events/game.cljs — start-turn event (begins a new turn)
   - events/game.cljs — cleanup phase handling (discard to hand size, expire grants)
   - engine/effects.cljs:263-294 — draw effect (already handles empty library → loss condition)
   - Look for how history entries are created (search for :history or ::add-history)

2. Write tests first (TDD)
   - test-opponent-draws-card-on-turn-start: start a new turn → opponent library shrinks by 1, hand grows by 1
   - test-opponent-draw-from-empty-library-sets-loss: opponent has 0 library cards → start new turn → :game/loss-condition :empty-library, :game/winner is player
   - test-opponent-draw-creates-history-entry: start a new turn → history contains opponent draw entry
   - test-opponent-draw-on-first-turn-skipped-or-not: decide if opponent draws on turn 1 (probably yes since player draws on turn 1 already handled separately)
   - test-opponent-partial-library-draws-until-empty: opponent has 1 card → draws it → next turn draw fails → loss

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] Determine integration point: either in start-turn event (before player's untap) or after cleanup (before start-turn dispatch)
   - [ ] Add opponent draw using existing draw effect machinery or direct zone transition
   - [ ] Add history entry for opponent draw
   - [ ] Ensure set-loss-condition fires naturally when opponent draws from empty library
   - [ ] Tests in appropriate test file (game_test.cljs or new opponent_turn_test.cljs)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Opponent library shrinks by 1 each turn (after turn 1 start)
- [ ] Opponent drawing from empty library sets :game/loss-condition :empty-library and :game/winner to player
- [ ] History entry visible for opponent draw
- [ ] Existing turn/phase tests pass unchanged
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations

**Integration point:** The opponent draw should happen between player turns. Two options:
- After cleanup completes, before start-turn — requires modifying the cleanup→start-turn transition
- At the beginning of start-turn, before player's untap step — simpler, just add opponent draw at top of start-turn

**History entry format:** Check how other history entries are structured. The opponent draw should match the existing pattern.

**Draw effect vs direct zone move:** Could use execute-effect with {:effect/type :draw :effect/amount 1 :effect/target :opponent} which naturally handles empty library. Or use direct zone transition if draw effect has unwanted side effects for opponent.

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO checking library size to determine death (use draw effect which naturally triggers set-loss-condition on failure)
- ❌ NO silent opponent draw (must have history entry)
- ❌ NO full opponent turn cycle (just draw, not untap/upkeep/main/etc)
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Make Brain Freeze a targeted spell with player selection at cast time
design:
## Goal
Make Brain Freeze prompt for target player (opponent or self) at cast time, and have the mill effect resolve against the chosen target. This is the foundation for storm copy splitting — without a targeted spell, there's nothing to split.

## Effort Estimate
6-7 hours (study pattern 1h, write tests 2h, card def 30min, wire resolution 1-2h, fix existing tests 1-2h)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing targeted spell pattern (Orim's Chant)

**Card targeting format** (orims_chant.cljs:24-27):
```clojure
:card/targeting [{:target/id :player
                  :target/type :player
                  :target/options #{:any-player}
                  :target/required true}]
```
Key: `:card/targeting` is a VECTOR of target spec maps, not a simple map.

**Effect target pattern** (orims_chant.cljs:31-33):
Effects reference the stored target. For Orim's Chant: `:effect/target :targeted-player`.
For Brain Freeze we use `:effect/target :any-player` because of the pre-resolve path.

**Cast-time flow** (targeting.cljs:72-107, 110-151):
1. `cast-spell-with-targeting` detects `:card/targeting` → builds `:cast-time-targeting` selection
2. Player selects from valid targets (both players shown)
3. `confirm-cast-time-target` casts spell → stores `{:player selected-target}` on stack-item

**Resolution flow for :any-player + stored targets** (resolution.cljs:109-130):
Critical path — `has-stored-player-target` check at line 109-112:
- Detects stored targets with `:player` key + `:any-player` effect
- Pre-resolves ALL effects via `stack/resolve-effect-target` (line 121)
- `resolve-effect-target` (stack.cljs:119-122) converts `:any-player` to stored player EID
- Executes resolved effects directly, moves spell to graveyard
- Does NOT prompt again at resolution (target already stored)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: brain_freeze_test.cljs (extend existing)

**New test cases:**
- test_cast_brain_freeze_triggers_player_target_selection: Cast BF → pending selection has type :cast-time-targeting, valid-targets includes both players. Catches: missing :card/targeting.
- test_brain_freeze_targeting_opponent_mills_opponent: Cast BF → select opponent → resolve → opponent library shrinks by 3, self library unchanged. Catches: target resolution failure.
- test_brain_freeze_targeting_self_mills_self: Cast BF → select self → resolve → self library shrinks by 3, opponent library unchanged. Catches: self-targeting not working (the key new behavior).
- test_brain_freeze_storm_copies_inherit_chosen_target: Cast 2 rituals + BF targeting self → storm copies all mill self. Catches: target inheritance regression.
- test_brain_freeze_storm_copies_inherit_opponent_target: Cast ritual + BF targeting opponent → copy also mills opponent. Catches: regression in default case.
- test_brain_freeze_empty_library_target_no_loss: Target player with 0 cards in library → mill is no-op, no loss condition set. Catches: mill incorrectly triggering loss.

**Existing tests to update:**
- Tests that cast Brain Freeze without targeting selection will break — they need to go through the new cast-time targeting flow (select opponent, then resolve).

### 3. Update Brain Freeze card definition (cards/iggy_pop.cljs:31-44)

FROM:
```clojure
{:card/effects [{:effect/type :mill
                 :effect/amount 3
                 :effect/target :opponent}]}
```

TO:
```clojure
{:card/targeting [{:target/id :player
                   :target/type :player
                   :target/options #{:any-player}
                   :target/required true}]
 :card/effects [{:effect/type :mill
                 :effect/amount 3
                 :effect/target :any-player}]}
```

Key: effect uses `:any-player` (NOT :opponent, NOT nil). This enables the pre-resolve path in resolution.cljs.

### 4. Verify mill effect handler works with resolved target

Mill handler (effects.cljs:111-132) already handles both keywords (:opponent) and player EIDs:
```clojure
(let [target (get effect :effect/target player-id)
      target-player (if (= target :opponent)
                      (q/get-opponent-id db player-id)
                      target)]
```
After `resolve-effect-target` runs, `:effect/target` contains a concrete player EID, so it passes through the else branch directly. No changes needed to mill handler.

### 5. Update existing Brain Freeze tests

All existing tests that cast Brain Freeze will need the targeting flow added:
1. Set up casting
2. Get pending target selection
3. Toggle-select opponent (or self)
4. Confirm selection (auto-confirms for single-select)
5. Then proceed to resolution

Pattern to follow: orims_chant_test.cljs test helpers.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Brain Freeze definition has :card/targeting vector with {:target/id :player ...}
- [ ] Brain Freeze effect uses :effect/target :any-player (not :opponent)
- [ ] Casting Brain Freeze triggers :cast-time-targeting selection type
- [ ] Selecting opponent → mill resolves against opponent's library (3 cards moved to GY)
- [ ] Selecting self → mill resolves against self's library (3 cards moved to GY)
- [ ] Storm copies inherit {:player chosen-eid} target from original stack-item
- [ ] All existing brain_freeze_test.cljs tests updated and passing
- [ ] storm_test.cljs tests still passing
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO :effect/target :opponent on Brain Freeze (broken: defeats purpose of targeting)
- ❌ NO :effect/target nil on Brain Freeze (broken: mill handler defaults to caster, resolve-effect-target won't enrich)
- ❌ NO :effect/target :targeted-player on Brain Freeze (wrong path: that uses the object/targets bridge path, not pre-resolve. :any-player is correct for the pre-resolve path at resolution.cljs:116)
- ❌ NO modifying mill effect handler for this change (unnecessary: handler already supports player EIDs)
- ❌ NO modifying resolve-spell-with-selection for this change (unnecessary: pre-resolve path already handles :any-player + stored targets)
- ❌ NO modifying interactive-effect? for this change (unnecessary: :any-player is already detected, pre-resolve path runs before interactive selection)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Resolution path selection is critical:**
The resolve-spell-with-selection function has 4 branches (resolution.cljs:113-168):
1. has-stored-player-target → pre-resolve path (THIS IS OUR PATH)
2. (seq stored-targets) → object/targets bridge (Orim's Chant path)
3. (nil? selection-idx) → normal resolve (no targeting)
4. :else → pause for selection (resolution-time targeting)

Brain Freeze MUST hit branch 1, not branch 2 or 4. This requires:
- stored-targets has :player key (from confirm-cast-time-target)
- An effect has :effect/target :any-player (from card definition)
- These combine to make has-stored-player-target truthy

**Fizzle checking:**
Pre-resolve path (line 120) checks `targeting/all-targets-legal?` before executing. If target player left the game (impossible in 2-player), effects skip. This is correct behavior.

**Storm copy flow:**
Storm copies go through resolve-spell-with-selection too (as :storm-copy type has object-ref). Each copy has inherited stack-item/targets. So copies also hit the pre-resolve path. No special handling needed.

**Edge case — existing tests:**
Every test that calls cast-spell on Brain Freeze will now get a pending target selection instead of going to stack directly. This is a significant test update — estimate 30-60 min for 10-15 tests that need the targeting flow added.

**Edge case — Brain Freeze in Ill-Gotten Gains:**
If Brain Freeze is returned from graveyard and re-cast, it should still prompt for targeting. Verify this works (should be fine since cast-spell-with-targeting is called on every cast).
close_reason: Brain Freeze updated to targeted spell with :card/targeting and :any-player effect target. Cast-time targeting flow triggers player selection. Pre-resolve path in resolution.cljs now handles copy cease-to-exist. 3 new tests (self-targeting, cast-time targeting trigger, storm copies inherit target) + all existing tests updated to use targeting flow. make validate passes.

title: Task 2: Add scry selection state creation in game.cljs
design:
## Goal
When a spell with scry effect resolves, create the scry selection state so UI can render the selection modal. This connects the effect detection (Task 1) to the UI (future task).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-1gy: :scry effect type exists, selection detection recognizes it.
Now need to create :selection/type :scry state when spell resolves.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing selection state creation
Reference implementations:
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:774-795 - build-tutor-selection
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:977-990 - discard selection state creation
- src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:144-161 - get-top-n-library query (ALREADY EXISTS)
- Pattern: When selection effect detected, build selection state with zone/count/candidates

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create tests in src/test/fizzle/events/scry_selection_test.cljs:

```clojure
;; Test: Scry 1 creates selection state with 1 card
;; Bug caught: Selection state missing or wrong type
(deftest test-build-scry-selection-scry-1
  (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
               (add-library-cards :player-1 [:a :b :c]))
        effect {:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 1}
        result (game/build-scry-selection db :player-1 (random-uuid) effect [])]
    (is (= :scry (:selection/type result)))
    (is (= 1 (count (:selection/cards result))))
    (is (= :player-1 (:selection/player-id result)))))

;; Test: Scry 2 gets correct 2 cards by position
;; Bug caught: Wrong cards selected (not top N by position)
(deftest test-build-scry-selection-scry-2-correct-order
  (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
               (add-library-cards :player-1 [:top :second :third]))
        effect {:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 2}
        result (game/build-scry-selection db :player-1 (random-uuid) effect [])
        card-ids (:selection/cards result)]
    (is (= 2 (count card-ids)))
    ;; Top 2 cards by position should be selected
    ))

;; Test: Library < N returns available cards only
;; Bug caught: Crash or incorrect behavior when library smaller than scry amount
(deftest test-build-scry-selection-library-smaller-than-amount
  (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
               (add-library-cards :player-1 [:only-card]))
        effect {:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 3}
        result (game/build-scry-selection db :player-1 (random-uuid) effect [])]
    (is (= 1 (count (:selection/cards result)))
        "Scry 3 with 1 card in library should scry 1")))

;; Test: Empty library returns nil (no selection needed)
;; Bug caught: Crash on empty library or creating invalid selection
(deftest test-build-scry-selection-empty-library
  (let [db (init-game-state)  ; no library cards
        effect {:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 2}
        result (game/build-scry-selection db :player-1 (random-uuid) effect [])]
    (is (nil? result)
        "Empty library should return nil (no selection needed)")))

;; Test: remaining-effects preserved for Opt pattern
;; Bug caught: Draw effect lost after scry selection
(deftest test-build-scry-selection-preserves-remaining-effects
  (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
               (add-library-cards :player-1 [:a :b]))
        scry-effect {:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 1}
        draw-effect {:effect/type :draw :effect/amount 1}
        result (game/build-scry-selection db :player-1 (random-uuid) scry-effect [draw-effect])]
    (is (= [draw-effect] (:selection/remaining-effects result))
        "Remaining effects must be preserved for execution after scry")))

;; Test: Selection piles initialized empty
;; Bug caught: UI crashes if piles not initialized
(deftest test-build-scry-selection-piles-initialized
  (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
               (add-library-cards :player-1 [:a :b]))
        effect {:effect/type :scry :effect/amount 1}
        result (game/build-scry-selection db :player-1 (random-uuid) effect [])]
    (is (= [] (:selection/top-pile result)))
    (is (= [] (:selection/bottom-pile result)))))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] game.cljs - Add build-scry-selection function after build-tutor-selection (~line 795)
- [ ] game.cljs - Use queries/get-top-n-library (already exists at queries.cljs:144)
- [ ] game.cljs - Handle library < N case (get-top-n-library returns available cards)
- [ ] game.cljs - Handle empty library case (return nil when no cards)
- [ ] game.cljs - Add scry case to resolve-spell selection branching (~line 977-990)
- [ ] game.cljs - Selection state structure:
  ```clojure
  {:selection/type :scry
   :selection/player-id player-id
   :selection/cards [...]  ; object-ids from top of library
   :selection/top-pile []
   :selection/bottom-pile []
   :selection/spell-id object-id
   :selection/remaining-effects [...]}
  ```

### 4. Implementation pattern
```clojure
(defn- build-scry-selection
  "Build pending selection state for a scry effect.
   Reveals top N cards from library for player to arrange.
   Returns nil if library is empty (no selection needed)."
  [db player-id object-id scry-effect effects-after]
  (let [amount (or (:effect/amount scry-effect) 0)
        library-cards (when (pos? amount)
                        (queries/get-top-n-library db player-id amount))]
    (when (seq library-cards)
      {:selection/type :scry
       :selection/player-id player-id
       :selection/cards (vec library-cards)
       :selection/top-pile []
       :selection/bottom-pile []
       :selection/spell-id object-id
       :selection/remaining-effects (vec effects-after)})))
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] build-scry-selection function exists in game.cljs
- [ ] Function uses existing queries/get-top-n-library (not new query)
- [ ] Scry selection state created when scry effect detected in resolve-spell
- [ ] Selection state includes correct cards from library top (verified by test)
- [ ] Library < N handled: scry available cards only (verified by test)
- [ ] Empty library handled: returns nil, no crash (verified by test)
- [ ] remaining-effects preserved for Opt pattern (verified by test)
- [ ] 6+ unit tests pass in scry_selection_test.cljs
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Library smaller than scry amount**
- Scry 3 with 2 cards in library = scry 2
- get-top-n-library already handles this (returns available cards)
- Verify with test

**Edge Case: Empty library**
- No selection needed, proceed to remaining effects
- Return nil from build-scry-selection
- Caller must handle nil (check resolve-spell branching)

**Edge Case: Missing or invalid :effect/amount**
- Default to 0 with `(or (:effect/amount scry-effect) 0)`
- amount <= 0 should not query library, return nil immediately

**Edge Case: Scry followed by draw (Opt pattern)**
- remaining-effects must be preserved and executed after scry confirms
- Critical for Opt: scry 1, then draw 1

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow build-tutor-selection pattern at game.cljs:774-795
- Use existing get-top-n-library query at queries.cljs:144-161

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO modifying library during selection creation (just reveal, don't move)
- ❌ NO hardcoding card count (use :effect/amount)
- ❌ NO crashing on empty library (return nil gracefully)
- ❌ NO creating new query when get-top-n-library exists
- ❌ NO assuming :effect/amount is always present (use or with default)
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Clean up app-db/game-db function signature boundary
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- No function in events/ takes both app-db AND game-db as parameters
- Helper functions in events/ that operate on app-db use `app-db` as parameter name (not `db`)
- Selection builder functions in events/ that operate on game-db use `game-db` as parameter name (not `db`)
- rf/reg-event-db handlers keep `db` as parameter name (re-frame convention)
- Engine/ and db/queries/ functions remain unchanged (already clean, unambiguous in context)
- All tests pass after changes

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] `initiate-cast-with-mode` takes only app-db (not both app-db and game-db)
- [ ] All event helper functions taking app-db use `app-db` param name
- [ ] All selection builder functions taking game-db use `game-db` param name
- [ ] No function in events/ takes both app-db and game-db as parameters
- [ ] All 872+ tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO functions taking both app-db AND game-db as separate parameters (clarity: unclear contract, callers must juggle two db references)
- NO renaming rf/reg-event-db handler params from `db` (convention: re-frame idiom, universally understood)
- NO renaming engine/ or db/queries/ `db` params (scope: unambiguous in isolated namespace context, unnecessary churn)
- NO adding docstrings/comments to unchanged functions (scope: only touch what we're fixing)

## Approach
File-by-file refactoring with tests between each file. Fix game.cljs first (highest severity: dual-param anti-pattern), then selection.cljs (builder naming), then verify abilities.cljs (already clean per investigation).

Changes are purely mechanical renames and one parameter removal — no behavioral changes.

## Architecture
Three categories of changes in events/ namespace only:

1. **Fix dual-parameter anti-pattern**: `initiate-cast-with-mode` in game.cljs — remove `game-db` param, extract from app-db internally. Update 2 call sites.
2. **Rename app-db helpers**: In game.cljs, helpers like `cast-spell-handler` — rename `db` to `app-db`.
3. **Rename game-db builders**: In selection.cljs, `build-*-selection` functions — rename `db` to `game-db`.

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Functions in events/ inconsistently handle the app-db vs game-db boundary. The worst case (`initiate-cast-with-mode`) takes both as separate parameters, creating an unclear contract. Other functions use the generic name `db` for both app-db and game-db, making it error-prone when refactoring.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- events/game.cljs:164-203 - `initiate-cast-with-mode` takes `[app-db game-db player-id object-id mode]` — only function taking both
- events/game.cljs:206-230 - `cast-spell-handler` uses `[db]` but is actually app-db
- events/selection.cljs - build-*-selection functions use `[db ...]` but all take game-db
- events/abilities.cljs - already clean, correct patterns throughout
- engine/* and db/queries.cljs - all consistently take game-db as `db`, unambiguous

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Events-only scoped rename (file-by-file) ✓
**What it is:** Rename parameters only in events/ namespace. Fix dual-param function. Leave engine/ and db/ alone. Process file-by-file with tests between each.

**Pros:**
- Minimal blast radius, only touches the inconsistent code
- Tests between files catches regressions early
- engine/ stays idiomatic (db is unambiguous there)

**Cons:**
- db means game-db in engine/ but app-db in event handlers — different conventions in different namespaces

**Chosen because:** The inconsistency only exists in events/ where both db types coexist. In engine/, db is always game-db and is unambiguous.

#### 2. Global rename everything to explicit names ❌
**What it is:** Rename db to game-db everywhere (engine/, db/queries/, events/) for full consistency.

**Why we looked at this:** Maximum explicitness would prevent any future confusion.

**Cons:**
- Massive churn across all engine files and tests
- engine/ is already unambiguous — no practical benefit
- Risk of introducing bugs in well-tested code

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Unnecessary churn in namespaces where the naming is already unambiguous. Risk outweighs benefit.

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** engine/ functions start being called directly from contexts where both db types exist.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- events/game.cljs parameter renames and dual-param fix
- events/selection.cljs builder parameter renames
- events/abilities.cljs verification (likely no changes needed)

**Out of scope:**
- engine/ parameter names (unambiguous)
- db/queries.cljs parameter names (unambiguous)
- Adding docstrings to unchanged functions

### Open Questions
- Exact count of selection builder functions needing rename (will enumerate during first task)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Rename rf/reg-event-db handler params? | Keep `db` (re-frame convention) | Don't touch handler signatures |
| Rename event helper app-db params? | Yes, `db` → `app-db` | Disambiguates from game-db in same namespace |
| Rename selection builder game-db params? | Yes, `db` → `game-db` | Makes Datascript db contract explicit |
| Rename engine/ params? | Leave as `db` | Unambiguous in isolated context, avoids churn |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### App-DB vs Game-DB Boundary Investigation
**Question explored:** Where are the inconsistencies in parameter naming?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/game.cljs - found 1 dual-param function, ~3 helpers with vague naming
- events/selection.cljs - found 5+ builder functions with vague naming
- events/abilities.cljs - already clean
- engine/* - all consistently use `db` for game-db

**Conclusion:** Inconsistencies are isolated to events/ namespace helper functions

### Dead-End Paths
None — the scope was clear from investigation.

### Open Concerns Raised
None — straightforward refactoring with clear mechanical changes.
close_reason: All tasks complete. game.cljs dual-param fixed + app-db renames (Task 1), selection.cljs 17 functions renamed db→game-db (Task 2). make validate passes: 872 tests, 0 failures.

title: Feature: Add Underground River card
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Underground River card defined with three mana abilities
- Colorless mana ability has no damage
- Blue mana ability deals 1 damage to controller
- Black mana ability deals 1 damage to controller
- Card added to Iggy Pop deck list
- Damage uses ability/effects pattern (NOT trigger pattern)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Card definition follows Cephalid Coliseum pain land pattern
- [ ] Colorless tap produces {:colorless 1} with no damage
- [ ] Blue tap produces {:blue 1} with 1 damage to controller
- [ ] Black tap produces {:black 1} with 1 damage to controller
- [ ] Card included in iggy-pop-deck vector
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO trigger-based damage (consistency: must use ability/effects like Cephalid Coliseum, not triggers like City of Brass)
- ❌ NO separate card file (consistency: deck cards go in iggy_pop.cljs)
- ❌ NO modifying engine code (scope: this is data-only, engine already supports pattern)

## Approach
Add Underground River as a pure data definition in iggy_pop.cljs following the established Cephalid Coliseum pattern. Three mana abilities: colorless (no damage), blue (1 damage), black (1 damage). Include in deck list.

## Architecture
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Add card definition and deck entry

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Iggy Pop deck needs Underground River for blue/black mana fixing. Pain lands are core to the mana base.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- cards/cephalid_coliseum.cljs:27-36 - Pain land pattern using ability/effects for damage
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:57-79 - City of Brass uses trigger pattern (different behavior)
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:136-167 - Basic land mana ability pattern

**Key insight:** Cephalid Coliseum's pattern is correct for pain lands - damage only when mana ability activated, not when tapped by other effects.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Ability/effects pattern (Cephalid Coliseum style) ✓
**What it is:** Damage as part of mana ability via :ability/effects
**Chosen because:** Matches existing pain land pattern, damage only fires on mana activation

#### 2. Trigger pattern (City of Brass style) ❌
**What it is:** :trigger/type :becomes-tapped fires damage
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Wrong behavior - would deal damage when tapped by any effect, not just mana ability
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** MTG rules change for pain lands

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** Card definition, deck inclusion
**Out of scope:** Engine changes, new effect types

## Design Discovery

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Add to Iggy Pop deck? | Yes | Include in deck list, put in iggy_pop.cljs |

### Research Deep-Dives
#### Pain Land Pattern
**Sources:** cephalid_coliseum.cljs, iggy_pop.cljs (City of Brass)
**Conclusion:** Use ability/effects, not triggers
notes: Bug fix applied: activate-mana-ability now correctly selects the mana ability matching the requested color. Previously it always used the first ability, causing Underground River to only produce colorless.
close_reason: Underground River card implemented with 3 mana abilities. Bug fixed in activate-mana-ability to support multi-ability lands. All tests pass.

title: Task 2: Unify toggle handlers — merge 6 toggle/select handlers into one ::toggle-selection
design:
## Goal
Merge 6 separate toggle/select handlers into one unified `::toggle-selection` handler. After this, all selection types use the same handler for user clicks.

## Current Handlers to Merge
1. `::toggle-selection` (selection.cljs:1017) — set-based for tutor/discard/graveyard-return
2. `::toggle-peek-card-selection` (selection.cljs:~1109) — set-based for peek-and-select
3. `::toggle-exile-card-selection` (selection.cljs:~1407) — set-based for exile-cards-cost
4. `::select-player-target` (selection.cljs:1341) — now set-based (was scalar, migrated in Phase 1)
5. `::select-cast-time-object-target` (selection.cljs:1528) — now set-based (migrated in Phase 1)
6. `::select-ability-object-target` (abilities.cljs:281) — now set-based (migrated in Phase 1)

## Unified Handler Logic
`::toggle-selection [_ id]`
1. If `:selection/valid-targets` exists, validate id is in set — reject invalid
2. If already selected, deselect (remove from set)
3. If select-count=1, replace selection (`#{id}`) — single-select behavior
4. If under limit, add to selection
5. If at limit, ignore

## Implementation Steps
1. Read all 6 handlers to understand their exact logic
2. Write tests for unified handler with each selection type
3. Implement unified `::toggle-selection` replacing all 6
4. Update modals.cljs dispatches to use `::toggle-selection` everywhere
5. Remove old handlers (5 removed, 1 rewritten)
6. Remove abilities.cljs select handler (selection.cljs handles all toggles)
7. Run make validate

## Key Considerations
- Peek and exile handlers use candidate-ids for validation (not valid-targets)
- Player-target handler writes a player keyword (not object-id)
- Object-target handlers validate against valid-targets set
- Existing ::toggle-selection already handles tutor/discard/graveyard-return

## Anti-Patterns
- NO changing any game behavior (unification only)
- NO breaking tests at any step
- NO circular dependencies (abilities.cljs handler moves to selection.cljs)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Only one `::toggle-selection` handler exists
- [ ] All 5 other toggle handlers removed
- [ ] modals.cljs dispatches `::toggle-selection` for all selection types
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes
close_reason: Closed

title: Add missing P0 corner case tests (engine modules)
design:
## Goal
Add tests for P0 corner cases that could cause production bugs in core engine modules.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Corner Cases to Add

### engine/effects (P0 - Core Logic)

| Corner Case | Bug It Catches | Test Name | File |
|-------------|----------------|-----------|------|
| Negative mana amounts in effect | Invalid game state with negative mana | test-add-mana-negative-amount | effects_test.cljs |
| Empty effect map | Crash on missing :effect/type | test-execute-effect-empty-map | effects_test.cljs |

### engine/mana (P0 - Core Logic)

| Corner Case | Bug It Catches | Test Name | File |
|-------------|----------------|-----------|------|
| Large mana pool values | Integer overflow | test-mana-pool-large-values | mana_test.cljs |
| Pay more than available | Negative pool without can-pay? check | test-pay-mana-without-can-pay | mana_test.cljs |

### engine/rules (P0 - Core Logic)

| Corner Case | Bug It Catches | Test Name | File |
|-------------|----------------|-----------|------|
| Cast non-existent object | Crash or wrong behavior | test-cast-spell-invalid-object-id | rules_test.cljs |
| High storm count (20+ copies) | Performance regression, memory | test-storm-high-count | storm_test.cljs |

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Create test-add-mana-negative-amount in effects_test.cljs
- [ ] Create test-execute-effect-empty-map in effects_test.cljs
- [ ] Create test-mana-pool-large-values in mana_test.cljs
- [ ] Create test-pay-mana-without-can-pay in mana_test.cljs
- [ ] Create test-cast-spell-invalid-object-id in rules_test.cljs
- [ ] Create test-storm-high-count in storm_test.cljs
- [ ] Run `make test` after each test addition
- [ ] Run `make validate` at the end

## Test Specifications

### test-add-mana-negative-amount
```clojure
(deftest test-add-mana-negative-amount
  (testing "add-mana with negative value is handled gracefully"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          effect {:effect/type :add-mana
                  :effect/mana {:black -3}}
          db' (effects/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
      ;; Document current behavior - either:
      ;; - Rejects and returns db unchanged, OR
      ;; - Allows (and we should fix this)
      (is (<= 0 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))
          "Mana pool should not go negative from add-mana"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** Invalid game state with negative mana pool

### test-execute-effect-empty-map
```clojure
(deftest test-execute-effect-empty-map
  (testing "execute-effect with empty map returns db unchanged"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          db' (effects/execute-effect db :player-1 {})]
      (is (= db db')
          "Empty effect should be no-op, not crash"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** Crash on missing :effect/type

### test-mana-pool-large-values
```clojure
(deftest test-mana-pool-large-values
  (testing "mana pool handles large values without overflow"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          db' (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:black 1000000})]
      (is (= 1000000 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))
          "Large mana values should be stored correctly"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** Integer overflow in mana arithmetic

### test-pay-mana-without-can-pay
```clojure
(deftest test-pay-mana-without-can-pay
  (testing "pay-mana when pool is insufficient - document behavior"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          ;; Pool starts at 0
          result (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:black 3})]
      ;; Current behavior: returns db with negative pool (no validation)
      ;; This test documents it - if we add validation, update test
      (is (some? result)
          "pay-mana should not crash on insufficient mana"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** Crash when pay-mana called without can-pay? check

### test-cast-spell-invalid-object-id
```clojure
(deftest test-cast-spell-invalid-object-id
  (testing "cast-spell with non-existent object ID"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          fake-id (random-uuid)]
      ;; Should either:
      ;; - Return db unchanged (graceful), OR
      ;; - Throw meaningful error
      ;; Should NOT crash with NPE
      (is (or (= db (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 fake-id))
              (thrown? js/Error (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 fake-id)))
          "Invalid object ID should be handled, not NPE"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** NullPointerException on invalid input

### test-storm-high-count
```clojure
(deftest test-storm-high-count
  (testing "storm with 20+ copies completes without timeout"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 ;; Set storm count to 20
                 (assoc-in [:player :player-1 :player/storm-count] 20)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:blue 1}))
          ;; Add Brain Freeze to hand and cast
          [db' bf-id] (add-card-to-zone db :brain-freeze :hand :player-1)
          db'' (rules/cast-spell db' :player-1 bf-id)]
      ;; Should create 21 copies (original + 20 storm)
      (is (<= 20 (count (filter #(= :stack (:object/zone %))
                                (q/get-all-objects db''))))
          "High storm count should create all copies"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** Performance regression or timeout on high storm counts

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 6 corner case tests written and passing
- [ ] Each test has clear comment stating what bug it catches
- [ ] Tests exercise actual production code paths
- [ ] No tautological assertions (no `some?` as primary assertion)
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] `make validate` passes

## Key Considerations

**Behavior Documentation Tests:**
Some tests document current behavior rather than asserting correctness. This is valuable because:
1. If behavior changes, test fails (regression detection)
2. Forces decision: is current behavior correct?
3. Comments explain intent

**Storm Performance:**
ClojureScript may handle 20+ storm copies fine, but this establishes baseline. If it slows down later, test catches regression.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Writing test that passes immediately (didn't test anything new)
- ❌ Testing mock/helper behavior instead of production code
- ❌ Happy path only (defeats purpose of corner case test)
- ❌ Using `some?` or `!= nil` as primary assertion
- ❌ Catching and swallowing all exceptions (hides real bugs)

title: Implement CSS Grid layout with left primary column and three right reference columns
design:
## Goal
Replace the current Flexbox layout in core.cljs with a CSS Grid layout matching the epic's approved wireframe:
- Left primary column: opponent, battlefield, hand, mana/storm
- Three right columns: stack, graveyard, history (full height, collapsible)
- Phase bar + controls anchored at bottom
- When a right-column zone collapses, remaining columns expand horizontally
- Reserve a grid slot at bottom for future calculator panel

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Epic fizzle-8305 specifies a CSS Grid layout. Current implementation uses Flexbox (core.cljs:25 uses \`flex\`). The \`collapsible-zone\` component exists in views/common.cljs but is currently unused by any zone component. The history sidebar (history.cljs) has its own fixed-width sidebar layout (\`w-[250px] min-w-[250px]\`).

Current core.cljs structure (line 25-39):
- Outer: \`flex min-h-screen\` with main content + history sidebar
- Main: \`flex-1 p-5 overflow-y-auto\` containing all zones stacked vertically
- History: rendered as standalone sidebar with fixed width

## Implementation

### 1. Add CSS Grid utilities to app.css
Add custom CSS after the @theme block in src/css/app.css:

\`\`\`css
/* Game layout grid */
.game-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr auto auto auto;
  grid-template-rows: auto 1fr auto auto auto auto;
  gap: 0;
  min-height: 100vh;
}

/* Right column collapsed state - shrink to header width */
.right-col-collapsed {
  width: 2rem;
  overflow: hidden;
}

/* Right column expanded state */
.right-col-expanded {
  width: 250px;
  min-width: 200px;
}
\`\`\`

Note: Using raw CSS classes rather than Tailwind arbitrary grid utilities because the grid template is complex enough to warrant named classes. Tailwind utilities (\`p-5\`, \`overflow-y-auto\`, etc.) still used for everything else.

### 2. Update core.cljs app function
Replace the flex layout with the CSS Grid container. The grid parent assigns each zone to its position. Zone components remain layout-agnostic (they render content only, no grid classes inside).

Structure:
\`\`\`clojure
(defn app []
  [:div {:class "game-grid bg-surface font-mono text-text"}
   ;; Row 1: Header spanning full width
   [:div {:class "col-span-full p-3"}
    [:h1 {:class "text-text"} "Fizzle"]]

   ;; Left column zones (col 1, stacked in rows)
   [:div {:class "p-5 overflow-y-auto row-span-4"}
    [phase-bar/phase-bar-view]
    [opponent/life-view]
    [battlefield/battlefield-view]
    [hand/hand-view]
    [controls/controls-view]
    [mana-pool/mana-pool-view]
    [mana-pool/storm-count-view]]

   ;; Right col 1: Stack (col 2, spans rows 2-5)
   [collapsible-right-column "Stack" stack-collapsed? [stack/stack-view]]

   ;; Right col 2: Graveyard (col 3, spans rows 2-5)
   [collapsible-right-column "Graveyard" gy-collapsed? [graveyard/graveyard-view]]

   ;; Right col 3: History (col 4, spans rows 2-5)
   [collapsible-right-column "History" history-collapsed? [history/history-sidebar]]

   ;; Bottom: Phase bar + calculator slot
   [:div {:class "col-span-full"}]  ;; Reserved for calculator panel

   ;; Modals (overlay, not in grid flow)
   [modals/selection-modal]
   [modals/mode-selector-modal]])
\`\`\`

### 3. Create collapsible-right-column component in core.cljs
A thin wrapper that manages collapse state and applies the grid-appropriate CSS classes. This lives in core.cljs (the grid parent), NOT in common.cljs, because it has grid layout knowledge.

\`\`\`clojure
(defn- collapsible-right-column
  "A right-column zone that can collapse to a narrow header.
   title: display name, collapsed?: reagent atom, children: zone content"
  [title collapsed? & children]
  [:div {:class (str "border-l border-border overflow-y-auto "
                     (if @collapsed? "right-col-collapsed" "right-col-expanded"))}
   [:div {:class "flex items-center cursor-pointer select-none gap-2 p-2"
          :on-click #(swap! collapsed? not)}
    [:span {:class "text-text-label text-xs font-bold"} title]
    [:span {:class "text-text-dim text-xs"}
     (if @collapsed? "▶" "▼")]]
   (when-not @collapsed?
     (into [:div {:class "p-2"}] children))])
\`\`\`

Define three reagent atoms at top of core.cljs for collapse state:
\`\`\`clojure
(defonce stack-collapsed? (r/atom false))
(defonce gy-collapsed? (r/atom false))
(defonce history-collapsed? (r/atom false))
\`\`\`

Requires adding \`[reagent.core :as r]\` to core.cljs requires.

### 4. Update history.cljs history-sidebar
Remove the fixed-width sidebar wrapper from history-sidebar. Currently it wraps itself in a fixed-width div. The grid parent now controls width. history-sidebar should just render its content (step controls, fork controls, log entries) without the outer sizing container.

Check the history-sidebar function and strip any \`w-[250px]\`, \`min-w-[250px]\`, \`h-screen\`, or similar sizing classes from its outermost container. The collapsible-right-column wrapper in core.cljs provides the container.

### 5. Verify collapsible-zone in common.cljs
The existing \`collapsible-zone\` in common.cljs may still be useful for within-zone collapsible sections (e.g., fork controls inside history). It is NOT the same as the grid-level \`collapsible-right-column\`. Leave it as-is.

### 6. Run make validate
\`\`\`bash
make validate
\`\`\`
Confirm 0 lint errors, 0 format issues, all tests pass (currently 996 tests, 2262 assertions).

## Success Criteria
- [ ] CSS Grid layout renders with left primary column and three right columns
- [ ] Left column contains: phase bar, opponent, battlefield, hand, controls, mana/storm stacked vertically
- [ ] Three right columns (stack, graveyard, history) appear side by side, spanning full height
- [ ] Clicking collapse toggle on a right column shrinks it to ~2rem wide header-only
- [ ] When a right column collapses, remaining columns stay at their width (auto sizing)
- [ ] Phase bar + controls visible at all times (not scrolled off screen)
- [ ] Calculator slot div exists at bottom (empty, reserved for future)
- [ ] Zone components (battlefield.cljs, hand.cljs, etc.) contain zero grid-related CSS classes — verify: grep for \`grid-\`, \`col-\`, \`row-\` in src/main/fizzle/views/ should only find hits in core.cljs
- [ ] make validate passes (0 failures, 0 errors, 0 lint warnings, 0 format issues)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO grid positioning classes inside zone component files (battlefield.cljs, hand.cljs, etc.) — only the grid parent in core.cljs assigns grid positions
- ❌ NO floating/draggable panels — zones fill grid cells (per epic anti-pattern)
- ❌ NO hardcoded pixel widths for the left column — use \`1fr\` to fill available space
- ❌ NO inline :style maps — all styling via Tailwind classes or custom CSS classes in app.css
- ❌ NO JavaScript-based layout calculations — pure CSS Grid handles all layout

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: All Three Right Columns Collapsed**
Left column should naturally fill available space because it uses \`1fr\`. When all right columns collapse to 2rem each, the left column expands. No special handling needed — CSS Grid \`1fr\` handles this automatically.

**Edge Case: Very Tall Content in Right Columns**
Right columns use \`overflow-y-auto\` so tall content scrolls within the column. The grid row height is determined by the left column content. Right columns should not push the grid taller.

**Edge Case: Narrow Desktop Window (< 1280px)**
The grid uses \`1fr auto auto auto\` — right columns have fixed widths (250px expanded, 2rem collapsed), left column gets remaining space. At very narrow widths, left column may get squeezed. Add \`min-width: 400px\` to the left column to prevent content crush. This is desktop-first; mobile layout is explicitly out of scope (deferred).

**History Sidebar Migration**
history.cljs currently has its own outer container with fixed sizing. When moving to grid, the outer fixed-width wrapper must be removed or the grid parent's width control will conflict. Check \`history-sidebar\` function carefully for any sizing that needs removal.

**Collapse State Persistence**
Using \`defonce\` reagent atoms means collapse state survives hot-reload but resets on page refresh. This is acceptable for now — state persistence is a future concern.

**Existing collapsible-zone vs new collapsible-right-column**
These are different components for different purposes:
- \`common/collapsible-zone\`: For collapsing sections WITHIN a zone (e.g., fork controls inside history)
- \`core/collapsible-right-column\`: For collapsing entire grid columns (grid-aware)
Do not merge or refactor these — they serve different abstraction levels.
close_reason: Closed

title: Refactor activate-mana-ability to use generic engine
design:
## Goal
Remove hardcoded card ID checks from activate-mana-ability and use the generic engine modules (abilities.cljs, triggers.cljs, state_based.cljs).

## Effort Estimate
4 hours

## File Path
src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

## Current Implementation (lines 355-394)

```clojure
(defn activate-mana-ability
  [db player-id object-id mana-color]
  (let [obj (queries/get-object db object-id)
        player-eid (queries/get-player-eid db player-id)]
    (if (and obj
             player-eid
             (= (:object/zone obj) :battlefield)
             (= (:db/id (:object/controller obj)) player-eid)
             (not (:object/tapped obj)))
      (let [card (:object/card obj)
            card-id (:card/id card)  ;; <-- VIOLATION: reading card-id
            db-after-tap (-> db
                             (tap-permanent object-id)
                             (mana/add-mana player-id {mana-color 1}))]
        ;; City of Brass deals 1 damage to controller when tapped
        (if (= card-id :city-of-brass)  ;; <-- VIOLATION: hardcoded card check
          (effects/execute-effect db-after-tap player-id
                                  {:effect/type :lose-life  ;; <-- VIOLATION: should be :deal-damage via trigger
                                   :effect/amount 1
                                   :effect/target player-id})
          db-after-tap))
      db)))
```

## New Implementation

```clojure
(defn activate-mana-ability
  "Activate a mana ability on a land.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     player-id - The player activating the ability
     object-id - The land to tap
     mana-color - The color of mana to produce (:white :blue :black :red :green)
   
   Flow:
     1. Find mana ability from card data
     2. Check can-activate? via abilities module
     3. Pay costs via abilities module (includes tap)
     4. Add chosen mana to pool
     5. Fire matching triggers (e.g., City of Brass :becomes-tapped)
     6. Check state-based actions (e.g., Gemstone Mine sacrifice)
   
   Returns unchanged db if any step fails."
  [db player-id object-id mana-color]
  (let [obj (queries/get-object db object-id)
        player-eid (queries/get-player-eid db player-id)]
    ;; Basic validation
    (if (and obj
             player-eid
             (= (:object/zone obj) :battlefield)
             (= (:db/id (:object/controller obj)) player-eid))
      ;; Find mana ability from card data
      (let [card (:object/card obj)
            abilities (:card/abilities card)
            mana-ability (first (filter #(= (:ability/type %) :mana) abilities))]
        (if (and mana-ability
                 (abilities/can-activate? db object-id mana-ability))
          ;; Execute ability
          (let [db-before-tap db
                ;; Step 1: Pay costs (tap, remove counters, etc.)
                db-after-costs (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id (:ability/cost mana-ability))]
            (when db-after-costs
              (let [;; Step 2: Add mana (mana-color is player's choice for {:any 1})
                    db-after-mana (mana/add-mana db-after-costs player-id {mana-color 1})
                    ;; Step 3: Fire matching triggers (City of Brass damage)
                    db-after-triggers (triggers/fire-matching-triggers db-before-tap db-after-mana)
                    ;; Step 4: Check state-based actions (Gemstone Mine sacrifice)
                    db-after-sbas (state-based/check-and-execute-sbas db-after-triggers)]
                db-after-sbas)))
          db))
      db)))
```

## Changes Required

### 1. Add require for state-based module
```clojure
(ns fizzle.events.game
  (:require
    ...
    [fizzle.engine.abilities :as abilities]  ;; NEW
    [fizzle.engine.state-based :as state-based]  ;; NEW
    ...))
```

### 2. Replace activate-mana-ability function (lines 355-394)
- Remove card-id variable
- Remove (= card-id :city-of-brass) check
- Use abilities/can-activate? for validation
- Use abilities/pay-all-costs for cost payment
- Use triggers/fire-matching-triggers after state change
- Use state-based/check-and-execute-sbas at end

### 3. Update play-land to fire ETB effects
```clojure
(defn play-land
  [db player-id object-id]
  ;; ... existing validation ...
  (if valid?
    (let [db-after-move (-> db
                            (zones/move-to-zone object-id :battlefield)
                            (d/db-with [[:db/add player-eid :player/land-plays-left (dec land-plays)]]))
          ;; NEW: Fire ETB effects
          obj (queries/get-object db-after-move object-id)
          card (:object/card obj)
          etb-effects (:card/etb-effects card)]
      (if (seq etb-effects)
        (reduce (fn [db' effect]
                  (let [;; Resolve :self target to object-id
                        resolved-effect (if (= :self (:effect/target effect))
                                          (assoc effect :effect/target object-id)
                                          effect)]
                    (effects/execute-effect db' player-id resolved-effect)))
                db-after-move
                etb-effects)
        db-after-move))
    db))
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] activate-mana-ability has no references to card-id or :city-of-brass
- [ ] activate-mana-ability has no references to :gemstone-mine
- [ ] `grep -n "city-of-brass\|gemstone-mine" src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs` returns nothing
- [ ] activate-mana-ability uses abilities/can-activate?
- [ ] activate-mana-ability uses abilities/pay-all-costs
- [ ] activate-mana-ability uses triggers/fire-matching-triggers
- [ ] activate-mana-ability uses state-based/check-and-execute-sbas
- [ ] play-land fires :card/etb-effects when land enters
- [ ] City of Brass damage fires via trigger (verified by test)
- [ ] Gemstone Mine counters added on ETB (verified by test)
- [ ] Gemstone Mine counters decremented on activation (verified by test)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO card-id checks in activate-mana-ability
- NO hardcoded card names anywhere in events/game.cljs
- NO bypassing abilities module for cost payment
- NO bypassing triggers module for state change notifications
- NO bypassing state-based module for SBA checks

## Edge Cases Handled
- Object has no mana ability: Return db unchanged
- Ability can't be activated (already tapped, no counters): Return db unchanged
- No ETB effects on card: Skip ETB processing
- No triggers match: fire-matching-triggers returns db unchanged
- No SBAs apply: check-and-execute-sbas returns db unchanged

## Test Updates Needed

### src/test/fizzle/events/tap_land_test.cljs

```clojure
(deftest test-city-of-brass-damage-via-trigger
  (testing "City of Brass damage fires via trigger, not hardcoded"
    ;; Setup: City of Brass on battlefield
    ;; Action: Activate mana ability
    ;; Verify: Player took 1 damage
    ;; Verify: Damage came from trigger resolution, not direct effect
    ))

(deftest test-gemstone-mine-etb-counters
  (testing "Gemstone Mine enters with 3 mining counters"
    ;; Setup: Gemstone Mine in hand
    ;; Action: Play land
    ;; Verify: Object has {:mining 3} counters
    ))

(deftest test-gemstone-mine-counter-cost
  (testing "Gemstone Mine activation removes mining counter"
    ;; Setup: Gemstone Mine on battlefield with 3 counters
    ;; Action: Activate mana ability
    ;; Verify: Object has {:mining 2} counters
    ))

(deftest test-gemstone-mine-sacrifice-at-zero
  (testing "Gemstone Mine sacrificed when counters reach 0"
    ;; Setup: Gemstone Mine on battlefield with 1 counter
    ;; Action: Activate mana ability
    ;; Verify: Object is in graveyard (sacrificed via SBA)
    ))
```

title: Add history sidebar view and integrate into app layout
design:
## Goal
Create views/history.cljs with sidebar components (action log, step controls, fork list) and modify core.cljs layout to render the sidebar alongside the game area in a flex row.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-3xmp: events (history/events.cljs) and subscriptions (subs/history.cljs) are in place.

## Implementation

### 1. Create views/history.cljs

Namespace: fizzle.views.history
Requires: fizzle.history.events, fizzle.subs.history, re-frame.core

**Components:**

a) `history-sidebar` — Top-level sidebar component
   - Container div: fixed width 250px, height 100%, background #1a1a2a, border-left 1px solid #444, padding 12px
   - display flex, flex-direction column (header + controls at top, log fills middle, forks at bottom)
   - Contains: header, step-controls, action-log, fork-list

b) `step-controls` — Back/Forward buttons
   - Subscribe to `::subs/can-step-back?` and `::subs/can-step-forward?`
   - Two buttons: [Back] and [Forward] (or unicode arrows)
   - Enabled style: background #2a6a2a, color #fff, cursor pointer (matches core.cljs:396-405 btn-style pattern)
   - Disabled style: background #222, color #555, cursor default
   - Dispatch `::events/step-back` / `::events/step-forward` on click
   - Flex row with gap 8px, margin-bottom 12px

c) `action-log` — Scrollable list of history entries
   - Subscribe to `::subs/entries` and `::subs/position`
   - Render each entry as div with: "T{turn}: {description}"
   - Current entry (index == position): border-left 3px solid #4A9BD9, background #1a2a3a
   - Entries after position (index > position): opacity 0.4
   - Entries at/before position: normal opacity
   - Container: overflow-y auto, flex 1 (takes remaining vertical space)
   - Font: monospace, 12px, color #eee, padding 4px 8px per entry

d) `fork-list` — Inline list of branches
   - Subscribe to `::subs/forks` and `::subs/current-branch`
   - Always show "main" as first entry (current-branch nil means main is active)
   - Show each fork by :fork/name
   - Current branch: bold text, blue color (#4A9BD9)
   - Other branches: clickable (#ccc), dispatch `::events/switch-branch` with fork-id (or nil for main)
   - Section divider: border-top 1px solid #444, padding-top 8px, margin-top 8px
   - Header: "Branches" in #999, font-size 11px, text-transform uppercase, letter-spacing 1px

### 2. Modify core.cljs app function (lines 423-443)

Current: single [:div] with padding 20px, all panels inside.
Modals use position:fixed (overlay-style in modals.cljs:14) so they float above everything regardless of DOM position.

Change to:
```clojure
(defn app []
  [:div {:style {:display "flex"
                 :min-height "100vh"
                 :background "#0d0d1a"
                 :font-family "monospace"
                 :color "#eee"}}
   [:div {:style {:flex 1 :padding "20px" :overflow-y "auto"}}
    [:h1 ...] [turn-bar] [life-view] ... [stack-view]]
   [history/history-sidebar]
   [modals/selection-modal]
   [modals/mode-selector-modal]])
```

Key changes:
- Outer div gets display:flex, loses padding (moves to inner wrapper)
- New inner div wraps all game panels with flex:1 and padding:20px
- history-sidebar added after game area div
- Modals stay at same level (position:fixed means they work anywhere)

Import: add `[fizzle.views.history :as history]` to core.cljs requires.

### 3. No new tests for views
View components are pure Reagent rendering — tested implicitly via manual browser verification. Underlying events and subscriptions already have 16 tests.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] views/history.cljs exists with history-sidebar, step-controls, action-log, fork-list components
- [ ] Sidebar renders to the right of game area (fixed 250px width)
- [ ] Action log displays entries with T{turn}: {description} format
- [ ] Current entry highlighted with blue left border and brighter background
- [ ] Entries past position are dimmed (opacity 0.4)
- [ ] Step buttons dispatch events and disable at boundaries (correct styling)
- [ ] Fork list shows main + all forks, current branch highlighted blue
- [ ] Clicking a non-current branch dispatches switch-branch
- [ ] core.cljs layout updated to flex row (outer div display:flex)
- [ ] All inline JS styles matching existing conventions (dark theme, monospace, color palette)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO CSS files or Tailwind (inline JS style objects only)
- NO direct app-db reads (use re-frame subscriptions only)
- NO history logic in views (dispatch events only, no calls to history/core)
- NO click-to-jump on action log entries (deferred to Complete UI epic)
- NO turn grouping or collapsible sections (deferred to Complete UI epic)
- NO manual fork creation button (auto-fork only per epic requirement)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Layout change safety**: The core.cljs app function is the root component (lines 423-443). Modals use position:fixed (modals.cljs:14 overlay-style) so moving them in the DOM tree won't affect their rendering. The flex wrapper is safe.

**Empty state**: Before game init, entries will be empty and position will be nil. Action log should render empty (no entries to show). Step buttons will both be disabled (can-step-back? and can-step-forward? return false/nil). Fork list shows just main.

**Overflow**: Action log needs overflow-y:auto to handle long game sessions. The sidebar itself should not overflow — it fills the viewport height.

## Reference Implementations
- Button enabled/disabled pattern: core.cljs:396-405 (btn-style with enabled? param)
- Color palette: #0d0d1a (page bg), #1a1a2a (container bg), #4A9BD9 (blue accent), #eee (text), #999 (dim), #555 (border)
- Modal overlay pattern: views/modals.cljs:14 (position:fixed, z-index:1000)
- Subscription usage: core.cljs:37-46 (hand-view subscribing and rendering)

title: Task 1: Create event bus and trigger registry infrastructure
design:
## Goal
Build the foundational event bus and trigger registry that all phase/card triggers will use.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- src/main/fizzle/engine/state-based.cljs:13-42 - Registry pattern with atom
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs:54-94 - Multimethod dispatch pattern
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs:82-113 - resolve-trigger multimethod (reuse for trigger resolution)

### 2. Create src/main/fizzle/engine/events.cljs
```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.events
  "Event creation helpers for the event-driven trigger system.
   Events are immutable maps describing game state changes.")

;; Event creation helpers
(defn phase-entered-event [phase turn player-id]
  {:event/type :phase-entered
   :event/phase phase
   :event/turn turn
   :event/player player-id})

(defn permanent-tapped-event [object-id controller-id]
  {:event/type :permanent-tapped
   :event/object-id object-id
   :event/controller controller-id})

(defn zone-change-event [object-id from-zone to-zone]
  {:event/type :zone-change
   :event/object-id object-id
   :event/from-zone from-zone
   :event/to-zone to-zone})
```

### 3. Create src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger-registry.cljs
```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.trigger-registry
  "Dynamic trigger registration for event-driven trigger system.
   Triggers register when cards enter active zones, unregister when leaving.")

;; Registry: {trigger-id -> trigger-def}
(defonce registry (atom {}))

(defn register-trigger!
  "Register a trigger. Idempotent - registering same ID overwrites.
   Returns the trigger-id."
  [trigger]
  (let [id (:trigger/id trigger)]
    (swap! registry assoc id trigger)
    id))

(defn unregister-trigger!
  "Remove a trigger by ID. No-op if ID doesn't exist."
  [trigger-id]
  (swap! registry dissoc trigger-id)
  nil)

(defn unregister-by-source!
  "Remove all triggers from a source (when card leaves zone).
   No-op if source has no triggers."
  [source-id]
  (swap! registry (fn [r] (->> r (remove (fn [[_ t]] (= source-id (:trigger/source t)))) (into {}))))
  nil)

(defn get-triggers-for-event
  "Find all registered triggers that match this event type.
   Returns empty seq if no matches."
  [event]
  (->> @registry vals (filter #(= (:event/type event) (:trigger/event-type %)))))

(defn get-all-triggers
  "Return all registered triggers. For debugging/testing."
  []
  (vals @registry))

(defn clear-registry!
  "Remove all triggers. Use in tests and game reset."
  []
  (reset! registry {})
  nil)
```

### 4. Create src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger-dispatch.cljs
```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.trigger-dispatch
  "Event dispatch and trigger matching.
   Handles both immediate (turn-based) and stacked (card) triggers."
  (:require [fizzle.engine.trigger-registry :as registry]
            [fizzle.engine.triggers :as triggers]))

(defn matches-filter?
  "Check if event matches trigger's filter criteria.
   :self in filter value matches trigger's source object.
   Returns true if no filter (matches all events of type)."
  [trigger event]
  (let [f (:trigger/filter trigger)]
    (if (and f (seq f))
      (every? (fn [[k v]]
                (cond
                  (= v :self) (= (get event k) (:trigger/source trigger))
                  :else (= (get event k) v)))
              f)
      true)))

(defn dispatch-event
  "Dispatch event to all matching triggers. Returns new db.
   Turn-based actions (uses-stack? false) execute immediately.
   Card triggers (uses-stack? true) go on stack.
   Order: immediate triggers first, then stacked triggers."
  [db event]
  (let [all-triggers (registry/get-triggers-for-event event)
        matching (filter #(matches-filter? % event) all-triggers)
        ;; Group by uses-stack? - note: false = immediate, true = stacked
        {stacked true, immediate false} (group-by #(get % :trigger/uses-stack? true) matching)]
    (as-> db db'
      ;; Execute immediate triggers first (turn-based actions)
      (reduce (fn [d t] (triggers/resolve-trigger d t)) db' (or immediate []))
      ;; Add stacked triggers to stack (card triggers)
      (reduce (fn [d t] (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack d t)) db' (or stacked [])))))
```

### 5. Write tests in src/test/fizzle/engine/trigger_registry_test.cljs

### 6. Write tests in src/test/fizzle/engine/trigger_dispatch_test.cljs

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events.cljs created with 3 event constructor functions (phase-entered, permanent-tapped, zone-change)
- [ ] trigger-registry.cljs created with register/unregister/query functions
- [ ] trigger-dispatch.cljs created with matches-filter? and dispatch-event
- [ ] 8+ registry tests pass covering:
  - test_register_trigger_adds_to_registry
  - test_register_trigger_idempotent_same_id_overwrites
  - test_unregister_trigger_removes_from_registry
  - test_unregister_nonexistent_trigger_no_op
  - test_unregister_by_source_removes_all_from_source
  - test_unregister_by_source_nonexistent_no_op
  - test_get_triggers_for_event_filters_by_type
  - test_get_triggers_for_event_empty_registry_returns_empty
- [ ] 8+ dispatch tests pass covering:
  - test_matches_filter_no_filter_matches_all
  - test_matches_filter_exact_match
  - test_matches_filter_self_matches_source
  - test_matches_filter_mismatch_returns_false
  - test_dispatch_event_immediate_executes_first
  - test_dispatch_event_stacked_goes_to_stack
  - test_dispatch_event_no_matching_triggers_returns_db_unchanged
  - test_dispatch_event_order_immediate_before_stacked
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Idempotent Registration:**
- Same trigger ID registered twice should overwrite, not duplicate
- This is intentional - allows updating trigger definition

**Graceful No-Ops:**
- Unregistering non-existent trigger: return nil, no error
- Unregistering by non-existent source: return nil, no error
- Get triggers for event with empty registry: return empty seq

**Default uses-stack? Value:**
- If trigger doesn't specify :trigger/uses-stack?, default to true (card triggers use stack)
- Turn-based actions must explicitly set :trigger/uses-stack? false

**Dispatch Order:**
- MUST execute immediate triggers before adding stacked triggers
- This matches MTG rules: turn-based actions happen, THEN triggered abilities go on stack

**Empty/Nil Handling:**
- Empty filter map should match all events of that type
- Nil filter should match all events of that type
- Empty matching list should return db unchanged

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO throwing on missing trigger (graceful no-op)
- ❌ NO mutating event maps (events are immutable)
- ❌ NO direct atom access outside registry module (use functions)
- ❌ NO assuming triggers exist (always handle empty case)
- ❌ NO changing dispatch order (immediate MUST come before stacked)
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Fork/Replay Engine
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

1. Global re-frame interceptor captures a snapshot of :game/db whenever it changes, excluding history navigation events
2. Each history entry stores: immutable Datascript db reference, event type keyword, human-readable description, turn number
3. Free stepping: player can move position forward/backward through any branch without creating forks; :game/db is swapped to the snapshot at that position
4. Auto-fork on divergence: taking a game action from a non-tip position automatically creates a named fork, preserving the original timeline
5. Branch switching: player can switch between main timeline and any fork
6. Nested forks: forks from forks supported via parent chain; effective entry list computed by walking parent chain
7. All history operations (step, fork, switch-branch, effective-entries) are pure functions on app-db
8. Description generator maps event types to human-readable strings using event args and game state

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] Playing a normal game records history entries for each player action
- [ ] Stepping to any position swaps game-db to that snapshot
- [ ] Taking action from rewound position creates auto-fork preserving original timeline
- [ ] Can switch between main and fork branches
- [ ] Nested forks work (fork from a fork)
- [ ] History navigation events (step, switch) do not create new entries
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (interceptor is transparent)
- [ ] History operations have comprehensive tests
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- NO replay engine / event sourcing (we use snapshots via structural sharing, not event replay — replay adds complexity with zero benefit given immutable Datascript)
- NO modifying existing event handlers (interceptor is transparent, zero changes to game.cljs/selection.cljs/abilities.cljs)
- NO serializing game-db in history entries (store the Datascript db reference for structural sharing, not EDN strings)
- NO single-level fork limitation (parent chain supports arbitrary nesting)
- NO interceptor on history navigation events (step/switch must not record themselves as new entries)
- NO UI components in this epic (engine only, UI is separate epics)
- NO description generation in the interceptor itself (delegate to a separate description generator function for testability)

## Approach

Build a history/snapshot layer that sits alongside the existing game state in app-db. A global re-frame interceptor transparently captures immutable Datascript db snapshots whenever game state changes. History is stored as a vector of entries per branch, with forks forming a tree via parent references. All operations (stepping, forking, branch switching) are pure functions.

Structural sharing in ClojureScript persistent data structures means storing a snapshot per action is nearly free — only the changed nodes are allocated. This gives instant rewind (swap db reference) with minimal memory overhead.

## Architecture

### Data Model
```clojure
;; New keys in app-db:
{:history/main []              ;; Vector of entries on main timeline
 :history/forks {}             ;; Map of fork-id -> fork descriptor
 :history/current-branch nil   ;; nil = main, or fork-id
 :history/position -1}         ;; Index in effective entry list

;; Entry:
{:entry/snapshot <datascript-db>   ;; Immutable db value (structural sharing)
 :entry/event-type :cast-spell     ;; Re-frame event keyword
 :entry/description "Cast Dark Ritual"
 :entry/turn 1}

;; Fork:
{:fork/id <uuid>
 :fork/name "Auto-fork at step 5"
 :fork/branch-point 5          ;; Index in parent where we diverged
 :fork/parent nil              ;; nil = from main, or another fork-id
 :fork/entries []}             ;; Entries specific to this fork
```

### Components
- history/core.cljs — Data model, pure operations (init, append, step, fork, switch, effective-entries)
- history/interceptor.cljs — Global re-frame interceptor for snapshot capture
- history/descriptions.cljs — Event type -> human-readable description mapping

### Integration Points
- core.cljs: Register global interceptor during init, add history keys to initial app-db
- No changes to events/game.cljs, events/selection.cljs, or events/abilities.cljs

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Players need to rewind to earlier game states and try different lines. The current architecture has no history tracking — each re-frame event overwrites the previous game state.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- All events use rf/reg-event-db (pure functions, no interceptors)
- App-db has :game/db (Datascript immutable value), :game/selected-card, :game/pending-selection, :game/pending-mode-selection
- No existing interceptors — clean slate for global interceptor
- Events dispatched from core.cljs: cast-spell, play-land, resolve-top, advance-phase, start-turn, activate-mana-ability, activate-ability
- Subscriptions use 3-layer pattern with ::game-db as source

**External:**
- ClojureScript persistent data structures use structural sharing (HAMTs) — storing old references is nearly free
- Datascript db values are immutable — d/db-with returns new value, old unchanged
- re-frame supports rf/reg-global-interceptor for transparent event interception

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Hybrid snapshots + event metadata (CHOSEN)
Store immutable Datascript db references at each action. Event metadata (type, description) alongside for display. No replay engine.

Chosen because: Instant rewind (swap reference), near-zero memory overhead (structural sharing), simplest implementation, no replay logic needed.

#### 2. Full event sourcing with replay engine (REJECTED)
Store event log as source of truth, replay events to reconstruct state.

REJECTED BECAUSE: Adds replay engine complexity with no benefit — Datascript immutability already gives us free snapshots. Replay would be slow for rewinding to early states. Event format would need to be stable across code changes.

DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS: We need cross-session persistence where snapshots cannot be serialized (but they can be — Datascript db values are serializable as EDN).

#### 3. Snapshot only at key points (periodic checkpoints) (REJECTED)
Store snapshots every N events, replay between checkpoints.

REJECTED BECAUSE: Still requires a replay engine for the gaps. More complex than snapshotting everything, without saving meaningful memory (structural sharing already handles this).

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** History data model, snapshot capture, stepping, forking, branch switching, description generation
**Out of scope:** UI components (separate epics), puzzle export (Phase 6), action log display (UI epic)

### Open Questions
- Exact description format for selection events (e.g., Intuition pile choices) — resolve during implementation
