- D. Storm count — casting increments storm count

**Conditional tests based on mechanics:**
- E. Selection/modal tests (cards with player choices) — selection state params, confirm behavior, edge counts
- F. Targeting tests (targeted spells) — valid/invalid targets, fizzle when target removed
- G. Edge case tests (always 2+) — empty zones, partial resources, already-tapped
- H. Flashback tests — cast from graveyard, correct cost, exile after, zone restriction
- I. Trigger/ability tests (permanents) — registration, firing, stack usage, self-entry exclusion

**Minimum test counts:**
- Simple instant/sorcery: 4-5 tests
- Selection spell: 6-8 tests
- Targeted spell: 8-12 tests
- Flashback spell: 12-20 tests
- Permanent with abilities: 6-9 tests
- Complex multi-mechanic (storm+targeting): 12-15 tests

**Exemplar tests to follow:**
- effects_test.cljs — engine gold standard (exhaustive, edge cases, boundary conditions)
- brain_freeze_test.cljs — complex integration (full storm pipeline)
- recoup_test.cljs — thorough boundary testing (targeting validation, flashback lifecycle)

### 4. Add Tests for Untested Cards (MEDIUM - fills gaps)
Cards in iggy_pop.cljs lacking dedicated test files include: Gemstone Mine, Chrome Mox, Duress, Vampiric Tutor, and others. Prioritize cards with complex mechanics (Chrome Mox has imprint, Duress has opponent hand selection, Vampiric Tutor has life payment + library top).

### 5. Add Property-Based Tests for Mana System (LOW - catches edge cases)
The mana system is well-tested with examples but has no property-based tests. Invariants to test:
- Adding mana then paying that exact amount should leave pool at original
- can-pay? should return true for any cost that pay-mana succeeds on
- Mana pool values should never go negative after valid operations

### 6. Data-Driven Selection Subs and View Modals (LOW - reduces per-card boilerplate)
Selection system currently requires 4 touch points per new type: builder, defmethod, subscription case in ::selection-cards (subs/game.cljs:316-382, 60-line cond), and modal component in views/modals.cljs (lines 741-822, 80-line cond).

**Consider:**
- Adding :selection/card-source key to selection state so the subscription can derive cards generically
- A selection-modal multimethod/registry to replace the cond in views/modals.cljs
- This reduces per-selection-type boilerplate from 4 touch points to 2

## Best/Worst Test Examples (from review)

**Best (follow these):**
- effects_test.cljs — 92 tests, covers happy path + edge cases + boundary conditions + error states. Rating: 10/10
- brain_freeze_test.cljs — 15 tests, full storm pipeline integration. Rating: 9/10
- recoup_test.cljs — 19 tests, exhaustive boundary testing. Rating: 9/10
- mana_test.cljs — 41 tests, comprehensive mana operations. Rating: 9/10

**Worst (fix/avoid these patterns):**
- careful_study_test.cljs:322-395 — reimplements production handler in test. Rating: 3/10
- lotus_petal_test.cljs — 5 identical tests varying only by color. Rating: 5/10

## Notes
- No property-based testing currently (everything example-based)
- No negative/fuzz testing of selection system inputs
- Precondition assertions in let bindings (is (= ...) "Precondition:") is actually a GOOD pattern — keep it
- Subscription tests are thin (only 4 files) but acceptable since subs are mostly thin wrappers
- db/init.cljs appears to be dead code used only by some tests — consider deleting or marking test-only
close_reason: Superseded by epic fizzle-8tl0 with full design and parallelism map

title: Task 3: Migrate trigger creation to stack-items and wire resolve-effect-target
design:
## Goal
Migrate all trigger creation sites to produce stack-items instead of trigger entities, and wire resolve-effect-target into all resolution paths. After this task, NO NEW trigger entities are created — only stack-items. Legacy trigger resolution code remains for the transition but is no longer the creation path.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Context
- Task 1 (fizzle-ndvj): Created engine/stack.cljs with create-stack-item, get-top-stack-item, remove-stack-item, resolve-effect-target, get-stack-item-by-object-ref. Schema additions in place. 17 tests.
- Task 2 (fizzle-nsdo): Wired spell casting and storm copies to create stack-items. ::resolve-top handles both stack-items (spells) and legacy triggers. Confirmation handlers remove spell stack-items.

## Implementation

### Step 1: Migrate trigger_dispatch.cljs to create stack-items

File: src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger_dispatch.cljs

Currently, dispatch-event (lines 62-90) does two things:
1. Executes immediate triggers (turn-based: untap, draw) directly via resolve-trigger
2. Adds stacked triggers (card: permanent-tapped, land-entered) via add-trigger-to-stack

**Change stacked triggers to use stack-items:**

Replace lines 86-90 (the stacked trigger reduce):
```clojure
;; OLD:
(reduce (fn [d t]
          (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack d (registry-trigger->stack-trigger t)))
        db'
        (or stacked []))
```

With:
```clojure
;; NEW:
(reduce (fn [d t]
          (let [stack-trigger (registry-trigger->stack-trigger t)]
            (stack/create-stack-item d
              {:stack-item/type (:trigger/type stack-trigger)
               :stack-item/controller (:trigger/controller stack-trigger)
               :stack-item/source (:trigger/source stack-trigger)
               :stack-item/effects (get-in stack-trigger [:trigger/data :effects] [])
               :stack-item/description (:trigger/description stack-trigger)})))
        db'
        (or stacked []))
```

**Keep immediate triggers unchanged** — :draw-step, :untap-step, :expire-grants are immediate (uses-stack? false) and don't go on the stack. They use resolve-trigger directly and don't create entities.

**Add require:** `[fizzle.engine.stack :as stack]`

### Step 2: Migrate abilities.cljs trigger creation to stack-items

File: src/main/fizzle/events/abilities.cljs

Two creation sites exist:

**2a. activate-ability (line 250-257, non-targeted abilities):**
```clojure
;; OLD:
(let [effects-list (:ability/effects ability [])
      ability-trigger (triggers/create-trigger
                        :activated-ability
                        object-id
                        player-id
                        {:effects effects-list
                         :description (:ability/description ability)})
      db-with-trigger (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack db-after-costs ability-trigger)]
  {:db db-with-trigger :pending-selection nil})
```

Replace with:
```clojure
;; NEW:
(let [effects-list (:ability/effects ability [])
      db-with-item (stack/create-stack-item db-after-costs
                     {:stack-item/type :activated-ability
                      :stack-item/controller player-id
                      :stack-item/source object-id
                      :stack-item/effects effects-list
                      :stack-item/description (:ability/description ability)})]
  {:db db-with-item :pending-selection nil})
```

**2b. confirm-ability-target (line 184-192, targeted abilities):**
```clojure
;; OLD:
(let [effects-list (:ability/effects ability [])
      ability-trigger (triggers/create-trigger
                        :activated-ability
                        object-id
                        player-id
                        {:effects effects-list
                         :description (:ability/description ability)
                         :targets {target-ref target-id}})
      db-with-trigger (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack db-after-costs ability-trigger)]
  {:db db-with-trigger :pending-selection nil})
```

Replace with:
```clojure
;; NEW:
(let [effects-list (:ability/effects ability [])
      db-with-item (stack/create-stack-item db-after-costs
                     {:stack-item/type :activated-ability
                      :stack-item/controller player-id
                      :stack-item/source object-id
                      :stack-item/effects effects-list
                      :stack-item/targets {target-ref target-id}
                      :stack-item/description (:ability/description ability)})]
  {:db db-with-item :pending-selection nil})
```

**Add require:** `[fizzle.engine.stack :as stack]`
**Remove require:** `[fizzle.engine.triggers :as triggers]` (no longer used here)

### Step 3: Update ::resolve-top to handle :activated-ability stack-items

File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs (lines 368-420)

Currently, ::resolve-top has a special branch for activated-ability triggers (lines 404-412). After Step 2, activated abilities create stack-items of type :activated-ability instead of trigger entities.

**Add new branch for :activated-ability stack-items** in ::resolve-top, after the spell/storm-copy branch:

```clojure
;; Resolve activated ability stack-item
(and resolve-stack-item? (= :activated-ability (:stack-item/type top-stack-item)))
(let [stack-item-eid (:db/id top-stack-item)
      result (selection/resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection game-db top-stack-item)]
  (if (:pending-selection result)
    (-> db
        (assoc :game/db (:db result))
        (assoc :game/pending-selection (:pending-selection result)))
    (maybe-continue-cleanup
      (assoc db :game/db (stack/remove-stack-item (:db result) stack-item-eid)))))
```

Also update resolve-top-of-stack (lines 246-288) to handle :activated-ability stack-items for the non-selection path.

### Step 4: Create resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection in selection.cljs

File: src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs

This is a new function that replaces resolve-activated-ability-with-selection but works with stack-items instead of trigger entities. It reuses resolve-effect-target from engine/stack.cljs.

```clojure
(defn resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection
  "Resolve a stack-item for an activated ability, pausing if a selection effect is encountered.
   Uses resolve-effect-target for all target resolution.
   
   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database
     stack-item - Stack-item entity map with :stack-item/* attributes
   
   Returns {:db db :pending-selection selection-or-nil}"
  [db stack-item]
  (let [controller (:stack-item/controller stack-item)
        source-id (:stack-item/source stack-item)
        stack-item-eid (:db/id stack-item)
        effects-list (or (:stack-item/effects stack-item) [])
        stored-targets (:stack-item/targets stack-item)
        selection-idx (find-selection-effect-index effects-list)
        stored-player (:player stored-targets)
        has-stored-player-target (and stored-player
                                      selection-idx
                                      (= :any-player (:effect/target (nth effects-list selection-idx))))]
    (cond
      ;; Stored targets for player targeting
      has-stored-player-target
      (let [player-target-effect (nth effects-list selection-idx)
            resolved-player-effect (stack/resolve-effect-target player-target-effect source-id controller stored-targets)
            effects-before (subvec (vec effects-list) 0 selection-idx)
            effects-after (subvec (vec effects-list) (inc selection-idx))
            db-after-before (reduce (fn [d effect]
                                      (effects/execute-effect d controller
                                        (stack/resolve-effect-target effect source-id controller nil)))
                                    db effects-before)
            db-after-player-effect (effects/execute-effect db-after-before controller resolved-player-effect)
            next-selection-idx (find-selection-effect-index effects-after)]
        (if next-selection-idx
          (let [effects-before-next (subvec (vec effects-after) 0 next-selection-idx)
                next-selection-effect (nth effects-after next-selection-idx)
                effects-after-next (subvec (vec effects-after) (inc next-selection-idx))
                db-after-before-next (reduce (fn [d effect]
                                               (effects/execute-effect d controller effect))
                                             db-after-player-effect effects-before-next)
                pending-selection (when (= :player (:effect/selection next-selection-effect))
                                    {:selection/type :discard
                                     :selection/player-id stored-player
                                     :selection/count (:effect/count next-selection-effect)
                                     :selection/selected #{}
                                     :selection/stack-item-eid stack-item-eid
                                     :selection/source-type :stack-item
                                     :selection/remaining-effects effects-after-next
                                     :selection/effect-type :discard})]
            {:db db-after-before-next :pending-selection pending-selection})
          (let [db-after-all (reduce (fn [d effect]
                                       (effects/execute-effect d controller effect))
                                     db-after-player-effect effects-after)]
            {:db db-after-all :pending-selection nil})))

      ;; No selection effect
      (nil? selection-idx)
      (let [db-after-effects (reduce
                               (fn [d effect]
                                 (let [resolved (stack/resolve-effect-target effect source-id controller stored-targets)]
                                   (effects/execute-effect d controller resolved source-id)))
                               db effects-list)]
        {:db db-after-effects :pending-selection nil})

      ;; Has selection effect without stored targets
      :else
      (let [effects-before (subvec (vec effects-list) 0 selection-idx)
            selection-effect (nth effects-list selection-idx)
            effects-after (subvec (vec effects-list) (inc selection-idx))
            db-after-before (reduce (fn [d effect]
                                      (effects/execute-effect d controller
                                        (stack/resolve-effect-target effect source-id controller nil)))
                                    db effects-before)
            pending-selection (cond
                                (= :any-player (:effect/target selection-effect))
                                (build-player-target-selection controller stack-item-eid
                                                               selection-effect effects-after)

                                (= :tutor (:effect/type selection-effect))
                                (build-tutor-selection db-after-before controller stack-item-eid
                                                       selection-effect effects-after)

                                (= :player (:effect/selection selection-effect))
                                {:selection/type :discard
                                 :selection/player-id controller
                                 :selection/count (:effect/count selection-effect)
                                 :selection/selected #{}
                                 :selection/stack-item-eid stack-item-eid
                                 :selection/source-type :stack-item
                                 :selection/remaining-effects effects-after
                                 :selection/effect-type :discard}

                                :else nil)]
        {:db db-after-before :pending-selection pending-selection}))))
```

**Key changes from legacy resolve-activated-ability-with-selection:**
1. Uses `stack/resolve-effect-target` instead of inline cond/assoc
2. Uses `:selection/stack-item-eid` instead of `:selection/trigger-id`
3. Uses `:selection/source-type :stack-item` instead of `:trigger`
4. Uses `build-player-target-selection` and `build-tutor-selection` (spell versions) instead of `-for-trigger` variants, BUT passing stack-item-eid where spell-id would go
5. Does NOT call triggers/remove-trigger at end — caller (::resolve-top) removes stack-item

### Step 5: Update selection builders to accept generic source-id

The spell selection builders (build-tutor-selection, build-player-target-selection, build-discard-selection) currently use :selection/spell-id. The -for-trigger variants use :selection/trigger-id.

**Approach:** Add a new key :selection/stack-item-eid alongside the existing keys. The confirmation handlers will check for this key first. This avoids breaking existing spell resolution paths during migration.

For the new resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection, pass stack-item-eid. The builders need a minor tweak to accept a source-ref map:

Actually, simpler approach: since resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection builds selection state inline (not via the existing builders for tutor/player-target), we just set :selection/stack-item-eid directly. For existing builders used by spells, we don't change them.

Wait — looking at the code in Step 4 again, I'm calling build-player-target-selection and build-tutor-selection. Those set :selection/spell-id. For stack-item abilities, we need :selection/stack-item-eid.

**Solution:** After calling the builder, override the source keys:
```clojure
(-> (build-tutor-selection db-after-before controller stack-item-eid selection-effect effects-after)
    (dissoc :selection/spell-id)
    (assoc :selection/stack-item-eid stack-item-eid
           :selection/source-type :stack-item))
```

This reuses the spell builders but patches the source identifier.

### Step 6: Update confirmation handlers to handle :stack-item source type

File: src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs

Each confirmation handler (::confirm-selection, ::confirm-tutor-selection, ::confirm-graveyard-selection, ::confirm-player-target-selection) currently has a branch:
```clojure
(if (= source-type :trigger)
  (triggers/remove-trigger db-after-remaining trigger-id)
  ;; spell cleanup ...
  )
```

Add a new branch for :stack-item:
```clojure
(cond
  (= source-type :stack-item)
  ;; Stack-item ability: just remove the stack-item
  (let [si-eid (:selection/stack-item-eid selection)]
    (stack/remove-stack-item db-after-remaining si-eid))
  
  (= source-type :trigger)
  (triggers/remove-trigger db-after-remaining trigger-id)
  
  :else
  ;; Spell cleanup (move to graveyard/exile + remove stack-item)
  ...)
```

This must be done in these handlers:
- ::confirm-selection (line 1089) - discard confirmation
- ::confirm-tutor-selection (line 1280) - tutor confirmation
- ::confirm-graveyard-selection (line 1228) - graveyard selection confirmation  
- ::confirm-player-target-selection (line 1442) - player target confirmation

### Step 7: Wire resolve-effect-target into trigger resolution methods

File: src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs

Replace inline target resolution in resolve-trigger methods with stack/resolve-effect-target:

**:permanent-tapped (lines 80-96):**
```clojure
;; OLD:
(let [resolved-effect (if (= :controller (:effect/target effect))
                          (assoc effect :effect/target controller)
                          effect)]

;; NEW:
(let [resolved-effect (stack/resolve-effect-target effect nil controller nil)]
```

**:land-entered (lines 99-121):**
```clojure
;; OLD:
(let [resolved-effect (cond
                        (= :self (:effect/target effect))
                        (assoc effect :effect/target source)
                        (= :controller (:effect/target effect))
                        (assoc effect :effect/target controller)
                        :else effect)]

;; NEW:
(let [resolved-effect (stack/resolve-effect-target effect source controller nil)]
```

**:activated-ability (lines 124-149):**
Same pattern as :land-entered. Replace with:
```clojure
(let [resolved-effect (stack/resolve-effect-target effect source controller nil)]
```

Note: The :activated-ability defmethod is still needed for legacy triggers that may still exist in-flight during this task. Once all creation paths use stack-items (Steps 1-2), no new :activated-ability triggers will be created, but existing ones may still be on the stack mid-game.

### Step 8: Wire resolve-effect-target into resolve-activated-ability-with-selection

File: src/main/fizzle/events/selection.cljs (lines 702-864)

Replace all 4 inline target resolution blocks with stack/resolve-effect-target. This function still handles legacy triggers, so keep its signature, but use the unified resolver internally:

**Line 746-751 (effects-before in stored-player path):**
```clojure
;; OLD:
(let [resolved-effect (cond
                        (= :self (:effect/target effect))
                        (assoc effect :effect/target source-id)
                        (= :controller (:effect/target effect))
                        (assoc effect :effect/target controller)
                        :else effect)]
  (effects/execute-effect d controller resolved-effect))

;; NEW:
(let [resolved-effect (stack/resolve-effect-target effect source-id controller nil)]
  (effects/execute-effect d controller resolved-effect))
```

**Lines 797-813 (no-selection path with stored targets):**
```clojure
;; OLD (complex cond with target-ref + self + controller):
;; NEW:
(let [resolved-effect (stack/resolve-effect-target effect source-id controller stored-targets)]
  (effects/execute-effect d controller resolved-effect source-id))
```

**Lines 828-833 (effects-before in selection path):**
Same pattern — replace cond with resolve-effect-target.

### Step 9: Wire resolve-effect-target into mana ability resolution

File: src/main/fizzle/events/abilities.cljs (lines 93-102)

Replace the inline target resolution in activate-mana-ability:
```clojure
;; OLD:
(let [resolved-effect (cond
                        (= :self (:effect/target effect))
                        (assoc effect :effect/target object-id)
                        (= :controller (:effect/target effect))
                        (assoc effect :effect/target player-id)
                        :else effect)

;; NEW:
(let [resolved-effect (stack/resolve-effect-target effect object-id player-id nil)
```

Note: The {:any N} mana resolution on lines 104-108 is NOT part of resolve-effect-target — it's mana-specific logic that stays inline.

**Add require:** `[fizzle.engine.stack :as stack]` (already added in Step 2)

### Step 10: Write tests (TDD)

Add to src/test/fizzle/engine/stack_test.cljs:

1. **test-activated-ability-creates-stack-item** — Activate a non-mana ability, verify a :stack-item/type :activated-ability entity exists with correct controller, source, effects. Bug caught: ability still creating trigger entity.

2. **test-activated-ability-with-target-creates-stack-item** — Activate targeted ability, confirm target, verify stack-item has :stack-item/targets populated. Bug caught: stored targets not migrated to stack-item.

3. **test-permanent-tapped-trigger-creates-stack-item** — Tap a permanent with a tapped trigger registered, verify stack-item is created (not trigger entity). Bug caught: dispatch still creating triggers.

4. **test-land-entered-trigger-creates-stack-item** — Play a land that fires land-entered event, verify stack-item. Bug caught: same as above for land triggers.

5. **test-resolve-top-handles-ability-stack-item** — Create an :activated-ability stack-item, call ::resolve-top, verify it resolves and removes the stack-item. Bug caught: ::resolve-top doesn't handle new stack-item type.

6. **test-resolve-effect-target-used-in-triggers** — Create a trigger with :self target, resolve it, verify the effect executed against the source object (not literally :self). Bug caught: resolve-effect-target wiring broken.

7. **test-ability-selection-uses-stack-item-eid** — Activate ability with tutor effect, verify pending-selection has :selection/stack-item-eid (not :selection/trigger-id). Bug caught: selection still using trigger-id.

8. **test-confirm-selection-removes-stack-item-for-ability** — Activate ability with selection, confirm selection, verify stack-item removed from DB. Bug caught: confirmation handler not cleaning up stack-items.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] abilities.cljs create-trigger/add-trigger-to-stack calls replaced with stack/create-stack-item (2 sites)
- [ ] trigger_dispatch.cljs stacked triggers use stack/create-stack-item (1 site)
- [ ] resolve-effect-target called in all target resolution paths (triggers.cljs 3 methods + selection.cljs 4 blocks + abilities.cljs 1 block = 8 total sites)
- [ ] ::resolve-top handles :activated-ability stack-items
- [ ] resolve-top-of-stack handles :activated-ability stack-items
- [ ] resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection function exists and works for abilities with and without selection effects
- [ ] Confirmation handlers (4 handlers) handle :source-type :stack-item cleanup
- [ ] All existing 852+ tests pass (no regressions)
- [ ] 8 new tests pass covering the migration
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] No new trigger entities are created by abilities or card triggers (only turn-based immediate triggers still use resolve-trigger directly)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO removing resolve-trigger defmethods yet (legacy triggers may still be in-flight during transition; removal is a later task)
- NO removing :trigger/* schema yet (same reason; removal is a later task)
- NO changing turn-based immediate triggers (draw-step, untap-step, expire-grants are not on the stack — they execute immediately and never create entities)
- NO modifying spell resolution paths (resolve-spell-with-selection is unchanged; only activated-ability resolution changes)
- NO adding stack-item/object-ref for abilities (abilities don't have a game object on the stack — source is just the permanent's object-id, stored as :stack-item/source)
- NO breaking the existing -for-trigger builder functions (they're still called by legacy resolve-activated-ability-with-selection which stays for backward compat)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: In-flight triggers during deployment**
If a game is in progress when this code deploys (hot-reload), there may be legacy trigger entities on the stack that were created before the migration. The ::resolve-top handler MUST continue to handle these. The legacy trigger branches in ::resolve-top remain untouched. This is safe.

**Edge Case: Ability with nil effects**
Some abilities may have nil :ability/effects. The create-stack-item call uses `(:ability/effects ability [])` to default to empty vector. resolve-effect-target handles empty effects-list (reduce over empty vec = identity).

**Edge Case: Stack-item EID vs trigger-id in selection state**
During the transition, selection state may have :selection/trigger-id (legacy) OR :selection/stack-item-eid (new). Confirmation handlers must check source-type first:
- :stack-item → use stack-item-eid, call stack/remove-stack-item
- :trigger → use trigger-id, call triggers/remove-trigger
- nil (default) → spell cleanup path (move to graveyard + remove stack-item)

**Edge Case: resolve-effect-target with nil stored-targets**
Most trigger resolution passes nil for stored-targets. This is safe: `(get nil key)` returns nil in ClojureScript. resolve-effect-target handles this in its :any-player branch via if-let.

**Edge Case: Mana ability {:any N} resolution**
The :any-mana resolution in abilities.cljs (lines 104-108) is separate from target resolution. resolve-effect-target handles :effect/target, not :effect/mana. The {:any N} → {color N} transform stays inline. Don't accidentally remove it.

**Migration Path:**
After this task:
- Spells → stack-items (Task 2, done)
- Storm copies → stack-items (Task 2, done)
- Activated abilities → stack-items (this task)
- Card triggers (permanent-tapped, land-entered) → stack-items (this task)
- Turn-based actions (draw, untap, expire-grants) → immediate execution, no entities (unchanged)
- Legacy code paths remain but no longer create new entities
close_reason: All 10 steps complete: trigger_dispatch, abilities, game.cljs, selection.cljs all migrated to stack-items. resolve-effect-target wired into all 8 target resolution sites. 856 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Lion's Eye Diamond enters tapped after being returned from Ill-Gotten Gains
description: When Lion's Eye Diamond is returned to hand via Ill-Gotten Gains and then cast (sacrificed from battlefield), it enters the battlefield tapped. LED should enter untapped - it's an artifact with no ETB tapped clause. Likely the graveyard-return or cast path is incorrectly setting tapped state.
close_reason: Fixed in zones/move-to-zone: now resets :object/tapped to false when entering battlefield (not just when leaving). Added test. 742 tests pass.

title: Standardize selection state keys
description:
## Problem
The :game/pending-selection map uses duplicate/legacy keys for the same concepts, creating inconsistency and bug risk.

## Specifics
- :selection/count vs :selection/select-count used interchangeably for the same concept
- Line 1764: (get selection :selection/count 0)
- Line 1857: select-count (:selection/select-count selection)
- Line 2099: select-count (or (:selection/select-count selection) 1)
- :selection/shuffle vs :selection/shuffle? inconsistency
- Used across 40+ locations in events/game.cljs

## Success Criteria
- Pick one key per concept and use it everywhere
- Remove legacy keys
- Document the selection state shape
- All selection flows still work
close_reason: Superseded by epic fizzle-whic with refined requirements and first task fizzle-ql1e

title: Test Quality Improvement Epic
description:
Improve test effectiveness by removing tautological tests, strengthening weak tests, and adding missing corner case coverage based on test effectiveness analysis.

## Goal
- Remove 4 RED tautological tests that provide false confidence
- Strengthen YELLOW tests with better assertions
- Add missing corner case coverage for P0-P2 modules

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All RED tests removed or replaced with meaningful tests
- [ ] All YELLOW tests strengthened with proper assertions
- [ ] All P0-P1 missing corner cases covered
- [ ] Test suite still passes after changes

## Scope
- cards/careful_study_test.cljs (4 RED tests)
- cards/lotus_petal_test.cljs, led_test.cljs (weak assertions)
- engine/effects, engine/triggers, events modules (missing corner cases)

## Anti-patterns
- Adding tests that only check != nil
- Adding tests that verify mock behavior
- Adding happy-path-only tests
- Leaving tautological tests "for coverage"

## Out of Scope
- Hybrid mana tests (not in Premodern format)
- Phyrexian mana tests (not in Premodern format)
close_reason: Closed

title: Unify spell/trigger stack systems
description:
## Problem
Spells and triggers are conceptually the same thing (resolvable stack items) but are implemented as two parallel systems, creating ~1000+ lines of duplicated logic.

## Specifics
- Dual stack representation: Spells use :object/position in :stack zone; triggers use :trigger/stack-order as separate entities. LIFO resolution requires comparing both.
- Parallel resolution paths in events/game.cljs: resolve-spell-with-selection (~370 lines) mirrors resolve-activated-ability-with-selection (~163 lines) with nearly identical logic.
- Duplicated selection builders: build-tutor-selection and build-tutor-selection-for-trigger are copy-pasted with only :spell-id vs :trigger-id differing.
- ::resolve-top handler has 4+ branches (spell-with-selection, trigger-with-selection, trigger-normal, empty) each duplicating effect execution + cleanup.
- Target resolution (:self, :controller) reimplemented 8+ times across spells, triggers, mana abilities, activated abilities.
- Effect before/after splitting logic appears 3+ times.

## Why Fix Now
Fork/replay (Phase 3) needs a uniform stack model to capture and replay game state.

## Success Criteria
- Single stack representation for both spells and triggers
- Unified resolution path (one function, not parallel branches)
- Selection builders shared between spell and trigger resolution
- Target resolution consolidated
- Net reduction of 500+ LOC
close_reason: All 4 tasks complete. Stack unified via stack-items. 831 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Task 1: Extend grants system to support player entities
design:
## Goal
Extend the grants system to work on player entities (not just objects), enabling restrictions to be attached to players that expire at end of turn.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
Reference implementations to follow:
- grants.cljs:21-27 - get-grants pattern (parallel this for players)
- grants.cljs:37-47 - add-grant pattern (uses d/q to find eid, then d/db-with)
- grants.cljs:65-74 - get-all-objects-with-grants (create parallel for players)
- grants.cljs:138-150 - expire-grants (extend to also process players)
- queries.cljs:10-17 - get-player-eid (use this for player entity lookup)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Create test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/grants_test.cljs

Test cases with specific assertions:
```clojure
;; Test 1: Add grant to player
(deftest add-player-grant-test
  (testing "adds grant to player with no existing grants"
    (let [db (-> (init-test-db)
                 (grants/add-player-grant :player-1 test-grant))]
      (is (= 1 (count (grants/get-player-grants db :player-1))))
      (is (= :restriction (:grant/type (first (grants/get-player-grants db :player-1)))))))

;; Test 2: Get grants by type
(deftest get-player-grants-by-type-test
  (testing "filters grants by type"
    (let [db (-> (init-test-db)
                 (grants/add-player-grant :player-1 {:grant/id (random-uuid)
                                                      :grant/type :restriction
                                                      :grant/data {}})
                 (grants/add-player-grant :player-1 {:grant/id (random-uuid)
                                                      :grant/type :alternate-cost
                                                      :grant/data {}}))]
      (is (= 1 (count (grants/get-player-grants-by-type db :player-1 :restriction))))
      (is (= 1 (count (grants/get-player-grants-by-type db :player-1 :alternate-cost)))))))

;; Test 3: Remove player grant
(deftest remove-player-grant-test
  (testing "removes specific grant by id"
    (let [grant-id (random-uuid)
          db (-> (init-test-db)
                 (grants/add-player-grant :player-1 {:grant/id grant-id :grant/type :restriction})
                 (grants/remove-player-grant :player-1 grant-id))]
      (is (= 0 (count (grants/get-player-grants db :player-1)))))))

;; Test 4: Expire grants removes from both objects AND players
(deftest expire-grants-includes-players-test
  (testing "expired player grants removed at cleanup"
    (let [expired-grant {:grant/id (random-uuid)
                         :grant/type :restriction
                         :grant/expires {:expires/turn 1 :expires/phase :cleanup}}
          db (-> (init-test-db)
                 (set-game-turn 2)  ; Past expiration
                 (grants/add-player-grant :player-1 expired-grant)
                 (grants/expire-grants 2 :cleanup))]
      (is (= 0 (count (grants/get-player-grants db :player-1)))))))

;; Test 5: Non-expired grants preserved
(deftest non-expired-player-grants-preserved-test
  (testing "player grants expiring later are not removed"
    (let [future-grant {:grant/id (random-uuid)
                        :grant/type :restriction
                        :grant/expires {:expires/turn 5 :expires/phase :cleanup}}
          db (-> (init-test-db)
                 (grants/add-player-grant :player-1 future-grant)
                 (grants/expire-grants 2 :cleanup))]
      (is (= 1 (count (grants/get-player-grants db :player-1)))))))

;; Test 6: Multiple players have independent grants
(deftest independent-player-grants-test
  (testing "grants on one player dont affect another"
    (let [db (-> (init-test-db)
                 (grants/add-player-grant :player-1 {:grant/id (random-uuid) :grant/type :restriction})
                 (grants/add-player-grant :opponent {:grant/id (random-uuid) :grant/type :restriction}))]
      (is (= 1 (count (grants/get-player-grants db :player-1))))
      (is (= 1 (count (grants/get-player-grants db :opponent)))))))

;; Test 7: Empty grants returns empty vector (not nil)
(deftest get-player-grants-empty-test
  (testing "player with no grants returns empty vector"
    (let [db (init-test-db)]
      (is (= [] (grants/get-player-grants db :player-1))))))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] schema.cljs:52 - Add `:player/grants {}` attribute after :player/is-opponent
- [ ] grants.cljs - Add get-player-grants function:
      ```clojure
      (defn get-player-grants [db player-id]
        (let [player-eid (q/get-player-eid db player-id)]
          (or (d/q '[:find ?grants .
                     :in $ ?e
                     :where [?e :player/grants ?grants]]
                   db player-eid)
              [])))
      ```
- [ ] grants.cljs - Add get-player-grants-by-type function (filter by :grant/type)
- [ ] grants.cljs - Add add-player-grant function:
      ```clojure
      (defn add-player-grant [db player-id grant]
        (let [player-eid (q/get-player-eid db player-id)
              current (get-player-grants db player-id)
              new-grants (conj current grant)]
          (d/db-with db [[:db/add player-eid :player/grants new-grants]])))
      ```
- [ ] grants.cljs - Add remove-player-grant function (parallel to remove-grant)
- [ ] grants.cljs - Add get-all-players-with-grants helper:
      ```clojure
      (defn- get-all-players-with-grants [db]
        (->> (d/q '[:find ?e ?pid ?grants
                    :where
                    [?e :player/id ?pid]
                    [?e :player/grants ?grants]]
                  db)
             (filter (fn [[_ _ grants]] (seq grants)))))
      ```
- [ ] grants.cljs - Extend expire-grants to process BOTH objects AND players:
      ```clojure
      (defn expire-grants [db current-turn current-phase]
        (let [;; Expire object grants (existing code)
              db-after-objects (expire-object-grants db current-turn current-phase)
              ;; Expire player grants (new)
              players-with-grants (get-all-players-with-grants db-after-objects)]
          (reduce (fn [db' [player-eid _player-id grants]]
                    (let [active (filterv #(not (grant-expired? % current-turn current-phase))
                                          grants)]
                      (if (= (count grants) (count active))
                        db'
                        (d/db-with db' [[:db/add player-eid :player/grants active]]))))
                  db-after-objects
                  players-with-grants)))
      ```
- [ ] grants.cljs - Update module docstring to mention player grants support

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 7+ unit tests pass in src/test/fizzle/engine/grants_test.cljs
- [ ] `make test` passes (all existing tests still work)
- [ ] `make lint` passes (no clj-kondo warnings)
- [ ] `make fmt-check` passes (formatting correct)
- [ ] Manual verification: can add grant to player, retrieve it, and see it expire

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Edge Case: Player has no :player/grants attribute yet**
- First grant to player requires creating the attribute
- MUST return empty vector for players without grants (not nil)
- Use `(or ... [])` pattern like get-grants does for objects

**Edge Case: Player doesn't exist**
- get-player-eid returns nil for non-existent players
- Functions should handle gracefully (return db unchanged or empty vector)
- Do NOT throw - follow existing pattern of no-op on missing entities

**Edge Case: Grant with nil fields**
- Grant must have :grant/id, :grant/type, :grant/expires
- Follow existing pattern: no validation in grant functions, caller responsibility
- Document expected grant structure in docstring

**Edge Case: Duplicate grant IDs**
- Follow existing pattern: no duplicate checking (see add-grant docstring line 39)
- Caller responsible for unique IDs

**Reference: Existing Object Grants Pattern**
- get-grants (line 21-26): Uses q/get-object, returns (or (:object/grants obj) [])
- add-grant (line 37-47): Finds eid, gets current, conj new, d/db-with
- expire-grants (line 138-150): Gets all with grants, filters expired, updates

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO throwing exceptions for missing players (follow no-op pattern)
- ❌ NO returning nil from get-player-grants (must return empty vector)
- ❌ NO modifying existing object grant functions (add parallel player functions)
- ❌ NO duplicate validation logic (reuse grant-expired? for player grants)
- ❌ NO direct datascript access in tests (use helper functions)
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: Consistent card sorting and grouping across all zones and dialogs
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All card lists use CMC-then-name sort order (ascending CMC, alphabetical within same CMC)
- Hand view displays cards sorted
- Battlefield view splits into non-land row and land row, each sorted
- Graveyard view has castable partition (flashback cards) at top, always sorted
- Graveyard remainder toggleable between entry order and sorted (default: entry order)
- All selection dialogs (tutor, discard, IGG return, exile cost, peek, pile choice) display sorted cards
- Opening hand views use CMC-then-name sort (replacing current name-only sort)
- Scry modal unassigned pile displays sorted
- Stack ordering unchanged (LIFO by position, rules-correct)
- Sort utility is a single reusable function in engine layer

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Hand cards display in CMC-then-name order
- [ ] Battlefield separates lands from non-lands in distinct rows, each sorted
- [ ] Graveyard shows flashback-castable cards in a sorted partition at top
- [ ] Graveyard remainder toggles between entry order and sorted
- [ ] All selection modals display cards in CMC-then-name order
- [ ] Opening hand uses CMC-then-name order
- [ ] Scry unassigned pile sorted
- [ ] Sort utility exists as reusable function in engine/sorting.cljs
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO sorting in view components (consistency: sort in subscription layer, views consume pre-sorted data)
- ❌ NO per-zone custom sort logic (consistency: one sort-cards function used everywhere)
- ❌ NO changing stack order (rules: LIFO by position is rules-correct for MTG)
- ❌ NO sorting graveyard entry-order mode (rules: Premodern graveyard order is game-relevant)
- ❌ NO breaking existing flashback-castable-ids subscription API (stability: graveyard view depends on it)

## Approach
Create a single sort-cards utility in engine/sorting.cljs that sorts by CMC ascending then name alphabetical. Apply this in the subscription layer (subs/game.cljs) so views always receive pre-sorted data. Battlefield subscription returns a map with :lands and :non-lands keys instead of a flat list. Graveyard gets a new toggle state in app-db and a composite subscription that partitions flashback cards to the top. Selection dialog subscriptions apply sort-cards before returning.

## Architecture
- engine/sorting.cljs — sort-cards utility (sort by CMC then name)
- subs/game.cljs — apply sorting to ::hand, ::battlefield (split), ::graveyard (partition + toggle), ::selection-cards, ::scry-cards subscriptions
- views/battlefield.cljs — render two rows (non-lands, lands) from split subscription
- views/graveyard.cljs — render castable partition + toggleable remainder
- views/opening_hand.cljs — switch sort-by from name to sort-cards
- app-db — add :graveyard/sort-mode key (:entry or :sorted, default :entry)

## Parallelism Map

### Independent Work Streams
1. **Sort utility** (engine/sorting.cljs) — Foundation sort function + tests
   - Estimated complexity: low
2. **Hand + selection dialogs + opening hand** (subs/game.cljs ::hand/::selection-cards/::scry-cards, views/opening_hand.cljs)
   - Apply sort-cards to all flat-list subscriptions
   - Estimated complexity: low
3. **Battlefield split** (subs/game.cljs ::battlefield, views/battlefield.cljs)
   - New subscription shape, two-row view layout
   - Estimated complexity: medium
4. **Graveyard enhancement** (subs/game.cljs ::graveyard, views/graveyard.cljs)
   - Toggle state, castable partition, composite subscription
   - Estimated complexity: medium

### Stream Dependencies
- Streams 2, 3, 4 all depend on Stream 1 (sort utility must exist first)
- Streams 2, 3, 4 are independent of each other (different subscriptions, different view files)

### Suggested Waves
- Wave 1: Stream 1 (sort utility — foundation)
- Wave 2: Streams 2, 3, 4 (all use sort utility, touch different subs/views)

### File Ownership Boundaries
| Stream | Owns (exclusive) | Shared (needs coordination) |
|--------|-------------------|-----------------------------|
| Sort utility | engine/sorting.cljs | — |
| Hand + dialogs | views/opening_hand.cljs | subs/game.cljs (::hand, ::selection-cards, ::scry-cards) |
| Battlefield | views/battlefield.cljs | subs/game.cljs (::battlefield) |
| Graveyard | views/graveyard.cljs | subs/game.cljs (::graveyard, new toggle sub) |

### Parallelism Assessment
- Independent streams: 3 (after Wave 1)
- Recommendation: solo execution
- Rationale: All 3 streams share subs/game.cljs — different subscriptions but same file, coordination overhead for agents not worthwhile. Sequential solo execution avoids merge conflicts.

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Cards display in non-deterministic Datascript query order across the UI. Opening hand sorts by name but nothing else sorts. This makes scanning hand/board/graveyard harder than it needs to be for a practice tool focused on speed.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- subs/game.cljs — subscriptions pass through raw query results unsorted
- views/opening_hand.cljs:37,66 — only place with sorting (by name)
- views/graveyard.cljs:31 — already subscribes to ::flashback-castable-ids (partition data exists)
- db/queries.cljs:79-89 — get-objects-in-zone uses Datascript pull, order is non-deterministic
- Card fields available: :card/cmc, :card/name, :card/types, :card/colors

**External:**
- MTG convention: CMC sort is standard in deck builders and digital MTG tools

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Sort in subscription layer ✓

**What it is:** Apply sort-cards in re-frame subscriptions so views always receive sorted data. Battlefield subscription returns grouped map.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed all subscriptions in subs/game.cljs — each returns raw query results
- Opening hand already sorts in view (views/opening_hand.cljs:37) — inconsistent with this approach but we'll fix it
- re-frame subscriptions are memoized so sort only runs when data changes

**Pros:**
- Single source of truth for ordering
- Views stay pure (just render what they receive)
- Subscription memoization prevents redundant sorts

**Cons:**
- Slightly more complex subscriptions

**Chosen because:** Keeps views simple, ensures consistency, leverages re-frame memoization.

#### 2. Sort in view components ❌

**What it is:** Each view component sorts its own card list before rendering.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler per-component changes, no subscription modifications.

**Investigation:**
- Checked existing opening_hand.cljs pattern (sorts in view)
- Multiple views render same subscription data differently

**Pros:**
- No subscription changes needed

**Cons:**
- Sort logic duplicated across views
- Easy to miss a view and leave it unsorted
- No single source of truth

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Duplication leads to inconsistency — the exact problem we're solving.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We discover subscription-layer sorting causes performance issues (unlikely with memoization).

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- CMC+name sorting for all card displays
- Battlefield land/non-land split
- Graveyard castable partition + entry/sorted toggle
- All selection dialog sorting

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Color-based grouping (deferred — could add later)
- Custom user sort preferences (YAGNI)
- Creature row on battlefield (future work, architecture supports it)
- Sorting by card type beyond land/non-land (YAGNI for current card pool)

### Open Questions
- None — design is straightforward

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Primary sort order? | CMC then name | Universal sort key for all zones/dialogs |
| Graveyard ordering? | Toggle entry/sorted + castable partition on top | Needs toggle state, flashback query, split display |
| Battlefield grouping? | Non-land row + land row (future: creatures) | Subscription returns map with :lands :non-lands keys |
| Castable definition? | Has flashback keyword | Use existing ::flashback-castable-ids subscription |
| Dialog sorting? | Same sort everywhere | Apply sort-cards to ::selection-cards subscription |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Current Sort State
**Question explored:** Where does sorting currently exist?
**Sources consulted:**
- views/opening_hand.cljs:37,66 — sorts by card name
- subs/game.cljs:56-62 — stack sorts by position (LIFO)
- All other views — no sorting

**Findings:**
- Only 2 of ~12 card displays have any sorting
- Datascript query return order is non-deterministic
- Opening hand sorts by name only (not CMC)

**Conclusion:** Need universal sort applied at subscription layer

#### Graveyard Rules Relevance
**Question explored:** Should graveyard be sorted?
**Sources consulted:**
- Premodern format rules — graveyard order matters
- Card examples: Gaea's Blessing, Corpse Dance care about order

**Findings:**
- Graveyard order is rules-relevant in Premodern
- Flashback cards are the main interactive elements

**Conclusion:** Toggle between entry order and sorted, with castable partition always sorted on top

### Dead-End Paths
None — design was straightforward with clear user requirements.

### Open Concerns Raised
- Future creature row on battlefield → Architecture supports it (just add another filter key)
close_reason: All child tasks complete: sort utility (fizzle-x19c), battlefield split (fizzle-gcs8), graveyard enhancement (fizzle-87bv). 1212 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Task 2: Rewrite Anti-Pattern Tests
design:
## Goal
Rewrite three test files to eliminate anti-patterns: careful_study reimplements production handler, lotus_petal/led have copy-pasted color variants.

## Pre-conditions
- Task 1 (migration) complete — all files use shared test helpers
- make validate passes (1412 tests, 3153 assertions)

## Effort Estimate
3-5 hours (mechanical rewrites with verification between each file)

## Implementation

### 1. Fix careful_study_test.cljs — Replace handler reimplementation with production code path

**Problem:** test-careful-study-discard-via-production-handler (line 227) defines a manual confirm-fn (lines 262-286) that reimplements the discard confirm logic. This anti-pattern means the test passes even if the real confirm-selection-impl is broken.

**Production code path (reference):**
- selection.core/confirm-selection-impl (core.cljs:143) takes app-db, calls execute-confirmed-selection multimethod
- execute-confirmed-selection :discard (zone_ops.cljs:76) moves selected cards to graveyard
- confirm-selection-impl then processes remaining-effects and calls cleanup-selection-source
- cleanup-selection-source moves spell from stack to graveyard AND removes stack-item entity

**Fix:**
a. Add require: [fizzle.events.selection.core :as core] (if not present)
b. Ensure zone_ops namespace is loaded (bare require [fizzle.events.selection.zone-ops] — needed for :discard defmethod registration)
c. Replace the manual confirm-fn call (lines 262-287) with: (core/confirm-selection-impl app-db)
d. The test already builds proper app-db with {:game/db db-after-draw :game/pending-selection sel-with-choice}
e. Extract result: result-app-db = (core/confirm-selection-impl app-db), final-db = (:game/db result-app-db)
f. Keep ALL existing assertions: empty hand, 3 cards in graveyard, CS in graveyard, no pending selection
g. Run make validate after this change

**CRITICAL edge case:** The production cleanup-selection-source also removes the stack-item entity (via remove-spell-stack-item). The manual confirm-fn did NOT do this. After switching to production code, the stack-item count will differ. This is correct behavior — the test now exercises the real cleanup path.

### 2. Fix lotus_petal_test.cljs — Parameterize 5 identical color tests

**Problem:** 5 separate deftest blocks (lines 13-77) test the same behavior for :black, :blue, :white, :red, :green. They differ ONLY in the color keyword and pool access key.

**Current assertion count:** 5 tests × 2 core assertions (sacrifice + mana) + ~5 precondition assertions = ~15 assertions total

**Fix:**
a. Replace 5 deftests with a single deftest containing doseq:
```clojure
(deftest test-lotus-petal-sacrifice-for-any-color
  (doseq [color [:black :blue :white :red :green]]
    (testing (str "Lotus Petal sacrifices for " (name color) " mana")
      (let [db (th/create-test-db)
            [db' obj-id] (th/add-card-to-zone db :lotus-petal :battlefield :player-1)
            initial-pool (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)
            _ (is (= 0 (get initial-pool color)) (str "Precondition: " (name color) " mana is 0"))
            db'' (ability-events/activate-mana-ability db' :player-1 obj-id color)]
        (is (= :graveyard (th/get-object-zone db'' obj-id))
            "Lotus Petal should be in graveyard after sacrifice")
        (is (= 1 (get (q/get-mana-pool db'' :player-1) color))
            (str (name color) " mana should be added to pool"))))))
```
b. Keep edge case tests (graveyard, hand, definition) completely unchanged
c. Final assertion count: 5 × 3 = 15 (precondition + sacrifice + mana) — same coverage
d. Run make validate

### 3. Fix led_test.cljs — Parameterize 3 color tests into comprehensive parameterized test

**Problem:** 3 separate deftests (lines 14-63) test black, blue, white. Black is detailed (4 assertions), blue/white are minimal (2 assertions each). Also missing :red and :green coverage.

**Current assertion count:** black(4 core + 3 preconditions) + blue(2 core + 1 precondition) + white(2 core + 1 precondition) = 13 total

**Fix:**
a. Replace 3 deftests with one parameterized test using doseq over ALL 5 colors:
```clojure
(deftest test-led-sacrifice-and-discard-for-any-color
  (doseq [color [:black :blue :white :red :green]]
    (testing (str "LED sacrifices and discards hand for 3 " (name color) " mana")
      (let [db (th/create-test-db)
            [db' led-id] (th/add-card-to-zone db :lions-eye-diamond :battlefield :player-1)
            [db'' _] (th/add-card-to-zone db' :dark-ritual :hand :player-1)
            [db''' _] (th/add-card-to-zone db'' :dark-ritual :hand :player-1)
            [db'''' _] (th/add-card-to-zone db''' :dark-ritual :hand :player-1)
            _ (is (= :battlefield (th/get-object-zone db'''' led-id))
                  "Precondition: LED starts on battlefield")
            _ (is (= 3 (th/get-zone-count db'''' :hand :player-1))
                  "Precondition: 3 cards in hand")
            initial-pool (q/get-mana-pool db'''' :player-1)
            _ (is (= 0 (get initial-pool color))
                  (str "Precondition: " (name color) " mana is 0"))
            db-after (ability-events/activate-mana-ability db'''' :player-1 led-id color)]
        (is (= :graveyard (th/get-object-zone db-after led-id))
            "LED should be in graveyard after sacrifice")
        (is (= 3 (get (q/get-mana-pool db-after :player-1) color))
            (str (name color) " mana should be 3"))
        (is (= 0 (th/get-zone-count db-after :hand :player-1))
            "Hand should be empty after discard")
        (is (= 4 (th/get-zone-count db-after :graveyard :player-1))
            "Graveyard should have 4 objects (3 hand cards + LED)")))))
```
b. Keep ALL edge case tests unchanged (empty hand, graveyard, hand, tapped, definition)
c. Final assertion count: 5 × 7 = 35 (3 preconditions + 4 core) — INCREASED from 13
d. Run make validate

### 4. Verify and commit
- Run make validate
- Record assertion count (should be >= 3153)
- No lint/format warnings
- Commit changes

## Success Criteria
- [ ] careful_study_test.cljs test-careful-study-discard-via-production-handler calls core/confirm-selection-impl (zero manual handler reimplementation)
- [ ] careful_study_test.cljs requires zone_ops namespace (ensures :discard defmethod loaded)
- [ ] lotus_petal_test.cljs has single parameterized deftest with doseq over 5 colors (replacing 5 separate deftests)
- [ ] led_test.cljs has single parameterized deftest with doseq over 5 colors (replacing 3 separate deftests)
- [ ] All existing tests pass (make validate green)
- [ ] No lint or format warnings
- [ ] Total assertion count >= 3153 (no coverage loss, LED gains coverage for :red/:green)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: zone_ops namespace loading**
- The :discard defmethod is registered in zone_ops.cljs, not core.cljs
- If zone_ops is not loaded, execute-confirmed-selection will dispatch to the default method (likely an error)
- MUST add bare require [fizzle.events.selection.zone-ops] to careful_study_test.cljs
- Verify: check if resolution.cljs already requires zone_ops transitively — if yes, bare require is defensive but not strictly needed

**Edge Case: stack-item cleanup difference**
- Current manual confirm-fn does NOT call remove-spell-stack-item
- Production confirm-selection-impl DOES call it via cleanup-selection-source
- This means after switching to production code, stack-item entity will be properly cleaned up
- If any assertion checks stack-item count, it may need updating (currently none do)

**Edge Case: cleanup? flag in selection**
- The :discard multimethod checks :selection/cleanup? flag — if true, takes cleanup path (expire grants, finalized)
- resolve-spell-with-selection creates a standard selection (not cleanup), so this flag should be false/nil
- No action needed, but be aware cleanup discard behaves differently

**Edge Case: LED produces :any mana**
- LED's ability/produces is {:any 3}, meaning it produces 3 mana of chosen color
- Parameterized test should cover all 5 colors to verify the :any production logic works for each
- Current tests only cover 3 colors — this is a coverage improvement

**Edge Case: doseq assertion reporting**
- cljs.test with doseq: failures report line number of the deftest, not the specific doseq iteration
- MUST include the color name in assertion messages (e.g., str "Black mana should be 3") so failures identify which color failed
- All assertion messages must be dynamically constructed with (str (name color) ...)

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO changing test BEHAVIOR — only change test STRUCTURE (parameterize, use production code)
- ❌ NO weakening assertions — each parameterized variant must assert the same things as the original
- ❌ NO mocking or stubbing production code — use real confirm-selection-impl
- ❌ NO removing edge case tests — only touch the duplicated/reimplemented tests
- ❌ NO removing unused requires without verifying they're truly unused (e.g., datascript.core may be needed elsewhere)
- ❌ NO hardcoding color lists — use a vector constant so new colors can be added later
close_reason: All three anti-pattern rewrites complete: careful_study uses production confirm-selection-impl, lotus_petal and led use doseq parameterization. make validate passes (1406 tests, 3174 assertions). Net -95 lines.

title: Add pay-mana-with-allocation to engine/mana.cljs
description: Engine foundation for manual mana allocation. Add a new function pay-mana-with-allocation that accepts an explicit allocation map for generic costs instead of using the greedy algorithm. Colored costs still auto-deducted. This is the foundation that selection events and integration will build on.
design:
## Goal
Add pay-mana-with-allocation function to engine/mana.cljs that deducts colored costs normally, then applies an explicit generic allocation map instead of greedy sorting.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours (one function + 10-12 tests)

## Key Design Decision
The function takes a ALREADY-RESOLVED cost (no :x key). The caller (selection confirm handler) calls resolve-x-cost first, then passes the resolved cost + allocation map. This keeps the function simple and matches the casting flow where X selection completes before mana allocation.

## Function Signature
```clojure
(defn pay-mana-with-allocation
  "Remove mana from player's pool using explicit allocation for generic costs.

   cost: Mana cost map with :colorless for generic, color keys for colored.
         MUST NOT contain :x — caller resolves X before calling.
   allocation: Map of {color amount} specifying how to pay generic portion.
               e.g., {:black 2 :blue 1} means 'pay 2 generic from black, 1 from blue'
               Sum of allocation values MUST equal (:colorless cost 0).

   IMPORTANT: Caller MUST verify can-pay? first AND that allocation is valid.
   Invalid allocation (wrong sum, exceeds pool) results in undefined behavior."
  [db player-id cost allocation]
  ...)
```

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - engine/mana.cljs:75-114 — current pay-mana with greedy allocation
   - engine/mana.cljs:42-62 — can-pay? validation
   - engine/mana.cljs:28-39 — add-mana (merge-with pattern for pool updates)
   - src/test/fizzle/engine/mana_test.cljs — all existing tests

2. Write tests first (TDD)

   **Core behavior tests:**
   - test-pay-allocated-single-color: pool {:black 5 :blue 0 ...}, cost {:colorless 2 :black 1}, allocation {:black 2} → pool {:black 2} (1 colored + 2 allocated from black)
   - test-pay-allocated-multi-color: pool {:black 3 :blue 2}, cost {:colorless 2 :black 1}, allocation {:black 1 :blue 1} → pool {:black 1 :blue 1}
   - test-pay-allocated-preserves-unused-colors: pool {:black 3 :blue 2 :red 1}, cost {:colorless 2}, allocation {:black 2} → red stays 1, blue stays 2
   - test-pay-allocated-zero-generic: cost {:black 2}, allocation {} → just deducts colored, pool updated correctly
   - test-pay-allocated-only-generic: cost {:colorless 3}, allocation {:black 2 :blue 1} → no colored deduction, just allocation

   **Edge case tests (SRE additions):**
   - test-pay-allocated-colorless-mana-in-allocation: pool {:colorless 2 :black 1}, cost {:colorless 2}, allocation {:colorless 1 :black 1} → uses actual colorless mana toward generic
   - test-pay-allocated-all-from-one-color: pool {:black 5}, cost {:colorless 3 :black 2}, allocation {:black 3} → pool {:black 0}
   - test-pay-allocated-empty-cost-empty-allocation: cost {}, allocation {} → pool unchanged (no-op)
   - test-pay-allocated-large-pool-values: pool {:black 100}, cost {:colorless 50}, allocation {:black 50} → pool {:black 50} (no precision issues)

   **Contract documentation tests:**
   - test-pay-allocated-without-can-pay-goes-negative: documents that calling without can-pay? results in negative pool (matches pay-mana behavior, caller responsibility)

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] engine/mana.cljs — Add pay-mana-with-allocation function with signature above
   - [ ] Implementation: separate colored-cost (dissoc :colorless) from cost, deduct colored via (merge-with - pool colored-cost), then deduct allocation via (merge-with - pool-after-colored allocation)
   - [ ] Handle empty cost as no-op (return db unchanged, matching pay-mana pattern)
   - [ ] Handle empty allocation (zero generic) — just deduct colored costs
   - [ ] src/test/fizzle/engine/mana_test.cljs — Add all test cases above in a new '=== pay-mana-with-allocation tests ===' section

## Success Criteria
- [ ] pay-mana-with-allocation deducts colored costs from specific color pools (e.g., {:black 1} cost removes 1 from :black)
- [ ] pay-mana-with-allocation applies allocation map to deduct generic (e.g., allocation {:black 2} removes 2 from :black)
- [ ] Untouched colors remain unchanged (pool {:red 1} stays {:red 1} when red not in cost or allocation)
- [ ] Colorless mana in pool can be allocated toward generic ({:colorless 2} in pool, {:colorless 2} in allocation works)
- [ ] Empty cost with empty allocation is a no-op
- [ ] Existing pay-mana function unchanged: grep for pay-mana calls shows no modifications needed
- [ ] All 10+ new tests pass
- [ ] All existing 25+ mana tests pass unchanged
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO modifying existing pay-mana function (preserve greedy behavior for internal use)
- ❌ NO validation inside pay-mana-with-allocation (matches pay-mana contract: caller MUST check can-pay? first)
- ❌ NO :x handling inside pay-mana-with-allocation (caller resolves X before calling — separation of concerns)
- ❌ NO atoms or mutable state (pure function: db in, db out)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Colorless mana in pool:**
- The pool has a :colorless key (from cards that add colorless mana or Eldrazi lands)
- Colorless mana in the pool CAN be used to pay generic costs via allocation
- Allocation map {:colorless 2} means 'use 2 colorless mana from pool for generic'
- This is distinct from :colorless in cost (which means generic requirement)

**Allocation map validity (caller responsibility):**
- Sum of allocation values must equal (:colorless cost 0)
- Each color in allocation must not exceed remaining pool after colored deduction
- These are enforced by the selection system (auto-confirm only when remaining=0, only clickable colors shown)
- Engine function trusts caller, matching pay-mana's contract pattern

**Shared code with pay-mana:**
- Both functions share: resolve empty cost, separate colored/generic, deduct colored via merge-with
- Do NOT extract a shared helper yet — wait to see if the pattern stabilizes across the full feature
- Duplication of 3-4 lines is acceptable; premature abstraction is not

**Reference implementation:**
- Follow pay-mana at mana.cljs:75-114 as the structural template
- Same let-binding pattern: player-eid, current-pool, colored-cost, pool-after-colored
- Different only in the generic deduction step: allocation map instead of greedy loop
close_reason: Closed

title: Add turn structure with phase advancement
design:
## Goal

Implement turn structure and phase advancement so players can take multiple turns. This is foundational for the 'Player can take multiple turns' success criterion.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-c69 (draw effect) and fizzle-yxj (lose/gain-life effects). The engine can now draw cards and track life. Next we need turn progression so players can:
- Advance through phases (untap, upkeep, draw, main1, combat, main2, end)
- Start new turns (which resets storm count, untaps permanents)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code:
- src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs:54-57 - :game/turn, :game/phase, :game/step already exist
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs - existing re-frame event patterns (see ::init-game, ::cast-spell)
- src/main/fizzle/engine/mana.cljs - has empty-pool function to clear mana

### 2. Write tests first (TDD):

**Test file:** src/test/fizzle/events/turn_test.cljs (new file)

| Test Name | Bug It Catches |
|-----------|----------------|
| test-advance-phase-from-main1-to-combat | Basic phase advancement wrong |
| test-advance-phase-from-combat-to-main2 | Phase sequence ordering wrong |
| test-advance-phase-from-cleanup-stays-cleanup | Auto-advancing to next turn (should require explicit start-turn) |
| test-advance-phase-clears-mana-pool | Mana persisting across phases |
| test-start-turn-increments-turn-number | Turn counter not incrementing |
| test-start-turn-resets-storm-count | Storm count carrying over between turns |
| test-start-turn-resets-land-plays-to-one | Land plays not resetting to 1 |
| test-start-turn-sets-phase-to-untap | Turn not starting at untap phase |
| test-start-turn-clears-mana-pool | Mana persisting from previous turn |

### 3. Implementation checklist:
- [ ] Define phase sequence constant in events/game.cljs:
  ```clojure
  (def phases [:untap :upkeep :draw :main1 :combat :main2 :end :cleanup])
  ```
- [ ] Add helper function to get next phase:
  ```clojure
  (defn next-phase [current-phase]
    (let [idx (.indexOf phases current-phase)]
      (if (= idx (dec (count phases)))
        current-phase  ;; Stay at cleanup, don't auto-advance
        (nth phases (inc idx)))))
  ```
- [ ] Add ::advance-phase event:
  - Get current phase from :game/phase
  - Calculate next phase using next-phase helper
  - Clear mana pool using (mana/empty-pool db player-id)
  - Update :game/phase in datascript
- [ ] Add ::start-turn event:
  - Increment :game/turn
  - Set :game/phase to :untap
  - Reset :player/storm-count to 0 for active player
  - Reset :player/land-plays-left to 1 for active player
  - Clear mana pool using (mana/empty-pool db player-id)
- [ ] Add subscriptions in subs/game.cljs:
  ```clojure
  (rf/reg-sub ::current-phase
    (fn [db] (some-> (:game/db db) queries/get-game-state :game/phase)))
  
  (rf/reg-sub ::current-turn
    (fn [db] (some-> (:game/db db) queries/get-game-state :game/turn)))
  ```

### 4. Reference patterns:
- Follow ::cast-spell pattern for event structure (events/game.cljs:86-95)
- Use d/db-with for datascript updates (see effects.cljs:68-69)
- Get game entity ID pattern (events/game.cljs:70-74)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] test-advance-phase-from-main1-to-combat passes: phase main1 → combat
- [ ] test-advance-phase-from-combat-to-main2 passes: phase combat → main2
- [ ] test-advance-phase-from-cleanup-stays-cleanup passes: cleanup → cleanup (NOT untap)
- [ ] test-advance-phase-clears-mana-pool passes: mana pool all zeros after advance
- [ ] test-start-turn-increments-turn-number passes: turn 1 → turn 2
- [ ] test-start-turn-resets-storm-count passes: storm count → 0
- [ ] test-start-turn-resets-land-plays-to-one passes: land-plays-left → 1
- [ ] test-start-turn-sets-phase-to-untap passes: phase → :untap
- [ ] test-start-turn-clears-mana-pool passes: mana pool all zeros
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (make test)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (make validate)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Cleanup Phase Boundary**
- Advancing from :cleanup MUST NOT auto-start next turn
- User must explicitly call ::start-turn to begin new turn
- Why: Allows UI to prompt "End turn?" or handle end-of-turn effects
- Test: test-advance-phase-from-cleanup-stays-cleanup verifies this

**Edge Case: Mana Pool Clearing**
- MUST clear mana pool at EVERY phase transition (not just end of turn)
- Per modern MTG rules, mana empties at each phase/step transition
- Use existing mana/empty-pool function (check it exists, may need to add)

**Edge Case: Land Plays Reset**
- Default land plays per turn is 1 (not 0, not unlimited)
- MUST reset to 1 at turn start, not at end of previous turn
- Why: Effects like "play an additional land" need to know base value

**Edge Case: Storm Count Scope**
- Storm count ONLY resets at turn start
- Does NOT reset at phase transitions
- Why: Storm triggers care about spells THIS TURN across all phases

**Reference Implementation**
- Study mana/empty-pool or mana/add-mana for pool manipulation pattern
- Study ::cast-spell event for re-frame event structure

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO infinite mana pools (mana empties at phase transitions)
- ❌ NO skipping untap step (turns MUST start at untap)
- ❌ NO auto-advancing from cleanup to next turn (must be explicit)
- ❌ NO hardcoded player-id :player-1 in events (use db to get active player)
- ❌ NO resetting storm count at phase transitions (only at turn start)
close_reason: Closed

title: Visual card styling: color and type differentiation
description: Cards in hand, battlefield, graveyard, and stack all look the same visually. Add styling variation based on card properties: color identity (black/blue/white border or background tint), card type (instant/sorcery/land/artifact with icons or distinct shapes). Helps with at-a-glance board reading during fast combo practice. Consider: color-coded borders or subtle background gradients, type indicator icons or labels, consistent scheme across all zones (hand, battlefield, graveyard, stack, setup deck lists).

title: Feature: Add Cephalid Coliseum card
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Card has two abilities as per oracle text
- Mana ability: {T} produces {U} and deals 1 damage to controller as part of ability resolution
- Threshold ability: {U}, {T}, Sacrifice - target player draws 3, then discards 3
- Threshold condition: 7+ cards in graveyard (use existing threshold check)
- Player targeting: user selects self or opponent for draw/discard effect
- Discard selection: user chooses which 3 cards to discard from hand

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Card definition follows existing patterns in cards/iggy_pop.cljs
- [ ] Mana ability produces {U} and deals 1 damage to self
- [ ] Threshold ability only activatable when 7+ cards in graveyard
- [ ] Threshold ability costs {U}, tap, and sacrifice
- [ ] Player targeting modal appears for draw effect
- [ ] Card selection modal appears for discard (player chooses 3)
- [ ] Card registered in all-cards vector
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO trigger for damage (damage is part of mana ability via :ability/effects, not a :becomes-tapped trigger like City of Brass)
- ❌ NO auto-selecting discard targets (must use :effect/selection :player for user choice)
- ❌ NO skipping player target selection (must use :effect/target :any-player)
- ❌ NO hardcoding threshold check (must use existing :condition/type :threshold)

## Approach
Define Cephalid Coliseum as a land with two abilities following existing codebase patterns. The mana ability uses :ability/effects to deal damage as part of resolution. The threshold ability uses :ability/condition to gate activation, with effects that leverage existing player targeting and card selection systems.

## Architecture
- src/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum.cljs - New card definition file
- src/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Register in all-cards vector
- src/test/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs - Test coverage

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Cephalid Coliseum is a key card in Iggy Pop storm for graveyard filling and card selection. Currently missing from the card pool.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:58-78 - City of Brass pattern (trigger-based damage, NOT what we want)
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:171-182 - Lotus Petal pattern (mana ability with sacrifice)
- cards/deep_analysis.cljs - Player targeting with :effect/target :any-player
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:204-218 - Careful Study pattern (draw then discard with selection)
- engine/conditions.cljs - Threshold check implementation

**External:**
- Scryfall oracle: {T}: Add {U}. Deals 1 damage. Threshold: {U}, {T}, Sacrifice: Target player draws 3, discards 3.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Two Separate Abilities with :ability/effects for damage ✓

**What it is:** Mana ability produces {U} with :ability/effects dealing damage. Separate activated ability for threshold effect using existing player targeting and card selection.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed mana abilities - can have :ability/effects for side effects
- Confirmed threshold conditions work on abilities
- Player targeting and discard selection already implemented

**Pros:**
- Matches oracle text exactly (damage part of mana ability)
- Leverages all existing systems
- Clean separation of concerns

**Cons:**
- None significant

**Chosen because:** Accurately implements oracle text using existing patterns

#### 2. City of Brass trigger pattern for damage ❌

**What it is:** Use :trigger/type :becomes-tapped like City of Brass to deal damage whenever land taps.

**Why we looked at this:** City of Brass is another painland, seemed similar

**Investigation:**
- Compared oracle texts - City of Brass triggers on ANY tap, Coliseum damage is part of mana ability only

**Pros:**
- Existing pattern in codebase

**Cons:**
- Incorrect rules implementation - trigger would fire even if tapped by other effects

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Oracle text specifies damage as part of mana ability, not a trigger

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Oracle text changes

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Mana ability with damage
- Threshold activated ability
- Player targeting for draw
- Card selection for discard

**Out of scope:**
- Opponent AI using the ability (goldfish mode sufficient)

### Open Questions
- None - design is straightforward using existing patterns

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Target player for threshold ability? | Choice: self or opponent | Use :effect/target :any-player |
| Discard selection mode? | Player chooses | Use :effect/selection :player |
| Damage implementation? | Part of mana ability, not trigger | Use :ability/effects, not trigger |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Damage Implementation Pattern
**Question explored:** How to implement 'deals 1 damage to you' on mana ability?
**Sources consulted:**
- City of Brass (trigger pattern - rejected)
- Mana ability :ability/effects field

**Findings:**
- City of Brass uses trigger because it fires on ANY tap
- Coliseum damage is specifically part of mana ability text
- :ability/effects allows side effects during ability resolution

**Conclusion:** Use :ability/effects for damage, not trigger

### Dead-End Paths

#### City of Brass Trigger Pattern
**Why explored:** Both are painlands, seemed like same pattern
**Investigation:** Compared oracle texts carefully
**Why abandoned:** Different rules meaning - trigger vs ability effect

### Open Concerns Raised
- None
close_reason: All success criteria met - Cephalid Coliseum card implemented with full test coverage
