title: Task 1: Write comprehensive tests for db/queries.cljs
design:
## Goal
Create queries_test.cljs covering all 19 public query functions in db/queries.cljs with positive, negative, and edge cases.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Pre-existing Coverage
schema_test.cljs (lines 47-71) already tests 3 query functions as edge cases:
- get-mana-pool nil for nonexistent player
- get-hand empty returns []
- get-card by object-id
DO NOT duplicate these. The new queries_test.cljs should test DIFFERENT scenarios for these functions (e.g., get-mana-pool with valid player, get-hand with multiple cards). Reference schema_test.cljs for shared context.

## Implementation

1. Study existing test patterns
   - src/test/fizzle/cards/led_test.cljs:14-35 - create-test-db pattern with all-cards
   - src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs:1-11 - imports and db/init usage
   - src/test/fizzle/db/schema_test.cljs:47-71 - existing query edge case tests (DO NOT duplicate)
   - Naming convention: function-name-scenario-test (e.g., get-hand-returns-objects-with-card-data-test)

2. Test DB setup helpers (define in queries_test.cljs)

   Basic setup - use init-game-state for tests needing just player + hand:
   \`\`\`clojure
   (let [db (init-game-state)]
     ;; Has: player-1 with 20 life, empty mana pool, 0 storm, 1 Dark Ritual in hand
     ;; Has: game state at turn 1, main1 phase
     ...)
   \`\`\`

   Trigger setup - transact trigger entities directly:
   \`\`\`clojure
   (let [db (init-game-state)
         hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
         source-id (:object/id (first hand))
         trigger-id (random-uuid)
         conn (d/conn-from-db db)]
     (d/transact! conn [{:trigger/id trigger-id
                         :trigger/type :storm
                         :trigger/source source-id
                         :trigger/controller :player-1
                         :trigger/stack-order 0
                         :trigger/description "Storm copy"}])
     @conn)
   \`\`\`

   Library with positions - add objects with :object/position:
   \`\`\`clojure
   (let [db (create-test-db) ;; LED test helper loads all-cards
         conn (d/conn-from-db db)
         player-eid (q/get-player-eid db :player-1)]
     (doseq [[idx card-id] (map-indexed vector [:dark-ritual :dark-ritual :brain-freeze])]
       (let [card-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] @conn card-id)]
         (d/transact! conn [{:object/id (random-uuid)
                             :object/card card-eid
                             :object/zone :library
                             :object/owner player-eid
                             :object/controller player-eid
                             :object/position idx
                             :object/tapped false}])))
     @conn)
   \`\`\`

   Opponent setup - add second player with :player/is-opponent:
   \`\`\`clojure
   (let [db (init-game-state)
         conn (d/conn-from-db db)]
     (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :opponent
                         :player/name "Opponent"
                         :player/life 20
                         :player/is-opponent true
                         :player/mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
                         :player/storm-count 0}])
     @conn)
   \`\`\`

3. Tests for all 19 functions (organized by group):

   **Player queries:**
   - get-player-eid: valid player-id returns integer eid; :nonexistent returns nil
   - get-mana-pool: valid player returns pool map (schema_test covers nil case)
   - get-storm-count: returns 0 initially, returns updated value after transact
   - get-life-total: returns 20 initially; nil for nonexistent player
   - get-max-hand-size: defaults to 7 when attribute not set; returns custom value when set
   - get-opponent-id: returns :opponent when opponent exists; nil when no opponent

   **Object queries:**
   - get-hand: returns vector of objects with :object/card pulled as map (not ref); (schema_test covers empty and nil)
   - get-card: returns card definition map with :card/name etc.; nil for nonexistent object-id
   - get-object: returns full object with card pulled in; nil for random-uuid (nonexistent)
   - get-objects-in-zone: returns vec for :hand, :graveyard, :battlefield; empty vec for zone with no objects; nil for nonexistent player

   **Game state queries:**
   - get-game-state: returns map with :game/turn, :game/phase; :game/active-player is PULLED (a map with :player/id and :player/name, not an integer ref)

   **Stack queries:**
   - get-trigger: returns trigger map by id; nil for nonexistent trigger-id
   - get-next-stack-order: returns 0 for empty stack; returns max(trigger-orders, object-positions)+1 when BOTH triggers and objects exist on stack
   - get-stack-items: returns triggers sorted by stack-order descending (LIFO); empty vec for empty stack; does NOT include spell objects (only triggers)
   - stack-empty?: true when no triggers AND no spells on stack; false when only triggers; false when only spells; false when both

   **Library queries:**
   - get-top-n-library: returns top N object-ids sorted by position ascending; returns all available when n > library size; returns [] when n=0; nil for nonexistent player

   **Trigger queries:**
   - get-trigger-source-card-name: returns card name string from trigger's source object; nil when trigger has no :trigger/source; nil when source object doesn't exist (removed)

   **Criteria queries:**
   - query-zone-by-criteria: types uses AND (must have ALL); colors uses OR (any match); subtypes uses OR; empty criteria {} matches all objects in zone; criteria matching nothing returns []; nil for nonexistent player
   - query-library-by-criteria: correctly delegates to query-zone-by-criteria with :library zone

4. Test setup approach
   - Use db/init/init-game-state for basic tests
   - Use led_test.cljs create-test-db pattern (loads all-cards) for tests needing multiple card types
   - Use d/conn-from-db + d/transact! for additional state
   - Pure Datascript - no re-frame

## Success Criteria
- [ ] src/test/fizzle/db/queries_test.cljs exists with 35+ test assertions
- [ ] All 19 public functions have at least one positive test AND at least one edge case test
- [ ] No duplication with existing schema_test.cljs tests (lines 47-71)
- [ ] Trigger setup tests verify get-trigger, get-stack-items, get-next-stack-order, stack-empty?
- [ ] get-next-stack-order tested with BOTH triggers and objects on stack simultaneously
- [ ] stack-empty? tested with 4 scenarios: empty/triggers-only/spells-only/both
- [ ] query-zone-by-criteria tested with AND logic (types), OR logic (colors, subtypes), empty criteria, and no-match criteria
- [ ] get-top-n-library tested with n=0, n < library size, n > library size
- [ ] get-game-state asserts :game/active-player is a pulled map (not integer ref)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: get-top-n-library with n=0**
- Docstring says returns [] for fewer than N cards
- n=0 should return [] (take 0 of anything)
- Verify this works, don't assume

**Edge Case: get-next-stack-order with mixed stack**
- Must check BOTH trigger stack-orders AND object positions
- Test: trigger at order 2, object at position 5 → should return 6
- This catches the bug where only triggers OR only objects are checked

**Edge Case: get-trigger-source-card-name with removed source**
- Trigger can reference source object-id that no longer exists (removed via remove-object)
- Should return nil gracefully, not throw

**Edge Case: query-zone-by-criteria with empty criteria {}**
- Empty criteria map should match ALL objects in zone (no filters = everything passes)
- Catches bug where empty set check fails

**Existing Test Overlap**
- schema_test.cljs already covers: mana-pool nil, hand empty, get-card by id
- DO NOT write tests for these exact same scenarios
- Write complementary tests (valid cases, different edge cases)

## Anti-patterns
- NO mocking Datascript (pure function tests against real DB)
- NO test-only methods in production code
- NO duplicating tests already in schema_test.cljs
- NO testing private functions directly (test matches-criteria? through query-zone-by-criteria)
- NO using (is (some? result)) as sole assertion - always assert specific values
close_reason: All 19 query functions tested with 51 tests/90 assertions. make validate passes (792 tests, 1677 assertions).

title: Test Quality Improvement: Fizzle Game Engine
design:
## Goal
Improve test effectiveness by removing tautological tests, strengthening weak tests, and adding missing corner case coverage.

## Analysis Summary
- Total tests analyzed: 591
- GREEN (keep): 487 (82%)
- YELLOW (strengthen): 89 (15%)
- RED (remove/replace): 15 (3%)
- Missing corner cases: 47

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 15 RED tests removed or replaced with meaningful tests
- [ ] All YELLOW tests with weak assertions strengthened
- [ ] P0 missing corner cases covered (engine/effects, engine/mana, engine/rules)
- [ ] No new tautological tests introduced

## Scope
Modules analyzed:
- engine/ (287 tests) - Core game logic
- cards/ (185 tests) - Card implementations
- events/ (107 tests) - Re-frame event handlers
- db/ (8 tests) - Schema

## Anti-patterns to Avoid
- Adding tests that only check `!= nil` or `some?`
- Adding tests that verify mock/helper behavior instead of production code
- Adding happy-path-only tests
- Keeping tautological tests "for coverage"
- Testing implementation details rather than behavior
close_reason: All success criteria met: RED tests removed/replaced, YELLOW tests strengthened, P0 corner cases covered, no tautological tests added. Test suite: 602 tests, 1321 assertions passing.

title: Data-Driven Ability System
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Card abilities defined as EDN data in :card/abilities field
- Card triggers defined as EDN data in :card/triggers field
- Card ETB effects defined as EDN data in :card/etb-effects field
- Cost payment uses multimethod dispatch on cost type (extensible)
- Ability activation uses generic engine, no card-specific checks in events
- State-based actions use registry pattern (extensible)
- City of Brass uses :card/triggers with :becomes-tapped trigger type
- Gemstone Mine uses :card/abilities with :remove-counter cost and :card/etb-effects
- :deal-damage effect type distinct from :lose-life (damage vs life loss)
- :add-counters effect type for ETB counter initialization
- No card IDs checked in engine code (data over code principle)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] City of Brass defined with :card/triggers and :card/abilities (no hardcoded logic)
- [ ] Gemstone Mine defined with :card/abilities and :card/etb-effects (no hardcoded logic)
- [ ] engine/costs.cljs exists with multimethod for :tap and :remove-counter
- [ ] engine/abilities.cljs exists with generic mana ability activation
- [ ] engine/state_based.cljs exists with registry pattern
- [ ] :becomes-tapped trigger type implemented in engine/triggers.cljs
- [ ] :deal-damage and :add-counters effect types in engine/effects.cljs
- [ ] events/game.cljs activate-mana-ability has NO card ID checks
- [ ] Gemstone Mine sacrifices when counters reach 0 (via state-based action)
- [ ] City of Brass deals damage when tapped (via trigger, not ability effect)
- [ ] All existing tests still pass
- [ ] New tests cover ability/trigger/cost/SBA systems
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO card ID checks in engine code (violates data-over-code: engine interprets data generically)
- ❌ NO hardcoded cost handling in cond-> blocks (use multimethod dispatch for extensibility)
- ❌ NO hardcoded SBA checks in single function (use registry for extensibility)
- ❌ NO conflating damage with life loss (MTG rules: damage can be prevented, life loss cannot)
- ❌ NO ability effects for tap triggers (City of Brass damage is trigger, not mana ability effect)
- ❌ NO modifying existing methods to add new cost types (add new multimethod instead)

## Approach

Extend the existing multimethod pattern (used for effects and triggers) to abilities and costs. Create three new engine modules: costs.cljs for cost payment dispatch, abilities.cljs for generic ability activation, state_based.cljs for SBA registry. Update card definitions to use pure data, refactor events/game.cljs to read card data and delegate to engine.

## Architecture

**New Modules:**
- engine/costs.cljs - Multimethod dispatch on cost type (:tap, :remove-counter)
- engine/abilities.cljs - Generic ability activation, can-activate?, pay-all-costs
- engine/state_based.cljs - Registry of SBA check functions

**Updated Modules:**
- engine/effects.cljs - Add :deal-damage, :add-counters effect types
- engine/triggers.cljs - Add :becomes-tapped trigger type
- db/schema.cljs - Add :card/etb-effects, :card/triggers fields
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Update City of Brass, Gemstone Mine definitions
- events/game.cljs - Refactor activate-mana-ability to use generic engine

**Data Flow:**
1. Player activates mana ability on land
2. events/game.cljs reads :card/abilities from card data
3. engine/abilities.cljs checks can-activate? using engine/costs.cljs
4. engine/costs.cljs pays each cost via multimethod dispatch
5. Mana added to pool
6. engine/triggers.cljs fires :becomes-tapped triggers
7. engine/state_based.cljs checks all registered SBAs

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Mana abilities currently hardcode card-specific logic in events/game.cljs (line 382: checking card-id for City of Brass). This violates the 'data over code' principle. Every new land requires code changes instead of just data.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- engine/effects.cljs:13-154 - Multimethod pattern for effects, works well
- engine/triggers.cljs:53-126 - Same pattern for triggers
- db/schema.cljs:25 - :card/abilities field exists but unused
- events/game.cljs:382 - Hardcoded City of Brass check (architecture violation)

**MTG Rules:**
- Damage vs life loss are distinct (damage preventable, life loss not)
- City of Brass trigger fires on ANY tap, not just mana ability
- State-based actions checked after any game action

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Multimethod Pattern for Costs and Abilities ✓

**What it is:** Apply same multimethod dispatch pattern used for effects/triggers to costs and abilities. Registry pattern for state-based actions.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed engine/effects.cljs - multimethod pattern extensible, tested
- Pattern allows adding new types without modifying existing code

**Pros:**
- Consistent with existing architecture
- Extensible without code modification
- Each cost/SBA type independently testable

**Cons:**
- More files/indirection than hardcoded approach

**Chosen because:** Matches existing patterns, truly extensible, testable in isolation.

#### 2. Conditional Cost Handling ❌

**What it is:** Use cond-> in pay-costs function to handle each cost type.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler initial implementation.

**Investigation:**
- Would require modifying pay-costs for every new cost type
- Same problem as current hardcoded approach

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Not extensible - adding :pay-life, :sacrifice, :discard would require modifying existing code.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We decide extensibility doesn't matter (it does for MTG).

#### 3. Keep Mana Abilities Special ❌

**What it is:** Only data-drive non-mana abilities, keep mana abilities as event handlers.

**Why we looked at this:** Less refactoring effort.

**Investigation:**
- Every new land still requires code changes
- Violates stated architecture principle

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Violates 'data over code' principle explicitly stated in CLAUDE.md.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We abandon the data-over-code architecture entirely.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Mana abilities with :tap and :remove-counter costs
- :becomes-tapped trigger type
- ETB effects for counter initialization
- State-based action for 0-counter sacrifice
- City of Brass and Gemstone Mine refactored

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Non-mana activated abilities (no cards need them yet)
- Other trigger types (no cards need them yet)
- Other cost types beyond :tap/:remove-counter (no cards need them yet)
- Damage prevention mechanics (Phase 2+)

### Open Questions
- Should triggers use the stack? (default: no for mana ability triggers, matches MTG)
- SBA check frequency? (default: after every game action that modifies state)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Tap cost explicit or implicit? | Explicit in ability data | {:ability/cost {:tap true}} |
| Mana color choice | Player chooses at activation | {:ability/mana {:any 1}}, color passed as param |
| ETB effects location | Separate :card/etb-effects field | Not part of :card/abilities |
| Counter costs | Generic cost system | {:ability/cost {:remove-counter {:mining 1}}} |
| Sacrifice timing | State-based action | Engine checks after resolution, not ability-specific |
| City of Brass damage | Triggered ability, not mana ability effect | Separate :card/triggers field |
| Damage vs life loss | Distinct effect types | :deal-damage preventable, :lose-life not |
| Extensibility pattern | Multimethods for costs, registry for SBAs | No cond-> blocks that need modification |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Effect System Pattern
**Question explored:** How does current effect system work?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/effects.cljs:13-154 - multimethod implementation
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - effect data structures

**Findings:**
- Multimethod dispatches on :effect/type
- Pure functions: (db, player-id, effect) -> db
- Unknown types return db unchanged (graceful degradation)

**Conclusion:** Apply same pattern to costs and abilities

### Dead-End Paths

#### Ability Effects for Tap Triggers
**Why explored:** Initially modeled City of Brass damage as part of mana ability
**Investigation:** User pointed out card text says 'whenever becomes tapped' - separate trigger

**Why abandoned:** Incorrect MTG rules modeling - damage should fire on ANY tap, not just mana ability

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Feels like a smell to keep adding to methods' → Multimethod pattern solves this
- 'Damage vs life loss distinction' → Added :deal-damage as separate effect type
close_reason: All success criteria met. 207 tests pass. Implementation verified via code review and automated tests. Manual UI validation deferred (UI doesn't support phases/turns yet).

title: Add land cards and play-land mechanics
design:
## Goal
Implement land card definitions (City of Brass, Gemstone Mine) and the play-land mechanics so players can play lands from hand to battlefield, tracked by :player/land-plays-left.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Completed:
- fizzle-c69: Draw effect (library → hand) works
- fizzle-dbg: Turn structure with phase advancement works (including land-plays reset in start-turn)
- fizzle-yxj: Lose-life and gain-life effects work

Schema already has:
- :player/land-plays-left (reset to 1 each turn in start-turn)
- :game/phase and :game/step fields
- :object/zone for :battlefield

Existing patterns to follow:
- zones.cljs:move-to-zone for zone transitions
- events/game.cljs:start-turn pattern for pure functions wrapped by re-frame events
- turn_test.cljs pattern for helper functions and test structure
- iggy_pop.cljs card definition format

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Write tests for play-land pure function
File: src/test/fizzle/events/play_land_test.cljs

Create test namespace with helpers similar to turn_test.cljs:
- add-card-to-hand helper (creates object in :hand zone)
- get-object-zone helper (query object's current zone)
- get-land-plays helper (reuse from turn_test.cljs)

Tests to write (follow existing test naming conventions):

```clojure
(deftest test-play-land-moves-to-battlefield
  ;; Bug caught: Zone transition doesn't happen
  ;; Setup: Land in hand, land-plays-left = 1
  ;; Assert: After play-land, object zone is :battlefield)

(deftest test-play-land-decrements-land-plays
  ;; Bug caught: Land plays not tracked
  ;; Setup: land-plays-left = 1
  ;; Assert: After play-land, land-plays-left = 0)

(deftest test-play-land-fails-when-no-plays-left
  ;; Bug caught: Can play unlimited lands
  ;; Setup: land-plays-left = 0
  ;; Assert: Returns unchanged db, card still in hand)

(deftest test-play-land-fails-for-non-land
  ;; Bug caught: Can play any card as land
  ;; Setup: Dark Ritual in hand (type :instant)
  ;; Assert: Returns unchanged db, card still in hand)

(deftest test-play-land-fails-for-card-not-in-hand
  ;; Bug caught: Can play cards from graveyard/library
  ;; Setup: Land in graveyard, not hand
  ;; Assert: Returns unchanged db, card still in graveyard)

(deftest test-play-land-fails-outside-main-phase
  ;; Bug caught: Can play lands during combat
  ;; Setup: Phase is :combat
  ;; Assert: Returns unchanged db, card still in hand)
```

### Step 2: Run tests RED
Command: make test
Expected: All 6 tests fail (play-land function doesn't exist)

### Step 3: Add land card definitions
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

Add cards following existing pattern (no mana abilities yet - just card data):

```clojure
(def city-of-brass
  {:card/id :city-of-brass
   :card/name "City of Brass"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "Whenever City of Brass becomes tapped, it deals 1 damage to you. {T}: Add one mana of any color."})

(def gemstone-mine
  {:card/id :gemstone-mine
   :card/name "Gemstone Mine"
   :card/cmc 0
   :card/mana-cost {}
   :card/colors #{}
   :card/types #{:land}
   :card/text "Gemstone Mine enters the battlefield with 3 mining counters. {T}, Remove a mining counter: Add one mana of any color. If there are no mining counters on Gemstone Mine, sacrifice it."})

;; Update all-cards vector
(def all-cards
  [dark-ritual brain-freeze cabal-ritual city-of-brass gemstone-mine])
```

### Step 4: Implement play-land pure function
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

Add helper function to check if card is a land:
```clojure
(defn land-card?
  "Check if a card entity has :land in its types."
  [db object-id]
  (let [obj (queries/get-object db object-id)
        card-eid (:object/card obj)
        card (d/pull db '[*] card-eid)]
    (contains? (:card/types card) :land)))
```

Add play-land pure function (following advance-phase pattern):
```clojure
(defn play-land
  "Play a land from hand to battlefield.
   Pure function: (db, player-id, object-id) -> db

   Validation:
   - Player must have land-plays-left > 0
   - Object must be in player's hand
   - Card must be a land type
   - Phase must be :main1 or :main2

   Returns unchanged db if any validation fails."
  [db player-id object-id]
  (let [game-state (queries/get-game-state db)
        phase (:game/phase game-state)
        player-eid (queries/get-player-eid db player-id)
        land-plays (d/q '[:find ?plays .
                          :in $ ?pid
                          :where [?e :player/id ?pid]
                          [?e :player/land-plays-left ?plays]]
                        db player-id)
        obj (queries/get-object db object-id)]
    (if (and (pos? land-plays)
             (= (:object/zone obj) :hand)
             (= (:object/owner obj) player-eid)
             (land-card? db object-id)
             (#{:main1 :main2} phase))
      (-> db
          (zones/move-to-zone object-id :battlefield)
          (d/db-with [[:db/add player-eid :player/land-plays-left (dec land-plays)]]))
      db)))
```

Add re-frame event wrapper:
```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::play-land
  (fn [db [_ object-id]]
    (let [game-db (:game/db db)]
      (assoc db :game/db (play-land game-db :player-1 object-id)))))
```

### Step 5: Run tests GREEN
Command: make test
Expected: All 6 play-land tests pass

### Step 6: Run full validation
Command: make validate
Expected: lint + fmt-check + all tests pass

### Step 7: Commit changes
Command: git add -A && git commit -m "Add land cards and play-land mechanics"

## Success Criteria
- [ ] city-of-brass and gemstone-mine defined in cards/iggy_pop.cljs with :types #{:land}
- [ ] play-land pure function in events/game.cljs moves land from :hand to :battlefield
- [ ] play-land decrements :player/land-plays-left by 1
- [ ] play-land returns unchanged db when land-plays-left = 0 (no land left to play)
- [ ] play-land returns unchanged db for non-land cards (prevents exploit)
- [ ] play-land returns unchanged db for cards not in hand (prevents cheating)
- [ ] play-land returns unchanged db outside :main1/:main2 phases (MTG rule)
- [ ] 6+ unit tests pass with meaningful assertions
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + fmt + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO playing lands from zones other than hand
- ❌ NO allowing land plays when land-plays-left = 0
- ❌ NO silently ignoring errors - return unchanged db for invalid plays
- ❌ NO mocking in tests - use real game state
- ❌ NO implementing mana abilities in this task (separate task)
- ❌ NO hardcoding player-id (:player-1) in pure function - accept as parameter
- ❌ NO tautological tests - each test must catch a specific bug

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Object ID doesn't exist**
- queries/get-object returns nil for non-existent object
- play-land should handle gracefully (check obj is not nil)
- Add test: test-play-land-handles-invalid-object-id

**Edge Case: Player doesn't exist**
- queries/get-player-eid returns nil for invalid player
- play-land should check player-eid is not nil
- Test: test-play-land-handles-invalid-player-id

**Phase Restriction (MTG Rules)**
- Lands can only be played during main phases (not untap, draw, combat, etc.)
- MUST check phase before allowing play-land
- Test: test-play-land-fails-outside-main-phase

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow zones.cljs:move-to-zone for zone transition
- Follow events/game.cljs:start-turn for pure function + re-frame event pattern
- Follow turn_test.cljs for test helper patterns

## Notes
- This task does NOT implement mana abilities (tap to produce mana) - next task
- This task does NOT implement land counters (Gemstone Mine) - part of mana abilities task
- This task does NOT implement untap logic - separate concern
- Lands bypass the stack per MTG rules (hand → battlefield directly)
close_reason: Closed

title: Add ::selected-card-info subscription and rewrite controls to Play + Yield
design:
## Goal
Replace the 3-button controls (Cast, Play Land, Resolve) with 2-button layout (Play, Yield) using contextual labels that show card name even when disabled.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- views/controls.cljs:16-34 — current 3-button layout with btn-class helper
- subs/game.cljs:11-12 — ::selected-card returns object-id from :game/selected-card
- subs/game.cljs:65-73 — ::can-cast? subscription (checks rules/can-cast? AND not land)
- subs/game.cljs:76-82 — ::can-play-land? subscription (checks events/can-play-land?)
- subs/game.cljs:38-62 — ::stack subscription (LIFO ordered)
- events/game.cljs:602-612 — land-card? checks (contains? (set types) :land)

### 2. Add ::selected-card-info subscription (subs/game.cljs)
Insert after line 82 (after ::can-play-land? sub). Pattern follows existing layer-3 subscriptions:

```clojure
(rf/reg-sub
  ::selected-card-info
  :<- [::game-db]
  :<- [::selected-card]
  (fn [[game-db selected] _]
    (when (and game-db selected)
      (let [obj (queries/get-object game-db selected)]
        (when obj
          {:name (get-in obj [:object/card :card/name])
           :land? (events/land-card? game-db selected)})))))
```

Key details:
- Uses queries/get-object (already required in subs/game.cljs namespace)
- Uses events/land-card? (already required as fizzle.events.game)
- Returns nil when: no game-db, no selected card, or selected object not found (stale selection)
- Returns {:name string-or-nil :land? boolean} when card is selected and exists

### 3. Rewrite views/controls.cljs
Replace controls-view function. Keep btn-class helper unchanged.

Subscriptions needed:
- ::subs/can-cast? — spell castable?
- ::subs/can-play-land? — land playable?
- ::subs/selected-card — object-id for ::play-land dispatch arg
- ::subs/selected-card-info — {:name :land?} for contextual label
- ::subs/stack — non-empty check for Yield

Play button logic:
- Label: (cond
    (and card-info (not (:land? card-info))) (str "Cast " (:name card-info))
    (and card-info (:land? card-info))       (str "Play " (:name card-info))
    :else                                     "Play")
- Enabled: (or can-cast? can-play-land?)
- On-click: (cond
    can-cast?     #(rf/dispatch [::events/cast-spell])
    can-play-land? #(rf/dispatch [::events/play-land selected]))
- Note: when disabled (neither can-cast? nor can-play-land?), on-click is irrelevant since :disabled is true

Yield button logic:
- Label: always "Yield"
- Enabled: (seq stack)
- On-click: #(rf/dispatch [::events/resolve-top])

### 4. Verify
- Run: make validate (lint + format + tests)
- All 1148+ existing tests must pass unchanged

## Success Criteria
- [ ] ::selected-card-info subscription added to subs/game.cljs, returns {:name :land?} or nil
- [ ] ::selected-card-info returns nil when no card selected or game not started
- [ ] controls.cljs renders exactly 2 buttons: Play and Yield
- [ ] Play label shows "Cast [Name]" when a spell is selected
- [ ] Play label shows "Play [Name]" when a land is selected
- [ ] Play label shows just "Play" when nothing selected
- [ ] Play shows card name even when disabled (e.g. "Cast Dark Ritual" grayed out)
- [ ] Play dispatches ::cast-spell for spells, ::play-land for lands (existing events)
- [ ] Yield label is always "Yield", dispatches ::resolve-top
- [ ] No files changed in events/ directory
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests, all 1148+ assertions)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO new event handlers (reuse: existing ::cast-spell, ::play-land, ::resolve-top work perfectly)
- ❌ NO changes to any events/ file (scope: view-layer change only)
- ❌ NO removal of existing ::can-cast? or ::can-play-land? subscriptions (stability: they may have other consumers)
- ❌ NO conditional show/hide of buttons (UX: both buttons always rendered, disabled state communicates affordance)
- ❌ NO direct deref of app-db in view (pattern: use subscriptions for all data access)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: game-db nil (pre-game)**
- Before a game starts, game-db is nil
- ::selected-card-info returns nil (guarded by when clause) — Play shows "Play" disabled ✅
- Same pattern as existing ::can-cast? and ::can-play-land? subscriptions

**Edge Case: Stale selection (card removed from hand)**
- If selected card was discarded/exiled, queries/get-object may return nil
- Inner (when obj ...) guard handles this — returns nil, Play shows "Play" disabled ✅
- Selection is typically cleared when cards leave hand, but guard prevents NPE regardless

**Edge Case: Card with nil name**
- If :card/name is nil, label would show "Cast null" or "Play null"
- In practice all card definitions have :card/name (required field)
- No special handling needed — would indicate a card definition bug

**Implementation Pattern Reference:**
- Follow the exact pattern of ::can-cast? and ::can-play-land? (layer-3 subs with :<- signal fns)
- The btn-class helper in controls.cljs handles enabled/disabled styling — reuse as-is
close_reason: Closed

title: Disable Next Phase/New Turn buttons when stack non-empty and add event guards
design:
## Goal
Prevent phase advancement when the stack is non-empty by: (1) disabling buttons in the UI, (2) adding guards in the game logic functions.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - core.cljs:346-376 - turn-bar component with Next Phase / New Turn button
   - events/game.cljs:367-383 - advance-phase pure function (add guard HERE)
   - events/game.cljs:404-425 - start-turn pure function (add guard HERE)
   - events/game.cljs:428-439 - re-frame handlers (delegate to pure fns, no changes needed)
   - subs/game.cljs:36-53 - existing ::stack subscription (reuse in UI)
   - queries.cljs:130-139 - get-stack-items returns triggers only
   - queries.cljs:79-84 - get-objects-in-zone returns spells in a zone
   - test/fizzle/events/turn_test.cljs - existing test file with helpers

2. Write tests first (TDD) in test/fizzle/events/turn_test.cljs
   Add helper to put items on stack:
   ```clojure
   (defn add-trigger-to-stack [db]
     (let [conn (d/conn-from-db db)
           order (q/get-next-stack-order db)]
       (d/transact! conn [{:trigger/type :test-trigger
                           :trigger/stack-order order
                           :trigger/controller :player-1}])
       @conn))

   (defn add-spell-to-stack [db player-id]
     ;; Create a game object in :stack zone with proper position
     (let [conn (d/conn-from-db db)
           player-eid (q/get-player-eid db player-id)
           order (q/get-next-stack-order db)]
       (d/transact! conn [{:object/id (str "test-spell-" order)
                           :object/owner player-eid
                           :object/zone :stack
                           :object/position order}])
       @conn))
   ```

   Tests (each catches a specific bug):
   a. test-advance-phase-blocked-by-trigger-on-stack
      - Setup: init-game-state, set-phase :main1, add trigger to stack
      - Call: (game/advance-phase db :player-1)
      - Assert: phase still :main1 (catches: trigger on stack ignored)
      - Assert: mana pool unchanged (catches: mana cleared despite guard)

   b. test-advance-phase-blocked-by-spell-on-stack
      - Setup: init-game-state, set-phase :main1, add spell to stack zone
      - Call: (game/advance-phase db :player-1)
      - Assert: phase still :main1 (catches: spell on stack ignored)

   c. test-advance-phase-blocked-by-both-triggers-and-spells
      - Setup: init-game-state, add both trigger and spell to stack
      - Call: (game/advance-phase db :player-1)
      - Assert: phase still :main1 (catches: partial check of only one type)

   d. test-start-turn-blocked-by-stack-items
      - Setup: init-game-state, set-phase :cleanup, add trigger to stack
      - Call: (game/start-turn db :player-1)
      - Assert: phase still :cleanup, turn number unchanged (catches: new turn with stack items)

   e. test-advance-phase-works-when-stack-empty (regression)
      - Setup: init-game-state, set-phase :main1, empty stack
      - Call: (game/advance-phase db :player-1)
      - Assert: phase is :combat (catches: guard too aggressive, blocks everything)

   f. test-start-turn-works-when-stack-empty (regression)
      - Setup: init-game-state, set-phase :cleanup, empty stack
      - Call: (game/start-turn db :player-1)
      - Assert: phase is :untap, turn incremented (catches: guard blocks normal flow)

3. Implementation checklist

   **Step 1: Create stack-empty? helper in queries.cljs**
   - [ ] queries.cljs after get-stack-items (after line 139) - Add:
     ```clojure
     (defn stack-empty?
       "Check if the stack has no items (no triggers and no spells)."
       [db player-id]
       (and (empty? (get-stack-items db))
            (empty? (get-objects-in-zone db player-id :stack))))
     ```

   **Step 2: Add guards to pure functions**
   - [ ] events/game.cljs advance-phase function (line 367): add early return
     ```clojure
     (defn advance-phase [db player-id]
       (if-not (queries/stack-empty? db player-id)
         db
         (let [game-state ...] ;; existing body unchanged
           ...)))
     ```
   - [ ] events/game.cljs start-turn function (line 404): add same early return
     ```clojure
     (defn start-turn [db player-id]
       (if-not (queries/stack-empty? db player-id)
         db
         (let [game-state ...] ;; existing body unchanged
           ...)))
     ```
   Note: re-frame handlers (lines 428-439) need NO changes - they delegate to the pure functions which now guard themselves.

   **Step 3: Disable button in UI**
   - [ ] core.cljs turn-bar component (line 346-376): add stack subscription and disable logic
     ```clojure
     (let [current-phase (or @(rf/subscribe [::subs/current-phase]) :main1)
           current-turn (or @(rf/subscribe [::subs/current-turn]) 1)
           at-cleanup? (= current-phase :cleanup)
           stack @(rf/subscribe [::subs/stack])
           stack-active? (boolean (seq stack))]
       ;; In button attrs, merge with existing styles:
       {:disabled stack-active?
        :style {:padding "6px 14px"
                :border "1px solid #555"
                :border-radius "4px"
                :cursor (if stack-active? "not-allowed" "pointer")
                :background (if stack-active? "#333" "#2a6a2a")
                :color (if stack-active? "#666" "#fff")
                :font-size "13px"
                :font-weight "bold"
                :opacity (if stack-active? 0.5 1)}
        :on-click #(when-not stack-active?
                     (rf/dispatch [(if at-cleanup?
                                     ::events/start-turn
                                     ::events/advance-phase)]))}
     ```

   **Step 4: Verify**
   - [ ] make test - all tests pass
   - [ ] make validate - lint + format + tests pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] advance-phase returns db IDENTICAL (= db db') when stack has triggers
- [ ] advance-phase returns db IDENTICAL when stack has spells
- [ ] advance-phase returns db IDENTICAL when stack has both triggers and spells
- [ ] start-turn returns db IDENTICAL when stack has items (phase and turn unchanged)
- [ ] Mana pool NOT cleared when advance-phase guard blocks
- [ ] Turn number NOT incremented when start-turn guard blocks
- [ ] advance-phase works normally (phase changes, mana clears) when stack empty
- [ ] start-turn works normally (turn increments, phase resets) when stack empty
- [ ] Button visually disabled (reduced opacity, not-allowed cursor, muted colors) when stack non-empty
- [ ] Button re-enables reactively when stack empties (re-frame subscription)
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no regressions)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Guard placement: pure functions (testable) + UI (visual)**
- Guards go in advance-phase and start-turn pure functions for testability
- Existing tests in turn_test.cljs test pure functions directly - guards must be at this level
- Re-frame handlers need NO changes since they delegate to the pure functions
- UI gets its own guard via ::subs/stack subscription + :disabled attribute

**Stack emptiness requires TWO checks**
- get-stack-items returns triggers (entities with :trigger/stack-order)
- get-objects-in-zone db player-id :stack returns spells (objects with :object/zone :stack)
- Stack is empty ONLY when both return empty
- Create a stack-empty? helper in queries.cljs to encapsulate this

**Edge case: pending-selection (OUT OF SCOPE)**
- :game/pending-selection (app-db level) tracks UI choices during resolution
- A trigger may be removed from stack but pending-selection still active
- This means stack could be empty while player is mid-resolution
- NOT fixed in this task - separate concern, would require additional guard

**Button :disabled + :on-click guard**
- HTML :disabled prevents most clicks but not all browser behaviors
- Add when-not guard in :on-click as defense-in-depth

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ DO NOT create new subscriptions - reuse existing ::subs/stack
- ❌ DO NOT remove or auto-resolve stack items on phase advance
- ❌ DO NOT check only triggers OR only spells - must check both via stack-empty?
- ❌ DO NOT change the re-frame handler signatures or add middleware
close_reason: All success criteria verified: guards in pure functions, button disabled in UI, 729/729 tests pass, make validate clean

title: Extract selection/library.cljs with tutor, scry, peek, and pile-choice domains
design:
## Goal

Extract library-related selection types from selection.cljs into events/selection/library.cljs. This includes tutor, scry, peek-and-select, and pile-choice builders, execution functions, defmethods, and event handlers.

## Context
Completed fizzle-3v2f (core.cljs) and fizzle-8d9x (resolution.cljs). selection.cljs still contains all builders, execution functions, defmethods, and event handlers. The build-selection-for-effect multimethod is wired and working.

## Effort Estimate
3-5 hours (larger than initially estimated due to 10 test files + views)

## Implementation

### 1. Create events/selection/library.cljs
Move these from selection.cljs:

**Builders:**
- build-tutor-selection (selection.cljs ~line 92)
- build-pile-choice-selection (selection.cljs ~line 127)
- select-random-pile-choice (selection.cljs ~line 164)
- build-scry-selection (selection.cljs ~line 177)
- build-peek-selection (selection.cljs ~line 197)

**Execution functions:**
- execute-tutor-selection (selection.cljs ~line 385)
- execute-peek-selection (selection.cljs ~line 438)
- execute-pile-choice (selection.cljs ~line 493)

**Scry helpers:**
- reorder-library-for-scry (private, selection.cljs ~line 576)
- confirm-scry-selection (selection.cljs ~line 618)

**Defmethods on core/execute-confirmed-selection:**
- :tutor (calls build-pile-choice-selection and execute-tutor-selection — both co-located)
- :peek-and-select (calls execute-peek-selection)
- :pile-choice (calls execute-pile-choice, core/remove-spell-stack-item)
- :scry (calls reorder-library-for-scry, core/remove-spell-stack-item)

**Defmethods on core/build-selection-for-effect:**
- :tutor (wrapper calling build-tutor-selection)
- :scry (wrapper calling build-scry-selection)
- :peek-and-select (wrapper calling build-peek-selection)

**Event handlers (4 events):**
- ::select-random-pile-choice
- ::scry-assign-top
- ::scry-assign-bottom
- ::scry-unassign

Requires for library.cljs:
- fizzle.events.selection.core :as core
- datascript.core :as d
- fizzle.db.queries :as queries
- fizzle.engine.effects :as effects
- fizzle.engine.zones :as zones
- clojure.set :as set (used by execute-pile-choice, execute-peek-selection)
- re-frame.core :as rf

Does NOT need: resolution.cljs, engine/rules, engine/targeting, engine/stack, engine/mana, engine/grants

### 2. Handle Event Handler Namespace Change (CRITICAL)

Moving event handlers from selection.cljs to library.cljs changes keyword namespaces:
- Old: :fizzle.events.selection/scry-assign-top
- New (if using ::): :fizzle.events.selection.library/scry-assign-top

All dispatchers (views/modals.cljs, test files) would break!

**Solution: Register events with explicit fully-qualified keywords** to preserve backward compatibility:
```clojure
;; In library.cljs — use explicit keyword, not :: syntax
(rf/reg-event-db
  :fizzle.events.selection/scry-assign-top
  (fn [db [_ obj-id]] ...))
```

This allows the event handlers to live in library.cljs while keeping the same dispatch keywords. Views and tests don't need to change their dispatch calls.

### 3. Update selection.cljs
- Remove all moved functions, defmethods, and event handlers
- Remove clojure.set require (no longer used after pile-choice/peek extraction)
- Keep build-selection-for-effect defmethod registrations for non-library types (:discard, :return-from-graveyard, :player-target, :default)
- Keep all non-library defmethods, builders, event handlers

### 4. Update 10 test files that directly call library functions
These test files call moved functions via selection/ alias and need updating:

1. src/test/fizzle/events/tutor_multi_select_test.cljs
   - selection/build-tutor-selection (5 calls) → library/build-tutor-selection
   - selection/execute-tutor-selection (4 calls) → library/execute-tutor-selection

2. src/test/fizzle/events/scry_confirm_test.cljs
   - selection/confirm-scry-selection (9 calls) → library/confirm-scry-selection

3. src/test/fizzle/events/scry_selection_test.cljs
   - selection/build-scry-selection (10 calls) → library/build-scry-selection

4. src/test/fizzle/events/peek_selection_test.cljs
   - selection/build-peek-selection (11 calls) → library/build-peek-selection
   - selection/execute-peek-selection (4 calls) → library/execute-peek-selection

5. src/test/fizzle/events/pile_choice_test.cljs
   - selection/build-pile-choice-selection (4 calls) → library/build-pile-choice-selection
   - selection/execute-pile-choice (4 calls) → library/execute-pile-choice
   - selection/build-tutor-selection (2 calls) → library/build-tutor-selection
   - selection/select-random-pile-choice (2 calls) → library/select-random-pile-choice

6. src/test/fizzle/cards/polluted_delta_test.cljs
   - selection/execute-tutor-selection (6 calls) → library/execute-tutor-selection

7. src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs
   - selection/execute-tutor-selection (3 calls) → library/execute-tutor-selection

8. src/test/fizzle/cards/opt_test.cljs
   - selection/confirm-scry-selection (1 call) → library/confirm-scry-selection

9. src/test/fizzle/cards/intuition_test.cljs
   - selection/build-tutor-selection (1 call) → library/build-tutor-selection

10. src/test/fizzle/cards/flash_of_insight_test.cljs
    - selection/execute-peek-selection (1 call) → library/execute-peek-selection

For each: add [fizzle.events.selection.library :as library] to requires.
Keep selection require if file also uses selection events or non-library functions.
Remove selection require if file ONLY used library functions from selection.

### 5. Update game.cljs
Add bare require for library.cljs namespace loading (ensures defmethods registered):
```clojure
[fizzle.events.selection.library]  ;; bare require for defmethod registration
```
No other changes needed — game.cljs doesn't call library functions directly.

### 6. Do NOT update views/modals.cljs
Since event handlers use explicit old-namespace keywords (Step 2), no view changes needed.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events/selection/library.cljs exists with all library domain code
- [ ] library.cljs requires only core.cljs and engine/* (no other selection domain files, no resolution.cljs)
- [ ] All 5 builders moved (build-tutor-selection, build-pile-choice-selection, select-random-pile-choice, build-scry-selection, build-peek-selection)
- [ ] All 3 execution functions moved (execute-tutor-selection, execute-peek-selection, execute-pile-choice)
- [ ] confirm-scry-selection and reorder-library-for-scry moved
- [ ] All 4 execute-confirmed-selection defmethods for library types in library.cljs
- [ ] All 3 build-selection-for-effect defmethods for library types in library.cljs
- [ ] All 4 event handlers moved with EXPLICIT old-namespace keywords (not :: syntax)
- [ ] 10 test files updated to require selection.library
- [ ] game.cljs has bare require for selection.library
- [ ] views/modals.cljs UNCHANGED (event keywords preserved)
- [ ] All ~1266 tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] No circular dependencies

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Event Handler Keyword Namespace**
Moving rf/reg-event-db handlers changes :: keyword namespace. MUST use explicit fully-qualified keywords (:fizzle.events.selection/scry-assign-top instead of ::scry-assign-top) to avoid breaking all dispatchers in views/modals.cljs. Without this, 6+ dispatch calls in modals.cljs silently stop working (no error, just no handler).

**Edge Case: confirm-scry-selection operates on app-db**
Unlike most pure functions that take game-db, confirm-scry-selection takes app-db (re-frame state root). This is intentional — it reads :game/pending-selection from app-db. Make sure re-frame.core is required.

**Edge Case: build-pile-choice-selection is NOT a multimethod**
build-pile-choice-selection is called ONLY from the :tutor defmethod (for Intuition-style pile choice chaining). It is NOT registered as a build-selection-for-effect defmethod because it's not dispatched from resolve-spell-with-selection. Both must be co-located in library.cljs.

**Edge Case: Test file require removal**
Some test files that only used selection for library functions can now drop the selection require entirely. Verify with linter (make lint). Files that also dispatch selection events (::selection/toggle-selection, ::selection/confirm-selection) MUST keep the selection require.

**Edge Case: clojure.set usage**
clojure.set is used by execute-pile-choice (set/difference) and execute-peek-selection (set/difference). Must be in library.cljs requires. Verify selection.cljs still needs it after extraction — remove if not.

**Reference: Existing pattern from resolution extraction**
Follow same pattern as fizzle-8d9x: create file, move functions, register defmethods, update callers, run make validate.

## Anti-Patterns
- NO importing domain files in library.cljs (only core.cljs and engine/*)
- NO leaving orphaned defmethods in selection.cljs for library types
- NO using :: syntax for event handlers in library.cljs (would change dispatch keywords)
- NO forgetting bare require in game.cljs for namespace loading
- NO removing selection require from test files that still use selection events
- NO forgetting clojure.set require in library.cljs
close_reason: All library domain code extracted: 5 builders, 3 execution functions, scry helpers, 7 defmethods, 4 event handlers. 10 test files updated. 1266 tests pass.

title: Task 3: Implement Seal of Cleansing using implement-card skill
design:
## Goal
Implement Seal of Cleansing as a test enchantment for Ray of Revelation, using implement-card skill for Scryfall oracle validation.

## Context
- Task 1 completed: :destroy effect type implemented
- Task 2 completed: Ray of Revelation implemented
- Need enchantment on battlefield to test Ray can destroy it
- Epic REQUIRES using implement-card skill for Scryfall validation

## Scryfall Reference (for implement-card validation)
- Name: Seal of Cleansing
- Mana Cost: {1}{W}
- Type: Enchantment
- Oracle Text: Sacrifice Seal of Cleansing: Destroy target artifact or enchantment.
- CMC: 2

## Implementation Steps
1. Invoke `/implement-card Seal of Cleansing` to start oracle-validated implementation
2. Follow skill guidance for card definition structure
3. Card definition goes in: src/main/fizzle/cards/seal_of_cleansing.cljs
4. Test file goes in: src/test/fizzle/cards/seal_of_cleansing_test.cljs
5. Use Cephalid Coliseum pattern for activated ability with :sacrifice-self true
6. Targeting must use `{:card/types #{:artifact :enchantment}}` for OR logic
7. Write comprehensive tests following ray_of_revelation_test.cljs pattern
8. Add integration test: Ray destroys Seal on battlefield
9. Run `make validate` before closing

## Card Definition Pattern (follow cephalid_coliseum.cljs)
```clojure
{:card/id :seal-of-cleansing
 :card/name "Seal of Cleansing"
 :card/cmc 2
 :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :white 1}
 :card/colors #{:white}
 :card/types #{:enchantment}
 :card/text "Sacrifice Seal of Cleansing: Destroy target artifact or enchantment."
 :card/abilities
 [{:ability/type :activated
   :ability/targeting [{:target/id :target-artifact-or-enchantment
                        :target/type :object
                        :target/zone :battlefield
                        :target/controller :any
                        :target/criteria {:card/types #{:artifact :enchantment}}
                        :target/required true}]
   :ability/cost {:sacrifice-self true}
   :ability/effects [{:effect/type :destroy
                      :effect/target-ref :target-artifact-or-enchantment}]}]}
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Seal of Cleansing passes Scryfall oracle validation (via implement-card skill)
- [ ] Card file exists at src/main/fizzle/cards/seal_of_cleansing.cljs
- [ ] Test file exists at src/test/fizzle/cards/seal_of_cleansing_test.cljs
- [ ] Card is type #{:enchantment} and costs {:colorless 1 :white 1}
- [ ] Activated ability has :sacrifice-self true in cost
- [ ] Targeting allows EITHER artifact OR enchantment (not both required)
- [ ] :destroy effect uses :effect/target-ref correctly
- [ ] Test: seal-can-target-artifact-test (catches missing artifact targeting)
- [ ] Test: seal-can-target-enchantment-test (catches missing enchantment targeting)
- [ ] Test: seal-cannot-target-creature-test (catches over-broad targeting)
- [ ] Test: seal-activation-destroys-and-sacrifices-test (catches missing sacrifice)
- [ ] Test: ray-destroys-seal-integration-test (verifies Ray works on Seal)
- [ ] All tests passing: `make test`
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing: `make validate`

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded oracle text (must use Scryfall via implement-card)
- ❌ NO :card/effects at card level (use :card/abilities for activated abilities)
- ❌ NO targeting that requires BOTH artifact AND enchantment (must be OR)
- ❌ NO skipping sacrifice in cost (Seal must leave battlefield when activated)
- ❌ NO mocking zone transitions (test real :destroy → graveyard movement)
- ❌ NO generic test names ("test-basic", "test-it-works")

## Edge Cases to Handle
**No valid targets for activation:**
- Activation requires valid target; if no artifacts/enchantments on battlefield, ability cannot be activated
- Test: seal-not-activatable-without-targets-test

**Seal destroyed before ability resolves:**
- If Seal is destroyed in response to its own activation, ability still resolves (sacrifice is cost, already paid)
- This is handled by standard MTG rules; no special implementation needed

**Artifact creature or enchantment creature:**
- Targeting #{:artifact :enchantment} correctly matches artifact creatures and enchantment creatures
- Test: Can target artifact creature (has :artifact in types)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Pattern Consistency:**
- Follow Cephalid Coliseum for activated ability structure
- Follow Ray of Revelation for test file structure
- Use same add-card-to-zone helper pattern

**Type-Based OR Targeting:**
- Criteria `{:card/types #{:artifact :enchantment}}` means "has artifact OR enchantment type"
- This is already how targeting/matches-criteria? works (checks intersection)
- Verify this behavior in tests

**Reference Implementations:**
- cephalid_coliseum.cljs: Activated ability with :sacrifice-self and targeting
- ray_of_revelation.cljs: :destroy effect with target-ref
- ray_of_revelation_test.cljs: Test structure and helpers
close_reason: Closed

title: Migrate target storage and resolution from object to stack-item
design:
## Goal
Change all target storage and reading from :object/targets to :stack-item/targets. This includes the write path (confirm-cast-time-target), both read paths in resolution (player-target and object-target branches), fizzle checking, and deletion of the duplicate resolution function.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (small LOC changes across 5 files, plus test updates)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing patterns
- events/selection/targeting.cljs:109-141 — confirm-cast-time-target (current: writes :object/targets)
- events/abilities.cljs:154-193 — confirm-ability-target (pattern to follow: writes :stack-item/targets)
- engine/stack.cljs — stack/create-stack-item and get-top-stack-item
- events/game.cljs:354-384 — resolve-one-item (has top-stack-item, calls resolve-spell-with-selection)
- events/game.cljs:316-339 — resolve-top-of-stack (calls rules/resolve-spell directly, does NOT use stored targets — pre-existing limitation, out of scope)

### 2. Write failing tests first (TDD RED)
- Test: cast Orim's Chant with targeting → stack-item has :stack-item/targets {:player :player-2}
- Test: cast Orim's Chant with targeting → object does NOT have :object/targets
- Test: resolve Orim's Chant → effects use target from stack-item
- Test: cast Orim's Chant, target becomes invalid → spell fizzles (fizzle from stack-item)
- Regression: cast Dark Ritual (no targeting) → resolves normally (no targets on stack-item, no crash)
- Regression: activate targeted ability → still works (unchanged path)

### 3. Implementation — Storage Migration (targeting.cljs)
In confirm-cast-time-target (targeting.cljs:109-141):
a. After rules/cast-spell-mode, find the stack-item EID via Datascript query:
   ```clojure
   stack-item-eid (d/q '[:find ?e .
                          :in $ ?obj-eid
                          :where [?e :stack-item/object-ref ?obj-eid]]
                        db-after-cast obj-eid)
   ```
b. Store targets on stack-item: `[:db/add stack-item-eid :stack-item/targets {target-id selected-target}]`
c. Delete the `[:db/add obj-eid :object/targets ...]` line (targeting.cljs:137-138)
d. If stack-item-eid is nil (should not happen), return db unchanged (defensive)

### 4. Implementation — Resolution Unification (resolution.cljs)
a. Change resolve-spell-with-selection signature from `[game-db player-id object-id]` to `[game-db player-id object-id stack-item]`
   - stack-item is already available in caller resolve-one-item (game.cljs:355 as top-stack-item)
b. Read targets: `stored-targets (:stack-item/targets stack-item)` instead of `(:object/targets obj)` (line 105)
c. Update BOTH target branches:
   - has-stored-player-target branch (line 119): use stored-targets from stack-item
   - has-stored-object-targets branch (line 138): use stored-targets from stack-item
d. Replace resolve-effect-with-stored-target calls (line 125) with stack/resolve-effect-target:
   ```clojure
   (let [source-id object-id
         controller player-id]
     (mapv #(stack/resolve-effect-target % source-id controller stored-targets) effects-list))
   ```
e. Delete resolve-effect-with-stored-target function (resolution.cljs:69-78)
f. Update caller in game.cljs:364 to pass top-stack-item:
   `(resolution/resolve-spell-with-selection game-db player-id object-id top-stack-item)`

### 5. Implementation — Fizzle Unification (engine/targeting.cljs)
a. Change all-targets-legal? signature from `[db object-id]` to `[db targets requirements]`
   - targets: map from stack-item (e.g. {:player :player-2})
   - requirements: vector from (get-targeting-requirements card)
b. Remove internal object lookup — caller provides data directly
c. Update caller in resolution.cljs to pass stack-item targets + card requirements:
   ```clojure
   (let [card (:object/card obj)
         requirements (targeting/get-targeting-requirements card)]
     (targeting/all-targets-legal? game-db stored-targets requirements))
   ```
d. This enables ability callers to also check fizzle (pass :stack-item/targets + ability requirements)

### 6. Verify and run tests (TDD GREEN)
- Run make validate
- Verify all Orim's Chant tests pass with new storage
- Verify Dark Ritual and other non-targeted spells resolve normally
- Verify ability targeting tests unchanged
- Verify storm copy tests pass (storm copies don't have targets yet, not a regression)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] confirm-cast-time-target stores targets on stack-item via :stack-item/targets
- [ ] confirm-cast-time-target does NOT write :object/targets (verify: grep for :object/targets in targeting.cljs returns 0)
- [ ] resolve-spell-with-selection reads from :stack-item/targets (new 4th param)
- [ ] has-stored-player-target branch (resolution.cljs:119) uses stack-item targets
- [ ] has-stored-object-targets branch (resolution.cljs:138) uses stack-item targets
- [ ] resolve-effect-with-stored-target deleted (grep for function name returns 0)
- [ ] all-targets-legal? accepts (db, targets-map, requirements) instead of (db, object-id)
- [ ] game.cljs:364 passes top-stack-item to resolve-spell-with-selection
- [ ] Non-targeted spell resolution still works (Dark Ritual test)
- [ ] Ability targeting still works (regression test)
- [ ] Orim's Chant tests pass with new target storage
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all 1265+ tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Stack-item not found after cast**
- After rules/cast-spell-mode, query for stack-item by :stack-item/object-ref
- Should always exist (cast-spell-mode creates it)
- Defensive: if nil, return db unchanged and log
- This is a 'should never happen' case — don't over-engineer error handling

**Edge Case: Non-targeted spells**
- Most spells have no targets (e.g., Dark Ritual, Brainstorm)
- stored-targets will be nil/empty from stack-item
- All resolution branches already handle nil targets (pass through)
- MUST test: Dark Ritual still resolves after change

**Edge Case: Object-targeting spells (Recoup)**
- resolution.cljs:138 has a separate branch for has-stored-object-targets
- This branch ALSO reads :object/targets — must update

title: Task 3: Migrate trigger dispatch to read from Datascript
design:
## Goal
Modify trigger_dispatch.cljs to query Datascript (via trigger-db functions) instead of the atom registry. After this task, the atom is no longer READ — only written (dual-write from Task 2 preserved until Task 4 cleanup).

## Context
Completed Task 1: trigger_db.cljs has get-triggers-for-event(db, event) with filter matching.
Completed Task 2: Datascript trigger entities are created at play-land and game init (dual-write).

The atom registry is read in ONE place: trigger_dispatch.cljs dispatch-event, which calls registry/get-triggers-for-event.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Approach Decision: Datascript-first with atom fallback

Use Datascript as primary trigger source, fall back to atom when Datascript returns empty results. This avoids modifying any existing test fixtures until Task 4.

Why this approach:
- Production: dual-write ensures Datascript always has triggers → atom never consulted
- Tests: most register triggers only in atom → Datascript empty → atom fallback works
- No test changes needed → smaller, safer scope
- No double-firing risk → we pick ONE source (Datascript if non-empty, else atom)
- Task 4 removes fallback + updates test fixtures

## Implementation

### 1. Add event UUID→entity ID enrichment helper
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger_dispatch.cljs

CRITICAL: Events carry UUIDs (e.g., {:event/object-id <UUID>}), but Datascript triggers store entity IDs as refs. trigger-db/matches-filter? resolves :self to the trigger's source entity ID. Without conversion, (= UUID entity-ID) is always false and triggers silently never fire.

```clojure
(defn- enrich-event-ids
  "Convert event UUIDs to entity IDs for Datascript filter matching.
   Events use UUIDs (:event/object-id is a UUID), but Datascript triggers
   use entity IDs for :trigger/source. This bridges the impedance mismatch."
  [db event]
  (cond-> event
    (:event/object-id event)
    (assoc :event/object-id
           (d/q '[:find ?e .
                   :in $ ?oid
                   :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
                 db (:event/object-id event)))))
```

### 2. Add Datascript trigger→dispatch format conversion helper
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger_dispatch.cljs

CRITICAL: Datascript pulled triggers have {:trigger/controller {:db/id 1}, :trigger/source {:db/id 42}}, but:
- resolve-trigger expects :player-1 keyword for controller
- stack-item creation expects UUID for source

```clojure
(defn- datascript-trigger->dispatch-format
  "Convert a pulled Datascript trigger entity to dispatch-compatible format.
   Resolves ref values ({:db/id N}) to the values the rest of the system expects:
   - :trigger/controller → player keyword (:player-1) via :player/id lookup
   - :trigger/source → object UUID via :object/id lookup"
  [db trigger]
  (let [controller-ref (:trigger/controller trigger)
        source-ref (:trigger/source trigger)
        controller-eid (if (map? controller-ref) (:db/id controller-ref) controller-ref)
        source-eid (if (map? source-ref) (:db/id source-ref) source-ref)]
    (cond-> trigger
      controller-eid
      (assoc :trigger/controller
             (d/q '[:find ?pid .
                     :in $ ?e
                     :where [?e :player/id ?pid]]
                   db controller-eid))
      source-eid
      (assoc :trigger/source
             (d/q '[:find ?oid .
                     :in $ ?e
                     :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
                   db source-eid)))))
```

### 3. Modify dispatch-event to use Datascript-first with atom fallback
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger_dispatch.cljs

```clojure
(defn dispatch-event [db event]
  (let [;; Try Datascript first (enriched event for entity ID matching)
        enriched-event (enrich-event-ids db event)
        ds-triggers (trigger-db/get-triggers-for-event db enriched-event)
        ;; If Datascript has triggers, use them (convert format).
        ;; Otherwise, fall back to atom (backward compat for tests).
        matching (if (seq ds-triggers)
                   (mapv #(datascript-trigger->dispatch-format db %) ds-triggers)
                   ;; Atom path: get candidates, then filter (existing behavior)
                   (filter #(matches-filter? % event)
                           (registry/get-triggers-for-event event)))
        ;; Group by uses-stack?
        {stacked true, immediate false}
        (group-by #(get % :trigger/uses-stack? true) matching)]
    ;; Rest of dispatch unchanged (immediate → resolve-trigger, stacked → stack-item)
    ...))
```

Key points:
- trigger-db/get-triggers-for-event ALREADY applies filter matching → no double-filtering
- Atom path preserves existing matches-filter? call (atom triggers not pre-filtered)
- datascript-trigger->dispatch-format converts refs to keywords/UUIDs
- Stack-item creation code unchanged (receives same format as before)

### 4. Update requires
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/trigger_dispatch.cljs

Add:
```clojure
[datascript.core :as d]
[fizzle.engine.trigger-db :as trigger-db]
```
Keep existing registry require (atom fallback, removed in Task 4).

### 5. Remove dispatch-level matches-filter? usage for Datascript path
The public matches-filter? function in trigger_dispatch.cljs can remain (tests use it directly). But dispatch-event should NOT call it for Datascript triggers because trigger-db already filters. Only call it for the atom fallback path.

### 6. Write failing test first (TDD)
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/trigger_db_integration_test.cljs (add to existing)

Test that dispatch-event works via Datascript path without atom:
```clojure
(deftest test-dispatch-uses-datascript-triggers
  (testing "dispatch-event fires trigger from Datascript even with empty atom registry"
    ;; Create triggers ONLY in Datascript (not atom) to prove Datascript path works
    (let [db (create-test-db)
          [db' obj-id] (add-card-to-hand db :city-of-brass :player-1)
          ;; Clear atom registry to ensure dispatch can't use it
          _ (registry/clear-registry!)
          ;; Play land creates Datascript triggers (dual-write also registers atom,
          ;; but we cleared it after to isolate the test)
          db-after-play (game/play-land db' :player-1 obj-id)
          ;; Clear atom again (play-land re-registered)
          _ (registry/clear-registry!)
          ;; Now dispatch permanent-tapped event — should find trigger in Datascript
          db-after-dispatch (dispatch/dispatch-event
                              db-after-play
                              (game-events/permanent-tapped-event obj-id :player-1))
          stack-items (stack/get-all-stack-items db-after-dispatch)]
      (is (= 1 (count stack-items))
          "Dispatch should find trigger in Datascript even with empty atom registry"))))
```

Bug this catches: If dispatch only reads atom, this test fails (atom is empty).

### 7. Verify existing tests pass unchanged
All cob_event_trigger_test.cljs and trigger_dispatch_test.cljs tests must pass unchanged.
- cob tests that use play-land: Datascript has triggers → Datascript path fires
- cob tests that use register-trigger!: Datascript is empty → atom fallback fires
- dispatch tests that use register-trigger!: Datascript is empty → atom fallback fires

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

### CRITICAL: Event UUID → Entity ID Conversion
Events carry UUIDs ({:event/object-id <UUID>}), but Datascript trigger :trigger/source stores entity ID refs. The :self filter resolution compares these values. Without enrich-event-ids, comparison is (= UUID entity-ID) → always false → triggers silently never fire. This is the single biggest risk in this task.

Test to prove it works: test-dispatch-uses-datascript-triggers (above). If UUID→eid conversion is missing, this test will fail because no trigger matches.

### CRITICAL: Datascript Ref Format Conversion
Pulled Datascript entities have {:db/id N} for ref attributes. The rest of the system expects:
- :trigger/controller → :player-1 keyword (used by resolve-trigger, effects/execute-effect)
- :trigger/source → UUID (used by stack-item, trigger resolution, damage targeting)

datascript-trigger->dispatch-format handles this. Without it, resolve-trigger :draw-step would pass {:db/id 1} to effects/execute-effect where :player-1 is expected → silent failure or crash.

### Filter Matching Responsibility
- Atom path: registry returns all triggers for event-type, dispatch applies matches-filter?
- Datascript path: trigger-db/get-triggers-for-event ALREADY applies filter matching
- Do NOT double-filter Datascript results
- dispatch/matches-filter? still exists (public, tested separately) but dispatch-event only calls it for atom fallback

### Edge Case: No Object in Event
phase-entered events have no :event/object-id. enrich-event-ids must handle this gracefully (cond-> handles nil naturally — no conversion if key absent).

### Edge Case: Game-Rule Triggers (No Source)
Game-rule triggers have no :trigger/source. datascript-trigger->dispatch-format must handle nil source (cond-> with nil source-eid → no conversion → :trigger/source stays nil).

### Edge Case: Both Datascript and Atom Have Triggers
With dual-write, production code creates triggers in BOTH. The fallback logic uses Datascript if non-empty, else atom. Since production always has Datascript triggers, atom is never consulted in production. Tests that only register atom triggers get atom path. No double-firing.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] dispatch-event queries Datascript for trigger matching (primary path)
- [ ] dispatch-event falls back to atom when Datascript returns empty (test compat)
- [ ] enrich-event-ids converts event UUIDs to entity IDs for filter matching
- [ ] datascript-trigger->dispatch-format converts refs to keywords/UUIDs
- [ ] dispatch-event still creates correct stack-items for City of Brass tap
- [ ] dispatch-event still creates correct stack-items for City of Traitors land-entered
- [ ] All existing cob_event_trigger_test.cljs tests pass (atom fallback for direct registration)
- [ ] All existing trigger_dispatch_test.cljs tests pass (atom fallback for direct registration)
- [ ] New test proves Datascript path works without atom (clearing atom, then dispatching)
- [ ] Game-rule triggers (draw-step, untap-step) still fire via Datascript path after init
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns
- NO double-filtering Datascript results (trigger-db already filters; dispatch matches-filter? only for atom fallback)
- NO modifying existing test files (test fixture changes deferred to Task 4)
- NO removing dispatch/matches-filter? function (it's public, has its own tests, used by atom fallback)
- NO storing UUIDs in Datascript trigger entities (they correctly use entity ID refs for schema integrity)
- NO ignoring the UUID vs entity ID format difference (this causes silent trigger failures)
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 1: Create comprehensive card DSL documentation
description: Create docs/card-dsl.md with comprehensive reference for all card definition patterns
design:
## Goal
Create docs/card-dsl.md documenting all effect types, ability types, cost types, trigger types, and conditions with examples from existing cards.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### Step 1: Extract complete type lists from source
Run grep/read to extract ALL dispatched types:
```bash
# Effect types (dispatch on :effect/type)
grep -E ":effect/type" src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs

# Ability types
grep -E ":ability/type" src/main/fizzle/engine/abilities.cljs

# Cost types
grep -E "defmethod pay-cost" src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs

# Trigger types
grep -E ":trigger/type" src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs
```
Create master list of ALL types before documenting.

### Step 2: Study source files for each type
For each type found:
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Find usage examples
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Find required/optional fields
- src/main/fizzle/engine/abilities.cljs - Mana vs activated distinction
- src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs - Cost map structure
- src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs - Trigger fields

### Step 3: Create document structure

```markdown
# Fizzle Card DSL Reference

## Overview
- What this document covers
- How cards are defined (pure EDN data)
- Link to comprehensive MTG rules for mechanics reference

## Base Card Attributes
| Field | Type | Required | Example |
| :card/id | keyword | yes | :dark-ritual |
| :card/name | string | yes | "Dark Ritual" |
[... all fields ...]

## Effect Types
### :add-mana
**Required fields:** :effect/mana
**Optional fields:** :effect/condition
**Example:**
\`\`\`clojure
{:effect/type :add-mana
 :effect/mana {:black 3}}
\`\`\`
**Used by:** Dark Ritual, Lotus Petal

[... repeat for each effect type ...]

## Ability Types
### Mana Abilities (don't use stack)
[...]
### Activated Abilities (use stack)
[...]

## Cost Types
[table format with examples]

## Trigger Types
[table format with examples]

## Conditions
### :threshold
[...]

## Mana Cost Conversion
| Scryfall | Fizzle |
| {B} | {:black 1} |
| {U} | {:blue 1} |
[...]

## Common Gotchas
1. Mana cost is map, not string
2. Colors/types are sets, not keywords
3. CMC is integer
[...]
```

### Step 4: Verify against existing cards
For EACH example in the doc:
- Verify it matches actual card definition in iggy_pop.cljs
- Cross-reference with Scryfall for accuracy
- DO NOT invent examples - only use real cards

### Step 5: Verify completeness
- [ ] Count effect types in doc vs grep output (must match)
- [ ] Count ability types in doc vs source (must match)
- [ ] Count cost types in doc vs source (must match)
- [ ] Count trigger types in doc vs source (must match)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] docs/card-dsl.md exists
- [ ] All effect types documented (verified: count matches grep of effects.cljs)
- [ ] All ability types documented (verified: mana + activated both covered)
- [ ] All cost types documented (verified: count matches pay-cost defmethods)
- [ ] All trigger types documented (verified: count matches triggers.cljs)
- [ ] Common gotchas section with 5+ items
- [ ] Mana cost conversion table complete (all 6 colors + colorless + generic)
- [ ] Every code example verified against actual card in iggy_pop.cljs
- [ ] No example contains incorrect MTG rules (cross-check with Scryfall)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Completeness Verification:**
Before marking complete, run these verification commands:
- Count effect types: grep for defmethod in effects.cljs
- Count documented types in card-dsl.md
- Counts must match - no undocumented types allowed

**Example Accuracy Risk:**
The Polluted Delta incident showed we can hallucinate MTG rules.
For EVERY example:
- Copy exact code from iggy_pop.cljs (don't retype)
- Cross-check card behavior with Scryfall oracle text
- If unsure about a ruling, note it as open question

**Undocumented/Experimental Types:**
If grep finds a type not used by any card:
- Document it anyway (it exists in engine)
- Mark as "No current usage" in notes
- Consult user if unsure whether to include

**Future Maintenance:**
- Add note at top: "Last updated: [date]"
- Add note: "Update this doc when adding new effect/ability/cost/trigger types"

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO invented examples (use only actual cards from iggy_pop.cljs)
- ❌ NO assumed MTG rules (verify with Scryfall oracle text)
- ❌ NO undocumented types (every type in engine must be in docs)
- ❌ NO retyped code examples (copy-paste from source to prevent typos)
- ❌ NO missing data types (every field must specify ClojureScript type: keyword, string, integer, set, map)
