title: Task 2: Migrate trigger creation from atom to Datascript tx-data
design:
## Goal
Replace all atom-based trigger registration with Datascript tx-data using the new trigger_db.cljs functions. After this task, triggers are created as Datascript entities in ADDITION to atom entries (dual-write). The old atom is still read by dispatch — that migration is Task 3.

## Context
Completed fizzle-ig4q (Task 1): Schema and pure query/tx functions are ready in engine/trigger_db.cljs.
Key learning: ref attributes return {:db/id N} from pull — accounted for in matches-filter? via ref->eid helper.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Dual-Write Strategy (RESOLVED)
Dual-write is REQUIRED because dispatch-event (trigger_dispatch.cljs:77) reads from the atom via registry/get-triggers-for-event. If we stop writing to the atom, triggers silently stop firing.

Approach:
- Keep ALL existing atom writes in place (register-trigger!, register-card-triggers!, register-turn-based-actions!)
- ADD Datascript tx-data creation alongside existing atom writes
- Both paths coexist — atom for dispatch (until Task 3), Datascript for new entities
- Task 3 will migrate dispatch to read Datascript, then Task 4 removes atom code

DO NOT remove any atom writes in this task. DO NOT modify trigger_dispatch.cljs.

## Implementation

### 1. Migrate events/game.cljs: add Datascript trigger creation alongside atom
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

**Current code (lines 813-815):**
```clojure
_ (when (seq (:card/triggers card))
    (register-card-triggers! object-id player-id card))
```

**New code:**
```clojure
;; Keep atom registration for dispatch (removed in Task 4)
_ (when (seq (:card/triggers card))
    (register-card-triggers! object-id player-id card))
;; Also create Datascript trigger entities
db-after-triggers (if (seq (:card/triggers card))
                    (let [obj-eid (queries/get-object-eid db-after-move object-id)
                          player-eid (queries/get-player-eid db-after-move player-id)
                          tx (trigger-db/create-triggers-for-card-tx
                               db-after-move obj-eid player-eid (:card/triggers card))]
                      (d/db-with db-after-move tx))
                    db-after-move)
```

Key considerations:
- Need object ENTITY ID (integer), not object UUID. Use queries/get-object-eid or equivalent.
- Need player ENTITY ID (integer), not player keyword. Use queries/get-player-eid.
- d/db-with returns new immutable db value (pure, no side effects).
- Pass db-after-triggers forward instead of db-after-move for subsequent operations.
- Add require for [fizzle.engine.trigger-db :as trigger-db] in game.cljs ns form.

### 2. Migrate engine/turn_based.cljs: add pure function alongside imperative one
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/turn_based.cljs

**Keep existing register-turn-based-actions! unchanged** (atom path for dispatch).

**Add new pure function:**
```clojure
(defn create-turn-based-triggers
  "Return tx-data for game-rule triggers. Pure function, no side effects.
   Caller must transact the returned data into the DB."
  [player-eid]
  (vec (concat
    (trigger-db/create-game-rule-trigger-tx
      {:trigger/type :draw-step
       :trigger/event-type :phase-entered
       :trigger/filter {:event/phase :draw}
       :trigger/controller player-eid})
    (trigger-db/create-game-rule-trigger-tx
      {:trigger/type :untap-step
       :trigger/event-type :phase-entered
       :trigger/filter {:event/phase :untap}
       :trigger/controller player-eid}))))
```

**Integrate in game init (events/game.cljs around line 155):**
```clojure
;; Keep atom registration (removed in Task 4)
(turn-based/register-turn-based-actions!)
;; Also create Datascript trigger entities
(d/transact! conn (turn-based/create-turn-based-triggers player-eid))
```

Add require for [fizzle.engine.trigger-db :as trigger-db] in turn_based.cljs ns form.

### 3. Add new tests verifying Datascript entities
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/trigger_db_integration_test.cljs (NEW)

Do NOT modify existing test files (they test the atom path which still works).

New integration tests to verify Datascript entities are created:

a) test-card-triggers-in-datascript-after-play-land
   - Set up game with City of Brass in hand
   - Play the land (via game/play-land)
   - Query Datascript for :trigger/event-type :permanent-tapped entities
   - Assert: exactly 1 trigger entity exists
   - Assert: trigger source refs the CoB object
   - Bug caught: tx-data not transacted, wrong eid, wrong event-type mapping

b) test-game-rule-triggers-in-datascript-after-init
   - Initialize game via start-game event
   - Query Datascript for :trigger/always-active? true entities
   - Assert: exactly 2 game-rule triggers (draw, untap)
   - Assert: draw trigger has filter {:event/phase :draw}
   - Assert: untap trigger has filter {:event/phase :untap}
   - Bug caught: create-turn-based-triggers not called, tx-data not transacted

c) test-card-triggers-linked-to-object
   - Play land with triggers
   - Query :object/triggers ref on the battlefield object
   - Assert: object has trigger entities linked via component ref
   - Bug caught: triggers created but not linked to object via :object/triggers

d) test-both-atom-and-datascript-triggers-exist
   - Play land with triggers
   - Assert: atom registry has trigger (via registry/get-all-triggers)
   - Assert: Datascript has trigger entity (via trigger-db/get-all-triggers)
   - Bug caught: one path works but the other doesn't (dual-write verification)

### 4. Resolve trigger-type->event-type duplication
The mapping {:becomes-tapped :permanent-tapped, :land-entered :land-entered} exists in BOTH:
- events/game.cljs:688-696 (defn- trigger-type->event-type)
- engine/trigger_db.cljs:8-12 (def trigger-type->event-type)

After this task, game.cljs still uses its local copy (for atom path). In Task 4 when atom code is removed, the game.cljs copy will be deleted. No action needed now — just be aware of the duplication.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Datascript trigger entities created when card with triggers enters battlefield (verified by new test)
- [ ] Datascript game-rule trigger entities created at game init (verified by new test)
- [ ] :object/triggers component ref links triggers to source object (verified by new test)
- [ ] Atom registry STILL populated (dual-write verified by new test)
- [ ] Existing 1258 tests pass unchanged (no regressions — atom path preserved)
- [ ] 4+ new integration tests pass in trigger_db_integration_test.cljs
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (task-specific)
- NO removing atom writes (reason: dispatch reads from atom until Task 3)
- NO modifying trigger_dispatch.cljs (reason: dispatch migration is Task 3)
- NO modifying trigger_db.cljs API (reason: foundation is stable from Task 1)
- NO modifying existing test files (reason: they test atom path which must keep working)
- NO removing trigger-type->event-type from game.cljs (reason: atom path still uses it)

## Key Considerations

**Entity ID vs UUID:**
- create-triggers-for-card-tx expects integer entity IDs, not UUIDs
- game.cljs play-land uses object-id (UUID) for zones and registry
- Must resolve UUID to entity ID: (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]] db object-id)
- Check if queries/get-object-eid or similar helper exists; if not, use inline query
- Same for player: :player-1 keyword -> player entity ID via queries/get-player-eid

**Transaction ordering in play-land:**
- db-after-move already exists (line 806-808)
- Trigger tx-data must be applied AFTER move (object must be on battlefield)
- Use d/db-with to thread the trigger tx-data through the let chain
- Pass the resulting db forward for ETB effects and land-entered dispatch

**Game init transaction ordering:**
- conn already has player transacted by the time turn-based-actions are registered (line 155)
- player-eid is available from the prior d/q (line 133 area)
- d/transact! conn with the tx-data after the existing register call

**Empty :card/triggers guard:**
- Current code checks (when (seq (:card/triggers card))) before registering
- Preserve this guard for Datascript path too — don't create empty tx-data
- create-triggers-for-card-tx with empty vector returns [{:db/id eid :object/triggers []}] which is valid but unnecessary

**Test isolation:**
- New integration tests need full game setup (not just init-game-state)
- May need to use the re-frame test helpers or create a mini game setup function
- Check how cob_event_trigger_test.cljs sets up its test DB for patterns to follow

title: Task 3: Extract confirm-selection multimethod from per-type confirm handlers
design:
## Goal
Extract confirm logic from per-type reg-event-db handlers into a multimethod `execute-confirmed-selection` dispatching on `:selection/type`. Create shared wrapper `confirm-selection-impl` that handles validation, calls multimethod, handles remaining-effects, cleanup, and clear/chain. One `::confirm-selection` event handler replaces all per-type confirms.

## Context
Completed fizzle-rld7 (Phase 1: unified data model) and fizzle-ahlr (Phase 2: unified toggle). Now need Phase 3 to unify confirm handlers before adding auto-confirm in Phase 4.

## Current Confirm Handlers to Unify
From selection.cljs:
1. `::confirm-tutor-selection` — executes tutor (move cards, shuffle), may chain to pile-choice
2. `::confirm-discard-selection` — executes discard
3. `::confirm-player-target-selection` — resolves player-targeted effect
4. `::confirm-peek-selection` — moves selected peek cards to hand, rest to bottom
5. `::confirm-exile-cards-selection` — exiles selected cards for X cost, proceeds to cast
6. `::confirm-graveyard-return` — returns selected graveyard cards to hand
7. `::confirm-cast-time-target` — sets target on spell being cast (pre-cast)

From abilities.cljs:
8. `::confirm-ability-target` — sets target on ability being activated

## Architecture
```clojure
(defmulti execute-confirmed-selection
  (fn [game-db selection] (:selection/type selection)))

;; Each method returns:
;; {:db game-db} — standard, wrapper handles remaining-effects + cleanup
;; {:db game-db :pending-selection next-sel} — chain to next selection
;; {:db game-db :finalized? true} — fully handled (pre-cast, ability)
```

Shared wrapper (`confirm-selection-impl`):
1. Get game-db and selection from app-db
2. Call multimethod
3. If `:pending-selection` → set as new pending-selection
4. If `:finalized?` → just clear pending-selection  
5. Else → execute remaining-effects → cleanup-selection-source → clear pending-selection

## Implementation Steps
1. Read ALL 8 confirm handlers thoroughly to understand their exact logic
2. Identify the common pattern and type-specific logic for each
3. Create the `execute-confirmed-selection` multimethod with defmethods for each type
4. Create `confirm-selection-impl` shared wrapper function
5. Create `::confirm-selection` reg-event-db handler that calls wrapper
6. Update modals.cljs to dispatch `::confirm-selection` instead of per-type events
7. Remove old per-type confirm handlers
8. Move ability confirm method registration to selection.cljs (or keep in abilities.cljs with multimethod defmethod)
9. Run make validate

## Key Considerations
- Tutor confirms may chain to pile-choice (return `:pending-selection`)
- Cast-time-targeting and ability-targeting are 'finalized' (they set target, not resolve effects)
- Player-target and discard chain through remaining-effects
- Ability confirm is in abilities.cljs — defmethod can be in abilities.cljs (extending multimethod from selection.cljs)
- Must NOT change any game behavior (pure refactoring)

## Anti-Patterns
- NO changing any game behavior
- NO breaking tests at any step
- NO circular namespace dependencies
- NO big-bang: convert one handler at a time, test between each

## Success Criteria
- [ ] One `execute-confirmed-selection` multimethod exists
- [ ] One `confirm-selection-impl` wrapper function exists
- [ ] One `::confirm-selection` event handler exists  
- [ ] All 8 per-type confirm handlers removed
- [ ] modals.cljs dispatches `::confirm-selection` for all confirmation actions
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 2: Add Linting and Formatting Commands
design:
## Goal

Add clj-kondo linting and cljstyle formatting to the Makefile. This completes the validation toolchain.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Completed Task 1: Makefile exists with repl, dev, test, clean, help targets. Java detection working.

## Implementation

### 1. Install clj-kondo via npm

```bash
npm install --save-dev clj-kondo
```

Verify: `npx clj-kondo --version` shows version number.

### 2. Install cljstyle via Homebrew

cljstyle has NO npm package. Install via Homebrew cask:

```bash
brew install --cask cljstyle
```

**Post-install (macOS Gatekeeper):** Binary is unsigned, may need:
```bash
xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /usr/local/bin/cljstyle
```

Verify: `cljstyle version` shows version number.

### 3. Add Makefile targets

```makefile
.PHONY: lint fmt-check fmt validate

lint:
	npx clj-kondo --lint src/

fmt-check:
	cljstyle check src/

fmt:
	cljstyle fix src/

validate: lint fmt-check test
```

### 4. Run lint on existing code and fix issues

Run `make lint` on existing code. If errors found:
- Fix legitimate issues in source files
- Add exceptions to .clj-kondo/config.edn ONLY for false positives
- Do NOT suppress real errors

### 5. Run fmt-check on existing code and fix formatting

Run `make fmt-check` on existing code. If formatting issues found:
- Run `make fmt` to auto-fix
- Review changes before committing
- Commit formatting fixes separately from Makefile changes

### 6. Create .clj-kondo/config.edn (if needed for exceptions)

Only create if false positives need suppression:

```clojure
{:linters {:unused-namespace {:level :warning}
           :unused-binding {:level :warning}}}
```

### 7. Update help target

Add new commands to help output:

```makefile
help:
	@echo "Fizzle Development Commands"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make repl      - Start ClojureScript REPL (node)"
	@echo "  make dev       - Start browser dev server"
	@echo "  make test      - Run all tests"
	@echo "  make lint      - Run clj-kondo linter"
	@echo "  make fmt-check - Check code formatting"
	@echo "  make fmt       - Auto-fix code formatting"
	@echo "  make validate  - Run lint + fmt-check + test"
	@echo "  make clean     - Remove build artifacts"
```

### 8. Verify all commands work

- [ ] `make lint` runs clj-kondo, exits 0 (no errors) or non-zero (errors found)
- [ ] `make fmt-check` exits 0 (formatted) or non-zero (needs formatting)
- [ ] `make fmt` modifies files and exits 0
- [ ] `make validate` runs all three in sequence, fails fast on first error
- [ ] `make help` shows all new commands

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `npm ls clj-kondo` shows clj-kondo in devDependencies
- [ ] `npx clj-kondo --version` outputs version
- [ ] `cljstyle version` outputs version (installed via Homebrew)
- [ ] `make lint` exits 0 on clean code (all lint issues fixed)
- [ ] `make fmt-check` exits 0 on formatted code (all formatting fixed)
- [ ] `make fmt` auto-fixes and exits 0
- [ ] `make validate` runs lint, fmt-check, test in sequence
- [ ] `make validate` fails immediately if lint fails (doesn't continue to fmt-check)
- [ ] `make help` lists all commands including lint, fmt-check, fmt, validate
- [ ] All 40 existing tests still pass
- [ ] Changes committed (Makefile changes + any code fixes in separate commits)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Existing Code Has Lint Errors**
- EXPECT this to happen - new linter on existing code
- Fix errors in code, don't suppress legitimate warnings
- May need to fix unused requires, shadowed vars, etc.
- Commit lint fixes separately from Makefile changes

**Edge Case: Existing Code Has Formatting Issues**
- EXPECT this to happen - new formatter on existing code
- Run `make fmt` to auto-fix all formatting
- Review diff before committing
- Commit formatting fixes separately from Makefile changes

**Edge Case: cljstyle Not Installed**
- cljstyle is NOT available via npm
- Makefile will fail with clear error if cljstyle not found
- Document Homebrew installation in error message or README
- Fallback: Could check for cljstyle and skip if not found, but this defeats purpose

**Edge Case: macOS Gatekeeper Blocks cljstyle**
- Binary is unsigned, macOS may block execution
- Solution: `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /usr/local/bin/cljstyle`
- Document in README or show in error message

**Portability**
- clj-kondo: npx works cross-platform (npm is prerequisite anyway)
- cljstyle: Homebrew is macOS-only; Linux users download binary manually
- Windows: Out of scope (WSL recommended)

**validate Target Behavior**
- MUST fail fast: if lint fails, don't run fmt-check or test
- Use `&&` chaining in Makefile: `validate: ; make lint && make fmt-check && make test`
- Alternative: Keep as dependencies but this runs all even on failure

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO suppressing real lint errors (fix the code, not the linter)
- ❌ NO auto-formatting on validate (fmt-check only; fmt is explicit action)
- ❌ NO hard-coded paths for tools (npx for clj-kondo, PATH for cljstyle)
- ❌ NO skipping lint/format steps even if tools missing (fail clearly)
- ❌ NO mixing Makefile changes with code fixes in same commit
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 2: Rename selection.cljs builder/helper functions db → game-db
design:
## Goal
Rename the db parameter to game-db in all 17 helper/builder functions in events/selection.cljs that take a Datascript game-db.

## Effort Estimate
~2 hours (mechanical renames, no behavioral changes)

## Context
Completed fizzle-y9tp: game.cljs is now clean. Selection.cljs has 17 functions using db for game-db.

## Implementation Checklist

### Functions to rename (all take game-db, rename db → game-db):

1. **remove-spell-stack-item** (line 56) - [db spell-id] → [game-db spell-id]
2. **build-tutor-selection** (line 149) - [db player-id object-id tutor-effect effects-after] → [game-db ...]
3. **build-scry-selection** (line 229) - [db player-id object-id scry-effect effects-after] → [game-db ...]
4. **build-peek-selection** (line 247) - [db player-id object-id effect effects-after] → [game-db ...]
5. **build-exile-cards-selection** (line 293) - [db player-id object-id mode exile-cost] → [game-db ...]
6. **build-x-mana-selection** (line 327) - [db player-id object-id mode] → [game-db ...]
7. **build-graveyard-selection** (line 370) - [db player-id object-id effect effects-after] → [game-db ...]
8. **execute-tutor-selection** (line 423) - [db selection] → [game-db selection]
9. **execute-peek-selection** (line 476) - [db selection] → [game-db selection]
10. **execute-pile-choice** (line 530) - [db selection] → [game-db selection]
11. **resolve-spell-with-selection** (line 575) - [db player-id object-id] → [game-db ...]
12. **cleanup-selection-source** (line 708) - [db selection] → [game-db selection]
13. **resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection** (line 733) - [db stack-item] → [game-db stack-item]
14. **reorder-library-for-scry** (line 836) - [db player-id selection] → [game-db ...]
15. **build-cast-time-target-selection** (line 911) - [db player-id object-id mode target-req] → [game-db ...]
16. **cast-spell-with-targeting** (line 934) - [db player-id object-id] → [game-db ...]
17. **confirm-cast-time-target** (line 972) - [db selection] → [game-db selection]

### DO NOT TOUCH (verified clean):
- select-random-pile-choice (line 215) — already uses [app-db]
- confirm-scry-selection (line 878) — already uses [app-db]
- build-pile-choice-selection (line 181) — no db param
- build-discard-selection (line 358) — no db param
- build-player-target-selection (line 406) — no db param
- resolve-effect-with-stored-target (line 563) — no db param
- has-selection-effect?, find-selection-effect-index, etc. — no db param
- All rf/reg-event-db handlers — keep db (re-frame convention)

### Rename approach:
For each function, rename the parameter and ALL internal references from db to game-db. Internal let-bindings that shadow db (like db-after-tutor) are NOT renamed — only the parameter and direct references to it.

### Run validation:
make validate must pass (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Internal let-bindings are NOT db references:**
Many functions have let-bindings like db-after-tutor, db-after-effect, db-final etc. These are NOT references to the db parameter — they are local bindings with descriptive names. Only rename the actual parameter and direct references to it.

**reduce (fn [d effect] ...) patterns stay as-is:**
Several functions use reduce with [d effect] as the accumulator binding. This d is a local binding, not the db parameter. Do NOT rename it.

**Callers in rf/reg-event-db handlers:**
These functions are called from reg-event-db handlers that extract game-db via (:game/db db). The callers pass game-db, so no caller changes needed — the variable name at the call site is already game-db.

**Callers within selection.cljs (function-to-function):**
resolve-spell-with-selection calls build-tutor-selection, build-scry-selection, build-graveyard-selection, build-peek-selection. It passes db-after-before (a local binding), not db. No call site changes needed.

## Anti-patterns
- NO renaming let-binding intermediate variables (db-after-*, db-final, etc.)
- NO renaming reduce accumulator variables (d, gdb, etc.)
- NO renaming rf/reg-event-db handler db params
- NO behavioral changes
- NO adding docstrings or comments to unchanged functions

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 17 functions have game-db as parameter name (not db)
- [ ] No internal let-bindings incorrectly renamed
- [ ] All rf/reg-event-db handlers still use db param name
- [ ] select-random-pile-choice and confirm-scry-selection unchanged (already app-db)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all 872+ tests)
close_reason: Renamed db → game-db in all 17 selection.cljs builder/helper functions. All 872 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Unify target storage on stack-items
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- All targets (spells and abilities) stored on :stack-item/targets, not :object/targets
- Single target resolution function (stack/resolve-effect-target) for all stack-item types
- Fizzle checking works for both spells and activated abilities via stack-item targets
- Storm copies inherit :stack-item/targets from original spell's stack-item
- :object/targets removed from schema after all consumers migrated
- History descriptions read targets from stack-item only (no fallback)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] confirm-cast-time-target stores targets on stack-item, not object
- [ ] resolve-spell-with-selection reads from stack-item/targets
- [ ] resolve-effect-with-stored-target deleted (replaced by stack/resolve-effect-target)
- [ ] all-targets-legal? works from stack-item targets for both spells and abilities
- [ ] create-spell-copy propagates stack-item/targets to storm copies
- [ ] get-stack-item-targets (descriptions.cljs) simplified — no object fallback
- [ ] :object/targets removed from db/schema.cljs
- [ ] All existing tests pass (orims_chant_test, ability targeting tests, storm tests)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO reading from :object/targets anywhere (consistency: single source of truth on stack-items)
- ❌ NO dual-path target resolution — one function, not two (simplicity: resolve-effect-with-stored-target must be deleted)
- ❌ NO fizzle checking that only works for spells (correctness: MTG rules say abilities can fizzle too)
- ❌ NO leaving :object/targets in schema after migration (cleanup: dead schema entries mislead future work)
- ❌ NO fallback pattern in descriptions.cljs — try stack-item, then try object (cleanup: was a workaround for this exact inconsistency)

## Approach
Move all target storage from :object/targets to :stack-item/targets. At cast time, after rules/cast-spell-mode creates the spell's stack-item, store targets on the stack-item instead of the object. At resolution time, read from the stack-item (same path abilities already use). Generalize fizzle checking to accept stack-item targets + card requirements so both spells and abilities can fizzle. Propagate targets through storm copy creation. Delete the spell-specific resolution function and schema entry.

## Architecture
Files modified:
- events/selection/targeting.cljs — confirm-cast-time-target writes to stack-item
- events/selection/resolution.cljs — resolve-spell-with-selection reads from stack-item, delete resolve-effect-with-stored-target
- engine/targeting.cljs — all-targets-legal? accepts targets map + requirements (not object-id)
- engine/triggers.cljs — create-spell-copy copies stack-item/targets
- engine/stack.cljs — possibly add helper to find stack-item by object-ref
- history/descriptions.cljs — simplify get-stack-item-targets
- db/schema.cljs — remove :object/targets

Data flow after refactor:
  Cast UI → confirm-cast-time-target → find stack-item EID → [:db/add si-eid :stack-item/targets {...}]
  Resolution → get top stack-item → read :stack-item/targets → stack/resolve-effect-target → execute
  Fizzle → stack-item targets + card requirements → all-targets-legal? → boolean
  Storm → create-spell-copy → copy :stack-item/targets from original stack-item

## Parallelism Map

### Independent Work Streams
1. **Storage migration** (events/selection/targeting.cljs)
   - Change confirm-cast-time-target to write to stack-item
   - Estimated complexity: medium
2. **Resolution unification** (events/selection/resolution.cljs)
   - Read from stack-item, use stack/resolve-effect-target, delete old function
   - Estimated complexity: medium
3. **Fizzle unification** (engine/targeting.cljs)
   - Generalize all-targets-legal? signature
   - Estimated complexity: low
4. **Storm copy inheritance** (engine/triggers.cljs)
   - Copy targets in create-spell-copy
   - Estimated complexity: low
5. **Cleanup** (descriptions.cljs, schema.cljs)
   - Remove fallback, remove schema entry
   - Estimated complexity: low

### Stream Dependencies
- Streams 2, 3, 4 depend on Stream 1 (targets must be on stack-items first)
- Stream 5 depends on all others (cleanup after migration)

### Suggested Waves
- Wave 1: Stream 1 (storage migration — must come first)
- Wave 2: Streams 2, 3, 4 (resolution, fizzle, storm — independent after wave 1)
- Wave 3: Stream 5 (cleanup)

### File Ownership Boundaries
| Stream | Owns (exclusive) | Shared (needs coordination) |
|--------|-------------------|-----------------------------|
| Storage | targeting.cljs | — |
| Resolution | resolution.cljs | — |
| Fizzle | engine/targeting.cljs | — |
| Storm copy | engine/triggers.cljs | — |
| Cleanup | descriptions.cljs, schema.cljs | — |

### Parallelism Assessment
- Independent streams: 3 (after Wave 1 completes)
- Recommendation: solo execution
- Rationale: Total change is ~15-20 lines across 7 files. Each stream is small (3-5 lines). Coordination overhead of team execution exceeds the work itself. Better to do sequentially with tests between each step (refactoring-safely pattern).

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Cast-time spell targeting stores targets on :object/targets while activated abilities store on :stack-item/targets. This creates two resolution code paths, asymmetric fizzle checking (spells only), and a workaround in descriptions.cljs. The inconsistency caused a bug where history descriptions couldn't find targets for targeted spells (Orim's Chant).

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- events/selection/targeting.cljs:137-138 — writes :object/targets on spells at cast time
- events/abilities.cljs:182-188 — correctly writes :stack-item/targets for abilities
- events/selection/resolution.cljs:69-78 — spell-only resolve-effect-with-stored-target (reads object)
- engine/stack.cljs:97-124 — ability resolve-effect-target (reads stack-item) — handles all target types
- engine/targeting.cljs:128-153 — all-targets-legal? reads :object/targets only
- engine/triggers.cljs:70-74 — create-spell-copy does NOT propagate targets
- history/descriptions.cljs:40-47 — try-both fallback workaround

**Confirmed:** Abilities already use the correct pattern. Spells need to match it.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Unify to stack-items ✓

**What it is:** Move all target storage from :object/targets to :stack-item/targets. Single resolution function. Single fizzle path.

**Investigation:**
- Abilities already work this way (events/abilities.cljs:187)
- stack/resolve-effect-target handles all target resolution cases already
- Storm copies need explicit target propagation (create-spell-copy doesn't copy targets today)

**Pros:**
- Single source of truth
- Eliminates duplicate resolution function
- Enables ability fizzle checking
- Removes descriptions.cljs workaround

**Cons:**
- Must find stack-item EID after cast-spell-mode (minor query)

**Chosen because:** Matches existing ability pattern, eliminates all duplication

#### 2. Keep the split, document it ❌

**What it is:** Leave :object/targets for spells, :stack-item/targets for abilities. Add documentation.

**Why we looked at this:** Minimal changes, lowest risk.

**Pros:**
- Zero code changes
- No risk of breaking anything

**Cons:**
- Two resolution functions persist
- Fizzle asymmetry persists
- Descriptions workaround persists
- Every new targeting feature must handle both paths

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** The inconsistency already caused a bug (descriptions.cljs). Documenting it doesn't fix it.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** The unification proves significantly more complex than estimated.

#### 3. Copy from object to stack-item ❌

**What it is:** Store on object at cast time, then copy to stack-item. Stack-item becomes canonical at resolution.

**Why we looked at this:** Preserves the object-target write pattern while giving resolution a unified read.

**Pros:**
- Less change to cast-time flow

**Cons:**
- Redundant data (targets on both entities)
- Extra copy step
- Still need to update resolution and fizzle
- More complex than just writing to the right place

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Adds complexity without benefit. If we're touching resolution anyway, just write to the right place from the start.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** There's a reason targets must exist on the object entity (e.g., a UI subscription reads them there).

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Unify target storage to stack-items
- Unify resolution function
- Fizzle checking for abilities
- Storm copy target inheritance
- Schema cleanup

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Brain Freeze storm copy target splitting (fizzle-zyrf — separate feature, not refactoring)
- New targeting types (e.g., multi-target spells)
- Target change effects (e.g., Redirect)

### Open Questions
- How to find stack-item EID after cast-spell-mode? Likely query by :stack-item/object-ref pointing to the spell. (resolve during implementation)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Which approach for unifying? | Unify to stack-items | All target storage moves to :stack-item/targets |
| Include ability fizzle? | Yes, unify fizzle too | all-targets-legal? generalized to work from stack-item targets |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Target Storage Locations
**Question explored:** Where are targets currently stored and read?
**Sources consulted:**
- db/schema.cljs — both :object/targets and :stack-item/targets in schema
- events/selection/targeting.cljs — spell targeting writes to object
- events/abilities.cljs — ability targeting writes to stack-item
- events/selection/resolution.cljs — two separate resolution functions

**Findings:**
- Spells: targets on object entity, read during resolution from object
- Abilities: targets on stack-item entity, read during resolution from stack-item
- Two different resolution functions: resolve-effect-with-stored-target vs stack/resolve-effect-target
- Fizzle checking only works for spells (reads :object/targets)

**Conclusion:** Abilities have the correct pattern. Spells should match it.

#### Storm Copy Target Propagation
**Question explored:** Do storm copies inherit targets?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/triggers.cljs:35-74 — create-spell-copy function

**Findings:**
- create-spell-copy does NOT copy :object/targets or :stack-item/targets
- Works today because Brain Freeze uses fixed :effect/target :opponent
- After refactor, must explicitly copy :stack-item/targets

**Conclusion:** Add target propagation to create-spell-copy as part of this refactor

### Dead-End Paths
None — investigation confirmed the approach is straightforward.

### Open Concerns Raised
- Storm copy inheritance confirmed needed after checking triggers.cljs
close_reason: All success criteria verified: targets unified on stack-items, resolution unified, storm copies inherit targets, schema cleaned, descriptions simplified. 1270 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Task 3: Storm split UI component and final integration
design:
## Goal
Add the storm-split UI modal to modals.cljs so players can distribute storm copies across targets using +/- stepper controls. Commit uncommitted Task 1 changes. Verify all epic success criteria.

## Context
Completed Task 1 (Brain Freeze targeting) and Task 2 (storm split selection infrastructure). Backend is fully functional — builder, confirm handler, and stepper events all work. 19 tests in storm_split_test.cljs + integration tests in brain_freeze_test.cljs pass. Uncommitted changes from Task 1 (iggy_pop.cljs, resolution.cljs, brain_freeze_test.cljs) need committing.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours (commit 10min, UI component 1.5h, subscription 30min, tests 30min, integration 30min, validation 30min)

## Implementation

### 1. Commit uncommitted Task 1 changes
Working tree has tested, passing changes from Task 1:
- src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs — Brain Freeze card definition with :card/targeting
- src/main/fizzle/events/selection/resolution.cljs — Copy cease-to-exist in pre-resolve path
- src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs — Updated tests with targeting flow
Commit these with descriptive message referencing Task 1 work.

### 2. Add storm-split-modal component to modals.cljs

**Target label helper** (pure function, testable):

    (defn- storm-split-target-label
      "Human-readable label for a storm-split target player-id."
      [target-id]
      (case target-id
        :opponent "Opponent"
        :player-1 "You"
        (name target-id)))

**Stepper row component** — renders one row per valid target with label, count, and +/- buttons:

    (defn- storm-split-stepper-row
      [target-id count copy-count total-allocated]
      ...)

Button disabled states:
- "-" button: disabled when count = 0 (can-decrement? = (pos? count))
- "+" button: disabled when total-allocated = copy-count (can-increment? = (< total-allocated copy-count))
- Both use :disabled attribute AND conditional cursor/text styling (cursor-not-allowed + text-perm-text-tapped when disabled)

Dispatch events:
- "-" dispatches [::storm-events/adjust-storm-split target-id -1]
- "+" dispatches [::storm-events/adjust-storm-split target-id +1]

**Main modal component** — storm-split-modal:

Structure:
1. modal-wrapper with title "Distribute Storm Copies", max-width 450px, text-align center
2. Summary paragraph: "N copy/copies of [Spell Name]" (spell name via subscription, gracefully omitted if nil)
3. Stepper rows: (for [target valid-targets] [storm-split-stepper-row ...])
4. Total indicator: "X / Y assigned" with health-good/health-danger color based on validity
5. Buttons: confirm-button (disabled when total != copy-count) + Reset button (dispatches ::storm-events/reset-storm-split)

Data sources:
- selection map (passed as argument): copy-count, valid-targets, allocation
- subscription ::subs/storm-split-source-name for spell name

### 3. Add subscription for source spell name (subs/game.cljs)

Register ::storm-split-source-name subscription:
- Derives from ::game-db and ::pending-selection
- Guards: selection type must be :storm-split, source-id must be non-nil
- Returns card name string from source object, or nil

### 4. Add :storm-split case to selection-modal router (modals.cljs line ~672)
Add BEFORE the mana-allocation case:
    ;; Storm split selection — distribute copies across targets
    (= selection-type :storm-split)
    [storm-split-modal selection]

### 5. Add require for storm events in modals.cljs ns form
Add to requires: [fizzle.events.selection.storm :as storm-events]

### 6. Write tests

**Subscription test** (subs/game_test.cljs — extend existing):
- test-storm-split-source-name-returns-spell-name: Set up app-db with storm-split pending-selection referencing a Brain Freeze object. Verify subscription returns "Brain Freeze". Catches: subscription not reading source-object-id correctly.
- test-storm-split-source-name-nil-when-not-storm-split: Set up app-db with discard selection. Verify returns nil. Catches: subscription activating for wrong selection type.

**Pure helper test** (views/storm_split_test.cljs — NEW):
- test-storm-split-target-label-opponent: (storm-split-target-label :opponent) returns "Opponent". Catches: wrong label mapping.
- test-storm-split-target-label-self: (storm-split-target-label :player-1) returns "You". Catches: wrong label mapping.
- test-storm-split-target-label-unknown: (storm-split-target-label :player-2) returns "player-2". Catches: crash on unknown player-id.

Note: Follows project view test pattern (pure function tests like mana-pool-test). No DOM rendering tests — component correctness verified by manual browser testing + existing 19 backend tests in storm_split_test.cljs.

### 7. Run make validate
Verify lint + format + all tests pass.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Uncommitted Task 1 changes committed with descriptive message
- [ ] storm-split-modal renders with one stepper row per valid target
- [ ] +/- buttons dispatch ::storm-events/adjust-storm-split with [target-id delta]
- [ ] "-" button disabled (visually + functionally) when target count = 0
- [ ] "+" button disabled (visually + functionally) when total = copy-count
- [ ] Confirm button disabled when total allocation != copy-count
- [ ] Reset button dispatches ::storm-events/reset-storm-split
- [ ] Source spell name shown in modal header (via subscription)
- [ ] selection-modal routes :storm-split to storm-split-modal
- [ ] Goldfish mode works: modal renders with single "You" target row
- [ ] Subscription tests pass (source name retrieval + nil for wrong type)
- [ ] Target label helper tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO Brain Freeze specific logic in the UI (generic: shows valid-targets from selection)
- NO hardcoded target count (renders dynamically from valid-targets vector)
- NO creating a separate view file (keep in modals.cljs with other selection modals)
- NO skipping confirm validation (must enforce total = copy-count)
- NO modifying storm.cljs event handlers (UI only task)
- NO using reagent test utilities for view tests (project pattern: pure fn tests only)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Goldfish Mode (1 Valid Target)**
- In goldfish mode, valid-targets has only [:player-1] (no opponent)
- Modal should render with single stepper row labeled "You"
- Default allocation puts all copies to :player-1
- Confirm should work immediately (total = copy-count with 1 target)
- No special handling needed — (for [target valid-targets] ...) handles any count

**Edge Case: Nil Source Spell Name**
- Source object could theoretically be removed before modal renders
- Subscription guards with (when source-id ...) and returns nil
- Modal handles nil source-name: shows "N copies" without spell name
- (when source-name (str " of " source-name)) gracefully omits

**Edge Case: Copy Count = 1**
- With 1 copy, singular "copy" label (not "copies")
- Stepper still functional: can assign 1 to either target
- (if (= 1 copy-count) "copy" "copies") handles this

**Edge Case: Button Bounds**
- "-" button: disabled when count = 0 (can-decrement? check)
- "+" button: disabled when total-allocated = copy-count (can-increment? check)
- Both use :disabled attribute AND conditional cursor/text styling
- This prevents dispatching invalid adjustments (backend also validates)

**Edge Case: Large Storm Count**
- Storm 50+ is possible in practice (loop combos)
- Stepper UI still works — shows number, +/- buttons
- No scrolling needed since only 2 target rows max (opponent + self)
- Number display uses min-w-[2ch] which handles 2+ digits
close_reason: Closed

title: Enrich describe-event with game-db access and card name lookups
description: First task for epic fizzle-o6ha. Modify history/descriptions.cljs to accept pre-game-db and game-db-after parameters, query them for card names/phases/targets, and update the interceptor to pass them through.
design:
## Goal
Enrich all 8 priority event descriptions with card names, phase names, turn numbers, and target info by modifying describe-event to accept game-db snapshots.

## Effort Estimate
5-7 hours (medium complexity, single file of logic + tests)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- history/descriptions.cljs:4-18 — current static describe-event, returns strings, ignores args
- history/interceptor.cljs:50-59 — where describe-event is called; pre-game-db at line 50, game-db-after at line 52
- engine/stack.cljs:50-58 — get-top-stack-item returns full entity map with [*] pull, or nil
- db/queries.cljs:69-76 — get-object takes (db, object-id) returns object with {:object/card [*]} pulled
- db/queries.cljs:93-98 — get-game-state returns game entity with :game/phase, :game/turn
- engine/grants.cljs:164-166 — phase-order: [:untap :upkeep :draw :main1 :combat :main2 :end :cleanup]

### 2. Key data chains per event

**cast-spell** `[db _]`:
- game-db-after: `(stack/get-top-stack-item game-db-after)` → stack item with :stack-item/object-ref (EID)
- `(d/pull game-db-after [:object/id {:object/card [:card/name]}] obj-ref-eid)` → card name
- Stack item :stack-item/targets → target map (may have :player key)
- Output: 'Cast Dark Ritual' or 'Cast Tendrils of Agony targeting opponent'

**resolve-top** `[db _]`:
- pre-game-db: `(stack/get-top-stack-item pre-game-db)` → stack item BEFORE resolution
- Handle by :stack-item/type:
  - :spell / :storm-copy → object-ref → card name → 'Resolve Dark Ritual'
  - :activated-ability → :stack-item/source → card name → 'Resolve Cephalid Coliseum ability'
  - :storm → :stack-item/description already has text → 'Resolve Storm - create N copies'
  - :etb / :permanent-tapped / :land-entered → source → card name → 'Resolve [card] trigger'

**advance-phase** `[db _]`:
- game-db-after: `(queries/get-game-state game-db-after)` → :game/phase
- Phase name map: {:untap "Untap" :upkeep "Upkeep" :draw "Draw" :main1 "Main 1" :combat "Combat" :main2 "Main 2" :end "End" :cleanup "Cleanup"}
- Output: 'Advance to Main 1'

**start-turn** `[db _]`:
- game-db-after: `(queries/get-game-state game-db-after)` → :game/turn
- Output: 'Start Turn 3'

**play-land** `[db [_ object-id]]`:
- game-db-after: `(queries/get-object game-db-after object-id)` → :object/card → :card/name
- Output: 'Play Swamp'

**activate-mana-ability** `[db [_ object-id mana-color]]`:
- pre-game-db: `(queries/get-object pre-game-db object-id)` → card name
- Use pre-game-db for safety (though land still exists after tap)
- Output: 'Tap City of Brass'

**activate-ability** `[db [_ object-id ability-index]]`:
- ⚠️ MUST use pre-game-db — source may be sacrificed as cost (e.g., Lion's Eye Diamond, fetchlands)
- pre-game-db: `(queries/get-object pre-game-db object-id)` → card name + :card/abilities
- `(nth abilities ability-index)` → :ability/description for disambiguation
- Output: 'Activate Cephalid Coliseum' (single ability) or 'Activate [card]: [ability description]' (multi-ability)

### 3. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/history/descriptions_test.cljs (NEW)

Tests need REAL Datascript dbs (not keyword mocks) because describe-event now queries them.
Use the test helper pattern from existing tests (create db via datascript/db-with).

Specific test cases:
- test-cast-spell-shows-card-name: Create db with Dark Ritual on stack → 'Cast Dark Ritual'
- test-cast-spell-with-target-shows-target: Create db with targeted spell on stack, :stack-item/targets has :player → 'Cast Tendrils of Agony targeting opponent'
- test-cast-spell-nil-db-falls-back: Pass nil game-db-after → 'Cast spell' (fallback)
- test-resolve-spell-shows-card-name: Create db with spell on stack (pre-db) → 'Resolve Dark Ritual'
- test-resolve-ability-shows-source: Create db with activated-ability on stack → 'Resolve [card] ability'
- test-resolve-storm-shows-description: Create db with storm stack-item → uses :stack-item/description
- test-resolve-trigger-shows-source: Create db with etb/trigger on stack → 'Resolve [card] trigger'
- test-resolve-empty-stack-falls-back: Empty stack in pre-db → 'Resolve top of stack' (fallback)
- test-advance-phase-shows-phase-name: Create db with :game/phase :main1 → 'Advance to Main 1'
- test-advance-phase-all-phases: Test all 8 phases produce correct human-readable names
- test-start-turn-shows-number: Create db with :game/turn 3 → 'Start Turn 3'
- test-play-land-shows-card-name: Event has object-id, db has that object → 'Play Swamp'
- test-play-land-missing-object-falls-back: Event has object-id not in db → 'Play land' (fallback)
- test-activate-mana-shows-card-name: Event has object-id, pre-db has land → 'Tap City of Brass'
- test-activate-ability-single-shows-card: Single ability card → 'Activate Cephalid Coliseum'
- test-activate-ability-multi-shows-description: Multi-ability card → 'Activate [card]: [ability desc]'
- test-activate-ability-sacrificed-uses-pre-db: Source not in post-db (sacrificed) but in pre-db → still shows card name
- test-init-game-unchanged: → 'Game started' (no db queries needed)

### 4. Implementation checklist
- [ ] Create src/test/fizzle/history/descriptions_test.cljs with all test cases above (RED)
- [ ] history/descriptions.cljs — Add requires: fizzle.db.queries, fizzle.engine.stack, datascript.core
- [ ] history/descriptions.cljs — Change signature: (describe-event [event-id & args] pre-game-db game-db-after)
- [ ] history/descriptions.cljs — Add helper: (defn- get-card-name [db object-id]) → string or nil
- [ ] history/descriptions.cljs — Add helper: (defn- get-stack-top-card-name [db]) → string or nil
- [ ] history/descriptions.cljs — Add helper: (defn- format-targets [stack-item]) → string suffix or empty
- [ ] history/descriptions.cljs — Add phase-names map constant
- [ ] history/descriptions.cljs — Implement each case with try/catch fallback to generic string
- [ ] history/interceptor.cljs:56 — Change `(descriptions/describe-event event)` to `(descriptions/describe-event event pre-game-db game-db-after)`
- [ ] Update interceptor_test.cljs — Tests that assert specific descriptions (lines 130, 145, 150-167, 183) need updating:
  - Line 130: 'Cast spell' → will now be fallback since mock db is keyword, not Datascript db
  - Line 145: 'Start new turn' → will now be fallback
  - Lines 150-167: test-priority-events-all-have-descriptions — these use keyword mocks, describe-event will hit fallback path
  - Line 183: 'Game started' → unchanged (init-game doesn't query dbs)
  - DECISION: interceptor tests can keep keyword mocks and test fallback behavior; new descriptions_test.cljs tests the enriched behavior with real dbs

### 5. Run tests and validate (GREEN)
- [ ] make test — all existing + new tests pass
- [ ] make validate — lint + format + tests all pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] cast-spell with card on stack → shows card name (e.g. 'Cast Dark Ritual')
- [ ] cast-spell with targeted spell → shows target (e.g. 'Cast Tendrils of Agony targeting opponent')
- [ ] resolve-top with spell on stack → shows card name (e.g. 'Resolve Dark Ritual')
- [ ] resolve-top with activated-ability → shows source card + 'ability' suffix
- [ ] resolve-top with storm item → uses stack-item/description text
- [ ] resolve-top with trigger → shows source card + 'trigger' suffix
- [ ] advance-phase → shows phase name (e.g. 'Advance to Main 1') for all 8 phases
- [ ] start-turn → shows turn number (e.g. 'Start Turn 3')
- [ ] play-land → shows card name (e.g. 'Play Swamp')
- [ ] activate-mana-ability → shows card name (e.g. 'Tap City of Brass')
- [ ] activate-ability single ability → shows card name (e.g. 'Activate Cephalid Coliseum')
- [ ] activate-ability multi-ability → shows card name + ability description
- [ ] activate-ability with sacrificed source → uses pre-game-db, still shows name
- [ ] All fallbacks work: nil db, missing object, empty stack → generic description
- [ ] init-game → 'Game started' (unchanged)
- [ ] 17+ new tests in descriptions_test.cljs
- [ ] Existing interceptor tests updated and passing
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Sacrificed source (activate-ability)**
- Cards like Lion's Eye Diamond sacrifice as cost
- After ability activation, source object gone from game-db-after
- MUST query pre-game-db for object-id → card name
- Test: create object in pre-db, remove in post-db, verify name still shows

**Edge Case: Multiple stack-item types for resolve-top**
- Stack items have types: :spell, :storm-copy, :activated-ability, :etb, :permanent-tapped, :land-entered, :storm
- Each type needs different description format
- :spell/:storm-copy → 'Resolve [card name]'
- :activated-ability → 'Resolve [card name] ability'
- :storm → use existing :stack-item/description (e.g. 'Resolve Storm - create 5 copies')
- :etb/:permanent-tapped/:land-entered → 'Resolve [card name] trigger'

**Edge Case: nil game-db (init-game)**
- init-game pre-game-db is nil (no game existed before)
- describe-event must handle nil dbs gracefully
- init-game case returns 'Game started' without querying any db

**Edge Case: Empty stack for resolve-top**
- Shouldn't happen in practice (UI disables resolve when stack empty)
- But defensively: if get-top-stack-item returns nil, fall back to 'Resolve top of stack'

**Edge Case: Existing interceptor tests use keyword mocks**
- Tests at interceptor_test.cljs:45-167 use :db-old/:db-new (keywords, not Datascript dbs)
- These will hit the fallback path in enriched describe-event (Datascript query on keyword → exception → fallback)
- This is acceptable: interceptor tests verify interceptor mechanics, descriptions_test.cljs verifies enriched descriptions
- BUT: ensure try/catch in describe-event handles non-Datascript db gracefully

**Error handling strategy**
- Wrap each event case in try/catch :default that returns generic fallback
- Pattern: (try (enriched-description ...) (catch :default _ "Generic fallback"))
- This preserves existing behavior if any lookup fails

## Anti-Patterns (from epic, enforced here)
- ❌ NO modifying event handler signatures or payloads
- ❌ NO scattering description logic across event files
- ❌ NO hardcoded card name strings in descriptions
- ❌ NO breaking existing description fallback behavior
- ❌ NO importing game event namespaces from descriptions.cljs
close_reason: Implemented enriched describe-event with game-db access. All 1170 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Feature: Implement Intuition card with multi-select tutor and pile choice
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Intuition card (2U instant) is playable and resolves correctly
- Player must select exactly 3 cards from library (or all cards if fewer than 3 exist)
- After selection, player chooses 1 card for hand, rest go to graveyard
- Random button available during pile choice phase
- Library shuffles after resolution
- Fail-to-find allowed only when library is empty

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Intuition card definition exists with correct mana cost, types, and effect data
- [ ] Multi-select tutor selection works (exact count required based on library size)
- [ ] Pile choice selection works (choose 1 for hand from revealed)
- [ ] Random button randomly selects 1 card for hand
- [ ] Cards distributed correctly: 1 to hand, rest to graveyard
- [ ] Library shuffles after all cards moved
- [ ] Edge cases handled: 0/1/2/3+ cards in library
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (make validate)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO allowing fewer than 3 selections when library has 3+ cards (rules: Intuition requires exactly 3)
- ❌ NO cast-time opponent targeting (simplification: goldfish mode, player makes all choices)
- ❌ NO shuffling before cards moved (consistency: existing tutor pattern shuffles after)
- ❌ NO new effect type (design: extend existing tutor with parameters)
- ❌ NO skipping pile choice when 1 card selected (UX: still show choice UI for consistency, auto-confirm allowed)

## Approach
Extend the existing tutor effect system with multi-select capability and a new pile-choice selection phase. The tutor effect gains :effect/select-count and :effect/pile-choice parameters. After multi-select tutor confirmation, if pile-choice is present, a second selection phase begins where player picks which card(s) go to hand. A Random button provides quick selection. This approach follows the data-over-code principle and will be reusable for similar cards (Gifts Ungiven, etc).

## Architecture
### Card Definition (intuition.cljs)
- Standard card structure with :tutor effect
- New parameters: :effect/select-count 3, :effect/pile-choice {:hand 1 :graveyard :rest}

### Selection System (events/game.cljs)
- build-tutor-selection: Extended for :selection/select-count and :selection/exact? true
- build-pile-choice-selection: NEW - shows revealed cards, player picks N for hand
- confirm-pile-choice-selection: NEW - handles Random button, distributes cards
- execute-pile-choice: Moves cards to hand/graveyard, triggers shuffle

### Data Flow
1. Cast Intuition → resolve-spell-with-selection
2. Detect tutor with select-count → build-tutor-selection (multi-select mode)
3. Player selects exactly 3 (or fewer if library smaller) → confirm-tutor-selection
4. Detect pile-choice → build-pile-choice-selection
5. Player picks 1 for hand (or Random) → confirm-pile-choice-selection
6. Execute: move to hand, move rest to graveyard, shuffle library

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Intuition is a key Premodern combo card that searches for 3 cards with opponent choosing which goes to hand. Need to implement for practice reps.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- events/game.cljs:911-930 - build-tutor-selection creates single-select state
- events/game.cljs:1385-1428 - execute-tutor-selection moves card and shuffles
- events/game.cljs:951-960 - build-discard-selection pattern for multi-card selection
- cards/deep_analysis.cljs - cast-time targeting pattern (not needed here)

**External:**
- Intuition Oracle: Search for 3, opponent chooses 1 for hand, rest to graveyard, shuffle
- Rules: Must find exactly 3 if 3+ exist in library

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Extend tutor with multi-select + pile choice ✓

**What it is:** Add :effect/select-count and :effect/pile-choice to tutor effects. Two-phase selection: pick cards, then pick distribution.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed build-tutor-selection - straightforward to add select-count
- Reviewed discard selection - similar multi-select pattern exists
- Pile choice is new selection type but follows existing patterns

**Pros:**
- Extends existing pattern minimally
- Reusable for Gifts Ungiven, Fact or Fiction
- Data-driven (card definition, not card-specific code)

**Cons:**
- Two-phase selection adds complexity

**Chosen because:** Follows data-over-code principle, minimal new concepts, reusable

#### 2. New dedicated :intuition effect type ❌

**What it is:** Single effect type handling entire Intuition flow.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler mental model, self-contained

**Investigation:**
- Would require new effect handler in effects.cljs
- Card-specific logic violates data-over-code principle

**Pros:**
- Self-contained

**Cons:**
- Not reusable
- Violates design principles

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Violates data-over-code principle. Tutor extension is more consistent.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We find tutor extension creates unacceptable complexity.

#### 3. Compose separate effects with state passing ❌

**What it is:** Chain of effects: search-reveal, pile-split, shuffle-library

**Why we looked at this:** Maximum composability

**Investigation:**
- Would require new state-passing mechanism between effects
- Significant infrastructure change

**Pros:**
- Maximum composability

**Cons:**
- Major infrastructure change
- Over-engineered for current needs

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Over-engineered. Would require state-passing mechanism that doesn't exist.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We have 5+ cards needing complex effect composition.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Intuition card implementation
- Multi-select tutor extension
- Pile choice selection system
- Random button for pile choice

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Actual opponent AI choosing (deferred - player makes choice in goldfish mode)
- Other pile-choice cards like Gifts Ungiven (future epic)
- Reveal animation/UI (not needed for practice tool)

### Open Questions
- Should pile choice auto-confirm when only 1 card selected? (default: yes, for UX)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Who makes opponent choice? | Player + random button | No opponent targeting, add Random button to pile choice |
| Allow <3 cards if library small? | Yes, select all available | :select-count is max, actual count = min(3, library-size) |
| Must select 3 if 3+ exist? | Yes, mandatory | :exact? true in selection state, no partial selection allowed |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Tutor Selection System
**Question explored:** How do existing tutors work?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/game.cljs:911-930 - build-tutor-selection
- events/game.cljs:1385-1428 - execute-tutor-selection

**Findings:**
- Tutor effect returns db unchanged in effects.cljs
- Selection built at event layer with candidates from library
- Fail-to-find always allowed
- Shuffle happens AFTER card moved

**Conclusion:** Extend existing pattern, don't create new effect type

#### Multi-Select Patterns
**Question explored:** How does multi-select work elsewhere?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/game.cljs:951-960 - build-discard-selection

**Findings:**
- Discard selection uses :selection/count for required selections
- :selection/selected is a set of card IDs
- Confirm button enables when count reached

**Conclusion:** Follow same pattern for multi-select tutor

### Dead-End Paths

#### Cast-Time Opponent Targeting
**Why explored:** Oracle text says 'target opponent'
**Investigation:**
- Reviewed deep_analysis.cljs cast-time targeting
- Considered goldfish mode implications

**Why abandoned:** In goldfish/practice mode, no opponent to target. Player makes all choices. Targeting adds complexity with no benefit.

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'What if only 1 card selected?' → Still show pile choice for consistency, but can auto-confirm
close_reason: All success criteria met. Intuition card fully implemented with multi-select tutor, pile choice UI, and Random button. User confirmed working in manual testing.

title: Modify init-game-state to accept must-contain and return :opening-hand screen
design:
## Goal
Modify game initialization to accept :must-contain config, place sculpted cards in the opening hand, draw remaining slots randomly, and return :opening-hand screen instead of :game. This is the critical flow change: start-game now goes to :opening-hand, not :game.

## Context
Completed fizzle-jxi8: must-contain state management in setup events/subs is done. The must-contain map lives at :setup/must-contain in db. Now we need init-game-state to consume it.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (modify init, update callers, fix existing tests)

## Implementation

1. Study init-game-state in events/game.cljs:
   - events/game.cljs:35-116 — current init-game-state
   - events/game.cljs:25-32 — deck-to-card-ids shuffles and expands
   - It takes {:main-deck [...] :clock-turns 4}
   - Shuffles via deck-to-card-ids, creates library objects with random-uuid, draws 7 via effects/execute-effect :draw
   - Returns {:active-screen :game :game/db db} merged with history/init-history

2. Write tests first (TDD) in events/init_game_test.cljs (extend existing):
   - init-game-state with :must-contain {} — hand has 7 cards, lib has 53, :active-screen :opening-hand
   - init-game-state with :must-contain {:dark-ritual 2} — hand contains exactly 2 dark-rituals + 5 random, lib has 53
   - init-game-state with :must-contain {:dark-ritual 4 :cabal-ritual 3} (7 total) — hand has 4 DR + 3 CR, lib has 53, 0 random draws
   - init-game-state with :must-contain {:orims-chant 1} (single-copy card) — hand has 1 orims-chant + 6 random
   - :opening-hand/mulligan-count initialized to 0
   - :opening-hand/sculpted-ids is set of exactly N UUIDs matching sculpted hand objects
   - :opening-hand/phase initialized to :viewing
   - Sculpted cards are NOT in library (verify by checking library card-ids)
   - Library count = deck-count - 7 (same as before)
   - Must-contain with small custom deck (7 cards, 4 of one + 3 of another, must-contain 2) — works correctly

3. Update existing tests that assert :active-screen :game (they will break):
   - init_game_test.cljs:181-185 — test-init-game-state-includes-active-screen: change :game to :opening-hand
   - setup_test.cljs:232 — test-start-game-with-valid-deck: change :game to :opening-hand
   - setup_test.cljs:245 — test-start-game-with-oversized-deck: change :game to :opening-hand
   - setup_test.cljs:268 — test-quick-start-with-saved-preset: change :game to :opening-hand
   - setup_test.cljs:339 — test-new-game-redeals-same-config: change :game to :opening-hand
   - routing_test.cljs:22 — test-set-active-screen-switches-back-to-game: this test switches TO :game manually, should still pass

4. Algorithm for sculpted hand (modify init-game-state):
   - [ ] Accept :must-contain key in config (default to {})
   - [ ] Calculate sculpted-count = sum of must-contain values
   - [ ] Call deck-to-card-ids as before to get shuffled flat card-id list
   - [ ] Extract sculpted cards: for each {card-id count} in must-contain, take count occurrences of card-id from the shuffled list, removing them from the pool. Use a loop that finds and removes matching card-ids one at a time.
   - [ ] Take (7 - sculpted-count) random cards from remaining pool for random hand slots
   - [ ] Remaining pool cards become library
   - [ ] Create library objects from remaining pool (same as current: map-indexed with random-uuid, :object/zone :library)
   - [ ] Create hand objects from sculpted + random cards (same pattern but :object/zone :hand)
   - [ ] Track sculpted object-ids: the UUIDs assigned to sculpted card objects
   - [ ] Do NOT use effects/execute-effect :draw anymore (we build hand directly)
   - [ ] Return merged map with :active-screen :opening-hand, :opening-hand/mulligan-count 0, :opening-hand/sculpted-ids (set of uuids), :opening-hand/phase :viewing

5. Modify start-game-handler in events/setup.cljs:
   - [ ] Pass :must-contain (:setup/must-contain db) to init-game-state config

6. Modify new-game-handler in events/setup.cljs:
   - [ ] Pass :must-contain (:setup/must-contain config) to init-game-state config

7. Modify core.cljs:
   - [ ] Add :opening-hand case to screen routing (placeholder div for now — actual view is a separate task)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] init-game-state with empty must-contain returns :active-screen :opening-hand (not :game)
- [ ] init-game-state with :must-contain {:dark-ritual 2} places exactly 2 dark-rituals in hand
- [ ] init-game-state with :must-contain totaling 7 places all 7 sculpted cards, 0 random
- [ ] init-game-state with :must-contain {:orims-chant 1} places 1 orims-chant in hand
- [ ] Hand always has exactly 7 cards regardless of must-contain count
- [ ] Library count = deck-count - 7
- [ ] :opening-hand/mulligan-count starts at 0
- [ ] :opening-hand/sculpted-ids is a set of UUIDs matching sculpted hand card objects
- [ ] :opening-hand/phase starts at :viewing
- [ ] Sculpted cards not present in library (verified by card-id check)
- [ ] start-game-handler passes must-contain from db
- [ ] new-game-handler passes must-contain from stashed config
- [ ] core.cljs handles :opening-hand screen routing
- [ ] All 8 existing :active-screen tests updated and passing
- [ ] make validate passing (lint + fmt + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Algorithmic Change: No More effects/execute-effect :draw**
The current init-game-state uses effects/execute-effect :draw to move 7 cards from library to hand. With sculpting, we need to build hand and library directly from the card-id list BEFORE transacting objects. This means:
- Don't transact all cards as library then draw
- Instead, split card-ids into hand-ids + library-ids, then transact each group with the correct :object/zone
- This is a structural change to the init flow, not just adding a parameter

**Edge Case: Must-Contain Exceeds Deck Copies**
If must-contain says {:dark-ritual 4} but deck only has 3 (e.g., after sideboarding), take min(must-contain-count, available-in-deck). The setup toggle handler already validates this, but defensive coding prevents crashes.

**Edge Case: Must-Contain 7 = All Sculpted**
When all 7 slots are sculpted, random-draw count = 0. The (take 0 ...) should return empty — verify this works and doesn't error.

**Edge Case: Must-Contain 0 = Backward Compat**
With empty must-contain, sculpted-count = 0, all 7 cards are random. Should produce same hand/library distribution as before (modulo the draw-effect path change). :opening-hand/sculpted-ids should be #{} (empty set).

**Existing Test Breakage**
8 tests across 3 files assert :active-screen :game. These must be updated to :opening-hand:
- init_game_test.cljs: 1 test
- setup_test.cljs: 4 tests  
- routing_test.cljs: 0 need changes (it manually sets screen, not checking init output)
Additionally, setup_test.cljs tests that create {:active-screen :game} as input fixtures (restore-setup, new-game-without-stash) are fine — they're testing the handler's behavior with arbitrary input, not init-game-state output.

**Object ID Tracking**
:opening-hand/sculpted-ids must contain the actual random-uuid values assigned to the hand objects during transact. This requires generating UUIDs first, creating objects, then recording which UUIDs were sculpted.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO drawing sculpted cards via the normal draw path (must be separated before shuffle)
- NO :active-screen :game from init-game-state (always :opening-hand now)
- NO modifying the opening hand events here (separate namespace, separate task)
- NO transacting all cards as library then drawing (build hand/library zones directly)
- NO mutating deck-to-card-ids function signature (create new helper if needed)
close_reason: Closed

title: Make interactive effects declare themselves explicitly
description:
## The Problem

Several effects — `:discard`, `:tutor`, `:scry`, `:peek-and-select` — return `db` unchanged in the engine. The *real* work happens in the event layer, which detects these effect types and sets up UI selection state. This is a protocol that exists in comments and conventions, not in code.

### How it works today

1. Engine's `execute-effect-impl` for `:tutor` (effects.cljs:498-500): literally just `db`
2. `resolve-spell-with-selection` in selection.cljs scans effects *before* executing them using `has-selection-effect?` (line 78) and `find-selection-effect-index` (line 90)
3. These helpers check for specific effect types: `:discard`, `:tutor`, `:scry`, `:peek-and-select`, `:return-from-graveyard`, plus `:effect/selection :player`
4. If found, the event layer pauses execution, builds selection state, and waits for UI confirmation

### What's wrong

This is two parallel dispatch systems that must stay in sync:
- The engine dispatches on `:effect/type` via multimethods
- The event layer dispatches on the same `:effect/type` via `cond`/`case` in helper functions

If you add a new interactive effect type, you must update both. There's no compiler enforcement. The contract is implicit.

### The Rich Hickey question

"Can we make the protocol explicit? Can the data tell us what it needs?" Right now you have to *know* that `:tutor` needs player interaction. The effect map doesn't say so. It just... returns db unchanged and hopes someone upstream noticed.

### Proposed direction (needs brainstorming)

Effects that need player interaction should declare it in the effect data itself:

Option A: Marker key on effect maps
```clojure
{:effect/type :tutor
 :effect/interactive true   ;; <-- engine can check this
 :effect/criteria {...}
 :effect/target-zone :hand}
```

Option B: Engine returns a richer result
```clojure
;; Instead of just returning db:
{:db db :awaiting {:selection-type :tutor :context {...}}}
```

Option C: Effect metadata registry (less invasive)
```clojure
(def interactive-effects #{:tutor :discard :scry :peek-and-select ...})
```
At minimum, the set of interactive effects should be defined once, not reconstructed by pattern-matching in `has-selection-effect?` and `find-selection-effect-index`.

### Files involved

- `engine/effects.cljs` — effect implementations that return db unchanged (lines ~456-551)
- `events/selection.cljs` — `has-selection-effect?`, `find-selection-effect-index`, `resolve-spell-with-selection`
- Card definitions — would gain `:effect/interactive` if Option A
- `events/game.cljs` — `resolve-one-item` delegates to selection-aware resolution

### Risks

- Option A changes card data shape (many card defs to update, but mechanical)
- Option B changes engine return type contract (bigger change)
- Option C is lowest risk but least principled
close_reason: Replaced by epic fizzle-igid with full brainstormed design

title: Task 2: Update stack-item-view to display card name + description
design:
## Goal
Update the stack-item-view component to display triggers with format: 'Trigger: [Card Name] [description]'

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-032: Added :trigger/description to schema and create-trigger. Description now flows from card definition to trigger on stack.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- core.cljs:172-186 - stack-item-view currently shows 'Trigger: ' + type name
- subs/game.cljs:36-49 - ::stack subscription combines triggers and spells
- db/queries.cljs:130-139 - get-stack-items returns triggers with all attributes

### 2. Data Flow Understanding
Current: Trigger has :trigger/source (object ID) and :trigger/description (string)
Need: Card name from the source object's card

Path: :trigger/source → look up object → :object/card → :card/name

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] db/queries.cljs - Update get-stack-items to join source object and get card name:
      ```clojure
      ;; Add helper function
      (defn get-trigger-source-card-name [db trigger]
        (when-let [source-id (:trigger/source trigger)]
          (when-let [obj (get-object db source-id)]
            (get-in obj [:object/card :card/name]))))
      ```
- [ ] subs/game.cljs - Update ::stack subscription to enrich triggers with :trigger/card-name:
      ```clojure
      (let [triggers (queries/get-stack-items game-db)
            enriched-triggers (mapv (fn [t]
                                      (assoc t :trigger/card-name
                                             (queries/get-trigger-source-card-name game-db t)))
                                    triggers)
            ...]
      ```
- [ ] core.cljs:172-186 - Update stack-item-view to display new format:
      ```clojure
      (if (:trigger/type item)
        (if-let [desc (:trigger/description item)]
          (str "Trigger: " (:trigger/card-name item) " " desc)
          (str "Trigger: " (name (:trigger/type item))))
        label)
      ```
- [ ] Manual test: Run dev server, tap City of Brass, verify stack display
- [ ] Run `make test` - all tests passing
- [ ] Run `make validate` - lint/format pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Triggers with description show 'Trigger: City of Brass deals 1 damage to you'
- [ ] Triggers without description show 'Trigger: becomes-tapped' (backwards compatible)
- [ ] Source object in graveyard still shows card name (handles moved objects)
- [ ] All tests passing (`make test`)
- [ ] `make validate` passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Data Access Pattern:**
- Subscription enriches triggers with card name (view stays pure)
- get-object handles objects that moved zones (graveyard) - still returns object
- Card name lookup happens once per render, not per item

**Edge Case: Source Object Moved/Destroyed:**
- :trigger/source is an object ID, not a ref
- Object may have moved to graveyard after trigger created
- get-object should still find it (objects persist across zones)
- If object somehow doesn't exist, :trigger/card-name will be nil
- View should handle nil card-name gracefully (fall back to type)

**Edge Case: No Description:**
- Must check for :trigger/description before accessing
- Fall back to current 'Trigger: type' format when nil
- Backwards compatible with existing triggers

**Performance:**
- Enrichment happens in subscription (cached by re-frame)
- Only re-computes when game-db changes
- N lookups for N triggers (acceptable for small stacks)

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO auto-generating descriptions (explicit only)
- ❌ NO changing the 'Trigger:' prefix
- ❌ NO breaking triggers without descriptions
- ❌ NO doing db lookups in view component (keep views pure)
close_reason: Closed

title: Split battlefield into land and non-land rows with sorting
design:
## Goal
Modify the battlefield subscription to return cards grouped into lands and non-lands, each sorted by CMC+name. Update the battlefield view to render two distinct rows.

## Context
Completed fizzle-x19c: sort-cards utility exists at engine/sorting.cljs, already imported in subs/game.cljs.

## Implementation

1. Modify ::battlefield subscription in subs/game.cljs (line 132-136)
   - Current: returns flat vector from queries/get-objects-in-zone
   - New: return map {:lands [...] :non-lands [...]} where each is sorted
   - Land detection: check if :card/types contains :land
   - Use sorting/sort-cards on each group

   Change from:
     (when game-db (queries/get-objects-in-zone game-db :player-1 :battlefield))
   To:
     (when game-db
       (let [all (queries/get-objects-in-zone game-db :player-1 :battlefield)
             {:keys [lands non-lands]} (group-by-land all)]
         {:lands (sorting/sort-cards lands)
          :non-lands (sorting/sort-cards non-lands)}))

   Helper (inline or in sorting.cljs):
     (defn- land? [obj] (contains? (get-in obj [:object/card :card/types]) :land))

2. Write tests first (TDD) in src/test/fizzle/engine/sorting_test.cljs (add to existing file)
   - test-group-lands-and-non-lands: mixed battlefield -> lands separate from non-lands
   - test-empty-battlefield-returns-empty-groups: no cards -> {:lands [] :non-lands []}
   - test-all-lands-battlefield: only lands -> {:lands [sorted] :non-lands []}
   - test-each-group-sorted: within each group, CMC+name order maintained

3. Update battlefield-view in views/battlefield.cljs (line 115-125)
   - Current: subscribes to flat list, renders single flex-wrap div
   - New: destructure {:keys [lands non-lands]} from subscription
   - Render non-lands row first (spells/artifacts), then lands row below
   - Each row has a subtle label (NON-LANDS, LANDS) or just visual separation
   - Keep 'No permanents' message when both groups empty

   Change from:
     (let [battlefield @(rf/subscribe [::subs/battlefield])]
       [:div ...
        (for [obj battlefield] ...)])
   To:
     (let [{:keys [lands non-lands]} @(rf/subscribe [::subs/battlefield])]
       [:div ...
        (when (seq non-lands)
          [:div ... (for [obj non-lands] [permanent-view obj])])
        (when (seq lands)
          [:div ... (for [obj lands] [permanent-view obj])])
        (when (and (empty? lands) (empty? non-lands))
          [:div ... 'No permanents'])])

4. Verify
   - make validate passes
   - Visually confirm in browser: lands in separate row from non-lands

## Success Criteria
- [ ] ::battlefield subscription returns {:lands [...] :non-lands [...]} map
- [ ] Each group sorted by CMC then name
- [ ] Battlefield view renders non-lands row above lands row
- [ ] Empty battlefield still shows 'No permanents'
- [ ] 4 new tests pass in sorting_test.cljs
- [ ] make validate passes with zero failures

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty groups**
- Battlefield with only lands should show lands row only
- Battlefield with only non-lands should show non-lands row only
- Both empty -> 'No permanents'

**Edge Case: Subscription API change**
- This changes ::battlefield from vector to map — any other subscriber will break
- Check: views/battlefield.cljs is the ONLY consumer (confirmed via codebase search)
- No other file subscribes to ::battlefield

**Performance**
- group-by + two sort-cards calls vs single flat list
- Battlefield typically has 3-8 cards, negligible performance impact

## Anti-Patterns (task-level)
- ❌ NO sorting in battlefield view (sort in subscription only)
- ❌ NO land detection by card name (use :card/types #{:land})
close_reason: Implemented group-by-land in sorting.cljs, updated ::battlefield subscription to return {:lands [...] :non-lands [...]}, updated battlefield view to render two rows. 5 new tests, all 1201 tests pass, make validate clean.
