title: Extract selection/zone_ops.cljs with discard unification and graveyard-return
design:
## Goal

Extract zone operations (discard, cleanup-discard, graveyard-return) from selection.cljs into events/selection/zone_ops.cljs. Also unify :discard and :cleanup-discard into a single :discard type with :selection/cleanup? flag (epic requirement #6).

## Context
Completed fizzle-0b5g (costs.cljs extraction). selection.cljs now has ~176 lines with only zone-ops code (discard/cleanup-discard/graveyard-return builders, defmethods, build-selection-for-effect registrations) plus shared re-frame event handlers (set-pending, toggle, confirm, cancel). After this extraction, the shared event handlers remain in selection.cljs as a thin shim.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Move :default defmethod to core.cljs
The build-selection-for-effect :default defmethod is a mechanism fallback, not domain-specific. Move it from selection.cljs to core.cljs next to the multimethod definition.

File: src/main/fizzle/events/selection/core.cljs
- Add defmethod core/build-selection-for-effect :default after the defmulti

### 2. Create events/selection/zone_ops.cljs
Move from selection.cljs:

**Builders:**
- build-discard-selection (was private, make public)
- build-graveyard-selection

**Defmethods on core/execute-confirmed-selection:**
- :discard (unified — checks :selection/cleanup? flag)
- :graveyard-return

**Defmethods on core/build-selection-for-effect:**
- :discard
- :return-from-graveyard

**Discard unification (epic requirement #6):**
- Remove :cleanup-discard type entirely
- Add optional :selection/cleanup? flag to build-discard-selection
- Single :discard defmethod checks flag:
  - true → discard + expire-grants + return {:db :finalized? true}
  - false → discard + return {:db} (standard path)
- The builder signature becomes: (build-discard-selection player-id object-id discard-effect effects-after & [{:keys [cleanup?]}])
  Or simply add an arity: (build-discard-selection player-id object-id discard-effect effects-after) for normal discard, and (build-cleanup-discard-selection player-id discard-count) as a separate builder that sets cleanup? true.

Requires for zone_ops.cljs:
- fizzle.db.queries :as queries (for build-graveyard-selection, expire-grants lookup)
- fizzle.engine.grants :as grants (for cleanup discard grant expiration)
- fizzle.engine.zones :as zones (for move-to-zone)
- fizzle.events.selection.core :as core (for defmethod registrations)

Does NOT need: datascript.core, rules, mana, effects

### 3. Update selection.cljs → thin event handler shim
After zone_ops extraction, selection.cljs will have ONLY the 4 shared re-frame event handlers:
- ::set-pending-selection
- ::toggle-selection
- ::confirm-selection
- ::cancel-selection

Plus require of core.cljs (for toggle-selection-impl, confirm-selection-handler).

Keep selection.cljs as thin shim — other namespaces (views, tests) reference ::selection/toggle-selection keyword namespace. Moving events to core.cljs would break all dispatch keyword references.

Remove requires no longer needed: queries, grants, zones (only used by zone-ops code).

### 4. Update game.cljs
- build-cleanup-discard-selection (line 392): change :selection/type from :cleanup-discard to :discard, add :selection/cleanup? true
- Add bare require [fizzle.events.selection.zone-ops] to ensure defmethod registration
- KEEP [fizzle.events.selection] bare require — still needed for event handler registrations (set-pending, toggle, confirm, cancel)
- NOTE: complete-cleanup-discard function (line 430) is a SEPARATE code path from the selection system. It is called directly by tests and does NOT use selection types. No changes needed to this function.

### 5. Update core.cljs comment
core.cljs line 148 comment says "cleanup-discard" — update to reference ":discard with :selection/cleanup?" instead.

### 6. Update test files
Specific changes needed:

**turn_test.cljs:**
- Line 338: Change (= :cleanup-discard ...) to (= :discard ...)
- Line 339: Update test description string
- Line 415: Change (= :cleanup-discard ...) to (= :discard ...)
- Also verify the selection has :selection/cleanup? true in these assertions

**auto_confirm_test.cljs:**
- Line 16: Comment mentions cleanup-discard — update to "discard (with cleanup?)"

**NOTE: history tests do NOT reference :cleanup-discard — no changes needed there.**
**NOTE: interceptor priority-selection-types does NOT include :cleanup-discard — no changes needed.**

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events/selection/zone_ops.cljs exists with discard (unified), graveyard-return builders and defmethods
- [ ] build-selection-for-effect :default defmethod lives in core.cljs (mechanism, not domain)
- [ ] :cleanup-discard type removed everywhere, unified into :discard with :selection/cleanup? flag
- [ ] zone_ops.cljs requires only core.cljs and engine/* (no other domain files)
- [ ] selection.cljs reduced to thin event handler shim (4 handlers + core require only)
- [ ] game.cljs build-cleanup-discard-selection uses :discard with :selection/cleanup? true
- [ ] game.cljs keeps both bare requires: selection (event handlers) and zone-ops (defmethods)
- [ ] turn_test.cljs assertions updated from :cleanup-discard to :discard
- [ ] All ~1266 tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] No circular dependencies

## Anti-Patterns
- NO type-specific logic leaking into core.cljs (except :default fallback, which is type-agnostic mechanism)
- NO leaving orphaned defmethods in selection.cljs
- NO removing selection.cljs bare require from game.cljs (event handlers still need loading)
- NO modifying complete-cleanup-discard function (separate code path, out of scope)
- NO modifying history interceptor or descriptions (they don't reference :cleanup-discard)
- NO putting :default defmethod in a domain file (it's mechanism fallback)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Discard unification approach:**
The unified :discard defmethod must check :selection/cleanup? to decide the return shape:
- false/nil → {:db updated-db} (standard, wrapper handles remaining-effects + cleanup)
- true → {:db final-db :finalized? true} (cleanup path, grant expiration included)
This flag-based dispatch inside a single defmethod is simple and correct.

**complete-cleanup-discard is a separate path:**
game.cljs has complete-cleanup-discard as a direct function (not selection system). Tests call it directly. It does the same thing as the :cleanup-discard defmethod. This duplication is pre-existing and out of scope for this task. Do not modify or remove it.

**Event handler keyword stability:**
selection.cljs must remain as a thin shim because ::selection/toggle-selection expands to :fizzle.events.selection/toggle-selection. Views and tests dispatch these keywords. Moving handlers to core.cljs would change all keyword namespaces.

**:default defmethod in core.cljs:**
The :default method on build-selection-for-effect is a mechanism fallback (creates generic selection for unknown effect types). It belongs in core.cljs with the defmulti, not in any domain file. This is consistent with Clojure convention (default near defmulti).
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 3: Add cannot-cast-spells restriction checking to can-cast?
design:
## Goal
Extend the can-cast? function in rules.cljs to check for :cannot-cast-spells restrictions on the player before allowing any spell to be cast.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-d3y: Player grants system with :player/grants attribute
Completed fizzle-w0x: :add-restriction effect creates :restriction grants on players

Now we need the enforcement point: can-cast? should check if the player has a :cannot-cast-spells restriction grant before allowing casting.

## Implementation

### 1. Study can-cast? function
**VERIFIED**: can-cast? is at rules.cljs:139-157
- Takes: db, player-id, object-id
- Returns: boolean
- Current flow: get-casting-modes → check can-cast-mode? → check targets
- Restriction check should be FIRST (before modes/mana checks)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Add tests to rules_test.cljs. Use existing test helpers or create minimal ones.

**Test helper needed:**
```clojure
(defn add-player-restriction
  "Add a restriction grant to a player for testing.
   Creates grant expiring at turn 99 (effectively permanent for tests)."
  [db player-id restriction-type]
  (let [grant {:grant/id (random-uuid)
               :grant/type :restriction
               :grant/source nil
               :grant/expires {:expires/turn 99 :expires/phase :cleanup}
               :grant/data {:restriction/type restriction-type}}]
    (grants/add-player-grant db player-id grant)))
```

**Tests:**
```clojure
;; Test 1: Player with :cannot-cast-spells restriction cannot cast spells
(deftest cannot-cast-with-restriction-test
  (testing "player with :cannot-cast-spells restriction cannot cast any spell"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (add-player-restriction :player-1 :cannot-cast-spells))
          ;; init-game-state adds Dark Ritual to hand with mana
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))]
      (is (not (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 object-id))
          "Should not be able to cast Dark Ritual with restriction"))))

;; Test 2: Player without restriction can cast normally
(deftest can-cast-without-restriction-test
  (testing "player without restriction can cast spells normally"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          ;; Add mana to cast
          db (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:black 1})
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))]
      (is (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 object-id)
          "Should be able to cast Dark Ritual without restriction"))))

;; Test 3: Restriction on opponent doesn't affect player
(deftest restriction-on-opponent-doesnt-affect-player-test
  (testing "restriction on opponent doesn't prevent player from casting"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (add-opponent)
                 (add-player-restriction :player-2 :cannot-cast-spells))
          db (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:black 1})
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))]
      (is (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 object-id)
          "Player 1 should still be able to cast"))))

;; Test 4: Expired restriction allows casting
(deftest expired-restriction-allows-casting-test
  (testing "after restriction expires, player can cast again"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (add-player-restriction-with-expiry :player-1 :cannot-cast-spells 1 :cleanup))
          db (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:black 1})
          ;; Expire grants (turn 2, cleanup)
          db' (grants/expire-grants db 2 :cleanup)
          hand (q/get-hand db' :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))]
      (is (rules/can-cast? db' :player-1 object-id)
          "Should be able to cast after restriction expires"))))

;; Test 5: has-restriction? returns true for multiple grants
(deftest has-restriction-multiple-grants-test
  (testing "has-restriction? returns true when multiple restriction grants exist"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (add-player-restriction :player-1 :cannot-cast-spells)
                 (add-player-restriction :player-1 :cannot-cast-spells))]
      (is (rules/has-restriction? db :player-1 :cannot-cast-spells)
          "Should find restriction among multiple grants"))))

;; Test 6: has-restriction? returns false for different restriction type
(deftest has-restriction-different-type-test
  (testing "has-restriction? returns false when restriction is different type"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (add-player-restriction :player-1 :cannot-attack))]
      (is (not (rules/has-restriction? db :player-1 :cannot-cast-spells))
          "Should not find :cannot-cast-spells when only :cannot-attack exists"))))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] rules.cljs line 6-11 - Add require for grants namespace:
      ```clojure
      [fizzle.engine.grants :as grants]
      ```
- [ ] rules.cljs - Add has-restriction? function (before can-cast?):
      ```clojure
      (defn has-restriction?
        "Check if a player has an active restriction of the given type.
         Returns true if restriction exists, false otherwise.
         
         Used by can-cast? to enforce player restrictions like 'cannot cast spells'.
         
         Edge cases:
         - Non-existent player: returns false (no grants)
         - Multiple matching grants: returns true (finds first match)
         - Different restriction type: returns false (specific matching)"
        [db player-id restriction-type]
        (let [restriction-grants (grants/get-player-grants-by-type db player-id :restriction)]
          (boolean
            (some #(= restriction-type (get-in % [:grant/data :restriction/type]))
                  restriction-grants))))
      ```
- [ ] rules.cljs:139-157 - Modify can-cast? to check restriction FIRST:
      ```clojure
      (defn can-cast?
        "Check if a player can cast a card.
      
         Checks if ANY valid casting mode is castable.
         A mode is valid if:
         - Player does not have :cannot-cast-spells restriction
         - The card is in the mode's required zone
         - Player can pay the mode's mana cost
         - Player can pay all additional costs
         - Valid targets exist for all required targeting
      
         Returns false if object doesn't exist or no modes are castable."
        [db player-id object-id]
        ;; Check player restrictions FIRST (before any other checks)
        (if (has-restriction? db player-id :cannot-cast-spells)
          false
          (if-let [obj (q/get-object db object-id)]
            (let [card (:object/card obj)
                  modes (get-casting-modes db player-id object-id)
                  has-mode (boolean (some #(can-cast-mode? db player-id object-id %) modes))
                  has-targets (targeting/has-valid-targets? db player-id card)]
              (and has-mode has-targets))
            false)))
      ```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] has-restriction? function added to rules.cljs
- [ ] can-cast? checks for :cannot-cast-spells restriction BEFORE other checks
- [ ] Player with restriction cannot cast any spells (can-cast? returns false)
- [ ] Player without restriction can cast normally
- [ ] Restriction on opponent doesn't affect player's casting
- [ ] Expired restrictions no longer prevent casting
- [ ] Different restriction type doesn't block casting
- [ ] Multiple restrictions still detected
- [ ] All existing tests pass (618+)
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint, fmt, tests)

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoding Orim's Chant logic (use generic restriction checking)
- ❌ NO modifying get-castable-cards separately (can-cast? is the enforcement point)
- ❌ NO checking restrictions after mana checks (restriction should be first check - more efficient)
- ❌ NO using count instead of some (multiple grants should work correctly)
- ❌ NO forgetting to require grants namespace

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases)

**Edge Case: Multiple restrictions from different sources**
- Player could have multiple :cannot-cast-spells grants (from different Orim's Chants)
- has-restriction? should return true if ANY matching restriction exists
- Use (some ...) pattern with boolean coercion
- Test: Add 2 restrictions, verify still blocked

**Edge Case: Player doesn't exist**
- get-player-grants-by-type returns [] for non-existent player
- some on [] returns nil (falsy)
- boolean coercion ensures consistent false return
- This is correct behavior - non-existent player has no restrictions

**Edge Case: Different restriction type**
- Player with :cannot-attack should still be able to cast
- has-restriction? checks specific restriction-type
- Test: Add :cannot-attack, verify casting still works

**Edge Case: Lands bypass can-cast?**
- Lands use play-land event, not can-cast?
- :cannot-cast-spells should NOT prevent land plays (correct MTG rules)
- No changes needed - play-land doesn't call can-cast?

**Performance Note: Check restriction first**
- Restriction check is O(n) where n = player grants (typically 0-2)
- Much faster than mana calculations and mode checks
- Check restriction FIRST for early exit on blocked players

**Reference Implementation**
- grants.cljs:113-118 - get-player-grants-by-type (already implemented)
- effects.cljs:530-570 - :add-restriction effect (already implemented)
- rules.cljs:139-157 - Current can-cast? implementation
close_reason: Implemented has-restriction? function and modified can-cast? to check restrictions before allowing casting. All tests pass.

title: Refactor opponent turn: model as real turn with active-player switching
description:
## Problem

The opponent's turn is currently a hack: a bare `opponent-draw` call wedged into the player's `::start-turn` event handler. This already caused bugs (double draw, infinite loop) and won't scale to the bot system which needs the opponent to take real turns with phases, priority, and decision points.

## Current State

- `opponent-draw` is called manually before `start-turn` in the `::start-turn` event handler
- `:game/active-player` never changes — always points to player-1
- Turn counter only tracks player turns (opponent turns are invisible)
- No phase progression for opponent (no untap, upkeep, draw step, main, etc.)
- History entry for opponent draw is manually constructed in the event handler

## Target State

- Turns alternate between player and opponent via `:game/active-player`
- Opponent gets a real (abbreviated) turn: at minimum draw step, expandable to full phases later
- Turn counter reflects both players' turns (or separate per-player turn tracking)
- `start-turn` works identically for both players — no special-casing
- Bot protocol hooks into opponent turn phases (`:on-turn-start`, `:on-main-phase`, etc.)
- The `opponent-draw` function and its manual orchestration in `::start-turn` are eliminated

## Architecture Already in Place

- Opponent is a real player entity (`:player/id :opponent`) with full schema
- `:game/active-player` exists as `:db.type/ref` in schema (just never switches)
- All effects accept `player-id` — draw, lose-life, deal-damage work for either player
- `get-opponent-id` query exists
- `IBot` protocol designed with turn triggers (`:on-turn-start`, `:on-priority`, etc.)

## Key Design Decisions Needed

1. **Turn numbering**: Increment for both players (MTG-correct) or track per-player?
2. **Goldfish turn phases**: Draw-only? Or untap+draw minimum?
3. **Player's end step → opponent turn → player turn**: How does the UI signal turn transitions?
4. **Bot integration point**: Does the bot get called during opponent's turn phases, or does it subscribe to events?

## Scope

- Refactor `start-turn` / `::start-turn` to alternate `:game/active-player`
- Add opponent turn flow (at minimum: draw step via existing machinery)
- Remove `opponent-draw` hack from `::start-turn` event handler
- Update history to naturally record opponent turn actions
- Update phase-bar UI to reflect whose turn it is
- Prerequisite for bot system (Phase 5 in roadmap)

## What This Unlocks

- Bot system can hook into opponent turn phases
- Burn bot can bolt during its main phase
- Control bot can hold up mana during opponent's turn
- Discard bot can cast Duress during its main phase
- Clean foundation for any future opponent interaction

title: Add two-screen routing (setup placeholder vs game)
design:
## Goal
Implement screen routing so the app can switch between a setup screen (placeholder for Epic 2) and the game screen, using a re-frame :active-screen key in app-db.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Epic fizzle-8305 requires two-screen routing. Currently core.cljs renders the game screen unconditionally with the new CSS Grid layout. Epic 2 (fizzle-m2sg) will fill in the setup screen content, but the routing shell must exist now. The game currently initializes via ::events/init-game dispatched in core.cljs init function.

## Implementation

### 1. Add :active-screen to app-db initialization
In events/game.cljs, update the ::init-game event handler to include :active-screen :game in the initial db map.

### 2. Create re-frame subscription
In subs/game.cljs, add:
\`\`\`clojure
(rf/reg-sub ::active-screen
  (fn [db] (:active-screen db)))
\`\`\`

### 3. Create re-frame event
In events/game.cljs, add:
\`\`\`clojure
(rf/reg-event-db ::set-active-screen
  (fn [db [_ screen]] (assoc db :active-screen screen)))
\`\`\`

### 4. Update core.cljs app function
Subscribe to ::active-screen. Conditionally render:
- :game → current game-grid layout with all zones
- :setup → placeholder div with centered "Setup Screen (Coming Soon)" text

Extract the game grid into a defn- game-screen component. The app function becomes a router:
\`\`\`clojure
(defn app []
  (let [screen @(rf/subscribe [::subs/active-screen])]
    [:div {:class "min-h-screen bg-surface font-mono text-text"}
     ;; Header with nav
     [:div {:class "flex items-center gap-4 p-3 border-b border-border"}
      [:h1 {:class "text-text"} "Fizzle"]
      [:button {:class (nav-btn-class (= screen :setup))
                :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/set-active-screen :setup])}
       "Setup"]
      [:button {:class (nav-btn-class (= screen :game))
                :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/set-active-screen :game])}
       "Game"]]
     ;; Screen content
     (case screen
       :setup [setup-screen]
       :game [game-screen])]))
\`\`\`

### 5. Add nav button style helper
\`\`\`clojure
(defn- nav-btn-class [active?]
  (str "px-3 py-1 text-sm rounded cursor-pointer "
       (if active?
         "bg-accent text-surface font-bold"
         "bg-surface-raised text-text-muted")))
\`\`\`

### 6. Add setup screen placeholder
\`\`\`clojure
(defn- setup-screen []
  [:div {:class "flex items-center justify-center h-[80vh] text-text-muted text-lg"}
   "Setup Screen (Coming Soon)"])
\`\`\`

### 7. Update game-screen to be the extracted grid
Move the current game-grid div into a defn- game-screen component. The grid classes stay on this component.

### 8. Add ::subs/active-screen require to core.cljs
Ensure core.cljs has the subs namespace aliased to subscribe.

### 9. Run make validate

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :active-screen key exists in app-db after init, defaults to :game
- [ ] Subscription ::active-screen returns current screen value
- [ ] Event ::set-active-screen switches between :setup and :game
- [ ] Game screen renders the full CSS Grid game UI
- [ ] Setup screen renders placeholder text
- [ ] Nav buttons visible in header, active button highlighted
- [ ] Clicking Setup/Game buttons switches screens
- [ ] make validate passes (0 failures, 0 errors, 0 lint warnings)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO URL-based routing (overkill for two screens, re-frame db is sufficient)
- ❌ NO lazy loading of game screen (game initializes on app start regardless)
- ❌ NO inline :style maps

## Key Considerations

**Game State on Screen Switch**
Game keeps running when switching to setup screen. No teardown/recreation. This is intentional — player can switch back and forth without losing state.

**Init Event Compatibility**
The init-game event must still work. Adding :active-screen to the db map should not break any existing event handlers that destructure or merge db state.

**Subscription Namespace**
core.cljs already requires subs.game — verify the alias name and use it for the new subscription.
close_reason: Implemented two-screen routing with :active-screen in app-db, subscription, event, nav buttons, setup placeholder, and game-screen extraction. All 1000 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Migrate City of Brass trigger to event system and remove dead code
design:
## Goal

Migrate City of Brass :becomes-tapped trigger from scanning-based system to event-based system, then delete the now-dead scanning code.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study Existing Patterns

**Event system (use this):**
- trigger_registry.cljs:12-23 - register-trigger! pattern
- trigger_registry.cljs:39-54 - unregister-by-source! on zone leave
- trigger_dispatch.cljs:34-57 - dispatch-event flow
- events.cljs:25-37 - permanent-tapped-event exists

**Old system (delete this):**
- triggers.cljs:220-253 - fire-matching-triggers
- triggers.cljs:274-310 - fire-and-resolve-triggers
- triggers.cljs:74-83 - check-trigger :becomes-tapped
- triggers.cljs:106-117 - resolve-trigger :becomes-tapped
- game.cljs:529 - call to fire-matching-triggers

### 2. Register City of Brass Trigger on ETB

In game.cljs play-land or a new registration helper:

\`\`\`clojure
;; When City of Brass enters battlefield, register its trigger
(defn register-card-triggers!
  [object-id controller-id card]
  (doseq [trigger (:card/triggers card)]
    (registry/register-trigger!
      {:trigger/id (random-uuid)
       :trigger/event-type :permanent-tapped
       :trigger/source object-id
       :trigger/controller controller-id
       :trigger/filter {:event/object-id :self}  ; only when THIS permanent taps
       :trigger/uses-stack? true
       :trigger/effects (:trigger/effects trigger)
       :trigger/description (:trigger/description trigger)})))
\`\`\`

### 3. Dispatch Event on Tap

In activate-mana-ability (game.cljs), after tapping:

\`\`\`clojure
;; Replace fire-matching-triggers with:
(dispatch/dispatch-event db (game-events/permanent-tapped-event object-id player-id))
\`\`\`

### 4. Add resolve-trigger :permanent-tapped

In triggers.cljs:

\`\`\`clojure
(defmethod resolve-trigger :permanent-tapped
  [db trigger]
  ;; Same logic as old :becomes-tapped resolver
  (let [controller (:trigger/controller trigger)
        effects-list (:trigger/effects trigger)]
    (reduce (fn [db' effect]
              (let [resolved-effect (if (= :controller (:effect/target effect))
                                      (assoc effect :effect/target controller)
                                      effect)]
                (effects/execute-effect db' controller resolved-effect)))
            db
            effects-list)))
\`\`\`

### 5. Unregister Triggers on Zone Leave

Ensure triggers are unregistered when permanent leaves battlefield:

\`\`\`clojure
;; In zone change handling (or sacrifice/destroy):
(registry/unregister-by-source! object-id)
\`\`\`

### 6. Delete Dead Code

**triggers.cljs - Remove:**
- Lines 58-71: check-trigger multimethod and :default
- Lines 74-83: check-trigger :becomes-tapped
- Lines 106-117: resolve-trigger :becomes-tapped
- Lines 212-253: fire-matching-triggers and get-battlefield-objects helper
- Lines 274-310: fire-and-resolve-triggers

**game.cljs - Remove:**
- Line 529: call to triggers/fire-matching-triggers
- Remove fire-matching-triggers from require if no other usages

### 7. Update Tests

**Delete tests for removed functions:**
- triggers_test.cljs: test-fire-matching-triggers-* (lines 238-340)

**Add tests for new event-based flow (in triggers_test.cljs or new file):**
- test-cob-trigger-registered-on-etb: Verify registry/get-all-triggers includes CoB trigger after ETB
- test-cob-trigger-unregistered-on-leave: Verify registry empty after sacrifice/destroy
- test-cob-tap-dispatches-event: Verify dispatch-event called with :permanent-tapped event type
- test-cob-trigger-deals-damage: Tap CoB, resolve trigger, assert player life reduced by 1
- test-cob-multiple-taps: Tap CoB twice, verify two triggers fire, life reduced by 2

## Success Criteria

- [ ] City of Brass trigger works via dispatch-event (verify: tap CoB, life decreases by 1)
- [ ] fire-matching-triggers function deleted: \`rg "fire-matching-triggers" src/\` returns 0 results
- [ ] fire-and-resolve-triggers function deleted: \`rg "fire-and-resolve-triggers" src/\` returns 0 results
- [ ] check-trigger multimethod deleted: \`rg "defmulti check-trigger" src/\` returns 0 results
- [ ] No references to deleted functions: \`rg "(fire-matching-triggers|fire-and-resolve-triggers|check-trigger)" src/\` returns 0 results
- [ ] 5+ new tests for event-based trigger flow pass
- [ ] Existing City of Brass tests still pass (if any)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Trigger Source Leaves Battlefield**
- If City of Brass is sacrificed/destroyed before trigger resolves, trigger should still resolve
- Trigger has controller-id, can still deal damage even if source gone
- Verify: sacrifice CoB with trigger on stack, damage still dealt

**Edge Case: Multiple Permanents with Same Trigger**
- Two City of Brass on battlefield, tap one
- Only the tapped one's trigger should fire (filter by :event/object-id :self)
- Verify: tap one CoB, only 1 damage dealt

**Edge Case: Rapid Multiple Taps**
- Tap same CoB multiple times (if untapped between)
- Each tap should dispatch event and add trigger
- Verify: untap, tap, untap, tap = 2 triggers = 2 damage

**Unregistration on Zone Leave**
- CRITICAL: Must unregister triggers when permanent leaves battlefield
- If not unregistered, ghost triggers remain in registry
- Verify: sacrifice CoB, registry/get-all-triggers no longer contains its trigger

**Reference Implementation**
- Study how turn-based triggers use dispatch-event in turn_based.cljs
- Follow same pattern for card triggers

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO leaving check-trigger or fire-matching-triggers code (even commented out)
- ❌ NO registering triggers without corresponding unregister on zone leave
- ❌ NO hardcoded object-ids in tests (use dynamic test fixture IDs)
- ❌ NO skipping unregister verification test (ghost triggers cause bugs)
- ❌ NO modifying City of Brass card definition (card data stays same, only engine changes)
close_reason: Closed

title: Show top stack item inline near Yield button to reduce visual distance
description: Currently the stack is in a collapsible right column, far from the control buttons. When deciding whether to yield (resolve), the player needs to look across the screen to see what's on top of the stack. This breaks gameplay flow. Add an inline preview of the top stack item near the Yield button (or in the controls area) so the player can see what they're resolving without scanning to the right panel. The full stack panel remains for viewing the complete stack. Consider: compact inline format (e.g. 'Resolve: Dark Ritual'), visual connection between preview and Yield button, updating dynamically as stack changes.
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Yield button label shows top stack item card name: 'Yield: {card-name}'
- Yield All button label shows stack item count: 'Yield All ({count})'
- When stack is empty, buttons show plain 'Yield' and 'Yield All' (both disabled)
- Card name extraction follows same priority logic as stack-item-view: card-name > description > type fallback
- Button width grows naturally to fit card name (no truncation)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Yield button shows 'Yield: Dark Ritual' when Dark Ritual is top of stack
- [ ] Yield button shows 'Yield' when stack is empty
- [ ] Yield All button shows 'Yield All (5)' when 5 items on stack
- [ ] Yield All button shows 'Yield All' when 1 or fewer items (disabled)
- [ ] Fallback labels work for stack items without card names (triggers, abilities)
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO new subscriptions (reuse existing ::stack subscription already in controls view)
- ❌ NO new UI elements/components (modify button labels only, not layout)
- ❌ NO card name truncation or ellipsis (let button grow naturally)
- ❌ NO changes to stack-item-view or stack panel (this feature is controls-only)

## Approach
Modify views/controls.cljs to extract the card name from (first stack) and interpolate it into the Yield button label. Add (count stack) to Yield All label. Use same label priority logic as stack-item-view in views/stack.cljs (card-name > description > type fallback). Single-file change plus tests.

## Architecture
- views/controls.cljs — Modify Yield and Yield All button labels
- No new files, subscriptions, or components

## Parallelism Map

### Independent Work Streams
1. **Yield button label** (views/controls.cljs)
   - Extract top item name, update button text
   - Estimated complexity: low

### Parallelism Assessment
- Independent streams: 1
- Recommendation: solo execution
- Rationale: Single file change, no parallelism opportunity

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Players must scan from the control buttons to the stack panel to see what resolves next. During fast storm reps this breaks flow.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- views/controls.cljs:40-47 — Yield/Yield All buttons already subscribe to ::stack
- views/stack.cljs:7-19 — stack-item-view has label priority logic (card-name > description > type)
- subs/game.cljs:42-66 — ::stack subscription enriches items with card names

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Inline in button label ✓
**What:** Button text becomes 'Yield: {name}' dynamically
**Chosen because:** Zero new UI, leverages existing subscription, minimal code change

#### 2. Adjacent text element ❌
**Why explored:** Standard pattern for showing context near buttons
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Adds layout complexity for no benefit. Button label IS the preview.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Button label approach causes layout issues

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** Yield and Yield All button label text changes
**Out of scope:** Stack panel changes, new subscriptions, card type icons
close_reason: Closed

Phase 1 auto-pay is acceptable stopgap.

title: Task 1: Extend casting rules with alternate cost modes
design:
## Goal
Extend the casting system to support alternate casting modes. Cards can define `:card/alternate-costs` and the engine finds all valid modes based on card's current zone.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- `src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:16-29` - Current `can-cast?` implementation
- `src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:76-94` - Current `cast-spell` implementation  
- `src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs` - Cost checking/payment patterns (`:pay-life` already exists)
- `src/main/fizzle/engine/mana.cljs` - Mana payment patterns
- `src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs` - Existing test patterns to follow

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Add to `src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs`:

```clojure
;; === Alternate casting modes tests ===

;; get-casting-modes tests
(deftest get-casting-modes-primary-only-hand-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes returns primary mode for card in hand without alternates"
    ;; Setup: Dark Ritual in hand (no alternate costs)
    ;; Assert: Returns vector with one mode {:mode/id :primary ...}
    ;; Bug caught: If returns empty, cards can't be cast at all
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-no-modes-wrong-zone-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes returns empty for card in graveyard without flashback"
    ;; Setup: Dark Ritual in graveyard
    ;; Assert: Returns empty vector
    ;; Bug caught: If returns mode, all cards could be cast from graveyard
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-flashback-in-graveyard-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes returns flashback mode for flashback card in graveyard"
    ;; Setup: Deep Analysis (with flashback) in graveyard
    ;; Assert: Returns vector with flashback mode {:mode/id :flashback ...}
    ;; Bug caught: If returns empty, flashback cards can't be cast from GY
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-flashback-not-in-hand-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes does NOT return flashback mode when card in hand"
    ;; Setup: Deep Analysis in hand
    ;; Assert: Returns only primary mode, not flashback
    ;; Bug caught: If returns flashback, cards could pay flashback cost from hand
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-multiple-modes-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes returns both modes for card with hand-alternate"
    ;; Setup: Gush-like card with {:alternate/zone :hand} in hand
    ;; Assert: Returns two modes (primary + alternate)
    ;; Bug caught: If returns only one, player can't choose casting mode
    ))

;; can-cast-mode? tests
(deftest can-cast-mode-sufficient-mana-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? returns true when player has sufficient mana"
    ;; Setup: Player has {B}, mode requires {B}
    ;; Assert: Returns true
    ;; Bug caught: Basic mana check regression
    ))

(deftest can-cast-mode-insufficient-mana-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? returns false when player lacks mana"
    ;; Setup: Player has {}, mode requires {B}
    ;; Assert: Returns false
    ;; Bug caught: If true, spells cast without mana
    ))

(deftest can-cast-mode-with-life-cost-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? checks additional life cost"
    ;; Setup: Player has 3+ life, mode has :pay-life 3
    ;; Assert: Returns true
    ;; Bug caught: If additional costs ignored, flashback is free
    ))

(deftest can-cast-mode-insufficient-life-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? returns false when can't pay life cost"
    ;; Setup: Player has 2 life, mode has :pay-life 3
    ;; Assert: Returns false
    ;; Bug caught: If true, player can pay life they don't have
    ))

;; Edge case tests
(deftest get-casting-modes-nil-alternate-costs-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes handles nil alternate-costs gracefully"
    ;; Setup: Card without :card/alternate-costs key
    ;; Assert: Returns primary mode only, no error
    ;; Bug caught: NPE on cards without alternate costs
    ))

(deftest get-casting-modes-empty-alternate-costs-test
  (testing "get-casting-modes handles empty alternate-costs vector"
    ;; Setup: Card with :card/alternate-costs []
    ;; Assert: Returns primary mode only
    ;; Bug caught: Empty vector treated differently than nil
    ))

(deftest can-cast-mode-empty-mana-cost-test
  (testing "can-cast-mode? returns true for zero mana cost mode"
    ;; Setup: Mode with :mode/mana-cost {}
    ;; Assert: Returns true (free spell)
    ;; Bug caught: Empty cost map treated as unpayable
    ))

;; Backwards compatibility
(deftest can-cast-unchanged-for-existing-cards-test
  (testing "can-cast? behavior unchanged for cards without alternate costs"
    ;; Run same tests as existing can-cast tests
    ;; Bug caught: Regression in existing functionality
    ))
```

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `get-primary-mode` helper (returns mode map for normal casting)
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `get-alternate-modes` helper (filters alternates by current zone)
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `get-casting-modes` function:
  ```clojure
  (defn get-casting-modes
    "Returns all valid casting modes for a card based on its current zone.
     Each mode is a map with :mode/id, :mode/mana-cost, :mode/additional-costs, :mode/on-resolve"
    [db player-id object-id]
    ...)
  ```
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `can-cast-mode?` function:
  ```clojure
  (defn can-cast-mode?
    "Check if player can pay all costs for a specific casting mode.
     Checks mana cost AND all additional costs."
    [db player-id object-id mode]
    ...)
  ```
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Update `can-cast?` to use `get-casting-modes`:
  ```clojure
  ;; Backwards compatible: true if ANY mode is castable
  (defn can-cast? [db player-id object-id]
    (let [modes (get-casting-modes db player-id object-id)]
      (some #(can-cast-mode? db player-id object-id %) modes)))
  ```
- [ ] `rules.cljs` - Add `get-castable-cards` function:
  ```clojure
  (defn get-castable-cards
    "Returns all cards (from hand and graveyard) with at least one valid casting mode."
    [db player-id]
    ...)
  ```
- [ ] `rules_test.cljs` - All 12+ tests listed above

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `get-casting-modes` returns primary mode for hand cards
- [ ] `get-casting-modes` returns flashback mode for graveyard cards with flashback
- [ ] `get-casting-modes` returns empty vector for cards in wrong zone without alternates
- [ ] `get-casting-modes` returns multiple modes when applicable (Gush pattern)
- [ ] `can-cast-mode?` validates mana cost via `mana/can-pay?`
- [ ] `can-cast-mode?` validates additional costs via `costs/can-pay?`
- [ ] `can-cast-mode?` returns false when any cost is unpayable
- [ ] Existing `can-cast?` tests pass unchanged (backwards compatible)
- [ ] All 12+ new tests pass: `make test`
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: `make validate`

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Card without mana cost**
- Some cards may have nil or missing `:card/mana-cost`
- Treat as zero cost `{}` (e.g., Lotus Petal pattern)
- Test: `get-casting-modes-nil-mana-cost-test`

**Edge Case: Empty alternate-costs**
- Card may have `:card/alternate-costs []` (empty vector)
- Must return primary mode only, not fail
- Treat same as nil

**Edge Case: Unknown additional cost type**
- If `:cost/type` not recognized by `costs/can-pay?`
- Current costs.cljs throws - let it bubble up (fail-fast)
- Document this behavior, fix in future task if needed

**Edge Case: Invalid player-id**
- If player-id doesn't exist, return empty modes
- Follow pattern from existing `can-cast?` which returns nil/false

**Backwards Compatibility**
- Existing cards have no `:card/alternate-costs` key
- `get-casting-modes` must handle missing key gracefully
- All existing `can-cast?` tests must continue passing

**Reference Implementation**
- Study `rules.cljs:16-29` for `can-cast?` pattern
- Study `costs.cljs:167-206` for `:pay-life` pattern

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO hardcoding specific cards (Deep Analysis, Gush) - use generic alternate-costs structure
- ❌ NO breaking existing can-cast? tests - backwards compatibility required
- ❌ NO adding new effect types - this task is casting rules only
- ❌ NO modifying stack resolution - that's a separate task
- ❌ NO modifying UI - this task is engine only
close_reason: Implemented get-casting-modes, can-cast-mode?, get-castable-cards. All 15 new tests pass. Pre-commit hooks pass.
