title: Feature: Yield All - resolve entire stack in one action
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Yield All resolves all stack items that were present when the button was clicked
- Resolution stops when a selection is needed (tutor, discard, targeting, scry, peek-and-select)
- Resolution stops when the top stack item was NOT present when Yield All was clicked (e.g., storm-created copies)
- Resolution stops when the stack is empty
- Each individual item still logs to history normally
- Cleanup phase handling (maybe-continue-cleanup) runs once at the end
- Existing Yield (single item) behavior is unchanged

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Yield All button visible and enabled when stack has 2+ items
- [ ] Yield All resolves all original stack items in LIFO order
- [ ] Yield All stops at selections (pending-selection set correctly)
- [ ] Yield All stops when new items appear on stack (storm copies)
- [ ] Existing ::resolve-top tests pass unchanged after refactor
- [ ] New tests cover: empty stack, single item, multiple items, selection interruption, new-item stop
- [ ] History entries created for each resolved item
- [ ] Cleanup phase handled correctly after Yield All
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] make validate passing (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO recursive re-frame dispatch for looping (flickering: multiple React renders between resolutions)
- ❌ NO modifying existing ::resolve-top behavior (refactor only: extract shared function, keep behavior identical)
- ❌ NO auto-resolving selections with defaults (safety: user must always make explicit choices)
- ❌ NO resolving newly-created stack items (scope: only items present at click time)
- ❌ NO infinite loop risk (correctness: loop must always make progress by removing an item or stopping)

## Approach
Extract a pure resolve-one-item function from the existing ::resolve-top event handler. This function takes db and returns {:db new-db} or {:db new-db :pending-selection data}. Refactor ::resolve-top to be a thin wrapper around resolve-one-item. Add new ::resolve-all event handler that snapshots current stack-item IDs, then loops calling resolve-one-item, stopping when: selection needed, stack empty, or top item not in snapshot. Add Yield All button to controls UI.

## Architecture
- events/game.cljs: Extract resolve-one-item, refactor ::resolve-top, add ::resolve-all
- views/controls.cljs: Add Yield All button next to Yield
- Subscription: Reuse existing stack subscription for button enable/disable logic

## Parallelism Map

### Independent Work Streams
1. **Extract + refactor** (events/game.cljs)
   - Extract resolve-one-item, refactor ::resolve-top, add ::resolve-all
   - Estimated complexity: medium
2. **UI button** (views/controls.cljs)
   - Add Yield All button, wire to ::resolve-all
   - Estimated complexity: low

### Stream Dependencies
- Stream 2 depends on Stream 1 (needs ::resolve-all event to exist)

### Suggested Waves
- Wave 1: Stream 1 (extract + refactor + new handler)
- Wave 2: Stream 2 (UI button)

### File Ownership Boundaries
| Stream | Owns (exclusive) | Shared (needs coordination) |
|--------|-------------------|-----------------------------|
| Extract + refactor | events/game.cljs | — |
| UI button | views/controls.cljs | — |

### Parallelism Assessment
- Independent streams: 1 (sequential dependency)
- Recommendation: solo execution
- Rationale: Only 2 streams with dependency, not worth team coordination overhead

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Storm decks generate stacks of 10+ items. Currently each Yield click resolves one item, requiring 12+ clicks for a single Brain Freeze storm. This makes practice tedious and breaks flow.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- events/game.cljs:399-439 - ::resolve-top has 4 paths: spell, storm-copy, activated-ability, other
- events/game.cljs:269-293 - resolve-stack-item-effects handles storm copy creation
- events/game.cljs:380-396 - maybe-continue-cleanup runs after each resolution
- events/selection.cljs:576-702 - resolve-spell-with-selection handles 5 selection types
- views/controls.cljs:40-43 - Yield button dispatches ::resolve-top
- engine/stack.cljs:50-78 - Stack-item queries (get-top, get-all, remove)

**Selection types that pause resolution:**
1. Player targeting (:effect/target :any-player)
2. Tutor (search library)
3. Discard (player selection)
4. Scry (order top N)
5. Peek-and-select (Flash of Insight)

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Loop in single event handler
**What it is:** New ::resolve-all handler with an internal loop. Simple but duplicates resolution path logic from ::resolve-top.
**Why we looked at this:** Simplest possible implementation.
**Pros:** Simple, atomic state update, no flickering
**Cons:** Duplicates resolution logic from ::resolve-top
**⚠️ NOT CHOSEN BECAUSE:** Code duplication between ::resolve-top and ::resolve-all would diverge over time.

#### 2. Recursive dispatch with flag
**What it is:** Set a flag in app-db, have ::resolve-top auto-dispatch itself when flag is set.
**Why we looked at this:** Reuses existing ::resolve-top directly.
**Pros:** No duplication
**Cons:** Multiple React re-renders (flickering), complex flag management
**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Multiple re-frame dispatches cause flickering. Anti-pattern.
**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** re-frame adds batched dispatch support.

#### 3. Extract shared resolution function ✓
**What it is:** Extract resolve-one-item from ::resolve-top. Both handlers call it. ::resolve-all loops over it.
**Chosen because:** No duplication, clean architecture, atomic state update. Matches codebase pattern of helper functions called by event handlers.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Extract resolve-one-item function
- Refactor ::resolve-top to use it
- New ::resolve-all event handler with snapshot loop
- Yield All button in controls UI
- Tests for new functionality

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Auto-resolving selections (never - user must choose)
- Keyboard shortcut for Yield All (deferred to fizzle-txiw)
- Resolving newly-created items (never - by design requirement)

### Open Questions
- Should Yield All button be disabled during pending-selection? (likely yes, same as Yield)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| What happens at selections? | Stop at selection | Loop breaks on pending-selection, user completes selection manually |
| Resolve storm-created copies? | No, stop after original items | Snapshot IDs at click time, stop when top item not in snapshot |
| Implementation approach? | Extract shared function | Refactor resolve-top, share resolve-one-item with resolve-all |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Stack Resolution Flow
**Question explored:** How does the current Yield button resolve one item?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/game.cljs:399-439 - 4-path resolution handler
- events/selection.cljs:576-702 - Selection detection and pausing
- engine/stack.cljs - Stack-item queries

**Findings:**
- Resolution has 4 distinct paths based on stack-item type
- 5 selection types can pause resolution
- Storm resolution creates new stack-items mid-resolution
- maybe-continue-cleanup runs after each resolution for cleanup phase handling

**Conclusion:** All 4 paths can be unified into resolve-one-item returning {:db db} or {:db db :pending-selection data}

### Dead-End Paths
None — approach was straightforward after codebase research.

### Open Concerns Raised
- History logging: Each resolved item should still create individual history entries → Confirmed: resolution functions handle their own logging internally
close_reason: All success criteria met. resolve-one-item extracted, ::resolve-top refactored, ::resolve-all with snapshot loop and per-item history logging, Yield All button added. 12 new tests, make validate passes.

title: Add must-contain state management to setup events and subscriptions
design:
## Goal
Add must-contain card selection state to the setup system: events for toggling/clearing must-contain cards, subscriptions for the UI, and preset persistence. No UI changes yet — this task delivers the pure event handlers and subscriptions that the chip picker UI will consume.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (pure handler functions + subscriptions + tests)

## Implementation

1. Study existing setup patterns:
   - events/setup.cljs:38-49 — init-setup-handler pattern (db -> db)
   - events/setup.cljs:65-75 — move-to-side-handler pattern (db, card-id -> db, reads deck from db)
   - events/setup.cljs:97-111 — save-preset-handler (preset config structure)
   - events/setup.cljs:114-124 — load-preset-handler
   - subs/setup.cljs:68-88 — derived subscription pattern (current-main-grouped)

2. Write tests first (TDD) in src/test/fizzle/events/setup_test.cljs (extend existing):
   - toggle-must-contain-handler: card not in must-contain -> adds with count 1
   - toggle-must-contain-handler: card at count 1 -> increments to 2
   - toggle-must-contain-handler: card at max copies (e.g., 4 Dark Rituals) -> cycles to 0 (removes)
   - toggle-must-contain-handler: card-id not in main deck -> no-op
   - toggle-must-contain-handler: total must-contain already 7 and adding new card -> no-op
   - toggle-must-contain-handler: total must-contain 6, card at count 1 -> allows increment to 2 only if total stays <= 7
   - clear-must-contain-handler: resets to empty map
   - init-setup-handler: result includes :setup/must-contain {}
   - save-preset-handler: saved config map includes :must-contain key
   - load-preset-handler: restores must-contain from preset config
   - load-preset-handler: defaults to {} when preset has no :must-contain key (backward compat with old presets)
   - move-to-side-handler: when card moved to sideboard reduces main count below must-contain count, must-contain count is clamped (e.g., 4 DR in main, must-contain 3, move 2 to side -> must-contain clamped to 2)
   - Subscription test: must-contain-cards returns vec of maps with shape {:card/id :dark-ritual :card/name "Dark Ritual" :count 2 :max-count 4}

3. Implementation checklist:
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — add toggle-must-contain-handler (db, card-id) -> db
         Reads :setup/main-deck and :setup/must-contain from db
         Finds max copies of card-id in main deck
         If card-id not in main deck: return db unchanged
         If not in must-contain: add with count 1 (if total < 7)
         If count < max AND total < 7: increment count
         If count = max OR would exceed 7: remove from must-contain (cycle to 0)
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — add clear-must-contain-handler (db) -> db
         Returns (assoc db :setup/must-contain {})
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — extend init-setup-handler to include :setup/must-contain {}
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — extend stash-setup-config to include :setup/must-contain
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — extend save-preset-handler config map: add :must-contain key
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — extend load-preset-handler: read :must-contain from config, default to {}
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — extend move-to-side-handler: after moving card, clamp must-contain count if it exceeds new main-deck count for that card
   - [ ] events/setup.cljs — register ::toggle-must-contain and ::clear-must-contain rf/reg-event-db
   - [ ] subs/setup.cljs — add ::must-contain subscription: (fn [db _] (:setup/must-contain db))
   - [ ] subs/setup.cljs — add ::must-contain-cards derived subscription:
         Input signals: ::must-contain, ::current-main
         Returns: vec of {:card/id kw, :card/name str, :count int, :max-count int}
         for each entry in must-contain map, enriched with card name from card-lookup
   - [ ] subs/setup.cljs — extend ::setup-config select-keys to include :setup/must-contain

## Success Criteria
- [ ] toggle-must-contain cycles count 0->1->...->max->0 for a card
- [ ] toggle-must-contain is no-op for card-id not in main deck
- [ ] Total must-contain capped at 7 (global cap across all cards)
- [ ] move-to-side clamps must-contain count when main deck copies decrease
- [ ] Must-contain persists in presets (save includes, load restores)
- [ ] Loading old presets without must-contain key defaults to {} (backward compat)
- [ ] Init includes :setup/must-contain {}
- [ ] ::must-contain-cards subscription returns vec of {:card/id :card/name :count :max-count}
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (no regressions)
- [ ] make validate passing (lint + fmt + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Sideboard Swap Invalidation**
When move-to-side reduces main deck copies of a card below its must-contain count, the must-contain count MUST be clamped. Example: 4 Dark Rituals in main, must-contain 3, move 2 to side -> main has 2, must-contain must clamp to 2. The move-to-side-handler already exists and must be extended — NOT a separate event.

**Edge Case: Card Not In Deck**
toggle-must-contain with a card-id not in :setup/main-deck must be a no-op. This prevents stale state if UI and deck state get out of sync.

**Edge Case: Backward Compatible Preset Loading**
Old presets saved before this feature won't have a :must-contain key. load-preset-handler must default to {} when key is missing. Use (get config :must-contain {}).

**Edge Case: Global Cap Interaction with Increment**
When total must-contain is 6 and player clicks a card already at count 1, increment to 2 would bring total to 7 (allowed). But if total is 7, clicking any card should cycle it to 0 (remove), not try to add. The toggle logic must check global cap BEFORE incrementing.

**Edge Case: Deck Change Resets Must-Contain**
When select-deck-handler changes the deck (e.g., from iggy-pop to another), must-contain should reset to {} since card availability changes entirely. Currently only iggy-pop exists, but design for correctness.

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO storing must-contain as a set (must be map of card-id -> count to support multi-copy sculpting)
- NO separate must-contain validation pass (inline validation in toggle handler — keep it simple)
- NO must-contain state outside app-db (no atoms, no separate storage — lives in re-frame db)
- NO breaking existing preset save/load format (additive only — old presets must still load)
close_reason: Closed

title: Add events and subscriptions for fork/replay UI components
design:
## Goal
Add re-frame events and subscriptions that wire the new data model functions to the UI layer. Events handle user actions (jump-to, create/rename/delete fork). Subscriptions derive turn-grouped entries and hierarchical fork tree for the view components.

## Context
Completed fizzle-4r1d: Four pure functions now exist in history/core.cljs:
- rename-fork, delete-fork, entries-by-turn, fork-tree
- All tested and working (21 tests)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- history/events.cljs — existing events: ::step-back, ::step-forward, ::switch-branch
- subs/history.cljs — existing subs: ::entries, ::position, ::current-branch, ::forks, ::can-step-back?, ::can-step-forward?, ::at-tip?, ::entry-count
- history/core.cljs:67-75 — step-to (used by ::jump-to)
- history/core.cljs:105-115 — create-named-fork (used by ::create-fork)
- history/core.cljs:130-134 — rename-fork (used by ::rename-fork)
- history/core.cljs:138-167 — delete-fork (used by ::delete-fork)
- history/core.cljs:175-183 — fork-tree (used by ::fork-tree sub)
- history/core.cljs:186-191 — entries-by-turn (used by ::entries-by-turn sub)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**Event tests (in history/events_test.cljs):**
- test-jump-to-event: dispatching ::jump-to with position updates position and game/db
- test-jump-to-event-out-of-bounds: out-of-bounds position leaves db unchanged
- test-create-fork-event: dispatching ::create-fork adds fork to :history/forks
- test-rename-fork-event: dispatching ::rename-fork updates fork name
- test-delete-fork-event: dispatching ::delete-fork removes fork from map
- test-delete-fork-event-cascade: deleting parent fork cascades to children

**Subscription tests (in subs/history_test.cljs):**
- test-entries-by-turn-sub: subscribe to ::entries-by-turn returns grouped structure
- test-fork-tree-sub: subscribe to ::fork-tree returns hierarchical tree

### 3. Implementation checklist

**Events (history/events.cljs):**
- [ ] ::jump-to [db [_ position]] — calls history/step-to
- [ ] ::create-fork [db [_ name]] — calls history/create-named-fork
- [ ] ::rename-fork [db [_ fork-id new-name]] — calls history/rename-fork
- [ ] ::delete-fork [db [_ fork-id]] — calls history/delete-fork

**Subscriptions (subs/history.cljs):**
- [ ] ::entries-by-turn — Layer 3 sub, derives from ::entries, calls history/entries-by-turn
- [ ] ::fork-tree — Layer 3 sub, derives from ::forks (the raw forks map from db), calls history/fork-tree

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 4 events work correctly (6 event tests)
- [ ] Both subscriptions return correct derived data (2 sub tests)
- [ ] Events delegate to history/core.cljs functions (no logic duplication)
- [ ] Subscriptions are Layer 3 (derived from existing Layer 2 subs)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations

**::forks subscription shape**: The existing ::forks sub returns `(vec (vals (:history/forks db)))` — a vector of fork maps. But fork-tree needs the forks MAP (fork-id → fork). May need a new Layer 2 sub `::forks-map` that returns `:history/forks` directly, or adjust fork-tree to accept a vector. Check existing sub shape before implementing.

**create-named-fork does NOT switch branch**: Per existing behavior (core.cljs:105-115), create-named-fork creates the fork but stays on current branch. The UI may want to switch after creation — handle that in the view layer (dispatch switch-branch after create), not in the event.

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO game logic in events (delegate to history/core.cljs functions)
- ❌ NO direct db manipulation — events call pure functions
- ❌ NO subscription that queries app-db directly for derived data (use Layer 2 → Layer 3 pattern)
close_reason: All 4 events (jump-to, create-fork, rename-fork, delete-fork) and 2 subscriptions (entries-by-turn, fork-tree) implemented. 8 new tests, all passing. make validate clean.

title: Create events/opening_hand.cljs with mulligan, keep, and bottom-card events
design:
## Goal
Create the events/opening_hand.cljs namespace with pure handler functions for:
1. Mulligan: return hand to library, shuffle, re-deal 7 (sculpted cards guaranteed), increment mulligan-count
2. Keep with 0 mulligans: transition to :game, clear opening-hand keys
3. Keep with N>0 mulligans: switch opening-hand/phase to :bottoming
4. Toggle bottom-card selection: add/remove card from bottom-selection set
5. Confirm bottom selection: move selected cards to bottom of library, transition to :game, clear opening-hand keys

## Context
Completed fizzle-f935: init-game-state now returns :opening-hand with mulligan-count 0, sculpted-ids set, phase :viewing. The game/db is live with hand and library objects. This namespace operates on the app-db (which contains :game/db, :opening-hand/* keys).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (5 handlers + tests + registration)

## Architecture Constraints
- Anti-pattern: NO opening hand logic in events/game.cljs or events/setup.cljs
- Anti-pattern: NO history entries for mulligans (history starts at :game transition)
- Anti-pattern: NO auto-keeping (player must explicitly keep)
- Anti-pattern: NO mulligan without bottom selection when mulligan-count > 0

## State Keys Used
- :game/db — Datascript db with hand/library objects
- :opening-hand/mulligan-count — integer, incremented on each mulligan
- :opening-hand/sculpted-ids — set of UUIDs for sculpted objects (regenerated on each mulligan)
- :opening-hand/must-contain — map of {card-id count} (REQUIRED: stored in app-db for mulligan re-sculpting)
- :opening-hand/phase — :viewing or :bottoming
- :opening-hand/bottom-selection — set of object-ids selected for bottoming (new key, only during :bottoming)
- :active-screen — :opening-hand during this phase, :game after keep+bottom

## PREREQUISITE: Store :opening-hand/must-contain in init-game-state output
The mulligan handler needs the must-contain config ({card-id count}) to re-sculpt hands. Currently init-game-state only stores :opening-hand/sculpted-ids (UUIDs). Add :opening-hand/must-contain to the init-game-state output map.

Modify events/game.cljs init-game-state to add:
  :opening-hand/must-contain (or must-contain {})
to the merge map alongside :opening-hand/mulligan-count, :opening-hand/sculpted-ids, :opening-hand/phase.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add :opening-hand/must-contain to init-game-state (prerequisite)
- Edit events/game.cljs: add :opening-hand/must-contain to the merge map in init-game-state
- Add test in init_game_test.cljs: verify :opening-hand/must-contain matches input config
- Add test: verify empty must-contain stored as {}
- Run tests RED then GREEN

### Step 2: Write tests for mulligan-handler (TDD RED)
File: src/test/fizzle/events/opening_hand_test.cljs

Test helper: create-opening-hand-app-db that calls game/init-game-state with configurable must-contain.

Tests (each catches a specific bug):
- test-mulligan-returns-seven-cards: After mulligan, hand has exactly 7 cards. Catches: hand size mismatch after re-deal.
- test-mulligan-preserves-library-count: Library count unchanged after mulligan (cards returned then re-drawn). Catches: card duplication or loss.
- test-mulligan-increments-count: mulligan-count goes from 0 to 1 to 2. Catches: counter not incrementing.
- test-mulligan-preserves-sculpted-card-ids: After mulligan with must-contain {:dark-ritual 2}, hand contains exactly 2 dark-rituals (by card-id). Catches: sculpting not re-applied.
- test-mulligan-generates-new-sculpted-uuids: After mulligan, sculpted-ids are different UUIDs than before (old objects gone, new ones created). Catches: stale UUID tracking.
- test-mulligan-with-empty-must-contain: Mulligan with no sculpting still returns 7 random cards. Catches: nil handling.
- test-mulligan-with-all-seven-sculpted: must-contain totaling 7 — mulligan returns same 7 card-ids. Catches: zero-random-draw edge case.
- test-mulligan-stays-on-opening-hand: :active-screen remains :opening-hand, :opening-hand/phase stays :viewing. Catches: premature screen transition.

### Step 3: Implement mulligan-handler (TDD GREEN)
File: src/main/fizzle/events/opening_hand.cljs

Algorithm:
1. Get all hand objects from game-db via queries/get-objects-in-zone
2. Move each hand object to :library via zones/move-to-zone (loop/reduce)
3. Shuffle library via zones/shuffle-library
4. Read :opening-hand/must-contain from app-db
5. Get all library objects, extract card-ids
6. Use sculpting algorithm: for each {card-id count} in must-contain, find library objects with that card-id, take min(count, available)
7. Take (7 - sculpted-count) random library objects for remaining hand slots
8. Move selected objects from :library to :hand via zones/move-to-zone
9. Update app-db: increment :opening-hand/mulligan-count, set :opening-hand/sculpted-ids to new set of sculpted object UUIDs

Key difference from init-game-state: mulligan works on existing Datascript objects (move between zones), not creating new objects.

### Step 4: Write tests for keep-handler (TDD RED)
- test-keep-zero-mulligans-transitions-to-game: :active-screen becomes :game, :opening-hand/* keys dissoc'd. Catches: screen not transitioning.
- test-keep-zero-mulligans-preserves-game-db: :game/db unchanged. Catches: accidental db mutation.
- test-keep-with-mulligans-enters-bottoming: mulligan-count 2 → :opening-hand/phase :bottoming, :opening-hand/bottom-selection #{}. Catches: skip to :game without bottom selection.
- test-keep-preserves-hand: hand cards unchanged during keep. Catches: card loss.
- test-keep-clears-opening-hand-keys: After keep with 0 mulligans, :opening-hand/mulligan-count, :opening-hand/sculpted-ids, :opening-hand/phase, :opening-hand/must-contain, :opening-hand/bottom-selection all nil. Catches: key leakage.

### Step 5: Implement keep-handler (TDD GREEN)
- If mulligan-count is 0: dissoc all :opening-hand/* keys, set :active-screen :game
- If mulligan-count > 0: set :opening-hand/phase :bottoming, assoc :opening-hand/bottom-selection #{}

### Step 6: Write tests for toggle-bottom-selection (TDD RED)
- test-toggle-adds-to-selection: Adds object-id to :opening-hand/bottom-selection. Catches: selection not updating.
- test-toggle-removes-if-selected: Toggling already-selected id removes it. Catches: toggle not working.
- test-toggle-noop-when-not-bottoming: If :opening-hand/phase is :viewing, toggle is no-op. Catches: selection in wrong phase.

### Step 7: Implement toggle-bottom-selection (TDD GREEN)
- Check phase is :bottoming, otherwise no-op
- If object-id in bottom-selection: disj; else: conj

### Step 8: Write tests for confirm-bottom-handler (TDD RED)
- test-confirm-bottom-moves-cards: Selected cards move from hand to library. Hand count drops by N, library grows by N. Catches: cards not moving.
- test-confirm-bottom-noop-wrong-count: If selected count != mulligan-count, no-op. Catches: allowing invalid selection.
- test-confirm-bottom-transitions-to-game: :active-screen becomes :game. Catches: stuck on opening-hand.
- test-confirm-bottom-clears-keys: All :opening-hand/* keys dissoc'd. Catches: key leakage.
- test-confirm-bottom-specific-cards: Verify the specific selected cards are in library and NOT in hand. Catches: wrong cards moved.

### Step 9: Implement confirm-bottom-handler (TDD GREEN)
- Validate count(bottom-selection) == mulligan-count, else no-op
- Move each selected object to :library via zones/move-to-zone
- Dissoc all :opening-hand/* keys
- Set :active-screen :game

### Step 10: Register re-frame events and core.cljs require
- Register all 4 events: ::mulligan, ::keep, ::toggle-bottom, ::confirm-bottom
- Add bare require [fizzle.events.opening-hand] in core.cljs
- Run make validate

## History Safety (Verified)
The history interceptor (history/interceptor.cljs) only records events in the priority-events set. Opening-hand events (namespace fizzle.events.opening-hand) are NOT in that set. Therefore:
- Mulligan events will NOT create history entries (correct)
- Keep/bottom events will NOT create history entries (correct)
- History arrays stay empty until first game action after :game transition
- No explicit history code needed in this namespace

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Mulligan Sculpting on Existing Objects**
Unlike init-game-state which creates new objects, mulligan operates on existing Datascript entities. Must find library objects by card-id, not create new ones. Algorithm:
1. Query library objects: (queries/get-objects-in-zone db :player-1 :library)
2. Group by card-id: (group-by #(get-in % [:object/card :card/id]) lib-objects)
3. For each {card-id count} in must-contain, take N objects from that group
4. Remaining hand slots filled from remaining library objects (random selection)
5. Move selected objects hand→library via zones/move-to-zone

**Edge Case: Mulligan with Empty Must-Contain**
sculpted-count = 0, all 7 are random. :opening-hand/sculpted-ids = #{}.

**Edge Case: Mulligan with All 7 Sculpted**
random-draw-count = 0. (take 0 ...) returns empty, no random draws. Verify this path works.

**Edge Case: Must-Contain Exceeds Available After Shuffle**
Defensive: take min(requested, available). Setup toggle already validates, but after sideboarding edge cases are possible.

**Edge Case: Multiple Sequential Mulligans**
Must work for mulligan 1, 2, 3+ in sequence. Each time: return hand, shuffle entire library, re-sculpt, re-draw 7. mulligan-count increments correctly each time.

**Edge Case: Bottom Selection with Sculpted Cards**
Player CAN select sculpted cards for bottoming (it's their choice). The view will mark sculpted cards to discourage it, but the handler must allow it. Don't filter sculpted cards from bottom selection.

**Edge Case: Confirm Bottom with Exact Count**
Must validate count(bottom-selection) == mulligan-count. If player selected 1 card but mulligan-count is 2, no-op. This prevents partial bottoming.

**Cleanup Completeness**
Both keep (0 mulligans) and confirm-bottom must dissoc ALL opening-hand keys:
- :opening-hand/mulligan-count
- :opening-hand/sculpted-ids
- :opening-hand/must-contain
- :opening-hand/phase
- :opening-hand/bottom-selection

## Success Criteria
- [ ] init-game-state stores :opening-hand/must-contain in app-db output
- [ ] mulligan-handler: hand has 7 cards after mulligan, library count unchanged
- [ ] mulligan-handler: mulligan-count increments correctly (0→1→2→...)
- [ ] mulligan-handler: sculpted cards preserved in hand (verified by card-id)
- [ ] mulligan-handler: new sculpted-ids generated (old UUIDs gone)
- [ ] mulligan-handler: works with empty must-contain (7 random cards)
- [ ] mulligan-handler: works with all-7-sculpted must-contain
- [ ] keep-handler: 0 mulligans → :active-screen :game, opening-hand keys cleared
- [ ] keep-handler: N>0 mulligans → :opening-hand/phase :bottoming
- [ ] toggle-bottom-selection: adds/removes from set, no-op if not bottoming
- [ ] confirm-bottom: moves exactly N cards, no-op if count wrong
- [ ] confirm-bottom: transitions to :game, clears opening-hand keys
- [ ] history arrays remain empty during entire opening-hand phase
- [ ] re-frame events registered, core.cljs has bare require
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] make validate passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO calling history functions during mulligan/keep (interceptor already excludes these events)
- NO modifying game.cljs or setup.cljs for opening hand logic (separate namespace)
- NO allowing keep without bottom selection when mulligan-count > 0
- NO filtering sculpted cards from bottom selection (player's choice)
- NO creating new Datascript objects during mulligan (reuse existing by moving zones)
- NO using effects/execute-effect :draw for mulligan re-deal (build hand directly from zone moves)
close_reason: All 4 handlers implemented with TDD (21 tests). make validate passes: 1094 tests, 0 failures.

title: Task 1: Extend tutor selection for multi-card select
design:
## Goal
Extend the existing tutor selection system to support selecting multiple cards (up to N) instead of just 1. This is the foundational change needed for Intuition.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing tutor selection
- events/game.cljs:911-930 - build-tutor-selection (current single-select, uses :selection/count 1)
- events/game.cljs:1385-1428 - execute-tutor-selection (moves card via `(first selected)`, shuffles)
- events/game.cljs:1431+ - confirm-tutor-selection event handler
- events/game.cljs:951-960 - build-discard-selection (example of multi-count selection)

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)
Create src/test/fizzle/events/tutor_multi_select_test.cljs:

```clojure
;; Test: Multi-select tutor selects exactly 3 from large library
;; Setup: Library with 10 cards, tutor effect with :effect/select-count 3
;; Action: Build selection, select 3 cards, confirm
;; Assert: All 3 cards moved to target zone, library shuffled, 7 cards remain

;; Test: Multi-select tutor with exact library size
;; Setup: Library with 2 cards, tutor effect with :effect/select-count 3
;; Action: Build selection
;; Assert: :selection/select-count is 2 (min of effect count and library size)
;; Assert: Selection requires exactly 2 (cannot confirm with 1)

;; Test: Multi-select tutor with smaller library
;; Setup: Library with 1 card, tutor effect with :effect/select-count 3
;; Action: Build selection, select 1 card, confirm
;; Assert: 1 card moved to target zone, library shuffled

;; Test: Multi-select tutor fail-to-find (empty library)
;; Setup: Empty library, tutor effect with :effect/select-count 3
;; Action: Build selection
;; Assert: :selection/allow-fail-to-find true, can confirm with 0 selected
;; Assert: Library shuffled, no cards moved

;; Test: Backwards compat - existing single-select tutors work
;; Setup: Library with 5 cards, tutor effect WITHOUT :effect/select-count
;; Action: Build selection
;; Assert: :selection/select-count defaults to 1
;; Assert: Can select exactly 1 card, confirm moves 1 card
```

### 3. Implementation checklist
- [ ] events/game.cljs:911-930 - Modify build-tutor-selection:
      - Add parameter extraction: `(or (:effect/select-count tutor-effect) 1)`
      - Compute actual count: `(min select-count (count candidate-ids))`
      - Add to selection state: `:selection/select-count actual-count`
      - Add to selection state: `:selection/exact? true` (must select exactly this many)
- [ ] events/game.cljs:1385-1428 - Modify execute-tutor-selection:
      - Change `(first selected)` to iterate over all selected cards
      - Move each card to target-zone in sequence
      - Shuffle once at end (not per card)
- [ ] events/game.cljs:1431+ - Verify confirm-tutor-selection handles set correctly (already uses set)
- [ ] Backwards compat: `(or (:effect/select-count tutor-effect) 1)` ensures default is 1

### 4. Verification
- Run: make test (all tests pass)
- Run: make validate (lint, format, tests)
- Manual test: Cast Merchant Scroll, verify still selects 1 card

## Success Criteria
- [ ] build-tutor-selection extracts :effect/select-count (default 1)
- [ ] :selection/select-count = min(effect-count, library-size)
- [ ] :selection/exact? = true in selection state
- [ ] execute-tutor-selection iterates over all selected cards (not just first)
- [ ] All selected cards moved to target-zone before shuffle
- [ ] Shuffle happens once after all moves (not per card)
- [ ] Existing tutors (no :select-count) default to 1 card
- [ ] 5+ unit tests pass with specific assertions
- [ ] make validate passes (lint, format, tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: select-count <= 0**
- If :effect/select-count is 0 or negative, treat as 1 (defensive)
- Use: `(max 1 (or (:effect/select-count tutor-effect) 1))`

**Edge Case: Empty candidates but select-count > 0**
- If no matching cards in library, :selection/select-count = 0
- :selection/allow-fail-to-find = true allows confirming with 0 selected
- This is existing behavior, preserve it

**Edge Case: Selection cancelled**
- User closes modal without confirming
- Existing behavior: selection state cleared, spell remains on stack
- No change needed, but verify tests cover this

**Performance: Multiple card moves**
- Moving 3 cards sequentially is fine (not hot path)
- Shuffle once at end, not per move

**Reference Implementation**
- build-discard-selection (line 951-960) uses :selection/count for multi-select
- Follow same pattern for consistency

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO shuffling between card moves (shuffle once at end)
- ❌ NO changing fail-to-find behavior (must always be allowed per existing anti-pattern)
- ❌ NO breaking single-select default (backwards compat required)
- ❌ NO using (first selected) when multiple cards possible (iterate over all)
close_reason: Multi-select tutor selection implemented with TDD. All tests passing, make validate passes.

title: Feature: Discard to hand size at cleanup
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- At cleanup step, if active player's hand exceeds max hand size, they must discard down to max
- Player chooses which cards to discard via interactive selection UI
- Discard happens BEFORE 'until end of turn' effects end (MTG Rule 514.1 before 514.2)
- Max hand size is stored as :player/max-hand-size in schema (default 7)
- Cleanup step repeats if triggered abilities fire during it (MTG Rule 514.3)
- Cannot start new turn while cleanup discard is pending

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Hand size check triggers automatically when phase advances to cleanup
- [ ] Selection UI appears showing hand cards when hand > max-hand-size
- [ ] Player must select exactly (hand-count - max-hand-size) cards to discard
- [ ] Selected cards moved to graveyard on confirm
- [ ] Grant expiration (expire-grants) occurs AFTER discard, not before
- [ ] If triggered abilities fire during cleanup, cleanup cycle restarts after they resolve
- [ ] New Turn button disabled/unavailable while cleanup discard selection is pending
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] New tests cover: no-discard path, discard path, cleanup repeat

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoding 7 as hand size limit (must read from :player/max-hand-size schema field - future cards modify this)
- ❌ NO auto-discarding or random discard (player MUST choose which cards to discard)
- ❌ NO expiring grants before discard completes (MTG Rule 514: discard is 514.1, expire is 514.2)
- ❌ NO skipping cleanup repeat check after grant expiration (must check for triggered abilities)
- ❌ NO adding test-only methods to production code (test via public API)

## Approach
Handle cleanup as a multi-step process at the re-frame event handler level, building on the existing selection/confirm pattern. Remove the auto-firing :cleanup-expire-grants turn-based trigger and replace it with explicit cleanup flow in the ::advance-phase, ::confirm-selection, and new ::continue-cleanup event handlers.

The key insight is that the selection UI pattern (game/pending-selection) already handles pausing game flow for player input. We reuse this with a new :cleanup-discard effect type. Grant expiration is called explicitly after discard completes rather than auto-firing via trigger dispatch.

## Architecture
### Schema Changes
- Add :player/max-hand-size to schema.cljs
- Initialize to 7 in init-game-state for player-1

### Query Changes  
- Add get-max-hand-size query to queries.cljs

### Turn-Based Trigger Changes
- Remove :cleanup-expire-grants from turn_based.cljs register-turn-based-actions!

### Event Handler Changes
- Modify ::advance-phase: detect cleanup phase, run begin-cleanup logic
- Add :cleanup-discard case to ::confirm-selection handler
- Add ::continue-cleanup event for cleanup repeat cycle
- Modify ::resolve-top: when stack empties during cleanup, dispatch ::continue-cleanup

### Cleanup Flow
1. Phase advances to :cleanup via advance-phase (pure fn, dispatches phase-entered)
2. Re-frame handler checks hand size vs max-hand-size
3. If hand > max: set game/pending-selection with :cleanup-discard type
4. If hand <= max: call grants/expire-grants directly, check for triggers
5. After discard confirmed: expire grants, check for triggers
6. If triggers on stack: player resolves them, then ::continue-cleanup restarts cycle
7. When no discard needed AND no triggers: cleanup complete, New Turn available

## Design Rationale
### Problem
MTG Rule 514 requires discarding to hand size during cleanup. Currently the cleanup step only expires 'until end of turn' grants. Players can accumulate cards beyond 7 without penalty, which is incorrect.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- events/game.cljs:367-386 - advance-phase pure function, dispatches phase-entered
- events/game.cljs:434-438 - ::advance-phase re-frame handler bridges pure fn to app-db
- engine/turn_based.cljs:42-50 - :cleanup-expire-grants fires on :phase-entered :cleanup
- events/game.cljs:1231-1240 - build-discard-selection pattern for interactive discard
- events/game.cljs:1680-1725 - ::confirm-selection handles :discard effect type
- db/schema.cljs:47-55 - Player schema, no max-hand-size field exists
- core.cljs:346-381 - Turn bar UI, cleanup shows New Turn button

**MTG Rules:**
- Rule 514.1: Active player discards to max hand size (first action in cleanup)
- Rule 514.2: Damage removed, 'until end of turn' effects end (second, simultaneous)
- Rule 514.3: If state-based actions or triggers exist, active player gets priority, then new cleanup step begins
- Default max hand size: 7

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Handler-level cleanup flow ✓
**What it is:** Handle cleanup as multi-step process in re-frame handlers. Remove auto-expire-grants trigger, explicitly manage cleanup ordering.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed existing selection pattern (build-discard-selection + confirm-selection)
- Confirmed pending-selection blocks other game actions naturally
- Verified grant expiration can be called as pure function outside trigger system

**Pros:**
- Follows existing selection/confirm pattern
- Minimal new concepts
- No new schema patterns (reuses pending-selection)

**Cons:**
- Cleanup logic across multiple handlers

**Chosen because:** Consistent with existing codebase patterns, minimal architectural change

#### 2. Cleanup state machine in game-db ❌
**What it is:** Add :game/cleanup-step field tracking progress through discrete states (:discard → :expire → :check-triggers → :complete).

**Why we looked at this:** State machines naturally handle multi-step flows with repeat cycles

**Pros:**
- Explicit state easy to reason about
- Natural cleanup repeat support

**Cons:**
- Introduces state machine pattern not used elsewhere in codebase
- More schema changes
- Cleanup step value would need to be managed/reset

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Introduces new pattern (state machines) when existing selection/confirm pattern handles the same flow

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Multiple other game phases need similar multi-step interactive flows

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Hand size check at cleanup
- Interactive discard selection
- Correct ordering (discard before expire)
- Cleanup repeat on triggered abilities
- Schema field for max hand size

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Cards that modify max hand size (future card implementations)
- Opponent hand size check (opponent is bot, not relevant for practice tool)
- Madness mechanic (discard trigger - separate card implementation)

### Open Questions
- Should hand count display show max hand size indicator? (decide during implementation)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Variable max hand size or hardcode 7? | Schema field, default 7 | Add :player/max-hand-size to schema, anti-pattern: no hardcoding |
| Cleanup repeat support? | Yes, support repeat | Need ::continue-cleanup event, hook into stack empty during cleanup |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Cleanup Step Rules (MTG Rule 514)
**Question explored:** What is the correct order of operations during cleanup?
**Sources consulted:**
- MTG Comprehensive Rules 514.1-514.3
- MTG Wiki ending phase article

**Findings:**
- 514.1: Discard to hand size FIRST
- 514.2: Remove damage, end 'until end of turn' effects SECOND
- 514.3: Check for triggers, if any exist grant priority and restart cleanup
- No priority normally during cleanup (simplified for practice tool)

**Conclusion:** Must enforce discard-before-expire ordering, support cleanup restart

#### Existing Selection Pattern
**Question explored:** Can we reuse the existing discard selection UI?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/game.cljs build-discard-selection (line 1231)
- events/game.cljs confirm-selection handler (line 1680)
- core.cljs card-selection-modal

**Findings:**
- Selection system fully supports player-choice discard
- :selection/effect-type dispatches behavior in confirm-selection
- Adding new effect type (:cleanup-discard) follows established pattern

**Conclusion:** Reuse selection pattern with new :cleanup-discard effect type

### Dead-End Paths
None - both approaches are viable, chose simpler one

### Open Concerns Raised
- Cleanup repeat with Madness cards → Deferred to card implementation, not cleanup infrastructure
close_reason: All child tasks complete. 8/9 success criteria fully met, 1 partially met (New Turn button functionally blocked but lacks visual disabled state for pending-selection - minor UX polish). 741 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Add effect condition system and sacrifice effect type
design:
## Goal
Add infrastructure for conditional effects and the sacrifice effect type. This enables effects to have an optional :effect/condition that gates execution.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code
- engine/effects.cljs:13-31 - Current execute-effect multimethod pattern, dispatches on :effect/type
- engine/effects.cljs:162-188 - Example effect implementation (:add-counters) showing target resolution pattern
- events/game.cljs:385-388 - How :self is resolved to object-id before calling execute-effect
- db/queries.cljs - Query patterns for object data

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

**File: src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs**

Add new test namespace section for conditional effects:

- test-effect-without-condition-executes-normally
  - Setup: Object with counters, effect with no :effect/condition
  - Execute: Run effect (e.g., :add-mana)
  - Assert: Effect executed (mana added)
  - Bug caught: Regression if condition check breaks normal effects

- test-no-counters-condition-skips-when-counters-positive
  - Setup: Object with {:mining 2} counters
  - Execute: {:effect/type :sacrifice :effect/target obj-id :effect/condition {:condition/type :no-counters :condition/counter-type :mining}}
  - Assert: Object still on battlefield
  - Bug caught: Premature sacrifice when counters remain

- test-no-counters-condition-executes-when-counters-zero
  - Setup: Object with {:mining 0} counters
  - Execute: Same effect as above
  - Assert: Object moved to graveyard
  - Bug caught: Failure to sacrifice when counters depleted

- test-no-counters-condition-executes-when-counter-type-missing
  - Setup: Object with {} (no counters map) or {:charge 1} (different type)
  - Execute: Same effect checking :mining
  - Assert: Object moved to graveyard (nil/missing counts as zero)
  - Bug caught: NPE or incorrect handling of missing counter type

- test-sacrifice-effect-moves-to-graveyard
  - Setup: Object on battlefield
  - Execute: {:effect/type :sacrifice :effect/target obj-id}
  - Assert: Object zone is :graveyard
  - Bug caught: Wrong zone transition

- test-sacrifice-effect-invalid-target-is-noop
  - Setup: No object with given ID
  - Execute: {:effect/type :sacrifice :effect/target (random-uuid)}
  - Assert: db unchanged, no exception
  - Bug caught: NPE on invalid target

- test-unknown-condition-type-skips-effect
  - Setup: Object on battlefield
  - Execute: {:effect/type :sacrifice :effect/target obj-id :effect/condition {:condition/type :unknown-type}}
  - Assert: Object still on battlefield (fail-safe: unknown condition = don't execute)
  - Bug caught: Executing effects with unhandled conditions

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] effects.cljs - Add check-condition multimethod
  ```clojure
  (defmulti check-condition
    "Check if an effect's condition is met. Dispatches on :condition/type.
     Returns true if condition met, false otherwise.
     Unknown condition types return false (fail-safe: don't execute)."
    (fn [_db _player-id _effect condition] (:condition/type condition)))
  
  (defmethod check-condition :default [_db _player-id _effect _condition] false)
  ```

- [ ] effects.cljs - Add :no-counters condition method
  ```clojure
  (defmethod check-condition :no-counters
    [db _player-id effect condition]
    (let [target-id (:effect/target effect)
          counter-type (:condition/counter-type condition)]
      (if-let [obj-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]] db target-id)]
        (let [counters (or (d/q '[:find ?c . :in $ ?e :where [?e :object/counters ?c]] db obj-eid) {})
              count-value (get counters counter-type 0)]
          (<= count-value 0))
        ;; Object doesn't exist - condition met (vacuously true, effect will be no-op anyway)
        true)))
  ```

- [ ] effects.cljs - Create execute-effect-impl as renamed current multimethod
  - Rename current execute-effect multimethod to execute-effect-impl
  - Keep all existing defmethods unchanged

- [ ] effects.cljs - Create wrapper execute-effect function
  ```clojure
  (defn execute-effect
    "Execute an effect, checking condition first if present.
     If :effect/condition exists and is not met, returns db unchanged.
     If no condition or condition met, dispatches to execute-effect-impl."
    [db player-id effect]
    (if-let [condition (:effect/condition effect)]
      (if (check-condition db player-id effect condition)
        (execute-effect-impl db player-id effect)
        db)
      (execute-effect-impl db player-id effect)))
  ```

- [ ] effects.cljs - Add :sacrifice effect to execute-effect-impl
  ```clojure
  (defmethod execute-effect-impl :sacrifice
    "Sacrifice a permanent - move it to the graveyard.
     Effect keys:
       :effect/target - Object ID (caller must resolve :self before calling)
     Handles edge cases:
       - Invalid target: no-op (returns db unchanged)"
    [db _player-id effect]
    (let [target-id (:effect/target effect)]
      (if (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]] db target-id)
        (zones/move-to-zone db target-id :graveyard)
        db)))
  ```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] check-condition multimethod exists and dispatches on :condition/type
- [ ] :no-counters condition returns true when counter type is 0, nil, or missing
- [ ] :no-counters condition returns false when counter type > 0
- [ ] Unknown condition types return false (fail-safe)
- [ ] Effects without conditions execute normally (no regression)
- [ ] Effects with conditions only execute when condition met
- [ ] :sacrifice effect moves target to graveyard
- [ ] :sacrifice effect is no-op for invalid target
- [ ] 7+ unit tests pass covering all scenarios above
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (lint, format, test)

## Key Considerations (ADDED BY SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: :self Resolution**
- :self MUST be resolved to object-id by caller before execute-effect is called
- Pattern already exists in events/game.cljs:385-388 (play-land ETB effects)
- Condition uses :effect/target, so resolving target also resolves for condition
- Document this requirement in execute-effect docstring

**Edge Case: Missing Counter Type**
- Object may have no :object/counters (nil)
- Object may have counters but not the requested type
- Both cases should be treated as "count is zero" (condition met)
- Use (get counters counter-type 0) with fallback

**Edge Case: Invalid Target**
- Object may not exist (moved to graveyard between cost payment and effect)
- :sacrifice should be no-op for invalid target (matches existing patterns)
- :no-counters condition on invalid target: return true (vacuously true, effect will no-op anyway)

**Edge Case: Unknown Condition Type**
- Multimethod :default should return false (fail-safe)
- If we can't evaluate condition, don't execute effect
- Log warning in future (not blocking for this task)

**Reference Implementation**
- Study engine/effects.cljs:162-188 (:add-counters) for target lookup pattern
- Study events/game.cljs:383-391 for :self resolution pattern

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO checking condition AFTER effect execution (must be before)
- ❌ NO panic/throw on unknown condition type (return false, fail-safe)
- ❌ NO assuming :effect/target is always valid UUID (may be invalid)
- ❌ NO modifying existing execute-effect behavior for effects without conditions
close_reason: Implemented effect condition system with check-condition multimethod, :no-counters condition type, and :sacrifice effect. All tests passing.

title: Task 4: Add scry modal UI component
design:
## Goal
Create the scry modal UI component that displays revealed cards and allows player to assign them to top or bottom piles. This connects the selection state (created by Task 2) to the confirmation handler (Task 3).

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-xzc: confirm-scry-selection handler exists
Selection state structure (from build-scry-selection):
- :selection/type :scry
- :selection/cards [obj-id-1 obj-id-2 ...]
- :selection/top-pile []
- :selection/bottom-pile []
- :selection/player-id
- :selection/spell-id
- :selection/remaining-effects

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing selection UI patterns
Reference implementations (ALL IN core.cljs):
- src/main/fizzle/core.cljs:501-603 - card-selection-modal (discard/tutor)
- src/main/fizzle/core.cljs:606-617 - selection-modal (dispatcher)
- src/main/fizzle/core.cljs:413-440 - selection-card-view (card display)
- Pattern: Modal displays cards, tracks selection state, dispatches confirm event

### 2. Add scry-modal component in core.cljs

Location: After card-selection-modal (~line 603), before selection-modal

```clojure
(defn- scry-card-view
  "A card in the scry selection modal, showing pile assignment."
  [obj pile]
  (let [card (:object/card obj)
        card-name (or (:card/name card) "Unknown")]
    [:div {:style {:width "100px"
                   :padding "8px"
                   :border-radius "6px"
                   :background (case pile
                                 :top "#2a4a2a"      ; green tint for top
                                 :bottom "#4a2a2a"   ; red tint for bottom
                                 "#1a2a3a")          ; neutral for unassigned
                   :border "1px solid #4A9BD9"
                   :text-align "center"
                   :font-size "11px"
                   :color "#eee"
                   :cursor "pointer"}}
     card-name]))

(defn- scry-modal
  "Modal for scry selection - assign cards to top or bottom piles."
  [selection]
  (let [scry-cards (:selection/cards selection)
        top-pile (:selection/top-pile selection)
        bottom-pile (:selection/bottom-pile selection)
        assigned (into (set top-pile) bottom-pile)
        unassigned (remove assigned scry-cards)
        ;; Get actual card objects for display
        db @(rf/subscribe [::subs/game-db])
        get-obj (fn [oid] (q/get-object db oid))]
    [:div {:style {:position "fixed" :top 0 :left 0 :right 0 :bottom 0
                   :background "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.8)"
                   :display "flex" :align-items "center" :justify-content "center"
                   :z-index 1000}}
     [:div {:style {:background "#1a1a2a" :border "2px solid #4A9BD9"
                    :border-radius "12px" :padding "24px"
                    :max-width "600px" :width "90%"}}
      ;; Header
      [:h2 {:style {:color "#eee" :margin "0 0 16px 0"}}
       (str "Scry " (count scry-cards))]
      
      ;; Top Pile section
      [:div {:style {:margin-bottom "16px"}}
       [:h3 {:style {:color "#5CB85C" :font-size "14px" :margin "0 0 8px 0"}}
        "Top of Library (first)"]
       [:div {:style {:display "flex" :flex-wrap "wrap" :gap "8px" :min-height "40px"}}
        (for [oid top-pile]
          ^{:key oid}
          [:div {:on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/scry-unassign oid])}
           [scry-card-view (get-obj oid) :top]])]]
      
      ;; Unassigned cards section  
      [:div {:style {:margin-bottom "16px"}}
       [:h3 {:style {:color "#aaa" :font-size "14px" :margin "0 0 8px 0"}}
        "Revealed Cards"]
       [:div {:style {:display "flex" :flex-wrap "wrap" :gap "8px" :min-height "40px"}}
        (for [oid unassigned]
          ^{:key oid}
          [:div {:style {:display "flex" :gap "4px" :align-items "center"}}
           [scry-card-view (get-obj oid) nil]
           [:div {:style {:display "flex" :flex-direction "column" :gap "2px"}}
            [:button {:style {:padding "4px 8px" :background "#2a4a2a" :border "1px solid #5CB85C"
                              :border-radius "4px" :color "#eee" :cursor "pointer" :font-size "10px"}
                      :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/scry-assign-top oid])}
             "Top"]
            [:button {:style {:padding "4px 8px" :background "#4a2a2a" :border "1px solid #D9534F"
                              :border-radius "4px" :color "#eee" :cursor "pointer" :font-size "10px"}
                      :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/scry-assign-bottom oid])}
             "Bottom"]]])]]
      
      ;; Bottom Pile section
      [:div {:style {:margin-bottom "20px"}}
       [:h3 {:style {:color "#D9534F" :font-size "14px" :margin "0 0 8px 0"}}
        "Bottom of Library (last)"]
       [:div {:style {:display "flex" :flex-wrap "wrap" :gap "8px" :min-height "40px"}}
        (for [oid bottom-pile]
          ^{:key oid}
          [:div {:on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/scry-unassign oid])}
           [scry-card-view (get-obj oid) :bottom]])]]
      
      ;; Confirm button (always enabled per epic anti-pattern)
      [:button {:style {:width "100%" :padding "12px" :background "#4A9BD9"
                        :border "none" :border-radius "8px" :color "#fff"
                        :font-size "16px" :cursor "pointer"}
                :on-click #(rf/dispatch [::events/confirm-scry-selection])}
       "Confirm"]]]))
```

### 3. Add scry events in events/game.cljs

After confirm-scry-selection (~line 1260):

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::scry-assign-top
  (fn [db [_ obj-id]]
    (-> db
        (update-in [:game/pending-selection :selection/top-pile] conj obj-id)
        (update-in [:game/pending-selection :selection/bottom-pile] 
                   (fn [pile] (vec (remove #{obj-id} pile)))))))

(rf/reg-event-db
  ::scry-assign-bottom
  (fn [db [_ obj-id]]
    (-> db
        (update-in [:game/pending-selection :selection/bottom-pile] conj obj-id)
        (update-in [:game/pending-selection :selection/top-pile]
                   (fn [pile] (vec (remove #{obj-id} pile)))))))

(rf/reg-event-db
  ::scry-unassign
  (fn [db [_ obj-id]]
    (-> db
        (update-in [:game/pending-selection :selection/top-pile]
                   (fn [pile] (vec (remove #{obj-id} pile))))
        (update-in [:game/pending-selection :selection/bottom-pile]
                   (fn [pile] (vec (remove #{obj-id} pile)))))))
```

### 4. Wire scry-modal into selection-modal dispatcher

Update selection-modal (~line 606-617):

```clojure
(defn- selection-modal
  "Modal overlay for player selection.
   Shows when :game/pending-selection exists.
   Handles discard, tutor, scry, and player-target effects."
  []
  (let [selection @(rf/subscribe [::subs/pending-selection])
        cards @(rf/subscribe [::subs/selection-cards])]
    (when selection
      (let [selection-type (:selection/type selection)
            effect-type (:selection/effect-type selection)]
        (cond
          ;; Scry selection (has :selection/type :scry)
          (= selection-type :scry)
          [scry-modal selection]
          
          ;; Player target selection
          (= effect-type :player-target)
          [player-target-modal selection]
          
          ;; Card selection (discard, tutor)
          :else
          [card-selection-modal selection cards])))))
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] scry-modal function exists in core.cljs (~line 603-660)
- [ ] scry-card-view function exists for card display
- [ ] Events ::scry-assign-top, ::scry-assign-bottom, ::scry-unassign registered in game.cljs
- [ ] selection-modal dispatches to scry-modal when :selection/type is :scry
- [ ] Manual test: Cast Opt, scry modal appears showing 1 card
- [ ] Manual test: Click "Top" assigns card to top pile (green background)
- [ ] Manual test: Click "Bottom" assigns card to bottom pile (red background)
- [ ] Manual test: Click assigned card unassigns it back to revealed section
- [ ] Manual test: Click "Confirm" resolves scry and draws card
- [ ] Manual test: Scry 2 shows 2 cards, both can be assigned independently
- [ ] make validate passes

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty scry (0 cards)**
- build-scry-selection returns nil for empty library
- selection-modal won't render if selection is nil
- No special handling needed in UI

**Edge Case: Many cards (Scry N)**
- UI uses flex-wrap, cards will wrap to multiple rows
- Max reasonable N in Premodern is ~5 (Serum Visions, etc.)
- Layout handles up to 5+ cards

**Edge Case: Re-assigning cards**
- Assign to top, then click "Bottom" should move to bottom
- Events handle this by removing from opposite pile first

**Reference: Existing modal pattern**
- core.cljs:501-603 card-selection-modal
- Same overlay style, position fixed, z-index 1000
- Same button styling for consistency

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-assigning cards (player must choose)
- ❌ NO disabling confirm until all assigned (epic anti-pattern)
- ❌ NO hiding card names (need to see what we're scrying)
- ❌ NO blocking UI interaction while processing
- ❌ NO new view files (add to core.cljs like other modals)
close_reason: Closed
