title: Extract shared get-object-eid query helper
description:
## The Problem

This Datalog query appears dozens of times across the codebase:

```clojure
(d/q '[:find ?e .
       :in $ ?oid
       :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
     db object-id)
```

It's the UUID-to-entity-ID lookup for game objects. Every file in `engine/` that needs to write a Datascript transaction has its own inline copy.

### Current state

- `costs.cljs` already has a private `get-object-eid` helper
- `queries.cljs` has `get-object` which returns the full entity (pulls all attributes)
- Many call sites only need the eid for `[:db/add eid ...]` transactions
- The inline queries are identical copy-paste

### The Rich Hickey question

"Is this accidental complexity?" Yes. It's the same question asked the same way in dozens of places. A function eliminates the repetition and names the concept.

### Proposed direction

Add to `db/queries.cljs`:
```clojure
(defn get-object-eid
  "Get the Datascript entity ID for a game object by its UUID.
   Returns nil if object doesn't exist."
  [db object-id]
  (d/q '[:find ?e .
         :in $ ?oid  
         :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
       db object-id))
```

Then mechanically replace all inline copies. Consider the same for `get-player-eid` if it's similarly duplicated (it likely is — the player-id to eid lookup is the same pattern).

### Files involved

- `db/queries.cljs` — add the helper (already has `get-player-eid`)
- `engine/effects.cljs` — multiple inline copies
- `engine/costs.cljs` — has private copy, switch to shared
- `engine/zones.cljs` — inline copy in `move-to-zone`
- `engine/triggers.cljs` — inline copy in `create-spell-copy`
- `engine/grants.cljs` — likely has copies
- `events/selection.cljs` — `remove-spell-stack-item` has one

### Risks

- Essentially zero. Mechanical refactoring. Run `make validate` after.

title: Phase 4 Epic 2: Setup Screen + Deck/Sideboard
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Setup screen is the app landing page (game no longer auto-starts)
- Deck selection UI via dropdown (Iggy Pop as built-in default, architected for more)
- Side-by-side maindeck/sideboard panels with click-to-swap interaction
- Card list grouped by card type (Lands, Instants, Sorceries, Artifacts) showing Nx format
- Sideboard swaps validate 60 main / ≤15 side with inline error display
- Opponent configuration: goldfish clock turns (numeric, default 4)
- Named presets saved to localStorage for configuration management
- Quick Start button loads most-recently-used preset and starts game immediately
- Save As / Delete preset management via dropdown UI
- Confirmation dialog before leaving game to return to setup
- Start Game button dispatches init-game with full config payload
- init-game-state refactored to accept config map (no more hardcoded make-test-deck)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] App opens to setup screen, not directly to game
- [ ] Can select deck from dropdown and see its contents grouped by type
- [ ] Maindeck and sideboard displayed side-by-side (grid-cols-[2fr_1fr])
- [ ] Can click-to-swap cards between main and side panels
- [ ] Validation shows red when main ≠ 60 or side > 15
- [ ] Start Game disabled when deck invalid
- [ ] Clicking Start Game creates properly initialized game with chosen config
- [ ] Goldfish clock turns configurable (default 4)
- [ ] Can save current config as named preset
- [ ] Can load/delete saved presets
- [ ] Quick Start loads MRU preset and starts game immediately
- [ ] Settings persist across browser refreshes via localStorage
- [ ] Confirmation dialog when navigating from game back to setup
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (no regressions)
- [ ] make validate passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded deck in init-game-state (configurability: deck comes from setup config)
- ❌ NO game state in localStorage (complexity: only settings/presets, not live game state)
- ❌ NO blocking API calls (performance: all data is local, no network needed)
- ❌ NO Datascript schema changes for setup state (simplicity: setup config lives in app-db maps, not entities)
- ❌ NO drag-and-drop for sideboard (scope: click-to-swap is sufficient for v1)
- ❌ NO bot archetype selection (scope: deferred to Epic 5, only clock turns for now)
- ❌ NO deck editing beyond sideboard swaps (scope: card counts within a deck are fixed, only main↔side movement)

## Approach
Add :active-screen :setup as default. New events/setup.cljs namespace handles setup state management (deck selection, sideboard swaps, presets, clock config). New views/setup.cljs renders the setup screen with stacked sections: preset bar, deck dropdown, side-by-side main/side panels, opponent config, action buttons. New storage.cljs module handles localStorage EDN serialization for presets. Refactor init-game-state to accept a config map. Built-in deck definitions stay as constants (iggy-pop-decklist map with :deck/main and :deck/side).

## Architecture

### Data Model (app-db)
Setup state stored as plain maps in re-frame app-db:
- :setup/selected-deck — keyword id of chosen deck
- :setup/main-deck — vec of {:card/id :count} maps (mutable via swaps)
- :setup/sideboard — vec of {:card/id :count} maps
- :setup/clock-turns — integer (default 4)
- :setup/presets — map of preset-name → config (loaded from localStorage)
- :setup/last-preset — string name of MRU preset

### New Files
- events/setup.cljs — Setup event handlers
- subs/setup.cljs — Setup subscriptions
- views/setup.cljs — Setup screen components
- storage.cljs — localStorage EDN persistence

### Modified Files
- core.cljs — Change init dispatch, update root component routing
- events/game.cljs — Refactor init-game-state to accept config, remove make-test-deck
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs — Add iggy-pop-decklist constant with :deck/main and :deck/side

### Event Flow
1. App init → ::init-setup (load presets from localStorage, set defaults)
2. User selects deck → ::select-deck (populate main/side from built-in deck)
3. User swaps cards → ::move-to-side / ::move-to-main (validate counts)
4. User sets clock → ::set-clock-turns
5. User saves preset → ::save-preset (persist to localStorage)
6. User clicks Start Game → ::start-game (validate → dispatch ::init-game with config)
7. ::init-game receives {:main-deck [...] :clock-turns N} → builds game state
8. In-game New Game → confirm dialog → ::set-active-screen :setup

### Subscriptions
- ::setup-config, ::available-decks, ::current-main (grouped by type), ::current-side
- ::deck-valid? (main=60, side≤15), ::saved-presets, ::clock-turns

### UI Layout
Side-by-side grid layout (2fr 1fr):
- Left panel: Maindeck grouped by card type, each card has → button
- Right panel: Sideboard flat list, each card has ← button
- Header counts turn red on validation failure
- Preset dropdown + Save As at top
- Opponent clock + Start Game / Quick Start at bottom

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Game currently auto-starts with a hardcoded Iggy Pop deck. No way to configure the deck, swap sideboard cards, or persist settings. This blocks hand sculpting (Epic 4), deck import (Epic 3), and goldfish bots (Epic 5).

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- core.cljs:50-53 — Placeholder setup screen ("Coming Soon")
- core.cljs:80-96 — Screen routing already works (:setup/:game via :active-screen)
- events/game.cljs:25-65 — make-test-deck hardcodes Iggy Pop 60-card list
- events/game.cljs:68-151 — init-game-state takes no config, always uses make-test-deck
- events/game.cljs:154-157 — ::init-game event takes no parameters
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:344-351 — all-cards vector collects card definitions
- subs/game.cljs:13 — :active-screen subscription already exists

**External:**
- No external APIs needed — all data is local
- localStorage API is synchronous, suitable for EDN blob storage
- Tailwind CSS grid already available for side-by-side layout

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. App-db config with built-in deck constants ✓

**What it is:** Deck config lives in re-frame app-db as plain maps. Built-in decks defined as ClojureScript constants. localStorage stores only presets (named configs).

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed app-db structure — already uses flat maps for game state
- Checked re-frame patterns — app-db maps are idiomatic for UI state
- Verified localStorage works with pr-str/reader for EDN

**Pros:**
- Consistent with existing app-db patterns
- Simple — no schema changes needed
- Presets are just EDN blobs, trivial to serialize

**Cons:**
- No Datascript query power for deck data (but we don't need it)

**Chosen because:** Simplest approach that meets all requirements. Deck config is UI state, not game entity state.

#### 2. Deck entities in Datascript ❌

**What it is:** Add :deck/* and :sideboard/* entity types to Datascript schema. Query decks with Datalog.

**Why we looked at this:** Datascript is the existing data store, might be natural fit.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed db/schema.cljs — schema is for game entities (cards, players, objects)
- Deck config is setup-time data, not game-time data
- Would need schema migration and new entity types

**Pros:**
- Could use Datalog queries for deck filtering

**Cons:**
- Overengineered — deck config is simple key-value, not relational
- Schema changes for non-game data muddies the data model
- Datascript DB gets recreated on each game init

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Deck config is UI/setup state, not game entity state. Adding it to Datascript conflates two concerns.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We need complex deck queries (e.g., "find all decks containing Brain Freeze").

#### 3. Separate deck registry namespace ❌

**What it is:** Create a decks/ namespace with deck definitions as separate files, a registry, and a loader.

**Why we looked at this:** Might scale better for many decks.

**Investigation:**
- Only one deck (Iggy Pop) exists now
- Design says "architected for more" but doesn't require multi-deck infrastructure yet

**Pros:**
- Clean separation of deck data
- Easy to add new decks

**Cons:**
- Overengineered for one deck
- Extra files and indirection for no current benefit

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** YAGNI — one deck constant in cards/iggy_pop.cljs is sufficient. When more decks arrive (via deck import Epic 3), that epic can establish the pattern.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We're adding 3+ built-in decks and need organization.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Setup screen as landing page
- Deck selection dropdown (one built-in deck)
- Side-by-side sideboard swap UI with click-to-swap
- Goldfish clock turns config
- Named presets in localStorage
- Quick Start from MRU preset
- Confirmation dialog for game→setup navigation
- Refactoring init-game-state to accept config

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Hand sculpting / mulligan (Epic 4 — depends on this)
- Deck import from Moxfield/MTGGoldfish (Epic 3 — depends on this)
- Bot archetype selection (Epic 5 — depends on this)
- Card art or images (not in any current epic)
- Deck editing beyond sideboard swaps (not needed for practice tool)

### Open Questions
- Should Quick Start skip the setup screen entirely or show it briefly? (Default: skip entirely)
- Exact Tailwind color classes for validation errors? (Resolve during implementation, match existing theme)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Deck data model? | App-db only | No separate namespace, config in re-frame app-db maps |
| Sideboard UI level? | Click-to-swap | Per-card → buttons, validate 60/15, no drag-and-drop |
| Opponent config? | Goldfish clock only | Simple number input, defer bot archetypes to Epic 5 |
| Setup screen layout? | Single page, stacked sections | Preset bar → deck dropdown → deck panels → opponent → buttons |
| Deck picker style? | Dropdown with preview | Dropdown selects deck, card list shows below grouped by type |
| localStorage scope? | Named presets | Multiple saved configs, not just last-used. Save As + Delete UI |
| Nav safety? | Confirm dialog | "Leave current game?" before navigating back to setup |
| Preset UX? | Dropdown + Save As | Preset dropdown at top of setup, Save As prompts for name, X to delete |
| Card display format? | Names + quantities grouped by type | "4x Dark Ritual" format, grouped as Lands/Instants/Sorceries/Artifacts |
| Main/Side layout? | Side-by-side | grid-cols-[2fr_1fr], main left, side right, both scrollable |

### Open Concerns Raised
- Side-by-side vs stacked sideboard → Resolved: side-by-side for better swap UX
- Named presets vs last-used-only → User chose named presets, adds Save As/Delete UI
close_reason: All child tasks complete. Setup screen is landing page with deck panels, presets, sideboard swaps, clock config, new game, and restore-setup.

title: Test Quality Improvement: Fizzle Test Suite
design:
## Goal
Improve test effectiveness by removing 20 RED tests, strengthening 164 YELLOW tests, and adding missing corner case coverage for the highest-risk modules.

## Analysis Summary
- 1110 tests analyzed across engine, events, cards, history
- 20 RED (2%) - provide false confidence, must remove/replace
- 164 YELLOW (15%) - weak assertions or partial coverage, must strengthen
- 926 GREEN (83%) - genuine quality
- 38+ missing corner cases identified

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 20 RED tests removed or replaced with meaningful tests
- [ ] Top YELLOW patterns addressed (some? removal, card def consolidation)
- [ ] HIGH priority missing corner cases covered
- [ ] make validate passes after all changes

## Anti-patterns
- Adding tests that only check != nil
- Adding tests that verify mock behavior (none in this codebase anyway)
- Keeping tautological tests for coverage
- Adding happy-path-only tests
close_reason: All 4 success criteria met: 20 RED tests removed, YELLOW patterns strengthened, 7 corner cases covered (10 new tests), make validate passes (1237 tests, 0 failures). Bug fix: grant-expired? nil handling.

title: Consistent card sorting and grouping across all zones and dialogs
description: Cards should be sorted and grouped consistently in most zones: hand, battlefield, selection dialogs (tutor, discard), setup deck lists. Sorting order: group by card type (Lands, Instants, Sorceries, Artifacts), then alphabetical within each group. EXCEPTIONS: Stack must preserve resolution order (top-to-bottom). Graveyard needs a toggle — default to sorted/grouped for easy scanning, but toggle to chronological order when graveyard order matters (e.g. checking what was milled recently, Ill-Gotten Gains choices). Consider: toggle button on graveyard panel header, extract setup screen's grouping logic into shared utility, apply to hand view, tutor/selection modals, battlefield, discard selection.
close_reason: Epic fizzle-d4ib completed: consistent CMC+name sorting across hand, battlefield (land/non-land split), graveyard (castable partition + toggle), all selection dialogs, opening hand, and scry. Sort utility in engine/sorting.cljs.

title: Feature: Turn Structure UI
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Turn counter displays current turn number
- Phase bar shows all 8 phases (untap, upkeep, draw, main1, combat, main2, end, cleanup)
- Current phase is visually highlighted
- 'Next Phase' button advances to next phase
- Button changes to 'New Turn' when at cleanup phase
- Clicking 'New Turn' starts the next turn (calls start-turn event)
- UI positioned at top of screen, above battlefield

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Turn number displays and updates when new turn starts
- [ ] All 8 phases visible in horizontal strip
- [ ] Current phase visually distinct from others
- [ ] Next Phase button dispatches ::advance-phase event
- [ ] Button text changes to 'New Turn' at cleanup phase
- [ ] New Turn button dispatches ::start-turn event
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO auto-advance of phases (user wants manual control for practice)
- ❌ NO complex animations/transitions (POC - keep simple)
- ❌ NO new subscriptions when existing ones work (use ::current-phase, ::current-turn)
- ❌ NO separate component files (POC - add to core.cljs like other components)

## Approach
Add a turn-bar component to core.cljs following existing patterns (mana-pool-view, life-view). Uses existing subscriptions and events - no engine changes needed.

## Architecture
- src/fizzle/core.cljs - Add turn-bar component, insert at top of app
- Uses: subs/game.cljs ::current-phase, ::current-turn (existing)
- Dispatches: events/game.cljs ::advance-phase, ::start-turn (existing)

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Turn/phase structure exists in engine but is invisible to user. Cannot practice turn sequencing or test phase-dependent behavior without UI.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- src/fizzle/subs/game.cljs:73-83 - ::current-phase and ::current-turn subscriptions exist
- src/fizzle/events/game.cljs:173-175 - phases vector defines order
- src/fizzle/events/game.cljs:240-250 - ::advance-phase and ::start-turn events exist
- src/fizzle/core.cljs - UI components follow Reagent + Tailwind pattern

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Display turn counter
- Display phase bar with current phase highlighted
- Next Phase / New Turn button

**Out of scope:**
- Opponent turn support (single player for now)
- Phase-specific styling/theming
- Keyboard shortcuts
close_reason: All success criteria met. Turn-bar component implemented with turn counter, 8-phase strip, current phase highlighting, and Next Phase/New Turn button. Commit a861b40.

title: Decompose selection.cljs — separate mechanism from policy
description:
## The Problem

`selection.cljs` is ~1440 lines handling 11 selection types, 11 `defmethod execute-confirmed-selection` implementations, and 12 event registrations. It does the work of a subsystem while wearing the clothes of a single namespace.

### Why size alone isn't the issue

The real problem is that different *concerns* share one space:
- **Library operations:** tutor, peek-and-select, pile-choice, scry
- **Hand operations:** discard, cleanup-discard
- **Targeting:** cast-time-targeting, ability-targeting, player-target
- **Cost selection:** x-mana-cost, exile-cards-cost
- **Zone operations:** graveyard-return

Each type has a builder, a confirm handler, and specific execution logic. The builder/confirm pattern is consistent — that's good — but consistency of *shape* doesn't mean unity of *concern*.

### Growth problem

When you add a new card that needs a new selection type, you modify the largest file in the codebase. Plus modals.cljs for the UI, subs.cljs for the cards subscription, and game.cljs for initiation. That's 4 files for every new player-choice type.

### Specific overlaps that beg for unification

- `:discard` and `:cleanup-discard` differ only in what happens after (continue resolution vs expire grants). That's a flag, not a type.
- `:cast-time-targeting` and `:ability-targeting` both select targets; they differ in what happens with the result. Context, not type.

### The Rich Hickey question

"Are we complecting mechanism and policy?" Selection is a *mechanism* (show cards, let player pick, return choices). What you do with the selected items is *policy* (move to graveyard, return to hand, set as target). Right now mechanism and policy are tangled together in every defmethod.

### Proposed direction (needs brainstorming)

Several possible approaches:

**Approach A: Subdirectory decomposition**
```
events/selection/
  core.cljs       — shared helpers, multimethod, confirm-selection-impl
  targeting.cljs   — cast-time + ability targeting
  library.cljs     — tutor, peek-and-select, pile-choice, scry
  discard.cljs     — discard, cleanup-discard
  costs.cljs       — x-mana-cost, exile-cards-cost
  graveyard.cljs   — graveyard-return
```

**Approach B: Mechanism/policy split**
```
events/selection.cljs     — the mechanism (toggle, confirm, cancel, state machine)
events/selection_types.cljs — the policy (what each confirmed selection does)
```

**Approach C: Data-driven selection registry**
```clojure
(def selection-types
  {:tutor    {:builder build-tutor-selection
              :executor execute-tutor-selection}
   :discard  {:builder build-discard-selection
              :executor execute-discard-selection}
   ...})
```

### Also consider

- `resolve-spell-with-selection` (107 lines) conflates fizzle checking, target resolution, and selection building. Could be decomposed into phases.
- `reorder-library-for-scry` is pure zone position manipulation — belongs in `engine/zones.cljs`.
- The `confirm-selection-impl` wrapper handles 3 different return shapes (`{:db}`, `{:db :pending-selection}`, `{:db :finalized?}`). Could standardize.

### Files involved

- `events/selection.cljs` — the 1440-line file being decomposed
- `views/modals.cljs` — `selection-modal` router has parallel `cond` dispatch (lines 605-674)
- `subs/game.cljs` — `::selection-cards` subscription routes on selection type
- `events/game.cljs` — `resolve-one-item` calls into selection resolution functions

### Risks

- Namespace extraction risks breaking requires (learned this lesson before — check ALL requires)
- `defn-` private functions become public when moved (known pattern)
- The multimethod must remain in one namespace for open dispatch
- Test files import selection events — keyword namespaces change
close_reason: All 6 extraction tasks completed — selection.cljs fully decomposed into events/selection/ subdirectory

title: Task 1: Migrate All Test Files to Shared Helpers
design:
## Goal
Migrate all 29 test files that define their own create-test-db to use the shared fizzle.test-helpers namespace. test_helpers.cljs and test_helpers_test.cljs already exist and are validated. This task is purely mechanical migration + commit.

## Pre-conditions
- src/test/fizzle/test_helpers.cljs EXISTS (uncommitted) with: create-test-db, add-card-to-zone, add-cards-to-library, add-cards-to-graveyard, get-zone-count, get-object-zone, get-hand-count, add-opponent
- src/test/fizzle/test_helpers_test.cljs EXISTS (uncommitted) with 11 tests

## Effort Estimate
8-12 hours (mechanical migration across 29 files in batches of ~5, with make validate between batches)

## Implementation

### 1. Validate existing helpers work
- Run: make test (confirm test_helpers_test.cljs passes)
- If any failures, fix before proceeding

### 2. Migrate test files in batches of ~5

For each file:
a. Add [fizzle.test-helpers :as th] to requires
b. Remove local create-test-db, add-card-to-zone, add-cards-to-library, add-cards-to-graveyard, get-zone-count, get-object-zone, get-hand-count, add-opponent definitions
c. Replace calls: create-test-db -> th/create-test-db, add-card-to-zone -> th/add-card-to-zone, etc.
d. For files with custom mana (careful_study, intuition): change (create-test-db) to (th/create-test-db {:mana {:blue 1}})
e. Remove zone-specific helpers (add-artifact-to-battlefield, add-land-to-battlefield) — replace with (th/add-card-to-zone db card-id :battlefield player-id)
f. Preserve any file-specific helpers that are NOT duplicated elsewhere
g. Run make validate after each batch of 5 files

Complete file list (29 files):
1. src/test/fizzle/cards/basic_lands_test.cljs
2. src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs
3. src/test/fizzle/cards/cabal_ritual_test.cljs
4. src/test/fizzle/cards/careful_study_test.cljs
5. src/test/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs
6. src/test/fizzle/cards/city_of_traitors_test.cljs
7. src/test/fizzle/cards/dark_ritual_test.cljs
8. src/test/fizzle/cards/flash_of_insight_test.cljs
9. src/test/fizzle/cards/ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs
10. src/test/fizzle/cards/intuition_test.cljs
11. src/test/fizzle/cards/led_test.cljs
12. src/test/fizzle/cards/lotus_petal_test.cljs
13. src/test/fizzle/cards/mental_note_test.cljs
14. src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs
15. src/test/fizzle/cards/opt_test.cljs
16. src/test/fizzle/cards/orims_chant_test.cljs
17. src/test/fizzle/cards/polluted_delta_test.cljs
18. src/test/fizzle/cards/recoup_test.cljs
19. src/test/fizzle/cards/underground_river_test.cljs
20. src/test/fizzle/db/queries_test.cljs
21. src/test/fizzle/engine/cob_event_trigger_test.cljs
22. src/test/fizzle/engine/trigger_db_integration_test.cljs
23. src/test/fizzle/events/integration/mana_allocation_test.cljs
24. src/test/fizzle/events/pile_choice_test.cljs
25. src/test/fizzle/events/play_land_test.cljs
26. src/test/fizzle/events/selection/costs_allocation_test.cljs
27. src/test/fizzle/events/selection/storm_split_test.cljs
28. src/test/fizzle/events/tap_land_test.cljs
29. src/test/fizzle/events/tutor_multi_select_test.cljs

### 3. Verify duplication eliminated in migrated files
- grep -r 'defn create-test-db' src/test/ should return ONLY test_helpers.cljs
- grep -r 'defn add-card-to-zone' src/test/ should return test_helpers.cljs + 11 unmigrated files (see Key Considerations)

### 4. Files NOT migrated in this task

**32 init-game-state files (DO NOT TOUCH):**
These use fizzle.db.init/init-game-state and have an INCOMPATIBLE add-card-to-zone signature:
- Signature: [db player-id card zone] — takes card MAP (not keyword), transacts def inline
- Returns: [obj-id db] (reversed from shared helper's [db obj-id])
- Cannot use shared th/add-card-to-zone because their db doesn't pre-load card defs

Files with incompatible add-card-to-zone that remain after this migration:
1. src/test/fizzle/engine/stack_test.cljs (own create-game-db, standard-ish signature)
2. src/test/fizzle/engine/grants_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
3. src/test/fizzle/engine/targeting_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
4. src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
5. src/test/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
6. src/test/fizzle/cards/ray_of_revelation_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
7. src/test/fizzle/events/mode_selection_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
8. src/test/fizzle/cards/seal_of_cleansing_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
9. src/test/fizzle/events/resolve_all_test.cljs (init-game-state, reversed tuple)
10. src/test/fizzle/events/auto_confirm_test.cljs (own create-game-db, defn-, standard signature)
11. src/test/fizzle/history/interceptor_test.cljs (own create-game-db, defn-, standard signature)

These 11 files need a FUTURE task that either:
a. Adds a card-map variant to test_helpers (th/add-card-def-to-zone), or
b. Migrates them to create-test-db (scope change for those tests)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] src/test/fizzle/test_helpers_test.cljs passes (all 11 tests green)
- [ ] Zero test files define their own create-test-db (verify: grep returns only test_helpers.cljs)
- [ ] All 29 migrated files use th/add-card-to-zone instead of local definition
- [ ] All return types in migrated files use [db result] tuple convention
- [ ] All existing tests pass identically (make validate green)
- [ ] No lint or format warnings
- [ ] Remaining add-card-to-zone definitions are ONLY in the 11 documented unmigrated files

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**CRITICAL: Two incompatible add-card-to-zone signatures exist**
- Standard (29 create-test-db files): [db card-id zone player-id] returns [db obj-id] — card-id is keyword, card defs pre-loaded
- Init-game-state variant (9 files): [db player-id card zone] returns [obj-id db] — card is full map, transacted inline
- Shared helper uses standard signature. Init-game-state files CANNOT use it without either adding a new helper variant or migrating their DB setup.
- DO NOT attempt to migrate init-game-state files in this task — different scope, different risk.

**Edge Case: Custom mana files**
- careful_study_test.cljs and intuition_test.cljs initialize with non-zero mana
- MUST use opts: (th/create-test-db {:mana {:blue 1}})
- Verify exact mana values match each file's original setup

**Edge Case: queries_test.cljs also defines add-card-to-zone-with-position**
- queries_test.cljs:58 defines add-card-to-zone-with-position (separate from add-card-to-zone)
- This is a unique helper, NOT duplicated elsewhere — PRESERVE it in queries_test.cljs
- Only migrate create-test-db and add-card-to-zone from this file

**Edge Case: Zone-specific helpers**
- add-artifact-to-battlefield / add-land-to-battlefield are just add-card-to-zone with hardcoded :battlefield
- Replace with (th/add-card-to-zone db card-id :battlefield player-id)
- May need to update variable names in let bindings

**Edge Case: Library position**
- Shared add-card-to-zone sets :object/position 0 when zone is :library
- Verify files that add cards to library don't rely on position being ABSENT

**Edge Case: Invalid card-id**
- Shared helper throws informative error on nil card-eid
- Existing local helpers may silently create nil-card objects — migration may surface latent bugs
- If a test fails after migration, check whether the card-id keyword matches a card def in iggy-pop

**Batch strategy**
- Migrate ~5 files at a time, run make validate between batches
- If a batch fails, narrow to individual file to find the issue
- Suggested batches:
  - Batch 1: basic_lands, brain_freeze, cabal_ritual, careful_study, cephalid_coliseum
  - Batch 2: city_of_traitors, dark_ritual, flash_of_insight, ill_gotten_gains, intuition
  - Batch 3: led, lotus_petal, mental_note, merchant_scroll, opt
  - Batch 4: orims_chant, polluted_delta, recoup, underground_river, queries_test
  - Batch 5: cob_event_trigger, trigger_db_integration, mana_allocation, pile_choice, play_land
  - Batch 6: costs_allocation, storm_split, tap_land, tutor_multi_select

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in shared helpers (helpers must be card-agnostic)
- ❌ NO breaking existing test assertions during migration (pure extraction)
- ❌ NO changing test logic — only change helper imports and call sites
- ❌ NO mutable conn leaking from helpers (return immutable db values, not connections)
- ❌ NO touching files that use init-game-state (incompatible signature, separate scope)
- ❌ NO migrating private (defn-) helpers from auto_confirm_test or interceptor_test (separate scope)
- ❌ NO removing queries_test.cljs add-card-to-zone-with-position (unique helper, not duplicated)
close_reason: All 29 files migrated to shared test_helpers. Zero create-test-db duplication. 1412 tests, 3153 assertions pass. No lint/format warnings. Remaining add-card-to-zone in 11 documented unmigrated files (incompatible init-game-state signature).

title: Manual mana allocation for generic costs
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Player MUST manually allocate mana for generic costs by clicking mana symbols in the pool
- Colored mana requirements MUST be auto-deducted before allocation begins
- Allocation MUST auto-confirm when allocated amount equals generic requirement (no confirm button)
- Applies to ALL mana payments with generic costs: spell casting AND activated abilities
- X costs resolved first via existing X-cost selection, then combined (X + fixed generic) allocated together
- Mana pool view becomes interactive during allocation mode (clickable symbols, hover states)
- Allocation mode shows remaining generic amount near the mana pool
- Player can reset allocation to restart (misclick recovery)
- Colors with 0 remaining mana MUST be visually dimmed/disabled during allocation
- No speed shortcuts or auto-pay options — player always manually allocates

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Casting a spell with generic costs enters allocation mode in mana pool
- [ ] Clicking mana symbols allocates from that color toward generic
- [ ] Auto-confirms when allocation matches generic requirement
- [ ] Colored costs auto-deducted before allocation begins
- [ ] Works for activated abilities with generic costs
- [ ] X cost selection chains into combined allocation (X + fixed generic)
- [ ] Reset restarts allocation without losing mana
- [ ] Dimmed colors when 0 remaining in pool
- [ ] Spells with 0 generic costs skip allocation entirely
- [ ] All existing mana/casting/ability tests still pass
- [ ] New tests cover allocation flow, auto-confirm, reset, edge cases
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO modal/dialog for allocation (UX: breaks flow, too intrusive for storm turns)
- ❌ NO confirm button (UX: auto-confirm on full allocation, MTGO-style immediate)
- ❌ NO auto-pay/greedy shortcut (design: user explicitly chose full manual control)
- ❌ NO mutable state outside datascript for allocation tracking (architecture: all game state in db for undo/replay)
- ❌ NO special-casing card names in allocation logic (architecture: data-driven, engine interprets generic costs uniformly)
- ❌ NO bypassing selection system (architecture: use pending-selection for state, enables undo/chaining)

## Approach
MTGO-style interactive mana pool. When a spell or ability has generic costs, colored requirements are auto-deducted and the mana pool view becomes clickable. Player clicks mana symbols to allocate toward generic. Auto-confirms when allocation equals requirement. Uses existing selection system under the hood (pending-selection with type :mana-allocation, auto-confirm? true when remaining hits 0).

## Architecture
### Components
- engine/mana.cljs: New pay-mana-with-allocation function accepting explicit allocation map
- events/selection/costs.cljs: Builder (build-mana-allocation-selection), custom events (allocate-mana-color, reset-mana-allocation), confirm handler (execute-confirmed-selection :mana-allocation)
- events/game.cljs: Chain mana allocation as last step before cast-spell-mode in initiate-cast-with-mode
- events/abilities.cljs: Chain mana allocation before ability activation
- views/mana_pool.cljs: Context-sensitive view — static normally, clickable during allocation with hover states, dimming, remaining counter
- subs/game.cljs: Subscription deriving allocation state from pending-selection

### Data Flow
1. Cast/ability triggers with generic > 0
2. Colored costs computed, remaining pool calculated
3. pending-selection created: {:selection/type :mana-allocation, :selection/generic-remaining N, :selection/allocation {}, :selection/remaining-pool {...}, :selection/colored-cost {...}}
4. Mana pool view detects allocation mode, renders clickable symbols
5. Click dispatches ::allocate-mana-color with color key
6. Event updates allocation map and decrements generic-remaining
7. When generic-remaining = 0, auto-confirm triggers
8. Confirm handler calls pay-mana-with-allocation and proceeds to cast/activate

### Chaining Order
Spells: mode → X cost → targeting → MANA ALLOCATION → cast-spell-mode
Abilities: MANA ALLOCATION → activate-ability

## Parallelism Map

### Independent Work Streams
1. **Engine** (engine/mana.cljs, engine/mana_test.cljs)
   - pay-mana-with-allocation function + unit tests
   - Estimated complexity: low
2. **Selection events** (events/selection/costs.cljs, tests)
   - Builder, allocate/reset events, confirm handler
   - Estimated complexity: medium
3. **View + subs** (views/mana_pool.cljs, subs/game.cljs)
   - Interactive mana pool, allocation subscription
   - Estimated complexity: medium
4. **Integration** (events/game.cljs, events/abilities.cljs, tests)
   - Chain allocation into cast and ability flows
   - Estimated complexity: medium

### Stream Dependencies
- Stream 2 (selection) depends on Stream 1 (engine) — confirm handler calls pay-mana-with-allocation
- Stream 3 (view) depends on Stream 2 (selection) — reads pending-selection schema
- Stream 4 (integration) depends on Stream 2 (selection) — calls builder functions

### Suggested Waves
- Wave 1: Stream 1 (engine layer — foundation)
- Wave 2: Stream 2 (selection events — core logic)
- Wave 3: Streams 3 + 4 (view + integration — can parallelize)

### File Ownership Boundaries
| Stream | Owns (exclusive) | Shared (needs coordination) |
|--------|-------------------|-----------------------------|
| Engine | engine/mana.cljs | — |
| Selection | events/selection/costs.cljs | — |
| View | views/mana_pool.cljs, subs/game.cljs | — |
| Integration | events/game.cljs, events/abilities.cljs | events/selection/costs.cljs (Stream 2) |

### Parallelism Assessment
- Independent streams: 2 (only in Wave 3)
- Recommendation: solo execution
- Rationale: Sequential dependency chain engine → selection → view/integration. Only 2 streams parallelize in Wave 3, not worth team coordination overhead.

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Current greedy mana allocation removes player agency. When paying generic costs with multiple colors available, the engine auto-deducts from the largest pool. This prevents strategic mana management (e.g., saving blue for Brain Freeze while spending black on generic). Critical for storm combo practice where mana sequencing decisions matter.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- engine/mana.cljs:75-114 — Current pay-mana uses greedy allocation (sort by pool size descending)
- engine/mana.cljs:42-62 — can-pay? validates total availability
- events/selection/costs.cljs:84-157 — X-cost selection pattern: builder + custom events + confirm handler
- views/mana_pool.cljs:24-34 — Current static mana pool display (only non-zero colors)
- engine/rules.cljs:346-365 — cast-spell-mode calls pay-mana, assumes can-pay? checked

**External:**
- MTGO mana payment: click mana in pool, auto-resolves when paid. No dialog.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Interactive mana pool with auto-confirm ✓

**What it is:** Mana pool view becomes clickable during allocation mode. Player clicks mana symbols to allocate toward generic. Auto-confirms when full. No dialog, no confirm button.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed MTGO payment UX — click-to-allocate with auto-resolve
- Reviewed X-cost selection pattern — custom events + auto-confirm already proven
- Reviewed mana_pool.cljs — simple component, easy to make context-sensitive

**Pros:**
- Fastest UX — no interruption to game flow
- MTGO-familiar for target audience (Premodern players)
- Uses existing selection system (pending-selection, auto-confirm)

**Cons:**
- Mana pool view becomes more complex (conditional rendering)
- Need to handle edge cases (0 remaining, single color)

**Chosen because:** Matches MTGO mental model, fastest interaction, leverages existing selection infrastructure

#### 2. Modal dialog with confirm button ❌

**What it is:** Popup dialog showing available mana with click-to-allocate and explicit confirm button.

**Why we looked at this:** Standard pattern for player input in the codebase (X-cost, targeting).

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed modal patterns in views/modals.cljs
- Considered user workflow during storm turns (10+ casts)

**Pros:**
- Consistent with existing dialog patterns
- Clear visual separation of allocation state

**Cons:**
- Breaks game flow with popup
- Too slow for rapid storm turn casting
- Confirm button adds unnecessary click

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User explicitly rejected dialog approach — too intrusive for storm turns. Auto-accept pattern preferred.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** User requests explicit confirmation mode for beginners.

#### 3. Inline selection overlay ❌

**What it is:** Selection system shows allocation state as overlay/banner near mana pool. Clicks go through selection events.

**Why we looked at this:** More framework-consistent than modifying mana pool view directly.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed how pending selections render in views
- Considered visual impact of overlay elements

**Pros:**
- Reuses selection rendering patterns
- Clear visual indicator of allocation mode

**Cons:**
- Adds visual clutter (overlay on top of existing UI)
- Less direct than clicking the pool itself
- Extra UI elements to maintain

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Unnecessary indirection — clicking the pool directly is simpler and more MTGO-like.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Mana pool view becomes too complex to hold allocation logic.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Generic cost allocation for spells and abilities
- X cost → generic allocation chaining
- Interactive mana pool view with auto-confirm
- Reset/undo for misclicks

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Colored mana choice (e.g., hybrid mana) — no hybrid cards in Premodern target set
- Phyrexian mana — not in scope
- Auto-pay toggle/setting — user chose full manual
- Mana pool click sounds/animations — cosmetic, defer

### Open Questions
- Should allocation show as annotation on mana symbols (e.g., 'B: 3 (1 allocated)') or separate line?
- When only one color has remaining mana, should it auto-allocate all? (User said no shortcuts, so probably not)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| When to show allocator? | Always when generic > 0 | Every spell/ability with generic triggers allocation mode |
| UX interaction model? | Click mana symbols (MTGO-style) | Mana pool view becomes interactive, no dialog |
| Scope? | Spells + abilities | Both casting and ability activation flows need allocation |
| Speed shortcut? | No shortcut | Full manual every time, no auto-pay option |
| X + generic interaction? | X first, then combined allocation | X-cost selection chains into allocation with (X + fixed generic) total |
| Dialog vs inline? | No dialog, interactive mana pool | Auto-confirm when full, no confirm button |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Mana Payment Engine
**Question explored:** How does current mana payment work?
**Sources consulted:**
- engine/mana.cljs:75-114 — pay-mana greedy algorithm
- engine/mana.cljs:42-62 — can-pay? validation
- engine/mana_test.cljs — 30+ tests covering edge cases

**Findings:**
- Greedy: sorts colors by pool size descending, takes from largest first
- can-pay? checks total availability but not allocation
- pay-mana assumes can-pay? already called (no internal validation)
- X costs resolved before payment via resolve-x-cost

**Conclusion:** Need parallel pay-mana-with-allocation that takes explicit map instead of greedy. Keep existing pay-mana for internal/non-interactive use.

#### Selection System Patterns
**Question explored:** How to integrate allocation into selection system?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/selection/costs.cljs:84-157 — X-cost builder + events + confirm
- Selection system architecture in MEMORY.md

**Findings:**
- X-cost uses custom events (increment/decrement) not toggle-selection
- Auto-confirm pattern exists (auto-confirm? flag on pending-selection)
- Confirm handler multimethod (execute-confirmed-selection) dispatches by type
- Chaining via {:db :pending-selection} return from multimethod

**Conclusion:** Follow X-cost pattern exactly. New :mana-allocation type with custom allocate/reset events.

### Dead-End Paths

#### Toggle-selection reuse
**Why explored:** toggle-selection is the standard selection interaction
**Investigation:** toggle-selection designed for entity selection (cards in zones), not abstract resources like mana colors
**Why abandoned:** Would require special-casing entity-vs-resource in toggle handler. Custom events cleaner.

### Open Concerns Raised
- 'Dialog breaks flow' → Switched to interactive mana pool with auto-confirm
- 'Speed during storm turns' → Full manual was user choice, no shortcuts needed
close_reason: Closed

title: Add mana-allocation selection type to events/selection/costs.cljs
description: Wave 2: Selection events for mana allocation. Build the selection builder, custom events (allocate-mana-color, reset-mana-allocation), and confirm handler (execute-confirmed-selection :mana-allocation) in events/selection/costs.cljs. Uses the X-cost pattern as template.
design:
## Goal
Add :mana-allocation selection type to events/selection/costs.cljs following the X-cost pattern. This includes a builder function, custom re-frame events (allocate-mana-color, reset-mana-allocation), and a confirm handler multimethod. The builder creates a pending-selection that tracks remaining generic cost and allocation map. Click events increment allocation per color. Auto-confirms when allocation equals generic requirement.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (builder + 3 events + confirm handler + 15-20 tests)

## Context
Completed fizzle-d5ro: pay-mana-with-allocation in engine/mana.cljs. This task builds the selection event layer that calls that function.

## Key Design Decisions

### Selection State Shape
\`\`\`clojure
{:selection/type :mana-allocation
 :selection/player-id player-id
 :selection/spell-id object-id
 :selection/mode mode                    ; casting mode (for confirm handler)
 :selection/generic-remaining N          ; decrements on each click
 :selection/allocation {}                ; {color amount} built up by clicks
 :selection/remaining-pool {...}         ; pool after colored costs deducted
 :selection/colored-cost {...}           ; colored portion for pay-mana-with-allocation
 :selection/original-cost cost           ; full resolved cost (for confirm handler)
 :selection/zone :mana-pool              ; signals view layer
 :selection/validation :always           ; always valid (can confirm at 0)
 :selection/auto-confirm? true}          ; auto-confirm triggers in toggle-selection-impl
\`\`\`

### Auto-Confirm Mechanism
The allocate-mana-color event decrements generic-remaining. When it hits 0, it dispatches ::confirm-selection. This matches the epic requirement: no confirm button, auto-confirm when full.

### Confirm Handler Flow
The :mana-allocation confirm handler:
1. Reads allocation map and colored cost from selection
2. Calls pay-mana-with-allocation with {colored + generic allocation}
3. Calls remaining cast-spell-mode steps (move to stack, storm, etc.)
4. Returns {:db :finalized? true :clear-selected-card? true} (same as X-cost pattern)

### Chaining from X-Cost
The existing :x-mana-cost confirm handler currently calls rules/cast-spell-mode directly. For allocation, it will need to check if resolved cost has generic > 0 and chain to :mana-allocation selection instead. This is an INTEGRATION concern (Wave 3 task), NOT this task. This task only builds the standalone selection type.

## Implementation Checklist

### 1. Add has-generic-mana-cost? helper
- File: src/main/fizzle/events/selection/costs.cljs
- After has-mana-x-cost? (line ~41)
- Signature: (defn has-generic-mana-cost? [cost] ...)
- Returns true if (:colorless cost 0) > 0 after dissoc :x
- Used by initiate-cast-with-mode (game.cljs) in Wave 3 integration

### 2. Add build-mana-allocation-selection builder
- File: src/main/fizzle/events/selection/costs.cljs
- After build-x-mana-selection (line ~114)
- Signature: (defn build-mana-allocation-selection [game-db player-id object-id mode resolved-cost] ...)
- resolved-cost MUST NOT contain :x (caller resolves X before calling)
- Computes: colored-cost = (dissoc resolved-cost :colorless), generic = (:colorless resolved-cost 0)
- Computes: remaining-pool = (merge-with - current-pool colored-cost)
- Returns selection map per shape above
- Returns nil if generic is 0 (caller handles skip)

### 3. Add ::allocate-mana-color event handler
- File: src/main/fizzle/events/selection/costs.cljs
- rf/reg-event-db handler
- Payload: [_ color] where color is keyword like :black, :blue, :colorless
- Guard: color must have > 0 in remaining-pool, generic-remaining must be > 0
- On valid click: increment allocation[color], decrement remaining-pool[color], decrement generic-remaining
- When generic-remaining hits 0 after update: dispatch [::sel-core/confirm-selection] (or directly call confirm-selection-impl)
- On invalid click (0 remaining or 0 generic-remaining): return db unchanged

### 4. Add ::reset-mana-allocation event handler
- File: src/main/fizzle/events/selection/costs.cljs
- rf/reg-event-db handler
- Resets allocation to {}, generic-remaining to original value, remaining-pool to original computed value
- Need to store original generic-remaining in selection (or recompute from original-cost)
- Use :selection/original-cost to recompute

### 5. Add execute-confirmed-selection :mana-allocation defmethod
- File: src/main/fizzle/events/selection/costs.cljs
- Follows :x-mana-cost pattern (lines 142-157)
- Reads allocation from selection
- Constructs full cost = colored-cost + {:colorless generic}
- Calls mana/pay-mana-with-allocation with cost and allocation
- Then does remaining cast-spell-mode steps: pay-additional-costs, move-to-zone :stack, create-spell-stack-item, set-cast-mode, increment-storm, maybe-create-storm-trigger
- Returns {:db :finalized? true :clear-selected-card? true}
- NOTE: Cannot call rules/cast-spell-mode directly because it uses pay-mana internally. Must replicate the steps with pay-mana-with-allocation instead.

### 6. Write tests (TDD - write before implementation)
- File: new test file src/test/fizzle/events/selection/costs_allocation_test.cljs
- Or add section to existing costs test file if one exists

## Test Specifications

### Builder Tests
1. **test-build-allocation-selection-basic**: Cost {:colorless 2 :black 1}, pool {:black 5} -> selection with generic-remaining 2, remaining-pool {:black 4, ...}, colored-cost {:black 1}
   - Bug caught: wrong generic/colored separation
2. **test-build-allocation-selection-no-generic**: Cost {:black 2} (no :colorless) -> returns nil
   - Bug caught: builder doesn't skip zero-generic costs
3. **test-build-allocation-selection-only-generic**: Cost {:colorless 3}, pool {:black 2 :blue 2} -> remaining-pool = pool (no colored deduction), generic-remaining 3
   - Bug caught: colored cost subtraction applied when no colored costs
4. **test-build-allocation-selection-multicolor-cost**: Cost {:colorless 1 :black 1 :blue 1}, pool {:black 3 :blue 2} -> remaining-pool {:black 2 :blue 1 ...}, generic-remaining 1
   - Bug caught: multiple colored costs not all deducted

### Allocate Event Tests
5. **test-allocate-mana-color-decrements**: Selection with generic-remaining 2, remaining-pool {:black 3}. Allocate :black -> generic-remaining 1, allocation {:black 1}, remaining-pool {:black 2}
   - Bug caught: allocation/remaining not updated together
6. **test-allocate-mana-color-zero-remaining-noop**: Selection with remaining-pool {:blue 0}. Allocate :blue -> no change
   - Bug caught: allows allocation from empty color
7. **test-allocate-mana-color-zero-generic-noop**: Selection with generic-remaining 0. Allocate :black -> no change
   - Bug caught: over-allocation beyond generic cost
8. **test-allocate-mana-color-auto-confirms**: Selection with generic-remaining 1. Allocate :black -> triggers confirm (pending-selection removed, spell cast)
   - Bug caught: auto-confirm not triggered when remaining hits 0

### Reset Event Tests
9. **test-reset-mana-allocation**: Selection with allocation {:black 2}, generic-remaining 0. Reset -> allocation {}, generic-remaining back to original, remaining-pool restored
   - Bug caught: reset doesn't restore original state
10. **test-reset-preserves-other-selection-fields**: Reset keeps player-id, spell-id, mode unchanged
    - Bug caught: reset clobbers unrelated fields

### Confirm Handler Tests
11. **test-confirm-mana-allocation-pays-correctly**: allocation {:black 2}, colored-cost {:blue 1}. Confirm -> pool deducted by colored + allocation
    - Bug caught: confirm doesn't call pay-mana-with-allocation correctly
12. **test-confirm-mana-allocation-moves-to-stack**: After confirm, spell is on stack
    - Bug caught: confirm skips stack placement
13. **test-confirm-mana-allocation-increments-storm**: After confirm, storm count incremented
    - Bug caught: storm count not updated (critical for combo deck tool)
14. **test-confirm-mana-allocation-clears-selection**: After confirm, pending-selection is nil
    - Bug caught: stale selection state after confirmation

### Edge Case Tests
15. **test-allocate-colorless-mana-from-pool**: Pool has {:colorless 2}, allocate :colorless -> works correctly
    - Bug caught: colorless pool mana not usable for generic allocation
16. **test-build-allocation-with-zero-pool-colors**: Pool {:black 0 :blue 3}, cost {:colorless 2} -> remaining-pool has :black 0 (view should dim)
    - Bug caught: zero-pool colors cause issues in builder

## Success Criteria
- [ ] build-mana-allocation-selection returns correct selection shape with generic-remaining, allocation, remaining-pool
- [ ] build-mana-allocation-selection returns nil when generic cost is 0
- [ ] has-generic-mana-cost? returns true for {:colorless N} where N > 0, false otherwise
- [ ] ::allocate-mana-color increments allocation, decrements remaining-pool and generic-remaining
- [ ] ::allocate-mana-color is no-op when color has 0 remaining or generic-remaining is 0
- [ ] ::allocate-mana-color auto-confirms when generic-remaining hits 0
- [ ] ::reset-mana-allocation restores initial allocation state
- [ ] execute-confirmed-selection :mana-allocation calls pay-mana-with-allocation and completes casting
- [ ] Storm count incremented after allocation confirm (critical for storm combo practice)
- [ ] All 16+ new tests pass
- [ ] All existing tests pass unchanged
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO modifying existing :x-mana-cost or :exile-cards-cost handlers (this task is standalone)
- ❌ NO calling rules/cast-spell-mode from confirm handler (it uses pay-mana internally; must replicate steps with pay-mana-with-allocation)
- ❌ NO confirm button logic (auto-confirm on generic-remaining = 0 per epic)
- ❌ NO auto-pay shortcuts (epic forbids: player always manually allocates)
- ❌ NO mutable state outside datascript (allocation tracked in pending-selection)
- ❌ NO special-casing card names (data-driven: engine interprets generic costs uniformly)
- ❌ NO modifying game.cljs or abilities.cljs (that's Wave 3 integration task)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Confirm handler replicating cast-spell-mode steps:**
- cast-spell-mode (rules.cljs:346-365) does: pay-mana -> pay-additional-costs -> move-to-zone :stack -> create-spell-stack-item -> set-cast-mode -> increment-storm -> maybe-create-storm-trigger
- Confirm handler MUST replicate all steps except pay-mana (replaced by pay-mana-with-allocation)
- Missing any step breaks casting. Verify each step has a corresponding call.
- Reference: rules.cljs lines 346-365 for exact function calls

**Auto-confirm timing:**
- The allocate event must dispatch confirm AFTER updating db state
- Use rf/dispatch for async confirm dispatch, or directly call confirm-selection-impl
- If using rf/dispatch, the confirm event reads the already-updated pending-selection from db
- If directly calling: must be careful about event-db vs event-fx patterns

**Colorless mana in pool vs :colorless in cost:**
- :colorless in cost map means generic requirement
- :colorless in pool means actual colorless mana (from Sol Ring, Eldrazi lands, etc.)
- Allocation {:colorless 1} means use 1 colorless from pool toward generic
- Builder must NOT filter out :colorless from remaining-pool

**Reset recomputation:**
- Store the original generic amount: (:colorless resolved-cost 0)
- On reset, recompute remaining-pool from current pool minus colored-cost
- OR store original-remaining-pool in selection (simpler, but more data)
- Recommendation: store original generic as :selection/generic-total for reset

**Interaction with additional costs:**
- Some modes have :mode/additional-costs (e.g., life payment, sacrifice)
- The confirm handler calls pay-additional-costs after mana payment
- This matches cast-spell-mode ordering and is already correct

**Namespace dependencies:**
- costs.cljs already requires engine/mana and engine/rules
- No new requires needed for the confirm handler
close_reason: Implemented mana-allocation selection type: has-generic-mana-cost? helper, build-mana-allocation-selection builder, allocate-mana-color-impl/reset-mana-allocation-impl handlers, execute-confirmed-selection :mana-allocation defmethod, cast-spell-mode-with-allocation in rules.cljs. 20 new tests, all 1321 pass. make validate clean.

title: Task 4: Underground River + Ill-Gotten Gains card tests (final cards)
design:
## Goal
Create integration tests for the last 2 untested cards: Underground River (pain land) and Ill-Gotten Gains (symmetric graveyard effect with selection). Completing this closes the epic's card coverage requirement.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Pre-existing Coverage
- Underground River: only in init_game_test.cljs (deck list check), no mana/damage tests
- Ill-Gotten Gains: zero tests anywhere
- City of Brass and Gemstone Mine already covered by 9+ tests in tap_land_test.cljs (no new tests needed)
- Graveyard selection system tested in graveyard_selection_test.cljs (but not for IGG specifically)

## Implementation

### Part A: Underground River integration tests

**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/underground_river_test.cljs (NEW)

**Card definition** (iggy_pop.cljs):
\`\`\`clojure
{:card/id :underground-river
 :card/name "Underground River"
 :card/types #{:land}
 :card/abilities [{:ability/type :mana
                   :ability/cost {:tap true}
                   :ability/produces {:colorless 1}}
                  {:ability/type :mana
                   :ability/cost {:tap true}
                   :ability/produces {:blue 1}
                   :ability/effects [{:effect/type :deal-damage
                                      :effect/amount 1
                                      :effect/target :controller}]}
                  {:ability/type :mana
                   :ability/cost {:tap true}
                   :ability/produces {:black 1}
                   :ability/effects [{:effect/type :deal-damage
                                      :effect/amount 1
                                      :effect/target :controller}]}]}
\`\`\`

**Required imports:**
\`\`\`clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.underground-river-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is use-fixtures]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
    [fizzle.engine.trigger-registry :as registry]
    [fizzle.engine.turn-based :as turn-based]
    [fizzle.events.abilities :as ability-events]))
\`\`\`

**IMPORTANT:** Underground River does NOT have :card/triggers (unlike City of Brass), so it does NOT need trigger registration. Its damage comes from :ability/effects, which execute inline during mana ability activation. However, we still need the registry fixture because turn-based module registers actions needed by activate-mana-ability.

**Helpers:** Use same create-test-db + add-land-to-battlefield pattern from tap_land_test.cljs. The add-land-to-battlefield helper does NOT need trigger registration for Underground River since it has no :card/triggers.

**Tests:**

1. \`underground-river-card-definition-test\`
   - Assert: 3 abilities, all :ability/type :mana
   - Assert: first produces {:colorless 1}, no :ability/effects
   - Assert: second produces {:blue 1}, has deal-damage effect
   - Assert: third produces {:black 1}, has deal-damage effect
   - Bug caught: Card data structure integrity

2. \`underground-river-colorless-no-damage-test\`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add underground-river to battlefield
   - Act: activate-mana-ability with :colorless
   - Assert: colorless mana = 1
   - Assert: life still 20 (no damage from colorless ability)
   - Assert: land is tapped
   - Bug caught: Verifies colorless ability has no pain effect

3. \`underground-river-blue-deals-1-damage-test\`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add underground-river to battlefield
   - Act: activate-mana-ability with :blue
   - Assert: blue mana = 1
   - Assert: life = 19 (1 damage from pain land effect)
   - Assert: land is tapped
   - Bug caught: Verifies pain land damage on colored mana production

4. \`underground-river-black-deals-1-damage-test\`
   - Setup: same as above
   - Act: activate-mana-ability with :black
   - Assert: black mana = 1
   - Assert: life = 19
   - Bug caught: Verifies black ability also deals damage

5. \`underground-river-cannot-activate-when-tapped-test\`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add underground-river, activate once (tapped)
   - Act: try activating again
   - Assert: mana unchanged (second activation fails, returns unchanged db)
   - Bug caught: Tap cost enforcement

### Part B: Ill-Gotten Gains integration tests

**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs (NEW)

**Card definition** (ill_gotten_gains.cljs - separate file):
\`\`\`clojure
{:card/id :ill-gotten-gains
 :card/mana-cost {:colorless 2 :black 2}
 :card/types #{:sorcery}
 :card/effects [
   {:effect/type :exile-self}
   {:effect/type :discard-hand :effect/target :self}
   {:effect/type :discard-hand :effect/target :opponent}
   {:effect/type :return-from-graveyard :effect/target :self :effect/count 3 :effect/selection :player}
   {:effect/type :return-from-graveyard :effect/target :opponent :effect/count 3 :effect/selection :random}]}
\`\`\`

**Test approach:** Use resolve-spell-with-selection from selection.cljs. This pauses at the :return-from-graveyard :selection :player effect, returning {:db updated-db, :pending-selection selection-state}. All effects before that point (exile-self, both discard-hands) will have executed.

**Required imports:**
\`\`\`clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.ill-gotten-gains-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
    [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]
    [fizzle.events.selection :as selection]))
\`\`\`

**Also need:** import ill-gotten-gains card. Check if it's in cards/all-cards or separate.

**Helpers:** create-test-db + add-card-to-zone (same pattern), plus:
- add-opponent helper
- add-cards-to-zone helper: adds N cards to specific zone for player

**Tests:**

6. \`ill-gotten-gains-card-definition-test\`
   - Assert: card exists with correct id, mana cost {2}{B}{B}, sorcery type
   - Assert: 5 effects in correct order
   - Assert: first is :exile-self, then two :discard-hand, then two :return-from-graveyard
   - Bug caught: Card data structure

7. \`ill-gotten-gains-exiles-self-and-discards-both-hands-test\`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add opponent, add IGG to hand, add 2 other cards to caster hand, add 3 cards to opponent hand, add mana
   - Act: cast-spell, then selection/resolve-spell-with-selection
   - Assert on (:db result): IGG is in exile zone (not graveyard, not stack)
   - Assert: caster hand is empty (discarded)
   - Assert: opponent hand is empty (discarded)
   - Assert: caster graveyard has 2 cards (the other hand cards)
   - Assert: opponent graveyard has 3 cards (their hand cards)
   - Assert: (:pending-selection result) is not nil (paused for selection)
   - Bug caught: Verifies effects before selection execute correctly, spell exiles

8. \`ill-gotten-gains-selection-state-structure-test\`
   - Setup: same as above, but also add 4 cards to caster graveyard before casting
   - Act: cast-spell, resolve-spell-with-selection
   - Assert on (:pending-selection result):
     - :selection/type is :graveyard-return
     - :selection/count is 3 (max cards to return)
     - :selection/min-count is 0 (can select none)
     - :selection/player-id is :player-1
     - :selection/candidate-ids contains the graveyard card IDs (hand cards that were discarded + pre-existing GY cards)
     - :selection/remaining-effects has 1 effect (opponent random return)
   - Bug caught: Selection state built correctly for IGG

9. \`ill-gotten-gains-cannot-cast-without-mana-test\`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add IGG to hand, no mana
   - Assert: can-cast? returns false
   - Bug caught: {2}{B}{B} mana cost enforcement

10. \`ill-gotten-gains-is-sorcery-speed-test\`
    - Setup: create-test-db, add IGG to hand, change phase to :combat
    - Assert: can-cast? returns false (sorcery can't be cast outside main phase)
    - Bug caught: Sorcery timing enforcement for IGG

## Success Criteria
- [ ] underground_river_test.cljs exists with 5 tests
- [ ] ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs exists with 5 tests
- [ ] Underground River colorless produces no damage, colored produces 1 damage
- [ ] Underground River tap-when-tapped fails gracefully
- [ ] IGG exiles self (not graveyard) during resolution
- [ ] IGG discards both players' hands before selection
- [ ] IGG pending selection has correct structure (type, count, candidates, remaining effects)
- [ ] IGG sorcery-speed enforced
- [ ] All 816+ existing tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Underground River mana-color parameter**
- activate-mana-ability takes a mana-color param that selects which ability to use
- For :colorless, it should find the first ability with :produces {:colorless 1}
- For :blue/:black, it should find the ability with :produces {:blue 1} or {:black 1}
- If wrong color requested (e.g., :red), it should return db unchanged (no ability matches)

**Edge Case: IGG exile-self happens first**
- The spell exiles itself before discarding hands
- This means IGG will NOT be in the graveyard when the player selects cards to return
- confirm-graveyard-selection checks if spell is still on stack; since exile-self moved it, no double-move
- Test must verify IGG is in exile, not graveyard

**Edge Case: IGG discards add to graveyard before selection**
- The discarded hand cards go to graveyard, increasing the selection pool
- If player had 3 cards in hand (IGG + 2 others), after casting IGG:
  - IGG goes to stack, then exile
  - 2 remaining hand cards get discarded to graveyard
  - Selection candidates include those 2 discarded cards + any pre-existing graveyard cards

**Edge Case: Opponent random return with empty graveyard**
- If opponent had 0 cards in graveyard at return time, (take 3 (shuffle [])) = () → no-op
- Not testing this explicitly (already covered by effects_test for :return-from-graveyard)

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow tap_land_test.cljs for Underground River helpers (add-land-to-battlefield, fixture)
- Follow careful_study_test.cljs for IGG selection tests (resolve-spell-with-selection pattern)
- See graveyard_selection_test.cljs for selection state assertions

## Anti-patterns
- NO mocking Datascript
- NO test-only methods in production code
- NO testing confirm-graveyard-selection directly (it's a re-frame event handler on app-db, not pure game-db)
- NO duplicating helpers verbatim from other test files
- NO using (is (some? result)) as sole assertion
close_reason: All 10 tests implemented: 5 Underground River (card def, colorless no damage, blue damage, black damage, tapped fails) + 5 Ill-Gotten Gains (card def, exile+discard before selection, selection state structure, mana cost, sorcery speed). make validate passes: 826 tests, 1826 assertions.
