title: Extract hand, mana pool, and storm count components
description: Extract hand-view, card-view, mana-pool-view, and storm-count-view from core.cljs into views/hand.cljs and views/mana_pool.cljs. Migrate all inline styles to Tailwind utility classes using @theme tokens. Follow the extraction pattern established in opponent.cljs (fizzle-ryvi).
design:
## Goal
Extract hand-view, card-view, mana-pool-view, storm-count-view, and the mana-colors constant from core.cljs into views/hand.cljs and views/mana_pool.cljs. Migrate all inline :style maps to Tailwind utility classes using @theme tokens. Follow the extraction pattern established in opponent.cljs.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-ryvi established the extraction pattern: create view file, use Tailwind classes, subscribe to re-frame subs, no inline styles. views/common.cljs provides collapsible-zone wrapper. This task extracts the next batch of left-column zones.

## Implementation

### 1. Add missing @theme tokens to src/css/app.css
Add tokens needed by these components that don't already exist:
- --color-perm-border: #6a6 (untapped permanent border)
- --color-perm-border-tapped: #444 (tapped permanent border)  
- --color-perm-bg: #1a2a1a (untapped permanent background)
- --color-perm-bg-tapped: #1a1a1a (tapped permanent background)
- --color-perm-text-tapped: #666 (tapped permanent text)
- --color-ability-bg: #4a3a2a (activated ability button background)
- --color-ability-text: #dd44aa (activated ability button text)
- --color-btn-enabled-bg: #2a6a2a (enabled action button background — shared with controls, added here)

Note: Many colors already exist as @theme tokens (surface-raised, surface-hover, border, border-selected, gold, mana-*, text, text-muted, text-dim, text-label). Only add tokens that are genuinely new.

### 2. Create views/hand.cljs
New file: src/main/fizzle/views/hand.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.views.hand

Move from core.cljs:
- card-view (lines 21-38) — private helper, renders a single hand card
- hand-view (lines 41-50) — public, subscribes to ::subs/hand and ::subs/selected-card

Tailwind conversions:
- Card border: border-2 + conditional border-border-selected / border-border
- Card styling: rounded-md px-3.5 py-2.5 mx-1.5 cursor-pointer min-w-[100px] text-center select-none
- Card background: conditional bg-surface-hover (selected) / bg-surface-raised (default)
- Card text: text-text
- Hand label: text-text-label mb-1.5 text-xs (matches opponent pattern)
- Hand container: flex flex-wrap
- Hand wrapper: mb-4

Subscriptions needed:
- fizzle.subs.game/hand
- fizzle.subs.game/selected-card

Events dispatched:
- fizzle.events.game/select-card (on card click)

Requires:
- [fizzle.events.game :as events]
- [fizzle.subs.game :as subs]
- [re-frame.core :as rf]

### 3. Create views/mana_pool.cljs
New file: src/main/fizzle/views/mana_pool.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.views.mana-pool

Move from core.cljs:
- mana-colors constant (line 17-18) — letter symbols map
- mana-pool-view (lines 53-72) — subscribes to ::subs/mana-pool
- storm-count-view (lines 75-80) — subscribes to ::subs/storm-count

Tailwind conversions for mana-pool-view:
- Container: mb-4
- Label: text-text-label mb-1.5 text-xs
- Pool flex: flex gap-2.5
- Each mana span: font-bold text-lg + conditional mana color class
- Mana color mapping function: returns Tailwind class string based on keyword
  - :white -> "text-mana-white"
  - :blue -> "text-mana-blue"  
  - :black -> "text-mana-black"
  - :red -> "text-mana-red"
  - :green -> "text-mana-green"
  - :colorless -> "text-mana-colorless"
  - default -> "text-mana-colorless"

Tailwind conversions for storm-count-view:
- Container: mb-4
- Label: text-text-label mb-1.5 text-xs
- Count display: text-2xl font-bold text-gold

Subscriptions needed:
- fizzle.subs.game/mana-pool
- fizzle.subs.game/storm-count

No events dispatched (read-only components).

Requires:
- [fizzle.subs.game :as subs]
- [re-frame.core :as rf]

### 4. Update core.cljs
- Add requires: [fizzle.views.hand :as hand], [fizzle.views.mana-pool :as mana-pool]
- Replace [hand-view] with [hand/hand-view]
- Replace [mana-pool-view] with [mana-pool/mana-pool-view]
- Replace [storm-count-view] with [mana-pool/storm-count-view]
- Remove from core.cljs: mana-colors constant, card-view, hand-view, mana-pool-view, storm-count-view
- Keep all other components in core.cljs (battlefield, graveyard, stack, controls, turn-bar, etc.)

### 5. Create tests
Create test files following opponent_test.cljs pattern:

src/test/fizzle/views/hand_test.cljs - not needed (card-view/hand-view are pure Reagent subscriptions with no extractable logic)

src/test/fizzle/views/mana_pool_test.cljs - test the mana-color-class function:
- test each of 6 mana colors returns correct Tailwind class
- test unknown color returns default (text-mana-colorless)

The mana-color-class function is the only testable pure function being extracted. card-view and hand-view are pure Reagent components that subscribe to state — testing them requires re-frame test infrastructure which is out of scope.

### 6. Verify
- make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- No inline :style maps in hand.cljs or mana_pool.cljs
- No hardcoded hex colors in hand.cljs or mana_pool.cljs

## Success Criteria
- [ ] views/hand.cljs exists with card-view and hand-view components
- [ ] views/mana_pool.cljs exists with mana-pool-view, storm-count-view, and mana-color-class
- [ ] Zero inline :style maps in hand.cljs and mana_pool.cljs
- [ ] No hardcoded hex colors in hand.cljs or mana_pool.cljs (all @theme tokens)
- [ ] mana-color-class pure function maps each mana keyword to correct Tailwind class
- [ ] core.cljs requires and uses hand/hand-view, mana-pool/mana-pool-view, mana-pool/storm-count-view
- [ ] card-view, hand-view, mana-pool-view, storm-count-view, mana-colors removed from core.cljs
- [ ] New @theme tokens added to app.css for permanent and ability colors
- [ ] mana_pool_test.cljs has 7+ tests for mana-color-class (6 colors + unknown default)
- [ ] Components are layout-agnostic (no grid/position styling)
- [ ] No changes to event/subscription contracts
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO inline :style maps (use Tailwind utility classes)
- NO hardcoded hex colors (use @theme tokens)
- NO layout positioning in components (grid parent handles placement)
- NO game logic in views (subscribe and dispatch only)
- NO breaking existing subscription/event contracts
- NO mana color tokens for non-mana purposes (semantic correctness)
- NO Tailwind v3 patterns (tailwind.config.js, @tailwind directives)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Mana Color Class Selection:**
The mana-color-class function uses a case expression to map mana keywords to Tailwind class strings. Tailwind v4 scans compiled JS for string literals, so the full class names (e.g., "text-mana-blue") must appear as complete strings in the source — NOT constructed dynamically (e.g., (str "text-mana-" (name color)) would NOT work because Tailwind can't see the generated class names at build time).

**mana-colors constant scope:**
The mana-colors constant (W/U/B/R/G/C symbol map) is only used by mana-pool-view. Moving it to mana_pool.cljs is correct. Verify no other file references it (grep for mana-colors).

**card-view dispatch event namespace:**
card-view dispatches ::events/select-card. In core.cljs, :: resolves to fizzle.core. But the actual event is registered as :fizzle.events.game/select-card. Check how it's aliased — core.cljs requires [fizzle.events.game :as events], so ::events/select-card resolves to :fizzle.events.game/select-card. In hand.cljs, we need the same require and alias to keep the dispatch working.

**Arbitrary Tailwind values:**
min-w-[100px] uses Tailwind's arbitrary value syntax. This works in v4 but verify the compiled CSS includes it. Alternative: min-w-24 (96px) or min-w-28 (112px) — close but not exact. Arbitrary value [100px] is correct here.

**No test for card-view/hand-view:**
These are pure Reagent components with no extractable business logic. Testing them would require re-frame-test setup (mock subscriptions, render components, check hiccup). The opponent.cljs pattern tested the extracted pure function (health-class), not the Reagent component. Same pattern here: test mana-color-class, don't test Reagent rendering.
close_reason: All success criteria met. hand.cljs and mana_pool.cljs extracted with Tailwind classes, 7 new tests for mana-color-class, 8 @theme tokens added, make validate passes (980 tests).

title: Implement tutor effect type and Merchant Scroll
design:
## Goal

Implement the :tutor effect type with library search, criteria filtering, selection modal, and fail-to-find option. Validate by implementing Merchant Scroll card.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Phase 1 complete: City of Brass trigger migrated to event system, dead code removed.

Phase 2 of epic fizzle-wjl: Tutor Infrastructure.

## Implementation

### 1. Study Existing Patterns

**Selection modal (Careful Study pattern):**
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:588-717 - pending-selection system
- src/test/fizzle/cards/careful_study_test.cljs - selection state tests
- Key: resolve-spell-with-selection handles selection-requiring effects

**Library access:**
- src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:142-161 - get-top-n-library pattern
- Library uses :object/position for ordering (0 = top)

**Shuffle implementation needed:**
- src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:28-30 - uses cljs `shuffle` at init
- Need new `shuffle-library` function in zones.cljs or queries.cljs

### 2. Add shuffle-library Function (NEW)

In src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs:

```clojure
(defn shuffle-library
  "Randomize the order of all cards in a player's library.
   Updates :object/position for all library cards with new random positions.
   Returns updated db."
  [db player-id]
  (let [player-eid (q/get-player-eid db player-id)
        library-cards (d/q '[:find ?e
                              :in $ ?owner
                              :where [?e :object/owner ?owner]
                                     [?e :object/zone :library]]
                           db player-eid)
        shuffled-eids (vec (shuffle library-cards))
        position-txs (map-indexed (fn [pos [eid]]
                                    [:db/add eid :object/position pos])
                                  shuffled-eids)]
    (d/db-with db position-txs)))
```

### 3. Add query-library-by-criteria Function

In src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:

```clojure
(defn query-library-by-criteria
  "Return objects in library matching criteria.
   Criteria map supports:
     :card/types    - Set of types, object must have ALL (AND)
     :card/colors   - Set of colors, object must have ANY (OR - blue OR white)
     :card/subtypes - Set of subtypes, object must have ANY (OR)
   Returns vector of objects with card data, or [] if no matches."
  [db player-id criteria]
  (let [player-eid (get-player-eid db player-id)
        library-objs (get-objects-in-zone db player-id :library)]
    (when player-eid
      (filterv
        (fn [obj]
          (let [card (:object/card obj)
                card-types (:card/types card #{})
                card-colors (:card/colors card #{})
                card-subtypes (:card/subtypes card #{})]
            (and
              ;; All required types must be present (AND logic)
              (every? card-types (get criteria :card/types #{}))
              ;; At least one required color (OR logic) - empty criteria = matches all
              (or (empty? (get criteria :card/colors #{}))
                  (some card-colors (get criteria :card/colors #{})))
              ;; At least one required subtype (OR logic)
              (or (empty? (get criteria :card/subtypes #{}))
                  (some card-subtypes (get criteria :card/subtypes #{}))))))
        library-objs))))
```

### 4. Add :tutor Effect Type to effects.cljs

In src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:

```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect-impl :tutor
  ;; Search library for a card matching criteria.
  ;; 
  ;; Effect keys:
  ;;   :effect/criteria - Map of card attributes to match (types, colors, subtypes)
  ;;   :effect/target-zone - Zone to move found card to (:hand for Merchant Scroll)
  ;;   :effect/shuffle? - Whether to shuffle after (default true)
  ;;
  ;; This effect returns db UNCHANGED. The actual tutor happens at the
  ;; app-db level via re-frame events when the player confirms their selection.
  ;; The calling code checks for :selection :tutor and sets up pending selection.
  [db _player-id _effect]
  ;; Return unchanged - selection handled at app-db level
  db)
```

### 5. Extend Selection System for Tutor

In src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs, extend resolve-spell-with-selection:

```clojure
;; In resolve-spell-with-selection, detect :tutor effect similar to :discard
;; Key differences from discard selection:
;;   :selection/zone :library (not :hand)
;;   :selection/count 1 (tutors find exactly 1)
;;   :selection/allow-fail-to-find true (always!)
;;   :selection/candidates <matching-object-ids>  ; Precomputed matches

;; Selection state for tutor:
{:selection/zone :library
 :selection/count 1
 :selection/player-id player-id
 :selection/selected #{}
 :selection/spell-id object-id
 :selection/remaining-effects effects-after
 :selection/effect-type :tutor
 :selection/target-zone :hand  ; Where card goes if found
 :selection/allow-fail-to-find true
 :selection/candidates matching-ids}  ; Pre-filtered valid choices
```

### 6. Add confirm-tutor-selection Event

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
 ::confirm-tutor-selection
 (fn [db _]
   (let [selection (:game/pending-selection db)
         selected (:selection/selected selection)  ; #{} or #{object-id}
         spell-id (:selection/spell-id selection)
         target-zone (:selection/target-zone selection)
         player-id (:selection/player-id selection)
         game-db (:game/db db)]
     (if (empty? selected)
       ;; Fail-to-find: just shuffle
       (-> db
           (assoc :game/db (zones/shuffle-library game-db player-id))
           (dissoc :game/pending-selection))
       ;; Found card: move to target zone, then shuffle
       (let [card-id (first selected)
             db-after-move (zones/move-to-zone game-db card-id target-zone)
             db-after-shuffle (zones/shuffle-library db-after-move player-id)]
         (-> db
             (assoc :game/db db-after-shuffle)
             (dissoc :game/pending-selection)))))))
```

### 7. Implement Merchant Scroll Card

In src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:

```clojure
(def merchant-scroll
  {:card/id :merchant-scroll
   :card/name "Merchant Scroll"
   :card/types #{:sorcery}
   :card/mana-cost {:blue 1 :generic 1}
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :tutor
                   :effect/criteria {:card/types #{:instant}
                                     :card/colors #{:blue}}
                   :effect/target-zone :hand
                   :effect/shuffle? true}]
   :card/text "Search your library for a blue instant card, reveal it, put it into your hand, then shuffle."})
```

### 8. Add Tests

In src/test/fizzle/cards/merchant_scroll_test.cljs:

**Query function tests (what bugs they catch):**
- test-query-library-single-match: Catches regression if criteria matching broken
- test-query-library-multiple-matches: Catches bug where only first match returned
- test-query-library-no-matches: Catches crash on empty result set
- test-query-library-type-and-color: Catches AND/OR logic bugs

**Tutor effect tests:**
- test-tutor-shows-selection-modal: Catches missing UI interaction
- test-tutor-fail-to-find-shuffles: Catches shuffle skip on empty selection
- test-tutor-card-to-hand: Catches zone movement bugs
- test-tutor-shuffles-after-find: Catches shuffle order bugs (must shuffle after move)

**Merchant Scroll integration tests:**
- test-merchant-scroll-finds-blue-instant: Happy path, catches basic integration
- test-merchant-scroll-ignores-non-blue: Catches color filter bug
- test-merchant-scroll-ignores-non-instant: Catches type filter bug
- test-merchant-scroll-fail-to-find-works: Catches fail-to-find path regression
- test-merchant-scroll-with-empty-library: Catches crash on edge case

## Success Criteria

- [ ] query-library-by-criteria function exists in queries.cljs
- [ ] shuffle-library function exists in zones.cljs
- [ ] :tutor effect type defined in effects.cljs
- [ ] Selection modal appears for tutor with matching cards + "Find Nothing" option
- [ ] Selecting card moves it to target zone (hand for Merchant Scroll)
- [ ] Selecting "Find Nothing" shuffles library without moving card
- [ ] Library shuffled after BOTH find and fail-to-find
- [ ] Merchant Scroll card definition in iggy_pop.cljs
- [ ] 12+ tests passing: `make test` shows tutor tests green
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty Library**
- If library is empty when tutor resolves, modal shows only "Find Nothing" option
- Must not crash, must still shuffle (shuffle of empty is no-op)
- Test: test-merchant-scroll-with-empty-library

**Edge Case: No Matching Cards**
- If library has cards but none match criteria, modal shows only "Find Nothing"
- Player must be able to legally fail-to-find (MTG rules)
- Test: test-merchant-scroll-no-blue-instants-in-library

**Edge Case: Exactly One Match**
- If exactly one card matches, still show modal with that card + "Find Nothing"
- NEVER auto-select (player may want to fail-to-find for bluffing)
- Anti-pattern enforces this

**Shuffle Timing**
- CRITICAL: Move card BEFORE shuffle
- If shuffle first: card ends up at random position then moved (wastes entropy)
- Correct order: move to hand → shuffle remaining library
- Test: test-tutor-shuffles-after-find verifies shuffle happens after move

**Selection Count**
- Tutor selects exactly 0 or 1 card (not 2 like Careful Study)
- :selection/count should be 1 but allow 0 for fail-to-find
- Different from discard which requires exact count

**Reference Implementation**
- Study careful_study_test.cljs:275-305 for resolve-spell-with-selection pattern
- Tutor is similar but: zone=library, count=0-1, has candidates filter

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO auto-selecting when only one match (always show modal with fail-to-find)
- ❌ NO omitting fail-to-find option (even when matches exist)
- ❌ NO shuffling before moving card (shuffle AFTER move)
- ❌ NO revealing non-revealed tutors (Merchant Scroll reveals - text says "reveal it")
- ❌ NO skipping shuffle on fail-to-find (always shuffle, even if nothing found)
- ❌ NO crashing on empty library (handle gracefully)
- ❌ NO leaking card order pre-shuffle (don't iterate library in visible order then shuffle)
close_reason: Tutor infrastructure implemented with Merchant Scroll card. Tests passing, make validate passed.

title: Refactor: Unify target storage on stack-item instead of object
description:
Cast-time targeting stores targets on :object/targets while activated abilities store on :stack-item/targets. This inconsistency caused a bug where history descriptions couldn't find targets for targeted spells (Orim's Chant). A workaround is in place (get-target-info fallback in descriptions.cljs) but the root cause needs a brainstorm and refactor to unify on :stack-item/targets.

## Problem
- Activated abilities: targets stored on :stack-item/targets (correct)
- Cast-time targeting: targets stored on :object/targets (inconsistent)
- Targets conceptually belong to the spell-on-the-stack, not the card object

## Known consumers of :object/targets
- confirm-cast-time-target (selection.cljs:999) — writes it
- resolve-effect-with-stored-target (selection.cljs:565) — reads it
- resolve-targeted-effect (effects.cljs:438) — reads it
- resolve-grant-alternate-cost (effects.cljs:570) — reads it
- resolve-add-restriction (effects.cljs:614) — reads it
- target validation (targeting.cljs:137) — reads it

## Approach
Requires brainstorm to design the refactor:
- Move target storage from object to stack-item for cast-time targeting
- Update all effect resolution points to read from stack-item instead of object
- Remove get-target-info fallback in descriptions.cljs once unified
- Consider impact on target validation (fizzle check on resolution)
- Ensure storm copies inherit targets correctly
close_reason: Superseded and completed by epic fizzle-etdk (Unify target storage on stack-items)

Helps measure improvement over time. Display as a stats panel accessible from setup screen. title: Add pay-life cost to Polluted Delta
design:
## Goal
Add the 1 life payment cost to Polluted Delta's activated ability.

## Context
Polluted Delta's Oracle text: "{T}, Pay 1 life, Sacrifice Polluted Delta: Search your library..."
Current implementation is missing the life payment.

## Current State
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs

Existing cost types (multimethod dispatch):
- `:tap` - tap the permanent
- `:remove-counter` - remove counters from permanent
- `:sacrifice-self` - sacrifice the permanent  
- `:discard-hand` - discard controller's hand (LED)

`:pay-life` is NOT implemented.

## Implementation

### Step 1: Add can-pay? :pay-life method
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs

```clojure
(defmethod can-pay? :pay-life [db object-id cost]
  ;; Can pay life if:
  ;; 1. Object exists
  ;; 2. Controller has life >= required amount
  ;; Note: Can pay life even if it would reduce to 0 (MTG rules)
  (if-let [obj-eid (get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (let [controller-eid (d/q '[:find ?c .
                                :in $ ?e
                                :where [?e :object/controller ?c]]
                              db obj-eid)
          current-life (d/q '[:find ?life .
                              :in $ ?p
                              :where [?p :player/life ?life]]
                            db controller-eid)
          required (:pay-life cost)]
      (>= (or current-life 0) required))
    false))
```

### Step 2: Add pay-cost :pay-life method
```clojure
(defmethod pay-cost :pay-life [db object-id cost]
  ;; Deduct life from controller
  ;; Note: No can-pay? guard - by time pay-cost called, already validated
  (if-let [obj-eid (get-object-eid db object-id)]
    (let [controller-eid (d/q '[:find ?c .
                                :in $ ?e
                                :where [?e :object/controller ?c]]
                              db obj-eid)
          current-life (d/q '[:find ?life .
                              :in $ ?p
                              :where [?p :player/life ?life]]
                            db controller-eid)
          required (:pay-life cost)
          new-life (- current-life required)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add controller-eid :player/life new-life]]))
    db))
```

### Step 3: Update Polluted Delta card definition
File: src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs

```clojure
:ability/cost {:tap true
               :sacrifice-self true
               :pay-life 1}  ;; ADD THIS
```

### Step 4: Add tests
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/costs_test.cljs (or new file)

Tests:
1. test-pay-life-can-pay-with-sufficient-life
   - Player has 10 life, cost is 1
   - can-pay? returns true

2. test-pay-life-cannot-pay-with-insufficient-life  
   - Player has 0 life, cost is 1
   - can-pay? returns false

3. test-pay-life-can-pay-exact-life
   - Player has 1 life, cost is 1
   - can-pay? returns true (going to 0 is allowed)

4. test-pay-life-deducts-correctly
   - Player starts at 20 life, pays 1
   - Player ends at 19 life

## Success Criteria
- [ ] can-pay? :pay-life returns true when controller has >= required life
- [ ] can-pay? :pay-life returns false when controller has < required life
- [ ] pay-cost :pay-life deducts life from controller
- [ ] Polluted Delta ability cost includes :pay-life 1
- [ ] Activating Polluted Delta deducts 1 life from player
- [ ] Cannot activate Polluted Delta if player has 0 life
- [ ] Existing tests still pass

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO special-casing Polluted Delta - use generic :pay-life cost type
- ❌ NO checking life > 0 after payment (going to 0 is legal, loss check is separate)
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 2: Implement turn-based actions using event system
design:
## Goal
Wire up the event system to game phase transitions. Implement draw step and untap step as turn-based actions (game-rule triggers) that fire on phase entry events.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Context
Completed Task 1: Event bus and trigger registry infrastructure now exists.
- events.cljs has phase-entered-event helper
- trigger-registry.cljs has registration functions
- trigger-dispatch.cljs handles immediate vs stacked triggers

Key codebase findings:
- advance-phase is at events/game.cljs:188-200 - updates phase and clears mana pool
- start-turn is at events/game.cljs:221-236 - has inline untap via untap-all-permanents
- untap-all-permanents is at events/game.cljs:203-218 - untaps all tapped permanents
- effects.cljs:191-222 has :draw effect that handles empty library (sets :game/loss-condition)
- Turn 1 is NOT stored in DB at init - current turn is in game-state

## Implementation

### 1. Study current phase handling (15 min)
- Read events/game.cljs:188-236 (advance-phase, start-turn, untap-all-permanents)
- Note: start-turn calls untap-all-permanents inline - we will move this to trigger
- Note: advance-phase just changes phase, doesn't fire events yet

### 2. Create turn-based trigger registration function
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/turn_based.cljs (new file)

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.turn-based
  "Turn-based game actions implemented as game-rule triggers."
  (:require
    [fizzle.engine.trigger-registry :as registry]))

(defn register-turn-based-actions!
  "Register all turn-based action triggers.
   Call once at game initialization."
  []
  ;; Draw step - draw a card (skip turn 1)
  (registry/register-trigger!
    {:trigger/id :game-rule-draw
     :trigger/source :game-rule
     :trigger/type :draw-step
     :trigger/event-type :phase-entered
     :trigger/filter {:event/phase :draw}
     :trigger/uses-stack? false
     :trigger/controller :player-1})  ; Active player
  
  ;; Untap step - untap all permanents
  (registry/register-trigger!
    {:trigger/id :game-rule-untap
     :trigger/source :game-rule
     :trigger/type :untap-step
     :trigger/event-type :phase-entered
     :trigger/filter {:event/phase :untap}
     :trigger/uses-stack? false
     :trigger/controller :player-1}))
```

### 3. Implement resolve-trigger methods for turn-based actions
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs (add methods)

```clojure
;; Add at top: require effects, queries
;; (:require [fizzle.engine.effects :as effects]
;;           [fizzle.db.queries :as queries])

(defmethod resolve-trigger :draw-step
  [db trigger]
  (let [game-state (queries/get-game-state db)
        turn (:game/turn game-state)
        active-player (:trigger/controller trigger)]
    ;; Skip draw on turn 1 (MTG rules: play/draw rule)
    (if (and turn (> turn 1))
      (effects/execute-effect db active-player {:effect/type :draw :effect/amount 1})
      db)))

(defmethod resolve-trigger :untap-step
  [db trigger]
  (let [active-player (:trigger/controller trigger)]
    ;; untap-all-permanents already handles empty battlefield gracefully
    (game/untap-all-permanents db active-player)))
```

### 4. Modify advance-phase to produce and dispatch events
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs (modify advance-phase)

```clojure
;; Add requires at top:
;; (:require [fizzle.engine.events :as game-events]
;;           [fizzle.engine.trigger-dispatch :as dispatch])

(defn advance-phase
  "Advance to the next phase, clear mana pool, and dispatch phase-entered event."
  [db player-id]
  (let [game-state (queries/get-game-state db)
        game-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :game/id _]] db)
        current-phase (:game/phase game-state)
        new-phase (next-phase current-phase)
        turn (:game/turn game-state)]
    (-> db
        (mana/empty-pool player-id)
        (d/db-with [[:db/add game-eid :game/phase new-phase]])
        ;; NEW: Dispatch phase-entered event to fire turn-based actions
        (dispatch/dispatch-event (game-events/phase-entered-event new-phase turn player-id)))))
```

### 5. Remove inline untap from start-turn
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs (modify start-turn)

Keep start-turn but remove the untap-all-permanents call since untap now happens via trigger when advancing to :untap phase.

```clojure
(defn start-turn
  "Start a new turn: increment turn counter, set phase to untap,
   reset storm count and land plays to 1, clear mana pool.
   Untap happens via :untap-step trigger when phase changes."
  [db player-id]
  (let [game-state (queries/get-game-state db)
        game-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :game/id _]] db)
        player-eid (queries/get-player-eid db player-id)
        current-turn (or (:game/turn game-state) 0)]
    (-> db
        ;; REMOVED: (untap-all-permanents player-id) - now via trigger
        (mana/empty-pool player-id)
        (d/db-with [[:db/add game-eid :game/turn (inc current-turn)]
                    [:db/add game-eid :game/phase :untap]
                    [:db/add player-eid :player/storm-count 0]
                    [:db/add player-eid :player/land-plays-left 1]])
        ;; Dispatch untap phase event to fire turn-based actions
        (dispatch/dispatch-event (game-events/phase-entered-event :untap (inc current-turn) player-id)))))
```

### 6. Call register-turn-based-actions! at game init
File: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs (modify init-game-state)

Add call to register turn-based triggers before returning game state.

### 7. Write tests
File: src/test/fizzle/engine/turn_based_test.cljs (new file)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] advance-phase calls dispatch-event with phase-entered event (verify via test)
- [ ] :draw-step trigger fires on draw phase: test_draw_step_fires_on_draw_phase
- [ ] :draw-step skips turn 1: test_draw_step_skips_turn_1
- [ ] :draw-step draws one card turn 2+: test_draw_step_draws_card_turn_2
- [ ] :untap-step trigger fires on untap phase: test_untap_step_fires_on_untap_phase
- [ ] :untap-step untaps all permanents: test_untap_step_untaps_all_permanents
- [ ] :untap-step handles no permanents gracefully: test_untap_step_empty_battlefield
- [ ] Existing tests still pass: make test
- [ ] 8+ new tests for turn-based actions
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty Library Draw**
- effects.cljs:191-222 already handles this
- Sets :game/loss-condition to :empty-library
- :draw-step trigger relies on this - no additional handling needed
- Test: test_draw_step_empty_library_sets_loss_condition

**Edge Case: No Permanents to Untap**
- untap-all-permanents already handles empty result gracefully
- Returns unchanged db if no tapped permanents
- Test: test_untap_step_empty_battlefield

**Edge Case: Turn Counter**
- Turn 1 check: `(> turn 1)` handles both turn 1 and nil turn
- If turn is nil (shouldn't happen), draws nothing (safe)
- Test with explicit turn 1 and turn 2 scenarios

**Edge Case: Multiple Phase Advances**
- Each advance-phase call produces one event
- No risk of double-firing since dispatch is per event
- Test: test_advance_multiple_phases_fires_each_event

**Trigger Registration Timing**
- register-turn-based-actions! called at game init
- Must happen BEFORE first advance-phase call
- Verify: test_triggers_registered_at_init

**Reference: untap-all-permanents**
- Location: src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs:203-218
- Already handles edge cases (no permanents, nil player)
- Reuse directly in :untap-step trigger

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded phase logic in advance-phase (use registered triggers)
- ❌ NO breaking existing untap behavior (tests must still pass)
- ❌ NO duplicate untap logic (reuse untap-all-permanents)
- ❌ NO calling register-turn-based-actions! multiple times (call once at init)
- ❌ NO skipping turn 1 check for draw (MTG rules requirement)
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: Implement Orim's Chant with Kicker and Restriction System
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Orim's Chant card definition: {W} instant with Kicker {W}
- Target player targeting: spell targets a player at cast time
- Base effect: targeted player cannot cast spells this turn
- Kicked effect: additionally, creatures cannot attack this turn
- Kicker as casting mode: get-casting-modes returns both normal and kicked modes when in hand
- Player grants system: extend grants to work on player entities via :player/grants
- Restriction grant type: :restriction grants prevent specific actions
- Cannot-cast-spells enforcement: can-cast? checks player restrictions before allowing casting
- Grant expiration: player grants expire at cleanup phase (extend expire-grants)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Orim's Chant card exists in src/fizzle/cards/ with correct oracle text
- [ ] Casting without kicker costs {W} and prevents target player from casting
- [ ] Casting with kicker costs {WW} and prevents casting AND attacking
- [ ] Player can choose between normal and kicked mode at cast time
- [ ] Restriction expires at end of turn (cleanup phase)
- [ ] Player with cannot-cast-spells restriction cannot cast any spells
- [ ] Bot can cast Orim's Chant against player (both scenarios work)
- [ ] Player can cast Orim's Chant against opponent (both scenarios work)
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded Orim's Chant logic in can-cast? (extensibility: use generic restriction checking)
- ❌ NO kicker as special-case flag on spell (consistency: treat as casting mode like flashback)
- ❌ NO separate expiration system for player grants (consistency: extend existing expire-grants)
- ❌ NO skipping player targeting (oracle-accuracy: card says 'target player')
- ❌ NO implementing attack prevention enforcement (scope: combat system doesn't exist yet)

## Approach
Implement Orim's Chant by extending three existing systems:

1. **Casting modes** - Kicker becomes an additional mode in get-casting-modes. When a card has :card/kicker, the function returns both a :primary mode (base cost, base effects) and a :kicked mode (combined cost, kicked effects). Player chooses at cast time.

2. **Grants system** - Extend grants to work on player entities. Add :player/grants attribute to schema. Add add-player-grant and get-player-grants functions. Extend expire-grants to process both object and player grants.

3. **Restriction checking** - New :add-restriction effect type creates a :restriction grant on target player. The can-cast? function checks for :cannot-cast-spells restrictions before allowing any spell.

## Architecture
### New Files
- src/fizzle/cards/orims_chant.cljs - Card definition

### Modified Files
- src/fizzle/db/schema.cljs - Add :player/grants attribute
- src/fizzle/engine/grants.cljs - Add player grant functions, extend expiration
- src/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs - Extend get-casting-modes for kicker, add restriction check to can-cast?
- src/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Add :add-restriction effect type
- src/fizzle/engine/turn_based.cljs - Extend cleanup to expire player grants

### Data Flow
1. Cast Orim's Chant → choose mode (normal/kicked) → pay costs → spell on stack
2. Resolve → execute :add-restriction effect → create grant on target player
3. Target tries to cast → can-cast? finds restriction → returns false
4. End of turn → cleanup → expire-grants removes player grants

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Orim's Chant is a key interactive card for storm practice. Without it, players can't practice against hate cards or use it as protection. It requires kicker (new system) and restrictions (new system).

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- grants.cljs:1-150 - Existing grant system with expiration, source tracking, type filtering
- rules.cljs:62-83 - Casting modes system (primary + alternates like flashback)
- rules.cljs:139-157 - can-cast? is the enforcement point for casting eligibility
- schema.cljs:46-52 - Player entities have :player/id, :player/life, etc.

**External:**
- Orim's Chant oracle: {W} instant, Kicker {W}, target player can't cast spells, if kicked creatures can't attack
- Kicker is optional additional cost paid at cast time, checked at resolution

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Kicker as additional casting mode ✓

**What it is:** Treat kicker like flashback - an alternate way to cast the spell with different costs and effects. get-casting-modes returns both :primary and :kicked modes.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed rules.cljs:62-83 - modes already support different costs and can have different destinations
- Reviewed rules.cljs:226-245 - mode stored on object, used at resolution
- Pattern matches how flashback works (different cost, different effects/destination)

**Pros:**
- Consistent with existing alternate cost pattern
- No new 'kicked' flag needed - mode selection is the mechanism
- UI already handles mode selection

**Cons:**
- Kicked effects must be complete (include base effects)

**Chosen because:** Consistent with flashback pattern, leverages existing mode infrastructure

#### 2. Kicker as boolean flag on spell ❌

**What it is:** Add :object/kicked flag, check at resolution to conditionally add effects.

**Why we looked at this:** Initial thought was simpler - just track if kicker was paid.

**Investigation:**
- Would require new resolution logic to check flag
- Inconsistent with how flashback (alternate cost) works
- Would need UI changes for a new pattern

**Pros:**
- Simpler card definition

**Cons:**
- Inconsistent with alternate cost pattern
- New mechanism when modes already exist

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Modes already solve cost/effect variation. Adding parallel system is unnecessary complexity.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Modes prove fundamentally incompatible with kicker semantics.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Orim's Chant card definition
- Generic kicker system via modes
- Player grants with restrictions
- cannot-cast-spells enforcement

**Out of scope (deferred/never):**
- Combat system (cannot-attack grant created but not enforced)
- Other kicker cards (system supports them, not implementing)
- Multiple kicker (multikicker) - different mechanic

### Open Questions
- UI for mode selection when casting - assume existing mode UI handles it
- Bot decision making for when to kick - defer to bot AI implementation

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Who casts Orim's Chant? | Both player and opponent | Full targeting needed, not simplified |
| Kicker importance? | Implement full kicker | Need kicker system, not just base spell |
| Effect scope? | Target player only (oracle-accurate) | Must use targeting system |
| Kicker design? | Generic kicker system | Reusable :card/kicker attribute |
| Restriction model? | Expand grants system | Add :player/grants, :restriction grant type |
| Kicker mechanism? | As casting mode | get-casting-modes returns both modes |
| Attack restriction? | Add grant only | Create grant but no enforcement (no combat) |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Grants System Analysis
**Question explored:** Can grants work for player restrictions?
**Sources consulted:**
- grants.cljs:1-150 - Full implementation
- grants.cljs:21-34 - get-grants, get-grants-by-type
- grants.cljs:112-135 - grant-expired? with turn/phase checking
- grants.cljs:138-150 - expire-grants processing

**Findings:**
- Grants currently only work on objects (via :object/grants)
- Expiration logic is generic - just needs turn/phase
- Source tracking built-in for removal effects
- Would need parallel :player/grants attribute

**Conclusion:** Extend grants to players rather than new system - reuses expiration, source tracking

#### Casting Modes Analysis
**Question explored:** How do alternate costs work?
**Sources consulted:**
- rules.cljs:35-42 - alternate-to-mode conversion
- rules.cljs:45-59 - get-alternate-modes filtering by zone
- rules.cljs:62-83 - get-casting-modes combining primary + alternates

**Findings:**
- Modes specify cost, zone, and on-resolve destination
- Mode stored on object at cast time
- Resolution uses mode for destination, get-active-effects for effects
- Could extend mode to include effects override

**Conclusion:** Kicker as mode fits naturally - just another way to cast from hand with different cost

### Dead-End Paths

#### Custom :object/kicked flag
**Why explored:** Seemed simpler than mode system
**Investigation:**
- Would need new field on objects
- Resolution would need conditional logic
- Inconsistent with how flashback works

**Why abandoned:** Modes already handle cost/effect variation, no need for parallel mechanism

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'What about combat system?' → Deferred - add grant but no enforcement
- 'What about other kicker cards?' → System supports them, not in scope
- 'UI for mode selection?' → Assume existing mode UI handles it
close_reason: All requirements implemented and manually validated. Kicker system, player grants, restriction checking, and Orim's Chant card all working correctly.

title: Rename :selection/count to :selection/select-count and add schema docstring
design:
## Goal
Rename all occurrences of :selection/count to :selection/select-count in source and test files, remove the dual-set pattern, and add canonical schema docstring.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours (mechanical rename across 30 known occurrences + docstring)

## Implementation Checklist

### Step 1: Inventory verification
- Run grep for :selection/count across src/ and test/
- Confirm 30 occurrences match the list below before changing anything

### Step 2: Update builders in selection.cljs (7 changes)
- Line 130: DELETE this line entirely (was dual-set :selection/count for backwards compat)
- Line 131: KEEP this line (already :selection/select-count) - remove the comment about "New key for multi-select"
- Line 327: :selection/count -> :selection/select-count (discard builder)
- Line 361: :selection/count -> :selection/select-count (graveyard-return builder)
- Line 659: :selection/count -> :selection/select-count (post-discard chained selection)
- Line 739: :selection/count -> :selection/select-count (post-graveyard chained selection)
- Line 786: :selection/count -> :selection/select-count (chained selection builder)
- Line 1351: :selection/count -> :selection/select-count (chained selection builder)

### Step 3: Update consumers in selection.cljs (3 changes + 1 simplification)
- Lines 990-999: SIMPLIFY the cond in toggle-selection. Currently:
    (cond
      (= selection-type :pile-choice)
      (get selection :selection/hand-count 1)
      (get selection :selection/select-count)
      (get selection :selection/select-count)
      :else
      (get selection :selection/count 0))
  After rename, the middle clause is redundant. Simplify to:
    (cond
      (= selection-type :pile-choice)
      (get selection :selection/hand-count 1)
      :else
      (get selection :selection/select-count 0))
- Line 1024: :selection/count -> :selection/select-count (confirm-discard reader)
- Line 1144: :selection/count -> :selection/select-count (confirm-graveyard reader)

### Step 4: Update game.cljs (1 change)
- Line 294: :selection/count -> :selection/select-count (cleanup-discard builder)

### Step 5: Update views/modals.cljs (2 changes)
- Line 232: :selection/count -> :selection/select-count (card-selection-modal reader)
- Line 366: :selection/count -> :selection/select-count (graveyard-selection-modal reader)

### Step 6: Update test files (17 occurrences across 6 files)
- careful_study_test.cljs: lines 295, 364, 371 (3 occurrences)
- polluted_delta_test.cljs: lines 188, 213, 232, 251, 331, 357 (6 occurrences)
- turn_test.cljs: lines 336, 358, 497 (3 occurrences)
- ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs: line 173 (1 occurrence)
- graveyard_selection_test.cljs: line 84 (1 occurrence)
- merchant_scroll_test.cljs: lines 301, 326, 355 (3 occurrences)

### Step 7: Add canonical schema docstring
Add a top-of-namespace docstring to selection.cljs documenting the 10 selection types:
1. Tutor (:effect-type :tutor) - zone, select-count, exact?, shuffle?, allow-fail-to-find, candidates, target-zone
2. Discard (:effect-type :discard) - zone :hand, select-count, player-id
3. Cleanup Discard (:effect-type :cleanup-discard) - zone :hand, select-count, player-id
4. Graveyard Return (:type :graveyard-return) - zone :graveyard, select-count, min-count, target-zone
5. Scry (:type :scry) - cards, top-pile, bottom-pile
6. Pile Choice (:type :pile-choice) - candidates, hand-count, selected, bottom-pile
7. Peek-and-Select (:effect-type :peek-and-select) - select-count, candidates
8. Exile Cards Cost (:effect-type :exile-cards-cost) - select-count, candidate-ids
9. X Mana Cost (:effect-type :x-mana-cost) - max-x, selected-x
10. Targeting (:type :cast-time-targeting / :ability-targeting) - target-requirement, valid-targets, selected-target

### Step 8: Verify
- grep -r ':selection/count[^-]' src/ test/ returns zero results
- grep for :selection/hand-count and :selection/min-count still present (NOT renamed)
- make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Zero occurrences of :selection/count in src/ (verified by grep)
- [ ] Zero occurrences of :selection/count in test/ (verified by grep)
- [ ] :selection/hand-count and :selection/min-count are UNCHANGED (different concepts)
- [ ] Dual-set pattern removed from tutor builder (line 130 deleted)
- [ ] toggle-selection cond simplified (redundant middle clause removed)
- [ ] Schema docstring added to selection.cljs namespace
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all 831+ tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**CRITICAL: Do NOT rename similar-looking keys**
- :selection/hand-count is pile choice hand size (different concept, NOT a duplicate)
- :selection/min-count is minimum allowed selection (different concept, NOT a duplicate)
- Only :selection/count is being renamed to :selection/select-count

**Edge Case: toggle-selection cond simplification**
Lines 990-999 have a 3-branch cond that checks :selection/select-count first, then falls back to :selection/count. After the rename, both the middle and :else branches would read the same key. The middle branch becomes redundant and should be removed, simplifying to a 2-branch cond. This is not just a rename but a small logic simplification - verify the default value (0) is correct for all selection types that flow through this path.

**Edge Case: Line number drift**
Line numbers in this checklist are from the current file state. If you make changes top-to-bottom in a single file, later line numbers may drift. Work from bottom to top within each file, or use search-and-replace on the specific key patterns.

**Verification approach: grep pattern**
Use :selection/count[^-] as the grep pattern (negative lookahead for hyphen) to match :selection/count but NOT :selection/hand-count or :selection/min-count.

## Anti-patterns
- NO renaming :selection/hand-count or :selection/min-count (different concepts entirely)
- NO adding a backwards-compat reader that checks both keys
- NO changing :selection/type or :selection/effect-type discriminators (separate ticket)
- NO structural changes to selection flow beyond the cond simplification
close_reason: Renamed :selection/count to :selection/select-count across 30 occurrences. Removed dual-set pattern. Simplified toggle-selection cond. Added schema docstring. All 831 tests pass.

title: Epic: Generic storm copy target splitting
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

1. Brain Freeze (and any future targeted storm spell) allows choosing target player (self or opponent) at cast time
2. After storm trigger resolves, player sees a split UI showing copy count with stepper controls per valid target
3. Each storm copy gets its individually assigned target stored on its stack-item
4. Mill effect reads target from stack-item (not hardcoded :opponent)
5. Yield All pauses for the storm split selection (interactive effect)
6. Infrastructure is generic — any targeted storm spell gets split targeting automatically
7. Split UI uses +/- stepper controls per target, total must equal copy count

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Brain Freeze cast-time prompts for target player selection
- [ ] Brain Freeze resolution mills the chosen target (not always opponent)
- [ ] Storm trigger for targeted spell shows split selection UI
- [ ] Stepper controls allow distributing copies across opponent and self
- [ ] Total allocation enforced to equal copy count
- [ ] Each storm copy resolves against its assigned target
- [ ] Yield All pauses for storm split (detected as interactive)
- [ ] Non-targeted storm spells (future) still auto-create copies with no split UI
- [ ] All existing tests pass (brain_freeze_test, storm_test, etc.)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoding Brain Freeze-specific logic in storm resolution (genericity: must work for any targeted storm spell)
- ❌ NO per-copy individual targeting UI (UX: split count UI was chosen for practice speed)
- ❌ NO modifying create-spell-copy to always require targets (backwards compatibility: non-targeted storm spells must still work unchanged)
- ❌ NO storing split assignment in game state atoms (immutability: split state lives in pending-selection, not mutable state)
- ❌ NO skipping interactive-effect? detection for storm-copies (consistency: Yield All must pause for this)

## Approach
Make Brain Freeze a properly targeted spell with :card/targeting. At cast time, player-target selection fires and stores the chosen player on the stack-item. The mill effect reads its target from the stored target (via resolve-effect-target) instead of hardcoded :opponent.

For storm copies, intercept the storm stack-item resolution in resolve-one-item (game.cljs). When the source spell has :card/targeting, build a :storm-split selection instead of immediately creating copies. The split UI shows stepper controls per valid target. On confirm, create copies with individual target overrides via an enhanced create-spell-copy.

Follow the mana-allocation pattern: non-card-picking selection with accumulating state in the selection map, custom event handlers for stepper interaction, and auto-confirm when allocation is complete.

## Architecture

### Card Definition Change
Brain Freeze gains :card/targeting and loses hardcoded :effect/target:
- Add {:card/targeting {:targeting/type :player :targeting/options [:opponent :self]}}
- Effect becomes {:effect/type :mill :effect/amount 3} (no :effect/target)
- Target resolved from stack-item at resolution time

### Storm Resolution Intercept
game.cljs resolve-one-item (line 354-394):
- Currently routes :storm stack-items to resolve-stack-item-effects
- New: detect if source spell has :card/targeting → route to storm-split selection
- Non-targeted storm spells continue through existing path unchanged

### New Selection Type: :storm-split
New domain module: events/selection/storm.cljs
- build-storm-split-selection: builds selection with copy count, valid targets, initial allocation
- execute-confirmed-selection :storm-split: creates copies with assigned targets
- Event handlers: ::adjust-storm-split (stepper), ::reset-storm-split

Selection state shape:
  {:selection/type :storm-split
   :selection/copy-count N
   :selection/valid-targets [opponent-eid player-eid]
   :selection/allocation {opponent-eid N, player-eid 0}
   :selection/source-object-id spell-eid
   :selection/controller-id player-eid
   :selection/stack-item-eid storm-si-eid
   :selection/remaining-effects [...]}

### create-spell-copy Enhancement
engine/triggers.cljs:
- Add optional target-override parameter to create-spell-copy
- When provided, use override instead of inheriting from original stack-item
- Signature: (create-spell-copy db source-id controller) or (create-spell-copy db source-id controller target-override)

### View Component
views/ — stepper component showing:
  [Opponent: 15  [-] [+]]
  [Self:      0  [-] [+]]
  [Confirm]

### Files Modified
1. cards/iggy_pop.cljs — Brain Freeze card definition
2. events/game.cljs — resolve-one-item storm routing
3. events/selection/storm.cljs — NEW: storm-split selection type
4. events/selection/resolution.cljs — interactive-effect? for storm-copies
5. engine/triggers.cljs — create-spell-copy target override
6. engine/effects.cljs — mill effect target resolution (if needed)
7. views/ — storm split UI component
8. subs/ — storm split subscriptions

## Parallelism Map

### Independent Work Streams
1. **Cast-time targeting** (cards/iggy_pop.cljs, engine/effects.cljs, events/selection/targeting.cljs)
   - Make Brain Freeze a targeted spell, mill reads stored target
   - Estimated complexity: medium
2. **Storm split selection** (events/selection/storm.cljs, events/selection/resolution.cljs)
   - New :storm-split type with builder, confirm handler, event handlers
   - Estimated complexity: high
3. **Storm resolution routing** (events/game.cljs, engine/triggers.cljs)
   - Intercept storm resolution, create-spell-copy target override
   - Estimated complexity: medium
4. **Split UI** (views/, subs/)
   - Stepper component and subscriptions
   - Estimated complexity: medium

### Stream Dependencies
- Stream 2 and 3 depend on Stream 1 (Brain Freeze must be targeted first)
- Stream 4 depends on Stream 2 (UI needs selection type to exist)
- Streams 2 and 3 are partially independent (selection type vs routing)

### Suggested Waves
- Wave 1: Stream 1 (cast-time targeting foundation)
- Wave 2: Streams 2 + 3 (selection type + routing — partially independent)
- Wave 3: Stream 4 (UI component)

### File Ownership Boundaries
| Stream | Owns (exclusive) | Shared (needs coordination) |
|--------|-------------------|-----------------------------|
| Cast-time targeting | iggy_pop.cljs, effects.cljs | — |
| Storm split selection | selection/storm.cljs | resolution.cljs (interactive-effect?) |
| Storm resolution routing | game.cljs routing | triggers.cljs (create-spell-copy) |
| Split UI | views/storm_split.cljs, subs/ | — |

### Parallelism Assessment
- Independent streams: 2 (after Wave 1 completes)
- Recommendation: solo execution
- Rationale: Streams 2 and 3 share conceptual coupling (selection type informs routing). Each stream is moderate complexity but tightly integrated. Solo execution with TDD ensures correctness at each step.

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Brain Freeze hardcodes :effect/target :opponent, preventing self-targeting (a real Premodern play pattern for threshold/flashback). Storm copies all inherit the same target with no split option. Players need to distribute copies across targets for optimal play.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:31-44 — Brain Freeze definition, no :card/targeting, hardcoded :opponent
- events/selection/resolution.cljs:23-40 — interactive-effect? predicate, 5 detection cases
- events/selection/core.cljs:35-54 — build-selection-for-effect multimethod, 6 dispatch values
- events/game.cljs:301-325 — resolve-stack-item-effects handles storm-copies in reduce loop
- events/game.cljs:354-394 — resolve-one-item routes storm to resolve-stack-item-effects
- engine/triggers.cljs:35-88 — create-spell-copy inherits targets from original stack-item
- events/selection/costs.cljs:125-156 — mana-allocation pattern: non-card selection with accumulating state

**Confirmed:** Mana allocation is the closest existing pattern for a non-card-picking selection type. Storm split should follow its conventions.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Split count UI with generic storm targeting ✓
**What it is:** Show a stepper-based split UI after storm trigger resolves. Each copy gets individually assigned target. Generic infrastructure works for any targeted storm spell.

**Investigation:**
- Mana allocation proves non-card selection types work cleanly
- Selection system multimethod dispatch supports arbitrary types
- create-spell-copy can accept target overrides with minimal change

**Pros:**
- Fast UX for practice (one dialog, not N clicks)
- Generic (Grapeshot, future cards get it free)
- Follows mana-allocation pattern
- Integrates with Yield All via interactive-effect?

**Cons:**
- New selection domain module needed
- Storm resolution routing needs modification

**Chosen because:** Balances MTG rules fidelity with practice tool speed. Generic infrastructure avoids future rework.

#### 2. Per-copy individual targeting ❌
**What it is:** Each storm copy prompts individually for its target player.

**Why we looked at this:** Most faithful to MTG rules (each copy is a separate spell with its own target).

**Pros:**
- Perfect MTG rules fidelity
- Simpler implementation (reuse player-target selection)

**Cons:**
- Terrible UX at storm count 15+ (15 clicks)
- Unusable for a practice tool focused on speed

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** UX is unacceptable for a practice tool. Brain Freeze at storm 15 means 15 individual target prompts.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We add an "advanced mode" for rules purists.

#### 3. Default-to-opponent with redirect ❌
**What it is:** All copies default to opponent. Player can click individual copies in the stack view to redirect to self.

**Why we looked at this:** Minimal UI change, leverages existing stack display.

**Pros:**
- No new selection type needed
- Works within existing UI

**Cons:**
- Stack view isn't designed for interaction
- Hard to redirect specific copies in a stack of 15 identical items
- No clear UX for "which ones did I redirect?"

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Stack view interaction is not designed for per-item editing. Confusing UX.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Stack view is redesigned to support per-item interactions.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Brain Freeze player targeting at cast time
- Storm copy split UI with stepper
- Generic infrastructure for targeted storm spells
- Mill effect target resolution from stack-item
- Yield All interaction

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Multi-target spells (e.g., spells targeting multiple permanents) — different feature
- Grapeshot implementation (card doesn't exist yet, but infrastructure supports it)
- Split targeting for non-storm copies (e.g., Fork/replicate) — different mechanic

### Open Questions
- How does resolve-effect-target enrich mill effect with stored target? (resolve during Task 1)
- Does the storm stack-item need the source spell's card-id to check targeting? (resolve during Task 2)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Storm copy targeting UX? | Split count UI | New :storm-split selection type with stepper |
| Original spell targeted too? | Yes | Add :card/targeting to Brain Freeze |
| Generic or BF-specific? | Generic | Infrastructure in storm resolution path, not card-specific |
| Split UI interaction model? | Slider/stepper | +/- buttons per target, total = copy count |
| Yield All interaction? | Pauses for split | Must register as interactive effect |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Storm Resolution Path
**Question explored:** Where do storm copies get created and how do they get targets?
**Sources consulted:**
- game.cljs:301-325 — resolve-stack-item-effects
- game.cljs:354-394 — resolve-one-item routing
- triggers.cljs:35-88 — create-spell-copy with target inheritance

**Findings:**
- Storm META stack-item routes through "Other" path (not spell resolution)
- Individual storm copies route through spell resolution path
- create-spell-copy inherits targets via Datascript query on original stack-item
- Target inheritance was added in the recent target storage unification epic

**Conclusion:** Intercept at resolve-one-item level, route targeted storm to split selection

#### Mana Allocation Pattern
**Question explored:** How do non-card-picking selections work?
**Sources consulted:**
- events/selection/costs.cljs:125-156 — build-mana-allocation-selection
- events/selection/costs.cljs:310-372 — allocation event handlers

**Findings:**
- Uses :selection/zone :mana-pool (not a game zone)
- Accumulates state in selection map (:selection/allocation)
- Custom event handlers for discrete interactions
- Auto-confirms when condition met
- Works with confirm-selection-impl wrapper

**Conclusion:** Storm split should follow this pattern exactly

### Dead-End Paths
None — the chosen approach aligns cleanly with existing architecture.

### Open Concerns Raised
- Storm resolution path is in the "Other" route, not spell resolution — addressed by intercepting in resolve-one-item
- Mill effect needs to read stored target — addressed by resolve-effect-target enrichment or card definition change
close_reason: All success criteria met. Brain Freeze targeting, storm split selection, stepper UI, and routing all implemented. 1367 tests passing, make validate clean.

title: Feature: Implement Flash of Insight
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Flash of Insight card implemented with exact oracle text behavior
- X cost system: generic support for X in mana costs (reusable by other cards)
- :peek-and-select effect: look at top X, select N for hand, remainder to destination zone
- :exile-cards cost type: pay costs by exiling cards matching criteria (reusable)
- Normal cast: {X}{1}{U} - peek top X, pick 1 for hand, rest to bottom (random order)
- Flashback cast: {1}{U} + exile X blue cards from graveyard - same effect with X = exiled count
- Card added to Iggy Pop deck pool

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Flash of Insight castable from hand with X cost working correctly
- [ ] Flash of Insight castable from graveyard via flashback
- [ ] X cost system works generically (can-pay?, pay-mana extended)
- [ ] :peek-and-select effect shows top X cards, player selects 1 for hand
- [ ] Non-selected cards go to bottom of library in random order
- [ ] :exile-cards cost type validates and executes correctly
- [ ] Flashback exiles Flash of Insight after resolution
- [ ] Card appears in Iggy Pop deck pool
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] make validate passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoded X handling for Flash of Insight only (reusability: X cost system must be generic)
- ❌ NO auto-selecting when only 1 card peeked (consistency: always show selection UI, allow fail-to-find)
- ❌ NO player-ordered bottom of library (scope: user chose random/shuffle for simplicity)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in engine files (architecture: cards are data, engine interprets)
- ❌ NO mocking cost behavior in tests (validation: test real integration with can-pay?)
- ❌ NO skipping flashback mana cost (correctness: flashback is {1}{U} PLUS exile, not just exile)

## Approach
Implement three new generic systems plus the card definition:
1. X Cost System in mana.cljs - extend can-pay? and pay-mana to handle :x in mana costs
2. :peek-and-select effect in effects.cljs + game.cljs - new effect type for top-of-library selection
3. :exile-cards cost type in rules.cljs - new additional cost for exiling cards by criteria
4. Card definition in cards/flash_of_insight.cljs following existing patterns

## Architecture
- engine/mana.cljs: Add X cost support to can-pay?, pay-mana, calculate-cmc
- engine/effects.cljs: Add :peek-and-select multimethod (returns db, signals selection)
- events/game.cljs: Add build-peek-selection and execute-peek-selection
- engine/rules.cljs: Add :exile-cards to additional costs system
- cards/flash_of_insight.cljs: Card definition as pure EDN data
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs: Add flash-of-insight to card pool

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Flash of Insight is a key card in Iggy Pop storm deck. It requires X cost support which doesn't exist, plus a peek-and-select mechanic distinct from tutoring.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- engine/mana.cljs:25-42 - can-pay? and pay-mana functions to extend
- events/game.cljs:947-977 - build-tutor-selection pattern to follow
- cards/deep_analysis.cljs:26-30 - flashback with additional costs pattern
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:306-317 - Intuition multi-select pattern

**External:**
- Scryfall API - Confirmed oracle: {X}{1}{U}, Flashback {1}{U} + exile X blue

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. New :peek-and-select effect type ✓
**What it is:** Distinct effect type for looking at top N cards and selecting some for hand.
**Chosen because:** Semantic clarity (peek \!= tutor), reusable for Impulse/Anticipate/Telling Time.

#### 2. Extend tutor system ❌
**What it is:** Add :source :top-of-library and :remainder-zone keys to existing tutor.
**Why explored:** Less new code, reuses selection UI.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Conceptual overload - tutor means search library, not peek top.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Tutor system is being redesigned anyway.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Generic X cost system
- Generic :peek-and-select effect
- Generic :exile-cards cost
- Flash of Insight card

**Out of scope:**
- Player-ordered bottom of library (deferred - random is simpler)
- Other X-cost cards (deferred - system ready for them)

### Open Questions
- Minimum X value enforcement (X>=1?) - decide during implementation

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| X cost infrastructure scope? | Full generic system | Must work for any X-cost card, not just Flash of Insight |
| Bottom of library ordering? | Random/shuffle | No ordering UI needed, use shuffle-remainder? true |
| Exile cost scope? | Generic :exile-cards type | Reusable for Force of Will, Unmask, pitch spells |
| Effect type approach? | New :peek-and-select | Distinct from tutor, clearer semantics |

### Research Deep-Dives
#### Existing Selection Patterns
**Sources:** game.cljs:947-1647, merchant_scroll_test.cljs, intuition_test.cljs
**Findings:** build-tutor-selection creates candidates, execute-tutor-selection moves cards. Pile-choice adds second selection phase. Pattern reusable for peek-and-select.

### Dead-End Paths
#### Extend Tutor System
**Why explored:** Less code, existing UI
**Why abandoned:** Semantic mismatch - tutor searches library, peek looks at top N

### Open Concerns Raised
- Oracle text mismatch initially → Corrected flashback to {1}{U} + exile (not just exile)
close_reason: All success criteria met. Flash of Insight fully implemented with X cost system, peek-and-select effect, exile-cards cost type, and UI selection flows. 715 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Eliminate duplicate resolve path for stack items
description:
## The Problem

There are two stack resolution functions in `events/game.cljs`:

1. `resolve-top-of-stack` (lines 311-334) — the simple path, doesn't handle selections
2. `resolve-one-item` (lines 337-379) — the selection-aware path, returns `{:db}` or `{:db :pending-selection}`

Both have multi-branch `cond` dispatch on stack-item properties:
- Has `:stack-item/object-ref`? → spell/storm copy path
- Is `:activated-ability` type? → ability path  
- Other stack-item? → generic effects path
- Empty stack? → no-op

### Why this matters

If you add a new stack-item type, you must update both functions. If you change resolution semantics, you must change both. They can drift apart silently.

### The Rich Hickey question

"Do we have one thing or two?" If `resolve-top-of-stack` is never called, it's dead code. If it is called, it's a simpler path that can't handle player choices — which means some resolutions silently skip interactivity. Either way, having two is worse than having one.

### Proposed direction (needs brainstorming)

- Determine if `resolve-top-of-stack` is actually used anywhere. If not, remove it.
- If both are needed, extract the dispatch logic into a single multimethod:
  ```clojure
  (defmulti resolve-stack-item :stack-item/type)
  ```
  Both functions would call this, with `resolve-one-item` adding the selection-awareness wrapper.
- The `cond` dispatch in `resolve-one-item` on `:stack-item/object-ref` is really checking "is this a spell?" — could be explicit via `:stack-item/type` dispatch instead.

### Files involved

- `events/game.cljs` — both resolve functions (lines 284-379)
- `events/selection.cljs` — `resolve-spell-with-selection`, `resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection`
- `engine/stack.cljs` — could host the multimethod

### Risks

- Low risk if `resolve-top-of-stack` is unused (just deletion)
- Medium risk if it's used — need to understand all callers and why they chose the simple path

title: Implement threshold condition and Cabal Ritual
design:
## Goal

Implement the threshold condition system and Cabal Ritual card with conditional effects. Threshold is a common Premodern mechanic that checks if you have 7+ cards in graveyard.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context

Completed:
- Mill effect (fizzle-wkb)
- Storm trigger infrastructure
- Brain Freeze with storm

Epic requires threshold for Cabal Ritual: BBB normally, BBBBB with threshold.

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

Reference implementations:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - effect multimethod pattern
- src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:79-90 - get-objects-in-zone for graveyard counting
- Epic's architecture section describes conditional effects design

### 2. Write tests first (TDD)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/conditions_test.cljs (new)

Test cases for threshold:
- [ ] test-threshold-false-with-zero-cards: Empty graveyard returns false (catches: incorrect boundary)
- [ ] test-threshold-false-with-six-cards: 6 cards returns false (catches: off-by-one)
- [ ] test-threshold-true-with-seven-cards: 7 cards returns true (catches: boundary handling)
- [ ] test-threshold-true-with-many-cards: 10+ cards returns true (catches: only-exact-7 bug)
- [ ] test-threshold-only-counts-owner-graveyard: Player 1's graveyard doesn't count opponent's cards (catches: wrong player)

Test file: src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs (add to existing)

Test cases for conditional effects:
- [ ] test-cabal-ritual-without-threshold: Resolving Cabal Ritual with <7 graveyard adds BBB
- [ ] test-cabal-ritual-with-threshold: Resolving Cabal Ritual with 7+ graveyard adds BBBBB
- [ ] test-threshold-checked-at-resolution: Milling to 7+ during resolution enables threshold for that effect (catches: check at wrong time)

### 3. Implementation checklist

Create new file src/main/fizzle/engine/conditions.cljs:
- [ ] (defn threshold? [db player-id])
  - Count cards in player's graveyard via get-objects-in-zone
  - Return true if count >= 7, false otherwise

Add to src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs (if not already present):
- [ ] (defn count-graveyard [db player-id])
  - Simple: (count (get-objects-in-zone db player-id :graveyard))
  - Or direct query for efficiency

Add conditional effect handling to src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs:
- [ ] Modify resolve-spell to check :card/conditional-effects
- [ ] If card has :card/conditional-effects map, select effects based on conditions
- [ ] Pattern: {:threshold effects-with-threshold, :default effects-without}
- [ ] Call conditions/threshold? to determine which effect set to use

Add to src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:
- [ ] def cabal-ritual
```clojure
(def cabal-ritual
  {:card/id :cabal-ritual
   :card/name "Cabal Ritual"
   :card/cmc 2
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :black 1}
   :card/colors #{:black}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Add {B}{B}{B}. Threshold - Add {B}{B}{B}{B}{B} instead."
   :card/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 3}}]
   :card/conditional-effects {:threshold [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 5}}]}})
```

### 4. Key design decisions

**Effect selection pattern:**
- Card has :card/effects (default) and :card/conditional-effects (map of condition -> effects)
- At resolution, check conditions and select appropriate effect set
- If threshold? true and :card/conditional-effects has :threshold, use those
- Otherwise use :card/effects

**Threshold check timing:**
- Check at resolution time, not cast time
- Graveyard count can change between cast and resolve (e.g., from other effects)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] threshold? function returns correct boolean for boundary cases
- [ ] Cabal Ritual adds BBB without threshold
- [ ] Cabal Ritual adds BBBBB with threshold (7+ graveyard)
- [ ] Threshold checked at resolution time, not cast time
- [ ] 5 threshold condition tests pass
- [ ] 3 Cabal Ritual tests pass
- [ ] All existing tests still pass: make test
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass: make validate

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO condition checks inside effect methods (epic requirement: card-level modes)
- ❌ NO hardcoded Cabal Ritual logic in engine (card is data)
- ❌ NO threshold check at cast time (must be resolution time)
- ❌ NO off-by-one errors (threshold is >= 7, not > 7)
- ❌ NO counting opponent's graveyard for player's threshold
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 5: Migrate Cephalid Coliseum threshold ability to cast-time targeting
description:
## Goal
Migrate Cephalid Coliseum threshold ability to use :ability/targeting for cast-time target declaration, matching the pattern established in Phase 4 (Deep Analysis).

## Context
Phases 1-4 complete:
- Grant system implemented (grants.cljs)
- Cleanup system implemented (expire-grants trigger)
- Targeting foundation implemented (targeting.cljs)
- Deep Analysis migrated to cast-time targeting

Cephalid Coliseum threshold ability currently uses :effect/target :any-player in its effects. Need to migrate to :ability/targeting.

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add :ability/targeting to threshold ability
Update cephalid_coliseum.cljs threshold ability to add targeting:
```clojure
{:ability/type :activated
 :ability/name "Threshold"
 :ability/targeting [{:target/id :player
                      :target/type :player
                      :target/options [:self :opponent :any-player]
                      :target/required true}]
 ...}
```

### Step 2: Extend ability activation flow for targeting
Modify ability activation to:
- Check if ability has :ability/targeting
- Prompt for target selection at activation time
- Store targets on ability stack item

### Step 3: Update ability resolution to use stored targets
- Read targets from stored ability state
- Pass to effect execution

### Step 4: Write tests
- Test ability targeting extraction
- Test activation with target selection
- Test resolution uses stored target

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Threshold ability has :ability/targeting attribute
- [ ] targeting/get-targeting-requirements works with abilities
- [ ] Activating threshold prompts for player target before stack
- [ ] Target stored and used at resolution
- [ ] Existing Cephalid Coliseum tests pass
- [ ] make test passes

## Anti-Patterns
- Do NOT break existing tests
- Do NOT duplicate targeting logic - reuse from targeting.cljs
design:
## Goal
Migrate Cephalid Coliseum threshold ability to use :ability/targeting for cast-time target declaration, matching the pattern established in Phase 4 (Deep Analysis).

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Context
Phases 1-4 complete:
- Grant system implemented (grants.cljs)
- Cleanup system implemented (expire-grants trigger)
- Targeting foundation implemented (targeting.cljs)
- Deep Analysis migrated to cast-time targeting

Cephalid Coliseum threshold ability currently uses :effect/target :any-player in its effects (lines 47-50 of cephalid_coliseum.cljs). Need to migrate to :ability/targeting for cast-time target declaration.

The targeting module already supports :ability/targeting extraction (targeting.cljs line 20-22).

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: Add :ability/targeting to threshold ability (TDD)
**File:** src/main/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum.cljs

Add targeting requirement to threshold ability (second ability, line 40):
\`\`\`clojure
{:ability/type :activated
 :ability/name "Threshold"
 :ability/targeting [{:target/id :player
                      :target/type :player
                      :target/options [:self :opponent :any-player]
                      :target/required true}]
 :ability/cost {:tap true
                :sacrifice-self true
                :mana {:blue 1}}
 :ability/condition {:condition/type :threshold}
 :ability/description "Target player draws 3 cards, then discards 3 cards"
 :ability/effects [{:effect/type :draw
                    :effect/amount 3
                    :effect/target :any-player}
                   {:effect/type :discard
                    :effect/count 3
                    :effect/selection :player}]}
\`\`\`

Keep :effect/target :any-player during migration for backwards compat.

**Test:** Write test verifying targeting/get-targeting-requirements returns player target for threshold ability.

### Step 2: Extend activate-ability for targeting prompts
**File:** src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

**Location:** activate-ability function (lines 672-726)

Modify flow to:
1. After checking can-activate?, check if ability has :ability/targeting via targeting/get-targeting-requirements
2. If targeting exists AND requires selection:
   - Build target selection state (similar to build-cast-time-target-selection but for abilities)
   - Return {:db db :pending-ability-target-selection selection-state}
3. When target selection confirmed (new function confirm-ability-target), continue with original flow:
   - Pay costs
   - Add ability trigger to stack WITH stored targets

**New helper function needed:**
\`\`\`clojure
(defn build-ability-target-selection
  "Build selection state for ability target selection."
  [db player-id object-id ability-index target-req]
  (let [valid-targets (targeting/find-valid-targets db player-id target-req)]
    {:selection/type :ability-targeting
     :selection/player-id player-id
     :selection/object-id object-id
     :selection/ability-index ability-index
     :selection/target-requirement target-req
     :selection/valid-targets valid-targets
     :selection/selected-target nil}))
\`\`\`

**Modified activate-ability return:**
- With targeting: {:db db :pending-ability-target-selection selection}
- Without targeting: {:db db-with-trigger :pending-ability-target-selection nil}

### Step 3: Add confirm-ability-target function
**File:** src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

New function to complete ability activation after target selection:
\`\`\`clojure
(defn confirm-ability-target
  "Complete ability activation after target selection.
   1. Pays costs
   2. Creates ability trigger with stored targets
   3. Adds trigger to stack"
  [db selection]
  (let [object-id (:selection/object-id selection)
        ability-index (:selection/ability-index selection)
        target-ref (get-in selection [:selection/target-requirement :target/id])
        target-id (:selection/selected-target selection)
        ;; Get ability and pay costs
        obj (queries/get-object db object-id)
        card (:object/card obj)
        ability (nth (:card/abilities card) ability-index)
        db-after-costs (abilities/pay-all-costs db object-id (:ability/cost ability))]
    (if db-after-costs
      (let [;; Create trigger with targets
            effects-list (:ability/effects ability [])
            ability-trigger (triggers/create-trigger
                              :activated-ability
                              object-id
                              (:selection/player-id selection)
                              {:effects effects-list
                               :description (:ability/description ability)
