                               :targets {target-ref target-id}})
            db-with-trigger (triggers/add-trigger-to-stack db-after-costs ability-trigger)]
        {:db db-with-trigger
         :pending-ability-target-selection nil})
      {:db db :pending-ability-target-selection nil})))
\`\`\`

### Step 4: Update ability resolution to use stored targets
**File:** src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs or src/main/fizzle/engine/triggers.cljs

Modify resolve-trigger or resolve-activated-ability to:
1. Read targets from trigger data (:targets in trigger context)
2. When executing effects with :effect/target :any-player, use stored target instead of prompting

**Location to investigate:** resolve-activated-ability-with-selection function

### Step 5: Add re-frame event for confirming ability targets
**File:** src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

\`\`\`clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::confirm-ability-target
  (fn [db [_ target-id]]
    (let [selection (get-in db [:game/pending-selection])
          selection-with-target (assoc selection :selection/selected-target target-id)
          game-db (:game/db db)
          result (confirm-ability-target game-db selection-with-target)]
      (-> db
          (assoc :game/db (:db result))
          (dissoc :game/pending-selection)))))
\`\`\`

### Step 6: Write tests for ability targeting
**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs

New tests to add:
1. test_threshold_ability_has_targeting_requirement
2. test_threshold_activation_prompts_for_target
3. test_threshold_target_stored_on_resolution
4. test_threshold_activation_cancelled_on_empty_selection

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Threshold ability has :ability/targeting attribute with player target
- [ ] targeting/get-targeting-requirements returns 1 requirement for threshold ability
- [ ] Activating threshold ability returns :pending-ability-target-selection (not immediately on stack)
- [ ] confirm-ability-target completes activation with stored target
- [ ] :targets map stored in trigger context, used at resolution
- [ ] All 15 existing Cephalid Coliseum tests pass without modification
- [ ] 4 new tests pass confirming ability targeting
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + fmt + tests)

## Key Considerations

**Edge Case: No Valid Targets**
- If no players exist (edge case: game not fully initialized), find-valid-targets returns empty
- has-valid-targets? returns false
- can-activate? should be extended to check has-valid-targets? for abilities with targeting
- Test: Mock scenario where find-valid-targets returns empty

**Edge Case: Cancelled Target Selection**
- If player dismisses target selection UI (clicks away)
- Ability should NOT activate (costs not paid, nothing on stack)
- Current UI pattern: dissoc :game/pending-selection, return to normal game state
- Test: Verify no cost payment or stack addition if selection cancelled

**Edge Case: Ability Uses Target on Resolution**
- Current effects use :effect/target :any-player which prompts for selection
- With stored targets, resolution should use :targets from trigger context
- May need to update effect execution to check for stored targets first
- Keep :effect/target :any-player for backwards compat during migration

**Trigger Context Structure**
- Existing: {:effects [...] :description "..."}
- New: {:effects [...] :description "..." :targets {:player :player-1}}
- Resolution code must handle both old (no :targets) and new (with :targets) format

**Reference Implementation**
- Study cast-spell-with-targeting (game.cljs ~line 1464)
- Study confirm-cast-time-target (game.cljs ~line 1502)
- Study build-cast-time-target-selection (game.cljs ~line 1450)
- Follow same pattern for abilities

**Storm Copy Consideration (Future)**
- When abilities create copies on stack (not current), copies should inherit :targets
- Not blocking for this task, but design should allow it

## Anti-Patterns
- ❌ Do NOT break existing Cephalid Coliseum tests (migration preserves behavior)
- ❌ Do NOT remove :effect/target :any-player yet (needed for backwards compat during resolution)
- ❌ Do NOT modify targeting.cljs (already supports :ability/targeting)
- ❌ Do NOT hardcode player IDs - use stored target from selection/trigger
- ❌ Do NOT duplicate targeting logic - reuse build-*-target-selection patterns
- ❌ Do NOT use unwrap/expect patterns - handle nil gracefully
- ❌ Do NOT leave TODO comments without issue numbers
- ❌ Do NOT skip testing cancelled selection edge case

## Specific Test Cases (must implement)

1. test_threshold_ability_has_targeting_requirement
   - Catches: Missing :ability/targeting attribute on ability definition
   - Verifies: targeting/get-targeting-requirements returns vector with 1 player target for threshold ability
   - Assert: (= 1 (count (targeting/get-targeting-requirements threshold-ability)))

2. test_threshold_activation_prompts_for_target
   - Catches: Target prompt not happening at activation time
   - Verifies: activate-ability returns :pending-ability-target-selection instead of immediately placing on stack
   - Assert: (some? (:pending-ability-target-selection result))
   - Assert: (empty? (q/get-stack-items (:db result))) ;; not on stack yet

3. test_threshold_target_stored_and_used
   - Catches: Target not stored or not used at resolution
   - Verifies: After confirm-ability-target, trigger has :targets in context
   - Assert: (= {:player target-id} (get-in trigger [:context :targets]))

4. test_threshold_activation_cancelled_preserves_state
   - Catches: Partial activation on cancellation
   - Verifies: If selection dismissed, costs not paid, stack empty
   - Assert: Land still on battlefield (not sacrificed)
   - Assert: Mana pool unchanged
   - Assert: Stack empty
close_reason: Implemented cast-time targeting for Cephalid Coliseum threshold ability. Added :ability/targeting, modified activate-ability to pause for target selection, added confirm-ability-target function, and wrote 4 new tests. All 524 tests pass.

title: Extract resolve-one-item and refactor ::resolve-top
design:
## Goal
Extract a pure resolve-one-item function from the existing ::resolve-top event handler, then refactor ::resolve-top to be a thin wrapper around it. Add ::resolve-all event handler that loops resolve-one-item with snapshot-based stop conditions. All existing behavior preserved — tests must pass unchanged.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Key Context

**Two db levels in the codebase:**
- app-db (re-frame): top-level map with :game/db, :game/pending-selection, etc.
- game-db (datascript): the actual game state, accessed via (:game/db app-db)

resolve-one-item MUST work at game-db level (pure datascript function).
::resolve-top and ::resolve-all are re-frame event handlers that wrap game-db results into app-db.

**Existing resolve-top-of-stack (game.cljs:296-319):**
A pure function at game-db level that resolves without selection handling. Has 30+ test usages across 6 test files (stack_test, storm_test, tap_land_test, brain_freeze_test, cob_event_trigger_test, city_of_traitors_test). This function handles 2 paths (spell via rules/resolve-spell, and non-spell via resolve-stack-item-effects) but NOT selection-aware resolution. Keep it as-is — it's used by tests that don't need selection handling.

**Player-id is hardcoded:** ::resolve-top uses :player-1 at line 412. resolve-one-item should accept player-id as a parameter for correctness.

## Implementation

1. Study existing code
   - events/game.cljs:296-319 - resolve-top-of-stack (existing pure function, non-selection)
   - events/game.cljs:399-439 - ::resolve-top event handler (3 paths with selection handling)
   - events/game.cljs:269-293 - resolve-stack-item-effects (handles storm copy creation)
   - events/game.cljs:380-396 - maybe-continue-cleanup (cleanup phase check)
   - events/selection.cljs - resolve-spell-with-selection, resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection
   - engine/stack.cljs:50-58 - get-top-stack-item
   - engine/stack.cljs:61-69 - get-all-stack-items
   - engine/stack.cljs:43-47 - remove-stack-item

2. Write tests first (TDD)

   **resolve-one-item tests (game-db level):**
   - test-resolve-one-item-spell: Spell stack-item with object-ref resolves, stack-item removed, returns {:db db-after}. Bug caught: spell resolution path broken during extraction.
   - test-resolve-one-item-spell-with-selection: Spell with :tutor effect returns {:db db :pending-selection data}, stack-item NOT yet removed. Bug caught: selection detection broken.
   - test-resolve-one-item-activated-ability: Activated-ability stack-item resolves via resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection. Bug caught: ability path dropped during extraction.
   - test-resolve-one-item-trigger: Non-spell, non-ability stack-item resolves via resolve-stack-item-effects. Bug caught: trigger path dropped.
   - test-resolve-one-item-empty-stack: No stack-items returns {:db db} unchanged. Bug caught: NPE on nil top-stack-item.
   - test-resolve-one-item-storm-creates-copies: Storm stack-item with :storm-copies effect creates new stack-items. Bug caught: storm copy creation broken.

   **::resolve-all tests (app-db level):**
   - test-resolve-all-multiple-items: 3 non-selection items on stack, all resolved in one dispatch. Bug caught: loop doesn't continue past first item.
   - test-resolve-all-stops-at-selection: 2 items, second needs selection. First resolved, second pauses with pending-selection. Bug caught: loop doesn't stop at selection.
   - test-resolve-all-stops-at-new-items: Storm meta-item on stack, resolves and creates copies. Loop stops because copies weren't in snapshot. Bug caught: loop resolves newly-created items (violates requirement).
   - test-resolve-all-single-item: 1 item on stack, resolves same as resolve-top. Bug caught: off-by-one in loop.
   - test-resolve-all-empty-stack: No items, returns unchanged. Bug caught: NPE on empty stack.
   - test-resolve-all-cleanup-phase: Resolve during cleanup, maybe-continue-cleanup called at end. Bug caught: cleanup not triggered after batch resolution.

   **::resolve-top regression:**
   - All existing tests in stack_test.cljs must pass unchanged after refactor. Bug caught: refactoring changes behavior.

3. Implementation checklist
   - [ ] Define resolve-one-item function in events/game.cljs (defn-, private)
     - Signature: (resolve-one-item game-db player-id) -> {:db game-db'} or {:db game-db' :pending-selection data}
     - Path 1: top-stack-item has :stack-item/object-ref → call selection/resolve-spell-with-selection, remove stack-item if no selection
     - Path 2: top-stack-item type is :activated-ability → call selection/resolve-stack-item-ability-with-selection, remove stack-item if no selection
     - Path 3: other stack-item → call resolve-stack-item-effects, remove stack-item, return {:db db'}
     - Path 4: nil top-stack-item → return {:db game-db} unchanged
     - IMPORTANT: For paths 1 and 2, when pending-selection is returned, do NOT remove the stack-item (it stays on stack until selection completes)
   - [ ] Refactor ::resolve-top to call resolve-one-item
     - Extract game-db from app-db: (let [game-db (:game/db db)] ...)
     - Call (resolve-one-item game-db :player-1)
     - If :pending-selection → (-> db (assoc :game/db (:db result)) (assoc :game/pending-selection (:pending-selection result)))
     - Else → (maybe-continue-cleanup (assoc db :game/db (:db result)))
     - Verify: behavior IDENTICAL to current ::resolve-top
   - [ ] Add ::resolve-all event handler
     - Extract game-db from app-db
     - Snapshot: (set (map :db/id (stack/get-all-stack-items game-db)))
     - Loop with recur:
       (loop [game-db game-db]
         (let [top (stack/get-top-stack-item game-db)]
           (if (and top (contains? snapshot (:db/id top)))
             (let [result (resolve-one-item game-db :player-1)]
               (if (:pending-selection result)
                 ;; Stop: selection needed
                 (-> db (assoc :game/db (:db result)) (assoc :game/pending-selection (:pending-selection result)))
                 ;; Continue
                 (recur (:db result))))
             ;; Stop: stack empty or new item on top
             (maybe-continue-cleanup (assoc db :game/db game-db)))))
   - [ ] Add Yield All button in views/controls.cljs
     - Next to existing Yield button (line 40-43)
     - Enabled when stack has 2+ items: (> (count stack) 1)
     - Dispatches [::events/resolve-all]
     - Label: "Yield All"
   - [ ] Run make validate — all tests must pass

## Success Criteria
- [ ] resolve-one-item function extracted, handles all 4 paths (spell, ability, other, empty)
- [ ] ::resolve-top refactored to call resolve-one-item — all existing tests pass UNCHANGED
- [ ] ::resolve-all resolves multiple items, stops at: selection, new stack items, empty stack
- [ ] 6 new tests for resolve-one-item (each path + selection + storm)
- [ ] 6 new tests for ::resolve-all (multi-item, selection stop, new-item stop, single, empty, cleanup)
- [ ] Yield All button visible, enabled when stack has 2+ items, disabled otherwise
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO modifying resolve-top-of-stack (game.cljs:296-319) — 30+ test usages depend on it, leave as-is
- ❌ NO working at app-db level in resolve-one-item — must be pure game-db function
- ❌ NO removing stack-item when pending-selection returned — item stays until selection completes
- ❌ NO recursive re-frame dispatch in ::resolve-all — single event handler with loop
- ❌ NO hardcoding player-id in resolve-one-item — accept as parameter

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Object-ref pointing to retracted entity**
- Possible if game state is corrupted or entity was retracted between stack creation and resolution
- d/pull on non-existent eid returns {} — :object/id would be nil
- resolve-spell with nil object-id would fail
- MITIGATION: Check that object exists after d/pull, skip resolution if not (same as resolve-top-of-stack does implicitly via rules/resolve-spell)

**Edge Case: Storm creates copies with selection needs**
- Storm meta-item resolves → creates N :storm-copy stack-items
- These copies may have effects needing selection (e.g., :any-player targeting)
- Since copies are NOT in the snapshot, ::resolve-all stops here ✅
- User resolves copies individually or clicks Yield All again

**Edge Case: Cleanup phase interaction**
- maybe-continue-cleanup only runs at END of ::resolve-all (not after each item in loop)
- This is correct: we want one cleanup check after all resolutions, not N checks
- Verify: cleanup discard selection works if triggered after batch resolution

**Reference Implementation:**
- resolve-top-of-stack (game.cljs:296-319) — similar pure function pattern to follow
- ::resolve-top (game.cljs:399-439) — the code being refactored
close_reason: Extracted resolve-one-item, refactored ::resolve-top, added ::resolve-all handler and Yield All button. 11 new tests. make validate passes.

title: Rewrite mode_selection_test.cljs to use production handlers
description: 5 of 7 tests define inline handler functions duplicating production logic. Rewrite to call actual re-frame handlers or the production functions directly. Tests affected: single-mode-card-auto-casts-test, multi-mode-card-shows-selector-test, select-mode-casts-spell-test, select-alternate-mode-casts-with-life-payment-test, cancel-mode-selection-test.
design:
## Goal
Rewrite 5 mode_selection_test.cljs tests to exercise production code instead of inline handler copies.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Problem
Tests define inline handler fns like:
  handler (fn [db _] (let [game-db (:game/db db) ...] (if (= 1 (count modes)) (-> db (assoc :game/db (rules/cast-spell-mode ...))))))
This duplicates production handler logic. If the production handler changes, tests still pass against stale inline copy.

## Approach
Two options for testing re-frame handlers without the full framework:

**Option A (Preferred): Call production handler functions directly**
The re-frame event handlers in events/game.cljs are registered as (rf/reg-event-fx ::cast-spell handler-fn). Extract or reference the handler fn and call it directly with the app-db map.

**Option B: Test via the underlying engine functions**
Since mode_selection only wraps rules/get-casting-modes, rules/can-cast-mode?, and rules/cast-spell-mode, test those production functions with the mode-selection scenarios. The 2 GREEN tests already do this.

## Tests to Rewrite
1. single-mode-card-auto-casts-test (line 92) — verify rules/cast-spell-mode is called when count modes = 1
2. multi-mode-card-shows-selector-test (line 130) — verify modes returned when count > 1
3. select-mode-casts-spell-test (line 192) — verify cast-spell-mode with selected mode
4. select-alternate-mode-casts-with-life-payment-test (line 228) — verify flashback mode with life payment
5. cancel-mode-selection-test (line 265) — verify cancel clears selection state

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Read events/game.cljs to find the actual cast-spell and select-casting-mode handlers
- [ ] For each test, replace inline handler with call to production handler or production functions
- [ ] Verify each rewritten test still tests the same scenario
- [ ] Run make validate
- [ ] Test count should remain the same (no tests added or removed)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] No inline handler (fn [db _] ...) patterns remain in mode_selection_test.cljs
- [ ] All 7 tests in the file call production code (functions from events/game.cljs or engine/rules.cljs)
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] Each rewritten test would FAIL if the production handler broke

## Anti-patterns
- Do NOT create a test-only wrapper function — call the real handler
- Do NOT add mocks — this codebase uses pure functions on Datascript, no mocks needed
- Do NOT change what scenarios are tested — same 7 scenarios, better implementation

## Edge Cases
- The production cast-spell handler likely dispatches other re-frame events — the test should call the handler fn directly, not go through rf/dispatch
- If handler is anonymous, may need to def it separately in game.cljs or call the inner fn
close_reason: Extracted 3 named handler functions (cast-spell-handler, select-casting-mode-handler, cancel-mode-selection-handler) from anonymous rf/reg-event-db registrations in game.cljs. Rewrote 5 tests to call these production functions instead of inline handler copies. All 7 tests pass, 818 total tests, make validate clean.

title: Task 1: Unify selection data model — replace :selection/selected-target with :selection/selected everywhere
design:
## Goal
Replace `:selection/selected-target` (scalar) with `:selection/selected` (set) across all selection types. After this task, all selections use the same field for tracking what's selected. Targeting types become set-based selections with select-count=1 and `:selection/valid-targets`.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code

**Builders that create `:selection/selected-target`:**
- `build-player-target-selection` (selection.cljs:408-417) — sets `:selection/selected-target nil`
- `build-cast-time-target-selection` (selection.cljs:910-930) — sets `:selection/selected-target nil`
- `build-ability-target-selection` (abilities.cljs:~130-147) — sets `:selection/selected-target nil`

**Confirm handlers that READ `:selection/selected-target`:**
- `::confirm-player-target-selection` (selection.cljs:1347) — `(:selection/selected-target selection)` at L1350
- `confirm-cast-time-target` standalone fn (selection.cljs:975-1003) — `(:selection/selected-target selection)` at L988
- `::confirm-cast-time-target` event (selection.cljs:1516) — wraps selection with target from arg at L1519
- `confirm-ability-target` standalone fn (abilities.cljs:150) — `(:selection/selected-target selection)` at L167
- `::confirm-ability-target` event (abilities.cljs:269) — wraps selection with target from arg at L272

**Toggle/select handlers that WRITE `:selection/selected-target`:**
- `::select-player-target` (selection.cljs:1341) — assoc-in selected-target at L1343
- `::select-cast-time-object-target` (selection.cljs:1528) — assoc-in selected-target at L1534, L1538
- `::select-ability-object-target` (abilities.cljs:281) — assoc-in selected-target at L287, L291

**UI that READS `:selection/selected-target`:**
- modals.cljs:108 — `object-target-modal` reads `(:selection/selected-target selection)`
- modals.cljs:142 — `player-target-modal` reads `(:selection/selected-target selection)`
- modals.cljs:155 — `player-target-modal` dispatches `[confirm-event selected-target]`
- modals.cljs:132 — `object-target-modal` dispatches `[confirm-event selected-target]`

**Subs:** No subscriptions reference `:selection/selected-target`. ✅

**Test files referencing `:selection/selected-target` (23 references — MUST UPDATE):**
- `recoup_test.cljs` — 11 references (L246, 269, 290, 310, 343, 369, 395, 418, 455, 476, 499): All use `(assoc selection :selection/selected-target sorcery-id)` to simulate target selection before confirm.
- `deep_analysis_test.cljs` — 6 references (L287, 363, 369, 446, 467, 493): Assert `selected-target` is nil on build, assoc target before confirm.
- `cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs` — 5 references (L351, 408, 516, 543, 581): Assoc target before calling `confirm-ability-target`.
- `seal_of_cleansing_test.cljs` — 1 reference (L283): Assoc target before calling `confirm-ability-target`.

### 2. Write failing tests first (TDD RED)

For each changed builder, write a test that asserts the new data shape:

a) **test-player-target-selection-uses-selected-set**: Build a player-target selection. Assert `:selection/selected` is `#{}`, `:selection/select-count` is 1, no `:selection/selected-target` key exists. Assert `:selection/valid-targets` is `#{:player-1 :opponent}`.
   - Bug caught: Builder still creates old field format.

b) **test-cast-time-target-selection-uses-selected-set**: Build a cast-time-target selection. Assert same set-based shape. Assert `:selection/valid-targets` contains the valid target object-ids.
   - Bug caught: Cast-time builder not updated.

c) **test-ability-target-selection-uses-selected-set**: Build an ability-target selection. Assert same.
   - Bug caught: Ability builder not updated.

d) **test-player-target-confirm-reads-selected-set**: Set up player-target selection with `:selection/selected #{:opponent}`. Dispatch confirm. Assert effect executed against opponent.
   - Bug caught: Confirm handler still reads old field.

e) **test-cast-time-confirm-reads-selected-set**: Set up cast-time selection with `:selection/selected #{target-obj-id}`. Dispatch confirm. Assert spell on stack with target stored.
   - Bug caught: Cast-time confirm still reads old field.

f) **test-ability-confirm-reads-selected-set**: Similar for ability targeting.
   - Bug caught: Ability confirm still reads old field.

g) **test-player-target-toggle-writes-selected-set**: Dispatch `::select-player-target` with :opponent. Assert `:selection/selected` is `#{:opponent}` (not `:selection/selected-target`).
   - Bug caught: Toggle handler still writes old field.

h) **test-object-target-toggle-writes-selected-set**: Dispatch `::select-cast-time-object-target` with valid target. Assert `:selection/selected` is `#{target-id}`.
   - Bug caught: Object toggle still writes old field.

i) **test-player-target-chains-to-discard-with-selected-player**: Set up player-target selection for an effect that chains to discard (e.g., Deep Analysis targeting opponent + discard). Select :opponent. Confirm. Assert next pending-selection has `:selection/player-id :opponent` (the target player discards, not the caster).
   - Bug caught: Player-target chaining breaks — `(first selected)` doesn't correctly propagate player-id to next selection.

### 3. Implementation checklist

**Order: player-target first (simplest), then cast-time-target, then ability-target. Run tests after each.**

- [ ] Update `build-player-target-selection` (selection.cljs:408-417): Replace `:selection/selected-target nil` with `:selection/selected #{}`. Add `:selection/select-count 1`. Add `:selection/valid-targets #{:player-1 :opponent}`.

- [ ] Update `::select-player-target` (selection.cljs:1341): Change `(assoc-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/selected-target] target-player-id)` to `(assoc-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/selected] #{target-player-id})`.

- [ ] Update `::confirm-player-target-selection` (selection.cljs:1347-1400): Change L1350 `selected-target (:selection/selected-target selection)` to `selected-target (first (:selection/selected selection))`. **CRITICAL at L1378**: `selected-target` is reused as `:selection/player-id` for the chained discard selection — verify `(first (:selection/selected selection))` returns the player keyword (:player-1 or :opponent) correctly.

- [ ] Update `player-target-modal` (modals.cljs:142): Change `selected-target (:selection/selected-target selection)` to `selected (or (:selection/selected selection) #{})`. Update L143 `valid?` to `(= 1 (count selected))`. Update L149 player-button highlight to `(contains? selected :player-1)` and `(contains? selected :opponent)`. Update L155 confirm dispatch to `[confirm-event]` (no arg).

- [ ] Update existing `deep_analysis_test.cljs` — 6 references: Change all `(assoc sel :selection/selected-target :player-1)` to `(assoc sel :selection/selected #{:player-1})`. Change L287 assertion from `(nil? (:selection/selected-target selection))` to `(empty? (:selection/selected selection))` or `(= #{} (:selection/selected selection))`.

- [ ] Run `make test` — all tests green before proceeding to cast-time.

- [ ] Update `build-cast-time-target-selection` (selection.cljs:910-930): Replace `:selection/selected-target nil` with `:selection/selected #{}`. Add `:selection/select-count 1`. `:selection/valid-targets` already present at L923.

- [ ] Update `::select-cast-time-object-target` (selection.cljs:1528-1541): Change L1534 `(:selection/selected-target selection)` to check `(contains? (:selection/selected selection) object-id)`. Change L1538 assoc-in to write `:selection/selected`. Deselect: `(assoc-in db [...:selection/selected] #{})`. Select: `(assoc-in db [...:selection/selected] #{object-id})`.

- [ ] Update `confirm-cast-time-target` standalone fn (selection.cljs:975-1003): Change L988 `selected-target (:selection/selected-target selection)` to `selected-target (first (:selection/selected selection))`. Change L990 guard to `(seq (:selection/selected selection))`.

- [ ] Update `::confirm-cast-time-target` event (selection.cljs:1516-1524): Currently `(fn [db [_ target-id]])` and L1519 `(assoc selection :selection/selected-target target-id)`. Change to `(fn [db _])` — no arg. Read selection directly from db (it already has `:selection/selected #{target}` set by toggle handler). Remove the assoc at L1519, just pass selection to confirm-cast-time-target fn.

- [ ] Update `object-target-modal` (modals.cljs:97-132): Change L108 `(:selection/selected-target selection)` to reading from `:selection/selected`. Use `(contains? selected object-id)` for highlight at L90. Confirm button L132: dispatch `[confirm-event]` without arg.

- [ ] Update `object-target-card-view` (modals.cljs:85-94): Change L90 `(= object-id selected-target)` to `(contains? selected object-id)`. Need to pass `selected` set from parent component.

- [ ] Update existing `recoup_test.cljs` — 11 references: Change all `(assoc selection :selection/selected-target sorcery-id)` to `(assoc selection :selection/selected #{sorcery-id})`.

- [ ] Run `make test` — all tests green before proceeding to ability.

- [ ] Update `build-ability-target-selection` (abilities.cljs:~130-147): Replace `:selection/selected-target nil` with `:selection/selected #{}`. Add `:selection/select-count 1`. `:selection/valid-targets` already present at L146.

- [ ] Update `::select-ability-object-target` (abilities.cljs:281-293): Same pattern as cast-time-object — use `:selection/selected` set. Deselect to `#{}`, select to `#{object-id}`.

- [ ] Update `confirm-ability-target` standalone fn (abilities.cljs:150-175): Change L167 `target-id (:selection/selected-target selection)` to `target-id (first (:selection/selected selection))`. Change L169 guard to `(seq (:selection/selected selection))`.

- [ ] Update `::confirm-ability-target` event (abilities.cljs:269-277): Currently takes target-id as arg at L270 and wraps at L272. Change to `(fn [db _])`. Remove the assoc — selection already has `:selection/selected` set by toggle handler.

- [ ] Update existing `cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs` — 5 references: Change all `(assoc selection :selection/selected-target :player-1)` to `(assoc selection :selection/selected #{:player-1})`. For L543 nil case: change to `(assoc selection :selection/selected #{})`.

- [ ] Update existing `seal_of_cleansing_test.cljs` — 1 reference at L283: Change `(assoc sel :selection/selected-target target-id)` to `(assoc sel :selection/selected #{target-id})`.

- [ ] Update namespace docstring (selection.cljs:1-40): Remove references to `selected-target` in the docstring type descriptions for targeting and player-target types.

- [ ] Run `grep -r 'selected-target' src/` — must return ZERO matches.

- [ ] Run `make validate` — all tests must pass.

### 4. Key Considerations

**Extracting single target from set:**
For targeting types, the selected element is extracted via `(first (:selection/selected selection))`. This is safe because select-count is 1 and validation ensures count <= 1. `(first #{})` returns nil (empty selection). `(first #{:opponent})` returns `:opponent`. Set ordering doesn't matter for single-element sets.

**Confirm handler interface change:**
`::confirm-cast-time-target` and `::confirm-ability-target` currently take target-id as argument from UI. After this change, they read from db. UI dispatches `[confirm-event]` without args. All confirm handlers become `(fn [db _])` interface.

**CRITICAL: Player-target → discard chaining (L1375-1378):**
In `::confirm-player-target-selection`, the selected target is reused as `:selection/player-id` for the chained discard selection at L1378:
```clojure
:selection/player-id selected-target  ; Target player discards
```
After migration, this becomes:
```clojure
:selection/player-id (first (:selection/selected selection))
```
The `(first ...)` returns a player keyword (`:player-1` or `:opponent`), which is what `:selection/player-id` expects. This is correct but must be verified with test (i).

**CRITICAL: standalone `confirm-cast-time-target` function (L975-1003):**
This is a separate function from the event handler. It reads `:selection/selected-target` at L988 and uses it at L1000 to store as `:object/targets`. Must update to `(first (:selection/selected selection))`.

**Test file migration pattern:**
All 23 test references follow the same pattern: `(assoc selection :selection/selected-target value)` simulating target selection before calling confirm. Mechanical change to `(assoc selection :selection/selected #{value})`. The deep_analysis_test at L287 also asserts `(nil? (:selection/selected-target selection))` which becomes `(= #{} (:selection/selected selection))` or `(empty? (:selection/selected selection))`.

**Player target validation:**
`::select-player-target` currently does no validation. With the unified model, `build-player-target-selection` now includes `:selection/valid-targets #{:player-1 :opponent}`. The toggle handler COULD validate, but since we're not unifying toggle handlers yet (that's Phase 2), keep the existing handler behavior — just change the field it writes. Validation comes in Phase 2.

**Backward compatibility during refactoring:**
Each type is migrated independently. Process:
1. Update builder + toggle + confirm + UI + tests for player-target
2. Run `make test` — green
3. Repeat for cast-time-target
4. Run `make test` — green
5. Repeat for ability-target
6. Run `make test` — green
7. Final grep verification

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO leaving both `:selection/selected-target` and `:selection/selected` coexisting (must fully migrate)
- ❌ NO changing toggle handler structure (that's Phase 2)
- ❌ NO changing confirm handler structure/multimethod (that's Phase 3)
- ❌ NO changing any game behavior (data model change only — same logic, new fields)
- ❌ NO breaking tests at any intermediate step (run make test after each type migration)
- ❌ NO forgetting the standalone `confirm-cast-time-target` function at L975 (not just the event handler)
- ❌ NO forgetting the 23 test file references (easy to miss, causes silent failures)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] `grep -r 'selected-target' src/` returns zero matches (includes main + test)
- [ ] All 3 targeting builders create `:selection/selected #{}` + `:selection/select-count 1` + `:selection/valid-targets`
- [ ] All targeting confirm handlers and standalone functions read from `:selection/selected` via `(first ...)`
- [ ] All targeting toggle handlers write to `:selection/selected` as a set
- [ ] modals.cljs reads `:selection/selected` for target highlight and dispatches confirm without args
- [ ] All confirm handlers use `(fn [db _])` interface (no target-id args from UI)
- [ ] Player-target → discard chain correctly uses `(first selected)` as player-id
- [ ] All 4 test files updated (23 references total): recoup_test (11), deep_analysis_test (6), cephalid_coliseum_test (5), seal_of_cleansing_test (1)
- [ ] All existing tests pass after each type migration (player → cast-time → ability)
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format + tests)
close_reason: All selected-target references migrated to selected set. 1265 tests passing.

title: Fix Gemstone Mine sacrifice to be part of mana ability
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Gemstone Mine sacrifice must happen as part of the mana ability resolution, not as a state-based effect
- Effect system must support conditional effects via :effect/condition field
- :no-counters condition type must check if specified counter type is zero on target
- Zero-counters SBA must be removed entirely
- Sacrifice effect type must exist to move permanents to graveyard

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Gemstone Mine sacrifices itself when mana ability resolves AND no mining counters remain
- [ ] Gemstone Mine does NOT sacrifice if counters removed by other means (no SBA)
- [ ] Effect conditions are checked before effect execution
- [ ] :sacrifice effect moves target to graveyard
- [ ] All existing tests pass (updated as needed)
- [ ] New tests verify conditional effect behavior
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO state-based effect for counter depletion (rules error: sacrifice is part of ability, not SBA)
- ❌ NO custom effect types per card (maintainability: use generic conditional system)
- ❌ NO hardcoding Gemstone Mine logic in effect executor (extensibility: condition system must be generic)

## Approach
Add effect-level condition support to the effect executor. Effects with :effect/condition are only executed if the condition evaluates to true. Implement :no-counters condition type that checks if a counter type is zero. Update Gemstone Mine card definition to include conditional sacrifice as part of mana ability effects. Remove the zero-counters SBA entirely.

## Architecture
- engine/effects.cljs - Add check-condition multimethod, update execute-effect to check conditions
- engine/conditions.cljs (new) - Condition evaluation logic, or inline in effects.cljs
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Update Gemstone Mine with conditional sacrifice effect
- engine/state_based.cljs - Remove zero-counters SBA code and registration

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Gemstone Mine's sacrifice is implemented as a state-based effect, but the actual card text says 'If there are no mining counters on Gemstone Mine, sacrifice it' as part of the mana ability. This means the sacrifice should only happen when activating the mana ability, not when counters are removed by other means.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- cards/iggy_pop.cljs:79-100 - Current Gemstone Mine definition uses ETB for counters, mana ability for tap+remove
- engine/state_based.cljs:60-94 - Zero-counters SBA incorrectly sacrifices when counters hit 0
- engine/effects.cljs - Effect executor has no condition checking currently
- engine/costs.cljs:73-103 - Counter removal happens during cost payment

**External:**
- Scryfall rules note: 'the land won't be sacrificed if the last counter is removed through means other than activating the mana ability'

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Effect-level conditions ✓
**What it is:** Add :effect/condition field to any effect. Effect executor checks condition before running effect. Condition system is a multimethod dispatching on :condition/type.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed effects.cljs - clean place to add condition check
- Existing condition system for threshold is card-level, not effect-level
- Multimethod pattern matches existing code style

**Pros:**
- Generic, reusable for future cards
- Data-driven, keeps card definitions declarative
- Clean separation of concerns

**Cons:**
- Slightly more complex than custom effect type

**Chosen because:** Extensibility - avoids proliferation of custom effect types for each conditional card

#### 2. Custom :sacrifice-if-no-counters effect ❌
**What it is:** Create a specific effect type that combines sacrifice with counter check.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler immediate implementation

**Pros:**
- Quick to implement
- No new systems needed

**Cons:**
- Would need new effect type for each conditional pattern
- Not reusable

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User explicitly said we need general conditional support to avoid many custom effects

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We decide effect conditions add too much complexity (unlikely)

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Effect-level condition system
- :no-counters condition type
- Gemstone Mine fix
- Remove zero-counters SBA

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Other condition types (:has-counters, :counter-equals, etc.) - add when needed
- Evaluating broader SBA system - separate future effort
- Other counter-based cards - handle if they come up

### Open Questions
- Should conditions.cljs be separate file or inline in effects.cljs? (decide during implementation, lean toward inline for now)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Fix scope? | Fix Gemstone Mine + remove zero-counters SBA | SBA system stays but zero-counters removed |
| Other cards to audit? | Only Gemstone Mine for now | No broader card audit needed |
| Condition system design? | Effect-level :effect/condition | Each effect can have optional condition |
| Condition types to support? | :no-counters only | Minimal implementation, extend later |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Gemstone Mine Rules
**Question explored:** How does the sacrifice actually work per MTG rules?
**Sources consulted:**
- Scryfall card page and rules notes
- Current implementation in state_based.cljs

**Findings:**
- Sacrifice is part of mana ability text, not a separate triggered ability
- Rules note explicitly states: won't sacrifice if counters removed by other means
- Current SBA implementation is a rules error

**Conclusion:** Must move sacrifice into ability effects with condition check

### Dead-End Paths

#### State-Based Effect Approach
**Why explored:** Was the original implementation approach
**Investigation:**
- Implemented as check-zero-counters SBA
- Fires after any action when counters <= 0

**Why abandoned:** Rules error - sacrifice should only happen as part of mana ability activation

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Is the SBA system correct in light of this?' → Deferred to future evaluation, not blocking this fix
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract opponent zone component + establish extraction pattern
design:
## Goal
Extract the opponent info view from core.cljs into views/opponent.cljs as the first zone component. Migrate its inline styles to Tailwind utility classes. This establishes the extraction + Tailwind migration pattern for all subsequent zones.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Completed fizzle-00y4: Tailwind v4 pipeline is working. @theme tokens defined. This is the first component extraction — kept small to establish patterns before tackling larger zones like hand or battlefield.

## Implementation

### 1. Add missing @theme tokens
Edit src/css/app.css to add:
- --color-health-good: #5CB85C (green, player healthy / opponent dead)
- --color-health-danger: #F0AD4E (orange, low life warning)
- --color-health-critical: #D9534F (red, player dead / opponent healthy)
- --color-text-dim: #888 (dim labels like 'You:' / 'Opponent:')
- --color-text-label: #999 (section headers like 'LIFE TOTALS')

These are semantic health-state colors, distinct from mana colors even when hex values overlap. Mana colors are for mana display, health colors are for life totals.

### 2. Create views/common.cljs
New file: src/main/fizzle/views/common.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.views.common

Collapsible zone wrapper component:
- Takes: title (string), collapsed? (reagent atom), child content
- Renders: header bar with title + chevron/toggle, conditionally renders children
- Uses Tailwind classes for styling
- Pattern for all zone components to use

### 3. Create views/opponent.cljs
New file: src/main/fizzle/views/opponent.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.views.opponent

Move life-view from core.cljs (lines 219-243). Current code:
```clojure
(defn- life-view
  []
  (let [player-life @(rf/subscribe [::subs/player-life])
        opponent-life @(rf/subscribe [::subs/opponent-life])]
    [:div {:style {:margin-bottom "16px"}}
     [:div {:style {:color "#999" :margin-bottom "6px" :font-size "12px"}} "LIFE TOTALS"]
     [:div {:style {:display "flex" :gap "30px"}}
      [:div
       [:span {:style {:color "#888" :font-size "13px"}} "You: "]
       [:span {:style {:font-size "20px" :font-weight "bold"
                       :color (cond (<= player-life 0) "#D9534F"
                                    (<= player-life 5) "#F0AD4E"
                                    :else "#5CB85C")}}
        player-life]]
      [:div
       [:span {:style {:color "#888" :font-size "13px"}} "Opponent: "]
       [:span {:style {:font-size "20px" :font-weight "bold"
                       :color (cond (<= opponent-life 0) "#5CB85C"
                                    (<= opponent-life 5) "#F0AD4E"
                                    :else "#D9534F")}}
        opponent-life]]]]))
```

Convert to Tailwind:
- {:margin-bottom "16px"} → mb-4
- {:color "#999" :margin-bottom "6px" :font-size "12px"} → text-text-label mb-1.5 text-xs
- {:display "flex" :gap "30px"} → flex gap-8
- {:color "#888" :font-size "13px"} → text-text-dim text-sm
- {:font-size "20px" :font-weight "bold"} → text-xl font-bold
- Conditional health colors: use ClojureScript cond to select class string
  - (cond (<= life 0) "text-health-critical" (<= life 5) "text-health-danger" :else "text-health-good")

Subscriptions needed:
- fizzle.subs.game/player-life
- fizzle.subs.game/opponent-life

No events dispatched (read-only component).

### 4. Update core.cljs
- Add require: [fizzle.views.opponent :as opponent]
- Replace inline life-view call with [opponent/life-view]
- Remove the defn- life-view from core.cljs
- Note: function was defn- (private) — becomes public in new namespace, which is correct since core.cljs calls it

### 5. Verify
- make validate passes
- No changes to event/subscription contracts

## Success Criteria
- [ ] src/css/app.css has 5 new @theme tokens (health-good, health-danger, health-critical, text-dim, text-label)
- [ ] views/common.cljs exists with collapsible zone wrapper component
- [ ] views/opponent.cljs exists with life-view component
- [ ] Zero inline :style maps in opponent.cljs (all Tailwind classes)
- [ ] No hardcoded hex colors in opponent.cljs (uses @theme tokens)
- [ ] Conditional life colors use text-health-good/danger/critical classes
- [ ] core.cljs requires and uses opponent/life-view
- [ ] life-view defn removed from core.cljs
- [ ] Component is layout-agnostic (no grid/position styling)
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] No changes to event/subscription contracts

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO inline :style maps (use Tailwind utility classes)
- ❌ NO hardcoded hex colors (use @theme tokens)
- ❌ NO layout positioning in component (grid parent handles placement)
- ❌ NO game logic in view (subscribe and dispatch only)
- ❌ NO breaking existing subscriptions/events
- ❌ NO using mana color tokens for health states (semantic mismatch: use health-good/danger/critical)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Conditional Class Selection in Reagent/Hiccup:**
Tailwind classes are strings. Conditional styling in Reagent uses ClojureScript expressions to select class strings:
```clojure
[:span {:class (str "text-xl font-bold "
                    (cond (<= life 0) "text-health-critical"
                          (<= life 5) "text-health-danger"
                          :else "text-health-good"))}
 life]
```
This works because Tailwind scans compiled JS for class name strings.

**Private to Public:**
life-view is defn- (private) in core.cljs. When moved to opponent.cljs it becomes defn (public), which is correct since core.cljs imports it.

**@theme Token Naming:**
Health colors are semantically distinct from mana colors even when hex values overlap (e.g., mana-red and health-critical are both #D9534F). Separate tokens allow independent theming.

**Collapsible Zone Pattern:**
The common.cljs zone wrapper establishes the collapsible pattern. Uses a reagent atom for collapsed? state. Other zones will wrap their content in this component.
close_reason: All success criteria met. opponent.cljs extracted with Tailwind classes, common.cljs zone wrapper created, 5 @theme tokens added, 10 new tests, make validate passes (973 tests).

title: Strengthen ~15 weak contains? assertions in card tests
description: Replace (is (contains? types :instant)) with (is (= #{:instant} types)) across card definition tests. Affected files: careful_study_test, opt_test, mental_note_test, polluted_delta_test, cephalid_coliseum_test, cabal_ritual_test, dark_ritual_test, recoup_test, ray_of_revelation_test, seal_of_cleansing_test, merchant_scroll_test. These allow extra spurious types to go undetected.
design:
## Goal
Replace weak contains? assertions with exact = assertions in card type/color tests.

## Effort Estimate
1-2 hours

## Pattern to Fix
Current: (is (contains? (:card/types card) :instant))
Replace: (is (= #{:instant} (:card/types card)))

Current: (is (contains? (:card/colors card) :blue))
Replace: (is (= #{:blue} (:card/colors card)))

## Files and Tests Affected
1. careful_study_test.cljs — types and colors checks
2. opt_test.cljs — types and colors checks
3. mental_note_test.cljs — types check (line ~125)
4. polluted_delta_test.cljs — types check (line ~134)
5. cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs — types check (line ~184), keywords check (line ~196)
6. cabal_ritual_test.cljs — types and colors checks (line ~77)
7. dark_ritual_test.cljs — types and colors checks (line ~65)
8. recoup_test.cljs — types check (line ~132)
9. ray_of_revelation_test.cljs — types and colors checks (line ~114)
10. seal_of_cleansing_test.cljs — types and colors checks (line ~108)
11. merchant_scroll_test.cljs — types check (line ~377)

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] For each file, grep for contains? in type/color assertions
- [ ] Look up the card definition to get the exact types/colors set
- [ ] Replace contains? with = using the exact set
- [ ] Run make validate after all changes

## Success Criteria
- [ ] No contains? assertions remain for :card/types or :card/colors in listed files
- [ ] All replacements use exact set equality (= #{:instant} ...)
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] Bug caught: extra spurious types/colors on a card would now be detected

## Anti-patterns
- Do NOT replace contains? checks on OTHER fields (e.g., :card/keywords may legitimately use contains? if card has multiple keywords)
- Do NOT change assertions for multi-type cards (e.g., artifact creature) — those should use the full set like #{:artifact :creature}
close_reason: Replaced all 24 weak contains? assertions with exact = set equality assertions across 15 card test files. All 818 tests pass.

title: Strengthen ~15 weak assertion YELLOW tests
description:
Fix tests with weak or misleading assertions:
- effects_test: mill order (check actual order, not counts), restriction default turn (exercise nil path)
- triggers_test: nil controller (verify db unchanged, not just some?)
- trigger_dispatch_test: ordering (document limitation or fix)
- exile_cost_test: insufficient cards (test actual insufficient case), zero count (test exile mechanism)
- state_based_test: register-sba (verify return value used)
- peek_selection_test: remainder to bottom (verify positions, not just zone)
- turn_test: cleanup assertions (use precise assertions)
- scry_confirm_test: empty piles (verify full library state)
design:
## Goal
Strengthen ~15 YELLOW tests with weak or misleading assertions so they catch real bugs.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Tests to Strengthen (grouped by file)

### effects_test.cljs
1. test-mill-effect-preserves-order (~line 156): Currently only checks counts. Strengthen by checking actual card-ids in graveyard match expected order from top of library.
2. test-deal-damage-preserves-source (~line 575): Only checks life total. Add assertion that the damage source object is still present/unchanged in db.
3. test-add-restriction-default-turn-when-nil (~line 1350): init-game-state sets :game/turn 1 so (or nil 1) default never exercised. Create db WITHOUT :game/turn to actually test the default path.

### triggers_test.cljs
4. test-trigger-with-nil-controller-graceful (~line 11): Only (is (some? result-db)). Strengthen to (is (= db result-db)) to verify db truly unchanged.

### trigger_dispatch_test.cljs
5. test-dispatch-event-order-immediate-before-stacked (~line 215): Has duplicate assertion and can't observe order. Either: (a) add observable side-effect to immediate trigger to verify order, or (b) document limitation and remove duplicate assertion.

### exile_cost_test.cljs
6. test-can-pay-exile-cost-insufficient-cards (~line 172): Misleading name — tests minimum castable case. Add actual insufficient case (0 blue cards, X must be >= 1).
7. test-can-pay-exile-cost-zero-count (~line 191): Tests mana/can-pay? not exile cost. Rewrite to test rules/can-cast-mode? with X=0 exile cost.

### state_based_test.cljs
8. test-register-sba (~line 56): Uses atom side-effect check. Strengthen by verifying the SBA's actual effect on db (e.g., register an SBA that removes an object, verify object removed).

### peek_selection_test.cljs
9. test-execute-peek-selection-moves-remainder-to-bottom (~line 277): Only checks zone is :library. Add position assertions: remainder cards should have positions HIGHER than pre-existing library cards.
10. test-execute-peek-selection-fail-to-find (~line 301): Same weakness. Add position assertions.

### turn_test.cljs
11. test-cleanup-discard-to-max-hand-size (~line 384): (is (map? result)) is weak. Strengthen to check specific expected keys/values in the result map.
12. test-cleanup-advance-blocked-during-selection (~line 411): Never puts pending selection in app-db. Add actual pending selection to test the blocking behavior.
13. test-cleanup-repeat-does-not-trigger-at-other-phases (~line 471): Tests via grant expiry proxy. Add direct assertion that db is unchanged after maybe-continue-cleanup in non-cleanup phase.

### scry_confirm_test.cljs
14. test-confirm-scry-empty-piles-allowed (~line 240): (is (some? result-db)) is weak. Add assertions for ALL library cards unchanged (not just card A).

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] For each test, read the production code it exercises
- [ ] Write the strengthened assertion based on actual production behavior
- [ ] Verify strengthened test WOULD fail if production code broke (mentally trace)
- [ ] Run make validate after each file changed
- [ ] Total test count should remain the same

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 14 listed tests have strengthened assertions
- [ ] No (is (some? ...)) or (is (map? ...)) weak assertions remain in listed tests
- [ ] No (is (true? true)) or equivalent tautologies
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] Each strengthened test catches a specific nameable bug

## Anti-patterns
- Do NOT weaken existing assertions while strengthening — only add/replace
- Do NOT change test names unless the name is actively misleading (e.g., insufficient-cards test)
- Do NOT add new deftest — strengthen existing ones
- Do NOT test mock/fixture behavior — assert on production code output
close_reason: All 14 tests strengthened. Assertions went from 1822 to 1830. make validate passes clean (0 lint warnings, 0 format issues, 818 tests, 0 failures).

title: Task 3: Create interactive UI components for goldfishing
design:
## Goal
Create Reagent view components that enable interactive goldfishing: clickable hand cards, Cast/Resolve buttons, mana pool display, storm count, and stack view. This completes the Phase 1 milestone.

## Context
Completed Task 1 (re-frame infrastructure) and Task 2 (events/subscriptions wrapping engine). Events and subscriptions are fully wired. Now need components that subscribe to state and dispatch events.

## Implementation

### 1. Update core.cljs with interactive app component

Replace the text-only display with a full interactive layout. All views can live in core.cljs for now (3 cards, simple UI) - extract to separate files only if it becomes unwieldy.

Component structure:
- Hand: shows each card as a clickable div with card name, highlights selected
- Mana pool: shows each color with non-zero amount
- Storm count: shows current storm count
- Stack: shows items on stack (triggers and spells) with names
- Controls: Cast button (enabled when card selected and castable), Resolve button (enabled when stack non-empty)

Key events to dispatch:
- `::events/select-card` on card click (pass object-id)
- `::events/cast-spell` on Cast button click
- `::events/resolve-top` on Resolve button click

Subscriptions to consume:
- `::subs/hand` - cards in hand
- `::subs/mana-pool` - mana by color
- `::subs/storm-count` - storm count
- `::subs/stack` - stack items
- `::subs/selected-card` - currently selected card
- `::subs/can-cast?` - whether selected card is castable

### 2. Styling
Inline styles only (anti-pattern forbids CSS frameworks).
- Cards: bordered divs, highlighted border when selected
- Mana: colored text or symbols for each mana color
- Buttons: basic button styling, disabled state visible

### 3. Verify in browser
- Run `make dev` and open localhost:8080
- Verify: 3 cards in hand, 2 black mana, 0 storm
- Click Dark Ritual -> Cast -> mana becomes BBB+1=4B (started with 2, -1 cost, +3 from effect... wait: start 2B, cast DR costs 1B -> 1B remaining, resolve DR adds 3B -> 4B)
- Actually: casting puts on stack (pays mana), resolving executes effects
- Flow: Select DR -> Cast (pays 1B, moves to stack, storm=1) -> Resolve (adds 3B, moves to graveyard) -> mana pool should show 4B

### 4. Run make validate

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Browser loads at localhost:8080 without console errors
- [ ] Hand shows 3 clickable cards with names (Dark Ritual, Brain Freeze, Cabal Ritual)
- [ ] Clicking a card highlights it and shows it as selected
- [ ] Cast button works when card selected and castable
- [ ] Resolve button works when stack has items
- [ ] Mana pool updates correctly after casting and resolving
- [ ] Storm count increments on each cast
- [ ] Stack shows triggers and spells
- [ ] Can complete full combo: DR -> CR -> BF with storm copies
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO game logic in components (subscribe and dispatch only)
- NO direct atom manipulation
- NO CSS frameworks (inline styles only)
- NO new engine code
close_reason: Interactive UI components implemented in core.cljs. Hand with clickable cards, mana pool display, storm count, stack view, and Cast/Resolve buttons. All lint/format/tests pass.

title: Task 1: Add auto-confirm routing to ::toggle-selection for single-select types
design:
## Goal
When a single-select selection reaches completion, automatically dispatch the appropriate confirm event. No separate Confirm click needed. Covers tutor (select-count=1), cast-time-targeting (object and player), player-target, and ability-targeting (object and player) selections.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours (includes TDD cycle)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code (READ-ONLY)

Four separate toggle/select handlers exist — each needs auto-confirm logic:

| Handler | File:Line | Field | Confirm Event | Confirm Interface |
|---------|-----------|-------|---------------|-------------------|
| `::toggle-selection` | selection.cljs:1017 | `:selection/selected` (set) | `::confirm-tutor-selection` | `(fn [db _])` — reads from db |
| `::select-player-target` | selection.cljs:1341 | `:selection/selected-target` (single) | Routes by type (see below) | Varies |
| `::select-cast-time-object-target` | selection.cljs:1528 | `:selection/selected-target` (single) | `::confirm-cast-time-target` | `(fn [db [_ target-id]])` — takes arg |
| `::select-ability-object-target` | abilities.cljs:281 | `:selection/selected-target` (single) | `::confirm-ability-target` | `(fn [db [_ target-id]])` — takes arg |

**`::select-player-target` routing** — shared by three selection types:
| `:selection/type` | Confirm Event | Interface |
|---------------------|---------------|-----------|
| `:player-target` | `::confirm-player-target-selection` | `(fn [db _])` — reads from db |
| `:cast-time-targeting` | `::confirm-cast-time-target` | `(fn [db [_ target-id]])` — takes arg |
| `:ability-targeting` | `::confirm-ability-target` | `(fn [db [_ target-id]])` — takes arg |

**Key detail**: `confirm-ability-target` at abilities.cljs:150 is already extracted as a standalone function. The other confirm handlers need similar extraction.

Reference code:
- selection.cljs:1017-1044 — `::toggle-selection` full handler
- selection.cljs:1202-1243 — `::confirm-tutor-selection` full handler
- selection.cljs:1340-1343 — `::select-player-target` handler
- selection.cljs:1346-1400 — `::confirm-player-target-selection` handler
- selection.cljs:1515-1524 — `::confirm-cast-time-target` handler
- selection.cljs:1527-1541 — `::select-cast-time-object-target` handler
- abilities.cljs:150-175 — `confirm-ability-target` standalone function (already extracted)
- abilities.cljs:268-277 — `::confirm-ability-target` reg-event-db wrapper
- abilities.cljs:280-293 — `::select-ability-object-target` handler
- modals.cljs:629-631 — cast-time player target routes to confirm-cast-time-target
- modals.cljs:643-644 — ability player target routes to confirm-ability-target
- modals.cljs:655-657 — spell-effect player target routes to confirm-player-target-selection

### 2. Write failing tests first (TDD RED)

**Specific test scenarios:**

a) **test-single-select-tutor-auto-confirms**: Set up game with single-select tutor pending (e.g., Merchant Scroll). Dispatch `::toggle-selection` with a valid library card. Assert: `:game/pending-selection` is nil, card is in hand, library is shuffled.
   - Bug caught: Auto-confirm doesn't fire → pending-selection persists, card stays in library.

b) **test-multi-select-tutor-does-not-auto-confirm**: Set up Intuition (select-count=3). Dispatch `::toggle-selection` with first card. Assert: `:game/pending-selection` still present, selected has 1 card.
   - Bug caught: Auto-confirm firing on multi-select breaks Intuition.

c) **test-deselect-does-not-auto-confirm**: Set up single-select tutor, select a card, then toggle same card (deselect). Assert: pending-selection still present, selected is empty.
   - Bug caught: Auto-confirm on deselect prevents changing mind.

d) **test-cast-time-object-target-auto-confirms**: Set up cast-time targeting (e.g., Recoup). Dispatch `::select-cast-time-object-target` with valid target. Assert: pending-selection is nil, spell on stack with target set.
   - Bug caught: Cast-time object targeting still requires 2 clicks.

e) **test-cast-time-object-deselect-does-not-auto-confirm**: Select target, then click same target (deselects to nil). Assert: pending-selection persists.
   - Bug caught: Deselect-to-nil triggers confirm with nil target.

f) **test-player-target-auto-confirms**: Set up spell-effect player target. Dispatch `::select-player-target` with :opponent. Assert: pending-selection is nil, effect executed.
   - Bug caught: Player target still requires 2 clicks.

g) **test-cast-time-player-target-auto-confirms**: Set up cast-time targeting that targets a player. Dispatch `::select-player-target`. Assert: pending-selection cleared, correct confirm event used (::confirm-cast-time-target, NOT ::confirm-player-target-selection).
   - Bug caught: Wrong confirm event dispatched for cast-time player targeting.

h) **test-ability-object-target-auto-confirms**: Set up ability targeting (e.g., Seal of Cleansing targeting artifact). Dispatch `::select-ability-object-target` with valid target. Assert: pending-selection is nil, ability activated.
   - Bug caught: Ability object targeting still requires 2 clicks.

i) **test-ability-player-target-auto-confirms**: Set up ability targeting a player (e.g., Cephalid Coliseum threshold). Dispatch `::select-player-target`. Assert: pending-selection cleared, correct confirm event used (::confirm-ability-target).
   - Bug caught: Wrong confirm event for ability player targeting.

j) **test-find-nothing-still-works**: Set up single-select tutor. Dispatch `::confirm-tutor-selection` directly with empty selection. Assert: pending-selection cleared, no card moved, library shuffled.
   - Bug caught: Find Nothing path broken by auto-confirm changes.

### 3. Implementation checklist

- [ ] Extract `confirm-tutor-selection-impl` function from `::confirm-tutor-selection` (selection.cljs:1202). Takes `db` (app-db), returns `db`. The reg-event-db handler becomes a thin wrapper calling this function.

- [ ] Extract `confirm-player-target-impl` function from `::confirm-player-target-selection` (selection.cljs:1346). Takes `db`, returns `db`.

- [ ] Extract `confirm-cast-time-target-impl` function from `::confirm-cast-time-target` (selection.cljs:1515). Takes `db` and `target-id`, returns `db`. (Note: `confirm-ability-target` at abilities.cljs:150 is already extracted.)

- [ ] Modify `::toggle-selection` (selection.cljs:1017): After computing `new-selected`, add auto-confirm check:
  ```clojure
  ;; After line 1043 (assoc-in new-selected)
  ;; Check: single-select tutor completed?
  (if (and (= selection-type :tutor) (= max-count 1) (= (count new-selected) 1))
    (confirm-tutor-selection-impl (assoc-in db [...] new-selected))
    (assoc-in db [...] new-selected))
  ```

- [ ] Modify `::select-player-target` (selection.cljs:1341): After setting selected-target, route auto-confirm by `:selection/type`:
  ```clojure
  (let [db-with-target (assoc-in db [...] target-player-id)
        selection-type (:selection/type (get db :game/pending-selection))]
    (case selection-type
      :player-target (confirm-player-target-impl db-with-target)
      :cast-time-targeting (confirm-cast-time-target-impl db-with-target target-player-id)
      :ability-targeting (... call ability confirm with target-player-id)
      db-with-target))  ;; fallback: no auto-confirm
  ```

- [ ] Modify `::select-cast-time-object-target` (selection.cljs:1528): After computing new-selection, if non-nil (not deselect), call `confirm-cast-time-target-impl` with object-id.

- [ ] Modify `::select-ability-object-target` (abilities.cljs:281): After computing new-selection, if non-nil, call `confirm-ability-target` (already extracted at L150) with appropriate args. Note: this handler is in abilities.cljs, so it may need to require selection helpers or the confirm logic lives in abilities.cljs already.

- [ ] For ability auto-confirm from `::select-player-target`: The player-target handler is in selection.cljs but ability confirm is in abilities.cljs. To avoid circular dependency, dispatch via `rf/dispatch-sync` (synchronous dispatch) for the ability confirm case, OR restructure to call the confirm function directly (abilities.cljs:150 is already a standalone fn — selection.cljs would need to require abilities namespace, which is fine since game.cljs already requires it).

### 4. Key Considerations

**Dispatch vs direct function call (RESOLVED):**
Using `rf/dispatch` would be async (next tick) causing modal flicker. Extract confirm logic into plain functions called synchronously within the toggle handler. `confirm-ability-target` is already extracted at abilities.cljs:150.

**Player-target routing by selection type:**
`::select-player-target` is shared between 3 selection types. Must check `:selection/type` to dispatch to correct confirm function.

**Cross-namespace dependency for ability confirm:**
`::select-player-target` is in selection.cljs but `confirm-ability-target` is in abilities.cljs. Current dep DAG: game -> selection, game -> abilities. Adding selection -> abilities would be new but not circular. Alternatively, use `rf/dispatch-sync` for the ability case.

**Confirm handler interface differences:**
- tutor and player-target confirm: read target from db state, no args needed
- cast-time and ability confirm: take target-id as argument
Auto-confirm must pass target-id for cast-time and ability cases.

**Edge Case: Empty library (0 candidates):**
Modal shows only Find Nothing button. No cards to click, so auto-confirm never fires. Naturally handled.

**Edge Case: Tutor with pile-choice (Intuition):**
Intuition has select-count=3, so max-count=3, auto-confirm condition (max-count=1) is false. Unaffected.

**Edge Case: Deselect to nil for object targets:**
`::select-cast-time-object-target` and `::select-ability-object-target` set selected-target to nil on deselect. Auto-confirm must check `(some? new-selection)` before firing.

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ NO removing Find Nothing button (fail-to-find is MTG rules-correct)
- ❌ NO auto-confirm on multi-select types (max-count > 1)
- ❌ NO auto-confirm when deselecting (count drops to 0 or selected-target becomes nil)
- ❌ NO rf/dispatch for auto-confirm (causes modal flicker — use direct function call or rf/dispatch-sync)
- ❌ NO modifying modals.cljs (behavior change is in event handlers only)
- ❌ NO auto-confirm on pile-choice, graveyard-return, or peek-and-select
- ❌ NO circular namespace dependencies (if selection needs ability confirm, check dep DAG is safe)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Single-select tutor (select-count=1) auto-confirms on card click — no pending-selection after
- [ ] Multi-select tutor (select-count>1) does NOT auto-confirm after first card click
- [ ] Deselecting a card does NOT trigger auto-confirm
- [ ] Cast-time object targeting auto-confirms on target click
- [ ] Cast-time object deselecting does NOT auto-confirm
- [ ] Cast-time player targeting auto-confirms with correct confirm event
- [ ] Spell-effect player target auto-confirms on player click
- [ ] Ability object targeting auto-confirms on target click
- [ ] Ability player targeting auto-confirms with correct confirm event
- [ ] Find Nothing still works for single-select tutors
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no regressions)
- [ ] `make validate` passes (lint + format + tests)
close_reason: Obsolete: scope expanded to full selection system refactoring

title: Epic: Move trigger registry from atom into Datascript
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- ALL triggers (permanent, zone-change, game-rule) are Datascript entities, not atom state
- Trigger entities are components of their source object via :object/triggers with :db/isComponent true
- zones.cljs is pure — zero trigger-related side effects in move-to-zone
- Trigger dispatch queries Datascript, not an atom
- Filter-based activation: trigger activation determined by filter matching against event data, not zone-aware queries
- Game-rule triggers (draw-step, untap-step) use :trigger/always-active? true
- Token trigger cleanup is automatic via Datascript component cascade retraction
- Trigger ordering: auto-order for now, but dispatch design MUST support future player-chosen ordering (dispatch returns matched trigger list before creating stack-items)
- History/fork correctness: trigger state captured in DB snapshots automatically

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] trigger_registry.cljs atom (defonce registry (atom {})) is deleted
- [ ] No calls to register-trigger!, unregister-by-source!, or clear-registry! anywhere in codebase
- [ ] zones.cljs move-to-zone has no side effects — pure (db, args) -> db
- [ ] Trigger entities in Datascript with proper schema attributes
- [ ] :object/triggers component ref with :db/isComponent true
- [ ] Game-rule triggers (draw-step, untap-step) are Datascript entities with :trigger/always-active? true
- [ ] dispatch-event is a pure function of (db, event) -> db
- [ ] Filter matching handles :self resolution, :exclude-self, zone checks, exact match
- [ ] All 10 test files no longer need clear-registry! in fixtures
- [ ] All existing tests pass (no regressions)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO mutable state outside Datascript for triggers (reason: the entire point is eliminating the atom — any atom/var/volatile for trigger state defeats the purpose)
- NO trigger side effects in zones.cljs (reason: zones must be pure functions for history/fork correctness)
- NO special-casing game-rule triggers outside Datascript (reason: unified model — all triggers are DB entities)
- NO zone-aware activation queries as primary dispatch mechanism (reason: breaks for zone-change triggers like Gaeas Blessing — use filter-based activation instead)
- NO breaking the existing trigger dispatch contract (reason: City of Brass and City of Traitors must work identically before and after migration)
- NO building trigger ordering UI (reason: deferred — auto-order for now, but dispatch must return trigger list to support future ordering)

## Approach
Replace the atom-based trigger registry with Datascript trigger entities using filter-based activation. Triggers are Datascript entities linked to source objects as components (:db/isComponent true). Three trigger categories share one model:

1. Permanent triggers (City of Brass, future token Prowess): Created when permanent enters battlefield via tx-data included in zone-change transaction. Filter handles activation (e.g., source-zone check, :self matching).

2. Zone-change triggers (future Gaeas Blessing): Created at game init. Filter matches event from-zone/to-zone. No timing paradox because filter checks event data, not current card zone.

3. Game-rule triggers (draw-step, untap-step): Created at game init with :trigger/always-active? true. No source object.

API shifts from side effects to transaction data: functions return tx-data vectors that callers batch into existing d/transact! calls. dispatch-event becomes a pure function of (db, event) -> db.

## Architecture

### Schema additions (db/schema.cljs)
:trigger/event-type, :trigger/source (ref), :trigger/controller (ref), :trigger/filter, :trigger/effects, :trigger/description, :trigger/uses-stack?, :trigger/always-active?, :trigger/type
:object/triggers (ref, many, isComponent true)

### Module changes
- engine/trigger_registry.cljs -> pure query/tx functions (no atom)
- engine/trigger_dispatch.cljs -> takes db arg, queries Datascript
- engine/zones.cljs -> remove unregister-by-source! call
- events/game.cljs -> replace register-card-triggers! with tx-data in transactions
- engine/turn_based.cljs -> replace atom registration with tx-data at init
- 10 test files -> remove clear-registry! fixtures

### Dispatch flow
Event fires -> query triggers by event-type (Datalog) -> filter matching in code (:self, zones, exact match) -> split immediate/stacked -> execute immediate -> create stack-items for stacked (auto-ordered, design supports future player ordering)

## Parallelism Map

### Independent Work Streams
1. Schema + query functions (db/schema.cljs, engine/trigger_registry.cljs)
   - Add schema, implement pure query/tx API, unit tests
   - Estimated complexity: medium
2. Migration: creation sites (events/game.cljs, engine/turn_based.cljs)
   - Replace atom registration with Datascript tx-data
   - Depends on Stream 1
   - Estimated complexity: medium
3. Migration: dispatch (engine/trigger_dispatch.cljs)
   - Query Datascript instead of atom
   - Depends on Stream 2
   - Estimated complexity: medium
4. Cleanup: zones + tests (engine/zones.cljs, 10 test files)
   - Remove side effects from zones, remove clear-registry! from tests
   - Depends on Stream 3
   - Estimated complexity: low

### Stream Dependencies
- Stream 2 depends on Stream 1 (schema must exist before creating entities)
- Stream 3 depends on Stream 2 (entities must be in DB before querying)
- Stream 4 depends on Stream 3 (old code removed after new code works)
- ALL streams are sequential

### Suggested Waves
- Wave 1: Stream 1 (schema + API foundation)
- Wave 2: Stream 2 (migrate creation)
- Wave 3: Stream 3 (migrate dispatch)
- Wave 4: Stream 4 (cleanup)

### Parallelism Assessment
- Independent streams: 0 (everything sequential)
- Recommendation: solo execution
- Rationale: Tight coupling between schema, entity creation, query, and cleanup — each step validates the previous

## Design Rationale

### Problem
The trigger registry holds a defonce atom outside the immutable Datascript database. This breaks history (rewind does not rewind triggers), fork (different timelines share one registry), and purity (zones.cljs has side effects). It is the single biggest architectural violation in the codebase.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- engine/trigger_registry.cljs — 88-line atom registry with register/unregister/get/clear
- engine/trigger_dispatch.cljs — dispatch-event queries atom, creates stack-items. Two paths: immediate (uses-stack? false) and stacked (uses-stack? true)
- engine/zones.cljs:62 — move-to-zone calls unregister-by-source! as side effect (impurity)
- events/game.cljs:718 — register-card-triggers! called on battlefield entry
- engine/turn_based.cljs:25,35 — game-rule triggers registered at init
- Only 2 cards have triggers: City of Brass (becomes-tapped) and City of Traitors (land-entered)
- 10 test files use clear-registry! in fixtures

**Edge cases explored:**
- Gaeas Blessing: triggers from library on zone-change to graveyard. Cannot use zone-aware activation (card already moved when event fires). Filter-based activation with event from-zone/to-zone solves this.
- Cycling (Decree of Justice, Lightning Rift): triggers from hand (self) or battlefield (permanent). Filter-based activation handles both.
- Tokens with triggers (Monastery Mentor monks with Prowess): tokens created mid-game, not at init. Need trigger creation on battlefield entry. Component cascade handles cleanup.

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Datascript entities with filter-based activation (CHOSEN)
Triggers as Datascript entities, linked to source objects as components. Activation determined by filter matching against event data. No registration/unregistration concept for permanent triggers that stay in DB.

Pros: Full immutability, history/fork correct, zones pure, token cleanup automatic, unified model, extensible
Cons: Schema additions (~10 new attributes), trigger creation at battlefield entry still needed

Chosen because: Eliminates the architectural violation completely. Filter-based activation handles all edge cases.

#### 2. Keep atom, extend for non-battlefield triggers (REJECTED)
Extend existing atom registry for library/hand triggers. Add snapshot/restore for history.

REJECTED BECAUSE: Does not fix the fundamental problem — mutable state outside DB. Snapshot is a band-aid.
DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS: Datascript query performance becomes a measured bottleneck (extremely unlikely with <10 triggers per game).

#### 3. Query card data directly, no trigger entities (REJECTED)
No new entities. Query :card/triggers EDN directly off battlefield objects.

REJECTED BECAUSE: Fails on token triggers (no card def to query) and game-rule triggers (no card at all). Every edge case requires a workaround.
DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS: We abandon token support entirely (not happening).

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Schema additions for trigger entities
- Pure query/tx API replacing atom
- Migration of all registration, dispatch, and cleanup sites
- Removal of atom and clear-registry! calls
- All existing tests passing

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Trigger ordering UI (auto-order for now, dispatch design supports future ordering)
- Gaeas Blessing card implementation (architecture supports it, card deferred)
- Cycling trigger implementation (architecture supports it, cards deferred)
- Token trigger creation (architecture supports it, no token-creating cards exist yet)

### Open Questions
- Should trigger_registry.cljs be renamed (e.g., trigger_db.cljs) or keep the name with new implementation?
- Should filter matching remain in trigger_dispatch.cljs or move into the registry module?

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Game-rule triggers model? | Datascript entities | All triggers unified, no special cases |
| Zone cleanup approach? | Fully pure zones.cljs | Trigger cleanup via Datascript transactions, not side effects |
| Gaeas Blessing/cycling? | Filter-based activation | Event data carries from/to zones, no zone-aware activation |
| Token trigger cleanup? | Component cascade | :db/isComponent true auto-retracts when token removed |
| Trigger ordering? | Auto-order, design for future | Dispatch returns matched list before stack-item creation |

### Dead-End Paths

#### Simple Zone-Aware Queries
Why explored: Elegant model — zone IS activation state, no lifecycle needed
Investigation: Works for battlefield triggers. Fails for zone-change triggers (timing paradox) and cycling (hand triggers).
Why abandoned: Only works for battlefield triggers. Non-battlefield triggers require workarounds.

### Open Concerns Raised
- Trigger ordering (multiple simultaneous triggers) -> Auto-order now, dispatch supports future player choice
- Performance of Datalog queries vs atom deref -> Negligible with <10 triggers per game
- Schema migration complexity -> Manageable, ~10 new attributes, backward compatible
close_reason: All 4 tasks complete. Atom registry eliminated. All triggers are Datascript entities. zones.cljs pure. dispatch-event pure. make validate passes: 1248 tests, 0 failures. Review approved.

title: Storm copy target inheritance and cleanup
design:
## Goal
Complete the remaining epic criteria: propagate targets through storm copies, simplify descriptions.cljs, and remove :object/targets from schema.

## Context
From fizzle-e2em: targets now stored on :stack-item/targets. Bridge pattern copies targets to :object/targets for execute-effect. Storm copies need targets propagated. Schema cleanup depends on all consumers migrated.

## Implementation

### 1. Storm copy target inheritance (engine/triggers.cljs)
- In create-spell-copy (triggers.cljs:35-74):
  a. Find the original spell's stack-item to get its :stack-item/targets
  b. Query for stack-item by :stack-item/source matching source-object-id
