  c. Copy :stack-item/targets from original to the new copy's stack-item
  d. Need to add the targets AFTER create-stack-item (line 70-74)
- Note: currently Brain Freeze uses fixed :effect/target :opponent so this is forward-looking

### 2. History descriptions cleanup (history/descriptions.cljs)
- Simplify get-stack-item-targets (descriptions.cljs:40-47)
- Remove the object-targets fallback — just read :stack-item/targets directly
- This was the original workaround that caused the bug

### 3. Schema cleanup (db/schema.cljs)
- Remove :object/targets from schema
- IMPORTANT: The bridge in resolution.cljs still writes :object/targets
- The bridge is needed because effects/execute-effect reads :object/targets for :targeted-player
- With schema entry removed, d/db-with will still work (Datascript allows unschema'd attributes)
- BUT: removing from schema is a signal that it's deprecated
- Check if any tests manually set :object/targets — they need updating

### 4. Update tests that manually set :object/targets
- orims_chant_test.cljs:217 — manually stores on object for rules/resolve-spell path
- targeting_test.cljs — tests all-targets-legal? with old signature using :object/targets
- deep_analysis_test.cljs:492 — calls all-targets-legal? with object-id

## Success Criteria
- [ ] create-spell-copy propagates :stack-item/targets from original to copy
- [ ] get-stack-item-targets reads only from :stack-item/targets (no fallback)
- [ ] :object/targets removed from db/schema.cljs
- [ ] Tests that manually set :object/targets updated to use stack-item
- [ ] make validate passes
close_reason: Storm copy target inheritance implemented: targets propagated from original to copy in create-spell-copy. descriptions.cljs simplified (no object fallback). all-targets-legal? 2-arity removed (3-arity only). :object/targets removed from schema. Tests updated to use stack-item targets. All 1270 tests pass, make validate clean.

title: Extract remaining core.cljs zone components (battlefield, graveyard, stack, controls, turn-bar)
design:
## Goal
Extract ALL remaining view components from core.cljs into dedicated view files under views/. Migrate every inline :style map to Tailwind utility classes with @theme tokens. After this task, core.cljs contains only the app shell (requires + composition), no rendering logic.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Context
Three extractions completed (opponent.cljs, hand.cljs, mana_pool.cljs) established a clear pattern:
- Namespace with re-frame requires
- Tailwind classes via :class strings (no :style maps)
- @theme tokens for all colors
- Pure functions extracted and tested separately
- Layout-agnostic components (no grid/position knowledge)

## Implementation

### 1. Add new @theme tokens to src/css/app.css

Add these tokens to the @theme block:

```css
/* Mana button backgrounds */
--color-mana-bg-white: #EEE;
--color-mana-bg-blue: #2a4a6a;
--color-mana-bg-black: #3a2a4a;
--color-mana-bg-red: #5a2a2a;
--color-mana-bg-green: #2a4a2a;

/* Graveyard state colors */
--color-gy-selected-bg: #3a2a5a;
--color-gy-card-bg: #2a1a2a;
--color-gy-flashback-border: #6a4a8a;
--color-gy-flashback-text: #cc88aa;
--color-flashback-badge-bg: #6a2a6a;

/* Disabled/dim UI states */
--color-btn-disabled-bg: #222;
--color-surface-dim: #333;
--color-perm-text: #ccc;
```

Total: 13 new tokens. Token reuse for existing colors:
- #555 -> border (existing)
- #666 -> perm-text-tapped (existing)
- #888 -> text-dim (existing)
- #999 -> text-label (existing)
- #eee -> text (existing)
- #aaa -> text-muted (existing)
- #1a1a2a -> surface-raised (existing)
- #2a2a3a -> surface-hover (existing, exact match for flashback-castable bg)
- #4A9BD9 -> accent (existing, for active phase bg)
- #FFD700 -> border-selected (existing)
- #2a6a2a -> btn-enabled-bg (existing)
- #444 -> perm-border-tapped (existing)

### 2. Create views/battlefield.cljs

New file: src/main/fizzle/views/battlefield.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.views.battlefield

Move from core.cljs (lines 19-153):
- mana-color-display constant (lines 19-24) - rename to mana-button-config, only used here
- get-producible-colors (lines 44-65) - pure function, extract and test
- get-activated-abilities (lines 68-75) - pure function, extract and test
- mana-button (lines 27-41) - private component
- ability-button (lines 78-92) - private component
- permanent-view (lines 95-140) - private component
- battlefield-view (lines 143-153) - public component

Requires:
```clojure
[fizzle.events.abilities :as ability-events]
[fizzle.subs.game :as subs]
[re-frame.core :as rf]
```

Tailwind conversions for mana-button:
- Container: py-0.5 px-1.5 mx-0.5 border border-border rounded text-[11px] font-bold
- Enabled: cursor-pointer + dynamic bg/text from mana-button-config via class function
- Tapped: cursor-default bg-surface-dim text-border

Mana button bg class function (mana-bg-class):
```clojure
(defn mana-bg-class [color]
  (case color
    :white "bg-mana-bg-white text-mana-white"
    :blue "bg-mana-bg-blue text-mana-blue"
    :black "bg-mana-bg-black text-mana-black"
    :red "bg-mana-bg-red text-mana-red"
    :green "bg-mana-bg-green text-mana-green"
    "bg-surface-dim text-mana-colorless"))
```
IMPORTANT: Tailwind scans compiled JS for complete class strings. Each full class name (e.g. "bg-mana-bg-blue") must appear as a literal string — NOT constructed dynamically with str+name.

Tailwind conversions for ability-button:
- Container: py-0.5 px-2 m-0.5 border border-border rounded text-[11px] font-bold

### 3. Create views/graveyard.cljs

New file: src/main/fizzle/views/graveyard.cljs

```

### 4. Create views/stack.cljs

New file: src/main/fizzle/views/stack.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.views.stack

Move from core.cljs (lines 220-251):
- stack-item-view (lines 220-238) - private component

```

Tailwind conversions for stack-item-view:
- Container: border border-perm-border-tapped rounded p-1.5 px-2.5 mb-1 bg-surface-raised text-perm-text text-[13px]

Tailwind conversions for stack-view:

### 5. Create views/phase_bar.cljs

New file: src/main/fizzle/views/phase_bar.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.views.phase-bar

Move from core.cljs (lines 254-300):
- phase-indicator (lines 254-262) - private component
- turn-bar (lines 265-300) - public, rename to phase-bar-view

Requires:
```clojure
[fizzle.events.game :as events]

[re-frame.core :as rf]
```

Tailwind conversions for phase-indicator:
- Container: py-1 px-2 rounded text-[11px]
- Active: bg-accent text-white
- Inactive: bg-transparent text-perm-text-tapped

Tailwind conversions for turn-bar (phase-bar-view):
- Container: flex items-center gap-4 py-2.5 px-4 mb-4 bg-surface-raised border-b border-surface-dim
- Turn label: font-bold text-text text-sm
- Phase strip: flex gap-1
- Button: py-1.5 px-3.5 border border-border rounded font-bold text-[13px]
- Button enabled: cursor-pointer bg-btn-enabled-bg text-white
- Button disabled: cursor-not-allowed bg-surface-dim text-perm-text-tapped opacity-50

Note: events/phases is referenced for phase list. Need to require events namespace or pass phases as data.
Check: core.cljs line 283 uses events/phases — this is the ordered phase vector from events.game namespace.
Require [fizzle.events.game :as events] and reference events/phases.

Subscriptions: ::subs/current-phase, ::subs/current-turn, ::subs/stack
Events: ::events/start-turn, ::events/advance-phase

### 6. Create views/controls.cljs

New file: src/main/fizzle/views/controls.cljs
Namespace: fizzle.views.controls

Move from core.cljs (lines 303-331):

### 7. Refactor core.cljs to app shell

Only app, mount-root, init remain.

### 8. Tests

Create tests for extracted pure functions:

src/test/fizzle/views/controls_test.cljs:
- Test btn-class with enabled=true and enabled=false if made public
- Skip if kept private (defn-) — consistent with pattern

### 9. Verify
- make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- Zero inline :style maps in all new view files
- Zero inline :style maps remaining in core.cljs
- No hardcoded hex colors in any new view file
- All existing functionality preserved

## Success Criteria
- [ ] views/battlefield.cljs exists with battlefield-view, permanent-view, mana-button, ability-button, get-producible-colors, get-activated-abilities, mana-bg-class
- [ ] views/graveyard.cljs exists with graveyard-view and graveyard-card-view
- [ ] views/stack.cljs exists with stack-view and stack-item-view
- [ ] views/phase_bar.cljs exists with phase-bar-view and phase-indicator
- [ ] views/controls.cljs exists with controls-view
- [ ] 13 new @theme tokens added to app.css (mana-bg-*, gy-*, btn-disabled-bg, surface-dim, perm-text, flashback-badge-bg)
- [ ] core.cljs reduced to app shell: only app, mount-root, init functions remain (no game rendering logic)
- [ ] Zero inline :style maps in ALL new view files
- [ ] Zero inline :style maps in core.cljs
- [ ] No hardcoded hex colors in any new view file (all @theme tokens)
- [ ] All components layout-agnostic (no grid/position styling)
- [ ] battlefield_test.cljs has tests for get-producible-colors (5+ cases), get-activated-abilities (4+ cases), mana-bg-class (6+ cases)
- [ ] No changes to event/subscription contracts
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO inline :style maps (use Tailwind utility classes)
- NO hardcoded hex colors (use @theme tokens)
- NO layout positioning in components (grid parent handles placement)
- NO game logic in views (subscribe and dispatch only)
- NO breaking existing subscription/event contracts
- NO dynamic Tailwind class construction via str+name (e.g., (str "bg-mana-bg-" (name color))) — Tailwind cannot detect dynamically generated class names at build time; use case/cond with literal strings
- NO Tailwind v3 patterns (tailwind.config.js, @tailwind directives)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Tailwind Class Detection:**
Tailwind v4 scans compiled JS for class name string literals. All class names must appear as complete strings in source code. The mana-bg-class function MUST use case with literal strings like "bg-mana-bg-blue", NOT (str "bg-mana-bg-" (name color)). This is the same pattern used successfully in mana_pool.cljs mana-color-class.

**mana-color-display Constant Scope:**
The mana-color-display map (W/U/B/R/G symbols + hex colors) is only used by mana-button in core.cljs. When moved to battlefield.cljs, the hex color values become unnecessary (replaced by Tailwind classes). Only the symbol characters are still needed. Simplify to just symbols if :color and :bg are eliminated. BUT: mana_pool.cljs already has its own mana-symbols map. battlefield.cljs can define its own or import from mana_pool. Since the battlefield needs symbol + bg-class pairing, a local mana-button-config or just using the mana-bg-class function with a separate symbol lookup is cleaner.

**events/phases Reference:**
turn-bar (line 283) references events/phases which is a def in fizzle.events.game. phase_bar.cljs must require [fizzle.events.game :as events] and use events/phases. Verify this var is public (not defn-).

**Private vs Public Functions:**
get-producible-colors and get-activated-abilities are defn- in core.cljs. When moved to battlefield.cljs they can remain defn- since only battlefield-view calls them. However, to test them, either:
1. Make them public (defn) — preferred for testability, follows opponent.cljs pattern
2. Test indirectly via battlefield-view — requires re-frame test infrastructure

Recommendation: Make get-producible-colors, get-activated-abilities, and mana-bg-class public (defn) for direct testing. Keep mana-button, ability-button, permanent-view as private (defn-).

**Graveyard Flashback Background Color:**
graveyard-card-view line 164 uses #2a2a3a for flashback-castable bg. This is an EXACT match for the existing surface-hover token (#2a2a3a). Reuse bg-surface-hover instead of creating a new token. Only the non-flashback graveyard card bg (#2a1a2a) needs a new gy-card-bg token.

**rotate Transform for Tapped Permanents:**
permanent-view line 111 uses :transform "rotate(6deg)" for tapped state. Tailwind arbitrary value: rotate-[6deg]. This works in Tailwind v4. Verify compiled CSS includes it.

**Bare Requires After Extraction:**
core.cljs currently has bare require [fizzle.events.selection] and [fizzle.events.abilities]. After extraction, battlefield.cljs requires abilities directly. But the bare require in core.cljs ensures the namespace is loaded for re-frame event registration. Keep bare requires in core.cljs for any event namespaces not directly required by view files.
Check: events.selection is required bare — no view file dispatches selection events directly (modals.cljs handles that). Keep it bare in core.cljs.
Check: events.abilities is now required by battlefield.cljs. Can remove bare require from core.cljs IF battlefield.cljs is loaded. But since core.cljs requires battlefield, the transitive require covers it. Safe to remove bare [fizzle.events.abilities] from core.cljs.

**font-family monospace in app:**
core.cljs line 339 has :font-family "monospace". Tailwind class: font-mono. This goes in the app root div.
close_reason: All success criteria met. 5 new view files created (battlefield, graveyard, stack, phase_bar, controls). core.cljs reduced to 53-line app shell. 13 new @theme tokens. Zero inline :style maps in new files or core.cljs. 16 new battlefield tests. make validate passes (996 tests, 2262 assertions).

title: Task 2: Implement Ray of Revelation card
design:
## Goal
Implement Ray of Revelation card with Scryfall-accurate oracle text, type-based enchantment targeting, and flashback.

## Effort Estimate
2-4 hours

## Context
Completed task fizzle-0uj: :destroy effect type now exists and handles target-ref resolution.

## Implementation

### 1. Use implement-card skill
REQUIRED: Use the implement-card skill for Scryfall oracle validation.
The skill will fetch oracle text and validate card definition.

### 2. Card definition structure
File: src/fizzle/cards/ray_of_revelation.cljs (new file)

Following deep_analysis.cljs and recoup.cljs patterns:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.ray-of-revelation)

(def ray-of-revelation
  {:card/id :ray-of-revelation
   :card/name "Ray of Revelation"
   :card/cmc 2
   :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :white 1}
   :card/colors #{:white}
   :card/types #{:instant}
   :card/text "Destroy target enchantment.\nFlashback {G}"
   
   ;; Type-based targeting for enchantments on battlefield
   :card/targeting 
   [{:target/id :target-enchantment
     :target/type :object
     :target/zone :battlefield
     :target/controller :any
     :target/criteria {:card/types #{:enchantment}}
     :target/required true}]
   
   ;; Destroy effect using target-ref
   :card/effects 
   [{:effect/type :destroy
     :effect/target-ref :target-enchantment}]
   
   ;; Flashback {G} from graveyard, exile on resolve
   :card/alternate-costs 
   [{:alternate/id :flashback
     :alternate/zone :graveyard
     :alternate/mana-cost {:green 1}
     :alternate/on-resolve :exile}]})
```

### 3. Register card
Add to src/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs card pool (or appropriate registry).

### 4. Write integration tests
File: src/test/fizzle/cards/ray_of_revelation_test.cljs (new file)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] implement-card skill validates oracle text matches Scryfall
- [ ] test-ray-can-cast-targeting-enchantment passes
- [ ] test-ray-cannot-cast-without-enchantment-on-battlefield passes
- [ ] test-ray-flashback-casts-from-graveyard passes
- [ ] test-ray-exiles-after-flashback-resolution passes
- [ ] test-ray-rejects-non-enchantment-targets passes
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + all tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: No enchantments on battlefield**
- Card should NOT be castable when no valid targets exist
- Test: has-valid-targets? returns false with empty battlefield
- Reference: targeting.cljs:80 has-valid-targets?

**Edge Case: Target removed before resolution**
- If enchantment destroyed/exiled in response, effect fizzles
- :destroy effect already handles this (returns db unchanged if target missing)
- Test: Verify spell resolves gracefully when target gone

**Edge Case: Flashback with no targets**
- Same as regular cast - cannot cast flashback if no valid targets
- Flashback changes cost and zone, not targeting requirements

**Oracle Text Accuracy**
- MUST use implement-card skill to validate against Scryfall
- Card text must match exactly: "Destroy target enchantment.\nFlashback {G}"
- Anti-pattern: NO hardcoding text without Scryfall validation

**Reference Implementations**
- src/fizzle/cards/recoup.cljs - Flashback pattern
- src/fizzle/cards/deep_analysis.cljs - Alternate costs with exile
- src/test/fizzle/engine/targeting_test.cljs - Type-based targeting tests

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoding oracle text without Scryfall validation
- ❌ NO skipping implement-card skill
- ❌ NO targeting artifacts (Ray only targets enchantments)
- ❌ NO :effect/target (must use :effect/target-ref with stored targets)
- ❌ NO manual card text that doesn't match Scryfall exactly
close_reason: Closed

title: Add missing P1 corner case tests (cards/events)
design:
## Goal
Add tests for P1 corner cases in card implementations and event handlers.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Corner Cases to Add

### cards/* (P1 - Card Implementations)

| Corner Case | Bug It Catches | Test Name | File |
|-------------|----------------|-----------|------|
| Flashback on exiled card | Playing spells from wrong zone | test-flashback-from-exile-blocked | recoup_test.cljs |
| Targeting object in wrong zone | Invalid target acceptance | test-target-wrong-zone-rejected | targeting_test.cljs |

### events/* (P1 - Event Handlers)

| Corner Case | Bug It Catches | Test Name | File |
|-------------|----------------|-----------|------|
| Event with nil coeffects | Crash on nil db | test-event-nil-cofx-handled | init_game_test.cljs |
| Selection cancel cleanup | State left in pending-selection | test-selection-cancel-cleanup | tutor_multi_select_test.cljs |
| Double-submit selection | Duplicate card movement | test-selection-double-submit | tutor_multi_select_test.cljs |

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] Create test-flashback-from-exile-blocked in recoup_test.cljs
- [ ] Create test-target-wrong-zone-rejected in targeting_test.cljs
- [ ] Create test-event-nil-cofx-handled in appropriate event test file
- [ ] Create test-selection-cancel-cleanup in tutor_multi_select_test.cljs
- [ ] Create test-selection-double-submit in tutor_multi_select_test.cljs
- [ ] Run `make test` after each test addition
- [ ] Run `make validate` at the end

## Test Specifications

### test-flashback-from-exile-blocked
```clojure
(deftest test-flashback-from-exile-blocked
  (testing "Flashback only works from graveyard, not exile"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          ;; Add card with flashback to EXILE (not graveyard)
          [db' obj-id] (add-card-to-zone db :recoup :exile :player-1)
          ;; Add mana to pay flashback cost
          db'' (mana/add-mana db' :player-1 {:red 1 :black 1})]
      ;; Should NOT be castable from exile
      (is (false? (rules/can-cast-flashback? db'' :player-1 obj-id))
          "Flashback should not work from exile"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** Allowing flashback from wrong zone

### test-target-wrong-zone-rejected
```clojure
(deftest test-target-wrong-zone-rejected
  (testing "Targeting rejects objects in wrong zone"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          ;; Add card to LIBRARY (not valid target zone)
          [db' obj-id] (add-card-to-zone db :dark-ritual :library :player-1)
          targeting {:target/type :card
                     :target/zone :hand  ;; Expects hand
                     :target/criteria {}}]
      (is (false? (targeting/valid-target? db' obj-id targeting))
          "Object in wrong zone should not be valid target"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** Accepting targets from any zone

### test-selection-cancel-cleanup
```clojure
(deftest test-selection-cancel-cleanup
  (testing "Cancelling selection clears pending state"
    (let [db (create-pending-selection-state)  ;; Helper to set up pending
          db' (game/cancel-selection db)]
      (is (nil? (:pending-selection db'))
          "Pending selection should be cleared after cancel"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** UI stuck in selection mode after cancel

### test-selection-double-submit
```clojure
(deftest test-selection-double-submit
  (testing "Submitting same selection twice is idempotent"
    (let [db (create-pending-selection-state)
          selection {:selection/selected #{card-id}}
          db' (game/execute-selection db selection)
          db'' (game/execute-selection db' selection)]
      ;; Second submit should either:
      ;; - Return db' unchanged (no-op), OR
      ;; - Throw error (rejected)
      ;; Should NOT move card twice
      (is (= 1 (count (get-cards-moved-to-hand db'')))
          "Double submit should not double-move cards"))))
```
**Bug it catches:** Race condition moving cards twice

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 5 corner case tests written and passing
- [ ] Each test has clear comment stating what bug it catches
- [ ] Tests exercise actual production code paths
- [ ] No tautological assertions
- [ ] `make test` passes
- [ ] `make validate` passes

## Key Considerations

**Flashback Zone Check:**
Flashback is specifically a graveyard mechanic. The rules engine should validate zone before allowing flashback cast. If `can-cast-flashback?` doesn't exist, may need to check `can-cast?` with flashback mode.

**Selection Idempotency:**
Selection handlers may need guards against re-processing. Test documents expected behavior.

**Helper Functions Needed:**
- `create-pending-selection-state` - may need to add this helper
- `get-cards-moved-to-hand` - may need to count zone changes

## Anti-patterns
- ❌ Testing mock behavior instead of production code
- ❌ Happy path only assertions
- ❌ Using `some?` as primary assertion
- ❌ Ignoring test if function doesn't exist (should note as blocked)
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature: Alternate Casting System with Flashback
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Cards can define alternate casting modes via `:card/alternate-costs`
- Alternate modes specify: zone restriction, mana cost, additional costs, on-resolve behavior
- Casting system finds all valid modes for a card based on current zone
- Mode selection UI appears when multiple valid modes exist
- Flashback spells exile when leaving stack (resolve, counter, or fizzle)
- Storm count increments on flashback casts (flashback is still casting)
- Deep Analysis card implemented with correct Oracle text

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] Deep Analysis castable from hand for {3}{U}, draws 2 cards
- [ ] Deep Analysis castable from graveyard for {1}{U} + 3 life, draws 2 cards
- [ ] Flashback Deep Analysis exiles after resolution
- [ ] Flashback Deep Analysis exiles if countered
- [ ] Storm count increments when casting via flashback
- [ ] Mode selection UI appears for cards with multiple valid modes from same zone
- [ ] All existing tests pass
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO hardcoding Deep Analysis logic in engine (must use generic alternate cost system)
- ❌ NO separate 'flashback zone' concept (flashback cards live in graveyard, casting checks zone)
- ❌ NO flashback spells going to graveyard on counter (must exile per MTG rules)
- ❌ NO breaking existing card definitions (alternate-costs is additive)
- ❌ NO skipping storm increment for flashback casts (flashback is casting a spell)

## Approach
Implement a generic Alternate Casting System that models flashback as one instance of alternate costs. Cards define `:card/alternate-costs` with zone restrictions, costs, and resolution behavior. The casting system is extended to find all valid modes (primary + alternates) and present choices when multiple modes are available. This approach enables future cards like Gush (return Islands), Foil (discard), Fireblast (sacrifice lands) without engine changes—only new cost types.

## Architecture
### Data Model
```clojure
{:card/alternate-costs 
 [{:alternate/id :flashback
   :alternate/zone :graveyard
   :alternate/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :blue 1}
   :alternate/additional-costs [{:cost/type :pay-life :cost/amount 3}]
   :alternate/on-resolve :exile}]}
```

### Key Components
- `src/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs` - Add `get-casting-modes`, `can-cast-mode?`
- `src/fizzle/engine/costs.cljs` - Already has `:pay-life`, add cost selection flow
- `src/fizzle/engine/stack.cljs` - Track `:stack/cast-mode`, use for resolution destination
- `src/fizzle/events/` - Cast event handles mode selection, storm increment
- `src/fizzle/views/` - Mode selection UI component
- `src/fizzle/cards/` - Deep Analysis definition

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Deep Analysis requires flashback (cast from graveyard for alternate cost). The engine currently only supports casting from hand. Need a system that handles flashback AND future alternate cost cards (Gush, Foil, Fireblast, Daze) without per-card special cases.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- `rules.cljs:28` - `can-cast?` hardcodes `(= :hand (:object/zone obj))`
- `costs.cljs:167-206` - `:pay-life` cost type already implemented
- `effects.cljs:251-282` - `:draw` effect type already implemented
- `iggy_pop.cljs` - Card definition patterns established

**External:**
- Scryfall Oracle: Deep Analysis = {3}{U} sorcery, target draws 2, flashback {1}{U} pay 3 life
- MTG Rules: Flashback exiles when leaving stack for ANY reason (resolve, counter, fizzle)
- MTG Rules: Flashback casts count toward storm

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Alternate Casting Abilities ✓

**What it is:** Generic system where cards define alternate casting modes with zone restrictions, costs, and resolution effects. Casting system finds all valid modes and presents choice when multiple exist.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed card definition patterns in iggy_pop.cljs - data-driven approach
- Verified :pay-life cost already exists in costs.cljs
- Checked stack resolution flow - can add cast-mode tracking

**Pros:**
- Enables Gush, Foil, Fireblast, Daze without engine changes
- Consistent with codebase's data-driven philosophy
- Flashback is just one alternate mode type

**Cons:**
- More upfront work than minimal flashback

**Chosen because:** User confirmed future decks need Gush, Foil, etc. Investment in generic system pays off.

#### 2. Zone-Aware Casting ❌

**What it is:** Minimal extension - just make can-cast? check graveyard for flashback keyword.

**Why we looked at this:** Simpler, faster for just Deep Analysis

**Investigation:**
- Would require special-casing flashback in can-cast?
- Wouldn't generalize to Gush (alternate cost from hand)

**Pros:**
- Less code initially

**Cons:**
- Doesn't handle non-flashback alternates
- Would need rewriting for Gush

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** User plans Premodern/Pauper support - Gush, Foil, Daze all need alternates from hand.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Scope permanently limited to flashback-only cards.

#### 3. Graveyard Action System ❌

**What it is:** Separate system for graveyard-castable cards.

**Why we looked at this:** Clean separation of concerns

**Investigation:**
- Would duplicate casting logic
- Storm interaction unclear

**Pros:**
- Clear separation

**Cons:**
- Code duplication
- Storm might not trigger correctly

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Duplicates casting logic, storm interaction risky.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Complete engine rewrite.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- Alternate cost framework
- Deep Analysis card
- Flashback mode (zone=graveyard, on-resolve=exile)
- Mode selection UI
- Storm interaction

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- Other cost types (:return-lands, :sacrifice-lands, :sacrifice-creature, :exile-from-gy)
- Recoup (grants flashback to other cards)
- Other flashback cards (Ray of Revelation, Cabal Therapy)

### Open Questions
- Exact UI styling for mode selector (decide during implementation)
- Whether to show mode picker for single-mode cards (decided: no, auto-cast)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| What is 'deep analysis'? | MTG card Deep Analysis | Implement flashback card |
| How handle flashback? | Full flashback mechanic | Need alternate casting system |
| Target player targeting? | Any player | Full rules accuracy |
| Other flashback cards? | Ray of Revelation, Recoup, Flash of Insight | System must be extensible |
| Storm interaction? | Yes, flashback counts | Ensure cast event increments storm |
| Which approach? | Approach 2 (Alternate Casting) | Generic system for Gush, Foil, etc. |
| Data model OK? | Yes | Proceed with :card/alternate-costs |
| Mode selection UX? | Yes, popover when multiple modes | Auto-cast when single mode |
| Build all cost types now? | No, framework + Deep Analysis | Incremental cost types later |
| Phase 1 scope? | Framework + Deep Analysis | Cost types in future epics |
| Edge cases (counter)? | Flashback still exiles | Handle in move-spell-off-stack |

### Research Deep-Dives

#### Flashback Implementation Status
**Question explored:** Does flashback exist in codebase?
**Sources consulted:**
- rules.cljs - can-cast? implementation
- fizzle-design.md - flashback design notes
- cards/ - existing card definitions

**Findings:**
- Flashback mentioned in design doc but not implemented
- can-cast? hardcodes hand zone check
- :pay-life cost already exists
- :draw effect already exists

**Conclusion:** Need to extend casting rules, not start from scratch

#### Alternative Cost Cards in Premodern/Pauper
**Question explored:** What other cards need alternate costs?
**Sources consulted:**
- User input on planned decks
- MTG card database knowledge

**Findings:**
- Gush: return 2 Islands instead of {4}{U}
- Foil: discard Island + card instead of {2}{U}{U}
- Daze: return Island instead of {1}{U}
- Fireblast: sacrifice 2 Mountains instead of {4}{R}{R}
- Cabal Therapy: flashback by sacrificing creature

**Conclusion:** Alternate casting system must be generic, not flashback-specific

### Dead-End Paths

#### Zone-Aware Casting (Approach 1)
**Why explored:** Simpler initial implementation
**Investigation:**
- Would require special-casing flashback
- Wouldn't handle Gush (alternate from hand)

**Why abandoned:** User confirmed Gush/Foil needed - can't special-case

#### Graveyard Action System (Approach 3)
**Why explored:** Clean separation of concerns
**Investigation:**
- Would duplicate casting logic
- Storm interaction unclear

**Why abandoned:** Duplication risk, storm might break

### Open Concerns Raised

- 'Build all cost types now?' → No, framework + Deep Analysis only. Cost types added when cards need them.
- 'Countered flashback spells?' → Still exile per MTG rules. Implemented in move-spell-off-stack.
- 'Remand-style effects?' → Deferred, not in Premodern. Can add full replacement effect later if needed.
close_reason: Closed

description: Storm practice requires rapid repetitive actions. Add keyboard shortcuts: space/enter for Yield (resolve top of stack), p for Play (cast/play land), z/ctrl-z for Undo. Should be discoverable (tooltip on buttons showing shortcut) and work only when on game screen. title: Quick Undo: pop last action without creating fork
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Quick undo pops the most recent history entry, deleting it entirely from the timeline
- Repeatable: each press pops one more entry (undo stack behavior)
- Only works when at the tip (most recent position); no-op otherwise
- Cannot undo past init-game (position 0 is the floor; position -1 = no entries)
- Works on any branch (main timeline or fork)
- When all entries on a fork are popped, auto-delete the fork (with cascade) and return to parent at branch-point
- Cascade-delete any child forks that branched from the popped entry position
- Restores game/db from the new tip snapshot after pop

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] pop-entry function in history/core.cljs correctly removes tip entry on main
- [ ] pop-entry correctly removes tip entry on fork
- [ ] pop-entry auto-deletes empty fork and returns to parent timeline
- [ ] pop-entry cascade-deletes child forks branching from popped position
- [ ] can-pop? predicate returns false at position 0, position -1, and non-tip
- [ ] ::pop-entry re-frame event handler wired in history/events.cljs
- [ ] ::can-pop? subscription in subs/history.cljs
- [ ] Undo button in step-controls UI, disabled when can-pop? is false
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (no regressions)
- [ ] New tests cover: main pop, fork pop, empty fork auto-delete, cascade, boundary conditions
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO modifying existing step-back/step-forward behavior (quick undo is additive, not a replacement)
- NO soft-delete or hidden entries (entries are destroyed, not hidden — user chose 'delete entirely')
- NO undo past init-game (position 0 is always preserved)
- NO undo from non-tip positions (only tip pop is supported — use step-back for navigation)
- NO orphaned child forks (if a popped entry had child forks, they must be cascade-deleted)

## Approach
Add a `pop-entry` function to `history/core.cljs` that removes the tip entry from the current branch. On main: pop from `:history/main`, decrement position, restore previous snapshot. On fork: pop from `:fork/entries`; if empty, delete fork via existing `delete-fork` (which already handles cascade) and switch to parent. Before popping, find and cascade-delete any child forks whose branch-point equals the entry being removed.

Wire through re-frame: `::pop-entry` event in `history/events.cljs`, `::can-pop?` subscription in `subs/history.cljs`, Undo button in `views/history.cljs` step-controls.

## Architecture
- `history/core.cljs:67-75` — existing `step-to` for snapshot restore pattern
- `history/core.cljs:53-64` — existing `append-entry` shows main vs fork branching
- `history/core.cljs:137-147` — existing `find-descendants` for cascade logic
- `history/core.cljs:150-167` — existing `delete-fork` with cascade (reuse for empty fork cleanup)
- `history/events.cljs` — new `::pop-entry` event
- `subs/history.cljs` — new `::can-pop?` subscription
- `views/history.cljs:17-29` — `step-controls` where Undo button goes

## Parallelism Map

### Independent Work Streams
1. **Core logic** (history/core.cljs + tests)
   - pop-entry, can-pop?, find-child-forks-at-position
   - Estimated complexity: medium
2. **Event + Sub wiring** (history/events.cljs, subs/history.cljs)
   - ::pop-entry event, ::can-pop? subscription
   - Estimated complexity: low
3. **UI** (views/history.cljs)
   - Undo button in step-controls
   - Estimated complexity: low

### Stream Dependencies
- Stream 2 depends on Stream 1 (event calls core function)
- Stream 3 depends on Stream 2 (UI dispatches event, subscribes to sub)

### Suggested Waves
- Wave 1: Stream 1 (core logic)
- Wave 2: Stream 2 + 3 (event, sub, UI — sequential but trivial)

### File Ownership Boundaries
| Stream | Owns (exclusive) | Shared (needs coordination) |
|--------|-------------------|-----------------------------|
| Core logic | history/core.cljs | — |
| Event+Sub | history/events.cljs, subs/history.cljs | history/core.cljs (imports) |
| UI | views/history.cljs | subs/history.cljs (subscribes) |

### Parallelism Assessment
- Independent streams: 0 (all sequential)
- Recommendation: solo execution
- Rationale: Linear dependency chain — each stream depends on the previous. Small scope (~50 lines of new code total).

## Design Rationale
### Problem
Current undo (step-back) preserves all history entries and auto-forks when you act from a non-tip position. For practice reps, players just want to undo a misclick without fork clutter.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- history/core.cljs:53-64 — append-entry shows main/fork branching pattern to mirror for pop
- history/core.cljs:150-167 — delete-fork already has cascade via find-descendants
- history/core.cljs:45-50 — at-tip? predicate exists, can-pop? builds on same pattern
- views/history.cljs:17-29 — step-controls is the natural home for Undo button

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. pop-entry in history/core.cljs ✓
**What it is:** New function that removes tip entry and restores previous snapshot. Mirrors append-entry in reverse.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed append-entry pattern — main vs fork branch is clean
- delete-fork already handles cascade-delete of descendants
- step-to pattern shows how to restore game/db from snapshot

**Pros:**
- Clean O(1) operation (pop + snapshot restore)
- Leverages existing cascade-delete infrastructure
- Mirrors append-entry for symmetry

**Cons:**
- Must handle child fork cascade (but find-descendants exists)

**Chosen because:** Direct, simple, leverages existing infrastructure

#### 2. Step-back + truncate ✗
**What it is:** Reuse step-to to go back, then truncate entries vector after new position.

**Why we looked at this:** Seemed like it would reuse more existing code.

**Investigation:**
- step-to only changes position, doesn't modify entries
- Truncation would need separate logic for main vs fork
- Fork empty-detection would still need new code

**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Two-step operation with no code reuse advantage. pop-entry is cleaner.

**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** The core data structures change fundamentally.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:**
- pop-entry core function with all edge cases
- re-frame event + subscription
- Undo button in step-controls

**Out of scope:**
- Keyboard shortcut (separate backlog item fizzle-txiw)
- Redo of popped entries (user chose delete entirely)
- Changing step-back/step-forward behavior

### Open Questions
- Exact button placement in step-controls (left of Back? Between Back/Fwd? Separate row?)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Undo depth? | Repeatable undo stack | Each press pops one entry, not just single-shot |
| Entry fate? | Delete entirely | No soft-delete, no redo buffer |
| Fork compatibility? | Works on any branch | Must handle fork-specific pop + empty fork auto-delete |
| Empty fork behavior? | Auto-delete, return to parent | Reuse delete-fork with cascade |
| Position restriction? | Tip only | No pop from middle of timeline |

### Open Concerns Raised
- Child fork cascade on pop → Handled by find-descendants + delete-fork (existing infra)
- Undo past init-game → Prevented by can-pop? checking position > 0
close_reason: Closed

title: Epic: Best-in-Class Developer Experience
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Single command REPL: `make repl` starts node REPL ready to eval ClojureScript
- Single command validation: `make validate` runs lint + format-check + tests, fails fast
- All dev commands documented in CLAUDE.md for agent use
- Makefile is primary interface (familiar to Clojure devs)
- clj-kondo for static analysis/linting
- cljstyle for code formatting (check and fix modes)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] `make repl` starts REPL in one command, ready to eval immediately
- [ ] `make test` runs all tests and reports pass/fail
- [ ] `make lint` runs clj-kondo on src/, reports issues
- [ ] `make fmt-check` verifies formatting without changes
- [ ] `make fmt` auto-fixes formatting
- [ ] `make validate` runs lint + fmt-check + test sequentially, fails on first error
- [ ] `make dev` starts browser watch mode
- [ ] `make clean` removes build artifacts
- [ ] CLAUDE.md updated with Development Commands section
- [ ] All existing tests still pass

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO multi-step REPL setup (simplicity: one command must work)
- ❌ NO npm scripts as primary interface (consistency: Makefile is standard for Clojure projects)
- ❌ NO manual PATH exports in commands (usability: Makefile handles Java path internally)
- ❌ NO hard-coded absolute paths (portability: must work on any machine)

## Approach

Create a Makefile that wraps shadow-cljs commands with proper Java PATH handling. Add clj-kondo via npm for linting and cljstyle as a standalone tool for formatting. Update CLAUDE.md with all commands so the agent uses them consistently.

## Architecture

```
Makefile              # Primary interface
├── repl              # npx shadow-cljs node-repl
├── dev               # npx shadow-cljs watch app  
├── test              # npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js
├── lint              # clj-kondo --lint src/
├── fmt-check         # cljstyle check src/
├── fmt               # cljstyle fix src/
├── validate          # lint && fmt-check && test
└── clean             # rm -rf out/ .shadow-cljs/

CLAUDE.md             # Agent instructions
└── Development Commands section
```

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Current DX requires multiple steps for REPL (watch + connect), complex test commands with PATH exports, and no linting/formatting. This slows development and causes friction.

### Research Findings

**External:**
- shadow-cljs node-repl provides one-command REPL for non-browser dev
- clj-kondo is the standard ClojureScript linter (2M+ weekly downloads)
- cljstyle is the recommended formatter (successor to cljfmt)
- Makefile is idiomatic for Clojure projects

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Makefile with node-repl ✓

**What it is:** Use Makefile as primary interface, shadow-cljs node-repl for instant REPL.

**Chosen because:** Familiar to Clojure devs, one-command REPL, handles PATH internally.

#### 2. npm scripts ❌

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Not familiar to Clojure devs, user explicitly requested Makefile.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** User changes preference.

#### 3. deps.edn aliases ❌

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** shadow-cljs standalone is recommended over JVM invocation per official docs.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Need tight integration with other Clojure tooling.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Makefile with all dev commands
- clj-kondo linting
- cljstyle formatting
- CLAUDE.md updates

**Out of scope:**
- Editor integration (Calva/CIDER) - user can configure separately
- CI/CD setup - future epic
- Pre-commit hooks - future epic

## Design Discovery

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| CLI interface? | Makefile (familiar to Clojure devs) | Primary interface is make commands |
| REPL experience? | One command, auto-connects | Use node-repl, no browser needed |
| Validation scope? | All: format + lint + test | make validate runs everything |
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 2: remove-object unit tests + Dark Ritual and Cabal Ritual card tests
design:
## Goal
Add unit tests for remove-object in zones_test.cljs, then create integration tests for Dark Ritual and Cabal Ritual (the two mana ritual spells). These are the simplest untested cards - pure effect spells with no selection UI.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### Part A: remove-object unit tests

**File:** src/test/fizzle/engine/zones_test.cljs (EXTEND existing file)

**Function under test:** `zones/remove-object` at src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs:78-90
```clojure
(defn remove-object [db object-id]
  (if-let [obj-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]] db object-id)]
    (d/db-with db [[:db.fn/retractEntity obj-eid]])
    db))
```

**Tests to add (append after line 107 in zones_test.cljs):**

1. `remove-object-removes-from-db-test`
   - Setup: init-game-state, get hand, get first object-id
   - Act: `(zones/remove-object db object-id)`
   - Assert: `(q/get-object db' object-id)` returns nil
   - Assert: `(q/get-hand db' :player-1)` is empty (object was the only hand card)
   - Bug caught: Verifies retractEntity actually removes the entity

2. `remove-object-nonexistent-returns-unchanged-test`
   - Setup: init-game-state
   - Act: `(zones/remove-object db (random-uuid))`
   - Assert: `(= db db')` - exact same db returned
   - Bug caught: Verifies graceful handling of missing objects (no crash)

3. `remove-object-nil-id-returns-unchanged-test`
   - Setup: init-game-state
   - Act: `(zones/remove-object db nil)`
   - Assert: `(= db db')` - exact same db returned
   - Bug caught: Verifies nil safety (Datascript query with nil param)

4. `remove-object-double-remove-is-noop-test`
   - Setup: init-game-state, get first object-id, remove once
   - Act: remove again with same object-id
   - Assert: `(= db-after-first db-after-second)` - second remove is no-op
   - Bug caught: Verifies idempotency (important for spell copies that might be resolved twice)

5. `remove-object-other-objects-unaffected-test`
   - Setup: init-game-state, add a second card to hand via d/conn-from-db + transact, get both object-ids
   - Act: remove first object only
   - Assert: second object still exists with correct zone/card data
   - Bug caught: Verifies retractEntity doesn't cascade to unrelated entities

**No new requires needed** - zones_test.cljs already imports `datascript.core`, `fizzle.db.init`, `fizzle.db.queries`, `fizzle.engine.zones`.

### Part B: Dark Ritual integration test

**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/dark_ritual_test.cljs (NEW)

**Card definition** (src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:19-28):
```clojure
{:card/id :dark-ritual
 :card/name "Dark Ritual"
 :card/cmc 1
 :card/mana-cost {:black 1}
 :card/colors #{:black}
 :card/types #{:instant}
 :card/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 3}}]}
```

**Test structure:**
```clojure
(ns fizzle.cards.dark-ritual-test
  (:require
    [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
    [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
    [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]))
```

**Helpers:** Use same create-test-db + add-card-to-zone pattern from led_test.cljs.

**Tests:**

1. `dark-ritual-card-definition-test`
   - Assert card exists in all-cards with correct :card/id, :card/name, :card/cmc, :card/mana-cost, :card/types, :card/colors
   - Assert single effect of type :add-mana with {:black 3}
   - Bug caught: Verifies card data integrity (catches typos in card defs)

2. `dark-ritual-cast-adds-bbb-mana-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add dark-ritual to hand, add {:black 1} mana to pool
   - Act: rules/cast-spell, then rules/resolve-spell
   - Assert: mana pool contains {:black 3} after resolution (paid 1B, got 3B = net +2B, but pool starts empty after payment, so 3B from effect)
   - Assert: card moved to graveyard after resolution (instant)
   - Assert: storm count incremented to 1
   - Bug caught: Verifies the full cast-resolve cycle produces correct mana

3. `dark-ritual-cannot-cast-without-mana-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add dark-ritual to hand, NO mana
   - Assert: `(rules/can-cast? db :player-1 obj-id)` returns false
   - Bug caught: Verifies mana cost enforcement

4. `dark-ritual-increments-storm-count-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add 2 dark rituals to hand, add {:black 1} mana
   - Act: cast and resolve first ritual (produces BBB), cast second ritual (costs B)
   - Assert: storm count is 2 after both casts
   - Assert: mana pool after second resolve = {:black 5} (3 from first - 1 for second + 3 from second = 5)
   - Bug caught: Verifies storm count accumulates correctly across multiple casts

### Part C: Cabal Ritual integration test

**File:** src/test/fizzle/cards/cabal_ritual_test.cljs (NEW)

**Card definition** (src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs:50-60):
```clojure
{:card/id :cabal-ritual
 :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :black 1}
 :card/conditional-effects {:threshold [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 5}}]}
 :card/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana :effect/mana {:black 3}}]}
```

**Test structure:** Same pattern as dark-ritual-test.

**Tests:**

1. `cabal-ritual-card-definition-test`
   - Assert card def has :card/id, :card/name, :card/cmc 2, :card/mana-cost {:colorless 1 :black 1}
   - Assert has both :card/effects and :card/conditional-effects with :threshold key
   - Bug caught: Verifies conditional-effects structure is correct

2. `cabal-ritual-produces-bbb-without-threshold-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add cabal-ritual to hand, add {:black 2} mana (1B + 1 generic)
   - Actually need to check how generic mana works... mana-cost is {:colorless 1 :black 1}
   - Add {:black 2} to pool (1B pays for B, 1B pays for {1})
   - Act: cast-spell, resolve-spell
   - Assert: mana pool after = {:black 3} (paid 2B, gained 3B)
   - Assert: graveyard count = 1 (moved there, but threshold not active since <7 cards)
   - Bug caught: Verifies base mana effect works without threshold

3. `cabal-ritual-produces-bbbbb-with-threshold-test`
   - Setup: create-test-db, add cabal-ritual to hand, add {:black 2} mana
   - Add 7 cards to graveyard to enable threshold (use d/conn-from-db + transact objects with :object/zone :graveyard)
   - Act: cast-spell, resolve-spell
   - Assert: mana pool after = {:black 5} (paid 2B, gained 5B instead of 3B)
   - Bug caught: Verifies threshold condition detection and conditional effect selection

4. `cabal-ritual-threshold-exactly-7-cards-test`
   - Setup: same as above but with exactly 7 graveyard cards (boundary)
   - Act: cast-spell, resolve-spell
   - Assert: threshold effect fires (5B produced, not 3B)
   - Bug caught: Verifies threshold boundary condition (>= 7, not > 7)

5. `cabal-ritual-threshold-6-cards-no-threshold-test`
   - Setup: same but with only 6 graveyard cards
   - Act: cast-spell, resolve-spell
   - Assert: normal effect fires (3B produced, not 5B)
   - Bug caught: Verifies threshold is NOT active at 6 cards (off-by-one)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] 5 remove-object tests added to zones_test.cljs covering: remove existing, nonexistent, nil, double-remove, unaffected others
- [ ] dark_ritual_test.cljs exists with 4 tests: card def, cast-resolve mana, cannot-cast, storm count
- [ ] cabal_ritual_test.cljs exists with 5 tests: card def, no-threshold, threshold, boundary-7, boundary-6
- [ ] Dark Ritual test verifies mana pool contains {:black 3} after cast-resolve cycle
- [ ] Cabal Ritual threshold test verifies {:black 5} with 7+ graveyard cards vs {:black 3} without
- [ ] Storm count increments correctly verified in Dark Ritual chained-cast test
- [ ] All existing 792+ tests still pass
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: remove-object with nil**
- Datascript query `[:find ?e . :in $ ?oid :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]` with nil ?oid
- Should return nil (no match), so if-let falls through to return db unchanged
- MUST test this explicitly - nil is a common runtime value for missing objects

**Edge Case: Mana payment for generic costs**
- Cabal Ritual costs {:colorless 1 :black 1}
- The :colorless 1 is a generic cost payable with any color
- Must verify how mana/pay-mana handles this - add enough black mana to cover both
- Test setup: add {:black 2} to pool (1B for the B cost, 1B for the {1} generic)

**Edge Case: Threshold boundary**
- Threshold checks >= 7 cards in graveyard
- The Cabal Ritual itself goes to graveyard AFTER resolution (it's on the stack during resolution)
- So the 7 cards must be in graveyard BEFORE casting
- threshold? is checked at resolution time via get-active-effects (rules.cljs:408)
- Important: the spell resolves while still on stack, threshold checked against graveyard WITHOUT the resolving spell

**Edge Case: Storm count starts at 0**
- init-game-state sets :player/storm-count to 0
- First cast increments to 1, so storm trigger gets copy-count = dec(1) = 0 copies
- This is correct: first spell of the game doesn't get storm copies
- Verify storm count = 1 after first Dark Ritual cast (not 0)

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow led_test.cljs pattern for test DB setup and helpers
- Follow rules_test.cljs pattern for cast-spell/resolve-spell assertions
- See conditions.cljs:10-20 for threshold? implementation

## Anti-patterns
- NO mocking Datascript (pure function tests against real DB)
- NO test-only methods in production code
- NO testing private functions directly (threshold? is public but test through resolve-spell)
- NO using (is (some? result)) as sole assertion - always assert specific values
- NO duplicating the exact same helper functions as led_test.cljs (copy the pattern, not the code verbatim - each test file has its own helpers)
close_reason: All 14 tests added: 5 remove-object unit tests in zones_test.cljs, 4 Dark Ritual integration tests, 5 Cabal Ritual integration tests (including threshold boundary). make validate passes: 806 tests, 1720 assertions.

title: Harden Core Architecture for Card Extensibility
description:
## Motivation

Architecture review identified several friction points that will compound as the card pool grows beyond the current ~58 definitions. None are fundamental flaws, but they represent implicit protocols, duplicated systems, and principle violations that erode confidence when adding new mechanics.

## Success Criteria

- All engine code maintains purity invariant (zero mutable state)
- Adding a new interactive effect type cannot silently no-op
- Single unified cost system for both spells and abilities
- Single unified condition-checking system
- No subscription -> event namespace dependencies
- Card definitions validated at load time

## Tasks

### 1. Make Selection Protocol Explicit (HIGH - prevents silent no-ops)
Effects that need player interaction (discard, tutor, scry, peek-and-select, return-from-graveyard) currently signal this by returning db unchanged. The event layer must know which effect types are interactive — if someone adds a new interactive effect and forgets to handle it in the event layer, it silently no-ops.

**Current locations:**
- effects.cljs:456-481 (:discard) — "Returns db unchanged. Selection handled at app-db level"
- effects.cljs:484-500 (:tutor) — same
- effects.cljs:503-525 (:scry) — same  
- effects.cljs:528-551 (:peek-and-select) — same
- effects.cljs:356-397 (:return-from-graveyard with :player selection) — same

**Proposed fix:** Add :effect/requires-selection true to interactive effect data maps in card definitions. Or have execute-effect-impl return a tagged value like {:db db :needs-selection {:type :discard ...}} instead of silently returning db unchanged. The event layer can then dispatch on the tag rather than maintaining a parallel list of interactive types.

### 2. Unify Cost Systems (MEDIUM - prevents confusion)
Two parallel cost systems exist:
- costs.cljs — multimethod-based, for ability costs (:tap, :sacrifice-self, :remove-counter, :pay-life, :mana, :discard-hand)
- rules.cljs:115-177 — case-based, for spell additional costs (:pay-life, :exile-cards)

Adding a new additional cost type (e.g., :discard-cards, :sacrifice-creature) requires knowing which system to modify. The case in rules.cljs is a closed switch.

**Proposed fix:** Route all cost checking/payment through the multimethod system in costs.cljs. The can-pay-additional-cost? and pay-additional-cost functions in rules.cljs should delegate to the costs.cljs multimethods.

### 3. Unify Condition Checking (MEDIUM - prevents drift)
Three condition-checking implementations:
- effects.cljs:19-29 — check-condition multimethod (the right pattern)
- abilities.cljs:12-22 — separate check-condition function with case dispatch
- rules.cljs:427-440 (get-active-effects) — inline conditions/threshold? check

**Proposed fix:** Route everything through the check-condition multimethod in effects.cljs.

### 4. Replace SBA Atom with Multimethod (LOW - restores purity)
state_based.cljs:12 has (defonce sba-registry (atom {})) — the only mutable state in the engine layer. The docstring claims "All functions are pure" which is false.

**Proposed fix:** Replace with a multimethod dispatching on SBA type keyword, matching the pattern used for effects, costs, and conditions throughout the engine.

### 5. Move Query Functions to Engine Layer (LOW - cleans dep graph)
subs/game.cljs requires events/game.cljs for land-card?, can-play-land?, and the phases constant. This creates a subs -> events dependency that should be subs -> engine.

**Current locations:**
- events/game.cljs: land-card?, can-play-land? (used by subs/game.cljs:77,86,98)
- events/game.cljs: phases constant (used by views/phase_bar.cljs:28)

**Proposed fix:** Move to engine/rules.cljs. Update requires in subs/game.cljs and views/phase_bar.cljs.

### 6. Add Card Definition Validation (MEDIUM - catches errors at load)
Cards are "data over code" but no spec/schema validates data shapes. A typo in :effect/type or missing :card/effects fails silently at runtime.

**Proposed fix:** Add load-time validation for card definitions. Could be clojure.spec or simple predicate functions. Check: required fields present, valid effect types, valid cost types, valid condition types, mana cost structure. Run at card-load time so errors are caught immediately.

### 7. Eliminate Duplicated Validation in play-land (LOW - bug risk)
can-play-land? (game.cljs:739-767) and play-land (game.cljs:770-836) both check the same 7 conditions with copy-pasted logic. If rules change, both need updating.

**Proposed fix:** Have play-land call can-play-land? rather than re-checking.

## Additional Notes from Review

- Hardcoded trigger type mapping in trigger_db.cljs:8-12 (closed map, consider registration)
- Targeting criteria matching duplicated between targeting.cljs and queries.cljs (unify)
- :self target resolution inconsistent (some effects handle internally, others expect pre-resolved)
- resolve-all-handler in game.cljs:525-557 bypasses history interceptor (document invariant)
- init-game-state in game.cljs:67-170 is 103 lines — consider extracting helpers
- Mixed abstraction levels in abilities.cljs activate-ability (5 nested if/let levels)

title: Epic: Composable Fork/Replay UI Components
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

1. Turn-grouped action log component: entries grouped by turn number with collapsible sections
2. Click-to-rewind: click any entry in action log to jump directly to that position
3. Fork CRUD controls component: create named fork, inline rename, cascade delete
4. Fork tree component: indented list showing parent-child fork relationships with branch switching
5. All components are self-contained, subscription-driven, and layout-agnostic (no assumptions about sidebar/panel/modal placement)
6. Cascade delete: deleting a fork removes it and all descendant forks; deleting active branch switches to main

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] action-log component renders entries grouped by turn with collapsible sections
- [ ] Clicking any entry in action-log jumps to that position (restores game state)
- [ ] Current position highlighted, future entries visually distinct
- [ ] fork-controls component supports create, inline rename, and delete
- [ ] Cascade delete removes fork and all descendants
- [ ] Cannot delete main branch
- [ ] fork-tree component shows indented parent-child hierarchy
- [ ] Clicking branch in fork-tree switches to it
- [ ] Active branch visually highlighted in fork-tree
- [ ] All components work independently of layout context
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO layout assumptions in components (no hardcoded widths, sidebar positioning, or flex assumptions — components must be layout-agnostic)
- ❌ NO game logic in view components (views dispatch events and subscribe to data, nothing else)
- ❌ NO component-local atoms for state that belongs in app-db (use re-frame subscriptions)
- ❌ NO direct history/core.cljs calls from views (always go through events/subscriptions)
- ❌ NO comparison view (deferred — premature before full UI design)

## Approach

Build composable, high-quality UI components for fork/replay interaction that can be assembled into any final layout. Components are self-contained Reagent components backed by re-frame subscriptions and events. No assumptions about where they'll be rendered.

Four layers:
1. **Data model** (history/core.cljs) — Pure functions: rename-fork, delete-fork, entries-by-turn, fork-tree
2. **Events** (history/events.cljs) — jump-to, create-fork, rename-fork, delete-fork
3. **Subscriptions** (subs/history.cljs) — entries-by-turn (grouped), fork-tree (hierarchical)
4. **View components** (views/history.cljs) — action-log, fork-controls, fork-tree

## Architecture

### Data Model Additions (history/core.cljs)
- rename-fork [db fork-id new-name] — Updates fork name in :history/forks map
- delete-fork [db fork-id] — Cascade removes fork + descendants. If active branch deleted, switches to main and resets position
- entries-by-turn [entries] — Groups effective-entries by :entry/turn, returns [{:turn N :entries [...]}]
- fork-tree [forks] — Structures flat fork map into tree: [{:fork {...} :children [...]}] rooted at forks with nil parent

### Events (history/events.cljs)
- ::jump-to [_ [_ position]] — Calls step-to to restore snapshot at position
- ::create-fork [_ [_ name]] — Calls create-named-fork at current position
- ::rename-fork [_ [_ fork-id new-name]] — Calls rename-fork
- ::delete-fork [_ [_ fork-id]] — Calls delete-fork with cascade

### Subscriptions (subs/history.cljs)
- ::entries-by-turn — Layer 3, derives from ::entries, returns turn-grouped structure
- ::fork-tree — Layer 3, derives from ::forks, returns hierarchical tree

### View Components (views/history.cljs)
- action-log: Turn headers (collapsible), entry rows (clickable), position highlighting
- fork-controls: New Fork button, inline rename (click-to-edit), delete button per fork
- fork-tree: Indented nested list, active branch highlighted, click to switch

## Design Rationale

### Problem
The minimal fork/replay UI (sidebar with step back/forward, flat fork list) is a POC. The full app UI hasn't been designed yet, so we need high-quality, composable interaction components that can be assembled into any layout later.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- history/core.cljs — Data model with fork/entry structures, step-to exists but no jump-to event
- history/events.cljs — Only step-back, step-forward, switch-branch events exist
- create-named-fork exists in core but not exposed as event
- views/history.cljs — POC sidebar with flat entry list and flat fork list
- subs/history.cljs — Basic subscriptions (entries, position, forks) but no grouped/tree derivations

**Key patterns:**
- Components use re-frame subscriptions for data, dispatch for actions
- History entries have :entry/turn for grouping
- Forks have :fork/parent for tree structure

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Composable layout-agnostic components ✓
**What it is:** Build self-contained components backed by subscriptions/events that work in any container.
**Chosen because:** Full UI not designed yet — components must be flexible for future composition.

#### 2. Complete sidebar redesign ❌
**What it is:** Rebuild the sidebar as a finished product with all features integrated.
**Why explored:** Natural extension of existing sidebar POC.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Full UI not designed yet. Hardcoding layout assumptions would constrain future design.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Full app UI layout is finalized.

#### 3. Side-by-side comparison view ❌
**What it is:** Component rendering two game states side-by-side for branch comparison.
**Why explored:** Listed in original stub epic requirements.
**REJECTED BECAUSE:** Premature — layout assumptions needed. Branch switching is sufficient for now.
**DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Full UI design specifically calls for comparison layout.

### Scope Boundaries
**In scope:** action-log, fork-controls, fork-tree components + backing data/events/subs
**Out of scope:** Comparison view, final layout/positioning, timeline scrubber, fork tree graph/SVG visualization

### Open Questions
- Exact styling of components (colors, spacing) — resolve during implementation, keep minimal
- Whether to update POC sidebar to use new components or leave it — decide after components built

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Build final UI or composable components? | Composable components | No layout assumptions, layout-agnostic requirement |
| Comparison view? | Skip for now | Not in scope, anti-pattern to build it |
| Fork tree style? | Indented list | Pure CSS nesting, no SVG complexity |
| Delete behavior? | Cascade delete | Simpler model, delete fork + all children |
| Which components? | All four: action-log (turn-grouped + click-to-rewind), fork-controls, fork-tree | Full component set |

### Dead-End Paths

#### Complete Sidebar Redesign
**Why explored:** Natural next step from POC sidebar
**Why abandoned:** Full UI not designed — would bake in layout assumptions

#### SVG Graph Visualization
**Why explored:** More visual representation of fork relationships
**Why abandoned:** Harder to compose into arbitrary layouts, indented list sufficient

### Open Concerns Raised
- Full UI layout unknown → Components must be layout-agnostic (captured as anti-pattern)
close_reason: All success criteria met. 3 child tasks completed: data model (21 tests), events/subs (8 tests), view components. Implementation review approved. 963 tests, make validate clean. All work committed on main.

title: Move trigger registry from atom into Datascript
description:
## The Problem

The trigger registry (`engine/trigger_registry.cljs`) holds a `(defonce registry (atom {}))` — mutable state that lives *outside* the immutable Datascript database. This is the single biggest architectural violation in the codebase.

The entire architecture bets on "game state is an immutable value." The trigger registry is a hole in that bet.

### What breaks

- **History/time-travel:** When you step back in history, the Datascript db rolls back but the trigger registry doesn't. Triggers from cards that "don't exist yet" at that history point remain registered. Triggers from cards that *were* on the battlefield are gone.
- **Fork/replay:** Same problem. Fork at turn 3, play differently — the trigger registry reflects the current timeline, not the forked one.
- **Testing:** Tests need `clear-registry!` in setup. Pure state doesn't need cleanup.
- **Purity claims:** `zones.cljs` claims to be pure in its docstring but calls `registry/unregister-by-source!` inside `move-to-zone`. The function signature lies.

### Why it exists today

Triggers register when cards enter the battlefield, unregister when they leave. An atom was the expedient choice. You get away with it today because `clear-registry!` runs on game init, and the practice tool doesn't heavily exercise fork-with-triggered-permanents paths.

### The Rich Hickey question

"Is the trigger registry game state?" Yes — triggers appear when cards enter zones and disappear when cards leave. They should be *values* in the database, not places that change behind the system's back.

### Proposed direction (needs brainstorming)

Model triggers as Datascript entities:
- Add `:trigger/id`, `:trigger/source`, `:trigger/event-type`, `:trigger/effects`, `:trigger/filter`, etc. to the schema
- Query them with Datalog instead of filtering an atom
- `zones.cljs` becomes actually pure — trigger cleanup happens via Datascript transactions
- History captures trigger state automatically (it's just more entities in the db snapshot)
- Forks work correctly (different timelines have different trigger sets)

### Files involved

- `engine/trigger_registry.cljs` — the atom-based registry (88 lines, would be replaced)
- `engine/trigger_dispatch.cljs` — `dispatch-event` queries registry, creates stack-items (98 lines)
- `engine/zones.cljs` — `move-to-zone` calls `registry/unregister-by-source!` (side effect)
- `events/game.cljs` — `register-card-triggers!` calls `registry/register-trigger!`
- `db/schema.cljs` — would need new trigger entity attributes
- All tests that call `registry/clear-registry!`

### Risks

- Performance: Datalog queries vs atom deref. Trigger count is small (handful per game), so likely negligible.
- Schema migration: Existing game init code creates triggers imperatively. Needs rethinking.
- Test changes: Every test using `clear-registry!` needs updating (but this is a *feature* — they become simpler).
close_reason: Completed: commits 830a612 through 7923a7b migrated trigger registry to Datascript and removed atom-based registry
