         - Get fork's :fork/parent and :fork/branch-point
         - Call delete-fork on current fork-id (handles cascade of any remaining children)
         - Set :history/current-branch to parent fork-id (or nil for main)
         - Set :history/position to branch-point
         - Set :game/db from snapshot at branch-point position in parent's effective entries
       - Else (fork still has entries):
         - Decrement :history/position
         - Set :game/db to snapshot of new tip entry

## Success Criteria
- [ ] can-pop? returns correct boolean for all 4 states: pos -1, pos 0, pos > 0 at tip, pos > 0 not at tip
- [ ] pop-entry on main: entry-count decreases by exactly 1, position decrements by exactly 1, game/db equals new tip's snapshot
- [ ] pop-entry on fork: fork/entries count decreases by exactly 1, position decrements by exactly 1
- [ ] pop-entry empties fork: fork removed from :history/forks, current-branch set to parent, position set to branch-point
- [ ] pop-entry cascades: child forks at popped position deleted, child forks at earlier positions preserved
- [ ] pop-entry nested: fork-of-fork empty returns to outer fork (not main)
- [ ] pop-entry no-op: returns db unchanged when can-pop? is false
- [ ] Minimum 12 test assertions across all scenarios
- [ ] All existing history/core_test.cljs tests still pass (no regressions)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO modifying append-entry, step-to, or any existing function behavior (additive only)
- NO throwing exceptions on invalid pop (must be no-op, return db unchanged)
- NO mutating the entries vector in place (use immutable operations: pop, subvec)
- NO skipping child fork cascade (must find and delete forks at popped position)
- NO using find-descendants directly for position-based search (it finds children of a fork-id, not forks at a position — write find-child-forks-at)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Pop to position 0**
When popping from position 1 to position 0, game/db must be set to the snapshot at position 0 (init-game snapshot). Verify this explicitly — off-by-one errors are likely here since position 0 is the floor.

**Edge Case: Fork branch-point snapshot restoration**
When popping empties a fork and returning to parent, the game/db must come from the parent's effective entries at the branch-point index. Use (nth (effective-entries-for-branch db parent-id) branch-point) to get the correct snapshot. Do NOT use the deleted fork's entries.

**Edge Case: Multiple child forks at same position**
It's possible (via auto-fork) for multiple forks to branch from the same position. find-child-forks-at must return ALL matching forks, and each must be deleted.

**Edge Case: delete-fork side effects on current state**
When calling delete-fork to cascade-delete child forks BEFORE popping, the current branch is still valid. But when calling delete-fork on the current fork (empty fork case), it will switch to main — we must then set the correct parent branch and position ourselves. Review delete-fork behavior at core.cljs:150-167 carefully.

**Reference Implementation Pattern**
append-entry (core.cljs:53-64) handles main vs fork branching. pop-entry should mirror this structure: check current-branch, branch on nil (main) vs fork-id (fork).
close_reason: Implemented can-pop?, find-child-forks-at, pop-entry in history/core.cljs. 15 new tests covering main pop, fork pop, empty fork auto-delete, cascade, nested forks, boundary conditions. make validate passes.

title: Task 2: Storm split selection infrastructure and routing
design:
## Goal
When a storm trigger resolves for a targeted spell (e.g., Brain Freeze), instead of auto-creating all copies with the inherited target, present a :storm-split selection that lets the player distribute copies across valid targets. Each copy gets its individually assigned target.

## Context
Completed Task 1 (fizzle-a6yu): Brain Freeze is now a targeted spell with :card/targeting. Cast-time player selection works. Storm copies inherit the chosen target. Mill reads target from stack-item.

## Effort Estimate
5-6 hours (create-spell-copy 30min, selection type 2h, routing 1h, tests 2h)

## Implementation

### 1. Enhance create-spell-copy with target override (engine/triggers.cljs:35-88)

Add optional target-override parameter. Multi-arity: 3-arg maintains backward compatibility.

FROM (line 49):
```clojure
(defn create-spell-copy [db source-object-id controller-id]
  ...
  (let [original-targets (:stack-item/targets original-si)]
    (stack/create-stack-item ... (cond-> {...}
                                   original-targets
                                   (assoc :stack-item/targets original-targets)))))
```

TO:
```clojure
(defn create-spell-copy
  ([db source-object-id controller-id]
   (create-spell-copy db source-object-id controller-id nil))
  ([db source-object-id controller-id target-override]
   ...
   (let [targets (or target-override original-targets)]
     (stack/create-stack-item ... (cond-> {...}
                                    targets
                                    (assoc :stack-item/targets targets))))))
```

### 2. Create events/selection/storm.cljs (NEW FILE)

New domain module following mana-allocation pattern (costs.cljs:125-156, 310-372).

**Required requires:**
```clojure
(ns fizzle.events.selection.storm
  (:require
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as queries]
    [fizzle.engine.stack :as stack]
    [fizzle.engine.triggers :as triggers]
    [fizzle.events.selection.core :as core]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]))
```

**Builder function:**
```clojure
(defn build-storm-split-selection
  [game-db player-id storm-stack-item]
  (let [source-id (:stack-item/source storm-stack-item)
        source-obj (queries/get-object game-db source-id)]
    ;; Guard: source object must still exist
    (when source-obj
      (let [effects (:stack-item/effects storm-stack-item)
            copy-count (:effect/count (first (filter #(= :storm-copies (:effect/type %)) effects)) 0)
            player-eid (queries/get-player-eid game-db player-id)
            opponent-id (queries/get-opponent-id game-db player-id)
            opponent-eid (when opponent-id (queries/get-player-eid game-db opponent-id))
            ;; Filter nil targets (goldfish mode has no opponent)
            valid-targets (filterv some? [opponent-eid player-eid])]
        (when (and (pos? copy-count) (seq valid-targets))
          {:selection/type :storm-split
           :selection/copy-count copy-count
           :selection/valid-targets valid-targets
           :selection/allocation (assoc (zipmap valid-targets (repeat 0))
                                        (first valid-targets) copy-count)
           :selection/source-object-id source-id
           :selection/controller-id player-id
           :selection/stack-item-eid (:db/id storm-stack-item)
           :selection/selected #{}
           :selection/validation :always
           :selection/auto-confirm? true})))))
```

Default allocation: all copies to first valid target (opponent if present, else self). Player adjusts with stepper.

**Key design decisions:**
- valid-targets uses filterv to exclude nil (handles goldfish mode)
- Default allocation uses zipmap + assoc for arbitrary target count
- :validation :always because view layer disables Confirm when total != copy-count
- Auto-confirm fires in adjust handler (not builder), so initial state is safe

**Confirm handler (defmethod execute-confirmed-selection :storm-split):**
```clojure
(defmethod core/execute-confirmed-selection :storm-split
  [game-db selection]
  (let [allocation (:selection/allocation selection)
        source-id (:selection/source-object-id selection)
        controller (:selection/controller-id selection)
        si-eid (:selection/stack-item-eid selection)
        copy-count (:selection/copy-count selection)
        total (apply + (vals allocation))]
    ;; Guard: only create copies if total allocation equals copy-count
    (if (not= total copy-count)
      {:db game-db}  ;; Reject partial allocation (no-op)
      (let [;; Create copies for each target (skip 0-count targets)
            db-with-copies (reduce-kv
                             (fn [d target-eid cnt]
                               (if (pos? cnt)
                                 (loop [d' d remaining cnt]
                                   (if (pos? remaining)
                                     (recur (triggers/create-spell-copy d' source-id controller {:player target-eid})
                                            (dec remaining))
                                     d'))
                                 d))
                             game-db allocation)
            ;; Remove the storm stack-item
            db-final (stack/remove-stack-item db-with-copies si-eid)]
        {:db db-final :finalized? true}))))
```

**Event handlers:**
```clojure
(defn- adjust-storm-split-impl
  [app-db target-eid delta]
  (let [selection (:game/pending-selection app-db)
        allocation (:selection/allocation selection)
        current (get allocation target-eid 0)
        new-val (+ current delta)
        copy-count (:selection/copy-count selection)
        total-others (- (apply + (vals allocation)) current)]
    (if (and (>= new-val 0) (<= (+ new-val total-others) copy-count))
      (let [updated (assoc-in app-db [:game/pending-selection :selection/allocation target-eid] new-val)
            new-total (+ new-val total-others)]
        (if (and (:selection/auto-confirm? (:game/pending-selection updated))
                 (= new-total copy-count))
          (core/confirm-selection-impl updated)
          updated))
      app-db)))

(rf/reg-event-db
  ::adjust-storm-split
  (fn [db [_ target-eid delta]]
    (adjust-storm-split-impl db target-eid delta)))

(rf/reg-event-db
  ::reset-storm-split
  (fn [db _]
    (let [selection (:game/pending-selection db)
          targets (:selection/valid-targets selection)
          copy-count (:selection/copy-count selection)]
      (assoc-in db [:game/pending-selection :selection/allocation]
                (assoc (zipmap targets (repeat 0))
                       (first targets) copy-count)))))
```

### 3. Add targeted-storm detection to resolve-one-item (events/game.cljs:389-393)

In the 'Other stack-items' branch, detect storm items with a targeted source spell. Add new branch BEFORE the existing catch-all:

```clojure
;; Targeted storm — needs split selection
(and top-stack-item
     (= :storm (:stack-item/type top-stack-item))
     (let [source-id (:stack-item/source top-stack-item)
           source-obj (queries/get-object game-db source-id)
           card (:object/card source-obj)]
       (:card/targeting card)))
(let [selection (storm/build-storm-split-selection game-db player-id top-stack-item)]
  (if selection
    {:db game-db :pending-selection selection}
    ;; No copies (count=0) or no valid targets — resolve normally
    (let [stack-item-eid (:db/id top-stack-item)
          db-resolved (resolve-stack-item-effects game-db top-stack-item)]
      {:db (stack/remove-stack-item db-resolved stack-item-eid)})))

;; Other stack-items (triggers, non-targeted storm, etc.)
top-stack-item
(let [stack-item-eid (:db/id top-stack-item)
      db-resolved (resolve-stack-item-effects game-db top-stack-item)]
  {:db (stack/remove-stack-item db-resolved stack-item-eid)})
```

### 4. Ensure game.cljs requires storm namespace

Add require to game.cljs:
```clojure
[fizzle.events.selection.storm :as storm]
```

### 5. Write tests (TDD: RED then GREEN)

**Test file: src/test/fizzle/events/selection/storm_split_test.cljs (NEW)**

Unit tests for builder:
1. test-build-storm-split-selection-correct-state: copy-count=3, verify allocation defaults all to opponent, valid-targets has both players. Catches: wrong fields, wrong defaults.
2. test-build-storm-split-selection-zero-copies-returns-nil: storm with count=0 returns nil. Catches: showing UI for 0 copies.
3. test-build-storm-split-selection-source-not-found-returns-nil: source object removed, returns nil. Catches: NPE on nil source.
4. test-build-storm-split-selection-goldfish-mode-only-self: no opponent in db, valid-targets has only player-eid, all copies default to self. Catches: nil EID in targets.

Unit tests for stepper:
5. test-adjust-increment-target: opp=3 self=0, increment self by +1 → opp=3 self=1. Catches: increment not working.
6. test-adjust-decrement-target: opp=3 self=0, decrement opp by -1 → opp=2 self=0. Catches: decrement not working.
7. test-adjust-rejects-negative: opp=0, decrement opp → unchanged. Catches: negative allocation.
8. test-adjust-rejects-over-allocation: total at copy-count, increment → unchanged. Catches: exceeding copy-count.
9. test-adjust-auto-confirms-when-total-matches: opp=2 self=0 copy-count=3, increment self to 1 (total=3) → auto-confirms, pending-selection cleared, copies on stack. Catches: auto-confirm not triggering.
10. test-reset-restores-default: after adjusting, reset returns to default allocation. Catches: reset broken.

Unit tests for confirm:
11. test-confirm-creates-correct-copy-count: allocation {opp 2, self 1} → 3 copy objects on stack. Catches: wrong copy count.
12. test-confirm-assigns-targets-per-copy: allocation {opp 2, self 1} → 2 copies have opp target, 1 has self target. Verify via stack-item/targets on each copy's stack-item. Catches: targets not individually assigned (KEY test for the feature).
13. test-confirm-removes-storm-stack-item: after confirm, storm SI no longer exists. Catches: stack-item leak.
14. test-confirm-rejects-partial-allocation: total < copy-count → no-op, no copies created. Catches: partial allocation creating wrong number of copies.

Integration tests in brain_freeze_test.cljs:
15. test-brain-freeze-storm-returns-split-selection: cast BF after rituals → resolve storm SI → returns pending :storm-split selection with correct copy-count. Catches: routing not detecting targeted storm.
16. test-brain-freeze-storm-split-all-to-opponent: full flow, split all copies to opponent, resolve copies → opponent milled correctly. Catches: end-to-end failure.
17. test-brain-freeze-storm-split-mixed: split copies across both targets, resolve → each target milled correct amount. Catches: mixed targets not resolving correctly (CRITICAL test).
18. test-dark-ritual-storm-no-split-selection: non-targeted storm → resolve-one-item does NOT return pending-selection. Catches: regression, non-targeted storm broken.
19. test-resolve-all-pauses-at-storm-split: resolve-all-handler with targeted storm → stops at pending-selection. Catches: Yield All not pausing.

### 6. Run make validate

## Files Modified
1. engine/triggers.cljs — create-spell-copy target override (multi-arity)
2. events/selection/storm.cljs — NEW: builder, confirm handler, event handlers
3. events/game.cljs — resolve-one-item new branch for targeted storm + require
4. src/test/fizzle/events/selection/storm_split_test.cljs — NEW: unit tests
5. src/test/fizzle/cards/brain_freeze_test.cljs — integration tests

## Success Criteria
- [ ] create-spell-copy accepts optional target-override parameter
- [ ] 3-arg create-spell-copy backward compatible (all existing storm_test.cljs pass)
- [ ] build-storm-split-selection produces correct selection state
- [ ] build-storm-split-selection returns nil for 0 copies, nil source, and no valid targets
- [ ] build-storm-split-selection handles goldfish mode (only self in valid-targets)
- [ ] ::adjust-storm-split stepper events work (increment, decrement, bounds)
- [ ] ::adjust-storm-split auto-confirms when total = copy-count
- [ ] execute-confirmed-selection :storm-split creates copies with individual targets
- [ ] execute-confirmed-selection :storm-split rejects partial allocation (total != copy-count)
- [ ] resolve-one-item returns :storm-split selection for targeted storm
- [ ] resolve-one-item does NOT return selection for non-targeted storm (regression)
- [ ] Storm copies created by split have correct targets stored on stack-items
- [ ] resolve-all-handler pauses at storm-split selection (Yield All behavior)
- [ ] make validate passes

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO modifying interactive-effect? for this (storm routing is in resolve-one-item, not effect-level detection)
- ❌ NO modifying existing resolve-stack-item-effects return type (add new branch in resolve-one-item instead)
- ❌ NO Brain Freeze specific logic in storm.cljs (must work for any targeted storm spell)
- ❌ NO modifying create-spell-copy 3-arg behavior (backward compatibility required)
- ❌ NO including nil EIDs in valid-targets or allocation keys (filter nil targets from builder)
- ❌ NO assuming opponent always exists (goldfish mode has only self as valid target)
- ❌ NO allowing partial allocation confirmation (confirm handler must validate total = copy-count)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Goldfish Mode (No Opponent)**
- get-opponent-id returns nil when no opponent player exists
- get-player-eid called with nil would return nil
- Builder MUST filter nil from valid-targets using filterv some?
- With only self as valid target, default allocation puts all copies to self
- Auto-confirm still works (only 1 target, total always equals copy-count on any adjust)

**Edge Case: Source Object Removed**
- Source spell could theoretically be exiled or removed before storm resolves
- Builder wraps logic in (when source-obj ...) guard
- Returns nil → resolve-one-item falls through to normal resolution (creates 0 copies)

**Edge Case: Premature Manual Confirm**
- :validation :always allows confirm-selection-handler to pass validation
- But allocation may have total != copy-count
- Confirm handler MUST validate total = copy-count before creating copies
- Returns {:db game-db} (no-op) for partial allocation
- View layer should also disable Confirm button when total != copy-count (Task 3)

**Edge Case: Auto-Confirm Timing**
- Initial allocation sums to copy-count (all to first target)
- Auto-confirm only fires in ::adjust-storm-split handler, not builder
- So initial state shows selection, user adjusts, auto-confirm fires when they finish
- If user only wants default (all to opponent), they click Confirm manually
- This is correct: default allocation is a valid choice that shouldn't auto-dismiss

**valid-targets Order Coupling**
- Default allocation puts all copies to (first valid-targets)
- valid-targets is [opponent-eid player-eid] when opponent exists, [player-eid] in goldfish
- Opponent is first because it's the most common target for Brain Freeze
- This ordering is intentional, not accidental — document in builder docstring

**Confirm Handler Return Type**
- Returns {:db db-final :finalized? true} — fully handled path in confirm-selection-impl
- This is correct because storm-split handles its own cleanup (removing storm SI)
- Remaining-effects and spell-cleanup don't apply (storm SI is not a spell)
- Partial allocation returns {:db game-db} — standard path, but with no changes and no remaining-effects, effectively a no-op
close_reason: Closed

title: Add missing corner case tests for events/db modules
description:
~8 missing corner cases in events/db:
- play_land: phases beyond :combat, stack non-empty check
- queries: stack-empty? player isolation, battlefield zone
- peek_selection: bottom position verification
- scry: duplicate cards in piles
- init_game: structural validation
- mode_selection: flashback mode test
design:
## Goal
Add ~8 missing corner case tests for events and db modules.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Tests to Add

### play_land_test.cljs
1. test-play-land-fails-during-upkeep — Verify can't play land during :upkeep phase. Bug caught: play_land only checks :combat, misses other non-main phases.

### mode_selection_test.cljs

## Implementation Checklist

- [ ] Write test with exact assertions
- [ ] Run make validate after each change

## Success Criteria

- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] Each test catches a specific named bug

## Anti-patterns
- Do NOT use inline handler copies (call production functions)
- Do NOT use weak assertions (some?, map?, true?)

title: Task 1: Fix game.cljs dual-param and rename app-db helpers
design:
## Goal
Fix the dual-parameter anti-pattern in initiate-cast-with-mode and rename helper function parameters from db to app-db in events/game.cljs.

## Effort Estimate
~2 hours (mechanical renames, no behavioral changes)

## Implementation Checklist

### 1. Fix initiate-cast-with-mode (line 164)
- Current signature: \`[app-db game-db player-id object-id mode]\`
- New signature: \`[app-db player-id object-id mode]\`
- Wrap existing body in \`(let [game-db (:game/db app-db)] ...)\`
- All internal game-db refs remain unchanged (they now bind to the extracted value)
- Update call site at line ~229 in cast-spell-handler:
  \`(initiate-cast-with-mode db game-db :player-1 selected (first castable-modes))\`
  → \`(initiate-cast-with-mode app-db :player-1 selected (first castable-modes))\`
  (note: db also renamed to app-db as part of step 2)
- Update call site at line ~721 in select-casting-mode-handler:
  \`(initiate-cast-with-mode db game-db :player-1 object-id mode)\`
  → \`(initiate-cast-with-mode app-db :player-1 object-id mode)\`
  (note: db also renamed to app-db as part of step 4)

### 2. Rename cast-spell-handler (line 206)
- Current: \`[db]\` → New: \`[app-db]\`
- Rename all internal \`db\` refs to \`app-db\`:
  - Line 211: \`(:game/db db)\` → \`(:game/db app-db)\`
  - Line 212: \`(:game/selected-card db)\` → \`(:game/selected-card app-db)\`
  - Line 219: bare \`db\` return → \`app-db\`
  - Line 223: \`(assoc db ...)\` → \`(assoc app-db ...)\`
  - Line 229: call to initiate-cast-with-mode (updated per step 1)
  - Line 230: bare \`db\` return → \`app-db\`

### 3. Rename select-casting-mode-handler (line 711)
- Current: \`[db mode]\` → New: \`[app-db mode]\`
- Rename all internal \`db\` refs to \`app-db\`:
  - Line 716: \`(:game/db db)\` → \`(:game/db app-db)\`
  - Line 717: \`(:game/pending-mode-selection db)\` → \`(:game/pending-mode-selection app-db)\`
  - Line 721: call to initiate-cast-with-mode (updated per step 1)
  - Line 723: bare \`db\` return → \`app-db\`

### 4. Rename cancel-mode-selection-handler (line 732)
- Current: \`[db]\` → New: \`[app-db]\`
- Line 736: \`(dissoc db ...)\` → \`(dissoc app-db ...)\`

### 5. DO NOT TOUCH (verified clean)
These functions are already correct — do not modify:
- \`maybe-continue-cleanup\` (line 350) — already uses \`[app-db]\`
- Pure engine functions using \`[db ...]\` for game-db:
  resolve-stack-item-effects, resolve-top-of-stack, begin-cleanup,
  complete-cleanup-discard, advance-phase, untap-all-permanents,
  start-turn, land-card?, can-play-land?, play-land, tap-permanent
- All rf/reg-event-db handlers — keep \`db\` (re-frame convention)

### 6. Run validation
- \`make validate\` must pass (lint + format + tests)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Safety of initiate-cast-with-mode extraction:**
Both call sites (line 229 in cast-spell-handler, line 721 in select-casting-mode-handler) extract game-db via \`(:game/db db)\` and pass it alongside db. The extracted game-db is always identical to what's in app-db. No caller passes a modified game-db. Safe to extract internally.

**Rename ordering:**
Steps 1-4 are interdependent (step 1 changes call sites in steps 2 and 3). Apply all changes to the file together, then run tests once. Do not try to validate between individual renames.

**re-frame handler params stay as db:**
The rf/reg-event-db handlers at lines 233-236, 496-516, 519-526, 675-679, 701-705, 726-729 all use \`db\` — this is correct re-frame convention and must NOT be renamed.

## Anti-patterns
- NO renaming pure engine function params (they use \`db\` for game-db, which is correct in context)
- NO renaming rf/reg-event-db handler \`db\` params (re-frame convention)
- NO behavioral changes (this is purely mechanical renaming)
- NO adding docstrings or comments to functions not being modified

## Success Criteria
- [ ] \`initiate-cast-with-mode\` signature is \`[app-db player-id object-id mode]\` (no game-db param)
- [ ] \`cast-spell-handler\` signature is \`[app-db]\`
- [ ] \`select-casting-mode-handler\` signature is \`[app-db mode]\`
- [ ] \`cancel-mode-selection-handler\` signature is \`[app-db]\`
- [ ] No function in game.cljs takes both app-db and game-db as separate parameters
- [ ] All rf/reg-event-db handlers still use \`db\` param name
- [ ] Pure engine functions still use \`db\` param name
- [ ] \`make validate\` passes (lint + format + all 872+ tests)
close_reason: All 4 functions fixed: initiate-cast-with-mode dual-param removed, cast-spell-handler/select-casting-mode-handler/cancel-mode-selection-handler renamed db→app-db. Unused game-db binding removed from select-casting-mode-handler. make validate passes (872 tests, 0 failures).

title: Task 2: Create re-frame events and subscriptions wrapping engine
design:
## Goal
Create the re-frame event and subscription layer that wraps existing engine functions. Events delegate to engine pure functions, subscriptions use existing query functions. This is the data bridge between UI components and the game engine.

## Context
Completed fizzle-1rd: Re-frame infrastructure is set up with core.cljs entry point and placeholder app-db. Now need the actual game events and subscriptions.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create src/main/fizzle/events/game.cljs

Events wrap engine functions. No game logic in events (anti-pattern).

```clojure
(ns fizzle.events.game
  (:require
    [datascript.core :as d]
    [fizzle.cards.iggy-pop :as cards]
    [fizzle.db.queries :as queries]
    [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]
    [fizzle.engine.triggers :as triggers]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]))
```

**Event 1: ::init-game**

Creates datascript db with all 3 Phase 1 cards in hand. Uses d/create-conn + d/transact! pattern (same as db/init.cljs) because ref attributes need entity IDs from prior transactions.

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::init-game
  (fn [_ _]
    (let [conn (d/create-conn schema)]
      ;; Transact card definitions
      (d/transact! conn cards/all-cards)
      ;; Transact player with 2 black starting mana
      (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :player-1
                          :player/name "Player"
                          :player/life 20
                          :player/mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 2
                                             :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
                          :player/storm-count 0
                          :player/land-plays-left 1}])
      ;; Transact opponent (needed for Brain Freeze mill effect)
      (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :opponent
                          :player/name "Opponent"
                          :player/life 20
                          :player/mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0
                                             :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
                          :player/storm-count 0
                          :player/land-plays-left 0
                          :player/is-opponent true}])
      ;; Get entity IDs for references
      (let [db @conn
            player-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?pid :where [?e :player/id ?pid]] db :player-1)
            dr-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :dark-ritual)
            cr-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :cabal-ritual)
            bf-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?cid :where [?e :card/id ?cid]] db :brain-freeze)
            opp-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :in $ ?pid :where [?e :player/id ?pid]] db :opponent)]
        ;; Create game objects (3 cards in hand)
        (d/transact! conn [{:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card dr-eid :object/zone :hand
                            :object/owner player-eid :object/controller player-eid :object/tapped false}
                           {:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card cr-eid :object/zone :hand
                            :object/owner player-eid :object/controller player-eid :object/tapped false}
                           {:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card bf-eid :object/zone :hand
                            :object/owner player-eid :object/controller player-eid :object/tapped false}])
        ;; Create opponent library (40 cards so mill has targets)
        (d/transact! conn (vec (for [i (range 40)]
                                 {:object/id (random-uuid) :object/card dr-eid :object/zone :library
                                  :object/owner opp-eid :object/controller opp-eid
                                  :object/tapped false :object/position i})))
        ;; Game state
        (d/transact! conn [{:game/id :game-1
                            :game/turn 1
                            :game/phase :main1
                            :game/active-player player-eid
                            :game/priority player-eid}]))
      {:game/db @conn})))
```

Player starts with {:black 2} mana (enough to cast Dark Ritual which costs {B}).
Opponent player with 40-card library needed so Brain Freeze mill effect has cards to mill.

**Event 2: ::select-card**

Pure UI state, no engine call. Toggles selection (deselect if clicking same card).

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::select-card
  (fn [db [_ object-id]]
    (let [currently-selected (:game/selected-card db)]
      (assoc db :game/selected-card
             (when (not= currently-selected object-id) object-id)))))
```

**Event 3: ::cast-spell**

Guard: no-op if no card selected or cant cast.

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::cast-spell
  (fn [db _]
    (let [game-db (:game/db db)
          selected (:game/selected-card db)]
      (if (and selected (rules/can-cast? game-db :player-1 selected))
        (-> db
            (assoc :game/db (rules/cast-spell game-db :player-1 selected))
            (dissoc :game/selected-card))
        db))))
```

**Event 4: ::resolve-top**

CRITICAL: The stack has TWO types of items:
1. Triggers - queried via queries/get-stack-items (entities with :trigger/stack-order)
2. Spell objects - queried via queries/get-objects-in-zone for :stack zone

Resolution order: triggers first (higher stack-order, added after spells). When no triggers remain, resolve spell objects.

```clojure
(rf/reg-event-db
  ::resolve-top
  (fn [db _]
    (let [game-db (:game/db db)
          stack-triggers (queries/get-stack-items game-db)]
      (if (seq stack-triggers)
        ;; Resolve top trigger (first = highest stack-order = most recent)
        (let [top-trigger (first stack-triggers)
              game-db' (-> game-db
                           (triggers/resolve-trigger top-trigger)
                           (triggers/remove-trigger (:trigger/id top-trigger)))]
          (assoc db :game/db game-db'))
        ;; No triggers - resolve top spell object on stack
        (let [stack-objects (queries/get-objects-in-zone game-db :player-1 :stack)]
          (if (seq stack-objects)
            (let [top-spell (first stack-objects)
                  game-db' (rules/resolve-spell game-db :player-1 (:object/id top-spell))]
              (assoc db :game/db game-db'))
            db))))))
```

NOTE: get-objects-in-zone filters by owner. Storm copies have same owner as original (see triggers/create-spell-copy). So this works for Phase 1.

### 2. Create src/main/fizzle/subs/game.cljs

```clojure
(ns fizzle.subs.game
  (:require
    [fizzle.db.queries :as queries]
    [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]))
```

Layer 2 (extraction) subscriptions:
```clojure
(rf/reg-sub ::game-db (fn [db _] (:game/db db)))
(rf/reg-sub ::selected-card (fn [db _] (:game/selected-card db)))
```

Layer 3 (derived) subscriptions using sugar syntax:
```clojure
(rf/reg-sub
  ::hand
  :<- [::game-db]
  (fn [game-db _]
    (when game-db (queries/get-hand game-db :player-1))))

(rf/reg-sub
  ::mana-pool
  :<- [::game-db]
  (fn [game-db _]
    (when game-db (queries/get-mana-pool game-db :player-1))))

(rf/reg-sub
  ::storm-count
  :<- [::game-db]
  (fn [game-db _]
    (when game-db (queries/get-storm-count game-db :player-1))))

(rf/reg-sub
  ::stack
  :<- [::game-db]
  (fn [game-db _]
    (when game-db
      (let [triggers (queries/get-stack-items game-db)
            spells (queries/get-objects-in-zone game-db :player-1 :stack)]
        (concat triggers spells)))))

(rf/reg-sub
  ::can-cast?
  :<- [::game-db]
  :<- [::selected-card]
  (fn [[game-db selected] _]
    (when (and game-db selected)
      (rules/can-cast? game-db :player-1 selected))))
```

IMPORTANT: All Layer 3 subs guard against nil game-db with (when game-db ...).
The ::stack sub combines triggers AND spell objects for unified view.

### 3. Update core.cljs

Replace placeholder with actual subscription data display for verification:

```clojure
(ns fizzle.core
  (:require
    [fizzle.events.game :as events]
    [fizzle.subs.game :as subs]
    [re-frame.core :as rf]
    [reagent.dom :as rdom]))

(defn app
  []
  (let [hand @(rf/subscribe [::subs/hand])
        mana @(rf/subscribe [::subs/mana-pool])
        storm @(rf/subscribe [::subs/storm-count])
        stack @(rf/subscribe [::subs/stack])]
    [:div {:style {:padding "20px"}}
     [:h1 "Fizzle"]
     [:p (str "Hand: " (count hand) " cards")]
     [:p (str "Mana: " mana)]
     [:p (str "Storm: " storm)]
     [:p (str "Stack: " (count stack) " items")]]))

(defn ^:dev/after-load mount-root
  []
  (rf/clear-subscription-cache!)
  (rdom/render [app] (.getElementById js/document "app")))

(defn init
  []
  (rf/dispatch-sync [::events/init-game])
  (mount-root))
```

### 4. Run make fmt to fix formatting
### 5. Run make validate

## Key Engine API Reference

```
;; Player ID: :player-1 (keyword, NOT string)
;; Opponent ID: :opponent

;; rules.cljs
(cast-spell [db player-id object-id]) -> new-db
(resolve-spell [db player-id object-id]) -> new-db
(can-cast? [db player-id object-id]) -> truthy/nil (uses when-let)

;; queries.cljs
(get-hand [db player-id]) -> [{:object/id uuid :object/card {:card/name ...} ...} ...]
(get-mana-pool [db player-id]) -> {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 2 ...}
(get-storm-count [db player-id]) -> integer
(get-stack-items [db]) -> triggers ONLY [{:trigger/id uuid :trigger/type :storm ...} ...]
(get-objects-in-zone [db player-id zone]) -> objects filtered by owner

;; triggers.cljs
(resolve-trigger [db trigger]) -> new-db (multimethod on :trigger/type)
(remove-trigger [db trigger-id]) -> new-db (retracts trigger entity)

;; cards/iggy_pop.cljs
cards/all-cards -> vector of all 3 card defs
cards/dark-ritual, cards/cabal-ritual, cards/brain-freeze -> individual defs
```

## Success Criteria
- [ ] events/game.cljs created with 4 events: ::init-game, ::select-card, ::cast-spell, ::resolve-top
- [ ] subs/game.cljs created with 7 subscriptions: ::game-db, ::hand, ::mana-pool, ::storm-count, ::stack, ::selected-card, ::can-cast?
- [ ] core.cljs updated to dispatch ::events/init-game and display subscription data
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] Browser shows Hand: 3 cards, Mana: map with :black 2, Storm: 0, Stack: 0 items
- [ ] No console errors in browser dev tools

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- NO game logic in events (delegate to engine functions only)
- NO raw datascript queries in events except in ::init-game setup (use queries.cljs functions)
- NO direct atom manipulation (re-frame events only)
- NO hardcoded entity IDs (use queries to look up eids)
- NO mutable state outside re-frame app-db
- NO string player IDs (use keyword :player-1, not string p1)

## Key Considerations (Edge Cases and Troubleshooting)

**Edge Case: ::cast-spell with no selected card**
- Guard: check (and selected (rules/can-cast? ...)) before calling engine
- Return db unchanged if guard fails

**Edge Case: ::resolve-top with empty stack**
- Guard: check (seq stack-triggers) and (seq stack-objects)
- Return db unchanged if nothing to resolve

**Edge Case: nil game-db in subscriptions (before init)**
- All Layer 3 subs MUST wrap in (when game-db ...)
- Returns nil if game-db not yet initialized

**Edge Case: can-cast? returns nil not false**
- rules/can-cast? uses when-let, returns nil for non-existent objects
- Treat nil as falsy in guards

**Stack has two item types (IMPORTANT)**
- Triggers: from get-stack-items (entities with :trigger/stack-order)
- Spell objects: from get-objects-in-zone for :stack zone
- These are SEPARATE queries - triggers are NOT objects
- ::resolve-top must check triggers first, then spell objects
- ::stack sub combines both for unified view

**Opponent player needed for mill**
- Brain Freeze mills opponent via :effect/target :opponent
- Need opponent player entity with :player/is-opponent true
- Need opponent library with cards so mill has targets
- Without opponent, mill effect silently fails

**If compilation fails:**
- Check namespace requires are alphabetically sorted
- Run make fmt before make validate
- Check browser console for runtime errors
close_reason: Closed

title: Add lose-life and gain-life effects
design:
## Goal

Implement :lose-life and :gain-life effect types that modify player life totals. These are foundational effects needed for City of Brass (deals damage when tapped) and win/loss condition checking.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours (simple effects, follows established patterns)

## Implementation

### 1. Study existing code:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Effect multimethod pattern (see :draw implementation)
- src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs:46 - :player/life already in schema
- src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs - Add get-life-total query

### 2. Write tests first (TDD):

**Test file:** src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs

| Test Name | Bug It Catches |
|-----------|----------------|
| test-lose-life-reduces-total | Basic arithmetic wrong |
| test-lose-life-zero-is-noop | Spurious state changes on no-op |
| test-lose-life-can-go-negative | Incorrect clamping at 0 |
| test-lose-life-negative-amount-is-noop | Missing input validation |
| test-lose-life-nonexistent-player-is-noop | Crash on invalid player |
| test-gain-life-increases-total | Basic arithmetic wrong |
| test-gain-life-zero-is-noop | Spurious state changes on no-op |
| test-gain-life-accumulates | State not persisting correctly |
| test-gain-life-negative-amount-is-noop | Missing input validation |
| test-gain-life-nonexistent-player-is-noop | Crash on invalid player |

**Assertions for each test:**
- Life total changes by expected amount (or stays same for no-op)
- Other game state unchanged (mana pool, hand, etc.)
- Works with :effect/target for targeting specific player

### 3. Implementation checklist:
- [ ] Add get-life-total query to db/queries.cljs:
  ```clojure
  (defn get-life-total [db player-id]
    (d/q '[:find ?life .
           :in $ ?pid
           :where [?e :player/id ?pid]
                  [?e :player/life ?life]]
         db player-id))
  ```
- [ ] Add (defmethod execute-effect :lose-life ...) to effects.cljs
- [ ] Add (defmethod execute-effect :gain-life ...) to effects.cljs
- [ ] Both read :effect/amount for how much life to change
- [ ] Both read :effect/target (default: player-id) for who is affected
- [ ] Guard: amount <= 0 returns db unchanged (no-op)
- [ ] Guard: nonexistent player returns db unchanged (no-op)
- [ ] Return db directly (not a map wrapper)

### 4. Loss condition check (separate concern):
- Do NOT set :game/loss-condition in these effects
- Loss condition will be checked separately after each action
- Keeps effects focused on single responsibility

## Success Criteria

- [ ] test-lose-life-reduces-total passes: lose 3 from 20 → 17 life
- [ ] test-lose-life-zero-is-noop passes: lose 0 → no change
- [ ] test-lose-life-can-go-negative passes: lose 25 from 20 → -5 life
- [ ] test-lose-life-negative-amount-is-noop passes: lose -5 → no change (not gain)
- [ ] test-lose-life-nonexistent-player-is-noop passes: invalid player → no crash
- [ ] test-gain-life-increases-total passes: gain 5 from 20 → 25 life
- [ ] test-gain-life-zero-is-noop passes: gain 0 → no change
- [ ] test-gain-life-accumulates passes: gain 3 twice from 20 → 26 life
- [ ] test-gain-life-negative-amount-is-noop passes: gain -5 → no change (not lose)
- [ ] test-gain-life-nonexistent-player-is-noop passes: invalid player → no crash
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (make test)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing (make validate)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Negative Amount**
- lose-life with negative amount should NOT be treated as gain-life
- gain-life with negative amount should NOT be treated as lose-life
- Both: amount <= 0 → return db unchanged (no-op)
- Why: Prevents confusing behavior from malformed effect data

**Edge Case: Nonexistent Player**
- If player-id doesn't exist in db, return db unchanged
- Follow same pattern as :draw effect (guard via if-let on query result)
- Why: Prevents crashes from bad effect targets

**Edge Case: nil Amount**
- If :effect/amount is nil, treat as 0 (no-op)
- Use (get effect :effect/amount 0) pattern
- Why: Defensive programming against malformed effects

**Reference Implementation**
- Follow :draw effect pattern at effects.cljs:71-102
- Same guard structure: check amount, check player exists, then modify

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO setting :game/loss-condition in lose-life (separate concern)
- ❌ NO capping life at 0 (MTG allows negative life)
- ❌ NO capping life gain (no maximum life in normal rules)
- ❌ NO crashing on invalid inputs (guard clauses, return db unchanged)
- ❌ NO treating negative amount as opposite effect (lose -5 ≠ gain 5)
close_reason: Closed

title: Task 3: Implement :return-from-graveyard effect type
design:
## Goal
Implement the :return-from-graveyard effect type that allows a player to select 0-N cards from their graveyard to return to hand.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Context
Completed Task 1 (fizzle-156): :exile-self effect implemented.
Completed Task 2 (fizzle-4fe): :discard-hand effect implemented.
Now need :return-from-graveyard for the 'returns up to three cards from graveyard to hand' portion of Ill-Gotten Gains.

## Effect Behavior

Two modes based on :effect/selection:
1. **:player** - Player selects cards via UI (for caster)
2. **:random** - Random selection (for opponent)

Effect keys:
- :effect/target - :self, :opponent, or player-id (defaults to caster)
- :effect/count - Max cards to return (e.g., 3 for Ill-Gotten Gains)
- :effect/selection - :player or :random

## Implementation

### Step 1: Study existing patterns (30 min)
Reference files:
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:111-132 - :mill effect (target resolution pattern)
- src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs:318-343 - :discard-hand effect (just implemented)
- src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs:79-90 - get-objects-in-zone function

**Key difference from tutor:**
- Tutor is from hidden zone (library) - fail-to-find allowed
- Graveyard is public zone - player sees all cards, selects 0 to max-count (NO fail-to-find)

### Step 2: Write 6 failing tests (1 hour)
Add to src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs

**Test scenarios (what bug they catch):**
1. test_return_from_graveyard_player_selection_returns_db_unchanged
   - Bug caught: Effect incorrectly modifies db instead of deferring to UI
2. test_return_from_graveyard_random_selects_up_to_count
   - Bug caught: Random selection ignores count limit
3. test_return_from_graveyard_random_empty_graveyard_is_noop
   - Bug caught: Nil pointer on empty graveyard
4. test_return_from_graveyard_random_fewer_than_count_returns_all
   - Bug caught: Crashes when fewer cards available than requested
5. test_return_from_graveyard_targets_opponent
   - Bug caught: :opponent target not resolved to actual player-id
6. test_return_from_graveyard_with_self_target
   - Bug caught: :self keyword passed directly to queries instead of resolving

### Step 3: Run tests to confirm RED (5 min)
- Command: make test
- Expected: 6 new tests fail (effect type not implemented)

### Step 4: Implement effect handler (2 hours)
File: src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs

Add defmethod after :discard-hand:
```clojure
(defmethod execute-effect-impl :return-from-graveyard
  [db player-id effect _object-id]
  (let [target (get effect :effect/target player-id)
        target-player (cond
                        (= target :opponent) (q/get-opponent-id db player-id)
                        (= target :self) player-id
                        :else target)
        selection (get effect :effect/selection :player)
        count-limit (get effect :effect/count 0)]
    (case selection
      :random (let [gy-cards (q/get-objects-in-zone db target-player :graveyard)
                    selected (take count-limit (shuffle gy-cards))]
                (reduce (fn [db' obj]
                          (zones/move-to-zone db' (:object/id obj) :hand))
                        db
                        selected))
      ;; :player and default - return unchanged (UI handles)
      db)))
```

### Step 5: Run tests to confirm GREEN (5 min)
- Command: make test
- Expected: All 6 new tests pass

### Step 6: Run make validate and commit (10 min)
- Command: make validate
- Commit message: "Add :return-from-graveyard effect type for graveyard selection"

## Success Criteria
- [ ] :return-from-graveyard with :selection :random moves random cards to hand
- [ ] :return-from-graveyard with :selection :player returns db unchanged (deferred to UI)
- [ ] Works with :effect/target :self (resolves to caster player-id)
- [ ] Works with :effect/target :opponent (resolves to opponent player-id)
- [ ] Works with missing :effect/target (defaults to caster)
- [ ] Empty graveyard returns db unchanged (no crash)
- [ ] Fewer cards than :effect/count returns all available cards
- [ ] 6 unit tests pass: make test
- [ ] Existing tests still pass (580+ total): make test
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks passing: make validate

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty Graveyard**
- get-objects-in-zone returns empty vector for empty graveyard
- reduce over empty vector returns db unchanged - graceful no-op
- Test: test_return_from_graveyard_random_empty_graveyard_is_noop

**Edge Case: :effect/count <= 0 or nil**
- (get effect :effect/count 0) defaults to 0 if missing
- (take 0 coll) returns empty seq - graceful no-op
- No special handling needed

**Edge Case: Player doesn't exist**
- get-objects-in-zone returns nil for nonexistent player
- reduce over nil returns db unchanged (nil is seqable)
- No crash, graceful no-op

**Edge Case: :self/:opponent keyword handling**
- MUST resolve :self to player-id before passing to queries
- MUST resolve :opponent via q/get-opponent-id before passing to queries
- Do NOT pass keywords directly to q/get-objects-in-zone
- Reference: :discard-hand implementation uses same pattern

**Random Selection Determinism**
- shuffle returns random permutation
- For testing: use fixed seed or mock (but don't mock zone movements per anti-pattern)
- Test verifies correct COUNT returned, not specific cards

**Reference Implementation Pattern**
- Follow target resolution from :mill (effects.cljs:123-126)
- Follow zone movement from :discard-hand (just implemented)

## Anti-patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO fail-to-find behavior (graveyard is public, player knows what's there)
- ❌ NO modifying tutor selection for this (separate selection type)
- ❌ NO requiring exact count (0 to max-count is valid)
- ❌ NO passing :self/:opponent keywords directly to queries (must resolve first)
- ❌ NO mocking zone movements in tests (test actual zone transitions)
- ❌ NO throwing exceptions on edge cases (graceful no-op)
close_reason: Closed

title: Add missing corner case tests for card modules
description:
~12 missing corner cases in cards:
- Flash of Insight: ANY integration test (only has definition tests), flashback with exile cost
- Orim's Chant: resolution applying restriction, restriction effectiveness
- Seal of Cleansing: activation/resolution flow
- LED: timing restriction
- Ill-Gotten Gains: graveyard return selection, opponent return
- Cephalid Coliseum: threshold resolution (draw 3/discard 3)
- Polluted Delta: full resolution from stack
- Intuition: pile choice flow, < 3 cards in library
- Careful Study: full discard confirmation through production handler
design:
## Goal
Add ~12 missing corner case tests for card modules to catch bugs in card resolution flows.

## Effort Estimate
6-8 hours

## Tests to Add (with specific bug each catches)

### flash_of_insight_test.cljs (HIGHEST PRIORITY — no integration tests exist)
1. test-flash-of-insight-peek-and-select-x2 — Cast with X=2, verify peek-and-select shows top 2 cards, player selects 1. Bug caught: entire card resolution flow untested.
2. test-flash-of-insight-flashback-with-exile-cost — Cast from graveyard via flashback, pay exile cost (remove blue cards from graveyard). Bug caught: flashback + exile cost interaction broken.

### orims_chant_test.cljs
3. test-orims-chant-resolution-applies-restriction — Cast and resolve, verify target player has :cant-cast-spells grant. Bug caught: restriction never applied.
4. test-orims-chant-restricted-player-cannot-cast — After resolution, verify rules/can-cast? returns false for restricted player. Bug caught: restriction exists but not enforced.

### seal_of_cleansing_test.cljs
5. test-seal-of-cleansing-activation-resolution-flow — Activate ability targeting enchantment, resolve from stack, verify enchantment in graveyard and seal sacrificed. Bug caught: ability resolution broken.

### led_test.cljs
6. test-led-timing-restriction — LED should only be activatable as an instant (not during spell casting). Bug caught: LED used to pay for spells in hand.

### ill_gotten_gains_test.cljs
7. test-ill-gotten-gains-graveyard-return-selection — After exile+discard, verify player can select up to 3 cards from graveyard. Bug caught: return selection not created.
8. test-ill-gotten-gains-empty-graveyard — Cast with empty graveyard, verify selection has 0 candidates. Bug caught: crash on empty graveyard.

### cephalid_coliseum_test.cljs
9. test-coliseum-threshold-resolution-draws-and-discards — Activate threshold, resolve from stack, verify target drew 3 and has discard pending. Bug caught: threshold ability resolution broken.

### polluted_delta_test.cljs
10. test-polluted-delta-full-resolution-from-stack — Activate, resolve from stack, verify land search selection created. Bug caught: fetchland stack resolution broken.

### intuition_test.cljs
11. test-intuition-with-fewer-than-3-cards — Cast with 2 cards in library. Bug caught: crash or wrong behavior with insufficient cards.

### careful_study_test.cljs
12. test-careful-study-discard-via-production-handler — Cast, resolve to pending selection, confirm discard through production confirm-discard-selection handler. Bug caught: discard handler broken (current test uses manual reduce).

## Implementation Checklist
- [ ] For each test, read the card definition AND the resolution code path
- [ ] Write test exercising the full cast -> resolve -> effect pipeline
- [ ] Use exact value assertions on zone counts, mana pools, life totals
- [ ] Run make validate after each new test
- [ ] Verify new test count: should add ~12 tests

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 12 tests written and passing
- [ ] Flash of Insight has at least 2 integration tests (currently 0)
- [ ] Each test exercises production resolution code, not just card definition data
- [ ] make validate passes
- [ ] No test is happy-path-only (each tests a specific edge or flow)

## Anti-patterns
- Do NOT test card definition data only (that coverage already exists)
- Do NOT create inline handler copies (the mode_selection anti-pattern)
- Do NOT skip the stack resolution step (cards resolve FROM the stack)
- Do NOT use (is (some? ...)) — use exact assertions
close_reason: Added 12 corner case tests across 9 card modules. All 864 tests pass (1958 assertions). make validate clean.

title: Life <= 0 triggers loss condition

Draw from empty library already sets loss condition - follow same pattern.

## Investigation Needed

- Check how draw effect sets :game/loss-condition :empty-library

2. GREEN: Add life check to lose-life effect
3. RED: Write test that life going negative also triggers loss
4. GREEN: Verify <= 0 check works
5. RED: Write test that game displays loss when condition set
6. VERIFY: All tests pass

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Life going negative also triggers loss
- [ ] Loss condition consistent with empty library pattern

title: Task 5: Core Rules (can-cast?, cast-spell, resolve-spell)
design:
## Goal

Implement the core rules functions that tie together mana, zones, and effects to enable the full Dark Ritual cast→resolve pipeline.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Context
Completed Tasks 1-4: Schema, mana system, zone transitions, effect interpreter.

We have all the building blocks:
- mana/can-pay?, mana/pay-mana, mana/add-mana
- zones/move-to-zone
- effects/execute-effect with :add-mana

Now we need rules.cljs to orchestrate them into can-cast?, cast-spell, resolve-spell.

## Implementation

### 1. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs

(ns fizzle.engine.rules-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
            [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
            [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]
            [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]
            [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]))

;; === can-cast? tests ===

(deftest can-cast-returns-false-without-mana-test
  (testing "can-cast? returns false when player lacks mana"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)]
      (is (false? (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 (:object/id ritual)))))))

(deftest can-cast-returns-true-with-mana-test
  (testing "can-cast? returns true when player has sufficient mana"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)]
      (is (true? (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 (:object/id ritual)))))))

(deftest can-cast-returns-false-wrong-zone-test
  (testing "can-cast? returns false when card not in hand (on stack)"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 2}))  ;; Extra mana for test
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          obj-id (:object/id ritual)
          ;; Move card to stack (simulating cast without using cast-spell)
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db obj-id :stack)]
      ;; Card is on stack, not in hand - should return false
      (is (false? (rules/can-cast? db' :player-1 obj-id))))))

;; === cast-spell tests ===

(deftest cast-spell-moves-to-stack-test
  (testing "cast-spell moves card from hand to stack"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 (:object/id ritual))]
      (is (= 0 (count (q/get-hand db' :player-1))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :stack)))))))

(deftest cast-spell-pays-mana-test
  (testing "cast-spell deducts mana cost from pool"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 2}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 (:object/id ritual))]
      (is (= 1 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest cast-spell-increments-storm-test
  (testing "cast-spell increments storm count"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 (:object/id ritual))]
      (is (= 1 (q/get-storm-count db' :player-1))))))

;; === resolve-spell tests ===

(deftest resolve-spell-executes-effects-test
  (testing "resolve-spell executes card effects"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (-> db
                  (rules/cast-spell :player-1 (:object/id ritual))
                  (rules/resolve-spell :player-1 (:object/id ritual)))]
      ;; Dark Ritual adds BBB
      (is (= 3 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest resolve-spell-moves-to-graveyard-test
  (testing "resolve-spell moves card from stack to graveyard"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (-> db
                  (rules/cast-spell :player-1 (:object/id ritual))
                  (rules/resolve-spell :player-1 (:object/id ritual)))]
      (is (= 0 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :stack))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :graveyard)))))))

;; === Full pipeline test ===

(deftest dark-ritual-full-pipeline-test
  (testing "Dark Ritual: cast with B, resolve to get BBB, storm = 1"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          obj-id (:object/id ritual)

          ;; Verify can cast
          _ (is (true? (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 obj-id)))

          ;; Cast it
          db-cast (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 obj-id)
          _ (is (= 0 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db-cast :player-1))))
          _ (is (= 1 (q/get-storm-count db-cast :player-1)))
          _ (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db-cast :player-1 :stack))))

          ;; Resolve it
          db-resolved (rules/resolve-spell db-cast :player-1 obj-id)]
      (is (= 3 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db-resolved :player-1))))
      (is (= 0 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db-resolved :player-1 :stack))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db-resolved :player-1 :graveyard)))))))
```

### 2. Implementation checklist

- [ ] Create src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs - Unit tests (9 tests)
- [ ] Run tests RED (namespace not found)
- [ ] Create src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs - Core rules
  - (can-cast? db player-id object-id) -> boolean
  - (cast-spell db player-id object-id) -> db
  - (resolve-spell db player-id object-id) -> db
- [ ] Run tests GREEN (all pass)
- [ ] Commit changes

### 3. Key implementation details

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.rules
  "Core game rules for Fizzle.

   Orchestrates mana, zones, and effects into casting operations.
   All functions are pure: (db, args) -> db"
  (:require [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
            [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]
            [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]
            [fizzle.engine.effects :as effects]))

(defn can-cast?
  "Check if a player can cast a card.
   
   Requires:
   - Card is in player's hand
   - Player has sufficient mana
   
   Returns false if object doesn't exist."
  [db player-id object-id]
  (let [obj (q/get-object db object-id)]
    (when obj
      (let [card (:object/card obj)
            cost (:card/mana-cost card)]
        (and (= :hand (:object/zone obj))
             (mana/can-pay? db player-id cost))))))

(defn- increment-storm
  "Increment a player's storm count."
  [db player-id]
  (let [player-eid (q/get-player-eid db player-id)
        current (q/get-storm-count db player-id)]
    (d/db-with db [[:db/add player-eid :player/storm-count (inc current)]])))

(defn cast-spell
  "Cast a spell from hand.
   
   - Pays mana cost
   - Moves card to stack
   - Increments storm count
   
   Caller should verify can-cast? first."
  [db player-id object-id]
  (let [obj (q/get-object db object-id)
        card (:object/card obj)
        cost (:card/mana-cost card)]
    (-> db
        (mana/pay-mana player-id cost)
        (zones/move-to-zone object-id :stack)
        (increment-storm player-id))))

(defn resolve-spell
  "Resolve a spell on the stack.
   
   - Executes all effects
   - Moves card to graveyard"
  [db player-id object-id]
  (let [obj (q/get-object db object-id)
        card (:object/card obj)
        effects-list (:card/effects card)]
    (as-> db db'
      (reduce (fn [d effect] (effects/execute-effect d player-id effect))
              db'
              (or effects-list []))  ;; Handle nil effects gracefully
      (zones/move-to-zone db' object-id :graveyard))))
```

### 4. Verification

```bash
export PATH="/usr/local/opt/openjdk/bin:$PATH"
npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] can-cast? returns false when player lacks mana
- [ ] can-cast? returns true when player has sufficient mana
- [ ] can-cast? returns false when card not in hand (on stack)
- [ ] cast-spell moves card from hand to stack
- [ ] cast-spell deducts mana cost from pool
- [ ] cast-spell increments storm count
- [ ] resolve-spell executes card effects (adds BBB)
- [ ] resolve-spell moves card from stack to graveyard
- [ ] Full pipeline test passes: cast with B, resolve to get BBB, storm = 1
- [ ] All 9 rules tests pass
- [ ] Verification command passes: npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Non-existent object-id**
- get-object returns nil for invalid object-id
- can-cast? wrapped in (when obj ...) to return nil/false gracefully
- cast-spell/resolve-spell: Caller should verify can-cast? first per contract
- Design decision: No defensive checks in cast-spell/resolve-spell (per anti-pattern "caller checks can-cast?")

**Edge Case: Card with nil effects**
- Dark Ritual has effects, but future cards might not
- resolve-spell uses (or effects-list []) to handle nil gracefully
- reduce on empty vector is no-op, safe

**Edge Case: Card with nil mana cost (free spell)**
- mana/can-pay? returns true for nil/empty cost (already tested in mana_test)
- mana/pay-mana handles nil/empty cost as no-op (already tested)
- No special handling needed in rules.cljs

**Why wrong-zone test requires zones/move-to-zone**
- Initial state has card in hand only
- Must move card to stack to test can-cast? returns false for non-hand cards
- Test explicitly calls zones/move-to-zone (not cast-spell) to isolate the can-cast? behavior
- Catches bug: can-cast? not checking :object/zone

**Function Contract: Caller Responsibility**
- can-cast? is safe to call with any inputs (returns false/nil gracefully)
- cast-spell and resolve-spell assume can-cast? was verified
- This matches MTG rules: you can't cast if not legal, validation happens before cast

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO validation/throwing in cast-spell (caller checks can-cast?)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in rules (effects are data-driven)
- ❌ NO side effects (purity: rules transform db)
- ❌ NO defensive nil checks in cast-spell/resolve-spell (violates contract)
close_reason: Closed

title: Split events/game.cljs into focused namespaces
description:
## Problem
events/game.cljs is 2,613 lines with 39 event handlers + 40 helper functions. Selection logic is scattered across 3 sections. Forward declarations indicate internal circular deps.

## Specifics
- Lines 1-170: Game init
- Lines 172-250: Card selection and casting
- Lines 253-540: Stack resolution, phases, turns
- Lines 543-707: Land play
- Lines 708-850: Mana ability activation
- Lines 843-1050: Ability activation and targeting
- Lines 1051-2100: Selection system (tutor, discard, scry, exile, X-costs)
- Lines 2093-2543: Selection confirmation handlers

## Proposed Split
- events/core.cljs - init-game, cast-spell, resolve-top, land play, phase management
- events/selection.cljs - All selection system (build, execute, confirm) ~1400 lines
- events/abilities.cljs - Ability activation handlers
- events/targeting.cljs - Targeting confirmation

## Why Fix Now
Unblocks refactoring the spell/trigger stack code. Also reduces cognitive load for Phase 3 development.

## Success Criteria
- events/game.cljs under 800 lines
- No forward declarations needed
- Clear namespace boundaries
- All tests still pass
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)
- Split events/game.cljs (2,613 lines) into 3 focused namespaces
- game.cljs retains core game loop: init, casting, resolution, phases, cleanup, land, tap
- selection.cljs gets ALL selection logic: detection, builders, resolution, confirmation handlers, cast-time targeting
- abilities.cljs gets ALL ability activation: mana abilities + non-mana abilities
- Dependency direction is a DAG: game→selection, game→abilities, abilities→selection (maybe), selection→(engine only)
- selection.cljs NEVER directly requires game.cljs (dispatches re-frame events instead)
- All 13 forward declarations eliminated by the namespace split
- All existing tests pass without modification to test logic (only import paths change)
- game.cljs under 800 lines after extraction

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)
- [ ] game.cljs under 800 lines
- [ ] Zero forward declarations across all 3 files
- [ ] No circular namespace dependencies
- [ ] All tests pass (make test)
- [ ] make validate passes (lint + format + tests)
- [ ] Each namespace has clear, cohesive responsibility
- [ ] Re-frame event registration still works (namespaces loaded at startup)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)
- ❌ NO circular namespace dependencies (architecture: DAG dependency direction is critical)
- ❌ NO new forward declarations in any split file (quality: declares indicate ordering problems)
- ❌ NO shared utility namespace for 'helpers' (simplicity: helpers belong with their callers)
- ❌ NO changing function signatures or behavior (safety: pure structural refactoring only)
- ❌ NO splitting selection into sub-namespaces (scope: single selection.cljs per brainstorming decision)
- ❌ NO modifying test assertions (safety: only import paths should change in tests)
- ❌ NO big-bang extraction (safety: extract one namespace at a time, test between each)

## Approach
Extract in 3 phases, testing after each:
1. Extract abilities.cljs (smallest, ~320 lines, least coupled)
2. Extract selection.cljs (largest, ~1,500 lines, most complex)
3. Clean up: verify zero declares, verify dependency direction, final validation

game.cljs stays as the core file to minimize import changes across codebase.
selection.cljs communicates back to game via rf/dispatch (runtime), not direct function calls (compile-time).

## Architecture

### Namespace Layout
- events/game.cljs (~625 lines) - init, casting, resolution, phases, cleanup, land, tap
- events/selection.cljs (~1,500 lines) - all selection detection, builders, resolution, confirmation
- events/abilities.cljs (~320 lines) - mana + non-mana ability activation

### Dependency Direction (DAG)
game.cljs → selection.cljs (resolve-spell-with-selection, resolve-activated-ability-with-selection)
game.cljs → abilities.cljs (if direct function calls needed)
abilities.cljs → selection.cljs (if ability resolution calls selection functions)
selection.cljs → engine/* only (plus rf/dispatch for game events)

### Function Placement

**game.cljs keeps:**
- init-game, init-test-game (game init)
- initiate-cast-with-mode, cast-spell (casting)
- resolve-top-of-stack (calls into selection.cljs)
- advance-phase, begin-upkeep, draw-step, begin-main-phase (turns)
- begin-cleanup, cleanup-discard, maybe-continue-cleanup (cleanup)
- play-land, land events (land play)
- tap-permanent (tap)

**selection.cljs gets:**
- has-selection-effect?, find-selection-effect-index (detection)
- All build-*-selection functions (builders)
- resolve-spell-with-selection (spell resolution)
- resolve-activated-ability-with-selection (ability resolution)
- All ::confirm-*-selection event handlers (confirmation)
- X-cost detection and selection (has-exile-cards-x-cost?, build-x-mana-selection, etc.)
- execute-peek-selection, execute-pile-choice, etc. (execution)
- build-cast-time-target-selection, confirm-cast-time-target (cast-time targeting)

**abilities.cljs gets:**
- Mana ability activation (lines 732-848)
- Non-mana ability activation (lines 850-1050)
- Related helper functions

## Design Rationale
### Problem
events/game.cljs is 2,613 lines with 39 event handlers and 13 forward declarations. This blocks refactoring the spell/trigger stack (fizzle-d3i P1). Selection logic (~1,400 lines) dominates the file.

### Research Findings
**Codebase:**
- 13 forward declarations are ordering issues, not true circular deps
- Selection system follows consistent builder/confirm pattern
- Cast-time targeting uses same pattern as other selections
- Re-frame event dispatch provides runtime decoupling

### Approaches Considered
#### 1. 3-file split (game, selection, abilities) ✓
Chosen because: fewest files while meeting <800 line target. Cast-time targeting naturally belongs with selection.

#### 2. 4-file split (+ separate targeting.cljs) ❌
REJECTED BECAUSE: targeting.cljs would be only ~95 lines and uses same builder/confirm pattern as other selections.
DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS: targeting grows to 200+ lines.

#### 3. 5-file split (+ separate turn.cljs) ❌
REJECTED BECAUSE: resolution and turn/phase logic are tightly coupled via cleanup cycle.
DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS: game.cljs exceeds 800 lines after extraction.

### Scope Boundaries
In scope: structural split, import updates, forward declaration elimination
Out of scope: behavior changes, new features, test logic changes, selection sub-splitting

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made
| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Split selection further? | Single selection.cljs | Keep all ~1,400 lines together |
| Forward declarations? | Eliminate via split | Split should naturally resolve ordering |
| Keep game.cljs or rename? | Keep game.cljs as core | Minimize import changes across codebase |
| Which split approach? | 3-file split | game.cljs + selection.cljs + abilities.cljs |

### Dead-End Paths
#### Selection sub-namespace tree
Why explored: Selection is ~1,400 lines with 6+ subsystems
Why abandoned: User prefers cohesion over granularity. Subsystems share pattern.

#### Renaming game.cljs to core.cljs
Why explored: Ticket proposed events/core.cljs
Why abandoned: Would require updating all imports. Keeping game.cljs minimizes changes.
close_reason: All 3 namespaces split successfully. game.cljs=728 lines, selection.cljs=1608 lines, abilities.cljs=302 lines. Zero forward declarations. No circular deps. All 741 tests pass. make validate clean.

title: Win detection and lethal announcement
description: Detect lethal conditions and announce victory with stats. For Brain Freeze: compare storm count * 3 vs opponent cards remaining in library. For drain effects: compare damage vs opponent life. Display announcement with stats: turn killed, storm count, cards left in opponent library. Consider: non-intrusive notification (banner or modal), option to continue playing after lethal detected, track near-misses (e.g. storm count was 1 short).
close_reason: Implemented via epic fizzle-8vuo

title: Brain Freeze: allow targeting any player with storm copy splitting
description: Brain Freeze currently targets only the opponent. Update to allow targeting any player (self or opponent). Complex because storm copies need target selection: each copy could target a different player. Need to decide UX for splitting storm copies across targets and the ordering of resolution. Key questions: (1) Do all copies share one target or can each copy be individually targeted? (2) If split targeting, what's the UI for assigning copies to targets? (3) Resolution order matters for threshold/graveyard count checks between copies.
close_reason: Implemented via epic fizzle-qmos: Brain Freeze now targets any player at cast time, storm copies distribute via split UI
